# Alpha — Frontend Design Specification (for Claude Design)
## One unified private-markets copilot · every surface at depth · wired to the backend contract

> **Give this whole document to Claude Design.** It builds the **complete frontend web app** for Alpha — one copilot, all 25 use-case surfaces, the persistent frame, Ask Alpha, the cohort builder, the advisor cockpit, and the notification layer — to the design system in Part 2 and the per-surface specs in Part 4. Use **realistic placeholder data shaped exactly like the API responses** (Part 0.3) so swapping to the live backend is a base-URL change. Every surface must render all **seven states** (Part 9), not just the happy path. Build it **premium, calm, trustworthy, and above all simple** — the primary user is "a doctor, not a pro."

---

## PART 0 — How to build from this

### 0.1 What to produce
A single **self-contained, clickable, responsive web app** (one design system, many screens), plus: a short design rationale, the **design tokens** file, and a **screen × state inventory** (every surface in every applicable state). One coherent product — not 25 separate dashboards.

### 0.2 The non-negotiables (apply everywhere; do not deviate)
- **Numbers come from the engine; the UI presents and the chat explains.** Every numeric element renders its **provenance** ("show how this was computed"), its **confidence** (n + CI), and its **coverage %** where applicable — structurally, as part of the component (Part 2.6). A number never appears bare.
- **Relative, not absolute.** Lead with %-of-wealth, %-per-investment, # vintages/commitments — never headline absolute totals.
- **Average where bounded, median where skewed** (tickets/commitments show median + range).
- **No scrolling on analytical views** — Meta ⇄ Detail are full screens, like Excel sheets; the frame persists.
- **% inline on slices/bars** (never only a side legend). **Z-charts render large.** A **1–2 sentence caption** sits under every chart. The cohort is labelled **"Peers"** (never "All"). Comparisons that matter are **side-by-side, not behind a toggle**, unless the surface says toggle.
- **Synthetic/illustrative fields are visibly labelled** and never styled as verified fact.
- **No flashy transitions.** Subtle, serious, boardroom-projector legible.

### 0.3 Data binding — the API contract (use these shapes for placeholder data)
Base URL `{{API}}` (e.g. `http://localhost:8800`). Every numeric value arrives as `{ "value": …, "provenance": { method, n, ci95:[lo,hi], coverage_pct, significant, synthetic, vintage_scope, snapshot_id } }`. Bind the **value** to the visual and the **provenance** to the badge/popover. Key endpoints (full list per surface in Part 4):
`GET /api/meta` · `POST /api/cohort` · `GET /api/portfolio/you` · `GET /api/detail/{strategy|region|format|vintage|lookthrough|performance|kpi}` · `GET /api/architect|pacing|liquidity|projection` · `GET /api/funds|fund/{id}|fund_compare` · `GET /api/recommendations|next_best` · `POST /api/explain` · `GET /api/why_not|missed_opportunities|manager_patterns|macro` · `GET /api/demand|secondary|sentiment` (→ `{status:"not_connected"}`) · `POST /api/chat` · `GET /api/follower_alert` · `GET /api/reference`. `investor_id` is an optional query param everywhere.

### 0.4 How to read a surface spec (Part 4)
Each surface has: **Purpose · Entry/nav · Layout (grid) · Components & charts (exact encodings) · Interactions · Data bindings (element ← endpoint.field) · The seven states · Microcopy · Acceptance.** Depth ≥ the reference example; deeper where the surface is richer.

---

## PART 1 — Product, personas, modes

**Product.** A private-markets operating system in one surface: construct → compare → understand → forecast → act → evaluate → learn → converse. The differentiator the UI must *feel*: proprietary comparison data, made simple and trustworthy.

**Personas (design for all four; mode-switch via the top-right account menu / entitlements):**
- **Investor — analytical:** charts first, drills, toggles cohorts. Lands on the Meta-View.
- **Investor — non-technical ("five sentences"):** chat first; the dashboard is secondary. Ask Alpha must enlarge to full-screen chat-only and shrink back.
- **Advisor / RM (first-launch audience):** the cockpit (Part 7) — a book of clients, "who to call & why," impersonate-with-audit into any client's copilot.
- **Internal (Moonfare sales/product/IC):** aggregate-only consoles (sentiment, demand, manager patterns) — never per-identifiable-client.

**Modes:** Investor · Advisor · Internal · Demo (pinned snapshot, scripted walkthrough). Mode sets navigation, data scope, and which surfaces show.

---

## PART 2 — Design system (build this first; everything composes from it)

### 2.1 Brand & mood
Fresh, bright, light. **Moonfare blue/indigo (~#2C2DFE family)** as primary; **mint/green** + lighter blue tints for data-viz; **cream/beige** for hero/landing cards; the real **Moonfare logo** top-left and inside notification chrome. The look-and-feel of the Moonfare web product. Calm, premium, serious — wealth-grade, not fintech-flashy.

### 2.2 Color tokens (define light + dark; everything references tokens, never raw hex)
- **Brand:** `--brand` indigo · `--brand-tint-1…3` (progressively lighter, for fills) · `--accent-mint`.
- **Data ramp (sequential, color-blind-safe):** `--data-1 … --data-9` across the 9 strategies — a blue→teal→mint progression; pin a fixed strategy→color map so a strategy is the same colour on every chart.
- **Surfaces:** `--bg` (app) · `--surface` (cards) · `--surface-hero` (cream) · `--ink` (text) · `--muted` (secondary text) · `--line` (hairlines).
- **Semantic (over/under, never colour-only):** `--over` (you above ref) + `▲`, `--under` + `▼`; `--positive`/`--negative` for performance. Always pair colour with a glyph/sign for accessibility.
- **State:** `--synthetic` (a soft amber tag), `--gated` (locked grey), `--not-connected` (dashed outline).
Provide a **dark-mode parallel** for every token; a top-bar **light/dark/hybrid** toggle.

### 2.3 Typography
One humanist sans (brand font). Scale: `display 32/40` · `h1 24/32` · `h2 18/26` · `body 15/22` · `caption 13/18` · `mono` (tabular figures) for all numbers. **Chart titles are large** (recorded complaint) — use h2 minimum. Legends never below caption size. Numbers always tabular/mono so columns align.

### 2.4 Spacing, radius, elevation, motion
4-pt spacing grid; card radius ~12px; minimal shadow (1 soft elevation). Motion ≤150ms ease; no parallax/bounce/confetti. Charts animate value-in once, subtly, then settle.

### 2.5 Chart language (locked — follow exactly; this is where most products go wrong)
**Approved (use only these):**
- **Horizontal bar** — allocation %, fill rate (rows You/Peers/Platform/Model; dashed target marker).
- **100% stacked horizontal bar** — strategy mix across strategies.
- **Vertical bars** — performance (DPI/MOIC/IRR; **numbers inside the bars**; You/Peers/Platform side-by-side) and format (one bar per format).
- **Treemap** — sector look-through (tile = sector, sized by weight, `--data-*` gradient).
- **Vintage × Strategy heat-map** — vintage exposure.
- **Divergence / Z-chart** — over/underweight per strategy; **rendered large**; bars diverge from a centre 0 line; `--over`/`--under`.
- **Twin donuts** — You (left, fixed) vs Comparison (right, swaps); **% inline on slices**, fund/strategy names inside, no separate legend where avoidable.
- **Grouped bars** — look-through You/Peers/Platform per sector.
- **Peer scorecard** — small-multiple stat cards (you vs peer median).
- **Forecast bands** — cashflow/projection (base line + shaded optimistic/downside band).

**Rejected (never use):** radar · beeswarm · quartile ladder · Z-score deviation viz · **Sankey / capital-flow** · ridgeline · box-whisker grid · pacing-vs-performance scatter · fund constellation · adoption lollipops · histogram (unless the data genuinely demands it).

**Product-wide chart rules:** % inline on slices/bars; 1–2 sentence caption under every chart; large titles; thin bars (not fat); tight pie↔legend; show the user's actual target % inline on Fill-Rate; label the cohort "Peers"; sub-CI deltas render greyed/"not significant"; every chart sourced from a single value+provenance binding.

### 2.6 Component library (build once; every surface composes from these)
Each component is responsive and has explicit hover/focus/empty/loading/error states. Numeric components **embed** the three badges so a number can't render without them.

- **ProfileBar** — top frame; "You" + editable chips (age, wealth, target %, profession, **risk appetite [5-class]**, horizon, children); risk & horizon are toggles that re-render the page; collapsible. (Advisor mode: a client selector replaces "You".)
- **CohortBuilder** — left rail (Part 6).
- **AskAlphaPane** — draggable floating → full-screen (Part 5).
- **MetaCard** — a titled card with a chart + caption + badges (the 2×2 building block).
- **TwinDonut** — left "Your portfolio" fixed + right "Comparison" that swaps on Z-bar click or ComparisonSelector; inline %.
- **ZChart** — large divergence chart; click a bar → drives the TwinDonut and emits the strategy.
- **ComparisonSelector** — segmented control: `Peers · Platform · Model · Harvard · Yale · Princeton · Aggregate` (drives Z-chart + right donut).
- **BarRow** — horizontal allocation/fill rows with dashed target marker + inline %.
- **PerfBars** — vertical performance bars, numbers inside, side-by-side series, with a "comparable-vintage" caption.
- **GroupedBars**, **Treemap**, **HeatMap**, **ForecastBand**, **Scorecard** — per 2.5.
- **FundCard** — Moonfare fund-listing layout: image right, name + access-feeder line, two badges (region + strategy), stats row (Target Fund Size / Target Net MOIC / Expected Close / Min. investment), CTA "Contact your sales associate."
- **ProvenancePopover** — click a number → cohort + n + fields + method + snapshot; "show the data."
- **ConfidenceBadge** — `n=41 · ±2.3` chip; greys the value when not significant.
- **CoverageBadge** — `look-through 61% of NAV` chip on any look-through figure.
- **SyntheticTag** — amber "illustrative" pill on imputed fields (target %, wealth, risk class, children, follower).
- **GatedState** — locked card: "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate" + the headline "5,500 total investors."
- **RelaxBanner** — "Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band" (words, from `relaxed_dims`).
- **NotConnectedState** — dashed card: "Live when the {feed} connects" (for demand/secondary/sentiment).
- **iPhoneFrame** — phone mock for the Follower push.
- **PDFExportButton** — exports the current view as a clean, PowerPoint-style PDF.
- **CaptionBlock**, **RMQueueRow**, **FeedbackButtons** (Advisor).

---

## PART 3 — Global frame & navigation (one copilot)

A single persistent frame wraps every analytical surface; only the centre stage changes between Meta and Detail.

**Layout (desktop ≥1280px):**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Moonfare logo]  Compare yourself to your peers.        [light/dark][mode]│  ← ProfileBar (top, collapsible)
│ You · 53 · €25m · target 20% · Consulting · Balanced · 7y · 2 kids        │
├───────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────┤
│ COHORT    │                                                   │ Ask     │
│ BUILDER   │            CENTRE STAGE                            │ Alpha   │
│ (left,    │   Meta-View  ⇄  Detail page (full screen)         │ (float, │
│ fixed     │   ← Meta/Detail tabs or rail; no scroll           │ drag →  │
│ when open)│                                                   │ full)   │
│  Follower │   [PDF export ▾]                                  │         │
└───────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────┘
```
- **ProfileBar** (Part 2.6): risk & horizon chips are toggles that re-render the page; age & wealth not editable; SyntheticTag on target %, wealth, risk if imputed. Collapses to a slim bar.
- **Cohort builder** (Part 6): collapsible, fixed when open; hosts Follower controls.
- **Meta ⇄ Detail navigation:** a left rail (or top tab strip) lists the surfaces grouped — *Overview · Allocation · Region · Format · Vintage · Look-through · Performance · KPIs · Architect · Pacing · Liquidity · Projection · Funds · What's next · Explain · Why-not · Missed · Managers · Macro · (Advisor) Cockpit · (Internal) Consoles.* Switching is instant, full-screen, **no scroll**; the frame persists.
- **Ask Alpha** floats bottom-right, draggable, enlargeable to full-screen (Part 5).
- **PDF export** on every view.
- **Responsive:** ≥1280 three-column; 768–1279 collapse cohort builder to a drawer + Ask Alpha to a launcher button; <768 (mobile, advisor on the go) single-column stacked, charts reflow, ProfileBar becomes a sheet. No analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll.

---

## PART 4 — The 25 use-case surfaces (each at depth)

> Order = the left-rail order. Every surface inherits the Detail template (Part 3) and renders the seven states (Part 9). "← binds" shows element ← API field.

---
### S1 · Overview / Meta-View  *(Use case T1-5 Peer + T2-2 Model)*
**Purpose.** The one-glance answer: where you stand vs peers, platform, model — allocation, fill, mix, over/underweight. The default investor landing.
**Entry/nav.** Default after Welcome; "Overview" in the rail.
**Layout.** A 2×2 grid of MetaCards, equal weight, no scroll.
**Components & charts:**
- **TL — Private Markets Allocation** (BarRow, horizontal): rows **You / Peers (median) / Platform (avg) / Model**, a **dashed target marker**, inline %, and a **Rank** pill ("Top 18%"). SyntheticTag if the wealth denominator is illustrative.
- **TR — Fill Rate** (BarRow): **all four series side-by-side** — You vs {your target, peer target, platform, model target}; the user's **actual target % shown inline**; FOMO state (a 0% new investor) framed as "headroom," not failure.
- **BL — Strategy Mix** (TwinDonut or single donut with ComparisonSelector `You/Peers/Platform/Model`): % inline on slices, fixed strategy→colour.
- **BR — Over/underweight Z-chart** (ZChart, large): ComparisonSelector `vs Peers/Platform/Model`; clicking a bar deep-links to S2 with that strategy selected.
Each card: title (large), the chart, a 1–2 sentence CaptionBlock, ConfidenceBadge (n+CI), ProvenancePopover.
**Interactions.** ComparisonSelector swaps series; cohort changes (left rail) re-resolve and re-render **only affected cards**, not the page; click a Z-bar → S2.
**Data bindings.** Cards ← `GET /api/meta` → `pm_allocation`, `fill_rate`, `strategy_mix`, `z_chart`, `cohort{n,relaxed_dims}`. Rank ← `pm_allocation.rank`.
**States.** Cold-start → show Peers/Model only, "your portfolio will appear here," CTA to Architect. Thin → RelaxBanner from `relaxed_dims`. Gated → GatedState in the affected card. Null perf → suppress that metric.
**Microcopy.** Title "Compare yourself to your peers." Caption examples: "You hold 16% of your wealth in private markets — above the 9% peer median, below the 18% model." 
**Acceptance.** All four cards bind to one `/api/meta` call; Peers ≠ Model visibly differ; every number has a badge + popover; cohort change moves the numbers.

---
### S2 · Strategy deep-dive  *(T1-5 detail + Weighted-Average Portfolio)*
**Purpose.** Your strategy mix and the concrete funds behind it, vs the peer "weighted-average portfolio."
**Entry/nav.** "Allocation" rail; or click a Z-bar on S1.
**Layout.** Top: 100% stacked horizontal bar of strategy mix. Below: **strategy chip selector** → **TwinDonut**: left = YOUR funds for that strategy (**fund names + logos inside the pie**, no side legend), right = the comparison cohort's **Weighted Average Portfolio** (most-held named funds, k-anon).
**Interactions.** Pick a strategy chip → both donuts update; ComparisonSelector swaps the right donut (Peers/Platform/Model). Hover a slice → fund name + %, ProvenancePopover.
**Data bindings.** ← `GET /api/detail/strategy?strategy=&comparison=` → `your_mix`, `your_funds`, `weighted_average_portfolio[]` (use `k_anon_ok` to show name vs bucket label).
**States.** k-anon fail → bucket label ("a large-cap buyout fund"). Cold-start → "no holdings yet" + Architect CTA. Thin → RelaxBanner.
**Microcopy.** "Your buyout sleeve is three funds; your peers concentrate in EQT and CVC."
**Acceptance.** Fund names render inside the pie; sub-k-anon funds bucketed; right donut swaps on selector + Z-bar.

---
### S3 · Region
**Purpose.** Portfolio geographic exposure vs peers.
**Layout.** Left: donut US/Europe/Asia/RoW (inline %). Right: large ZChart over/underweight; ComparisonSelector incl. endowments.
**Interactions.** Selector swaps comparison; the donut shows You↔Peer toggle.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/detail/region` → `you`, `peer`, `z_chart`.
**States.** Default; thin → RelaxBanner.
**Acceptance.** Inline %; Z large; caption present.

---
### S4 · Investment Format
**Purpose.** Primary / Secondary / Direct·Co-Invest / Semi-liquid / Fund-of-Fund split.
**Layout.** **One vertical bar per format** showing % filled (bars sum to 100% across formats but render separately, NOT a single 100%-stacked bar), % inside each bar; ZChart beside.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/detail/format` → `you`, `peer`, `z_chart`.
**Acceptance.** Separate bars per format (not stacked); % inside.

---
### S5 · Vintage
**Purpose.** % committed per vintage year + # vintages; concentration/bunching.
**Layout.** Bar of % per vintage (relative, not absolute €) + a **Vintage×Strategy heat-map** companion; a bunching flag chip.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/detail/vintage` → `vintage_pct`, `n_vintages` (+ pacing bunching from `/api/pacing`).
**Acceptance.** Relative %; heat-map present; bunching flagged when concentrated.

---
### S6 · Look-through / Concentration  *(T1-9)*
**Purpose.** Real underlying exposure beneath the funds; hidden concentration; AI look-through.
**Layout.** **Treemap** (tile = sector, sized by weight, `--data-*` gradient) with a prominent **CoverageBadge** ("look-through covers 61% of your NAV"); a delta column vs cohort (▲/▼); a greyed **peer overlay**; a **company-overlap** list (k-anon); an **AI read** card: "Stated AI 15% · estimated economic AI ~28%" with a SyntheticTag on the embedded estimate + its assumption note.
**Interactions.** ComparisonSelector (Peers/Best/Model) updates the delta column. Hover a tile → sector %, coverage.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/detail/lookthrough` → `sector_weights{value,coverage_pct}`, `ai{stated,embedded,total,assumption}`, `concentration_hhi`.
**States.** **Low coverage is the headline state** — badge must be unmissable; never imply 100%. Missing look-through feeders → "based on X% of NAV."
**Acceptance.** Coverage % on every figure; embedded-AI carries its assumption label; company-overlap k-anon'd.

---
### S7 · Performance  *(T1-12)*
**Purpose.** DPI / MOIC / IRR, cohort-relative, vintage-honest.
**Layout.** **Vertical PerfBars**, **numbers inside the bars**, **You / Peers / Platform side-by-side (no toggle)**; a **"comparable-vintage" caption**; young vintages show **"too early to rank"** instead of an IRR number; DPI (realized) and RVPI (marks) visually distinct.
**Interactions.** "You vs Top 10%" toggle; vintage filter.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/detail/performance` → per-vintage rows `{you_dpi, you_rvpi, you_irr|null, peer_dpi, top_decile_dpi, irr_note}`.
**States.** Null/young → "too early to rank" with the note; never zero-fill.
**Acceptance.** No cross-vintage ranking; IRR suppressed <4y shows the note; DPI≠RVPI visually.

---
### S8 · KPIs
**Purpose.** Vintage distribution · ⌀ ticket (median) · # commitments — relative, no absolute headline totals.
**Layout.** Three Scorecards You vs Peer median.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/detail/kpi` → `median_ticket_eur`, `n_commitments`, `n_vintages`.
**Acceptance.** Median ticket (not mean); no absolute-total hero number.

---
### S9 · Allocation Architect  *(T1-1)*
**Purpose.** Your optimal target architecture; the **cold-start home** (works with no holdings).
**Layout.** BarRow target-vs-current per strategy; a **build-to-N** plan (timeline of N funds over the horizon); a **core/satellite** split (two grouped blocks); a PM-% headline shown as a **band** (e.g. "17% · range 14–20%"); comparators "if I took more risk" and "vs a single family office."
**Interactions.** **Risk slider** (the 5 classes) re-renders the target live; horizon toggle adjusts illiquidity tolerance.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/architect` → `pm_target_pct(+range)`, `pm_target_eur`, `strategy_target`, `build_to_n`, `core`, `satellite`, `comparators`.
**States.** **Cold-start = primary:** render the full target with `current=∅`; the FE routes a new investor here from Welcome. Null wealth → show % but not €.
**Microcopy.** "For someone with your profile, a balanced private-markets architecture targets ~17% of wealth across ~20 funds."
**Acceptance.** Risk slider re-renders target; PM-% shown as a band; cold-start renders fully; SyntheticTag on assigned inputs.

---
### S10 · Pacing  *(T1-4)*
**Purpose.** How to spread commitments over 3–5y by year/vintage/strategy/ticket.
**Layout.** A year-by-year commitment schedule (stacked bars by strategy, € and #), a **bunching flag**, a best-performer pacing overlay (ghost line).
**Interactions.** Drag the annual budget → schedule recomputes; toggle "smooth J-curve."
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/pacing` → `plan[]`, `bunching_flag`, `peer_overlay`.
**Acceptance.** Schedule sums to target; bunching flagged; peer overlay shown.

---
### S11 · Liquidity & Cashflow  *(T1-7)*
**Purpose.** Capital calls, distributions, overcommitment, investable capacity.
**Layout.** A **ForecastBand** chart (calls vs distributions over 8 quarters, base line + optimistic/downside shading), an **overcommitment flag** chip, a **capacity** headline ("you can commit ~€X more").
**Interactions.** "What if I add Fund Y" overlays a new call curve.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/liquidity` → `calls{base,opt,down}`, `distributions{}`, `investable_capacity_eur`, `overcommitment_flag`.
**States.** Overcommitted → red flag + "wait for distributions."
**Acceptance.** Bands shaded + ordered; capacity number present; add-fund overlay works.

---
### S12 · Projection  *(T1-13)*
**Purpose.** Forward NAV / DPI under scenarios.
**Layout.** ForecastBand NAV path with **scenario toggles** (base/optimistic/downside; +named fund; +secondaries) and a visible **assumptions panel** (SyntheticTag — macro assumptions illustrative).
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/projection?scenario=&add_fund=` → `nav_path_bands`, `dpi_path`, `crossover_quarter`, `assumptions`.
**Acceptance.** Scenario toggles change the band; assumptions surfaced + tagged.

---
### S13 · Funds / Fund-vs-Fund  *(T1-8) — the POC surface*
**Purpose.** Compare named opportunities richer than a screener; pick funds; fit-to-your-gap.
**Layout.** A fund **picker** (filter by strategy, "most attractive fees") → select A and B → **side-by-side FundCards** with: structured metrics (TVPI/DPI/IRR **within comparable vintages**, fees/carry/hurdle, geography/sector, deployment pace, consistency), a doc-derived **strategy thesis** (with citations, or "corpus not indexed"), a **fit-to-your-gap** banner ("Fund A closes a 4.2pp underweight; Fund B doesn't").
**Interactions.** Swap A/B; "vs a fund I passed" pulls a historical fund.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/funds`, `GET /api/fund/{id}`, `GET /api/fund_compare?a=&b=&investor_id=` → the full compare object; honour `comparable_vintage` + `caveat`.
**States.** Different vintages → show the caveat, no cross-vintage ranking. Single-vintage → "consistency not computable." Doc corpus absent → thesis shows "not indexed," rest renders. Unresolved → suggest `candidates`.
**Acceptance.** Comparable-vintage caveat honoured; fees apples-to-apples; fit banner from `fit.better_fit`.

---
### S14 · What's next / Completion  *(T2-1)*
**Purpose.** The funds that best close your gap.
**Layout.** "Your missing piece is {X}" + a ranked set of **FundCards** in the Moonfare-listing layout; each with the "Contact your sales associate" CTA and an info-not-advice disclaimer.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/recommendations` → `recommendations[]` with `underweight_ppt`, `fund`, `rationale`, `disclaimer`.
**States.** No significant gap → "Your portfolio is well-aligned — no action needed."
**Acceptance.** FundCard matches the Moonfare listing; disclaimer present; do-nothing state exists.

---
### S15 · Explain (a recommendation)  *(T1-3)*
**Purpose.** Why Moonfare recommends a named fund, transparently.
**Layout.** "Top reasons" list (each a **doc citation** chip), a manager/team note, a fit statement, a risk list, a "who should not invest" line.
**Bindings.** ← `POST /api/explain {fund_id}` → `reasons[{text,citation}]`, `risks`, `who_should_not`, `grounding`.
**States.** No corpus → structured reasons only, flagged.
**Acceptance.** Every doc reason shows a citation chip; unsupported claims absent.

---
### S16 · Why-not / Suitability  *(T3-2)*
**Purpose.** Why an attractive fund may still not be right *now*.
**Layout.** A single clear statement: "Not right now because {binding constraint}; it becomes suitable when {threshold}," with the deterministic reasons listed + a rules-version footnote.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/why_not?fund_id=` → `binding_constraint`, `explanation`, `would_change`, `rules_version`.
**Acceptance.** Binding constraint named; "would change" present.

---
### S17 · Missed opportunities  *(T1-11)*
**Purpose.** Good funds you plausibly should have done but passed.
**Layout.** A list of past offerings with **ex-post realized** outcomes ("you passed on X (2020), it returned 1.8×"), a pattern summary, and "current funds that resemble a past missed winner."
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/missed_opportunities` → `missed[]`, `pattern`, `resembles[]`.
**Acceptance.** Ex-post realized (not marks); pattern shown; engagement-aware note when feed absent.

---
### S18 · Manager patterns  *(T2-9)*
**Purpose.** What top managers do differently.
**Layout.** A "top-manager DNA" ranking per strategy + a team-stability blueprint (DD-doc-cited).
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/manager_patterns?strategy=` → `top_managers[]`, blueprint.
**Acceptance.** Ranking vintage-cohorted; team claims cited.

---
### S19 · Macro / Regime  *(T3-3)*
**Purpose.** Offense/defense positioning + macro sensitivities + hidden bets.
**Layout.** An offense/defense gauge; a **grouped-bar** factor-sensitivity panel (NOT radar); hidden-bet flags; peer macro comparison; a "client-flow: coming soon" note (engagement feed).
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/macro` → `offense_defense_score`, `sector_exposure`, factor betas, `client_flow{status}`.
**Acceptance.** No radar; betas labelled illustrative; coverage-bound.

---
### S20 · Next Best Action  *(T1-2) — the integrator*
**Purpose.** The single best next move, assembled from the others.
**Layout.** A prominent ribbon on the Overview ("What should I do next?") + a dedicated page: one **ranked action** card (or a justified "do nothing"), with ticket size, a rationale that chains the contributing surfaces (gap + liquidity + fit), a provenance chain, and the "Contact your sales associate" CTA.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/next_best` → `action`, `ranked[]`, `do_nothing`, `ticket_eur`, `rationale`, `liquidity_capacity_eur`.
**States.** Overcommitted/no-gap → "do nothing" with the reason. Secondary/demand inputs show "not connected" sub-states.
**Acceptance.** Never prescriptive in tone (info-not-advice); "do nothing" can render; rationale references only engine numbers.

---
### S21 · Demand  ·  S22 · Secondary  ·  S23 · Sentiment  *(T3-1 / T2-6 / T1-10 — signal-gated)*
**Purpose.** Platform demand / internal secondary market / investor sentiment (the last two largely internal-mode).
**Layout.** Fully designed surfaces (heatmap, liquidity ranking, concern heatmap) rendered in a **NotConnectedState** by default: a dashed, greyed preview of the intended layout + "Live when the {feed} connects" + a synthetic-preview toggle for the demo.
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/demand|secondary|sentiment` → `{status:"not_connected", feed, message}`; when live, the real shape.
**Acceptance.** Renders the not-connected state cleanly (not an error); the layout is designed so the swap is purely data.

---

## PART 5 — Ask Alpha (conversational UI)

**Placement & states.** A **draggable floating pane** bottom-right over the stage (chart stays visible). A control **enlarges it to full-screen chat-only** (for the "five sentences" persona) and shrinks back. Persists across Meta/Detail. Minimisable to a pill.
**Message rendering.** User bubbles right, Alpha left. Alpha answers render **inline mini-charts/tables** where useful (reuse the chart components). **Every number in an answer is a clickable chip → ProvenancePopover** (cohort, n, fields, method). A small "grounded · snapshot {id}" footer on each answer.
**Two registers.** Default "five sentences" plain-language summary with an **"show detail" expander** to the analytical version. The pane remembers the user's preferred register (UI state only).
**Suggested prompts.** Context-aware chips under the input ("How do I compare to my peers?", "What should I do next?", "Am I over-concentrated in AI?", "Can I commit more?").
**Safety/empty/error.** Empty input disabled; if the backend returns the refusal ("I can only answer with figures I can compute…"), render it plainly — never invent. Loading = a calm typing indicator (no spinner spam).
**Bindings.** ← `POST /api/chat {question, investor_id}` → `{answer, tools_called, grounded, snapshot_id}`.
**Acceptance.** Draggable + full-screen + shrink; numbers are provenance chips; refusal renders gracefully; never shows a number without a chip.

---

## PART 6 — Cohort builder (left rail)

**Modes.** "Your Profile" (default = people-like-you) vs "Custom Cohort." Presets as quick chips: *People like you · PE professionals · Family Offices · by Profession · Endowment-style · Best performers (Top 10%)*.
**Dimensions (chip groups, each multi-select):** Age bucket · Family (marital, kids — **SyntheticTag "limited/illustrative data"**) · Wealth (bucket + investable target %) · Investment horizon · **Risk appetite (the 5 private classes, hover-defs)** · Investment targets (shown, **not** used to slice — visually distinct/disabled-for-slicing) · Profession (10 + Family Offices) · Region (8-way + All) · Performance (Top 10%) · Endowment (Harvard/Yale/Princeton/Aggregate).
**Set-algebra feedback (must be legible):** selecting **more values inside one dimension makes the cohort bigger** (UNION) — show the live `n` ticking up; selecting across dimensions makes it **smaller** (INTERSECT). A small inline helper: "Adding ages widens your peer group; adding a region narrows it."
**Live state.** A persistent **`n = 312`** readout + the subtle **"5,500 total investors."** When the backend relaxes, show the **RelaxBanner** in words (from `relaxed_dims`). When over-granular, the **GatedState**: "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate" (never "too small," never "I can't").
**Bindings.** ← `POST /api/cohort {dims}` → `{n, strict_n, relaxed_dims, gated, peer_allocation}`. Chip change re-resolves and re-renders only affected centre-stage charts (not a full reload).
**Acceptance.** Within-dim selection grows n; across-dim shrinks n; relax shown in words; gated message exact; live n + "5,500 total."

---

## PART 7 — Advisor cockpit (first-launch mode)

**Book view.** A table/cards of the RM's clients with at-a-glance flags: gap-to-model, liquidity headroom, last contact, (when live) churn-risk. Sort/filter.
**"Who to call this week & why."** A prioritized **RMQueueRow** list — each row = a client + the Next-Best-Action trigger reason; click → open that client's full copilot (**impersonate-with-audit**; an "acting as {client}" banner + audit note).
**Recommendation queue + FeedbackButtons.** For each AI recommendation: Helpful / Wrong-product / Wrong-timing / Wrong-context / Client-not-ready / Compliance-concern / Better-action — captured structured.
**Acceptance.** Book scoped to the RM; impersonation shows the audit banner; feedback buttons present; no advice language.

---

## PART 8 — Internal consoles & Notifications/Follower

**Internal consoles (Internal mode, aggregate-only).** Sentiment heatmap, Demand board, Manager-pattern intelligence, cohort trends — firm-level, never per-identifiable-client; gated to internal roles; the signal-gated ones use NotConnectedState until wired.
**Follower & push.** Follower controls in the cohort panel (follow All / Peers / Top-10%, anonymised) — **SyntheticTag "illustrative; production needs a follow-graph + consent."** The push renders inside an **iPhoneFrame** as a Moonfare-App home-screen notification (real logo), **animating in**: *"Moonfare Alert — investors in your follower group just committed to {Fund} ({Strategy}), lifting the group's {Strategy} allocation. You're currently {gap}pp underweight {Strategy} vs the model."*
**Bindings.** ← `GET /api/follower_alert` → `{headline, body, synthetic}`.
**Acceptance.** Push inside the phone frame, animates in, labelled illustrative; logo in chrome.

---

## PART 9 — Global state & edge-case catalog (every surface renders all seven)

Style each once; reuse everywhere:
1. **Happy** — full data, significant deltas.
2. **Cold-start** — no/low holdings → route to Architect/Completion; never a broken analytics screen. (Welcome detects this.)
3. **Thin cohort** — RelaxBanner in words.
4. **Low coverage** — CoverageBadge "based on X% of NAV."
5. **Null/insufficient** — "too early to rank" / "not enough history"; never zero-fill.
6. **Gated** — GatedState "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate."
7. **Error/degraded** — engine/model down → cached last-good + a quiet banner; chat falls back gracefully; never a blank or a fabricated number.
Plus: **not-significant** deltas render greyed; **SyntheticTag** on every imputed value; **loading** = calm skeletons, not spinners.

**Welcome / cold-start screen.** Chat-first on a **cream** hero: "What can I do for you?" + the value-prop line ("Moonfare screens 500+ opportunities a year; ~2.9% reach the platform — the database of excellence you compare yourself with."). Returning-with-holdings → "Welcome, {name}" + snapshot → Overview. New investor → the FOMO-safe path: show the peer/model picture + an Architect CTA, and onboard target %/horizon/risk via chat.

---

## PART 10 — Accessibility, responsive, performance, i18n

- **A11y:** WCAG-AA contrast; never colour-only (sign/glyph on over/under); full keyboard nav; focus states; chart data available as a table behind a "view data" affordance; ARIA on interactive controls.
- **Responsive:** Part 3 breakpoints; no analytical chart needs horizontal scroll; advisor cockpit usable on tablet/mobile.
- **Performance:** instant Meta⇄Detail switches; re-render only affected charts on cohort change; calm skeleton loads.
- **i18n:** DACH-heavy audience — structure copy for **English + German**; numbers/currency localized (€, EU formatting); don't hard-code strings.
- **Light/dark/hybrid** toggle wired to the token sets.

---

## PART 11 — Deliverable & acceptance (what Claude Design returns)

**Deliverable.** A single self-contained clickable web app: the global frame + all surfaces S1–S23 + Welcome + Ask Alpha + cohort builder + advisor cockpit + internal consoles + the Follower push; the **design tokens** file (light+dark); a short design rationale; and a **screen × state inventory** (each surface in each applicable state, incl. cold-start, thin, low-coverage, gated, not-connected, error).

**Acceptance (visual + interaction):**
- It feels like **one copilot** — one frame, one cohort builder, one chat, consistent components — not 23 dashboards.
- Every numeric element shows ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover; look-through shows CoverageBadge; imputed fields show SyntheticTag.
- The chart language matches Part 2.5 exactly (approved only; % inline; Z large; captions; "Peers" not "All"; performance vintage-honest with "too early to rank").
- Meta⇄Detail is full-screen, no scroll; cohort changes re-render only affected charts; ComparisonSelector + Z-bar drive the TwinDonut.
- All seven states render; cold-start routes to Architect; gated shows the exact paid-viewers copy; signal-gated surfaces show NotConnectedState.
- Ask Alpha is draggable + full-screenable; numbers are provenance chips.
- Placeholder data matches the Part 0.3 API shapes so the live swap is a base-URL change.
- Brand: Moonfare indigo/mint/cream, real logo, light/dark, no flashy transitions, PDF export on every view.

---
*Build to this document. Where a surface needs data, bind to the Part 0.3 endpoint; where a number renders, attach its provenance. The goal is the thing the sponsor asked for: the proprietary-data advantage made premium, trustworthy, and — above all — simple.*


---

# PART 12 — DEEP PER-SURFACE SPECIFICATIONS (all 25, build-grade)

> Each surface below is at backend-spec depth: exact layout, components, chart encodings, data bindings to API JSON paths, interactions, all seven states, copy deck, badges, responsive, accessibility, acceptance. Read with Parts 0–11 (design system, frame, states).


---

## Surface S1 — Overview / Meta-View

> **Use cases:** T1-5 Multi-Layer Peer Benchmarking (the benchmarking spine) + T2-2 Model Portfolio comparison.
> **Build-from contract:** This is a *frontend* build spec for Claude Design. It does **not** redefine the design system — it composes from the **Alpha Frontend Design Specification** (Part 2 tokens/typography/chart-language; Part 3 global frame; Part 9 seven-state model). Every component referenced here (`MetaCard`, `BarRow`, `TwinDonut`, `ZChart`, `ComparisonSelector`, `ProvenancePopover`, `ConfidenceBadge`, `CoverageBadge`, `SyntheticTag`, `RelaxBanner`, `GatedState`, `CaptionBlock`, `PDFExportButton`) is defined there. Every number is bound to the real `GET /api/meta` / `POST /api/cohort` response shown in the backend deep spec (UC T1-5, §5).
> **The worked snapshot used throughout this doc** is the backend's own example (UC T1-5 §3, §5): cohort `n=41`, `you.pm_actual_pct=15.7`, `peer_median=8.9` (CI mean `[7.4,10.4]`), `platform=10.5`, `model=12`, rank `Top 18%`, z-vs-peer AI `+3.1 (sig)` / Private Credit `−9.8 (sig)`, median ticket €128,000, modal mode `selective` share 0.46, `synthetic:true`, `snapshot_id:"synthetic-2026-06-14"`. Use these as the canonical placeholder values so the live swap is a base-URL change.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions (UI-framed)

S1 is the **default investor landing** and the one-glance answer to "where do I stand?". It must answer, visually and within one screen with no scroll, the six T1-5 questions and the T2-2 model question:

| User question (chat-voice) | Card that answers it | Eval id |
|---|---|---|
| "How much of my wealth is in private markets vs people like me?" | TL — PM Allocation | Q1 |
| "Am I ahead or behind — where do I rank?" | TL — Rank pill | Q3 (rank) |
| "Am I filling my own target? My peers'? The model's?" | TR — Fill Rate | Q1/Q5 |
| "How is my strategy mix shaped vs peers / platform / the model?" | BL — Strategy Mix | Q1 / **T2-2 model** |
| "Where am I over- or under-weight, and by how much?" | BR — Over/Underweight Z-chart | Q1 + Q6 framing |
| "Do these peers differ from the recommended model?" | BL + BR (model in ComparisonSelector) | **T2-2** |

The surface embodies two product non-negotiables: **relative, not absolute** (everything is %-of-wealth or %-of-portfolio — no headline € total appears on S1) and **peer ≠ model is visibly true** (backend gate G1 guarantees `sum|peer−model| > 5pp`; the UI must make that divergence legible at a glance).

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

**Reached by:**
- **Default after Welcome** when the returning investor has holdings (Welcome → "Welcome, {name}" → snapshot → lands on S1). New/cold-start investors are routed to S9 Architect by Welcome, *but* S1 still renders its cold-start variant if reached directly (§7.2).
- **"Overview"** — the first entry in the left navigation rail (Part 3). Always available in Investor and Demo modes; in Advisor mode it renders inside an impersonated client context with the "acting as {client}" banner.
- **Next-Best-Action ribbon** ("What should I do next?") sits at the top of S1 as an entry *into* S20, not the reverse.

**Deep-links out of S1:**
- **Z-bar click (BR) → S2 Strategy deep-dive**, with that strategy preselected: navigate to `S2?strategy={key}&comparison={current ComparisonSelector value}`. The strategy chip and right TwinDonut on S2 open already bound to the clicked strategy + the comparison series carried over from BR.
- **Rank pill click (TL) → S7 Performance** is *not* wired (rank here is allocation-rank, not performance-rank); the pill opens its own ProvenancePopover instead.
- **CaptionBlock "model" reference** is plain text on S1; the model architecture lives on S9 — no deep-link required, but a subtle "see your target architecture →" text-link under the BL card routes to S9.
- **ComparisonSelector** never navigates; it swaps series in place (§6.1).

**Navigation contract:** switching to S1 from any Detail surface is instant and full-screen (Part 3 — no scroll, frame persists). The cohort builder (left) and Ask Alpha (floating) stay mounted; only the centre stage swaps.

---

### §3 Layout — exact grid

S1 centre stage is a **2×2 grid of equal-weight `MetaCard`s**, filling the available stage height with **no vertical scroll** (Part 3 / non-negotiable 0.2). The grid sits inside the persistent frame; the cohort rail (left) and Ask Alpha (floating right) are owned by Part 3, not by S1.

```
┌───────────────────────────── CENTRE STAGE (S1) ─────────────────────────────┐
│  [ Next-Best-Action ribbon — full width, 56px, optional ]   [ PDF export ▾ ] │
│                                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────┐   ┌──────────────────────────────┐        │
│  │ TL  Private Markets           │   │ TR  Fill Rate                 │        │
│  │     Allocation  (BarRow)      │   │     (BarRow, 4 series)        │        │
│  │     [ConfBadge][Rank pill]    │   │     [ConfBadge]               │        │
│  │     chart · CaptionBlock      │   │     chart · CaptionBlock      │        │
│  └──────────────────────────────┘   └──────────────────────────────┘        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────┐   ┌──────────────────────────────┐        │
│  │ BL  Strategy Mix              │   │ BR  Over / Under-weight       │        │
│  │  [ComparisonSelector]         │   │  [ComparisonSelector]         │        │
│  │  TwinDonut · CaptionBlock     │   │  ZChart (large) · Caption     │        │
│  │  [ConfBadge]                  │   │  [ConfBadge]                  │        │
│  └──────────────────────────────┘   └──────────────────────────────┘        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Region sizing (desktop ≥1280px, inside a stage of width `W` and height `H`):**
- Grid: CSS Grid `grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr;` `gap: 16px` (4pt grid ×4). Outer stage padding `24px`.
- Ribbon (if present): full-width row above the grid, fixed `56px`, `margin-bottom 16px`. When suppressed (no action / do-nothing), the grid expands to full height.
- PDFExportButton: top-right of the stage header strip, aligned baseline with the ribbon; `PDFExportButton` component (Part 2.6), label "Export view".
- Each `MetaCard`: `radius 12px`, `1px --line` border, single soft elevation, internal padding `20px`. Card header row holds title (h2, large) + right-aligned badge cluster (ConfidenceBadge always; Rank pill on TL; ComparisonSelector on BL/BR). Chart fills remaining height; `CaptionBlock` (caption type, max 2 lines) pinned to card bottom with `12px` top margin.
- **Ordering is fixed**: TL=Allocation, TR=Fill Rate, BL=Strategy Mix, BR=Z-chart. This reading order moves from "how much" → "vs whose target" → "what shape" → "where the gaps are."

**Responsive columns:**
- **≥1280px** — 2×2 grid as above. ZChart (BR) renders large; all four series in TR render side-by-side.
- **768–1279px** — grid collapses to **2 columns × auto rows** *or*, where height is constrained, **1 column** with cards stacked in the fixed order (TL, TR, BL, BR). Cohort builder collapses to a drawer; Ask Alpha to a launcher button (Part 3). Charts reflow; ZChart stays full card-width. No horizontal scroll on any chart.
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go)** — single column, cards stacked TL→TR→BL→BR. ProfileBar becomes a sheet (Part 3). Each card is full viewport-width; the ZChart bars stay horizontal-divergence (never rotate to require horizontal scroll). ComparisonSelector becomes a horizontally scrollable segmented strip *within* its card only (not the page). Next-Best-Action ribbon stacks above the cards. The 2×2 becomes a vertical 4-up; this is the only S1 layout that scrolls, and only on mobile.

---

### §4 Components & charts — every instance

All four cards are `MetaCard` instances: `title` (h2), chart slot, `CaptionBlock`, and an embedded `ConfidenceBadge`. Every numeric label opens a `ProvenancePopover` on click. Colours reference tokens only (Part 2.2); the **fixed strategy→colour map** (`--data-1…--data-9`) is shared across BL and BR so a strategy is the same colour everywhere on S1.

#### §4.1 TL — Private Markets Allocation (`BarRow`, horizontal)

- **Component:** `BarRow` with **4 rows**, top-to-bottom: **You · Peers (median) · Platform (avg) · Model**.
- **Chart type / axes:** horizontal bars; single quantitative axis = **% of investable wealth in private markets**, `0 → max(rows, target)+headroom`, tick at 0/5/10/15/20% (auto-stepped, mono labels). Bars are **thin** (Part 2.5), `height 14px`, `8px` row gap. Row label left (`body`, `--ink`), bar centre, inline value right of bar end (`mono`, `--ink`).
- **Color tokens:** `You` = `--brand`; `Peers` = `--brand-tint-2`; `Platform` = `--brand-tint-3`; `Model` = `--accent-mint`. (Series colours are consistent with TR so the same actor reads the same colour across the top row.)
- **Dashed target marker:** a vertical dashed line at the user's `your_target_pct` (e.g. 20%) spanning all rows, `1.5px dashed --muted`, with a tiny top cap label "target 20%". This is the user's *own* target (synthetic — see badge rules §9).
- **Rank pill:** in the card header, right of the title — a pill reading **"Top 18%"** (`pm_allocation.rank.label`). Background `--brand-tint-3`, text `--brand`, `radius 999px`, `caption` size. Click → ProvenancePopover scoped to rank (`method: rank over all N`, `of: 2000`, `position: 111`).
- **Inline % labels:** every bar shows its value inline (e.g. `15.7%`, `8.9%`, `10.5%`, `12%`). The You bar value is bold.
- **Caption (`CaptionBlock`):** 1–2 sentences, see copy deck §8.
- **ConfidenceBadge:** `n=41 · ±2.3` (n from `cohort.n`; CI half-width derived from `pm_allocation.provenance.ci95` = `(10.4−7.4)/2 = 1.5` → render `±1.5`; example doc value `±2.3` shown if CI differs). Greys the Peers value if not significant (here it is significant).
- **SyntheticTag:** on the target marker label *and* the You value **only if** the wealth denominator is illustrative (`provenance.synthetic == true` on the wealth field). In the worked snapshot `synthetic:true`, so an amber "illustrative" tag sits on the dashed target cap.
- **ProvenancePopover content:** cohort label "Peers", `n=41`, method string `"%wealth in PM; peer=median, platform=mean over n_with_wealth"`, fields used, `snapshot_id`.

#### §4.2 TR — Fill Rate (`BarRow`, 4 series side-by-side)

- **Component:** `BarRow` rendering **four series side-by-side** (not behind a toggle) comparing the user's actual PM% against four target/actual references: **Your target · Peer target · Platform (actual) · Model target**.
- **Chart type / axes:** horizontal bars, one per reference, with **the user's actual PM% drawn as a fill against each reference's target**, expressed as fill-rate %. Each row: label left, a track bar = the reference target (light, `--brand-tint-3` outline), a fill bar = `your_pm_pct / reference` clamped at 100%+, **fill % shown inline** and the **reference target % shown inline** at the track end (per Part 2.5 "show the user's actual target % inline on Fill-Rate"). Axis = % (0–110, allowing >100% overfill).
- **Series & tokens:**
  - Your target ← `fill_rate.your_target_pct` (track) vs `fill_rate.your_pm_pct` (fill) — fill `--brand`.
  - Peer target ← `fill_rate.peer_target_pct` — fill `--brand-tint-2`.
  - Platform ← `fill_rate.platform_pm_pct` (this row compares actual-to-actual) — fill `--brand-tint-3`.
  - Model target ← `fill_rate.model_target_pct` — fill `--accent-mint`.
- **FOMO / 0% state framing:** if `your_pm_pct == 0` (a brand-new investor reaching S1), the fills render at 0% but the copy frames it as **headroom, not failure**: the inline label reads "0% filled — 20% headroom to your target" and the caption uses the headroom language (§8). Never red, never "you are behind." Track bars still show the reference targets so the goal is visible.
- **Overfill:** if `your_pm_pct > reference` (e.g. 15.7% vs peer target 12%), the fill bar extends past the track to 100%+ with a subtle `--over` cap and a `▲` glyph; inline reads "131% of peer target".
- **Caption:** §8.
- **ConfidenceBadge:** `n=41 · ±1.5` (same cohort/CI as TL for the peer-target row; the your-target and model rows are synthetic references and carry SyntheticTag, not CI).
- **SyntheticTag:** on **Your target** and **Model target** values (both `synthetic:true` in the contract: `your_target_pct{...,synthetic:true}`). Peer target and platform are measured.
- **ProvenancePopover:** per row; the synthetic rows' popover states "illustrative target — assigned from your risk class," the measured rows state cohort/n/method.

#### §4.3 BL — Strategy Mix (`TwinDonut` + `ComparisonSelector`)

- **Component:** `TwinDonut` — **left donut = Your portfolio (fixed)**, **right donut = the comparison cohort**, driven by a `ComparisonSelector` (segmented control) with options **`You · Peers · Platform · Model`** (the T2-2 model is a first-class option here). Default right-donut selection = **Peers**.
- **Chart type / encoding:** two donuts, each a 100%-of-portfolio split across the (up to 9) strategies. **% inline on each slice** (Part 2.5 / non-negotiable), strategy name inside or adjacent to its slice — **no separate side legend** where slices are large enough; tiny slices collapse into an "Other" arc with a hover breakdown. Inner radius `0.6`. Donuts are equal size, left/right, centred labels.
- **Fixed strategy→colour map:** Buyout=`--data-1`, Growth=`--data-2`, Venture=`--data-3`, Private Credit=`--data-4`, Infrastructure=`--data-5`, Real Estate=`--data-6`, Secondaries=`--data-7`, AI/Tech=`--data-8`, Other=`--data-9`. **This map is shared with the BR Z-chart** so a clicked Z-bar lights the matching donut slice colour.
- **Slice value labels:** `mono`, e.g. AI `18%`, Private Credit `2%`. Left vs right slices for the same strategy share colour, so the eye reads the shift.
- **ComparisonSelector behaviour:** see §6.1 — swaps the **right** donut's data source (`strategy_mix.peer | .platform | .model`); the left donut (`strategy_mix.you`) never changes. Selecting **You** on the right collapses to a single donut comparison-disabled state (right mirrors left, greyed selector hint "pick a comparison").
- **Caption:** §8.
- **ConfidenceBadge:** `n=41 · ±1.5` shown when comparison = Peers; hidden / replaced by SyntheticTag when comparison = **Model** (model is a reference, not a sampled cohort — show "illustrative model" tag instead of a CI). Platform shows `n=2000` (the full pool).
- **ProvenancePopover:** on any slice click → strategy name, your % vs comparison %, cohort/n/method, snapshot.

#### §4.4 BR — Over / Under-weight (`ZChart`, large)

- **Component:** `ZChart` (divergence chart, **rendered large** — it is the visual anchor of S1, Part 2.5) + a `ComparisonSelector` with options **`vs Peers · vs Platform · vs Model`**. Default = **vs Peers**.
- **Chart type / encoding:** horizontal divergence bars, one row per strategy, diverging from a **centre 0 line**. Bar to the **right** = overweight (`--over`, `▲`); to the **left** = underweight (`--under`, `▼`). X-axis = **delta in percentage points** (`delta` from the contract), symmetric scale `−max…+max` (e.g. −12 to +12), tick every 4pp, 0-line emphasised (`--ink`, `1.5px`). Bars thin (`16px`), sorted by absolute delta descending so the biggest gaps read first.
- **Significance:** bars where `significant == false` render **greyed** (`--muted`, 40% opacity fill) with the label appended "(not significant)". Significant bars use full `--over`/`--under`. Each bar carries **both a colour and a sign glyph** (`▲`/`▼`) for accessibility (never colour-only).
- **Inline labels:** strategy name at the bar's base side, signed delta at the bar end, e.g. **"AI ▲ +3.1pp"**, **"Private Credit ▼ −9.8pp"**.
- **Colour map:** the bar *fill* uses `--over`/`--under` semantic tokens (divergence semantics dominate here), but a small **strategy colour chip** (`--data-*` from the §4.3 map) precedes each strategy label so the link to the BL donut is visible.
- **Click interaction:** clicking any Z-bar **deep-links to S2** with `strategy={key}` + carries the current ComparisonSelector value (§2, §6.3). On hover (before click) the matching BL donut slice highlights to preview the relationship.
- **ComparisonSelector behaviour:** swaps the data source `z_chart.vs_peer | .vs_platform | .vs_model` and re-sorts/re-renders the bars (§6.1).
- **Caption:** §8.
- **ConfidenceBadge:** `n=41` (Peers/Platform); Model comparison shows "illustrative model" SyntheticTag instead of CI.
- **ProvenancePopover:** per bar → "delta = your % − comparison %; significance from cohort dispersion", n, snapshot.

---

### §5 Data bindings — element ← exact JSON path

**Single source call:** all four cards bind to **one** `GET /api/meta?investor_id={id?}` response. No card issues its own fetch on first load (acceptance: "all four cards bind to one /api/meta call"). `POST /api/cohort` (§6.2) re-resolves cohort-dependent fields only.

**The `{value, provenance}` convention.** Per the design foundation (Part 0.3) and the contract, numeric fields arrive either as a scalar (e.g. `pm_allocation.you: 15.7`) or as `{value, provenance:{method,n,ci95,coverage_pct,significant,synthetic,vintage_scope,snapshot_id}}` (e.g. `fill_rate.your_pm_pct`). The frontend renders **`value` → the visual** and **`provenance` → the ConfidenceBadge / ProvenancePopover / SyntheticTag**. Where the contract gives a flat scalar (`pm_allocation.*`), the shared `pm_allocation.provenance` block supplies the badge for the whole card.

| Element | ← JSON path (`/api/meta`) |
|---|---|
| **TL** You bar | `pm_allocation.you` (15.7) |
| TL Peers (median) bar | `pm_allocation.peer_median` (8.9) |
| TL Platform (avg) bar | `pm_allocation.platform_avg` (10.5) |
| TL Model bar | `pm_allocation.model` (12) |
| TL dashed target marker | `fill_rate.your_target_pct.value` (synthetic) |
| TL Rank pill label | `pm_allocation.rank.label` ("Top 18%") |
| TL Rank popover | `pm_allocation.rank.{position, of, percentile}` |
| TL ConfidenceBadge | `pm_allocation.provenance.{n, ci95}` |
| TL SyntheticTag | `pm_allocation.provenance.synthetic` (+ wealth field synthetic) |
| **TR** Your-target track / fill | `fill_rate.your_target_pct` / `fill_rate.your_pm_pct` |
| TR Peer-target row | `fill_rate.peer_target_pct` |
| TR Platform row | `fill_rate.platform_pm_pct` |
| TR Model-target row | `fill_rate.model_target_pct` |
| TR badges | each row's `…provenance.{n,ci95,synthetic}` |
| **BL** Left donut (You) | `strategy_mix.you` (`{strategy:pct}`) |
| BL Right donut (Peers/Platform/Model) | `strategy_mix.peer` / `.platform` / `.model` |
| **BR** Z-bars | `z_chart.vs_peer[strategy].delta` (+ `.significant`) |
| BR (selector → Platform/Model) | `z_chart.vs_platform[...]` / `z_chart.vs_model[...]` |
| Cohort label & n (all cards) | `cohort.n` (41) |
| RelaxBanner trigger/copy | `cohort.relaxed_dims` (array) |
| Gated trigger | `cohort.gated` (bool) + `gated_message` |
| "5,500 total" / headline | `cohort.headline_total` (2000 in snapshot) |
| Cold-start trigger | `pm_allocation.you == null` / `cold_start:true` flag |
| snapshot id (badge footer) | `pm_allocation.provenance.snapshot_id` |

**`POST /api/cohort`** (on cohort-builder change) returns `{n, strict_n, relaxed_dims, gated, peer_allocation, peer_targets_pct}`. The FE maps: `peer_allocation` → TL Peers bar + BL right-donut(Peers) + BR vs_peer deltas (recomputed FE-side as `you − peer`); `peer_targets_pct` → TR peer-target row; `n` → all ConfidenceBadges; `relaxed_dims` → RelaxBanner; `gated`/`gated_message` → GatedState (§6.2, §7.6).

**Loading skeletons (calm, not spinners — Part 9):** on first `/api/meta` and on cohort re-fetch, each affected card shows a skeleton: title + a shimmer block sized to the chart (4 grey rows for BarRows, two grey rings for TwinDonut, a centred grey divergence stack for ZChart), badge area shows a pulsing `n=…` chip. Skeletons hold the exact final layout (no reflow on data arrival). Charts animate value-in once (≤150ms, Part 2.4) then settle.

---

### §6 Interactions — each with state transition + what re-fetches

#### §6.1 ComparisonSelector swap (BL and BR, independent)
- **Trigger:** user selects a segment (`You/Peers/Platform/Model` on BL; `vs Peers/Platform/Model` on BR).
- **Data:** **no network call** — all comparison series for `strategy_mix.*` and `z_chart.*` already arrived in the single `/api/meta` payload. Pure client-side rebind.
- **Transition:** selected segment gets `--brand` fill; the affected donut/chart re-renders with a single ≤150ms value-in animation; ConfidenceBadge updates (Peers→CI; Model→SyntheticTag "illustrative model"; Platform→`n=2000`); CaptionBlock swaps to the matching sentence (§8).
- **Scope:** BL selector affects only the BL right donut; BR selector affects only the BR z-bars. They are independent and do not sync.

#### §6.2 Cohort change (left rail) → re-render ONLY affected cards
- **Trigger:** any chip change in the Cohort builder (Part 6).
- **Network:** `POST /api/cohort {age?,wealth?,risk?,region?,prof?,kids?}` → `{n, relaxed_dims, gated, peer_allocation, peer_targets_pct, ...}`.
- **Affected cards (re-render):** **TL** (Peers bar ← `peer_allocation` PM%), **TR** (peer-target row ← `peer_targets_pct`), **BL** (right donut *when comparison=Peers* ← `peer_allocation` vector), **BR** (vs-peer deltas recomputed `you − peer`). **NOT affected:** TL You/Platform/Model bars, BL left donut, BR vs-platform / vs-model series, the Rank pill (rank is over all N, not the cohort). Those cards keep their `/api/meta` values and do **not** flicker.
- **Transition:** affected cards show their skeleton (§5) during the in-flight POST; ConfidenceBadges across all cards update `n`; if `relaxed_dims` non-empty → RelaxBanner appears (§7.3); if `gated` → the affected (cohort-dependent) cards swap to GatedState (§7.6). Live `n=…` readout in the rail ticks; set-algebra feedback (Part 6) is owned by the rail, not S1.
- **Acceptance:** "cohort change moves the numbers" — TL Peers / TR peer-target / BL right(Peers) / BR vs-peer visibly change; everything else holds.

#### §6.3 Z-bar click → S2
- **Trigger:** click any bar in BR.
- **Action:** navigate `S2?strategy={key}&comparison={BR selector value}`. No data is prefetched by S1; S2 issues `GET /api/detail/strategy?strategy=&comparison=` on mount.
- **Pre-nav affordance:** on hover, the matching BL donut slice (both donuts) highlights + a tooltip shows "{Strategy}: you {x}% vs {comparison} {y}% (Δ {±d}pp)". On click, a brief ≤150ms press state, then route.

#### §6.4 Hover tooltips
- **BarRow (TL/TR):** hovering a bar shows a tooltip with the exact value, the actor label, and "vs target {marker}%". Keyboard focus on the bar shows the same tooltip.
- **TwinDonut (BL):** hovering a slice shows strategy name + % + the paired comparison % (e.g. "AI — you 18% · peers 15%").
- **ZChart (BR):** per §6.3.

#### §6.5 ProvenancePopover open
- **Trigger:** click any **number** (bar value, donut slice %, z-delta, rank pill) or its adjacent ConfidenceBadge.
- **Action:** opens `ProvenancePopover` anchored to the number: cohort label ("Peers"), `n`, the `method` string, the fields used, `snapshot_id`, and a "show the data" affordance. Closes on outside-click / Esc. No network call (provenance is in the payload). Only one popover open at a time.

---

### §7 The seven states — concrete visuals + copy

Each state is styled once in the foundation (Part 9) and reused; below is exactly how S1 expresses each, keyed to the contract's §7 branch logic.

#### §7.1 Happy (full data, significant deltas)
All four cards render with the worked snapshot. TL shows 15.7 / 8.9 / 10.5 / 12 with target marker at 20% and "Top 18%". TR shows the four fill rows (You over peer target → ▲ overfill; under model target → headroom). BL TwinDonut You vs Peers with inline %. BR shows AI ▲ +3.1 (full colour) and Private Credit ▼ −9.8 (full colour), both significant. All badges populated; snapshot footer "grounded · synthetic-2026-06-14".

#### §7.2 Cold-start (`pm_allocation.you == null` / `cold_start:true`)
Backend omits `you`/`z_chart`/`peer_portfolio`. S1 must **not** render broken charts:
- **TL** shows only Peers / Platform / Model bars (no You bar, no Rank pill); a dashed ghost "You" row reads "Your allocation will appear here." Target marker still shown.
- **TR** shows the target/peer/platform/model references with a 0%-headroom framing on the (missing) You fill.
- **BL** left donut is an empty dashed ring "No holdings yet"; right donut renders the comparison normally (so the peer/model picture is visible — FOMO-safe).
- **BR** is replaced by an inline empty-state panel: "We'll show your over/under-weights once you hold a fund."
- **CTA:** a prominent **"Build your target architecture →"** button routing to S9 Architect, plus copy: *"Your portfolio will appear here. Start by seeing your recommended architecture."*
- Welcome normally routes cold-start to S9 before S1 is reached; this variant is the safety net.

#### §7.3 Thin cohort (`cohort.relaxed_dims` non-empty)
- A `RelaxBanner` appears across the top of the grid (below the ribbon), in **words** built from `relaxed_dims`: e.g. *"Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band."* (foundation copy; the specific dims relaxed are named).
- All cards still render; ConfidenceBadges show the relaxed `n`. No error styling — this is a normal, calm state.

#### §7.4 Low coverage
- **Not applicable on S1** (per contract §7: "low-coverage → n/a here"). Coverage is a look-through (S6) concern. S1 therefore renders **no** `CoverageBadge`. If a future field carried `coverage_pct`, it would attach as a CoverageBadge per the foundation, but S1's metrics are allocation %, not look-through.

#### §7.5 Null / insufficient performance (`null perf`)
- Contract: "omit that metric, keep allocation." S1 does not display DPI/MOIC/IRR, so this state is largely invisible here — but the **behavior** block (median ticket / mode), if surfaced in a future S1 footer, would suppress any null metric and show "not enough history" rather than zero-fill. No zero-filling anywhere on S1.

#### §7.6 Gated (`cohort.gated == true`)
- The cohort builder produced an over-granular selection. **Cohort-dependent cards** (TL Peers row, TR peer row, BL right-donut-when-Peers, BR vs-peer) swap to a `GatedState` inside the card: the exact copy *"Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate."* plus the subtle headline *"5,500 total investors"* (from `cohort.headline_total`, rendered as the foundation's standard gated headline).
- **Non-cohort cards/series** (You bar, Model, Platform) still render so the screen isn't empty. The selector on BL/BR can switch to Platform/Model to escape the gated view.
- Never show "too small" or "I can't" — only the paid-viewers copy.

#### §7.7 Error / degraded (404 / engine down)
- On `404 {"error":"unknown investor"}`: full-screen calm empty-state "We couldn't find this portfolio" + a retry and a "back to Welcome" link. No fabricated numbers.
- On engine/model down: render **cached last-good** values for all four cards with a quiet top banner *"Showing your last saved snapshot — live data is reconnecting."* and a muted timestamp. Charts remain interactive (cached). Never blank, never a fabricated number. Ask Alpha falls back to its own graceful copy (Part 5).

---

### §8 Copy deck

**Surface title (frame header, Part 3):** *"Compare yourself to your peers."*

**Card titles:** TL *"Private Markets Allocation"* · TR *"Fill Rate"* · BL *"Strategy Mix"* · BR *"Over / Under-weight vs Peers"* (the trailing reference updates with the BR ComparisonSelector: "…vs Platform" / "…vs the Model").

**Captions (`CaptionBlock`, 1–2 sentences, with example sentences from the worked snapshot):**
- **TL:** *"You hold 16% of your wealth in private markets — above the 9% peer median, below the 18% model. That puts you in the top 18% of all investors."* (Numbers interpolate from `pm_allocation.*` + `rank.label`; rounds to whole % in caption, exact in chart.)
- **TR:** *"You've filled 131% of your peers' target but only 79% of the model's — you still have headroom to your own 20% target."* FOMO/0% variant: *"You haven't started yet — that's 20% of headroom to your target, not a gap to apologise for."*
- **BL (Peers):** *"Your buyout-heavy mix leans further into AI than your peers and lighter on private credit."* (Selector=Platform): *"Compared with the whole platform, your mix concentrates more in growth and AI."* (Selector=Model): *"Versus the recommended model for your risk profile, you over-index AI and under-index private credit."*
- **BR (Peers):** *"You're 3.1pp overweight AI and 9.8pp underweight private credit versus your peers — click a bar to see the funds behind it."* (Selector=Model): *"Versus the model, your biggest gap is private credit. Click a bar to drill in."*

**Tooltips:**
- TL bar: *"{Actor}: {x}% of wealth in private markets · target {t}%"*
- TR row: *"{x}% filled of {reference} target ({ref}%)"*
- BL slice: *"{Strategy} — you {x}% · {comparison} {y}%"*
- BR bar: *"{Strategy}: {±d}pp vs {comparison}{ · not significant}"*
- Rank pill: *"Ranked {position} of {of} investors by PM allocation."*

**ComparisonSelector labels:** BL — `You · Peers · Platform · Model`; BR — `vs Peers · vs Platform · vs the Model`.

**Badge / provenance strings:**
- ConfidenceBadge: `n={cohort.n} · ±{ci_halfwidth}` (e.g. `n=41 · ±1.5`).
- Not-significant suffix: `(not significant)` appended to greyed z-bars.
- SyntheticTag (target/model/wealth): pill text `illustrative`; popover line *"Illustrative target — assigned from your risk class; not a verified figure."*
- ProvenancePopover header: *"How this was computed"*; body uses the `method` string verbatim (e.g. *"%wealth in PM; peer=median, platform=mean over n_with_wealth"*); footer *"Cohort: Peers · n={n} · snapshot {snapshot_id}"*.
- Snapshot footer (each card, small): *"grounded · snapshot {snapshot_id}"*.

**State strings:**
- Cold-start CTA: *"Your portfolio will appear here. Start by seeing your recommended architecture."* / button *"Build your target architecture →"*.
- RelaxBanner: *"Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band."* (dims-specific variant interpolated from `relaxed_dims`).
- Gated: *"Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate."* + headline *"5,500 total investors."*
- Error (404): *"We couldn't find this portfolio."* / Degraded: *"Showing your last saved snapshot — live data is reconnecting."*

---

### §9 Badge rendering — how each badge appears on each number

**ConfidenceBadge** — appears on every **cohort-sampled** number on S1:
- TL: one card-level badge `n=41 · ±1.5` in the header (CI from `pm_allocation.provenance.ci95`). Greys the **Peers** value if `significant` were false.
- TR: per-row badges on the Peers and Platform rows (measured). The Your-target and Model-target rows carry **SyntheticTag instead** (they are references, not samples).
- BL: header badge when comparison=Peers (`n=41`); =Platform → `n=2000`; =Model → SyntheticTag.
- BR: header badge `n=41` for Peers/Platform; per-bar, sub-CI deltas render greyed (foundation rule: "sub-CI deltas render greyed / not significant").

**ProvenancePopover** — attached to **every** number (bar value, slice %, z-delta, rank pill). Click-to-open; one at a time; content per §8. This satisfies the global non-negotiable: *a number never appears bare.*

**SyntheticTag** (amber "illustrative" pill, `--synthetic`) — on:
- TL: the dashed **target marker** label, and the You/wealth value **iff** `provenance.synthetic == true` (the **wealth denominator** is the synthetic field — the snapshot has `synthetic:true`, so the tag is present on S1).
- TR: **Your target** and **Model target** rows (both `synthetic:true`).
- BL/BR: the **Model** comparison, whenever selected (model portfolios are illustrative references) — tag replaces the ConfidenceBadge.
- The tag is never styled as verified fact (non-negotiable 0.2): muted amber, small, with the popover note in §8.

**CoverageBadge** — **not rendered on S1** (§7.4; coverage is a look-through concern). Documented here only to confirm its deliberate absence.

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive** (full breakpoint behaviour in §3): ≥1280 = 2×2, ZChart large, TR four-series side-by-side; 768–1279 = 2-col or 1-col stack, cohort→drawer, Ask Alpha→launcher; <768 = single-column 4-up (the only scrolling S1 variant), ComparisonSelector becomes an in-card scrollable strip, charts reflow, ProfileBar→sheet. **No analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll** at any breakpoint (Part 10).

**Accessibility (WCAG-AA, Part 10):**
- **Never colour-only:** over/under on BR carry **both** `--over`/`--under` colour **and** a `▲`/`▼` glyph **and** a signed number; TL/TR series are distinguishable by label and value, not colour alone.
- **Contrast:** all text and bar fills meet AA against `--bg`/`--surface` in both light and dark token sets.
- **Keyboard:** full tab order — ribbon → PDF export → TL (bars focusable, each opens tooltip/popover) → TR → BL (selector segments + slices) → BR (selector + bars; Enter on a bar performs the §6.3 deep-link). ComparisonSelector is an ARIA `radiogroup`. ProvenancePopover is a focus-trapped dialog, Esc-closable, focus returns to the number.
- **Focus states:** visible focus ring (`--brand`, 2px) on every interactive element.
- **Chart-as-table affordance:** each MetaCard exposes a "view data" control that opens the chart's underlying values as an accessible `<table>` (actor/strategy rows × value/comparison/delta columns), reading from the same `/api/meta` binding — required by Part 10 for all charts.
- **ARIA:** charts have `role="img"` + an `aria-label` summarising the caption; the data table is the accessible long-description target; live region announces cohort-change re-renders ("Peers updated, n now {n}").

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

**Visual**
- [ ] Centre stage is a fixed 2×2 of equal-weight MetaCards (TL Allocation, TR Fill, BL Mix, BR Z-chart), no vertical scroll ≥768px.
- [ ] TL BarRow shows 4 rows (You/Peers/Platform/Model), a dashed target marker with inline "target {t}%", inline % on every bar, and a "Top 18%" Rank pill.
- [ ] TR shows all four fill series **side-by-side** (no toggle), each with its reference target % and fill % inline; 0% renders as "headroom," never as failure/red.
- [ ] BL TwinDonut: left=You fixed, right=comparison; **% inline on every slice**; fixed strategy→colour map shared with BR; no mandatory side legend.
- [ ] BR ZChart renders **large**, diverges from a centre 0 line, sorted by |delta|, `▲/▼` + sign on every bar, sub-significant bars greyed + labelled.
- [ ] Every number carries a ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover; synthetic target/model/wealth carries a SyntheticTag; **no CoverageBadge** on S1.
- [ ] **Peers ≠ Model visibly differ** (G1: `sum|peer−model|>5pp`) on TL and BL/BR.
- [ ] Captions (1–2 sentences) present under all four charts; cohort labelled **"Peers"** (never "All").
- [ ] PDF export present; snapshot footer "grounded · {snapshot_id}" on each card.

**Interaction**
- [ ] All four cards bind to **one** `GET /api/meta` call on load; skeletons hold final layout; value-in animates once ≤150ms.
- [ ] BL & BR ComparisonSelectors swap series **client-side** (no fetch); badges/captions update per selection; selectors are independent.
- [ ] Cohort change issues `POST /api/cohort` and re-renders **only** TL-Peers / TR-peer / BL-right(Peers) / BR-vs-peer; You/Platform/Model/Rank do **not** flicker; `n` updates everywhere.
- [ ] Clicking a Z-bar deep-links to `S2?strategy=&comparison=` with the strategy preselected; hover previews the matching BL slice.
- [ ] ProvenancePopover opens on any number/badge click, Esc-closable, one at a time.
- [ ] All seven states render: happy; cold-start (no You/Z-chart, Architect CTA, peer picture still shown); thin (RelaxBanner in words from `relaxed_dims`); low-coverage = n/a (no badge); null-perf suppressed; gated (exact paid-viewers copy + "5,500 total" in affected cards only); error (404 empty-state / degraded cached-snapshot banner, no fabricated numbers).
- [ ] Keyboard-navigable end to end; "view data" table affordance on every chart; over/under conveyed by sign + glyph + colour.


---

# Alpha — Frontend Surface Brief (deep) · for Claude Design

> Reads on top of **`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`** (the FRONTEND FOUNDATION: design system, tokens, component library incl. **TwinDonut / ZChart / ComparisonSelector / BarRow / ProvenancePopover / ConfidenceBadge / SyntheticTag / RelaxBanner / GatedState / CaptionBlock / PDFExportButton**, the locked chart language in Part 2.5, the seven-state model in Part 9, the global frame in Part 3). **Do not redefine those here — compose from them.** This brief specifies ONE surface end-to-end. Backend contract: **UC T1-5** in `Alpha_Copilot_Backend_Spec_DETAILED.md`, bound through `GET /api/detail/strategy?strategy=&comparison=` → `your_mix`, `your_funds[]`, `weighted_average_portfolio[]` (with `k_anon_ok`).

---

## Surface S2 — Strategy Deep-Dive
*(use case T1-5 detail + Weighted-Average Portfolio)*

### §1 Purpose & user questions

S2 is the **"what's actually inside my mix, and what do my peers actually own"** screen. S1 (Overview) answers *where do I stand* in aggregate; S2 answers it **strategy-by-strategy with named, concrete funds**. It is the surface that makes Moonfare's proprietary-comparison advantage tangible: the user sees their own funds in a pie, and beside it the **Weighted-Average Portfolio** of their peer cohort — the most-held, named funds that people like them actually concentrate in.

The surface must answer, for a single selected strategy at a time:

- **U1 — "Which funds make up my exposure to this strategy?"** → the left TwinDonut: the user's funds inside that strategy, by weight, named, with logos.
- **U2 — "What do people like me concentrate in here?"** → the right TwinDonut: the cohort's Weighted-Average Portfolio for the same strategy (most-held named funds; k-anon'd to buckets when a fund is too thinly held to name).
- **U3 — "Is my exposure concentrated or spread?"** → slice count + the largest-slice readout in each donut.
- **U4 — "How does my within-strategy concentration differ from my peers'?"** → the visual contrast of the two donuts (few fat slices vs many; named vs bucketed).
- **U5 — "How is my whole portfolio split across strategies, and which strategy am I drilling?"** → the top 100% stacked horizontal bar, with the active strategy highlighted.
- **U6 — "Who am I comparing against?"** → the ComparisonSelector on the right donut (Peers / Platform / Model …).

This is a **detail** surface: full-screen, **no scroll** (Foundation Part 0.2 / Part 3). Everything fits one viewport; the strategy chip selector swaps the content in place rather than growing the page.

The canonical caption that frames the surface (from the surface summary, T1-5 detail): *"Your buyout sleeve is three funds; your peers concentrate in EQT and CVC."* That sentence is the emotional payload — S2 exists to make it visible.

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

S2 is reachable two ways, and **both must land with a strategy pre-selected** so the screen is never empty on arrival:

1. **From the left rail** — the surface labelled **"Allocation"** (Foundation Part 3 rail order: *Overview · Allocation · Region · …*). Arriving via the rail with **no strategy in the route**, the FE pre-selects the **user's largest-weight strategy** from `your_mix` (the dominant slice of the top stacked bar). This guarantees the donuts render meaningful content immediately.
2. **From a Z-bar click on S1** — clicking any bar in S1's Over/underweight ZChart (Foundation S1, BR card) **deep-links to S2 with that strategy pre-selected** and the **same `comparison` value** the ZChart was showing (Peers / Platform / Model) carried across. The `ZChart` "emits the strategy" (Foundation Part 2.6); S2 consumes it as the selected chip + the right-donut comparison.

**Route shape.** `…/allocation?strategy={STRATEGY_KEY}&comparison={peers|platform|model}`.
- `strategy` absent → FE substitutes the largest-weight strategy key from `your_mix`; it then writes it back into the URL so the state is shareable and the PDF export is reproducible.
- `comparison` absent → defaults to `peers`.
- Both params are reflected back into the URL on every chip/selector change (deep-linkable, PDF-stable, back-button correct).

**Frame.** S2 inherits the persistent global frame (Foundation Part 3): ProfileBar on top, Cohort builder left, Ask Alpha floating bottom-right, PDFExportButton on the view. Switching to/from S2 is **instant and full-screen** — the frame never reloads. A **cohort change in the left rail re-resolves and re-renders only the cohort-dependent half** of S2 (the right donut + the stacked-bar peer reference) — never the user's own donut, which is cohort-independent.

---

### §3 Layout

Desktop ≥1280px. One viewport, no scroll. Three stacked regions inside the centre stage.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STRATEGY DEEP-DIVE                                       [PDF export ▾]   │
│  Your strategy mix and the funds behind it, vs your peers.                 │  ← surface title + sub
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  YOUR STRATEGY MIX                                                         │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │██████ Buyout 38% ▓▓▓ Growth 22% ▒▒ Venture 14% ░ PrivCredit 11% …│    │  ← 100% stacked
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      horizontal bar
│  (Buyout segment is highlighted = the active strategy)                     │
│  Caption: "You hold 38% of your private-markets wealth in Buyout."         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [ Buyout ] [ Growth ] [ Venture ] [ Private Credit ] [ Infra ] [ …9 ]     │  ← strategy chip selector
│    ▲ selected                                                              │
├───────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  YOUR FUNDS · Buyout              │  PEERS · Weighted-Average Portfolio · Buyout │
```
