```
│  ┌─────────────────────────┐      │  [ Peers · Platform · Model … ]  ← Comparison │
│  │      ◐ TwinDonut L      │      │  ┌─────────────────────────┐               │
│  │   fund names + logos    │      │  │      ◑ TwinDonut R      │               │
│  │   inside the slices     │      │  │  named funds + logos,   │               │
│  │      inline %           │      │  │  k-anon buckets, %      │               │
│  └─────────────────────────┘      │  └─────────────────────────┘               │
│  3 funds · largest 52%            │  8 funds · top 2 = EQT, CVC = 41%          │
│  ConfidenceBadge · Provenance     │  ConfidenceBadge n=41 · Provenance         │
├───────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Caption (spans both): "Your buyout sleeve is three funds; your peers       │
│  concentrate in EQT and CVC." · grounded · snapshot {id}                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Region A — Surface header.** Title (h1, 24/32) "Strategy Deep-Dive"; sub-line (caption) "Your strategy mix and the funds behind it, vs your peers." PDFExportButton top-right (Foundation Part 2.6).

**Region B — Top 100% stacked horizontal bar (`your_mix`).** A single full-width bar, all 9 strategies as segments, **% inline inside each segment** (Foundation chart rule: % inline, never legend-only), thin bar (not fat), large title "YOUR STRATEGY MIX". Fixed strategy→colour map (Foundation `--data-1…9`, pinned so a strategy is the same colour on every Alpha chart). The **active strategy segment is emphasised** (full saturation + a 2px `--ink` keyline; the others sit at ~70% opacity). A 1-sentence CaptionBlock under the bar reflects the active strategy ("You hold 38% of your private-markets wealth in Buyout."). The bar is also a control (see §6): clicking a segment selects that strategy.

**Region C — Strategy chip selector.** A single horizontal row of chips, one per strategy present in `your_mix` (9 max), in the same fixed colour order. Selected chip = filled with that strategy's `--data-*` colour + `--ink` label; unselected = outline. Chips wrap to a second line only below 768px (§10). This is the primary strategy control; the stacked-bar click is a secondary affordance for the same state.

**Region D — TwinDonut pair (the heart of the surface).** Composed from the Foundation **TwinDonut** (Part 2.6): **left = "Your funds" (fixed)**, **right = "Comparison" (swaps)**.

- **Left donut — YOUR FUNDS for the selected strategy** (`your_funds[]`). Each slice = one of the user's funds within that strategy, sized by within-strategy weight. **Fund NAMES + logos rendered inside the pie** (no side legend — Foundation: "fund/strategy names inside, no separate legend where avoidable"). Inline % on each slice. Header "YOUR FUNDS · {Strategy}". Footer micro-stats: "{n} funds · largest {x}%". This donut is **cohort-independent** — it never changes on a cohort edit, only on a strategy change.
- **Right donut — PEERS · Weighted-Average Portfolio for the selected strategy** (`weighted_average_portfolio[]`). Each slice = a most-held fund in the cohort within that strategy, sized by aggregate weight. Named + logo where `k_anon_ok:true`; **bucket label where `k_anon_ok:false`** (e.g. "a large-cap buyout fund" — §7 state 6). Inline %. Header "PEERS · Weighted-Average Portfolio · {Strategy}" — the header noun changes to **Platform / Model** as the ComparisonSelector swaps. The **ComparisonSelector sits directly above the right donut** (segmented control, Foundation Part 2.6: `Peers · Platform · Model · Harvard · Yale · Princeton · Aggregate`). Footer micro-stats: "{n} funds · top 2 = {a}, {b} = {sum}%".

Both donuts carry a **ConfidenceBadge** (the right donut's is the load-bearing one: `n=41 · ±…` from the cohort) and a **ProvenancePopover** (Foundation Part 2.6). A **shared CaptionBlock spans both donuts** (1–2 sentences contrasting the two), with the "grounded · snapshot {id}" provenance footer.

**Responsive.** ≥1280px: donuts side-by-side, equal width (Region D is a 2-column grid). 768–1279px: donuts remain side-by-side but shrink; chip row may wrap; ComparisonSelector collapses to a compact segmented control. <768px: single column — stacked bar → chips (horizontally scrollable strip, the only permitted horizontal scroll, and only for the chip strip, never a chart) → **Your donut** → **Peers donut** stacked vertically, each full-width; the shared caption sits under the lower donut. Donuts never require horizontal scroll (Foundation Part 3 / Part 10).

---

### §4 Components & charts (exact encodings)

#### 4.1 The 100% stacked horizontal bar (`your_mix`)
- **Type:** Foundation-approved **100% stacked horizontal bar** ("strategy mix across strategies" — Part 2.5). The ONLY correct chart for this region; do not substitute a donut or vertical bars here.
- **Encoding:** one bar; one segment per strategy; segment width = that strategy's % of the user's private-markets allocation; segments sum to 100%. Order = the fixed strategy order (so colour and position are stable across sessions).
- **Colour:** `--data-1 … --data-9`, pinned strategy→colour map (shared with both donuts so a strategy reads identically everywhere on the surface).
- **Labels:** `%` rendered **inside** each segment in mono/tabular figures; the strategy name inside if the segment is wide enough (>56px), else only `%` inside and the name in a thin caption rail below the bar aligned to the segment's centre. Never a detached side legend.
- **Active emphasis:** selected strategy segment at full saturation + 2px `--ink` keyline; non-selected segments at ~70% opacity. Transition ≤150ms, no bounce.
- **Caption:** single sentence keyed to the active strategy.

#### 4.2 The strategy chip selector
- **Type:** segmented chip group (not a dropdown — the strategies must be visible at a glance).
- **Items:** one chip per strategy key present in `your_mix`. Label = the human strategy name; a small colour dot (the strategy's `--data-*`) precedes the label.
- **Selected state:** filled with the strategy colour, `--ink` label, subtle elevation. Unselected: `--surface` fill, `--line` border, `--muted` label.
- **Keyboard:** arrow-key roving tabindex across chips; Enter/Space selects; `role="tablist"` with each chip `role="tab"` and the donut pair as the `tabpanel` (§10).

#### 4.3 The TwinDonut with inline % and fund-name-in-slice
- **Base:** Foundation **TwinDonut** — left fixed "Your portfolio", right swappable "Comparison", **% inline on slices** (Part 2.6).
- **Within-S2 specialisation:** both donuts are scoped to the **selected strategy** (not the whole portfolio). Left = `your_funds` for that strategy; right = `weighted_average_portfolio` for that strategy.
- **Fund-name-in-slice rendering rules:**
  - Each slice carries: the fund **logo** (small, leading), the **fund name**, and the **inline %**.
  - Placement: for slices ≥ ~8% of the circle, render `logo + name + %` *inside* the slice along the radial midline, name truncated with ellipsis to fit the arc; tabular `%`.
  - For slices < ~8% (too thin for inside text): render only a **leader line** from the slice to a compact label chip just outside the ring (`logo · name · %`), kept tight to the donut (Foundation rule: "tight pie↔legend"). This is a per-slice callout, **not** a detached block legend.
  - The donut centre shows the **strategy name + that donut's total** (left: "Buyout · your 38%"; right: "Buyout · peer mix"). The centre is also where the §7 empty/low states write their message.
- **Logo handling:** logos come from a `logo_url` on each fund object (left and right). Rendering rules: (a) if `logo_url` present → render at a fixed square box (e.g. 20px) with `--line` 1px ring, `alt="{fund name} logo"`; (b) if absent → render a **monogram fallback** (first 1–2 letters of the fund name on a `--brand-tint` chip), same box size; (c) **bucket slices carry NO logo** — they get a neutral lock/grey glyph instead (see k-anon below). Logos never determine slice order or size; weight does. Logos are decorative-but-labelled (always paired with the text name, so a missing/blocked image never loses meaning — A11y, §10).
- **Inline % format:** mono/tabular, one decimal only if any slice <10% otherwise integer; sum of visible slices = 100% of that strategy (a residual "other funds" slice absorbs the long tail beyond the top-N so the donut always closes to 100%).

#### 4.4 k-anon bucket labels (right donut only)
- Driven by **`k_anon_ok`** on each `weighted_average_portfolio[]` item (Foundation S2 binding; backend k-anon rule from T1-5 §6 — a fund held by `feeder_n_investors < threshold` returns `fund_id:null` and a generic descriptor).
- `k_anon_ok:true` → render the named fund (logo + name + %).
- `k_anon_ok:false` → render the **bucket label** verbatim from the field (e.g. **"a large-cap buyout fund"**), prefixed with a small `--gated`-grey lock glyph, **no logo**, slice in a desaturated/grey fill (visually distinct from named slices so the user understands it is anonymised, not a real fund name). The `%` still renders inline. Tooltip on the bucket slice: "Fewer than the minimum number of investors hold this fund, so it can't be named." Bucket slices are **never styled as a verified named holding** (Foundation Part 0.2: synthetic/limited fields visibly labelled).

#### 4.5 ComparisonSelector (right donut)
- Foundation **ComparisonSelector** segmented control. On S2 it drives **only the right donut** (and that donut's header noun + ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover). The left "Your funds" donut never responds to it.
- Value maps to the `comparison` query param and the `/api/detail/strategy?comparison=` call (§5).

#### 4.6 Badges & caption
- **ConfidenceBadge** on each donut (Foundation): right donut shows `n={cohort.n} · ±{ci}`; greys the donut's stats when the cohort is not significant. Left donut shows your own coverage note (no cohort n — it's your own data).
- **ProvenancePopover** on each donut: cohort definition, n, fields used, method ("weighted-average portfolio: most-held funds by aggregate within-strategy weight across the cohort"), snapshot_id.
- **CaptionBlock** spanning both donuts + the "grounded · snapshot {id}" footer.

---

### §5 Data bindings (element ← JSON path)

All of S2 binds to a single call:

**`GET /api/detail/strategy?strategy={KEY}&comparison={peers|platform|model|harvard|yale|princeton|aggregate}&investor_id={str?}`**

The call refetches on (a) strategy chip change (`strategy` param) and (b) ComparisonSelector change (`comparison` param) and (c) cohort change from the left rail (cohort body is resolved server-side from the active cohort; the FE re-issues the same call). Expected response shape (consistent with the T1-5 Foundation summary and the `weighted_average_portfolio` / k-anon contract in `Alpha_Copilot_Backend_Spec_DETAILED.md` §3.5/§6):

```jsonc
{
  "strategy": "buyout",                      // echoed selected strategy
  "comparison": "peers",                     // echoed comparison basis
  "your_mix": {                              // → Region B stacked bar
    "buyout":   { "value": 38.0, "provenance": {…} },
    "growth":   { "value": 22.0, "provenance": {…} },
    "venture":  { "value": 14.0, "provenance": {…} },
    "private_credit": { "value": 11.0, "provenance": {…} },
    "…": {}                                  // all 9 strategies; values sum ≈100
  },
  "your_funds": [                            // → LEFT donut (selected strategy only)
    { "fund_id":"f_0012", "name":"Apex Buyout III", "logo_url":"…",
      "weight_pct": 52.0, "provenance": {…} },
    { "fund_id":"f_0044", "name":"Northstar PE II", "logo_url":"…", "weight_pct": 31.0 },
    { "fund_id":"f_0091", "name":"Helios Buyout",   "logo_url": null, "weight_pct": 17.0 }
  ],
  "weighted_average_portfolio": [            // → RIGHT donut (selected strategy only)
    { "fund_id":"f_eqt9",  "name":"EQT X",  "logo_url":"…", "weight_pct": 22.0, "k_anon_ok": true },
    { "fund_id":"f_cvc8",  "name":"CVC IX",  "logo_url":"…", "weight_pct": 19.0, "k_anon_ok": true },
    { "fund_id": null,     "name":"a large-cap buyout fund", "logo_url": null,
      "weight_pct": 7.0, "k_anon_ok": false },
    { "fund_id":"…", "…": {} }
  ],
  "cohort": { "n": 41, "strict_n": 41, "relaxed_dims": [], "gated": false, "headline_total": 2000 },
  "cold_start": false                        // true when investor has no holdings (state 2)
}
```

**Binding table:**

| UI element | ← JSON path |
|---|---|
| Stacked-bar segment widths + inline % | `your_mix[strategy].value` (each strategy) |
| Stacked-bar active segment | the `strategy` echo / selected chip |
| Stacked-bar caption | `your_mix[strategy].value` of the active strategy |
| Chip set | keys of `your_mix` |
| Left donut slices (size) | `your_funds[].weight_pct` |
| Left donut slice name | `your_funds[].name` |
| Left donut slice logo | `your_funds[].logo_url` (→ monogram fallback if null) |
| Left donut inline % | `your_funds[].weight_pct` |
| Left donut footer "{n} funds · largest {x}%" | `your_funds.length`, `max(weight_pct)` |
| Right donut slices (size) | `weighted_average_portfolio[].weight_pct` |
| Right donut slice name **or** bucket label | `weighted_average_portfolio[].name` gated by `.k_anon_ok` |
| Right donut slice logo | `weighted_average_portfolio[].logo_url` (none when `k_anon_ok:false`) |
| Right donut header noun (Peers/Platform/Model) | `comparison` |
| ConfidenceBadge (right) | `cohort.n` + `provenance.ci95` |
| ProvenancePopover | per-slice / per-donut `provenance` + `cohort` |
| RelaxBanner | `cohort.relaxed_dims` (non-empty) |
| GatedState | `cohort.gated == true` |
| Cold-start routing | `cold_start == true` |
| "grounded · snapshot {id}" footer | `provenance.snapshot_id` |

**Provenance.** Every `%` on this surface (stacked-bar segments, both donuts' slices) is backed by a `value+provenance` object and is therefore a **provenance chip** — clickable to the ProvenancePopover (Foundation Part 0.2: "a number never appears bare"). Synthetic/illustrative fields (e.g. an imputed wealth denominator behind `your_mix`) carry the **SyntheticTag** (§9).

**Loading.** On strategy/comparison/cohort change, render Foundation **calm skeletons** (Part 9 / Part 10 — "calm skeletons, not spinners"): the stacked bar becomes a single shimmer bar; each donut becomes a grey ring with a centred shimmer; chips and ComparisonSelector stay interactive (so the user can re-select). Skeletons hold the exact final layout (no layout shift). The left donut, being cohort-independent, does **not** re-skeleton on a cohort-only change — only the right donut + stacked-bar peer reference do.

---

### §6 Interactions & state transitions

1. **Pick a strategy chip → both donuts update.**
   - User clicks chip `Growth` (or arrow-keys + Enter). → URL `strategy` param := `growth`; stacked-bar active segment moves to Growth (≤150ms emphasis transition); chip selected state moves; **both donuts re-skeleton then re-render** from a fresh `/api/detail/strategy?strategy=growth&comparison={current}` call. Caption updates. Footer micro-stats update.
   - Transition guard: if the new strategy has **no `your_funds`** (user holds none of it) → left donut enters its **empty-slice state** (§7 state 5b: centre message "You hold no Growth funds yet"), while the right donut still renders the peer mix (this is a valuable comparison — "here's what peers hold that you don't").

2. **Click a segment of the top stacked bar → selects that strategy.** Identical effect to clicking the matching chip (single source of truth: both write the `strategy` param). The stacked bar and the chip row stay in sync.

3. **ComparisonSelector swaps the right donut.**
   - User picks `Platform` → URL `comparison` := `platform`; **only the right donut + its header noun + ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover re-render** (right-donut skeleton only); the left "Your funds" donut and the stacked bar are untouched; refetch `/api/detail/strategy?strategy={current}&comparison=platform`. Shared caption updates its comparison clause.

4. **Hover / focus a slice → fund + % + provenance.**
   - Hover (or keyboard-focus) any slice in either donut → a tooltip: `logo · fund name · weight %`; on the right donut, a bucket slice shows the k-anon explanation instead of a name.
   - Click a slice's `%` (it is a provenance chip) → **ProvenancePopover** opens (cohort, n, fields, method "weighted-average portfolio", snapshot_id). For a left-donut slice the popover shows your-own-holding provenance (no cohort n).

5. **Cohort change (left rail) → right half re-resolves.**
   - Editing the cohort (Foundation Part 6) re-resolves `cohort` server-side; the FE re-issues `/api/detail/strategy` with the current `strategy`+`comparison`. **Only the right donut + stacked-bar peer reference + ConfidenceBadge re-render**; the left donut does not (Foundation Part 3: "re-render only affected charts"). If the new cohort triggers relax → RelaxBanner appears above the right donut; if it triggers gating → the right donut swaps to GatedState (§7).

6. **Deep-link arrival from S1 Z-bar.** Route carries `strategy` + `comparison`; S2 mounts with that chip selected, that segment emphasised, and that comparison basis on the right donut — no intermediate empty frame.

7. **PDF export** (Foundation PDFExportButton) → exports the current S2 state (active strategy, both donuts, comparison basis, captions, badges) as a clean PowerPoint-style page; the URL params make the export reproducible.

---

### §7 The seven states (visuals + copy)

S2 renders all seven Foundation states (Part 9). The two donuts can be in **different** states simultaneously (e.g. left = happy, right = gated), and the surface must compose that gracefully.

**State 1 — Happy.** Full data, significant cohort. Stacked bar with all strategies; left donut with named funds; right donut with named peer funds (+ any k-anon buckets); ConfidenceBadge shows a healthy `n`; the contrast caption renders. *Copy (caption):* "Your buyout sleeve is three funds; your peers concentrate in EQT and CVC."

**State 2 — Cold-start (`cold_start:true`, no holdings).** The user has no portfolio yet. The stacked bar and the **left donut are replaced by a cold-start panel**: a calm card where the left donut would be, centre text **"No holdings yet"** + sub "Build your first private-markets allocation and your funds will appear here." + a **primary CTA "Open the Allocation Architect"** routing to S9 (Foundation: cold-start routes to Architect, never a broken analytics screen). The **right donut still renders the peer Weighted-Average Portfolio** for the (defaulted) strategy — this is deliberately FOMO-safe and useful: "here's what people like you own." The top stacked bar shows a dashed empty outline with the label "Your mix appears here once you invest." *Copy (CTA):* "Open the Allocation Architect". *Copy (left donut centre):* "No holdings yet".

**State 3 — Thin cohort (`relaxed_dims` non-empty).** The cohort was too small at full strictness and the backend broadened it. Render the Foundation **RelaxBanner** directly above the right donut, in words from `relaxed_dims` (Foundation Part 2.6): *"Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band to show enough peers."* The right donut renders normally on the relaxed cohort; the ConfidenceBadge reflects the relaxed `n`. The left donut is unaffected.

**State 4 — Low coverage.** Not a primary state for S2 (no look-through here), but if `your_mix`/`your_funds` are computed on partial allocation data, a **CoverageBadge** ("based on {x}% of your committed capital") attaches to the left donut and the stacked-bar caption gains "based on {x}% of your committed capital." Never imply 100%.

**State 5 — Null / insufficient.**
- **5a (right donut, no peer data):** if `weighted_average_portfolio` is empty for the selected strategy (peers hold none of it) → right donut centre message **"No peers in this cohort hold {Strategy} funds"** + sub "Try a broader cohort or a different strategy." Never zero-fill, never a fake slice.
- **5b (left donut, you hold none):** selected strategy has empty `your_funds` → left donut centre **"You hold no {Strategy} funds yet"** + a small link "See funds that fit →" (to S14). Right donut still shows the peer mix — the contrast is the point.

**State 6 — k-anon / gated.**
- **k-anon fail (per-slice):** any `weighted_average_portfolio[]` item with `k_anon_ok:false` renders as a **bucket slice** — grey fill, lock glyph, label verbatim **"a large-cap buyout fund"**, inline %, no logo (§4.4). The donut can mix named and bucketed slices freely. *Copy (tooltip):* "Fewer than the minimum number of investors hold this fund, so it can't be named."
- **Gated cohort (`cohort.gated:true`):** the cohort is too granular to show peer data at all. The **right donut is replaced by the Foundation GatedState** card: lock icon, headline **"Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate"** + the subtle **"5,500 total investors"** line (Foundation GatedState copy — use verbatim). The left "Your funds" donut and the stacked bar still render (your own data is never gated). ComparisonSelector is disabled while gated.

**State 7 — Error / degraded.** `/api/detail/strategy` fails or the engine is down → render the **last-good cached** S2 state with a quiet top banner (Foundation Part 9): *"Showing your last saved view — live data is reconnecting."* Never a blank screen, never a fabricated slice. If no cache exists, show calm skeletons with the same banner and a "Retry" affordance. Ask Alpha falls back gracefully (Foundation Part 5). The 404 (unknown investor) path shows a single calm card "We couldn't find this portfolio."

Plus the cross-cutting rules: **not-significant** cohort → ConfidenceBadge greys the right-donut stats and the contrast caption is softened to "indicative" (Foundation: sub-CI deltas render greyed). **SyntheticTag** on any imputed input (§9). **Loading** = calm skeletons (§5).

---

### §8 Copy deck (verbatim strings)

| Key | String |
|---|---|
| Surface title | **Strategy Deep-Dive** |
| Surface sub-line | Your strategy mix and the funds behind it, vs your peers. |
| Region B title | YOUR STRATEGY MIX |
| Stacked-bar caption (template) | You hold **{pct}%** of your private-markets wealth in **{Strategy}**. |
| Left donut header (template) | YOUR FUNDS · {Strategy} |
| Left donut footer (template) | {n} funds · largest {pct}% |
| Right donut header — Peers | PEERS · Weighted-Average Portfolio · {Strategy} |
| Right donut header — Platform | PLATFORM · Weighted-Average Portfolio · {Strategy} |
| Right donut header — Model | MODEL PORTFOLIO · {Strategy} |
| Right donut footer (template) | {n} funds · top 2 = {a}, {b} = {sum}% |
| Contrast caption (canonical) | Your buyout sleeve is three funds; your peers concentrate in EQT and CVC. |
| Contrast caption (template) | Your {Strategy} sleeve is **{your_n} fund(s)**; your peers concentrate in **{top1}** and **{top2}**. |
| Provenance footer (template) | grounded · snapshot {snapshot_id} |
| k-anon bucket label | a large-cap buyout fund *(verbatim from backend `name`; do not paraphrase)* |
| k-anon tooltip | Fewer than the minimum number of investors hold this fund, so it can't be named. |
| Cold-start left-donut centre | No holdings yet |
| Cold-start sub | Build your first private-markets allocation and your funds will appear here. |
| Cold-start CTA | Open the Allocation Architect |
| Cold-start stacked-bar placeholder | Your mix appears here once you invest. |
| RelaxBanner | Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band to show enough peers. |
| Right-donut empty (5a) | No peers in this cohort hold {Strategy} funds |
| Right-donut empty sub | Try a broader cohort or a different strategy. |
| Left-donut empty (5b) | You hold no {Strategy} funds yet |
| Left-donut empty link | See funds that fit → |
| Gated headline | Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate |
| Gated sub | 5,500 total investors |
| Error banner | Showing your last saved view — live data is reconnecting. |
| 404 card | We couldn't find this portfolio. |
| Coverage note (template) | based on {x}% of your committed capital |

Voice: calm, plain, "doctor not a pro" (Foundation). Comparisons say **"Peers"**, never "All". Never advice language ("you should buy…") — S2 is descriptive; action lives on S14/S20.

---

### §9 Badge rendering (on S2 specifically)

- **ConfidenceBadge** (Foundation Part 2.6) — on the **right donut**, bound to `cohort.n` + `provenance.ci95`: chip `n={n} · ±{ci}`. When the cohort is not significant, the badge greys the right-donut footer stats and the contrast caption. The left donut's badge is a lighter "your data" variant (no cohort n, just a provenance affordance).
- **ProvenancePopover** (Foundation Part 2.6) — attached to every `%` chip and to each donut header. Right-donut popover: cohort definition + `n` + fields + method "weighted-average portfolio (most-held funds by aggregate within-strategy weight)" + `snapshot_id`. Left-donut popover: "your holdings as of {snapshot}".
- **SyntheticTag** (Foundation Part 2.6, soft amber) — attached where an input is imputed/illustrative: if the **wealth denominator** behind `your_mix` is illustrative, tag the stacked-bar title; if any peer field is synthetic (the synthetic snapshot carries `provenance.synthetic:true` per T1-5 §5/§8), tag the right donut "illustrative". Never style synthetic data as verified fact.
- **CoverageBadge** (Foundation) — only in state 4 (partial allocation data); "based on {x}% of your committed capital".
- **The k-anon bucket** is not a Foundation badge but a **slice treatment** (grey fill + lock glyph + verbatim bucket label, no logo) — §4.4. It must read as "anonymised peer holding," distinct from both a named fund and a SyntheticTag.
- **RelaxBanner** (state 3) and **GatedState** (state 6) per §7.

Badge stacking order on the right donut, top→bottom: RelaxBanner (if any) → ComparisonSelector → donut → footer stats → ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover affordance. SyntheticTag sits inline on the relevant title.

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive (Foundation Part 3 breakpoints).**
- **≥1280px:** three regions; Region D is a 2-column equal-width donut grid; chips on one row.
- **768–1279px:** donuts stay side-by-side but scale down; chip row may wrap to 2 lines; ComparisonSelector becomes a compact segmented control; cohort builder is a drawer (frame-level). Donuts never shrink below legibility — if a slice's inside label can't fit, it falls back to the leader-line callout (§4.3).
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go):** single column, stacked: title → stacked bar (full-width) → **chip strip (horizontally scrollable — the only permitted horizontal scroll, and only the chip strip, never a chart)** → Your donut (full-width) → Peers donut (full-width, with its ComparisonSelector above it) → shared caption. No analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll (Foundation Part 3/10). The donuts stack so the comparison reads top-to-bottom; the contrast caption sits at the bottom tying them together.

**Accessibility (Foundation Part 10, WCAG-AA).**
- **Never colour-only.** Strategy segments and donut slices are distinguished by colour **and** by their inline text label (strategy/fund name + %), so a colour-blind or grayscale viewer loses nothing. The active stacked-bar segment is marked by the `--ink` keyline (a shape cue), not colour alone. k-anon buckets carry the lock glyph (shape cue) in addition to grey fill.
- **"View data" affordance.** Each chart exposes its data as a table behind a "View data" control (Foundation Part 10): the stacked bar → a strategy/% table; each donut → a fund/% (or bucket/%) table. Bucket rows in the table read "a large-cap buyout fund — 7%".
- **Keyboard.** Chip selector is a `role="tablist"` with roving tabindex (arrow keys move, Enter/Space selects), the donut pair is the `role="tabpanel"`. ComparisonSelector is a labelled radio/segmented group, arrow-key operable. Donut slices are focusable (`tabindex`), Enter opens the ProvenancePopover; Escape closes. Full focus-visible rings on every interactive element.
- **ARIA & alt.** Each donut has an `aria-label` summarising it ("Your Buyout funds: Apex Buyout III 52%, Northstar PE II 31%, Helios Buyout 17%"). Logos `alt="{fund} logo"`; monogram fallbacks are `aria-hidden` (the adjacent text name carries the meaning). Bucket slice `aria-label` = "{bucket label}, {pct} percent, anonymised". Provenance popovers are dialog-role, focus-trapped, dismissible.
- **Contrast & type.** Numbers in mono/tabular; chart title h2 minimum (Foundation: chart titles large — a recorded complaint); legends/labels never below caption size; AA contrast on slice labels (use the slice's text-on-fill contrast pair from the token set, swapping to `--ink` or white per fill luminance).
- **Motion.** Value-in animates once, ≤150ms, no bounce/parallax; respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant render).
- **i18n.** All strings from the copy deck are externalised (EN + DE); `%`/currency localised (EU formatting); the contrast-caption template composes grammatically in German (no hard-coded word order).

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

A build of S2 is correct when:

1. **Single source.** The whole surface binds to one `GET /api/detail/strategy?strategy=&comparison=` call; changing strategy or comparison or cohort re-issues that one call with the right params and re-renders only the affected regions.
2. **Entry.** Reachable from the rail ("Allocation") **and** from an S1 Z-bar click; in both cases S2 mounts with a strategy **pre-selected** (S1 carries the clicked strategy + comparison; rail defaults to the largest-weight strategy) — never an empty screen. URL reflects `strategy` + `comparison` and is deep-linkable / PDF-stable.
3. **Stacked bar.** Top region is a 100% stacked horizontal bar of `your_mix` with **% inline inside segments**, fixed strategy→colour map, the active strategy emphasised, and a 1-sentence caption. Clicking a segment selects that strategy (in sync with the chips).
4. **TwinDonut.** Left = your funds for the selected strategy (cohort-independent); right = peer Weighted-Average Portfolio (cohort-dependent). **Fund names + logos render inside the slices** with inline % (no detached legend); thin slices use tight leader-line callouts.
5. **k-anon.** Items with `k_anon_ok:false` render as grey lock-glyph **bucket slices** with the verbatim label "a large-cap buyout fund", no logo, % intact; named and bucketed slices coexist; tooltip explains the anonymisation.
6. **ComparisonSelector** swaps **only the right donut** (header noun + badge + provenance update); the left donut never responds to it; the right donut also swaps on an S1 Z-bar-carried comparison.
7. **Interactions.** Pick a chip → both donuts update; hover/focus a slice → fund + %; every % is a provenance chip → ProvenancePopover; cohort change re-renders only the right half.
8. **Logos.** Present → image with ring; absent → monogram fallback; bucket → no logo. A blocked/missing image never loses the fund's meaning (text name always present).
9. **Seven states.** All render: happy; cold-start (left replaced by Architect CTA, right still shows peer mix); thin (RelaxBanner in words); low-coverage (CoverageBadge); null (5a right-empty / 5b left-empty messages, never zero-fill); gated (right donut → exact "Only for paid viewers…" + "5,500 total investors"); error (cached last-good + quiet banner). Left and right donuts can be in different states at once and compose cleanly.
10. **Badges.** ConfidenceBadge `n + ±CI` on the right donut (greys stats when not significant); ProvenancePopover on every number; SyntheticTag on imputed inputs; CoverageBadge in state 4.
11. **Copy.** Matches the §8 deck verbatim (gated, RelaxBanner, cold-start, k-anon tooltip, contrast caption); says "Peers" not "All"; no advice language.
12. **Responsive.** ≥1280 side-by-side donuts; <768 single-column stacked (Your donut over Peers donut), only the chip strip may scroll horizontally — no chart ever does.
13. **A11y.** Colour never sole encoder (text labels + ink keyline + lock glyph); "View data" tables behind every chart; full keyboard nav (tablist chips, focusable slices, dialog popovers); aria-labels on donuts and bucket slices; reduced-motion respected; EN/DE externalised strings.
14. **No-scroll.** S2 fits one viewport at desktop; the frame persists; Meta⇄Detail switches are instant.


---

## Surface S3 — Region

> **Frontend surface spec, depth grade.** This document specifies the complete frontend build for one Alpha copilot surface — **S3 · Region** (use case **T1-5 detail**, the geographic cut of "compare yourself to your peers"). It composes entirely from the design system, component library, chart language, seven-state model, and API contract defined in `Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md` (the FRONTEND FOUNDATION). Tokens, components, and global rules are **referenced, not redefined** — where this spec says `--over`, `ZChart`, `ComparisonSelector`, or `RelaxBanner`, it means exactly the artefact defined in the foundation (Parts 2.2, 2.5, 2.6, 6, 9). This spec resolves every open decision for S3 so it can be built without further questions.

---

### §1 — Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** S3 answers the geographic question inside the peer-comparison family: *Where in the world is my private-markets capital, and how does that compare to the people I benchmark against?* It is the regional sibling of S1 (Overview) and S2 (Strategy deep-dive) — same comparison grammar (You vs a swappable cohort, divergence rendered as a Z-chart), applied to the four-region axis **US · Europe · Asia · RoW** (Rest of World). It is a Detail surface in the persistent frame (Part 3): full-screen, no scroll, the cohort builder on the left and Ask Alpha floating.

The differentiator S3 must make *feel* obvious: a Moonfare investor can see — instantly, calmly, with provenance — that their geographic mix is (say) European-heavy and US-light relative to peers, and relative to **how endowments allocate geographically**. Region is the one cut where the endowment comparators (Harvard / Yale / Princeton / Aggregate) are most rhetorically powerful — sophisticated allocators are famously global — so S3's ComparisonSelector explicitly includes them.

**The concrete user questions S3 must answer at a glance:**

1. *What is my geographic split across US, Europe, Asia, and Rest of World?* → the left donut, inline %.
2. *Where am I over- or under-exposed versus my peers?* → the right Z-chart, large, diverging from a 0 line.
3. *Does that picture change if I compare to the whole platform, to the model, or to endowments?* → the ComparisonSelector.
4. *What does a peer / endowment geographic mix actually look like, not just my deviation from it?* → the donut's **You ↔ Peer toggle**, which flips the same donut to show the comparison cohort's own regional shares.
5. *How solid is this — how many peers, how is it computed, is anything illustrative?* → ConfidenceBadge, ProvenancePopover, SyntheticTag, and the caption.

**What S3 deliberately does NOT do.** It does not show absolute € by region (relative-only rule, Part 0.2). It does not rank the investor ("you are the most European investor"). It does not editorialise about whether US tech or European buyout is "better" — it states position and deviation, and lets Ask Alpha explain. It does not introduce a new chart type: region uses the **twin-of-one donut + large Z-chart** pattern already established for the comparison family.

**Primary persona.** Investor — analytical (charts first, drills, toggles cohorts). Secondary: Investor — non-technical, who will more often ask Ask Alpha "am I too European?" and get the five-sentence answer; S3 is the surface that answer is grounded in. Advisor mode reuses S3 verbatim with the client selector in the ProfileBar.

---

### §2 — Entry & navigation

**Rail position.** S3 is **"Region"** in the left-rail surface list, third in the Allocation family ordering (`Overview · Allocation · Region · Format · Vintage · …`, Part 3). Selecting it swaps the centre stage instantly to S3 — full-screen, no scroll, frame persists (ProfileBar top, cohort builder left, Ask Alpha floating, PDF export available).

**Entry paths (all land on the same surface, differing only in pre-selected state):**

1. **Rail click "Region."** Default entry. Renders with the cohort builder's current cohort as the comparison, ComparisonSelector defaulting to **Peers**, donut showing **You**, no Z-bar pre-selected.
2. **Deep-link from S1 / S2.** If the user clicks a regional element elsewhere (e.g. an Ask Alpha answer chip "you're 12pp underweight US"), the link carries `?comparison=peers&region=US`: S3 opens with the **US** Z-bar pre-highlighted and the caption pre-scoped to US. The selector and cohort are inherited from the originating surface so the comparison is continuous.
3. **Ask Alpha "where am I overexposed geographically?"** Alpha's answer renders an inline mini Z-chart; a "View full region breakdown →" affordance routes to S3 with the same cohort.
4. **Cold-start (no holdings).** S3 is reachable but renders the cold-start state (§7.2): no You-donut, peer/endowment picture shown, CTA to the Architect. The rail entry is never disabled — a new investor can always *look* at the regional comparison.

**Navigation out.** Standard frame navigation: any other rail surface, full-screen instant switch. There is **no intra-S3 routing** (no sub-tabs) — the surface is a single stage; the only state lives in the ComparisonSelector, the donut toggle, and the selected Z-bar, all of which are in-place, non-navigational.

**State preservation.** When the user leaves S3 and returns within the session, the surface restores its last ComparisonSelector value, donut toggle position, and selected Z-bar (UI state only, not persisted server-side). Changing the cohort in the left rail while away and returning re-resolves S3's data but preserves the selector/toggle choices.

---

### §3 — Layout (grid)

**Desktop (≥1280px) — the centre stage, two-pane horizontal split.** S3 is a single MetaCard-scale stage divided into a **left pane (donut, ~38% width)** and a **right pane (Z-chart, ~62% width)**, both full-height within the no-scroll stage, with the ComparisonSelector spanning the top of the right pane (driving both panes). Captions sit beneath each pane.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Region — where your capital sits                       [PDF export ▾]     │  ← surface title (h1) + export
├───────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  YOUR GEOGRAPHIC MIX          │  [ Peers · Platform · Model ·               │  ← ComparisonSelector (right-pane top)
│  [ You ⇄ Peer ] toggle        │    Harvard · Yale · Princeton · Aggregate ] │
│                               │                                            │
│        ╭───────────╮          │   OVER / UNDERWEIGHT vs Peers              │  ← Z-chart title (h2, large)
│       ╱   US 22%    ╲         │                                            │
│      │  Europe 54%   │        │   US        ████████▶ +6pp   ▲             │  ← ZChart, large, diverges from 0
│      │  ╭───────╮    │        │   Europe  ◀████ −9pp        ▼             │
│       ╲ │ Asia  │   ╱         │   Asia      ██▶ +2pp                       │
│        ╲│ 16%   │  ╱          │   RoW     ◀█ −1pp  (greyed: n.s.)          │
│         ╰───────╯─ RoW 8%     │                   │0                       │
│                               │                                            │
│  Caption (1–2 sentences)      │   Caption (1–2 sentences)                  │  ← CaptionBlock under each pane
│  ConfidenceBadge · prov.      │   ConfidenceBadge · prov.                  │
└───────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Left pane — the geo donut.**
- A single donut of four slices in fixed region→colour order **US · Europe · Asia · RoW** (the regional palette, §4), each slice carrying its **inline %** label (e.g. "Europe 54%") on or adjacent to the slice — never only in a side legend (Part 0.2 rule). The donut centre is empty or holds a small region-count glyph; no headline absolute € (relative-only).
- Above the donut: a small section label **"Your geographic mix"** and the **You ⇄ Peer toggle** (a two-position segmented control, §4/§6). When toggled to Peer, the section label becomes **"{Comparison} geographic mix"** and the slices re-animate to the comparison cohort's own regional shares.
- Beneath the donut: a CaptionBlock and the ConfidenceBadge / ProvenancePopover for the donut's figures.

**Right pane — the large Z-chart.**
- The ComparisonSelector across the top (segmented control with the seven comparators incl. endowments).
- A large h2 title that names the active comparison: **"Over / underweight vs Peers"** (or vs Platform / Model / Harvard / …).
- The **ZChart** rendered large (this is the surface's hero per the "Z large" rule): four horizontal divergence bars, one per region, diverging left/right from a central **0pp** line; **over** bars extend right in `--over` with a ▲ and a `+Xpp` inline label, **under** bars extend left in `--under` with a ▼ and a `−Xpp` label. Region order matches the donut (US, Europe, Asia, RoW top-to-bottom) so eye-tracking between panes is trivial.
- Beneath: a CaptionBlock that synthesises the headline ("You are 9pp underweight Europe and 6pp overweight US vs your peers") and the ConfidenceBadge / ProvenancePopover.

**Responsive.**
- **≥1280px** — the two-pane split above; cohort builder fixed-open on the far left, Ask Alpha floating bottom-right.
- **768–1279px** — the cohort builder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher button (Part 3). The two panes **stack vertically**: donut on top (full stage width, centred, capped max-width so it doesn't balloon), Z-chart below at full width (which actually *helps* the Z-chart breathe). The ComparisonSelector moves to sit above the Z-chart in the stacked order. No horizontal scroll on either chart.
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go)** — single column: surface title → ComparisonSelector (full-width, may wrap to two rows of chips with the endowments on the second row) → You⇄Peer toggle → donut (full width) → donut caption → Z-chart (full width, bars remain horizontal, labels inside/adjacent) → Z caption. ProfileBar becomes a sheet. The Z-chart stays the visual anchor; the donut shrinks gracefully. Charts reflow, never horizontally scroll.

**No-scroll guarantee.** On desktop the entire surface (both panes, both captions, both badge rows) fits one screen at ≥1280×800. If vertical space is constrained, captions truncate to one line with a "more" affordance rather than introducing scroll on the analytical stage.

---

### §4 — Components & charts (exact encodings)

S3 composes from four foundation artefacts. Each is specified here with its exact regional encoding.

**4.1 The geo donut (left pane).**
- Component: a single-donut instance of the foundation donut family (the **TwinDonut** reduced to one donut, since region uses a *toggle* not a side-by-side pair — see §6.2 for why). It carries the donut's standard hover, focus, empty, loading, and error states.
- **Slices & order (fixed):** `US`, `Europe`, `Asia`, `RoW`, always in that clockwise order starting at 12 o'clock, so the same region is in the same position on every render and across the You⇄Peer toggle.
- **Region → colour tokens (pinned, exactly as the strategy→colour map is pinned in 2.2; reuse the sequential `--data-*` ramp so region colours sit in the same family as the rest of the product):**
  - **US** → `--data-2` (the brighter blue end of the ramp)
  - **Europe** → `--data-4` (mid blue-teal)
  - **Asia** → `--data-6` (teal)
  - **RoW** → `--data-8` (mint end)
  These four are spaced across the 9-step ramp for maximum color-blind-safe separation while remaining the same blue→teal→mint progression as strategy and format colours, so the product reads as one system. Pin this map globally so US is the same blue everywhere it appears (Z-chart bar fills inherit it as a subtle tint behind the over/under semantic colour — see 4.2).
  - The 0-state / no-data slice (if a region is genuinely 0%) renders as a hairline gap, not a coloured sliver, and is omitted from labels.
- **Inline %:** each slice shows `Region NN%` as a tabular/mono number (numbers always mono, 2.3) placed on the slice if it fits, else on a short leader line just outside it. No separate legend block; the region name lives with its %.
- **Centre:** empty by default (calm). Optionally a tiny "4 regions" count in `--muted` caption size; never an absolute € total.
- **You ⇄ Peer toggle:** a small two-segment control above the donut (`You` | `Peer`). Default `You`. Flipping it re-binds the donut from `you.regions` to `peer.regions` (the *active comparison's* own shares, §5/§6) and re-animates slices once (≤150ms, value-in, settle — no spin). The toggle label "Peer" dynamically reads the active comparison ("Harvard" when Harvard is selected, etc.), so the toggle and selector stay consistent.

**4.2 The large Z-chart (right pane).**
- Component: **ZChart** (foundation 2.6 / 2.5 divergence chart), **rendered large** — this is the surface hero and satisfies the product-wide "Z large" rule and S3's acceptance criterion.
- **Bars:** four horizontal bars, one per region, in donut order (US, Europe, Asia, RoW), diverging from a central vertical **0pp** baseline. Bar length encodes the deviation magnitude in **percentage points** (pp) of regional weight: `you.regions[r] − comparison.regions[r]`.
- **Semantic encoding (colour + glyph + sign, never colour-only, 2.2):**
  - Overweight (you > comparison): bar extends **right**, fill `--over`, prefixed/suffixed glyph **▲**, inline label `+Xpp` at the bar end.
  - Underweight (you < comparison): bar extends **left**, fill `--under`, glyph **▼**, label `−Xpp`.
  - The pinned region colour (4.1) appears as a thin leading cap or subtle tint on each bar so the bar is still identifiably "the US bar," but the dominant read is the over/under semantic colour.
- **Not-significant deltas:** any region whose delta is within the cohort CI (`significant:false`) renders **greyed** (reduced-opacity bar + muted label) with a small "n.s." marker (2.5 / Part 9 "not-significant"). The number still shows; it is visibly de-emphasised, not hidden.
- **0 line & scale:** the 0pp line is a solid hairline (`--line`); a symmetric scale so +6pp and −6pp are equidistant. Max scale auto-fits the largest absolute delta with a little headroom; ticks at sensible pp intervals, mono labels.
- **Region row labels** sit on the outer (left) edge in `--ink`, aligned with the donut's slice order.
- **Interaction:** clicking a bar selects that region (§6.3) — highlights the bar, scopes the caption to that region, and (matching the family's deep-link grammar) could carry the region into Ask Alpha; it does **not** swap the donut (region's donut is toggled by You⇄Peer, not by Z-bar — this differs from S1/S2 where the Z-bar drives the donut, because S3's donut already shows all regions at once and the comparison is a *cohort*, not a *strategy*; documented here so the build doesn't wire S3 like S1).

**4.3 The ComparisonSelector (incl. endowments).**
- Component: **ComparisonSelector** (2.6), the segmented control. S3 uses the **full seven-comparator set including endowments**, because region is the cut where endowment comparison is most meaningful:
  `Peers · Platform · Model · Harvard · Yale · Princeton · Aggregate`
- Default **Peers**. Selecting a comparator re-binds **both** the Z-chart (the deviation) **and** the donut's Peer side (the comparison cohort's own shares), and updates both pane titles/captions and the You⇄Peer toggle's "Peer" label.
- The endowment comparators (Harvard / Yale / Princeton / Aggregate) carry a **SyntheticTag** if the endowment regional figures are illustrative/modelled rather than reported (see §9) — the tag rides on the selector chip and on the resulting caption, so the user never mistakes an illustrative endowment mix for verified fact.
- On narrow viewports the selector wraps; the four endowments may form a second row, visually grouped (a subtle divider or a "Endowments" mini-label) so it's clear they're a distinct comparator family.

**4.4 CaptionBlock (×2).** Each pane has a 1–2 sentence CaptionBlock (2.6, mandatory under every chart). They are written in §8. The Z-chart caption is the surface's headline sentence; the donut caption describes the mix in plain terms.

**4.5 Badges (per pane).** ConfidenceBadge (`n=… · ±…pp`) and ProvenancePopover trigger on every numeric pane (2.6). SyntheticTag appears on the donut/Z if the underlying weights rely on imputed inputs, and always on endowment comparisons that are illustrative.

---

### §5 — Data bindings

**Endpoint.** S3 binds to a single call: `GET /api/detail/region?comparison={peers|platform|model|harvard|yale|princeton|aggregate}&investor_id={id?}` (Part 0.3; `investor_id` optional, defaults to the active "You"/impersonated client). One call resolves the whole surface; changing the ComparisonSelector re-issues this call with the new `comparison` and re-renders **only** the two panes (Part 6 / performance rules — re-render only affected charts).

**Response shape (per the Part 0.3 envelope — every numeric value arrives as `{value, provenance:{method,n,ci95,coverage_pct,significant,synthetic,vintage_scope,snapshot_id}}`):**

```
GET /api/detail/region  →
{
  "you":  { "regions": { "US":  {value, provenance},
                         "Europe": {value, provenance},
                         "Asia":   {value, provenance},
                         "RoW":    {value, provenance} } },
  "peer": { "label": "Peers" | "Harvard" | …,
            "regions": { "US": {value, provenance}, "Europe":…, "Asia":…, "RoW":… },
            "synthetic": false },          // true for illustrative endowment mixes
  "z_chart": { "comparison": "peers",
               "deltas": [ { "region":"US",     "value": +6.0, "significant": true,  provenance },
                           { "region":"Europe", "value": -9.0, "significant": true,  provenance },
                           { "region":"Asia",   "value": +2.0, "significant": true,  provenance },
                           { "region":"RoW",    "value": -1.0, "significant": false, provenance } ] },
  "cohort": { "n": 312, "strict_n": 287, "relaxed_dims": ["age"], "gated": false }
}
```

**Element ← field map (exact):**

| UI element | ← binding |
|---|---|
| Donut slices (You) | `you.regions.{US,Europe,Asia,RoW}.value` (each → inline %) |
| Donut slices (Peer toggle on) | `peer.regions.{…}.value` |
| Donut section label / toggle "Peer" text | `peer.label` |
| Donut SyntheticTag | `peer.synthetic` (when Peer side shown) and any `you.regions[*].provenance.synthetic` |
| Z-chart bars (length + side + sign) | `z_chart.deltas[].value` (sign → over/under, magnitude → length) |
| Z-chart greyed / "n.s." | `z_chart.deltas[].significant === false` |
| Z-chart title comparison name | `z_chart.comparison` (mapped to label) |
| ConfidenceBadge (donut) | derived from `you.regions[*].provenance.n` + `ci95` (cohort n + ±pp) |
| ConfidenceBadge (Z) | `z_chart.deltas[*].provenance.n` + `ci95` |
| ProvenancePopover (any number) | that value's `provenance{method,n,ci95,coverage_pct,significant,snapshot_id, vintage_scope}` |
| RelaxBanner | `cohort.relaxed_dims` (rendered in words) when `relaxed_dims` non-empty |
| GatedState trigger | `cohort.gated === true` |
| Cohort `n` readout (shared with left rail) | `cohort.n` (+ `strict_n`) |
| "grounded · snapshot {id}" footer (if shown) | any `provenance.snapshot_id` |

**Cohort coupling.** The `comparison` query param is set by the ComparisonSelector. The *cohort definition* (who "Peers" are) comes from the left-rail CohortBuilder's `POST /api/cohort` resolution (Part 6); S3 passes the resolved cohort context so "Peers" on S3 means exactly the cohort the user built. When the user edits the cohort in the rail, S3 re-resolves `/api/detail/region` and re-renders the two panes only.

**Coverage.** Region weights are computed from fund domicile/geography mappings; if look-through-level geography is partial, `provenance.coverage_pct` is surfaced via the donut's ProvenancePopover, but region is generally fund-level (not deep look-through), so a CoverageBadge is shown **only** when `coverage_pct < 100` on the region figures — it is not a headline state here the way it is on S6.

---

### §6 — Interactions

**6.1 ComparisonSelector swaps the comparison.** Clicking `Peers → Platform → Model → Harvard → …`:
- re-issues `GET /api/detail/region?comparison=…`;
- re-animates the **Z-chart** to the new deltas (bars grow/shrink/flip side once, ≤150ms, settle);
- re-binds the **donut's Peer side** so that *if* the toggle is on Peer, the donut shows the newly selected cohort's regional mix; if the toggle is on You, the donut is unchanged (You doesn't depend on the comparison) but its caption's comparative clause updates;
- updates the Z title ("Over / underweight vs Harvard"), the You⇄Peer toggle's "Peer" label ("Harvard"), and both captions;
- shows/removes the **SyntheticTag** depending on whether the selected comparator is illustrative (endowments).
- The selector is keyboard-navigable (arrow keys between segments, Enter to select) with visible focus (2.5 / Part 10).

**6.2 The You ⇄ Peer donut toggle.** This is S3's distinctive interaction and the reason the left pane is a *single toggled donut* rather than a side-by-side TwinDonut:
- **You** (default): donut = your four regional shares. Section label "Your geographic mix."
- **Peer**: donut flips to the active comparison's *own* shares (e.g. Harvard's US/Europe/Asia/RoW split). Section label "{label} geographic mix." Slices re-animate in place (same colour map, same slice order — only the magnitudes change), so the user reads the *shape difference* directly.
- Rationale (documented so the build doesn't substitute a twin-donut): region has only four slices and the Z-chart already carries the side-by-side deviation, so a static twin would be redundant and crowd the no-scroll stage; the toggle lets the user A/B the two mixes on the *same* donut, which reads the shape change more cleanly than two small pies. (S1/S2, with 9 strategies, use the full TwinDonut; S3 intentionally uses the single toggled donut.)
- Toggle is keyboard-operable; its state persists across selector changes within the session.

**6.3 Z-bar selection.** Clicking a Z-chart bar:
- highlights that region's bar (elevated, others slightly dimmed);
- scopes the **Z caption** to that region ("You're 9pp underweight Europe vs your peers — the largest geographic gap in your portfolio");
- subtly emphasises the corresponding **donut slice** (the matching slice gets a focus ring) so the two panes link;
- does **not** swap the donut (§4.2 rationale) and does **not** navigate away;
- makes the selected region the default subject if the user then opens Ask Alpha from this surface ("Why am I underweight Europe?").
- Click again / click elsewhere / Esc deselects.

**6.4 Hover.**
- **Donut slice hover:** tooltip with `Region · NN% · n=… · ±…pp`; the slice lifts subtly; the matching Z bar gets a light highlight (cross-pane mirroring).
- **Z bar hover:** tooltip with `Region · you NN% vs {comparison} NN% · Δ ±Xpp · n=… · significant?`; matching donut slice highlights.
- All hover affordances have keyboard-focus equivalents.

**6.5 Provenance.** Every number is a provenance entry point (Part 0.2 — "a number never appears bare"). Clicking any %, any pp delta, or the ConfidenceBadge opens the **ProvenancePopover**: cohort definition + `n` (and `strict_n` if relaxed) + the region fields used + `method` (e.g. "fund-domicile-weighted") + `snapshot_id` + `vintage_scope` + `coverage_pct` if <100. This is identical behaviour to the rest of the product; S3 adds no bespoke popover.

**6.6 Cohort change (left rail).** Editing the cohort (Part 6 set-algebra) re-resolves S3's single endpoint and re-renders only the two panes; the live `n` updates in the rail; if the backend relaxes, the **RelaxBanner** appears on S3 (§7.3) in words. Selector/toggle/selected-bar states are preserved across the re-resolve.

**6.7 PDF export.** The PDFExportButton (2.6) exports the current S3 view — both panes, active comparison, current toggle state, captions, badges — as a clean PowerPoint-style page, with provenance summarised in a footnote and any SyntheticTag carried through as a visible "illustrative" mark.

---

### §7 — Seven states

S3 renders all seven foundation states (Part 9). Each is styled from the shared state components; described here in S3 terms.

**7.1 Happy (full data, significant deltas).** Both panes populated; donut shows four labelled slices with inline %; Z-chart shows four bars with at least the major ones significant; captions carry the headline; ConfidenceBadge shows a healthy `n`. This is the layout in §3.

**7.2 Cold-start (no / negligible holdings).** No `you.regions` (or all ~0). The **You** side of the donut and the Z-chart's "you" leg are empty. S3 then:
- renders the **comparison picture only** — the donut defaults to **Peer** (showing the peer/endowment regional mix) with the section label "How your peers are positioned geographically," and the Z-chart is replaced by a calm placeholder: "Your geographic over/underweight will appear here once you hold positions";
- shows a prominent CTA: **"Build your target allocation → Architect"** (routes to S9), consistent with the global cold-start routing (Part 9 — never a broken analytics screen);
- keeps the ComparisonSelector live so a new investor can still explore how Peers / Harvard / Yale are positioned;
- the Welcome flow routes genuinely-new investors here only via the FOMO-safe path (peer picture + Architect CTA), never as a dead end.

**7.3 Thin cohort → RelaxBanner.** When `cohort.relaxed_dims` is non-empty, the **RelaxBanner** renders across the top of the stage (above the panes), in words from `relaxed_dims`: e.g. "Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band to reach a reliable peer group." The numbers below are the relaxed-cohort numbers; the ConfidenceBadge reflects the relaxed `n`; the ProvenancePopover shows both `n` and `strict_n`. Never "too small," never an error.

**7.4 Low coverage → CoverageBadge.** If region weights cover `< 100%` of NAV (some funds lack a geography mapping), a **CoverageBadge** ("based on 92% of NAV") attaches to the donut caption and the figures' ProvenancePopover. Region is fund-level, so this is usually high; the badge appears only when genuinely partial, and the captions never imply 100%.

**7.5 Null / insufficient.** If a comparison cohort has too few members to compute a region's share (e.g. an endowment with no reported Asia figure), that region's Z bar and the Peer-donut slice render as **"not enough history / not reported"** (a muted dash, not 0). Never zero-fill. The caption notes "Asia not reported for {comparison}."

**7.6 Gated.** When `cohort.gated === true` (the user has built an over-granular cohort and isn't a paid viewer), the panes are replaced by the **GatedState** card: the exact copy "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate," plus the headline "5,500 total investors." (Part 2.6 / Part 6 — exact copy, never "too small"/"I can't.") The ComparisonSelector and donut chrome are dimmed behind the gate.

**7.7 Error / degraded.** If `/api/detail/region` or the engine is down, S3 shows the **last-good cached** panes (if any) behind a quiet banner — "Showing your last saved snapshot · {timestamp}" — and disables the live selector with a tooltip; if no cache exists, a calm empty state ("We couldn't load your region breakdown — try again"), never a blank stage and never a fabricated number. Ask Alpha falls back gracefully if queried here.

**Plus (cross-state):** not-significant deltas render greyed with "n.s." (§4.2); SyntheticTag on any imputed input and on illustrative endowment comparisons; loading = calm skeletons of the two panes (a grey donut ring + four grey Z-bars), not spinners.

---

### §8 — Copy deck

All copy is structured for EN + DE (Part 10); strings below are the English source. Numbers in examples are placeholders shaped like the API.

**Surface title (h1):** `Region — where your capital sits`
**Donut section label (You):** `Your geographic mix`
**Donut section label (Peer):** `{comparison} geographic mix` → e.g. `Harvard's geographic mix`
**You⇄Peer toggle:** `You` | `{Peer label}` (Peer label = active comparison, e.g. `Peers`, `Harvard`)
**ComparisonSelector segments:** `Peers` · `Platform` · `Model` · `Harvard` · `Yale` · `Princeton` · `Aggregate`
**Z-chart title (h2, dynamic):** `Over / underweight vs {comparison}` → e.g. `Over / underweight vs Peers`
**Z-chart axis label:** `percentage points of regional weight (pp)`
**0-line label:** `0`

**Donut caption (CaptionBlock, mandatory, 1–2 sentences):**
- Happy: `Just over half your private-markets capital (54%) sits in Europe, with 22% in the US, 16% in Asia, and 8% in the rest of the world.`
- Peer-toggle on: `Your peers hold a more balanced mix — 28% US, 45% Europe, 18% Asia, 9% rest of world.`

**Z-chart caption (CaptionBlock, the headline sentence):**
- Happy: `You're 9pp underweight Europe… — correction: you're 6pp overweight US and 9pp overweight Europe versus your peers; your largest geographic tilt is toward Europe.`
  → use the data-driven form: `Your biggest geographic gap is {largest_abs_region}: you're {sign}{X}pp {over|under}weight {region} versus {comparison}. {second clause for the next-largest}.`
  Example rendered: `Your biggest geographic gap is Europe: you're 9pp underweight versus your peers. You're also 6pp overweight the US.`
- Region-selected (Z-bar click): `You're 9pp underweight Europe versus your peers — the largest geographic gap in your portfolio.`

**Caption for endowment comparison (adds the illustrative note):** `Endowment geographic mixes are illustrative, modelled from public disclosures — read as direction, not exact figures.`

**ConfidenceBadge:** `n=312 · ±2.1pp` (cohort size · CI in pp)
**Not-significant marker:** `n.s.` with tooltip `Difference is within the margin of error for this peer group.`
**CoverageBadge (when <100%):** `Based on 92% of NAV with a mapped geography.`

**RelaxBanner (thin cohort):** `Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band so the comparison stays reliable.`
**GatedState:** `Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate.` + headline `5,500 total investors.`
**Cold-start placeholder (Z pane):** `Your geographic over/underweight will appear here once you hold positions.`
**Cold-start CTA:** `Build your target allocation →` (routes to Architect)
**Cold-start donut label:** `How your peers are positioned geographically`
**Null/not-reported:** `{Region} not reported for {comparison}.`
**Error (degraded):** `Showing your last saved snapshot · {timestamp}.` / no cache: `We couldn't load your region breakdown — try again.`
**Provenance footer (where shown):** `Grounded · snapshot {snapshot_id}`

**Suggested Ask Alpha prompts (context-aware chips when Alpha is opened on S3):**
`How does my geography compare to peers?` · `Am I too European?` · `How do endowments allocate by region?` · `What would balance my US exposure?`

**Tone rules (apply to all S3 copy):** state position and deviation; never prescribe ("you should buy US funds") — that's S14/S20 territory and even there it's info-not-advice; never editorialise on regional quality; always pair an over/under statement with its comparator name; never use "All" for the cohort — always "Peers."

---

### §9 — Badge rendering

S3 renders the foundation badges (2.6) with these S3-specific rules:

**ConfidenceBadge** — one per pane, attached to the caption row. Shows `n=<cohort n> · ±<ci in pp>`. The `n` is the resolved cohort size (`cohort.n`); when relaxed, the badge uses the relaxed `n` and the ProvenancePopover discloses `strict_n` too. When a Z delta is **not significant**, the badge does not change but that specific bar greys and carries `n.s.` (the badge describes the pane's overall confidence; the bar describes the per-region significance).

**ProvenancePopover** — opens from any %, any pp delta, or either ConfidenceBadge. Contents: cohort definition (in words, e.g. "investors like you, 50–59, €10–50m, balanced"), `n` (+ `strict_n` if relaxed), the fields used ("fund-level geography weighting"), `method`, `coverage_pct` (if <100), `vintage_scope`, and `snapshot_id`. Standard component; no S3 bespoke fields.

**CoverageBadge** — rendered **only when** region `coverage_pct < 100` (§7.4). Attaches to the donut caption. Copy: "Based on {coverage_pct}% of NAV with a mapped geography." Not a headline state on S3 (unlike S6).

**SyntheticTag (the amber "illustrative" pill)** — rendered in two cases on S3:
1. **Endowment comparisons** — whenever the ComparisonSelector is on Harvard / Yale / Princeton / Aggregate **and** `peer.synthetic === true`, the tag rides on the selector chip, on the donut (when toggled to Peer), on the Z-chart title row, and is echoed in the caption ("illustrative, modelled from public disclosures"). Endowment regional mixes are not reported in Moonfare-comparable form, so they are modelled — this must never be styled as verified fact (Part 0.2).
2. **Imputed You inputs** — if any of the user's own region weights derive from imputed/illustrative holdings data (`you.regions[*].provenance.synthetic === true`), the tag attaches to the donut.
The SyntheticTag is carried through PDF export as a visible "illustrative" mark.

**not-connected / iPhoneFrame / gated-specific** — S3 has no signal-gated feed (region is computed from holdings, not a live external feed), so **NotConnectedState does not apply** here; the only locked state is the paid-viewer **GatedState** (§7.6). No iPhoneFrame on S3.

**Badge layering rule.** When multiple badges apply (e.g. relaxed cohort + endowment comparison), they stack in a single caption-adjacent row in priority order: SyntheticTag → CoverageBadge → ConfidenceBadge, with the RelaxBanner always at the top of the stage (above the panes), never inline with the per-pane badges.

---

### §10 — Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive (per Part 3 breakpoints; recap of §3 with the rules made explicit):**
- **≥1280px:** two-pane horizontal split (donut ~38% / Z-chart ~62%), cohort builder fixed-open, Ask Alpha floating. Entire surface fits one screen, no scroll.
- **768–1279px:** cohort builder → drawer, Ask Alpha → launcher; panes **stack vertically** (donut capped-width on top, Z-chart full-width below); ComparisonSelector above the Z-chart in stacked order. No horizontal scroll.
- **<768px:** single column (title → selector → toggle → donut → donut caption → Z-chart → Z caption); selector chips wrap (endowments on a second grouped row); ProfileBar → sheet. Z-chart bars stay horizontal and never require horizontal scroll; donut shrinks to a min legible size with labels on leader lines.
- **No analytical chart ever horizontally scrolls** (Part 0.2 / Part 10) — at the smallest size the Z-chart compresses its scale and the donut its diameter rather than overflowing.

**Accessibility (WCAG-AA, Part 10):**
- **Never colour-only:** every over/under bar pairs `--over`/`--under` with **▲/▼ and an explicit +/−pp sign**; the donut pairs colour with the inline region name + %. Colour-blind users read region and direction from glyph/label alone.
- **Contrast:** all text and chart labels meet AA against `--bg`/`--surface` in both light and dark token sets; mono numbers are large enough to read on a boardroom projector (caption-min for legends, 2.3).
- **Keyboard:** full keyboard nav across ComparisonSelector segments (arrow + Enter), the You⇄Peer toggle (Space/Enter), and Z-bars (Tab to focus a bar, Enter to select). Visible focus rings on every interactive element.
- **Screen readers:** ARIA roles on the selector (tablist/tab), the toggle (switch), and the charts. Each chart exposes a **"view data" table** affordance (Part 10) — the donut as a 4-row region/% table; the Z-chart as a region / you% / comparison% / Δpp / significant table — so the numbers are available non-visually. The caption is an `aria-describedby` for its chart.
- **Motion:** value-in animation ≤150ms, single settle, no parallax/bounce; respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (renders final state instantly).
- **Hover parity:** every hover tooltip has a focus-triggered equivalent so nothing is mouse-only.
- **i18n:** all strings externalised for EN + DE; % and pp formatted per locale (EU number formatting, comma decimal in DE); region names localised ("Europe"/"Europa", "Rest of World"/"Restliche Welt"); no hard-coded strings; layout tolerates the longer German labels (the donut leader lines and selector chips flex).

---

### §11 — Acceptance checklist

S3 is accepted only when **all** of the following hold (the three starred items are the surface's headline acceptance criteria from the brief):

1. **★ Inline % present** — every donut slice shows its region name + % inline on/adjacent to the slice; there is **no** standalone side legend carrying the only copy of the %.
2. **★ Z rendered large** — the ZChart is the right-pane hero, rendered large (not a thumbnail), diverging from a central 0pp line with one bar per region in donut order.
3. **★ Caption present** — a 1–2 sentence CaptionBlock sits under **each** pane (donut and Z-chart); the Z caption is data-driven and names the largest geographic gap and the comparator.
4. **One call resolves the surface** — all panes bind to a single `GET /api/detail/region`; the ComparisonSelector re-issues it with `comparison=` and re-renders **only** the two panes.
5. **Selector incl. endowments** — the ComparisonSelector offers all seven comparators (`Peers · Platform · Model · Harvard · Yale · Princeton · Aggregate`); endowment comparisons carry a **SyntheticTag** and the illustrative caption note.
6. **You ⇄ Peer toggle works** — the donut flips between the user's mix and the active comparison's own mix, re-animating in place with the same colour map and slice order; the toggle's "Peer" label tracks the selected comparator.
7. **Z-bar selection** — clicking a Z bar highlights it, scopes the Z caption to that region, and mirror-highlights the matching donut slice; it does **not** swap the donut (S3-specific, by design).
8. **Region→colour map pinned** — US/Europe/Asia/RoW use the fixed `--data-2/4/6/8` tokens in the same slice order on every render and across the toggle; the Z bars carry the pinned region tint behind the over/under semantic colour.
9. **Never colour-only** — every over/under encoding pairs colour with ▲/▼ and a +/−pp sign; not-significant deltas render greyed with `n.s.`.
10. **Every number has a badge + popover** — ConfidenceBadge per pane; ProvenancePopover from every %, every pp delta, and the badge; numbers are tabular/mono.
11. **Cohort changes move the numbers** — editing the left-rail cohort re-resolves S3 and re-renders the two panes; the live `n` updates; relaxing shows the **RelaxBanner** in words.
12. **All seven states render** — happy, cold-start (Peer picture + Architect CTA, no broken screen), thin (RelaxBanner), low-coverage (CoverageBadge only when <100%), null (not-reported dash, never zero-fill), gated (exact paid-viewer copy + "5,500 total investors"), error (last-good cache + quiet banner, never a fabricated number).
13. **Responsive & a11y** — two-pane→stacked→single-column with no horizontal scroll on either chart; full keyboard nav; "view data" table behind each chart; AA contrast in light + dark; EN/DE strings externalised.
14. **PDF export** — exports the current S3 view (both panes, active comparison, toggle state, captions, badges) cleanly, carrying any SyntheticTag through as a visible illustrative mark.
15. **Tone** — copy states position and deviation, never prescribes or editorialises; the cohort is always labelled "Peers," never "All."


---

## Surface S4 — Investment Format

> Deep frontend surface spec for Claude Design. Inherits the design system, chart language, 7-state model, ProfileBar/CohortBuilder/AskAlpha frame, and component library defined in **Alpha — Frontend Design Specification** (the FRONTEND FOUNDATION). This document does **not** redefine tokens, the global frame, the cohort builder, or Ask Alpha — it specifies, at build depth, the centre-stage of the **Investment Format** detail surface and its binding to `GET /api/detail/format`. Where the foundation already rules (e.g. "% inline on bars," "Z-charts render large," "label the cohort 'Peers'"), this spec obeys it and adds the surface-specific decisions so there are **zero open questions** at build time.

The single most important structural rule for this surface, repeated up front because it is the thing that goes wrong: **format allocation is rendered as one vertical bar per format — five separate bars standing side by side — NOT a single 100%-stacked horizontal bar.** The five formats' percentages do sum to 100% of the private-markets NAV, but they are presented as discrete, independently-scaled vertical columns so the user reads each format's weight on its own axis. The percentage is printed **inside** each bar. This is the inverse of the S2 Strategy-Mix surface (which legitimately uses a 100%-stacked bar). Do not let visual convergence collapse them.

---

### §1 — Purpose & the user questions it answers

**Purpose.** Show how the investor's private-markets capital is distributed across the **five investment formats** — Primary, Secondary, Direct / Co-Invest, Semi-liquid, and Fund-of-Fund — and how that distribution compares to the chosen peer cohort. Format is the "how you access private markets" lens, distinct from *strategy* (what you own: buyout, growth, infra…) and *region* (where it sits). A portfolio can be perfectly diversified by strategy yet entirely concentrated in a single access format, which carries its own liquidity, fee, and J-curve consequences. S4 surfaces that hidden dimension.

This is the **format facet of the T1-5 peer-comparison use case** ("compare yourself to your peers"). It is an analytical detail surface, reached from the Overview, and it must read at a boardroom-projector glance: five labelled columns, a divergence chart beside them, one caption, done.

**The concrete questions a user (a doctor, not a pro) brings to this screen, and where each is answered:**

1. *"How much of my private-markets money is in plain primary fund commitments versus the more exotic formats?"* → The five vertical format bars (§3, §4); the Primary bar is almost always the tallest and is read first.
2. *"Am I doing secondaries / co-investments at all, and how much?"* → Each format's own bar shows its % inside; a 0% format renders as a labelled empty column (the "headroom," never "failure," framing — §7 cold-start / null).
3. *"Is my format mix normal for people like me, or am I an outlier?"* → The ZChart (divergence) beside the bars: each format diverges left (under) or right (over) from the peer centre line, with `▲`/`▼` glyphs.
4. *"Which format am I most over- or under-exposed in versus my peers?"* → The longest diverging bar in the ZChart, surfaced verbally in the caption ("You are 11pp over-weight Primary and hold no Secondaries, where peers hold 14%").
5. *"Says who — how do you know what my peers do?"* → Every percentage is a provenance chip; the ProvenancePopover gives cohort label, n, CI, method, snapshot (§6).
6. *"Why does this matter?"* → The CaptionBlock translates the format skew into a plain-language consequence (liquidity / fee / pacing), never advice.

**Non-goals (explicitly out of scope for S4, to prevent scope creep):** no fund-level drill-down (that is S13 Funds), no liquidity forecast (S11), no fee comparison table (S13), no recommended action (S14 / S20). S4 *describes* the format split and its peer divergence; it does not prescribe. A single "What should I do about this?" affordance hands off to Ask Alpha / Next-Best-Action rather than answering inline.

---

### §2 — Entry & navigation

**Entry points.**
- **Left rail / top tab strip:** "Format" in the surface group *(Overview · Allocation · Region · Format · Vintage · Look-through · Performance · KPIs · …)*. Selecting it swaps the centre stage to S4 instantly, full-screen, no scroll; the persistent frame (ProfileBar, CohortBuilder, Ask Alpha, PDF export) stays mounted.
- **Deep-link from elsewhere:** Ask Alpha answers about format ("how much of my portfolio is in secondaries?") render a "View in Format" affordance that routes here. The S1 Overview does not carry a format Z-bar (Overview's Z-chart is strategy-based), so there is no S1→S4 Z-bar deep-link; the route in is the rail or chat.
- **Cohort context carries in.** Whatever cohort is active in the left rail (People like you / PE professionals / Family Offices / Top 10% / custom) is the cohort S4 compares against. Switching surfaces never resets the cohort; the `n` readout and any RelaxBanner persist.

**Navigation out.**
- Surface switching via rail is instant and stateful — returning to S4 restores the last ComparisonSelector position and any hovered/pinned format.
- "What should I do about this?" link (bottom-right of the caption row) opens Ask Alpha pre-seeded with a format-context prompt; it does not navigate away.
- PDF export (frame-level) captures the current S4 state — the five bars + ZChart + caption + visible badges — as a clean PowerPoint-style page.

**State persistence.** ComparisonSelector position, bar pin/hover, and the "view data" table toggle are **UI-only state** held per surface for the session. They are not written to the backend and do not survive a hard reload (which returns to the surface default: ComparisonSelector = "Peers").

---

### §3 — Layout (centre stage)

S4 is a **single full-screen detail surface, no scroll**, composed of two side-by-side regions inside one analytical card area: the **Format Bars** (left, ~58% width on desktop) and the **Format Divergence ZChart** (right, ~42%). A title sits above both; one CaptionBlock spans the full width below both; badges attach to the relevant numbers.

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Your investment format mix                            [vs Peers ▾ selector]│  ← h1 title + ComparisonSelector
│  How you access private markets — by format                                 │  ← h2 / muted subtitle
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┤
│  FORMAT BARS (separate vertical columns)       │  FORMAT DIVERGENCE (ZChart) │
│                                                │                              │
│   100% ┤                                       │   Primary      ████▶ +11pp ▲ │
│        │  ┌──┐                                  │   Secondary  ◀███   −14pp ▼ │
│    75% ┤  │  │                                  │   Direct/Co  ◀█     −3pp  ▼ │
│        │  │62│                                  │   Semi-liq      █▶  +2pp  ▲ │
│    50% ┤  │% │   ┌──┐                           │   FoF        ◀█      −1pp    │
│        │  │  │   │  │       ┌──┐                │           0 (peer centre)    │
│    25% ┤  │  │   │24│  ┌──┐ │  │   ┌──┐         │                              │
│        │  │  │   │% │  │ 0│ │ 9│   │ 5│         │  diverges from a centre 0    │
│     0% ┤  │  │   │  │  │%*│ │% │   │% │         │  line; --over / --under;     │
```
