```
│        └──┴──┴───┴──┴──┴──┴─┴──┴───┴──┴──       │  ▲ / ▼ glyph + sign          │
│          Prim  Sec  Dir/CI Semi  FoF            │                              │
│         (You bar solid · Peer bar ghost behind) │                              │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┤
│  Caption: "62% of your private-markets capital is in primary fund commitments │
│  — 11pp above the People-like-you median. You hold no secondaries, where      │
│  peers hold 14%." · [n=312 · ±2.1] · [show how this was computed]            │
│                                          [What should I do about this? →]     │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**The format bars — the load-bearing decision.**

- **Five separate vertical bars, one per format**, in fixed canonical order left→right: **Primary · Secondary · Direct / Co-Invest · Semi-liquid · Fund-of-Fund**. Order is fixed (never sorted by value) so the same format is always in the same horizontal position across sessions, snapshots, and the peer ghost overlay — positional memory is the whole point.
- **Each bar is independently scaled against the SAME shared vertical axis (0–100%).** A 62% Primary bar reaches ~62% of the plot height; a 9% Semi-liquid bar reaches ~9%. They are **NOT** segments of one 100% column. There is visible gutter space between the bars (4-pt grid, ~24px at desktop). The shared 0–100% y-axis with gridlines at 0 / 25 / 50 / 75 / 100% makes "how full is this format" legible per column.
- **The % is printed INSIDE each bar**, in tabular/mono, vertically centred near the top of the fill (or just above the fill for very short bars — see §4 small-bar rule). Never only in a side legend.
- **Peer comparison renders as a ghost bar** behind/beside each You bar (not a separate chart): a thin, lower-opacity `--muted`/`--brand-tint-3` column at the peer median height for that format, with the peer % as a small caption-size label at its cap. This lets the user read You-vs-Peer per format *and* read the overall shape, without a toggle. (ComparisonSelector swaps which cohort the ghost + ZChart represent.)
- **The bars sum to 100% across formats** (a "sums to 100%" micro-affordance under the axis confirms this for the analytical user — "bars total 100% of PM NAV"), but they are five discrete columns. This is the explicit guard against the 100%-stacked-bar failure mode.

**The ZChart (divergence) beside the bars.**

- The approved **Divergence / Z-chart** from the foundation chart language, rendered **large** (it is one of two co-equal regions, not a thumbnail). One horizontal diverging bar per format, same canonical top-to-bottom order (Primary at top). Bars diverge from a vertical centre **0 line** = the peer cohort weight for that format. `--over` to the right with `▲`, `--under` to the left with `▼`. Magnitude label in **percentage points (pp)**, signed (`+11pp` / `−14pp`).
- The ZChart answers "where am I most off-cohort" at a glance; the format bars answer "what is my actual mix." They are complementary, side by side, never behind a toggle (foundation rule: comparisons that matter are side-by-side).

**Responsive behaviour.**
- **≥1280px (desktop, 3-col frame):** side-by-side as drawn — bars ~58%, ZChart ~42%. Full-screen, no scroll.
- **768–1279px (cohort builder collapses to drawer, Ask Alpha to launcher):** the two regions stack vertically — format bars on top (full width, taller), ZChart below (full width). Still no horizontal scroll; the page may grow one viewport height with a single soft scroll only at this breakpoint, bars region prioritised above the fold.
- **<768px (mobile, advisor-on-the-go):** single column. Format bars stay **vertical** but the plot compresses; if five vertical bars become too narrow to legibly print the inside %, the surface switches the bars to **horizontal BarRow** orientation (one row per format, % inline at the bar end, peer marker as a dashed tick) — this is the documented mobile fallback, still five separate bars, still % inline, still NOT stacked. ZChart follows below. ProfileBar becomes a sheet (frame behaviour).

---

### §4 — Components & charts (exact encodings)

**FormatBars (new composition of the foundation `PerfBars` family — vertical bars, numbers inside).**
S4 reuses the vertical-bar primitive but in its "format" configuration (the foundation's PerfBars caption notes the vertical-bar primitive serves both performance and format). Encoding:

- **Geometry.** Five columns, fixed order, fixed slot positions. Shared y-axis 0–100% with hairline gridlines (`--line`) at 25% intervals. Bar width: thin, not fat (foundation rule) — ~44px at desktop, never balloon to fill the gutter. Card radius and 1-soft-elevation per tokens. No drop shadows on bars.
- **You bar fill.** Solid. Each format gets its **own fixed colour from the `--data-*` ramp**, pinned so a format is the same colour everywhere it appears in the product (Primary = `--data-1`, Secondary = `--data-2`, Direct/Co-Invest = `--data-3`, Semi-liquid = `--data-4`, Fund-of-Fund = `--data-5`; this format→colour map is registered alongside the strategy→colour map). The blue→teal→mint progression reads as a calm sequence, not a traffic-light.
- **Peer ghost bar.** Same slot, behind/offset, lower-opacity neutral (`--brand-tint-3` at ~35% or a hairline-outlined hollow column), peer % in caption-size at its cap. The ghost is visually subordinate so the You bar reads first.
- **Inside % label.** Tabular/mono, `body` size, high-contrast against the fill (`--bg`/white knockout on the saturated portion). Vertically positioned near the top of the fill. **Small-bar rule:** when a fill is too short to legibly contain its label (fill height < label height + 8px, i.e. roughly < 10–12%), the label moves to sit **just above the bar cap** in `--ink`, with a 2px leader gap — it is still "on" the bar, never relegated to a legend. A literal **0%** format renders a baseline-height stub (2px) with the "0%" label above it and a `*` linking to the headroom note.
- **No values bare.** Each format's % carries the ConfidenceBadge (n + CI) at the surface level (one shared badge in the caption row, since all five share the cohort `n`) and is individually clickable → ProvenancePopover (per-format provenance object). The peer ghost % shares the same cohort provenance.
- **Axis + sums affordance.** Y-axis labelled in %. A muted micro-line under the x-axis: "Bars total 100% of your private-markets NAV" — present for the analytical reader, suppressed at mobile to save space.

**FormatZChart (the foundation `ZChart` component, format-configured).**
- Large divergence chart, one horizontal bar per format, canonical order top→bottom, diverging from a centre 0 line = peer weight. `--over` + `▲` right, `--under` + `▼` left. Magnitude in signed pp, tabular/mono, at the bar end.
- **Not-significant divergences render greyed** (foundation rule: sub-CI deltas grey + flagged "not significant"). A divergence whose CI crosses zero is drawn in `--muted` with a small "ns" tag and no bold sign colour — the user must not over-read noise.
- **Click/hover a Z-bar** highlights the corresponding format column in the FormatBars (cross-highlight) and surfaces that format's pair of numbers in a small inline readout. Unlike S1/S2, the format ZChart does **not** drive a TwinDonut (there is no donut on S4); its interaction target is the adjacent FormatBars cross-highlight (§6).
- Centre-line label: the peer cohort name (e.g. "Peers (median) = 0"), so the 0 line is explicitly the cohort, never "All."

**ComparisonSelector (foundation segmented control).**
- Options on S4: `Peers · Platform · Model · Best (Top 10%)` (a sensible subset of the full selector; endowment options Harvard/Yale/Princeton are not exposed on Format — format granularity is not meaningful for endowment comparators, so they are omitted here rather than gated). Default = **Peers**.
- Drives **both** the peer ghost bars (FormatBars) and the centre line + divergences (ZChart) simultaneously. Switching re-resolves and re-renders only these two charts, not the frame.

**CaptionBlock (foundation, 1–2 sentences).**
- Always present, full width under both regions. Auto-composed from the binding (template in §8). Leads with the dominant-format % and its peer delta, then names the single largest divergence (over or under). Plain language, consequence-aware, never advice.

**Color tokens used (reference only, defined in foundation §2.2):**
`--data-1…--data-5` for the five format fills (pinned map above) · `--brand-tint-3` / `--muted` for peer ghost · `--over` + `▲` and `--under` + `▼` for ZChart divergence · `--ink` / `--muted` for labels and captions · `--line` for gridlines · `--synthetic` (amber) for any imputed input badge · `--bg`/white for inside-label knockout. No raw hex anywhere; dark-mode parallels inherited.

---

### §5 — Data bindings (element ← endpoint.field)

**Single source call:** `GET /api/detail/format` (with optional `?investor_id=` and `?comparison=peers|platform|model|best`, and the active cohort applied server-side from the last `POST /api/cohort`). The whole surface binds to one response; ComparisonSelector changes re-issue this call (or read a pre-resolved comparison block in the same payload — FE may request all comparisons once and switch client-side; either satisfies "re-render only affected charts").

**Response shape (Part 0.3 contract — every value is `{value, provenance{method,n,ci95,coverage_pct,significant,synthetic,vintage_scope,snapshot_id}}`):**

```
GET /api/detail/format  →
{
  "you":  {                         // the investor's own format split (sums to 100%)
     "primary":        { value: 62, provenance{…} },
     "secondary":      { value: 0,  provenance{…} },
     "direct_coinvest":{ value: 24, provenance{…} },
     "semi_liquid":    { value: 9,  provenance{…} },
     "fund_of_fund":   { value: 5,  provenance{…} }
  },
  "peer": {                         // cohort comparator (median), sums to 100%
     "primary":        { value: 51, provenance{ method:"median", n:312, ci95:[49,53], coverage_pct:100, significant:true, synthetic:false, vintage_scope:"all", snapshot_id:"…" } },
     "secondary":      { value: 14, provenance{…} },
     "direct_coinvest":{ value: 21, provenance{…} },
     "semi_liquid":    { value: 8,  provenance{…} },
     "fund_of_fund":   { value: 6,  provenance{…} }
  },
  "z_chart": [                      // divergence You−Peer per format, in pp
     { format:"primary",         delta_pp:+11, significant:true,  provenance{…} },
     { format:"secondary",       delta_pp:-14, significant:true,  provenance{…} },
     { format:"direct_coinvest", delta_pp:+3,  significant:false, provenance{…} },
     { format:"semi_liquid",     delta_pp:+1,  significant:false, provenance{…} },
     { format:"fund_of_fund",    delta_pp:-1,  significant:false, provenance{…} }
  ],
  "cohort": { n:312, strict_n:188, relaxed_dims:["age"], gated:false, label:"People like you" }
}
```

**Element → field map.**

| UI element | Binds to |
|---|---|
| FormatBars · You column (per format) | `you.{format}.value` → bar fill height + inside % label |
| FormatBars · inside % label | `you.{format}.value` (tabular/mono) |
| FormatBars · per-bar provenance chip | `you.{format}.provenance` → ProvenancePopover |
| FormatBars · peer ghost column + cap label | `peer.{format}.value` (height) + `peer.{format}.provenance` |
| FormatBars · SyntheticTag on a bar | `you.{format}.provenance.synthetic === true` |
| FormatZChart · bar direction/length/sign | `z_chart[].delta_pp` (sign → `--over`/`▲` or `--under`/`▼`) |
| FormatZChart · greyed "ns" bars | `z_chart[].significant === false` |
| FormatZChart · centre-line label | `cohort.label` ("Peers (median) = 0") |
| ComparisonSelector | re-issues call with `?comparison=` (or switches client-cached block) |
| ConfidenceBadge (caption row, shared) | `peer.*.provenance.n` + `ci95` → "n=312 · ±2.1" |
| CaptionBlock text | composed from `you`, `peer`, `z_chart`, `cohort.label` (§8) |
| RelaxBanner | `cohort.relaxed_dims` (in words) |
| GatedState | `cohort.gated === true` |

**Provenance binding rule (foundation §0.2):** the FE binds **`.value`** to the visual and **`.provenance`** to the badge/popover for every one of the ten figures (5 you + 5 peer) plus the five z deltas. No number renders without its provenance object available to a chip. If a `value` arrives without a `provenance`, the FE treats the figure as degraded (§7 error state) rather than printing it bare.

---

### §6 — Interactions

1. **ComparisonSelector (`Peers · Platform · Model · Best`).** Default Peers. On change: re-resolve the comparison (refetch or client-switch), then re-render **only** the peer ghost bars and the ZChart with a single subtle value-in animation (≤150ms, no bounce). The You bars do not move (they don't depend on the comparator) — only the ghosts and divergences animate, which visually reinforces "you are fixed, the comparison swaps." The active option is the highlighted segment; keyboard-focusable, arrow-key cycles.

2. **Hover / focus a format bar.** Raises the bar slightly (1-soft-elevation, no scale jump), reveals a compact tooltip: format name, `You X% · {cohort} Y% · Δ ±Zpp`, and the format's one-line definition (e.g. "Secondary — buying existing LP stakes on the secondary market"). The corresponding ZChart bar cross-highlights. Tooltip is `caption` size, tabular figures.

3. **Hover / focus / click a ZChart divergence bar.** Cross-highlights the matching FormatBars column (and vice-versa). Click pins the highlight (toggle) so the user can read a specific format's pair without holding hover — useful on touch and for the projector. A "ns" (not-significant) bar shows a tooltip "Difference not statistically distinguishable from your peers (CI crosses 0)" and is not emphasised.

4. **Provenance (click any % / chip).** Opens the **ProvenancePopover** anchored to that figure: cohort label ("People like you"), `n` (and `strict_n` if relaxed), `±CI`, `method` (median/avg), the fields used, `vintage_scope`, and `snapshot_id` — plus a "show the data" affordance (the foundation "view data" table). The peer ghost % and the ZChart delta each open their own provenance; they share the cohort but state their own method (median vs delta-of-medians).

5. **"View data" table (a11y + analytical).** A frame-level "view data" affordance renders the five formats as a small table: Format · You % · {cohort} % · Δpp · significant. This is the keyboard/screen-reader-accessible equivalent of the two charts (foundation a11y rule: chart data available as a table).

6. **"What should I do about this?"** (caption row, right). Opens Ask Alpha pre-seeded with "Given my format mix, what should I consider?" — S4 itself stays put; no inline advice. This is the only outbound action and it is explicitly a hand-off, keeping S4 descriptive.

7. **Cohort change (left rail).** Re-resolves the cohort server-side; S4 re-renders **only** its two charts + caption + badges (not the frame, not other surfaces). A changed cohort visibly moves the peer ghosts and the divergences; the `n` readout and any RelaxBanner update. (Foundation: chip change re-resolves and re-renders only affected centre-stage charts.)

8. **PDF export.** Frame-level button captures S4's current state (selector position, pinned highlight, visible badges) as a clean static page.

---

### §7 — The seven states (every one rendered)

S4 renders all seven foundation states; here is exactly how each looks on this surface.

1. **Happy.** Full `you` + `peer` + `z_chart`, significant deltas. Five You bars with inside %, peer ghosts, large ZChart with `▲`/`▼` signed deltas, composed caption, shared ConfidenceBadge, per-figure provenance. The reference rendering above.

2. **Cold-start (no / negligible holdings).** A brand-new investor has no format split yet. **Do not render an empty or broken bar chart.** Instead: render the **peer ghost bars at full opacity as the foreground** (so the user sees "this is what people like you do, by format") with a quiet overlay line "Your format mix will appear here once you commit," and route the primary CTA to the **Allocation Architect (S9)** ("See your target architecture →"). The ZChart shows the peer shape against a flat 0 with a "no comparison yet" note. FOMO-safe framing: opportunity, never failure.

3. **Thin cohort.** When `cohort.relaxed_dims` is non-empty, show the **RelaxBanner** in words above the charts: e.g. "Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band (age relaxed) to reach a reliable peer group of 312." The peer ghosts and ZChart use the relaxed cohort; `n` reflects it; provenance shows `strict_n` vs `n`.

4. **Low coverage.** Format split is computed from committed capital and is normally fully covered, but if the engine reports `coverage_pct < 100` on any `you.{format}` (e.g. some commitments unclassified by format), attach a **CoverageBadge** "based on 87% of your NAV — 13% unclassified by format" to the affected figure(s) and add a caption clause. Never imply 100% when it isn't. (This is rarer here than on S6, but the badge mechanism is wired so it renders correctly when it occurs.)

5. **Null / insufficient.** A format the investor genuinely has 0% of (e.g. Secondary) is **not** a null — it renders as a labelled 0% stub with the headroom note. *Null* on S4 means a comparator value can't be computed (e.g. peer cohort too thin to give a median for one format): that format's peer ghost and its ZChart bar render as **"not enough peer history"** in `--muted` with no fabricated number — never zero-fill. The You bar still renders.

6. **Gated.** When `cohort.gated === true` (cohort relaxed past the privacy floor), replace the **peer ghosts + ZChart** region with the **GatedState** card — exact copy: "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate" with the headline "5,500 total investors." The investor's own five You bars still render (their own data is never gated); only the comparison region locks. No "too small," no "I can't."

7. **Error / degraded.** Engine/model down or a figure arrives without provenance: render **cached last-good** S4 with a quiet top banner "Showing your last saved snapshot — live comparison is temporarily unavailable," timestamped. If no cache, render the You bars from `portfolio/you` if available and show the comparison region as a calm empty state — never a blank screen, never a fabricated peer number. Ask Alpha falls back gracefully per its own spec.

**Cross-cutting:** not-significant ZChart deltas always render greyed + "ns" (state-independent rule). SyntheticTag (amber) on any `you.{format}` or comparator with `provenance.synthetic === true` — e.g. if a format split is partially imputed for a sparse profile, the affected bar carries the tag and is never styled as verified fact. Loading = calm skeleton bars (five grey columns rising once), not spinners.

---

### §8 — Copy deck

All strings are i18n keys (EN + DE; numbers/€/pp localized — German uses comma decimal and "Pp." where appropriate). No hard-coded strings.

**Titles & chrome.**
- Surface title (h1): **"Your investment format mix"** / DE: "Ihr Anlageformat-Mix"
- Subtitle (muted): **"How you access private markets — by format"** / "Wie Sie auf Private Markets zugreifen — nach Format"
- ComparisonSelector label (sr-only): "Compare against"
- Sums affordance (muted micro): **"Bars total 100% of your private-markets NAV"**
- ZChart centre-line label: **"{cohort} (median) = 0"** → e.g. "Peers (median) = 0"

**Format display names (canonical, fixed order).**
1. **Primary** (DE: "Primär") — def: "New commitments to a fund at its first close."
2. **Secondary** (DE: "Sekundär") — def: "Buying existing LP stakes on the secondary market."
3. **Direct / Co-Invest** (DE: "Direkt / Co-Investment") — def: "Investing directly into a company alongside a fund."
4. **Semi-liquid** (DE: "Semi-liquide") — def: "Evergreen / open-ended structures with periodic liquidity."
5. **Fund-of-Fund** (DE: "Dachfonds") — def: "A fund that invests across multiple underlying funds."

**Caption templates (auto-composed; lead with dominant format, then largest divergence).**
- Dominant + delta: **"{you.dominant_pct}% of your private-markets capital is in {dominant_format} commitments — {abs_delta}pp {above|below} the {cohort} median."**
- Zero-format clause (appended when a notable format is 0%): **" You hold no {zero_format}, where peers hold {peer_pct}%."**
- Balanced case (no significant divergence): **"Your format mix tracks the {cohort} median closely — no format is significantly over- or under-weight."**
- Example happy: *"62% of your private-markets capital is in primary fund commitments — 11pp above the People-like-you median. You hold no secondaries, where peers hold 14%."*

**Per-format tooltip:** "{Format} — {definition} · You {you}% · {cohort} {peer}% · Δ {sign}{abs}pp"

**State copy.**
- Cold-start overlay: **"Your format mix will appear here once you commit. This is how investors like you are positioned by format."** CTA: **"See your target architecture →"** (routes S9).
- Cold-start ZChart note: "No comparison yet — start with the Architect."
- Thin (RelaxBanner): **"Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band ({relaxed_dims} relaxed) to reach a reliable peer group of {n}."**
- Low coverage: **"Based on {coverage}% of your NAV — {remainder}% not yet classified by format."**
- Null comparator: **"Not enough peer history to compare {format}."**
- Gated (exact): **"Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate."** + headline **"5,500 total investors."**
- Error banner: **"Showing your last saved snapshot — live comparison is temporarily unavailable."** + "{relative time}".
- ns tooltip: **"Difference not statistically distinguishable from your peers (CI crosses 0)."**
- Headroom note (0% format): **"You have full headroom here — peers allocate {peer_pct}% to {format}."** (Never "you are missing"/"you failed.")

**Action / provenance.**
- Caption-row link: **"What should I do about this? →"** (opens Ask Alpha)
- Provenance trigger (sr / tooltip): **"Show how this was computed"**
- "View data" affordance: **"View data as table"**

**Tone guardrails.** Descriptive, calm, info-not-advice. Never "you should," never "you're behind." A skew is "headroom" or "concentration," framed as information. Numbers always relative (% / pp), never an absolute € total as a headline.

---

### §9 — Badge rendering

Badges are structural (foundation §0.2 / §2.6) — they are part of the numeric component, never optional decoration. S4 attaches them as follows.

- **ConfidenceBadge** (`n=312 · ±2.1`). One **shared** badge in the caption row representing the peer cohort `n` + CI (all five peer figures share the cohort). When the active ComparisonSelector is "You-only" contexts it is hidden; for any peer/platform/model/best comparison it is present. Greys the affected ZChart deltas when `significant === false` (the badge does not grey the whole surface — only the individual ns divergences grey, per the chart rule).
- **ProvenancePopover** — attached to **every** figure individually: each of the five You %, each of the five peer ghost %, and each ZChart delta. Click/Enter opens it. Content: cohort label, `n` (+`strict_n` if relaxed), `±CI`, `method`, fields, `vintage_scope`, `snapshot_id`, "show the data" → table. This is the "a number never appears bare" guarantee.
- **CoverageBadge** (`based on 87% of NAV`). Renders on any `you.{format}` whose `provenance.coverage_pct < 100`, attached to that bar's % and echoed in the caption. Unmissable styling per foundation (it is the headline on S6; here it is conditional but uses the same unmissable chip). Never shown when coverage is 100%.
- **SyntheticTag** (amber "illustrative" pill). On any bar (You or comparator) whose `provenance.synthetic === true` — e.g. an imputed format split for a sparse profile. The bar is then visibly tagged and never styled as verified fact. Also appears if a ProfileBar input feeding the cohort (target %, risk class) is itself imputed and materially shapes the comparison — surfaced via the cohort badge, not per-bar.
- **RelaxBanner** — words, above the charts, from `cohort.relaxed_dims` (not a badge but the thin-cohort signal; §7.3).
- **GatedState** / **NotConnectedState** — S4 uses GatedState (§7.6); it does **not** use NotConnectedState (no external signal feed underlies format — it's portfolio-derived). NotConnectedState is therefore N/A for S4 and explicitly not rendered.

**Badge precedence (when multiple apply to one figure):** SyntheticTag (most important — "don't trust as fact") > CoverageBadge > ConfidenceBadge significance grey. They stack visually in that priority; the SyntheticTag is never suppressed by another badge.

---

### §10 — Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive (breakpoints inherited from foundation §3 / Part 10).**
- **≥1280px:** bars (~58%) + ZChart (~42%) side by side, full-screen, no scroll. Bar width ~44px, gutters ~24px, inside labels comfortable.
- **768–1279px:** regions stack — FormatBars full-width on top, ZChart full-width below; cohort builder is a drawer, Ask Alpha a launcher. Bars widen; at most one soft page scroll, bars above the fold. No horizontal scroll.
- **<768px:** single column. FormatBars switch to **horizontal BarRow orientation** if vertical columns can't legibly hold the inside % (documented fallback): five rows, format name + % at the bar end, peer median as a dashed tick, still five separate bars, still NOT stacked. ZChart below. ProfileBar → sheet. No analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll (hard rule).

**Accessibility (WCAG-AA, foundation Part 10).**
- **Never colour-only.** ZChart over/under always pairs colour with `▲`/`▼` and a signed `±pp` value. Format bar identity is reinforced by the fixed left→right order and text labels, not colour alone (the `--data-*` ramp is colour-blind-safe regardless).
- **Tabular figures** for every %/pp so columns and the ZChart align; mono numerals.
- **Keyboard nav.** Tab order: ComparisonSelector → each FormatBar (left→right) → each ZChart bar (top→bottom) → "view data" → "What should I do about this?". Enter/Space opens provenance or pins a highlight; arrow keys cycle the selector. Visible focus ring on every interactive element.
- **Screen reader.** Each bar exposes an ARIA label: "Primary, 62 percent, 11 percentage points above peers, statistically significant." The ZChart bars likewise. The "View data as table" affordance gives the full five-row table as the canonical accessible equivalent of both charts. Caption is read in document order after the title.
- **Contrast.** Inside-label knockout text meets AA against the saturated `--data-*` fill; small-bar labels (above the cap in `--ink`) meet AA against `--bg`. Peer ghost is subordinate but its cap label still meets AA.
- **Motion.** Single value-in animation ≤150ms ease on render and on comparison swap; respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (then bars/deltas appear without animation). No parallax, bounce, or confetti.
- **i18n.** EN + DE strings via keys; pp/€ and decimal separators localized; German format names per §8; layout tolerates ~30% longer German labels (Dachfonds, Co-Investment) — bar slot labels wrap to two lines rather than truncate.

---

### §11 — Acceptance checklist

A build of S4 is correct only if **all** of the following hold:

1. **Separate vertical bars per format — NOT a single 100%-stacked bar.** Five discrete columns (Primary · Secondary · Direct/Co-Invest · Semi-liquid · Fund-of-Fund), each scaled against a shared 0–100% y-axis, with visible gutters between them. The 100%-stacked horizontal bar (the S2 strategy chart) must NOT appear on S4. **This is the primary acceptance gate.**
2. **% printed INSIDE each bar** (tabular/mono), with the documented small-bar rule (label moves just above the cap when the fill is too short); never only in a side legend.
3. **Bars sum to 100% across formats**, with the "Bars total 100% of your private-markets NAV" affordance present at ≥768px — yet rendered as separate columns, not segments.
4. **A 0% format renders** as a labelled stub with the headroom note (FOMO-safe framing), not as a missing column.
5. **Peer comparison is side-by-side, not behind a toggle** — peer ghost bars in the FormatBars + the ZChart divergences, both updating from ComparisonSelector.
6. **ZChart renders large**, diverging from a centre 0 line labelled with the cohort, `--over`/`▲` and `--under`/`▼`, signed pp, with not-significant deltas greyed + "ns".
7. **Single source binding:** the whole surface binds to `GET /api/detail/format` → `you`, `peer`, `z_chart`, `cohort`; `.value` → visual, `.provenance` → badge/popover for all ten figures + five deltas.
8. **Every number has a badge + popover** — shared ConfidenceBadge in the caption row, per-figure ProvenancePopover, conditional CoverageBadge, SyntheticTag on imputed bars; no number renders bare.
9. **Cohort label is "Peers" (never "All")**; the ZChart centre line names the cohort; RelaxBanner shows relaxation in words.
10. **ComparisonSelector swaps only the comparison** (peer ghosts + ZChart) with a single subtle animation; You bars stay fixed; cohort change re-renders only S4's charts, not the frame.
11. **All seven states render:** happy, cold-start (route to Architect, peer-shape foreground), thin (RelaxBanner), low-coverage (CoverageBadge), null comparator ("not enough peer history," never zero-fill), gated (exact paid-viewers copy + "5,500 total investors," You bars still render), error (cached last-good + quiet banner, never blank, never fabricated). NotConnectedState is correctly N/A.
12. **One CaptionBlock**, 1–2 sentences, auto-composed, leading with dominant format + delta then largest divergence; info-not-advice tone; relative figures only.
13. **Responsive:** side-by-side ≥1280; stacked 768–1279; single-column <768 with the horizontal-BarRow fallback — no analytical chart ever scrolls horizontally; bars stay non-stacked at every breakpoint.
14. **A11y:** colour never alone (glyph + sign), tabular figures, full keyboard nav with visible focus, ARIA labels per bar/delta, "View data as table" equivalent, AA contrast, reduced-motion respected, EN/DE i18n with German labels wrapping not truncating.
15. **No scroll on the analytical view** at desktop; instant surface switch; calm skeleton loads; no flashy transitions; PDF export captures the current S4 state.


---

## Surface S5 — Vintage

> Deep frontend surface spec. Composes from the FRONTEND FOUNDATION (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`) — design system (Part 2), chart language including the **Vintage × Strategy heat-map** (Part 2.5), the seven-state model (Part 9), and the S5 mid-spec + `/api/detail/vintage` binding (Part 4). This document deepens S5 to zero open decisions; it does **not** redefine tokens, components, or the global frame, which are inherited as-is.

---

### §1 — Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** S5 answers a single, narrow, deceptively important question that the headline allocation surfaces (S1, S2) cannot: **how is the investor's private-markets capital spread across vintage years, and is it dangerously bunched into one or two of them?** Vintage diversification is the temporal dimension of diversification — the discipline of not committing your whole programme to a single market environment. A portfolio that is 16% of wealth in private markets, perfectly weighted across strategy and region, can still be fragile if 80% of that capital went in during a single vintage. S5 makes that temporal concentration visible, relative, and actionable.

The surface is built around the principle from the foundation (Part 0.2): **relative, not absolute.** S5 leads with **% committed per vintage year** — never the absolute € total committed per year. The investor sees *shape* ("you are bunched in 2021–2022"), not *size* ("you committed €4.1m in 2021"). The absolute € is available only behind a provenance popover, never as a headline. This is the load-bearing design decision of the surface and the first acceptance criterion.

**The user questions S5 must answer at a glance:**
- *"Across how many vintage years am I committed?"* → the **# vintages readout** (`n_vintages`).
- *"Is my capital spread evenly across years, or piled into one or two?"* → the **per-vintage % bars** (the relative shape) plus the **bunching-flag chip**.
- *"Within a heavy vintage, what strategy did I actually commit to?"* → the **Vintage × Strategy heat-map** companion, and the per-vintage hover breakdown.
- *"Is my bunching a real risk, or just how everyone's portfolio looks?"* → the bunching flag is sourced from `/api/pacing`'s `bunching_flag`, which is computed against pacing discipline, and the heat-map can overlay a greyed peer comparison.
- *"Am I under-deployed — too few vintages to be diversified?"* → the thin / cold-start states, which frame low `n_vintages` as *headroom to pace into more years*, never as failure.

**What S5 is NOT.** It is not a performance surface — vintage *returns* live on S7 (Performance), which is vintage-honest with "too early to rank." S5 shows *exposure by vintage*, not *return by vintage*. It is not a forward plan — the forward year-by-year commitment schedule lives on S10 (Pacing). S5 is the **current-state diagnosis** that S10 prescribes against. The bunching flag is the explicit bridge between the two: S5 *detects* bunching from the same `/api/pacing` signal that S10 *plans to resolve*, so the two surfaces tell one continuous story.

---

### §2 — Entry & navigation

**Rail entry.** S5 is reached from the left navigation rail under the **"Vintage"** item, in the foundation's rail order (*Overview · Allocation · Region · Format · **Vintage** · Look-through · Performance · KPIs · …*). Switching is instant, full-screen, **no scroll** — S5 is a Detail page on the Detail template (Part 3), the persistent frame (ProfileBar, cohort builder, Ask Alpha) stays in place; only the centre stage swaps.

**Deep-link entries (inbound).**
- From **S8 (KPIs)**: the `# vintages` Scorecard links to S5 ("see the spread"). This is the primary lateral entry, because S8 shows `n_vintages` as a bare count and S5 is where that count becomes a distribution.
- From **S10 (Pacing)**: when S10 surfaces a bunching flag on the *current* portfolio (as opposed to the forward plan), a "see your vintage concentration" affordance deep-links to S5. The two surfaces share the `bunching_flag` from `/api/pacing`.
- From **Ask Alpha**: questions like *"Am I too concentrated in one vintage?"* or *"How many years am I spread across?"* route Alpha to render an inline mini-version of the per-vintage bars, with a "see full vintage view" chip that opens S5.

**Deep-link entries (outbound).**
- Clicking a vintage bar or a heat-map cell that is heavily weighted into one strategy can offer "spread future commitments" → opens **S10 (Pacing)** with that gap context.
- The bunching chip, when active, exposes a "plan to diversify" action → **S10**.

**Cohort coupling.** S5 reads the active cohort (left rail) for its peer overlay only. The investor's own `vintage_pct` and `n_vintages` are not cohort-dependent — they are facts about the investor's portfolio. The cohort affects only the optional greyed peer comparison in the heat-map and the contextualizing caption ("your peers spread across a median of 6 vintages"). A cohort change therefore re-renders the peer overlay and caption, **not** the investor's own bars (foundation Part 6: re-render only affected charts).

---

### §3 — Layout

S5 is a **two-panel Detail page**: a dominant left panel carrying the per-vintage % bars (the primary relative shape), and a companion right panel carrying the Vintage × Strategy heat-map. A slim header strip above both carries the surface title, the **# vintages readout**, and the **bunching-flag chip**. No scroll at desktop; the two panels share the centre stage at roughly a 5:4 width ratio.

**Desktop (≥1280px):**

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Vintage                                          [PDF export ▾]            │
│ Across 8 vintage years.   ● Bunched: 64% in 2021–22   [chip]               │  ← header strip
├──────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  COMMITTED BY VINTAGE (relative)  │  VINTAGE × STRATEGY                      │
│                                   │                                          │
│  2018 ▏ 4%                        │        Buyout Growth VC  Infra ... Credit│
│  2019 ▎ 7%                        │  2018   ░     ░     ·     ▒        ·      │
│  2020 ▍ 11%                       │  2019   ▒     ░     ░     ░        ·      │
│  2021 ████████ 38%   ◀ heavy      │  2020   ▒     ▒     ░     ·        ░      │
│  2022 █████ 26%      ◀ heavy      │  2021   ███   ██    ▒     ░        ▒      │
│  2023 ▍ 9%                        │  2022   ██    █     ░     ▒        ░      │
│  2024 ▎ 5%                        │  2023   ▒     ░     ·     ·        ·      │
│  2025 — (no commitments)          │  2024   ░     ·     ·     ·        ·      │
│                                   │                                          │
│  ⌀ peer spread: 6 vintages (grey) │  [color scale: 0% ──────── 12%+ ]       │
│  Caption: You concentrated 64% of │  [ Peer overlay ▢ ]  [ View as table ]   │
│  your commitments in 2021–22 …    │  Caption: Darkest cells = 2021 buyout …  │
│  [n=312 · conf badge] [provenance]│  [coverage if <100%] [provenance]        │
└──────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Header strip.** Title "Vintage" (h1). Sub-line carries two things side by side: the **# vintages readout** as plain words ("Across 8 vintage years.") and the **bunching-flag chip** when active. PDF export top-right per foundation Part 3.

**Left panel — Committed by vintage.** A vertical stack of horizontal **BarRow**s, one row per vintage year, ordered ascending by year (oldest top, newest bottom — chronological reading order). Each bar's length encodes **% of total committed capital** (relative), with the **% inline** at the end of the bar (foundation Part 2.5: % inline on bars, never legend-only). Years with zero commitments render as an explicit empty row ("— no commitments") so the *gaps* in the programme are visible, not silently collapsed. Heavy vintages (those flagged in bunching) carry a small "◀ heavy" marker and a subtly emphasized fill. A greyed **peer-spread reference** ("⌀ peer spread: 6 vintages") sits beneath. CaptionBlock + ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover anchor the panel bottom.

**Right panel — Vintage × Strategy heat-map.** The foundation's approved **HeatMap** component (Part 2.5): rows = vintage years (aligned to the left panel's year order), columns = the 9 strategies (fixed strategy→colour identity is *not* used here — heat-map uses a single sequential intensity ramp; see §4), cell intensity = % of total committed capital in that (vintage, strategy) pair. The darkest cells reveal *where* the bunching concentrates — e.g. "2021 buyout" being the single darkest cell tells the investor their concentration is not just temporal but doubly concentrated in one strategy-year. A discrete color-scale legend sits below the grid. A **Peer overlay** toggle and a **View as table** affordance (a11y, Part 10) sit beneath. CaptionBlock + CoverageBadge (if look-through <100%) + ProvenancePopover anchor the panel bottom.

**Responsive.**
- **1280px+** — two panels side by side as drawn, 5:4 ratio.
- **768–1279px** — the cohort builder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher (foundation Part 3). The two S5 panels **stack vertically**: bars on top (full width), heat-map below (full width). The heat-map remains a full grid — no horizontal scroll; cell width compresses but stays ≥28px (tap-legible). The header strip wraps the bunching chip onto its own line if needed.
- **<768px (mobile, advisor on the go)** — single column. Order: header strip → # vintages readout as a prominent stat → bunching chip → per-vintage bars (full width, the primary artefact) → heat-map. On the narrowest viewports the heat-map columns reduce to the **strategies with non-zero exposure only** (others collapsed behind a "+3 more" expander) so the grid never forces horizontal scroll (foundation Part 10: no analytical chart needs horizontal scroll). ProfileBar becomes a sheet.

---

### §4 — Components & charts

All components are inherited from the foundation Part 2.6 library and follow the Part 2.5 chart language. S5 introduces no new component types; it configures four.

**4.1 Per-vintage % bars (BarRow stack).**
- Encoding: one horizontal BarRow per vintage year; bar length = `vintage_pct[year].value` (a fraction of total committed capital, summing to 100% across years). **Relative only** — the axis is 0–100% of the programme, never €.
- **% inline** at the bar end in mono/tabular figures (foundation Part 2.3). Year label left, in mono.
- Bars are **thin, not fat** (Part 2.5 rule). Fill colour: `--brand-tint-2` for normal vintages; heavy/bunched vintages use `--brand` (full saturation) plus the "◀ heavy" glyph so concentration is conveyed by **shape + glyph, not colour alone** (Part 2.2 accessibility rule).
- Zero-commitment years render as a hairline track with "— no commitments" in `--muted`, preserving the temporal gap visually.
- Peer reference: a greyed inline note "⌀ peer spread: {peer_median_vintages} vintages" beneath the stack (from cohort). Not a per-bar overlay — vintage *years* don't align across investors, so a per-bar peer comparison would mislead; the honest peer comparison is on *spread* (count), shown as a single reference figure.
- One **CaptionBlock** (1–2 sentences) beneath, e.g. *"You concentrated 64% of your commitments in 2021–22; your peers spread across a median of 6 vintage years."*
- **ConfidenceBadge** (`n=… · ±…`) and **ProvenancePopover** embedded — the absolute € per vintage lives **only** inside this popover.

**4.2 Vintage × Strategy heat-map (HeatMap).**
- Grid: rows = vintage years (same order as the bars), columns = the 9 strategies in the fixed strategy order used product-wide.
- Cell value = % of total committed capital in that (vintage, strategy) cell. The grid therefore sums to 100% across all cells (it is the same total as the bars, decomposed by strategy).
- **Colour scale.** A single **sequential intensity ramp** built from the data ramp tokens — `--data-1` (lightest, near-zero) → `--data-9` (darkest, the maximum cell). This is *intensity*, not strategy identity: the heat-map's job is to surface concentration, so a darker cell always means "more capital here," regardless of which strategy column it sits in. The scale is **anchored, not relative-per-render**: 0% maps to the lightest token, and the top of the scale is pinned at `max(12%, observed_max)` so two investors' heat-maps are visually comparable and a single heavy cell can't make a moderate one look extreme. Empty cells (0%) render as `--bg` with a faint hairline so the grid structure stays legible. A discrete **legend** ("0% ──── 12%+") sits below the grid with 4–5 stops, each labelled with its % band.
- Each cell carries its **% inline** when the cell is wide enough (desktop); on compressed widths the % moves into the hover/tooltip and the cell shows colour only. The single darkest cell is given a thin `--ink` outline ("the peak") so the eye lands on the concentration immediately.
- **Peer overlay toggle** (off by default): when on, a greyed companion read renders the cohort's median (vintage-relative) intensity as a small inset or a "ghost" second swatch per cell, clearly de-saturated and labelled "Peers" (never "All", Part 0.2). Because vintage years are absolute calendar years, the peer overlay aligns on *relative* vintage position where the backend provides it; if it cannot align honestly it stays disabled with a tooltip ("peer vintage alignment unavailable").
- **CaptionBlock**, **CoverageBadge** (only if the underlying commitment→strategy mapping covers <100% of NAV; S5 commitments are typically fully attributed, so this is usually absent but must render when coverage is partial), and **ProvenancePopover** embedded.

**4.3 Bunching-flag chip (state chip).**
- A compact pill in the header strip. Two visual states:
  - **Flagged (concentrated):** filled pill using `--over`/warning treatment paired with a `●` glyph (colour + glyph, never colour alone). Text: "Bunched: {pct}% in {year-range}." Clicking opens a short popover explaining the rule and offering "plan to diversify" → S10.
  - **Clear (well-spread):** a quiet outline pill with a `✓` glyph, text "Well-spread across {n} vintages." Low-emphasis; it confirms health without alarming.
- The chip is **never** a raw boolean with no explanation — clicking always reveals *why* (the threshold, the contributing years) via the popover, with a rules-version note where the backend supplies one.
- The chip sources its truth from `/api/pacing` → `bunching_flag` (see §5), **not** from the FE re-deriving concentration from `vintage_pct`. The FE may compute a *display* concentration figure for the caption, but the flag's on/off authority is the engine's.

**4.4 # vintages readout.**
- Rendered as plain words in the header sub-line: "Across {n_vintages} vintage years." Bound to `n_vintages.value`.
- It also appears (on mobile) as a prominent single Scorecard-style stat. It carries its own ConfidenceBadge inline (since `n_vintages` is a count from the portfolio, its provenance is the snapshot, not a cohort sample — the badge reflects that, showing `snapshot {id}` rather than `n=… · ±…`).
- The readout and the number of non-empty bar rows must always agree; if they disagree the FE renders the engine's `n_vintages` and logs a console warning (the engine is source of truth, Part 0.2).

---

### §5 — Data bindings

Element ← endpoint.field. All numeric values arrive in the foundation's `{value, provenance{method,n,ci95,coverage_pct,significant,synthetic,vintage_scope,snapshot_id}}` envelope (Part 0.3); the **value** binds to the visual and the **provenance** binds to the badge/popover. `investor_id` is an optional query param.

**Primary endpoint — `GET /api/detail/vintage?investor_id=`:**
- `vintage_pct` → an array of `{year, value, provenance}` per vintage year → drives the **per-vintage % bars** (one BarRow per element; `value` = bar length and the inline %). Zero/absent years are rendered explicitly as empty rows.
- `vintage_strategy` (the cell matrix; foundation lists `vintage_pct` + `n_vintages` for S5 — the heat-map cells are the strategy-decomposed companion to `vintage_pct` and arrive as `vintage_strategy[{year, strategy, value, provenance}]` or an equivalent matrix on the same call) → drives the **Vintage × Strategy heat-map** cells. If the call returns only `vintage_pct` without the strategy decomposition, the heat-map renders its **thin state** (see §7) rather than fabricating a split.
- `n_vintages` → `{value, provenance}` → drives the **# vintages readout**.
- `cohort{n, relaxed_dims}` (echoed on the call) → drives the ConfidenceBadge `n` and the RelaxBanner wording on the peer reference.
- `peer_vintage_spread` (peer median vintage count, cohort-resolved) → the greyed "⌀ peer spread: N vintages" reference and the caption comparator. Absent → suppress the peer reference, keep the investor's own data.

**Bunching — `GET /api/pacing?investor_id=`:**
- `bunching_flag` → `{flagged: bool, pct, year_range, threshold, rules_version}` (or equivalent) → drives the **bunching-flag chip** on/off state, its label text ("{pct}% in {year_range}"), and the explanatory popover. This is the **single source of truth** for the flag (foundation Part 0.2 — the FE presents, the engine decides). S5 reads only the bunching sub-object of `/api/pacing`; it does not render the pacing schedule (that is S10).

**Binding discipline.**
- Every bar, every readout, and every heat-map cell is one value+provenance binding — no derived headline number exists without a provenance trail (Part 2.5: every chart sourced from a single value+provenance binding).
- The **absolute € committed per vintage** is available inside the per-vintage ProvenancePopover (sourced from the same `vintage_pct` provenance object's underlying figures) but is **never** bound to a visual element. This is the structural guarantee of the "relative, not absolute" acceptance criterion.
- A cohort change (`POST /api/cohort`) re-resolves `peer_vintage_spread` and the peer overlay only; the investor's `vintage_pct`, `vintage_strategy`, and `n_vintages` are cohort-independent and are **not** re-fetched on a cohort change (re-render only affected charts, Part 6).

---

### §6 — Interactions

**6.1 Hover a vintage bar → strategy breakdown.** Hovering (or keyboard-focusing) a per-vintage BarRow surfaces a compact popover showing that single year's **strategy breakdown** — the row of the heat-map for that year, rendered as a tiny inline stacked bar or a 3–5 line list ("2021: Buyout 22% · Growth 9% · VC 5% · Credit 2%"), with each line's % inline. This connects the two panels: the left bar tells you *how much* in a year; the hover tells you *into what*. The corresponding heat-map row is simultaneously highlighted (a subtle `--line` emphasis on that row) so the eye links the bar to its decomposition. The popover footer carries the year's ProvenancePopover trigger and, here only, the absolute € (foundation rule: € available behind provenance, never headline).

**6.2 Hover a heat-map cell.** Surfaces the (vintage, strategy, %) triple plus, when partial, the coverage % for that cell. The cell's row and column headers highlight. Clicking a heavy cell offers a contextual action: "spread future {strategy} commitments" → deep-links to **S10 (Pacing)** carrying that strategy. The single darkest "peak" cell additionally shows a one-line "this is your most concentrated commitment" note.

**6.3 Toggle the heat-map.** The heat-map is present by default (it is a required companion, not an optional add-on — acceptance §11). The available toggles *within* the heat-map are: **Peer overlay** (greyed cohort comparison on/off, §4.2) and **View as table** (a11y, renders the cell matrix as a real `<table>` behind a "view data" affordance, foundation Part 10). There is no toggle that *hides* the heat-map, because comparisons that matter are side-by-side, not behind a toggle (Part 0.2) — the bars and the heat-map are the two halves of the same answer. (On <768px they stack rather than hide.)

**6.4 Bunching chip interaction.** Clicking the chip opens its explanatory popover: the rule in plain words ("we flag bunching when more than half your commitments fall in two consecutive vintages"), the contributing years and their combined %, the `rules_version` footnote, and a "plan to diversify" CTA → S10. The chip is keyboard-focusable and announces its state via ARIA.

**6.5 Provenance (everywhere).** Every number — each bar's %, each readout, the bunching pct, every heat-map cell — exposes a **ProvenancePopover** (click/Enter): cohort label ("Peers"), `n`, the fields used, the `method`, and the `snapshot_id`. The per-vintage popover additionally reveals the absolute € (the only place it appears). No number renders bare (Part 0.2).

**6.6 Ask Alpha coupling.** With S5 in the centre stage, Ask Alpha's context-aware suggested chips include "Am I over-concentrated in one vintage?" and "How many years am I spread across?". Alpha's answers about vintage render an inline mini-version of the per-vintage bars, with every number a provenance chip (Part 5).

---

### §7 — Seven states

Every state from the foundation Part 9 renders; below are S5's concrete realizations, with **bunching-flagged**, **cold-start**, and **thin** detailed as required.

1. **Happy (full data, well-spread or bunched).** All bars render with inline %, the heat-map is fully populated, `n_vintages` reads plainly. If the engine's `bunching_flag.flagged` is **true**, the **bunching-flagged** sub-state applies: the warning chip is active in the header ("Bunched: 64% in 2021–22"), the contributing bars carry the "◀ heavy" marker and full-saturation fill, the corresponding heat-map cells are the darkest, the peak cell is outlined, and the caption names the concentration ("You concentrated 64% of your commitments in 2021–22 — above a balanced spread"). The chip popover offers "plan to diversify → S10." If `bunching_flag.flagged` is **false**, the quiet clear chip ("Well-spread across 8 vintages ✓") renders and no bars are emphasized.

2. **Cold-start (no / very few commitments).** A new investor has no vintage history. S5 does **not** render a broken chart. Instead: the per-vintage panel shows a calm empty state — "You haven't committed to any vintages yet" — and the surface routes the user toward building a programme: the heat-map is replaced by a short explainer ("Vintage diversification means spreading commitments across market years — here's how peers spread theirs: median 6 vintages") with a primary CTA to **S9 (Allocation Architect)** and a secondary to **S10 (Pacing)**. The # vintages readout shows "0 vintages — let's build your first." No bunching chip (nothing to bunch). This is the FOMO-safe framing (Part 9 / S1): absence is *headroom*, framed as the start of a plan, never as failure.

3. **Thin cohort.** The investor's own bars and heat-map render normally (they are cohort-independent), but the **peer reference** ("⌀ peer spread") and the **peer overlay** are based on a relaxed cohort. The **RelaxBanner** renders in words from `relaxed_dims` ("Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band") above the peer reference. If the peer overlay would be based on too few peers, it stays disabled with a tooltip rather than showing a noisy comparison. The investor's bunching flag is unaffected (it is portfolio-intrinsic, not cohort-derived). A second "thin" axis specific to S5: if the **strategy decomposition** (`vintage_strategy`) is missing or too sparse to populate the heat-map honestly, the heat-map renders a **thin sub-state** — the grid frame with "strategy breakdown not available for these vintages" — while the per-vintage bars (which only need `vintage_pct`) render fully. The two halves degrade independently.

4. **Low coverage.** If commitments cannot be fully attributed to strategies (e.g. a fund's strategy is unresolved), the heat-map carries a prominent **CoverageBadge** ("based on 88% of committed capital") and the unattributed remainder renders as a faint "unclassified" column at the right edge, explicitly labelled — never silently dropped, never implied to be 100% (Part 9 #4).

5. **Null / insufficient.** A vintage year with genuinely zero commitments is **not** a null — it renders as an explicit "— no commitments" empty row (the gap is informative). True nulls (e.g. `n_vintages` unavailable) suppress the affected element with "not enough history" rather than zero-filling. The heat-map never zero-fills a missing decomposition — it shows the thin sub-state instead.

6. **Gated.** If the cohort selection is over-granular and the peer comparison is gated, the **peer reference and peer overlay** show the exact **GatedState** copy — "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate" + the "5,500 total investors" line (Part 2.6) — while the investor's own bars, heat-map, and bunching chip render fully (the investor's own portfolio is never gated to the investor). Gating affects only the peer companion.

7. **Error / degraded.** If `/api/detail/vintage` is down, S5 shows the cached last-good vintage view behind a quiet "showing your last snapshot" banner (Part 9 #7) — never a blank, never a fabricated bar. If `/api/pacing` is down but `/api/detail/vintage` is up, the bars and heat-map render and the **bunching chip degrades gracefully** to a neutral "bunching check unavailable" outline pill (it does **not** guess the flag from `vintage_pct`, preserving the engine-as-source-of-truth rule). Ask Alpha falls back gracefully.

**Cross-cutting:** not-significant peer deltas render greyed; **SyntheticTag** appears on any imputed value (e.g. if the wealth/commitment attribution behind a figure is illustrative); **loading** = calm skeletons of the bar stack and heat-map grid, not spinners.

---

### §8 — Copy deck

All strings are externalized for EN + DE (foundation Part 10 i18n); below is the EN master. Numbers are placeholders bound to live data.

**Header**
- Title: "Vintage"
- # vintages readout: "Across {n_vintages} vintage years." · Mobile stat label: "Vintage years" · Cold-start: "0 vintages — let's build your first."

**Bunching chip**
- Flagged: "Bunched: {pct}% in {year_range}"
- Clear: "Well-spread across {n_vintages} vintages"
- Degraded: "Bunching check unavailable"
- Chip popover (flagged): "We flag bunching when more than half your commitments fall in two consecutive vintage years. {pct}% of your commitments are in {year_range}. Spreading future commitments across more years reduces your exposure to any single market environment." · CTA: "Plan to diversify" · Footnote: "Rule v{rules_version}"

**Per-vintage bars**
- Panel title: "Committed by vintage"
- Axis note: "Share of your total commitments (relative)"
- Empty year row: "— no commitments"
- Peer reference: "⌀ peer spread: {peer_median} vintages"
- Heavy marker tooltip: "A large share of your programme landed in this year."
- Caption (bunched): "You concentrated {pct}% of your commitments in {year_range}; your peers spread across a median of {peer_median} vintage years."
- Caption (well-spread): "Your commitments are spread across {n_vintages} vintage years, in line with a balanced programme."

**Heat-map**
- Panel title: "Vintage × Strategy"
- Color scale labels: "0%" … "{top}%+"
- Peak-cell note: "This is your most concentrated commitment — {strategy}, {year}."
- Caption: "Darkest cells show where your capital concentrated — {strategy} in {year} is your single largest commitment."
- Peer overlay toggle: "Peer overlay" · disabled tooltip: "Peer vintage alignment unavailable."
- View-as-table affordance: "View as table"
- Unclassified column label: "Unclassified ({pct}%)"
- Thin sub-state: "Strategy breakdown not available for these vintages."

**Cold-start**
- "You haven't committed to any vintages yet."
- "Vintage diversification means spreading commitments across market years. Your peers spread across a median of {peer_median} vintages." · Primary CTA: "Build your target architecture" (→ S9) · Secondary CTA: "Plan your pacing" (→ S10)

**Thin / relax / gated / coverage / error**
- RelaxBanner: "Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band."
- GatedState (peer companion): "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate." · "5,500 total investors."
- CoverageBadge: "Based on {coverage}% of committed capital."
- Error banner: "Showing your last snapshot — live data will refresh shortly."

**Provenance (per-vintage popover, € appears here only)**
- "Computed from your committed capital, {year}. Absolute commitment this year: €{amount} (shown here only; the chart is relative). Snapshot {snapshot_id}."

---

### §9 — Badge rendering

S5 renders the foundation's badge family (Part 2.6) on every numeric element; the specifics for this surface:

- **ConfidenceBadge.** On the per-vintage panel and the peer reference, the badge shows the cohort `n` and CI where a peer comparison is involved (`n=312 · ±…`). On the **# vintages readout** and the investor's own bars — which are portfolio facts, not cohort samples — the badge instead shows the **snapshot** provenance (`snapshot {id}`) rather than `n=… · ±…`, because there is no sampling uncertainty in counting the investor's own vintages. Not-significant peer deltas grey out (Part 2.5).
- **CoverageBadge.** Appears on the heat-map (and only the heat-map) **when and only when** the commitment→strategy attribution covers <100% of committed capital — "Based on {coverage}% of committed capital." The per-vintage bars sum to 100% of *committed* capital by construction, so they do not carry a coverage badge unless the underlying total itself is partial. Never imply 100% when coverage is lower (Part 9 #4).
- **SyntheticTag.** The soft amber "illustrative" pill (Part 2.2) attaches to any figure whose inputs are imputed — e.g. if a peer-spread figure is modelled rather than observed, or if a commitment's vintage was inferred. Synthetic figures are never styled as verified fact (Part 0.2).
- **ProvenancePopover.** On every number, click/Enter to open: cohort ("Peers"), `n`, fields, `method`, `snapshot_id`. The per-vintage popover uniquely also reveals the absolute €.
- **Bunching chip** is itself a state badge, not a numeric badge: it carries a glyph (`●` flagged / `✓` clear) so its meaning is never colour-only, and its popover carries the `rules_version` footnote in place of a confidence interval.

Badge placement is structural — a number cannot render in S5 without its three badges, per the foundation's component contract (Part 2.6).

---

### §10 — Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive** (recap of §3 with the rules made explicit):
- Two-panel side-by-side ≥1280px (5:4); stacked full-width 768–1279px; single-column <768px with the bars prioritized above the heat-map.
- **No analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll** (Part 10). The heat-map honours this by compressing cell width to a ≥28px floor and, below that, collapsing zero-exposure strategy columns behind a "+N more" expander rather than scrolling.
- The bunching chip wraps to its own line on narrow headers; the # vintages readout becomes a prominent Scorecard stat on mobile.

**Accessibility** (WCAG-AA, Part 10):
- **Never colour-only.** Heavy/bunched vintages are marked with the "◀ heavy" glyph and an emphasized fill, not colour alone. The bunching chip pairs colour with `●` / `✓`. The heat-map's concentration is conveyed by intensity *and* the peak-cell outline *and* the inline % — a colour-blind user reads the % and the outline.
- **Heat-map is the highest-risk element for a11y** (sequential colour encoding). Mitigations: (a) inline % in each cell where width allows; (b) the **View as table** affordance renders the full cell matrix as a real, screen-reader-navigable `<table>` with row/column headers and % values (the "view data" pattern, Part 10); (c) the colour ramp is drawn from the color-blind-safe data tokens (Part 2.2); (d) cell hover/focus announces "{strategy}, {year}: {pct}%" via ARIA.
- **Keyboard navigation.** Every bar, cell, chip, and toggle is focusable in a logical order (header → bars top-to-bottom → heat-map row-major → toggles). Focus states are visible (Part 2.6). Enter/Space opens provenance popovers and toggles; Escape closes them.
- **ARIA.** The bunching chip exposes `role="status"` with its state and an `aria-label` describing the concentration. The bars are an accessible list; the heat-map exposes its table-equivalent. Captions are programmatically associated with their charts.
- **Contrast.** Bar fills, the heat-map ramp, and text all meet AA against `--bg`/`--surface` in both light and dark token sets; empty-cell hairlines remain perceivable in dark mode.
- **Motion.** Value-in animation once, ≤150ms, then settle (Part 2.4); respects `prefers-reduced-motion` by rendering final state immediately. No flashy transitions.

---

### §11 — Acceptance checklist

S5 is accepted when **all** of the following hold, with **zero open decisions**:

**Relative, not absolute (load-bearing):**
- [ ] The per-vintage bars encode **% of total committed capital**, summing to 100% across years — **no absolute € is bound to any visual element**.
- [ ] Absolute € per vintage appears **only** inside the per-vintage ProvenancePopover, never as a headline or bar label.
- [ ] The axis/legend reads as a relative share; no "€" headline total exists anywhere on the surface.

**Heat-map present (required companion):**
- [ ] The **Vintage × Strategy heat-map** renders by default alongside the bars — it is not hidden behind a toggle and not optional.
- [ ] Cells encode % of committed capital on an **anchored sequential intensity ramp** (0% → light, pinned top at max(12%, observed)); the legend shows labelled % stops.
- [ ] The single most-concentrated cell is outlined ("the peak"); % renders inline where width allows and in hover otherwise.
- [ ] A **View as table** a11y affordance reproduces the full cell matrix.

**Bunching flagged when concentrated:**
- [ ] The **bunching-flag chip** reads its on/off truth from `/api/pacing.bunching_flag` — the FE does **not** re-derive the flag from `vintage_pct`.
- [ ] When `flagged` is true, the chip is active with "{pct}% in {year_range}", the contributing bars carry the "◀ heavy" marker, the matching heat-map cells are darkest, and the caption names the concentration.
- [ ] When `flagged` is false, the quiet "Well-spread" chip renders; when `/api/pacing` is down, the chip degrades to "Bunching check unavailable" — never guesses.
- [ ] The chip pairs colour with a glyph (`●`/`✓`) and its popover explains the rule + `rules_version` + a "plan to diversify → S10" CTA.

**Bindings & provenance:**
- [ ] Bars ← `vintage_pct`; heat-map ← `vintage_strategy`; readout ← `n_vintages` (all from `GET /api/detail/vintage`); chip ← `bunching_flag` (from `GET /api/pacing`).
- [ ] Every number carries ConfidenceBadge (or snapshot provenance for portfolio-intrinsic counts) + ProvenancePopover; the heat-map carries CoverageBadge only when coverage <100%; imputed figures carry SyntheticTag.
- [ ] A cohort change re-renders only the peer reference/overlay; the investor's own bars, heat-map, and chip do not re-fetch.

**# vintages readout:**
- [ ] Rendered as plain words ("Across {n} vintage years") and agrees with the count of non-empty bar rows; on disagreement the engine value wins.

**Interactions:**
- [ ] Hovering a vintage bar reveals that year's strategy breakdown and highlights the matching heat-map row.
- [ ] Hovering a heat-map cell reveals (vintage, strategy, %) and a heavy cell offers "spread future commitments → S10".
- [ ] Provenance opens on every number; Ask Alpha vintage answers render inline bars with provenance chips.

**Seven states:**
- [ ] All seven render. Cold-start routes to S9/S10 with FOMO-safe framing (never a broken chart). Thin shows RelaxBanner in words and degrades the heat-map and bars **independently**. Low coverage shows the CoverageBadge + explicit "unclassified". Gated shows the exact paid-viewers copy on the peer companion only. Error shows cached last-good behind a quiet banner; the chip degrades, never fabricates.

**Surface coherence:**
- [ ] No scroll on the analytical view (desktop); no chart requires horizontal scroll at any breakpoint.
- [ ] Captions (1–2 sentences) sit under both charts; the cohort is labelled "Peers", never "All".
- [ ] Brand, tokens, light/dark, PDF export, and the persistent frame are inherited unchanged from the foundation.


---

## Surface S6 — Look-through / Concentration

> **Frontend surface spec for Claude Design.** Use case **T1-9** — Portfolio Look-Through & Concentration Radar. This brief takes the one-line sketch in PART 4 · S6 of the Frontend Foundation (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`) and the deep backend contract (`T1-09_Lookthrough_Concentration.md`) to full frontend depth. It **references, never redefines**, the foundation design system: tokens (`--brand`, `--data-1…9`, `--over`/`--under`, `--synthetic`), the **Treemap**, **GroupedBars**, **ZChart**, **ComparisonSelector**, **CoverageBadge**, **ConfidenceBadge**, **SyntheticTag**, **ProvenancePopover**, **RelaxBanner**, **PDFExportButton**, the persistent frame (PART 3), and the seven-state model (PART 9). Bind every figure to `GET /api/detail/lookthrough`. The headline behaviour of this surface is **coverage honesty**: a look-through number never renders without its `coverage_pct`, and the UI **never implies 100%**.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Reveal the *hidden concentration* and the *real underlying exposure* that sit **beneath the fund layer**. An investor sees a tidy list of feeders; what they actually hold is a commitment-weighted blend of hundreds of portfolio companies across sectors, geographies, currencies, managers and vintages — and the same company can sit underneath several funds at once (the *hidden bet*). S6 rolls each holding's feeder-level GICS look-through up to the investor, commitment-weighted, **always carrying a coverage %**, computes a Herfindahl–Hirschman index per dimension, detects company-overlap across funds (k-anonymised to name), maps theme exposure, and triangulates AI exposure three ways (stated / embedded-assumption / empirical look-through). It then compares the investor's look-through sector vector to peers / best performers / a model portfolio.

This is the **trust-defining surface** of Alpha. Every other surface presents one clean number; S6 deliberately presents a number *and the fraction of the book it actually describes*. The design job is to make partial coverage feel like rigour, not a defect — the investor should come away thinking "this product tells me what it does and doesn't know," which is the whole proprietary-data value proposition made visible.

**The user is a doctor, not a quant.** The headline takeaways must read in plain language ("You're more concentrated on one manager than you think — 43% sits with Apex") with the HHI math available on demand but never required to grasp the point.

**User questions this surface answers (→ eval id → bound field):**

- **Q1 "Am I more concentrated than I think?"** → `concentration_radar` → `concentration.{sector,geo,manager,vintage}.{hhi,band,top_share_pct,effective_n}`.
- **Q2 "Do I have too much indirect software / tech exposure?"** → `theme_exposure` → `theme.value["Software/Tech"]` + `gaps.vs_peer` on that theme.
- **Q3 "Where do multiple funds overlap underneath?"** → `company_overlap` → `company_overlap[]` (named where k-anon ≥10, bucketed otherwise).
- **Q4 "How does my look-through exposure compare to PE professionals / best performers / the model?"** → `lookthrough_gap` → `gaps.{vs_peer,vs_best,vs_model}`.
- **Q5 "In which AI companies am I indirectly invested?"** → `ai_lookthrough` → `ai.{stated,embedded_assumption,lookthrough_derived,ai_companies}`.
- **Q6 "What is my real sector / geography / currency mix once I look through?"** → `lookthrough_vectors` → `exposure.{sector,geo,currency}` each with `coverage_pct`.
- **Q7 "Which single manager am I most exposed to underneath?"** → `manager_concentration` → `concentration.manager.{hhi,top_manager}`.

**Design north star.** A boardroom-projector-legible *concentration radar* where the treemap shows *what you hold underneath*, the HHI strip says *how concentrated that makes you*, the company-overlap list names *the hidden bets*, and the AI card shows *the gap between what you think you own and what you economically own* — all under one unmissable coverage banner that says "this describes X% of your book."

---

### §2 Entry & nav

- **Rail label:** "Look-through" — position 6 in the left-rail surface order (Overview · Allocation · Region · Format · Vintage · **Look-through** · Performance · KPIs · …), under the *Understand* grouping.
- **Primary entry:** click "Look-through" in the persistent left rail (PART 3). Full-screen Detail page; the global frame (ProfileBar top, CohortBuilder left, Ask Alpha float) persists; **no scroll** on the analytical view.
- **Deep-link entries:**
  - From **S1 Overview**, a Z-bar click on the over/underweight chart does *not* land here (that goes to S2 Strategy); but the Overview's "Am I over-concentrated?" Ask-Alpha suggested prompt routes here.
  - From **S19 Macro**, the "hidden bet" flags link into S6's company-overlap list with that company pre-highlighted.
  - From **Ask Alpha**, the suggested-prompt chip *"Am I over-concentrated in AI?"* (PART 5) opens S6 with the **AI read card** scrolled into focus / pulsed once.
- **URL / route state:** `/detail/lookthrough?investor_id=&compare=&dim=` — `compare` persists the ComparisonSelector choice (`peer|best|model`); `dim` persists which exposure vector the treemap is showing (`sector|geo|currency`, default `sector`). Both are reflected to the URL so a shared link reopens the same view.
- **Cohort context:** the active cohort (from the left-rail CohortBuilder, PART 6) is the peer set used in `gaps.vs_peer`; changing the cohort re-resolves and re-renders **only** the delta column and the peer overlay, not the whole page.
- **Exit:** any rail item; PDFExportButton exports the current S6 state (current `compare` + `dim`) as a PowerPoint-style PDF including the coverage banner.

---

### §3 Layout

**Grid (desktop ≥1280px).** A full-screen Detail page, **no vertical scroll**. A persistent **coverage banner** spans the top of the centre stage; beneath it a 12-column grid splits into a dominant treemap region (left, ~7 col) and a stacked right column (~5 col) holding the HHI concentration strip, the AI read card, and the company-overlap list. The delta column lives *inside* the treemap region as a right-attached rail.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ▣ COVERAGE BANNER  "Look-through covers 76.7% of your NAV — figures below      │  ← always on, full width
│     describe that slice only. 23.3% of your commitment has no look-through."    │     CoverageBadge style, large
├───────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┤
│  REAL SECTOR EXPOSURE (look-through)           │  CONCENTRATION (HHI strip)    │
│  [dim: Sector ▾ Geo Currency]                  │  Sector   ●○○○  moderate 0.19 │
│ ┌───────────────┬────────┬─────┐   ┌────────┐  │  Geo      ●●●○  concentr 0.39 │
│ │  Tech         │  AI    │ Fin │   │ ΔvsPeer│  │  Manager  ●●●○  concentr 0.30 │
│ │  29.1%        │ 18.7%  │16.5%│   │ ──────│  │  Vintage  ●●○○  moderate 0.21 │
│ │  cov 76.7%    │cov76.7%│     │   │ AI ▲4.1│  │  → top: Apex 43.3% (manager)  │
│ ├───────┬───────┼────┬───┤     │   │ Fin▼6.2│  ├──────────────────────────────┤
│ │Indust │Consum │Hlth│ … │     │   │ Tech ▲ │  │  AI READ                      │
│ │13.9%  │12.7%  │9.1%│   │     │   │  …      │  │  Stated 13%  ·  Look-thru 18.7│
│ └───────┴───────┴────┴───┴─────┘   └────────┘  │  Embedded ~23.2% ⚑illustr.    │
│  ░ uncovered 23.3% wedge (greyed, hatched) ░   │  [assumption note]            │
│  ┄ peer overlay (greyed dashed outline) ┄      ├──────────────────────────────┤
│  caption: "Your real exposure once you look…"  │  COMPANY OVERLAP (hidden bets)│
│  [ComparisonSelector: Peers · Best · Model]    │  Helix AI   7.31%  2 funds    │
│                                                │  a Financial co. 3.10% 2 funds│
│                                                │  …  (k-anon'd rows)           │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
  [PDF export ▾]
```

**Left region — Real exposure treemap (the hero).**
- A **Treemap** (foundation component, PART 2.5/2.6): one **tile = sector**, sized by look-through weight, filled with the **blue→teal→mint `--data-*` gradient** (fixed strategy/sector→colour map so a sector is the same colour everywhere across the app). Larger weight → darker/more-saturated blue; smaller → mint. Tile label = sector name + **% inline** + a small inline **CoverageBadge** ("cov 76.7%") on the tile footer.
- A **dim switcher** above the treemap — a small segmented control `Sector · Geo · Currency` (default Sector) re-renders the treemap from `exposure.{sector|geo|currency}`. Coverage % is identical across these three (all inherit the covered slice) but is re-asserted on each.
- An **uncovered wedge**: a final greyed, **diagonally-hatched** tile labelled "No look-through · 23.3%" pinned to the bottom-right of the treemap, sized to `uncovered_pct` relative to the covered area's implied 100%. This is the visual proof that the surface never renormalizes to 100% — the hatched block is the honesty. It carries no `--data-*` fill (uses `--gated` grey) and is non-interactive except a tooltip "This share of your commitment has no underlying look-through and is excluded from the figures above."
- A **greyed peer overlay**: a thin **dashed outline** drawn over each sector tile at the *peer cohort's* look-through weight for that sector — so the investor sees their tile filled (you) with the peer's footprint outlined on top. Rendered in `--muted` dashed stroke, no fill, so it reads as "where peers sit" without competing with the saturated tiles. Toggleable via a small "show peers" checkbox on the treemap header (default on).
- A **CaptionBlock** under the treemap (1–2 sentences): "Your real exposure once you look through the funds — over the 76.7% of your book we can see inside. Tech and AI together are 47.8% of the covered slice."
- A **ComparisonSelector** (`Peers · Best · Model`, foundation segmented control) beneath the caption — drives both the **peer overlay** on the treemap and the **delta column**.

**Delta column (right-attached rail inside the treemap region).**
- A compact vertical list, one row per sector, showing the **gap vs the selected comparison** on the look-through sector vector: sector name · signed delta · a **▲ (`--over`)** or **▼ (`--under`)** glyph · the value. Significant deltas render solid; **non-significant deltas render greyed** with a "not significant" micro-label (G2). Sorted by absolute delta descending so the biggest divergences sit at top.
- Header: "Δ vs Peers" (relabels to "Δ vs Best" / "Δ vs Model" with the selector). A block-level **CoverageBadge** at the column foot: "comparison covers min(76.7%, peer 71%) = 71% of both books."

**Right column — three stacked cards.**

1. **Concentration (HHI strip).** Four rows — **Sector · Geo · Manager · Vintage** — each a labelled mini-meter: a 4-dot band indicator (`diversified ○○○○` → `highly_concentrated ●●●●`) coloured by band, the **HHI value**, the **band word**, and `effective_n` ("≈5.2 equal bets"). Each row carries a **CoverageBadge**: Sector/Geo show "cov 76.7%"; **Manager/Vintage show "cov 100% (full book)"** — a deliberate, reassuring contrast (these don't need look-through). A highlighted footer line surfaces the single biggest concentration: **"Most exposed: Apex — 43.3% of commitment (manager)."** This is the headline hidden-concentration insight and is visually emphasised (`--brand` accent).
2. **AI read card.** Three numbers side-by-side, never collapsed (G5): **Stated 13%** · **Look-through 18.7%** (CoverageBadge "cov 76.7%") · **Embedded ~23.2%** (with a **SyntheticTag** "illustrative" + an assumption note). A one-line plain-language read beneath. (Full spec §4.)
3. **Company-overlap list.** A short ranked list of "hidden bets" — companies appearing under ≥2 funds — each row: company name (or k-anon bucket), look-through %, # funds underneath, a small sector colour-dot. (Full spec §4.)

**Responsive.**
- **≥1280px:** the layout above (treemap left ~7col, stacked cards right ~5col, delta rail attached).
- **768–1279px:** the CohortBuilder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher (per PART 3). Centre stage reflows to **two stacked bands**: band 1 = coverage banner + treemap (full width) + ComparisonSelector + delta column moves below the treemap as a horizontal scroll-free row of delta chips; band 2 = HHI strip + AI card + overlap list as a 3-up card row. Still no horizontal scroll on the treemap (tiles reflow to a taller aspect).
