- **<768px (mobile, advisor on the go):** single column, stacked: coverage banner (sticky) → treemap (square aspect, fewer tiles, "more" expander) → dim switcher as a dropdown → HHI strip (rows) → AI card → overlap list → delta as a "compare vs Peers" expandable section. The treemap exposes its **"view as table"** affordance (PART 10) prominently since small tiles are hard to read on phone. ProfileBar becomes a sheet.

---

### §4 Components & charts (exact encodings)

#### 4.1 Treemap — full encoding (foundation `Treemap`, PART 2.5/2.6)
- **Tile = sector** (or geo/currency per `dim`). **Area ∝ look-through weight** (`exposure.{dim}.value[k]`). Tiles laid out squarified, largest top-left, descending — deterministic order matches the API's descending-share sort (so layout is stable across reloads; the backend guarantees stable ordering).
- **Fill:** the fixed sector→colour map drawn from `--data-1…9` (blue→teal→mint). The *same* sector is the *same* colour here, on S1's strategy mix, and everywhere — never re-hue per view. Saturation is **not** used to encode weight (area already does); the gradient is categorical, not sequential-by-value, to keep cross-surface colour identity. (Where the foundation says "blue/green gradient," that is the categorical `--data-*` ramp, not a per-tile luminance scale.)
- **Tile contents:** sector name (body), **% inline** (mono, tabular — e.g. `29.1%`), and a footer micro **CoverageBadge** ("cov 76.7%"). Small tiles (<6%) drop the badge to a hover-only tooltip but keep the % if it fits; below ~3% they collapse into an "Other (n)" tile that expands on click.
- **Uncovered wedge:** a terminal `--gated` grey, diagonally-hatched tile sized to `uncovered_pct`, labelled "No look-through · 23.3%", non-interactive (tooltip only). Always present whenever `uncovered_pct > 0`; it is the structural anti-100%-renormalization affordance.
- **Peer overlay:** per-tile dashed `--muted` outline at the comparison cohort's weight for that sector (no fill). A legend chip "┄ Peers" sits in the treemap header. Hidden when "show peers" is unchecked or when comparison coverage is null.
- **Hover:** raises the tile (1px elevation), shows a tooltip: sector · your % · coverage % · peer % (if overlay on) · delta with ▲/▼. Click a tile → opens the **ProvenancePopover** (method, n feeders covered/total, covered_eur/total_eur, snapshot_id, source).
- **Caption:** mandatory 1–2 sentence CaptionBlock beneath (PART 2.5 rule).
- **Empty / null dim:** if a dim vector is null (zero coverage), the treemap renders a single full `--gated` tile "No look-through available for this view — figures describe 0% of your book" (state 4, §7).

#### 4.2 Delta column (the over/underweight vs cohort)
- **Source:** `gaps.{vs_peer|vs_best|vs_model}` — a map of `{sector: {delta, significant}}` on the look-through *sector* vector. The delta column is **sector-only** (the only vector all parties share); when the treemap `dim` is geo/currency, the delta column shows a quiet note "Δ comparison is on sector exposure" and keeps showing sector deltas (the gap object has no geo/currency).
- **Row:** `[colour-dot] Sector  ▲ +4.1` — sign + glyph + value (mono). `--over` colour + `▲` for positive (you above cohort), `--under` + `▼` for negative. Colour is **always** paired with the glyph and sign (a11y; never colour-only).
- **Significance:** `significant:false` → the whole row renders in `--muted`, value de-emphasised, a "not significant" caption appended. Significant rows are full-ink. (G2.)
- **Sort:** by `|delta|` descending. Cap visible rows at ~8; an "n more" expander reveals the tail.
- **Header / coverage:** header reads "Δ vs Peers/Best/Model"; foot carries a CoverageBadge at `gaps.coverage_pct` (the min of the two sides' coverage) with the explanatory tooltip.

#### 4.3 Company-overlap rows (hidden bets, k-anon)
- **Source:** `company_overlap[]` — each entry is a company under **≥2 funds** in your book. Single-fund companies are *not* here (they're exposure, not a hidden bet) but still feed the treemap.
- **Row layout:** `[sector colour-dot]  {company name}   {lookthrough_pct}%   {n_funds} funds`. Right-aligned mono for the % and fund count.
- **k-anon rendering (≥10):** when `k_anon_ok:true` → show the real `company` name, the row is clickable to reveal `fund_ids` ("appears in: Nimbus Growth II, Vertex AI Fund"). When `k_anon_ok:false` → render the **bucket label** verbatim from the API (`"a Financial company"`), `company_id` null, **not clickable**, and a tiny lock/`--gated` glyph with tooltip "Named only when ≥10 investors hold a fund containing it." Never invent a name; never show fund_ids for a bucketed row (`fund_ids:null`).
- **Ordering:** by `lookthrough_pct` descending (API-sorted). Cap ~6 rows with an "n more overlaps" expander.
- **Empty state:** if `company_overlap == []` (single-fund book, or no ≥2-fund overlaps) → "No companies sit under more than one of your funds." If all overlaps are sub-k-anon → "You have overlapping bets, but they're below our naming threshold" with bucketed rows only (state 6, §7).
- **Header note:** "Hidden bets — the same company underneath two or more of your funds." Each row's % carries the covered-slice coverage implicitly via the card-level CoverageBadge ("over 76.7% of your book").

#### 4.4 AI read card (three numbers, never collapsed — G5)
- **Three values, side-by-side**, each in its own mini-cell so they read as *different measurements of the same thing*:
  - **Stated AI · 13%** — sub-label "what you chose (your AI sleeve)". Source `ai.stated.value`. No coverage badge (it's a stated allocation, not look-through).
  - **Look-through AI · 18.7%** — sub-label "found underneath, over what we can see". Source `ai.lookthrough_derived.value`. Carries a **CoverageBadge "cov 76.7%"** (it is coverage-bound).
  - **Embedded AI · ~23.2%** — sub-label "estimated economic AI". Source `ai.embedded_assumption.value`. Carries a **SyntheticTag** "illustrative" (amber `--synthetic`) and the **assumption note** (copy in §8). The `~` prefix and the tag together signal "modelled, not measured."
- **Plain-language read** beneath (one line, the headline for the doctor-not-quant): "You set aside 13% for AI, but looking through your funds you're really at 18.7% — and under our house assumptions your *economic* AI exposure may be closer to 23%." Numbers in this sentence are **ProvenancePopover chips**.
- **AI companies** (Q5): a small expander "Which AI companies?" listing `ai.ai_companies[]` with k-anon naming identical to §4.3 (named ≥10, else bucketed). Each named company links to its overlap row.
- **Ordering of the three values is fixed:** Stated → Look-through → Embedded (chosen → measured → modelled). The card **never** sums or averages the three (G5); a small "Why three numbers?" info link opens a popover explaining the distinction.
- **Visual hierarchy:** Look-through AI (the empirical one) is the largest/boldest; Stated and Embedded are secondary. The Embedded cell has the amber SyntheticTag visually distinct so it can *never* be mistaken for measured fact (PART 2.6 SyntheticTag rule).

---

### §5 Data bindings (← `GET /api/detail/lookthrough?investor_id=&dim=&compare=`)

Every numeric element below binds `value` to the visual and `provenance` (incl. `coverage_pct`) to its badge/popover (PART 0.2). **No figure renders without its coverage.**

| UI element | ← API path |
|---|---|
| **Coverage banner** %, uncovered %, covered/total € | `coverage.{coverage_pct, uncovered_pct, covered_eur, total_eur, n_feeders_covered, n_feeders_total}`; banner provenance ← `coverage.provenance` |
| **Treemap tiles** (Sector) | `exposure.sector.value{k:pct}`; tile CoverageBadge ← `exposure.sector.provenance.coverage_pct`; uncovered wedge ← `exposure.sector.provenance.uncovered_pct` |
| **Treemap tiles** (Geo) | `exposure.geo.value`; coverage ← `exposure.geo.provenance.coverage_pct` |
| **Treemap tiles** (Currency) | `exposure.currency.value`; coverage ← `exposure.currency.provenance.coverage_pct` |
| **Treemap ProvenancePopover** | `exposure.{dim}.provenance.{method, source, coverage_pct, uncovered_pct, covered_eur, total_eur, n_feeders_covered, n_feeders_total}` + top-level `snapshot_id` |
| **Peer overlay** (dashed outline weights) | peer look-through sector vector — derived from `gaps.vs_peer` applied to your vector, OR a dedicated peer exposure block when `compare` overlay is fetched; coverage ← `gaps.coverage_pct` |
| **Delta column** rows | `gaps.{vs_peer\|vs_best\|vs_model}[sector].{delta, significant}`; column CoverageBadge ← `gaps.coverage_pct` |
| **HHI strip — Sector** | `concentration.sector.{hhi, effective_n, top_share_pct, band, coverage_pct}` |
| **HHI strip — Geo** | `concentration.geo.{hhi, effective_n, top_share_pct, band, coverage_pct}` |
| **HHI strip — Manager** | `concentration.manager.{hhi, effective_n, top_share_pct, band, coverage_pct(=100), top_manager{name, share_pct, k_anon_ok}}` |
| **HHI strip — Vintage** | `concentration.vintage.{hhi, effective_n, top_share_pct, band, coverage_pct(=100), top_vintage{year, share_pct}, vintage_excluded_n}` |
| **"Most exposed" footer** | `concentration.manager.top_manager` (name shown iff `k_anon_ok`, else "a manager") |
| **Company-overlap rows** | `company_overlap[].{company, company_id, sector, lookthrough_pct, n_funds, fund_ids, k_anon_ok}` |
| **AI — Stated** | `ai.stated.value`; provenance ← `ai.stated.provenance.{method, source}` |
| **AI — Look-through** | `ai.lookthrough_derived.value`; CoverageBadge ← `ai.lookthrough_derived.provenance.coverage_pct` |
| **AI — Embedded** | `ai.embedded_assumption.value`; SyntheticTag + assumption note ← `ai.embedded_assumption.provenance.{method:"assumption", formula, source, version, label}` |
| **AI companies expander** | `ai.ai_companies[].{company, company_id, lookthrough_pct, k_anon_ok}` |
| **Theme exposure** (for Q2, surfaced in AI card "Why three numbers?" / or a theme sub-tab) | `theme.value{theme:pct}`; coverage ← `theme.provenance.coverage_pct`, version ← `theme.provenance.version` |
| **PDF export** snapshot stamp | top-level `snapshot_id` |

**Query params the FE sends:** `dim` (treemap vector), `compare` (delta + overlay). The endpoint also supports `&dim=`/`&compare=` returning *just* that block for cheap re-fetch on selector change — the FE uses the partial fetch on ComparisonSelector / dim-switch and only re-renders the affected component.

**Coverage rule (restated, binding):** the coverage banner reads `coverage.coverage_pct`; **every** look-through leaf (`exposure.*`, `concentration.{sector,geo}`, `theme`, `ai.lookthrough_derived`, `company_overlap` block, `gaps` block) shows its own `coverage_pct` even though they share the same value — redundancy here is intentional (G4). Manager/Vintage HHI show `coverage_pct:100` with the explicit "full book" label so the user understands those don't depend on look-through.

---

### §6 Interactions

- **ComparisonSelector (`Peers · Best · Model`).** Segmented control beneath the treemap caption. Changing it:
  1. re-fetches `&compare={peer|best|model}` (partial),
  2. updates the **delta column** values, glyphs, significance styling, and header label,
  3. updates the **peer overlay** dashed outlines on the treemap to the new comparison cohort,
  4. updates the **delta column CoverageBadge** to the new `gaps.coverage_pct`.
  It does **not** re-render the treemap fills (your own exposure) or the HHI strip (those are you-only). "Peers" is always labelled "Peers," never "All" (PART 2.5).
- **dim switcher (`Sector · Geo · Currency`).** Re-fetches `&dim=` (partial) and re-renders only the treemap tiles + the Sector/Geo HHI row context. The delta column stays on sector with the quiet "Δ is on sector exposure" note when dim ≠ sector.
- **Hover a treemap tile.** Tooltip: `{sector} · your {pct}% · coverage {cov}%` and, if peer overlay on, `· peer {peer_pct}% · Δ {±delta} ▲/▼`. The hover never hides the coverage — coverage is in the tooltip even on hover.
- **Click a treemap tile.** Opens **ProvenancePopover** (PART 2.6): "show how this was computed" — method string ("commitment-weighted feeder look-through (sector); normalized over covered feeders only"), `n_feeders_covered / n_feeders_total`, `covered_eur / total_eur`, source view, `snapshot_id`. The popover explicitly states the covered-only normalization so a curious user sees *why* it isn't 100%.
- **Provenance on every figure.** The coverage banner %, each HHI value, the top-manager %, each company-overlap %, and each AI number are **ProvenancePopover-clickable chips** (PART 0.2 — a number never appears bare). The AI Embedded chip's popover leads with the `label:"ASSUMPTION — not measured"` and shows the `formula` and table `version`.
- **Company-overlap row click.** Named rows expand to show `fund_ids` as fund-name chips ("appears in: Nimbus Growth II · Vertex AI Fund") — each chip deep-links to S13 Funds for that fund. Bucketed rows are inert (cursor not-allowed, tooltip explains k-anon).
- **Cohort change (left-rail CohortBuilder).** Re-resolves the peer set → re-renders **only** the delta column + peer overlay + their coverage badges (not the treemap, not the HHI strip). A **RelaxBanner** (in words, from `relaxed_dims`) appears above the delta column if the cohort was broadened.
- **"show peers" checkbox.** Toggles the dashed peer overlay on/off on the treemap (default on); state persists in URL.
- **Ask Alpha integration.** The suggested-prompt chip "Am I over-concentrated in AI?" routes here and pulses the AI card once; an Alpha answer about concentration renders an inline mini-treemap whose numbers are the same provenance chips (PART 5).
- **PDF export.** Captures the current `dim` + `compare` state, including the coverage banner, the uncovered wedge, the HHI strip, the AI card (with SyntheticTag rendered), and the overlap list with k-anon intact.
- **Motion.** Treemap tiles animate area-in once on first load (≤150ms, no bounce); selector changes cross-fade delta values and overlay outlines subtly; no flashy transitions (PART 2.4).

---

### §7 Seven states (LOW-COVERAGE is the headline)

Every state below keeps the **coverage discipline** front and centre; the surface **never** implies 100%.

1. **Happy.** Full payload (§5). Coverage banner reads the real figure (e.g. 76.7%); treemap with uncovered wedge; HHI strip with the Apex headline; AI card three-up; overlap list named where k-anon. All deltas significant render solid. This is the reference render — but note even the *happy* state shows the uncovered wedge and per-figure coverage; "happy" never means "100% covered."

2. **Cold-start (`cold_start:true`, `n_funds==0`).** No holdings → `exposure`/`concentration`/`company_overlap`/`ai.lookthrough_derived` omitted. The page shows a friendly empty centre: "Your look-through will appear here once you hold funds." `ai.stated`/`ai.embedded_assumption` still render *if* `allocation_json` exists (a stated/assumed AI read with no empirical column, the Look-through cell showing "—, no holdings yet"). A prominent CTA routes to **S9 Allocation Architect** (the cold-start home). No broken charts.

3. **Thin / single-fund (`n_funds==1`).** HHI of every dimension ≈1.0, band `highly_concentrated`. `company_overlap == []` (no second fund to overlap). The surface renders validly with a plain-language frame: "You hold one fund — concentration is structural here, not a flag." HHI strip shows the four full meters; overlap list shows the empty-state copy. The coverage banner still applies to that one fund's look-through.

4. **LOW-COVERAGE — THE HEADLINE STATE (`low_coverage:true`, `coverage_pct < COVERAGE_FLOOR` default 40).** This is the state S6 is *designed around*. All look-through blocks **still return and still render** — but:
   - The **coverage banner turns prominent / cautionary** (a stronger `--synthetic`-adjacent treatment, larger type): **"Only 31% of your commitment has look-through. The figures below describe that slice only — they are not your whole portfolio."**
   - The **treemap is visually de-emphasised / greyed** (reduced saturation) so the user reads it as partial, and the **uncovered wedge dominates** the layout proportionally (69% hatched grey) — the picture itself communicates "we can't see most of this."
   - Every per-figure CoverageBadge is rendered in the cautionary treatment, not the quiet one.
   - **Manager and Vintage HHI are unaffected** (full-book, coverage 100%) and shown normally — a deliberate reassurance: "we *can* still tell you your manager and vintage concentration with confidence; it's the sector/company look-through that's partial."
   - **Never renormalized to 100%.** The uncovered slice is the hatched wedge, never folded into the tiles.
   - At **`coverage_pct == 0`** (no covered feeders): `exposure`, sector/geo HHI, `ai.lookthrough_derived`, `company_overlap`, theme are **null** and render as `--gated` "No look-through available — 0% of your book." Manager/Vintage HHI and `ai.stated`/`ai.embedded_assumption` **still render**. The banner reads "We have no look-through for your current holdings yet."
   The unmissable-badge requirement: in this state the coverage badge is the single most prominent element on the page, above the treemap title.

5. **Null / insufficient — AI-assumption-only (covered slice has no AI sector, embedded > 0).** `ai.lookthrough_derived.value == 0.0` (coverage-bound), `ai.embedded_assumption.value > 0`. The AI card surfaces the divergence rather than hiding it: "No AI found in your covered look-through (0% over 76.7%), but under house assumptions your embedded AI exposure is ~X%." The Look-through cell shows `0.0%` with its coverage badge — never zero-filled silently, never blank.

6. **k-anon-suppressed overlaps (all overlaps below 10 investors).** `company_overlap` returns only bucketed rows (`company_id:null`, names like "a Financial company"). The list header copy: "You have overlapping bets, but they're below our naming threshold." HHI strip, treemap and AI numbers are unaffected. The bucketed rows still show % and fund count (the *fact* of overlap), just not the name — k-anon protects the name, not the existence.

7. **Error / degraded.** Engine/model down → render the **last-good cached snapshot** with a quiet banner "Showing your last look-through from {snapshot_date}; live engine is reconnecting." Never a blank screen, never a fabricated number. `404 unknown investor` → a clean "We couldn't find this portfolio" state (advisor-mode mis-selection), not a stack trace. Ask Alpha falls back gracefully (PART 5).

**Cross-cutting:** non-significant deltas always render greyed (G2); the Embedded-AI SyntheticTag is present in every state where `ai.embedded_assumption` renders (G5); loading uses calm skeletons (a shimmer over the treemap grid and card outlines), not spinners.

---

### §8 Copy deck

**Coverage banner (happy):**
> "Look-through covers **76.7% of your NAV.** The figures below describe that covered slice only — 23.3% of your commitment has no underlying look-through and is shown as the grey wedge."

**Coverage banner (low-coverage, < floor):**
> "Heads up — only **31% of your commitment has look-through.** Everything below describes that slice, not your whole portfolio. The grey area is what we can't yet see inside."

**Coverage banner (zero coverage):**
> "We don't have look-through for your current holdings yet. We can still show your **manager and vintage concentration** (computed on your full book) below."

**Uncovered wedge label / tooltip:**
> "No look-through · 23.3% — this share of your commitment has no underlying data and is excluded from the figures above. We never spread the covered shape onto it."

**Treemap caption (happy):**
> "Your real exposure once you look through the funds — over the 76.7% of your book we can see inside. Tech and AI together are 47.8% of that covered slice."

**HHI strip — band words & companion:** `diversified` · `moderate` · `concentrated` · `highly concentrated`; each row appends "≈{effective_n} equal bets" (e.g. "moderate · 0.19 · ≈5.2 equal bets").

**HHI "most exposed" footer (the headline insight):**
> "Most exposed: **Apex — 43.3% of your commitment** sits with one manager. You hold five funds, but nearly half your money is behind a single GP."

**HHI manager/vintage coverage label:**
> "cov 100% — computed on your full book; no look-through needed."

**AI card — the three sub-labels:**
- Stated: "what you chose · your AI sleeve"
- Look-through: "found underneath · over what we can see (76.7%)"
- Embedded: "estimated economic AI · illustrative"

**AI card — plain-language read:**
> "You set aside **13% for AI**, but looking through your funds you're really at **18.7%** — and under our house assumptions your *economic* AI exposure may be closer to **23%.** The three numbers disagree on purpose: one is what you chose, one is what we measured, one is what we estimate."

**THE AI ASSUMPTION NOTE (on the Embedded figure — mandatory, G5):**
> "**Illustrative — not measured.** *Embedded AI* estimates the AI baked into your other strategies using a fixed house-view table (`ai_embed_pct`, version {version}): e.g. growth/tech books are assumed ~35% AI-exposed, large-cap buyout ~8%. It is an assumption, not a look-through of your actual companies — that empirical figure is the *Look-through AI* number beside it (18.7% over 76.7% of your book)."

**AI "Why three numbers?" popover:**
> "**Stated** is the AI you explicitly allocated. **Look-through** is the AI we actually find in the companies underneath your funds, over the portion we can see. **Embedded** is a modelled estimate of AI hidden inside non-AI strategies. We keep them separate so a modelled estimate is never mistaken for a measured fact."

**Company-overlap header:**
> "Hidden bets — the same company sitting underneath two or more of your funds."

**Company-overlap k-anon bucket tooltip:**
> "Named only when at least 10 investors hold a fund containing this company. Below that, we show the sector but not the name."

**Company-overlap empty:**
> "No company sits under more than one of your funds — no hidden overlaps detected (over your covered slice)."

**Delta column header:** "Δ vs Peers" / "Δ vs Best" / "Δ vs Model". **Non-significant micro-label:** "not significant — within peer spread." **Delta coverage tooltip:** "Comparison runs over the smaller of the two coverages — min(your 76.7%, peers' 71%) = 71%."

**Single-fund frame:** "You hold one fund — concentration is structural here, not a flag to act on."

**Cold-start:** "Your look-through will appear here once you hold funds. In the meantime, see what a balanced architecture looks like →" (CTA to S9).

**RelaxBanner (thin cohort):** rendered in words from `relaxed_dims`, e.g. "Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band to find enough peers for this comparison."

---

### §9 Badge rendering

**CoverageBadge — on EVERY look-through figure (G4, non-negotiable).** Foundation `CoverageBadge` (PART 2.6: "look-through 61% of NAV" chip). In S6 it appears:
- as the **top coverage banner** (the largest instance, surface-defining),
- on **every treemap tile footer** (or hover for small tiles),
- on the **Sector and Geo HHI rows** (real coverage),
- on the **Manager and Vintage HHI rows** as **"cov 100% (full book)"** — a deliberate, distinct treatment so the user reads these as *not* coverage-limited,
- at the **foot of the delta column** (`gaps.coverage_pct`, the min of both sides),
- on the **Look-through AI** cell,
- as a **card-level badge** on the company-overlap list ("over 76.7% of your book") and the theme block.
A look-through number rendering **without** a CoverageBadge is a build failure (mirrors backend G4). The badge has two visual registers: **quiet** (happy, coverage ≥ floor) and **cautionary** (low-coverage state, §7.4) — the cautionary register is larger, higher-contrast, and never dismissible.

**SyntheticTag — on embedded-AI (G5).** Foundation `SyntheticTag` (PART 2.6: amber "illustrative" pill). In S6 it appears **only** on the **Embedded AI** figure (`ai.embedded_assumption`) and is paired with the assumption note (§8). It must be visually unmistakable — embedded AI can *never* be styled as verified fact (PART 0.2). The `~` numeric prefix reinforces the tag. SyntheticTag does **not** appear on Stated or Look-through AI (those are a stated allocation and an empirical measurement respectively). If any look-through feeder's contribution was synthesised in a demo snapshot (`provenance.synthetic=true`, §8 real-data notes), a SyntheticTag also appears on the coverage banner with "demo snapshot — some look-through is illustrative."

**ConfidenceBadge.** Where the delta column shows a significant/insignificant gap, the significance is carried as the greyed/solid styling + "not significant" label (G2) rather than a separate n±CI chip; the underlying cohort n surfaces in the ProvenancePopover. (The HHI figures are deterministic point statistics over the book, so they carry coverage + provenance but not a CI.)

**ProvenancePopover.** On every numeric chip (coverage %, each HHI, top-manager %, each overlap %, all three AI numbers, every delta) — "show how this was computed" (§6). The Embedded-AI popover leads with `label:"ASSUMPTION — not measured"`.

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Treemap-as-table affordance (mandatory, PART 10).** Every treemap exposes a **"view as table"** toggle in its header. The table renders: one row per sector with columns **Sector · Your look-through % · Coverage % · Peer % · Δ (signed, with ▲/▼ word) · Significant?** plus a final **"No look-through · 23.3%"** row for the uncovered wedge. This is the accessible equivalent of the treemap and the primary mobile reading mode (small tiles are unreadable on phone). The HHI strip, AI card, and overlap list are already list/row-based and are inherently screen-reader-friendly.

**A11y (WCAG-AA):**
- **Never colour-only.** Every over/under delta pairs `--over`/`--under` with a **▲/▼ glyph and a sign**; every HHI band pairs colour with the **band word**; the uncovered wedge pairs grey with a **diagonal hatch pattern** and the word "No look-through" (so it survives greyscale and colour-blindness).
- **`--data-*` ramp is color-blind-safe** (foundation) and sectors carry text labels in-tile, so the treemap never relies on hue alone to distinguish sectors.
- **Coverage is text, not just colour.** The cautionary low-coverage banner reads as text — a colour-blind user gets the warning from the words and the dominant hatched wedge, not the tint.
- **Keyboard nav:** treemap tiles are a focusable list (arrow-key traversal, Enter opens ProvenancePopover); the ComparisonSelector and dim switcher are standard segmented controls with roving tabindex; the "view as table" toggle and "show peers" checkbox are keyboard-reachable. Focus rings on all interactive elements.
- **ARIA:** treemap has `role="img"` with an `aria-label` summarising the top sectors + coverage ("Look-through sector exposure over 76.7% of NAV: Tech 29%, AI 19%, …"); the table alternative has proper `<th>`/`scope`; the coverage banner is an `aria-live="polite"` region so a coverage change (cohort/holdings update) is announced; SyntheticTag and CoverageBadge expose their text to screen readers (not decorative).
- **Tabular figures:** all numbers in `mono`/tabular so columns in the table and HHI strip align (PART 2.3).

**Responsive recap (from §3):** ≥1280 three-region; 768–1279 two stacked bands (delta becomes a chip row, no horizontal scroll on the treemap — tiles reflow taller); <768 single column with the treemap defaulting to a square low-tile-count render *and surfacing "view as table"* prominently, coverage banner sticky. No analytical view requires horizontal scroll (PART 0.2 / PART 10). Light/dark/hybrid via the token sets (treemap `--data-*` and `--gated` have dark-mode parallels; the hatched wedge and dashed peer overlay remain legible in both).

**i18n:** English + German; band words, the assumption note, and coverage copy are externalised strings (no hard-coded text); `%`, `€`, and EU number formatting localized (covered/total € in the provenance popover).

**Performance:** dim-switch and ComparisonSelector use the partial `&dim=`/`&compare=` fetch and re-render only the affected component; the HHI strip and treemap fills (you-only) never re-render on a comparison change; calm skeletons on load.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

**Coverage (G4) — the headline requirement:**
- [ ] **Coverage % renders on EVERY look-through figure** — the top banner, every treemap tile, Sector/Geo HHI rows, the Look-through AI cell, the delta-column foot, the company-overlap card, the theme block. A look-through number without a CoverageBadge is a fail.
- [ ] Manager and Vintage HHI render **"cov 100% (full book)"** — visibly distinct from the coverage-limited figures, so the user understands they don't depend on look-through.
- [ ] The **uncovered wedge** (`uncovered_pct`) renders as a greyed/hatched tile whenever > 0; the treemap **never renormalizes to 100%** and the UI **never implies 100% coverage** in any state.
- [ ] The Sector treemap tiles sum to ≈100 over the *covered slice* (covered-only normalization), with the uncovered wedge shown alongside — the two together reading as "covered + uncovered = your whole book."

**Low-coverage state (the surface's defining state):**
- [ ] When `low_coverage:true`, the **coverage banner is the single most prominent element**, in the cautionary register, above the treemap title; the treemap is de-emphasised and the uncovered wedge dominates proportionally; manager/vintage HHI still render normally.
- [ ] At `coverage_pct == 0`, exposure/sector-geo-HHI/look-through-AI/overlap/theme render as `--gated` "0% of your book"; manager/vintage HHI and stated/embedded AI **still render**.

**AI (G5):**
- [ ] The AI card shows **three separate numbers** (Stated · Look-through · Embedded), never summed or averaged, in fixed order chosen→measured→modelled.
- [ ] **Embedded AI carries a SyntheticTag** ("illustrative") **and the assumption note** quoting the `ai_embed_pct` table + version; its ProvenancePopover leads with `label:"ASSUMPTION — not measured"`.
- [ ] Look-through AI carries its CoverageBadge; Stated AI carries no coverage badge (it's a stated allocation).
- [ ] The AI-assumption-only state (look-through 0%, embedded > 0) surfaces the divergence in words, never blank, never zero-filled silently.

**Company-overlap (k-anon):**
- [ ] Companies with `k_anon_ok:true` show the real name and (on click) `fund_ids`; companies with `k_anon_ok:false` show the **bucket label verbatim** ("a {sector} company"), no name, no fund_ids, inert, with the k-anon tooltip.
- [ ] Single-fund companies are **absent** from the overlap list (but present in the treemap); the empty and all-bucketed states render their exact copy.

**Comparison & deltas (G2):**
- [ ] ComparisonSelector (Peers · Best · Model) updates the **delta column** and the **peer overlay**; the cohort is labelled "Peers," never "All."
- [ ] Non-significant deltas render **greyed** with "not significant"; significant deltas solid; sorted by |delta|.
- [ ] The delta column carries a CoverageBadge at `min` of both sides' coverage.

**Provenance, badges, frame:**
- [ ] Every numeric element is a ProvenancePopover chip — no bare numbers (PART 0.2).
- [ ] Hover a tile → sector % + coverage % (+ peer % and Δ when overlay on).
- [ ] All seven states render (happy, cold-start, single-fund, low-coverage, AI-assumption-only, k-anon-suppressed, error) — none is a broken screen; cold-start routes to S9.
- [ ] Treemap exposes the **"view as table"** affordance; over/under is glyph+sign, not colour-only; coverage warning survives greyscale.
- [ ] Cohort change re-renders only the delta column + peer overlay (+ RelaxBanner in words if relaxed); PDF export captures the current dim/compare state with coverage and SyntheticTag intact.
- [ ] Placeholder data matches the `GET /api/detail/lookthrough` shape (§5) so the live swap is a base-URL change.


---

## Surface S7 — Performance

> **Frontend surface spec for Claude Design.** This is the deep build brief for the **Performance** detail surface (use case **T1-12**). It inherits everything in the Frontend Foundation (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`) — the global frame (Part 3), the design system and tokens (Part 2), the **PerfBars** component (Part 2.6), the locked chart language (Part 2.5), the seven-state model (Part 9), and the provenance/confidence/coverage badge contract (Part 0.2, 2.6). **Do not redefine those here.** This document specifies how S7 composes them, what it binds to, exactly how every state renders, and the acceptance bar S7 must clear. Where the foundation already locks a behaviour (e.g. "numbers inside the bars," "Peers not All," "no flashy transitions," "% inline"), this spec assumes it and builds on top.

The governing discipline of S7, distilled from the T1-12 brief: **performance is vintage-honest, realized-vs-marks-honest, and k-anon-honest.** A 2024-vintage fund and a 2017-vintage fund are never ranked against each other; an IRR that the engine has not let mature is shown as a *note*, not a number; realized cash (DPI) and unrealized marks (RVPI) are visually separable at a glance; and no fund is named below the k-anon floor. The number comes from the engine; the UI presents it and the chat explains it.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** S7 answers, calmly and at a glance: *"How is my private-markets portfolio actually performing — in realized cash and in marks — relative to people like me and to the platform, judged fairly by vintage?"* It is the surface a doctor (not a PE professional) opens when they want to know whether their money is doing well, without being misled by a flattering blended IRR or by a young vintage that simply hasn't had time to return capital yet.

S7 is the **per-vintage performance comparison** view. It is deliberately *not* the full attribution console (the NAV bridge, contributor/detractor ranking, PME, sector attribution from the T1-12 brief live in the richer Attribution surface and in Ask Alpha answers). S7 is the **headline performance read**: three metrics (DPI, MOIC, IRR), three series (You / Peers / Platform), broken out per vintage, with the young-vintage and realized-vs-marks honesty baked into the visual grammar. Keeping S7 focused is what lets it stay a no-scroll, one-glance Detail page consistent with the rest of the product.

**The questions S7 must let the user answer without reading a manual:**

1. **"Am I getting my money back?"** → DPI per vintage, You vs Peers vs Platform, with the realized portion visually distinct from marks.
2. **"What's my total return on a vintage, cash plus marks?"** → MOIC (= DPI + RVPI) per vintage, with the realized (DPI) base and the marked (RVPI) top rendered as two visually distinct segments of the same bar.
3. **"What's my IRR — and where is it even meaningful yet?"** → IRR per vintage where the vintage is mature enough; **"too early to rank"** with the engine's note where it isn't. Never a fabricated or zero IRR for a young vintage.
4. **"How do I compare to people like me?"** → the Peers series sitting side-by-side with You on every bar group, same vintage only.
5. **"How do I compare to the platform overall?"** → the Platform series, same vintage only.
6. **"Am I top-tier or middle of the pack?"** → the optional **You vs Top 10%** toggle, which swaps the comparison series to the top-decile DPI for that vintage. Off by default (the default comparison is the broad peer median, which is the honest baseline).
7. **"Is this vintage old enough to judge at all?"** → the **"comparable-vintage" caption** plus the per-vintage maturity treatment make clear which vintages are rankable and which are still in the J-curve / too-early zone.

**Explicit non-goals for S7 (handled elsewhere, link out, do not duplicate):** the reconciling NAV bridge, period-over-period quarter/year decomposition, named contributor/detractor ranking, J-curve detractor lists, PME vs public benchmark, and sector/geography attribution. S7 may surface a single "See full attribution →" affordance that deep-links to the Attribution surface (and Ask Alpha can answer "what drove this?" inline), but S7 itself stays the clean comparative read. This keeps S7's promise — *one glance, vintage-honest* — intact.

---

### §2 Entry & nav

**Rail entry.** S7 is **"Performance"** in the left navigation rail (Part 3 order: *… Vintage · Look-through · **Performance** · KPIs · Architect …*). Selecting it switches the centre stage instantly, full-screen, no scroll; the persistent frame (ProfileBar, CohortBuilder rail, Ask Alpha pane, PDF export) stays put. Switching is a stage swap, not a page load.

**Deep-link entries.**
- From **S1 Overview** or **S8 KPIs**, a "How am I performing?" / "See performance" affordance routes here.
- From **Ask Alpha**, an answer to "Am I getting my money back?" / "How's my DPI vs peers?" renders an inline mini-PerfBars and offers "Open the Performance view →" which lands on S7 with the relevant metric tab pre-selected and (if the question named a vintage) the **vintage filter** pre-applied to that vintage.
- From **S5 Vintage**, clicking a vintage in the vintage exposure chart can deep-link to S7 with that vintage pre-filtered (so the user goes from "how much did I commit in 2021" to "how is 2021 performing").
- From the **Next Best Action** rationale chain, "underdelivering on the 2021 sleeve" can link here, vintage-filtered, metric = DPI.

**State on entry.** Default landing config: **metric tab = DPI**, **comparison = Peers** (not Top 10%), **vintage filter = All vintages**. The surface remembers the last-used metric tab and comparison toggle within a session (UI state only — does not persist server-side). Deep-links override the defaults with their carried parameters.

**Exit / lateral moves.** The metric tabs (DPI / MOIC / IRR) and the vintage filter keep the user inside S7. The only outbound affordances are the global ones (rail, Ask Alpha, PDF export) plus the single "See full attribution →" link described in §1. No accidental navigation away from the comparison.

---

### §3 Layout

**Frame.** S7 renders inside the standard Detail template (Part 3): ProfileBar on top, CohortBuilder rail left (fixed when open), Ask Alpha floating bottom-right, PDF export available. The centre stage is the S7 body. **No vertical or horizontal scroll** on the analytical body at the desktop breakpoint — it is a single full screen, Excel-sheet style.

**Centre-stage composition (desktop ≥1280px).** Top-down:

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Performance                                          [See full attribution →]│  ← h1 title row
│  Realized cash vs marks, judged by vintage.                                   │  ← subtitle (caption size)
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [ DPI ]  [ MOIC ]  [ IRR ]        Compare to: ( Peers ) [ Top 10% ]         │  ← metric tabs + comparison toggle
│                                    Vintage: [ All ▾ ]  Legend: ◧ realized ▢ marks│
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                               │
│   1.8 ┤                                                                        │
│       │   ▆You ▅Peers ▄Platform   ▆ ▅ ▄        ▆ ▅ ▄       ╎too early╎        │  ← PerfBars (vertical)
│   1.2 ┤   [1.42][1.31][1.28]      [..][..][..]  [..][..][..]  to rank          │
│       │      ████  ███   ███        ███ ██  ██     ██ █   █                     │
│   0.6 ┤      ████  ███   ███  ◧realized base ███ ██  ██     ██ █   █             │
│       │      ████  ███   ███   ▢marks top    ███ ██  ██     ██ █   █             │
│   0.0 ┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────     │
│            2017          2019          2021          2023        2024          │  ← vintage axis (x)
│                                                                               │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Comparable vintages only — each group compares same-vintage funds. Younger    │
│  vintages return capital later; we don't rank them until there is enough        │  ← CaptionBlock (1–2 sentences)
│  history.  n=37 peers · ±0.06  ·  [show how this was computed]                  │  ← ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**The chart is vertical PerfBars (Part 2.5 / 2.6), grouped by vintage along the x-axis.** Each vintage year on the x-axis owns a **group of side-by-side bars**: **You · Peers · Platform** (three bars), rendered in the fixed You/Peers/Platform encoding from the foundation. When the **Top 10%** toggle is on, the Platform/Peers comparison bar set is augmented or swapped to show the **Top-decile** series for DPI (see §4, §6). The numeric value of each bar is rendered **inside the bar** (foundation rule), in tabular mono, oriented to read at boardroom-projector distance.

**Series are side-by-side, never behind a toggle.** You, Peers, and Platform are always all three visible together within each vintage group (foundation Part 0.2: "comparisons that matter are side-by-side, not behind a toggle, unless the surface says toggle"). The only thing the user *toggles* on S7 is (a) the metric (DPI/MOIC/IRR — these are genuinely different scales so they're separate tabs, not co-plotted), (b) the Top-10% comparison overlay, and (c) the vintage filter. The You/Peers/Platform comparison itself is always co-present.

**Comparable-vintage caption.** Directly under the chart, the **CaptionBlock** states the comparable-vintage principle in plain words and names the peer n + CI for the currently selected metric/vintage scope. This caption is mandatory and is part of the honesty contract: it tells the doctor *why* groups are not compared across the x-axis.

**Young vintages show "too early to rank," not a number.** For any vintage where the engine returns a maturity signal that suppresses the metric (IRR `null` with an `irr_note`, or a vintage too young to have meaningful DPI), that vintage group does **not** render a bar with a fabricated value. Instead it renders a **muted, dashed "too early to rank" placeholder column** carrying the engine's note (see §4, §7). It is visually present (so the user sees the vintage exists and is deliberately not ranked) but visually *quiet* and clearly non-numeric.

**DPI (realized) vs RVPI (marks) are visually distinct.** This is the single most important visual encoding on S7 and applies primarily in the **MOIC** tab, where each bar = DPI base + RVPI top:
- The **realized (DPI) base segment** is the **solid, fully saturated** lower portion of the bar — it represents cash actually returned.
- The **marks (RVPI) top segment** is rendered in a **distinct lighter / hatched treatment** (a diagonal-hatch fill at reduced opacity over the same series colour) — it represents unrealized value that is a manager's estimate, not cash in hand.
- A compact legend (`◧ realized · ▢ marks`) sits in the controls row; the segment boundary is a thin `--line` hairline so the realized/marked split is legible even in greyscale and on a projector.
- In the **DPI** tab the bars are realized-only (solid). In the **IRR** tab there is no realized/marks split (IRR is a rate, not a multiple), so the hatch treatment is absent and the "too early" suppression dominates.

**Responsive.**
- **≥1280px:** three-column frame as drawn; full vintage axis; all three series per group.
- **768–1279px:** CohortBuilder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher (foundation Part 3). The PerfBars chart keeps the vintage groups but **thins bar widths** and, if vintages exceed the comfortable count, enables a **horizontal vintage pager** (chunks of vintages, prev/next) rather than horizontal scroll — *no analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll* (foundation Part 10). Metric tabs and toggles wrap to a second control row.
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go):** single column. The vintage groups **reflow to a vertical stack** — one card per vintage, each card showing the You/Peers/Platform bars as short horizontal mini-bars with numbers inside, plus the maturity treatment. The metric tabs become a full-width segmented control; the comparison toggle and vintage filter become a sheet. The comparable-vintage caption sits under the stack. ProfileBar becomes a sheet. Numbers stay tabular and inside the bars at every breakpoint.

---

### §4 Components & charts

S7 is built almost entirely from foundation components. This section specifies the exact encodings S7 needs and the three S7-specific visual treatments (realized-vs-marks split, the "too early" placeholder, and the Top-10% overlay).

**PerfBars — full encoding on S7.**
- **Orientation:** vertical bars (foundation: performance is vertical PerfBars). X-axis = vintage year (ascending left→right). Y-axis = the metric's natural scale (DPI/MOIC as a multiple anchored at 0.0 with a `1.0×` reference hairline; IRR as a percentage anchored at 0%).
- **Grouping:** one **group per vintage**, three bars per group in fixed order **You · Peers · Platform**, fixed colours from the foundation series map (You = brand indigo, Peers = mint/teal tint, Platform = lighter blue tint), consistent across every Alpha surface so a series is always the same colour.
- **Numbers inside the bars:** each bar prints its value inside, near the top of the fill, in mono/tabular figures (`1.42`, `1.31`). If a bar is too short to hold its label legibly, the label floats just above the bar cap (still associated with that bar, never moved to a side legend). This preserves the foundation rule "% / numbers inline on bars, never only a side legend."
- **`1.0×` reference line:** for DPI and MOIC, a thin dashed `--line` hairline at `1.0×` marks the break-even (got-your-money-back) threshold — a quiet, high-value cue for the non-technical user. Absent on the IRR tab.
- **Thin bars, not fat** (foundation Part 2.5). Subtle value-in animation once, then settle; no bounce (foundation Part 2.4).
- **Caption:** the mandatory CaptionBlock under the chart (§3, §8).
- **Badges:** ConfidenceBadge (peer n + CI for the metric) and ProvenancePopover sit with the caption; CoverageBadge appears only where a metric is coverage-bound (not typical for DPI/MOIC/IRR, which are not look-through-derived — so CoverageBadge is usually absent on S7, unlike S6).

**The realized-vs-marks visual split (MOIC tab).**
- Each **You** MOIC bar is a stacked composition: **DPI base (solid, saturated brand indigo)** + **RVPI top (same indigo at ~45% opacity with a 45° diagonal hatch)**. The total bar height = MOIC = DPI + RVPI.
- The **Peers** and **Platform** MOIC bars use the same split logic in their own series colours (peer DPI base solid mint, peer RVPI top hatched mint; platform likewise) so the split is comparable across series.
- Inside each bar, **two numbers** are printed when space allows: the DPI value inside the solid base and the RVPI value inside the hatched top (e.g. base `1.10`, top `+0.32`), with the MOIC total floated at the bar cap (`1.42×`). When space is tight, only the MOIC total renders inside and the DPI/RVPI split surfaces on hover and in the ProvenancePopover.
- A one-line inline note next to the legend on the MOIC tab: *"The lighter, hatched portion is unrealized marks — estimates, not cash returned."* This is the plain-language explanation of why the split exists.

**The "too early to rank" treatment.**
- Triggered per-vintage-per-series when the engine suppresses the metric: **IRR `null`** carries an `irr_note`; a vintage younger than the rankable threshold likewise suppresses DPI/MOIC ranking.
- The group renders a **dashed, muted "ghost column"** at a fixed low height (purely a placeholder footprint — it carries **no numeric meaning**, so it is visually disconnected from the y-axis value scale by being dashed and de-saturated to `--muted`).
- Inside / beneath the ghost column: the label **"too early to rank"** and, on hover or directly beneath, the engine's note text (e.g. *"IRR not shown before 4 years — too few cashflows to be meaningful"* from `irr_note`). The note is the engine's words, surfaced verbatim where provided.
- The vintage's x-axis tick still renders (the vintage exists and is acknowledged), so the user sees a complete, honest timeline rather than a silently dropped column.
- **Never** zero-fill, never render `0.00×` or `0%` for a suppressed metric, never let a suppressed vintage participate in any ranking, max/min highlighting, or "best/worst" copy.

**The "You vs Top 10%" toggle.**
- A segmented control in the controls row: **`( Peers )` | `[ Top 10% ]`**, default **Peers**.
- **Peers** (default): the comparison bars are the broad peer median (`peer_dpi` and the corresponding peer MOIC/IRR for the metric). This is the honest, representative baseline.
- **Top 10%**: the comparison series swaps to **`top_decile_dpi`** for the DPI metric (and the analogous top-decile field for MOIC where available). The You bar is unchanged; the comparison bar's colour shifts to a distinct "top-decile" treatment (a darker mint with a small `★`/decile marker) and the caption updates to *"Compared to the top 10% of same-vintage funds."* When Top-10% data is unavailable for a vintage, that comparison bar shows the standard "not enough data" muted treatment rather than falling back silently to peer median.
- The toggle affects **only the comparison series**, never the You series, and never crosses vintages.

**Vintage filter.**
- A dropdown/segmented control **`Vintage: [ All ▾ ]`** listing **All** plus each vintage year present in the response (derived from the row set).
- **All** (default): every vintage group renders along the x-axis.
- A single vintage: the chart focuses to that one vintage group, enlarged, with You/Peers/Platform (and Top-10% if toggled) and, on the MOIC tab, the realized/marks split shown at large size — useful for the deep "how is my 2021 sleeve doing" read and for PDF export of a single vintage.
- The filter and the metric tabs are independent (any metric × any vintage scope). The filter never invents a vintage that isn't in the data and never merges vintages.

**Metric tabs.**
- **DPI** (default), **MOIC**, **IRR** as a three-segment tab control. They are tabs (not co-plotted) because they are different units (multiple vs multiple vs rate) and co-plotting would force a misleading shared axis. Switching tabs re-renders only the chart + caption + badges, not the frame.

---

### §5 Data bindings

S7 binds to **`GET /api/detail/performance`** (foundation Part 0.3 / S7 entry; `investor_id` optional query param). The response is **per-vintage rows**, each shaped:

```jsonc
{
  "vintage_year": 2019,
  "you_dpi":        { "value": 1.42, "provenance": { ... } },
  "you_rvpi":       { "value": 0.31, "provenance": { ... } },
  "you_irr":        { "value": 0.184, "provenance": { ... } } | null,
  "peer_dpi":       { "value": 1.31, "provenance": { ... } },
  "top_decile_dpi": { "value": 1.78, "provenance": { ... } },
  "irr_note":       "IRR not shown before 4 years — too few cashflows to be meaningful" | null
}
```

(Per the foundation contract, every numeric value arrives as `{ value, provenance:{ method, n, ci95:[lo,hi], coverage_pct, significant, synthetic, vintage_scope, snapshot_id } }`. Bind **`value`** to the visual, **`provenance`** to the badge/popover. The shape above abbreviates the provenance object for readability.)

**Element ← field map:**

| Visual element | Bound field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Vintage group (x-tick) | `row.vintage_year` | One group per row; ascending. Rows with `vintage_year == null` are excluded (cannot be placed on a vintage axis) and counted in a "k unattributed vintages" footnote. |
| **DPI tab** — You bar | `row.you_dpi.value` | Solid (realized cash). |
| **DPI tab** — Peers bar | `row.peer_dpi.value` | Comparison series, default. |
| **DPI tab** — Platform bar | *Platform DPI series* | If a platform field is present in the live shape, bind it; if the placeholder shape carries only Peers + Top-decile, render You + Peers (+ Top-10% when toggled) and omit the Platform bar gracefully rather than zero-fill. **Never invent a Platform value.** |
| **DPI tab** — Top-10% comparison bar | `row.top_decile_dpi.value` | Rendered only when the Top-10% toggle is on. |
| **MOIC tab** — You bar (base) | `row.you_dpi.value` | Realized base segment (solid). |
| **MOIC tab** — You bar (top) | `row.you_rvpi.value` | Marks segment (hatched). MOIC total = `you_dpi.value + you_rvpi.value`, computed in the UI from the two engine-provided components (so the split is always exact and reconciles). |
| **MOIC tab** — Peers / Platform | peer DPI (+ peer RVPI if present) | If peer RVPI is not in the shape, render the peer MOIC bar as DPI-only solid with a hover note "peer marks not available," never hatch a value that wasn't returned. |
| **IRR tab** — You bar | `row.you_irr.value` | **Only when `you_irr != null`.** |
| **IRR tab** — suppressed | `row.you_irr == null` → ghost column | Render "too early to rank" + `row.irr_note` (verbatim). See §7. |
| Maturity note text | `row.irr_note` | Surfaced verbatim in the too-early treatment; if `irr_note` is null but `you_irr` is also null, fall back to the generic "too early to rank — not enough history" copy (§8). |
| ConfidenceBadge | `provenance.n`, `provenance.ci95` | Of the currently displayed metric/series (peer n + CI is the typical badge). |
| Greyed / not-significant | `provenance.significant == false` | A bar whose delta to the comparison is not significant renders greyed with the standard "not significant" note (foundation Part 2.5, Part 9). |
| SyntheticTag | `provenance.synthetic == true` | Amber "illustrative" pill on any synthetic value (foundation). |
| ProvenancePopover | full `provenance` of the clicked bar | cohort + n + fields + method + snapshot. |
| Caption peer n | `peer_dpi.provenance.n` (or active metric) | Drives the "n=37 peers" in the caption. |

**Honour `irr_note` / `null` — the binding rules that are non-negotiable:**
1. **`you_irr == null` ⇒ no IRR bar.** Render the too-early treatment with `irr_note`. Do **not** coerce null to 0, do not interpolate, do not borrow another vintage's IRR.
2. **`irr_note` present ⇒ surface it verbatim** in the too-early placeholder (it is the engine's authoritative reason, e.g. the `MIN_MATURE_VINTAGE`/4-year suppression from the T1-12 brief).
3. **Each metric is independent per vintage** — a vintage may have a valid DPI but a null IRR; render the DPI bar normally on the DPI tab and the too-early treatment on the IRR tab for that same vintage. The maturity verdict is per-metric, per-vintage.
4. **No cross-vintage value carrying.** A bar's value comes only from its own vintage row.
5. **Cold-start / no holdings** (`n_funds == 0` or no rows) → S7 renders the cold-start state (§7) and routes to Architect; it does not draw an empty axis.

---

### §6 Interactions

All interactions re-render **only the affected chart + caption + badges**, never the whole frame (foundation performance rule). Motion ≤150ms ease, value-in once, no bounce.

**Metric tab switch (DPI ↔ MOIC ↔ IRR).** Re-renders the PerfBars to the selected metric: changes the y-axis scale and reference line, applies/removes the realized-vs-marks hatch (present on MOIC, absent on DPI/IRR), and re-evaluates the too-early treatment per vintage for that metric (a vintage rankable on DPI may be too-early on IRR). The caption updates to name the metric. The active tab is visually distinct (foundation focus states). Remembered within the session.

**You vs Top 10% toggle.** Swaps the comparison series between peer median and `top_decile_dpi` (and analogous top-decile for the metric where available). Only the comparison bar changes — the You bar holds. The comparison bar's colour/marker shifts to the top-decile treatment and the caption rewrites to "Compared to the top 10% of same-vintage funds." Toggling back restores Peers. If a vintage lacks top-decile data, that comparison bar shows the muted "not enough data" treatment for the duration the toggle is on (never a silent peer-median substitution).

**Vintage filter.** Selecting **All** shows every vintage group; selecting a single vintage focuses and enlarges that one group (with the full series set and, on MOIC, the enlarged realized/marks split). The filter is independent of the metric tab and the Top-10% toggle, which both retain their state across a filter change. Deep-links may pre-set the filter.

**Hover.**
- Hovering a **bar** raises a tooltip with: vintage year, series (You / Peers / Platform / Top 10%), the exact value (more precision than the in-bar label), and — on the MOIC tab — the DPI / RVPI split (e.g. `DPI 1.10 · RVPI 0.32 · MOIC 1.42×`). The tooltip restates that marks are unrealized estimates.
- Hovering a **too-early ghost column** reveals the full `irr_note` text and the vintage's maturity context (e.g. "vintage 2024 · age 1y · IRR suppressed until 4y").
- Hovering the **realized/marks legend** explains the encoding in one line.

**Provenance.** Every bar is a click target → **ProvenancePopover** (foundation): cohort, peer n, CI95, the fields and method behind the metric, the snapshot id, and any `synthetic`/`perf_is_proxy` flags. A number on S7 is never bare — clicking it always opens "show the data." On the MOIC tab the popover separates the DPI provenance and the RVPI provenance (different engine bases — realized cashflows vs marks). The caption's "show how this was computed" link opens the popover for the currently active metric/scope.

**Cohort change (left rail).** When the user edits the CohortBuilder (foundation Part 6), the **Peers** and **Top-10%** comparison series re-resolve and re-render; the **You** series is identity-invariant and does not change. If the new cohort is thin, the RelaxBanner appears (foundation) and the caption updates to the relaxed-dimension wording. If over-granular, the comparison series enters the gated state (§7). The vintage axis (driven by the user's own holdings) is unaffected by cohort changes.

**PDF export.** Exports the current S7 view — selected metric, comparison toggle, vintage scope — as a clean PowerPoint-style PDF (foundation PDFExportButton), caption and badges included, suppressed vintages rendered as "too early to rank" in the export too (the honesty travels with the document).

**Ask Alpha cross-talk.** Asking a performance question in the pane renders an inline mini-PerfBars and can drive S7 (e.g. "show me 2021" sets the vintage filter). Numbers in Alpha answers are provenance chips, consistent with S7's bars.

---

### §7 Seven states

S7 renders all seven foundation states (Part 9). The S7-critical ones are **null/young** (the whole reason this surface exists) and **thin cohort**; the others inherit their standard treatment.

1. **Happy** — full data, significant deltas. All vintages rankable, You/Peers/Platform side-by-side, DPI/MOIC/IRR all available, MOIC showing the realized/marks split, comparable-vintage caption with a healthy peer n. The reference example state.

2. **Cold-start** — `n_funds == 0` / no rows (the T1-12 cold-start branch returns `{cold_start:true}`). S7 does **not** draw an empty axis. It shows a calm cold-start panel: *"Your performance will appear here once you hold funds."* plus the peer/model context where available, and a CTA routing to **S9 Allocation Architect**. Never a broken analytics screen.

3. **Thin cohort** — peer baseline below threshold (or all peers `< PEER_MIN` for a vintage). The **RelaxBanner** appears in words (foundation, from `relaxed_dims`) and the caption reflects the relaxed dimensions. Where a *single vintage* has too few same-vintage peers, that vintage's comparison bars show the muted "no peer baseline for this vintage" treatment while the You bar still renders — the user always sees their own number even when the comparison can't be drawn. The peer baseline here is vintage×strategy honest, so relaxation is shown but the comparison is never silently widened across vintages.

4. **Low coverage** — generally **not applicable** to S7's DPI/MOIC/IRR (these are not look-through-derived, so CoverageBadge is normally absent). The one exception: if a value carries a coverage_pct < 100 in its provenance, the CoverageBadge renders on that bar's popover with "based on X% of NAV." S7 never *implies* full coverage where the engine reports partial.

5. **Null / insufficient (THE S7 SIGNATURE STATE)** — `you_irr == null` (and/or a vintage too young to rank on DPI/MOIC). The vintage renders the **"too early to rank"** ghost column carrying the engine's `irr_note` verbatim (or the generic fallback copy when `irr_note` is null). **Never zero-fill, never `0.00×`/`0%`, never rank a suppressed vintage.** This state is treated as first-class and styled deliberately (muted, dashed, note-bearing), not as an error or a gap. It is what makes S7 vintage-honest.

6. **Gated** — cohort over-granular → the comparison series (Peers / Top-10%) renders the **GatedState** card with the exact copy "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate" and the "5,500 total investors" headline (foundation). The You series still renders where the user's own data permits; only the comparison is gated. Never "too small," never "I can't."

7. **Error / degraded** — engine/model fault → cached last-good S7 with a quiet "showing last available figures" banner; if no cache, a calm empty state, never a blank screen and never a fabricated number. Ask Alpha falls back gracefully.

**Cross-cutting:** not-significant deltas render greyed with the "not significant" note (`provenance.significant == false`); SyntheticTag on any `synthetic == true` value; `perf_is_proxy` (from the T1-12 holding shape) surfaces a "based on a fund-level proxy" disclosure in the popover so a verdict built on a proxy is never asserted as hard fact; loading = calm skeleton bars, not spinners.

---

### §8 Copy deck

All copy is structured for **English + German** (foundation Part 10); no hard-coded strings; numbers/currency EU-localized. Tone: plain, calm, "a doctor, not a pro." Never prescriptive (info-not-advice).

**Title & subtitle**
- Title (h1): **"Performance"**
- Subtitle (caption): **"Realized cash vs marks, judged by vintage."**

**Metric tab labels**
- `DPI` — tab; hover-def: *"Distributions to Paid-In — the cash actually returned to you, as a multiple of what you put in. 1.0× means you've gotten your money back."*
- `MOIC` — tab; hover-def: *"Multiple on Invested Capital — total value (cash returned plus current marks) per euro invested."*
- `IRR` — tab; hover-def: *"Internal Rate of Return — your annualized return. We only show it once a vintage is old enough for it to mean something."*

**Comparable-vintage caption (the mandatory CaptionBlock, varies by metric/toggle):**
- DPI, Peers: *"Comparable vintages only — each year compares funds of the same vintage. You've returned {you_dpi}× on your {vintage} funds, against a {peer_dpi}× peer median. Younger vintages return capital later, so we don't rank them yet."*
- MOIC, Peers: *"Each bar is realized cash (solid) plus current marks (hatched) — marks are estimates, not cash. Compared within the same vintage only."*
- IRR, Peers: *"IRR shown only for vintages with enough history to be meaningful; younger vintages read 'too early to rank.' Same-vintage comparison only."*
- Top-10% on (any metric): append *"Compared to the top 10% of same-vintage funds."*
- General fallback: *"Comparable vintages only — we never rank a young vintage against an old one."*

**Realized-vs-marks inline note (MOIC tab):**
- *"The lighter, hatched portion is unrealized marks — estimates, not cash returned."*
- Legend chips: `◧ realized` / `▢ marks`

**"Too early to rank" copy:**
- Primary label: **"Too early to rank"**
- With `irr_note` present: surface the engine's note verbatim, e.g. *"IRR not shown before 4 years — too few cashflows to be meaningful."*
- Generic fallback (when `irr_note` is null): *"Too early to rank — not enough history yet for this vintage."*
- Hover context: *"Vintage {year} · age {n}y · {metric} suppressed until this vintage matures."*
- Crucially never: "0.00×", "0%", "—", "N/A" styled as a value, or any phrasing that implies poor performance. The young vintage is *unjudged*, not *bad*.

**Top-10% toggle:**
- Control labels: `Peers` / `Top 10%`
- On-state caption clause: *"Compared to the top 10% of same-vintage funds."*
- No top-decile data for a vintage: *"Not enough data to identify the top 10% for this vintage."*

**State copy**
- Cold-start: *"Your performance will appear here once you hold funds."* + CTA *"Build your target allocation →"* (to Architect).
- Thin (per-vintage no peers): *"No peer baseline for this vintage yet."*
- Gated comparison: *"Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate."* + *"5,500 total investors."*
- Error/degraded: *"Showing last available figures."*
- Not-significant: *"Difference not statistically significant."*
- Synthetic value: SyntheticTag pill *"illustrative."*
- Proxy disclosure (popover): *"Based on a fund-level proxy."*

**Microcopy / examples**
- *"You've gotten back 1.42× on your 2019 funds — ahead of the 1.31× peer median."*
- *"Your 2024 vintage is too young to rank — that's normal; it hasn't had time to return capital."*

---

### §9 Badge rendering

S7 uses the foundation badge contract verbatim (Part 0.2, 2.6). Per-surface specifics:

- **ConfidenceBadge** (`n=37 peers · ±0.06`): rendered with the caption, reflecting the active metric's peer cohort (`provenance.n`, `provenance.ci95`). When a single vintage is filtered, the badge shows that vintage's peer n/CI. When a value's `significant == false`, the corresponding bar is greyed and the badge carries the "not significant" qualifier.
- **CoverageBadge** (`based on X% of NAV`): **normally absent** on S7 (DPI/MOIC/IRR are not look-through-derived). Rendered only if a bound value's `provenance.coverage_pct < 100` — then it appears on that bar's popover. S7 must never imply full coverage where partial is reported.
- **SyntheticTag** (amber "illustrative"): on any bar whose value `provenance.synthetic == true` (e.g. a synthetic snapshot). Visible on the bar, never styled as verified fact.
- **ProvenancePopover** (click any number): cohort + peer n + CI95 + fields + method + snapshot id. On the MOIC tab it separates DPI provenance (realized cashflows) from RVPI provenance (marks). Surfaces `perf_is_proxy` as a "based on a fund-level proxy" disclosure when set.
- **"Too early" is not a badge — it is a state.** The maturity suppression is rendered as the ghost-column state (§4/§7), not as a badge over a real value, because there is no value to badge. (Do not attempt to put a ConfidenceBadge on a suppressed metric.)
- **Badge placement:** badges cluster under the chart with the caption (one row), not scattered per-bar, to keep the no-scroll boardroom-clean layout; per-bar detail lives in hover + popover. Every visible number remains one click from its provenance.

---

### §10 Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive** (recap of §3, with the analytical guarantees):
- ≥1280px: full vintage axis, three series per group, no scroll.
- 768–1279px: collapsed rail/Ask-Alpha; thinner bars; **vintage pager** if needed (never horizontal scroll); controls wrap.
- <768px: vintage groups reflow to a **vertical stack of per-vintage cards** (horizontal mini-bars, numbers inside); tabs become a full-width segmented control; toggle/filter become sheets. No analytical chart requires horizontal scroll at any breakpoint (foundation Part 10).

**Accessibility (WCAG-AA, foundation Part 10):**
- **Never colour-only.** You/Peers/Platform are distinguished by position (fixed order) and by an in-bar/legend text label, not colour alone. The realized-vs-marks split is encoded by **fill pattern (solid vs diagonal hatch) + a hairline boundary + text labels**, so it survives greyscale and colour-blindness — not by colour alone. Over/under and significance pair colour with a glyph/sign.
- **The "too early" state is conveyed in text** ("Too early to rank" + the note), not by colour/shape alone — a screen reader announces the suppression and its reason.
- **Chart data behind a "view data" affordance:** the full per-vintage table (vintage, You DPI, You RVPI, You IRR or "too early", Peer DPI, Top-decile DPI, note) is available as an accessible table — the same data the bars encode, satisfying the foundation "chart data available as a table" rule and giving keyboard/screen-reader users the complete picture.
- **Full keyboard navigation:** tab through metric tabs, the comparison toggle, the vintage filter, and each bar (bars are focusable; focus opens the same tooltip/popover content); visible focus states throughout.
- **ARIA:** metric tabs as a tablist; the comparison toggle and vintage filter as labelled controls; each bar with an `aria-label` reading "{series} {metric} for vintage {year}: {value}" or "vintage {year}: too early to rank, {note}"; the caption associated with the chart via `aria-describedby`.
- **Tabular figures** for all numbers (foundation typography) so columns and in-bar labels align; mono numerals.
- **Contrast:** in-bar number labels meet AA contrast against their bar fill (including against the lighter hatched RVPI segment — labels there sit on the hairline-bounded solid base or float above if contrast can't be met).
- **Motion:** respects reduced-motion; the single value-in animation is suppressed under `prefers-reduced-motion`.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

S7 is accepted when **all** of the following hold (the three brief-mandated invariants are listed first and are non-negotiable):

**The three load-bearing invariants**
- [ ] **No cross-vintage ranking.** Bars are only ever compared within a single vintage group; nothing on S7 (no "best," no max-highlight, no ordering, no Top-10% comparison) ever ranks one vintage's value against another's. The comparable-vintage caption states this in words.
- [ ] **IRR suppressed before maturity shows the note, not a number.** Any vintage with `you_irr == null` renders the "too early to rank" treatment carrying `irr_note` verbatim (or the generic fallback when `irr_note` is null). No vintage younger than the maturity threshold shows an IRR value; no zero-fill, no interpolation, no borrowed IRR.
- [ ] **DPI ≠ RVPI visually.** On the MOIC tab, realized cash (DPI base) and unrealized marks (RVPI top) are rendered as two visually distinct segments — solid vs hatched, with a hairline boundary and distinct labels — legible in greyscale. The "marks are estimates, not cash" note is present.

**Layout & chart**
- [ ] Vertical PerfBars, grouped by vintage; **You / Peers / Platform side-by-side within each group, no toggle** hiding any of the three.
- [ ] Numbers render **inside the bars** (mono/tabular), floating above only when a bar is too short — never relegated to a side legend.
- [ ] `1.0×` reference hairline present on DPI and MOIC tabs; absent on IRR.
- [ ] The mandatory comparable-vintage **CaptionBlock** is present and updates with metric / toggle / scope.
- [ ] No scroll on the analytical body at desktop; no horizontal scroll at any breakpoint (vintage pager / vertical stack instead).

**Data & honesty**
- [ ] All bars bind to `GET /api/detail/performance` per-vintage rows; `value`→visual, `provenance`→badge/popover.
- [ ] `null` IRR and `irr_note` honoured exactly (§5 rules 1–5); MOIC total computed in-UI from `you_dpi + you_rvpi` and reconciles.
- [ ] No fabricated Platform value when the field is absent; the bar is gracefully omitted, not zero-filled.
- [ ] Rows with `vintage_year == null` excluded from the axis and noted in a footnote, not placed arbitrarily.

**Interactions & states**
- [ ] Metric tabs, Top-10% toggle, and vintage filter each re-render only chart + caption + badges; states persist independently within a session.
- [ ] Top-10% toggle swaps only the comparison series (to `top_decile_dpi`), never the You series, never across vintages; shows "not enough data" when top-decile absent rather than silently substituting peer median.
- [ ] Cohort change re-resolves Peers/Top-10% only; You series invariant; thin → RelaxBanner; over-granular → GatedState with exact paid-viewers copy + "5,500 total investors."
- [ ] All seven states render; cold-start routes to Architect (no empty axis); the null/young "too early to rank" state is first-class and styled (not an error/gap).

**Badges, provenance, a11y**
- [ ] Every visible number is one click from its ProvenancePopover; none renders bare. MOIC popover separates DPI vs RVPI provenance; `perf_is_proxy` and `synthetic` disclosed.
- [ ] ConfidenceBadge (n + CI) on the caption; CoverageBadge only when coverage_pct < 100; not-significant bars greyed with note.
- [ ] No colour-only encoding (series by position+label; realized/marks by pattern+label); "too early" conveyed in text; full per-vintage data available behind "view data"; full keyboard nav + ARIA; reduced-motion respected.

**Brand & export**
- [ ] Fixed You/Peers/Platform colour map consistent with every other Alpha surface; calm motion (single value-in, no bounce); Moonfare look.
- [ ] PDF export reproduces the current metric/toggle/scope with caption, badges, and "too early to rank" treatment intact.


---

## Surface S8 — KPIs / Peer Behavior

> **Scope.** The deep frontend specification for one Alpha copilot surface: **S8 — KPIs / Peer Behavior** (use case **T2-3 — Peer Behavior & Activity Benchmarking**, plus the per-metric KPI detail drill-down). This surface answers *how peers behave in practice* — not where they allocate (that is S1/S2/S14) but how they act: ticket size, deal count, vintage pacing, current deployment mode, concentration. It binds to **`GET /api/detail/kpi`** and **`GET /api/activity`** (and **`POST /api/activity/cohort`** for cohort changes).
>
> **Reference, do not redefine.** This brief composes from the FRONTEND FOUNDATION (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`): the global frame (Part 3), the **Scorecard** component (Part 2.6 — "small-multiple stat cards, you vs peer median"), the **chart language** (Part 2.5), the three numeric badges — **ConfidenceBadge / CoverageBadge / SyntheticTag** + **ProvenancePopover** (Part 2.6), the **ComparisonSelector / cohort builder** (Part 6), **Ask Alpha** (Part 5), and the **seven-state model** (Part 9). Where this brief says "Scorecard," "RelaxBanner," "GatedState," or "ProvenancePopover," it means exactly the foundation component — only the S8-specific configuration is specified here. Tokens (`--brand`, `--data-*`, `--over`/`--under`, `--muted`, `--synthetic`, `--line`) are referenced, never re-invented.
>
> **The two load-bearing invariants for this surface (acceptance-critical):** **(1) the ticket headline is the MEDIAN, never the mean** — the mean is rendered only as a muted footnote, because tickets are power-law (gate G6); **(2) there is NO absolute-total hero number** — S8 leads with *relative/behavioral* figures (median ticket, # commitments, # vintages, mode, concentration), never a headline "€X total committed."

---

### §1 — Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** S8 is the *behavioral mirror*. Where S1 shows where you stand on allocation, S8 shows **how you act compared to people like you**: do you write bigger or smaller cheques, do more or fewer deals, spread them across more or fewer vintages, are you actively deploying or waiting, and are you concentrated or diversified. It turns the proprietary cross-investor behavior data into a small, calm grid of "You vs Peer-MEDIAN" comparisons that a non-technical investor ("a doctor, not a pro") reads in one glance, with a per-metric drill-down for anyone who wants the distribution behind a headline.

S8 deliberately frames behavior, not performance — it never ranks the user as "good" or "bad." A larger ticket is not better; a higher concentration is not worse. The copy is descriptive ("you write larger cheques than your peers, across fewer funds"), and every behavioral read is anchored to a **peer median**, not a target or model. The one place a "model" appears is the concentration baseline (the model-portfolio HHI), and even there it is a context line, not a goal.

**The seven user questions S8 must answer** (mapped to T2-3 eval ids → the binding that resolves them):

| # | User question (plain language) | Resolved by | Rendered in |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "What's the average ticket size in my cohort?" | `activity.ticket.median_eur` (headline) + `mean_eur` (footnote) | Ticket Scorecard + KPI detail |
| Q2 | "How many investments do people like me typically make?" | `activity.n_investments.median` | # Commitments Scorecard |
| Q3 | "Are my peers actively investing right now?" | `activity.current_mode.{distribution, peers_actively_investing_share}` | Behavior row — Mode indicator |
| Q4 | "Do PE professionals write larger tickets but fewer deals?" | `activity.segments.pe_professionals.vs_you` | Segment-contrast strip (ComparisonSelector) |
| Q5 | "Do family offices invest more gradually across vintages?" | `activity.segments.family_offices.vintage_spread` vs `people_like_you` + `pacing.series` | # Vintages Scorecard + segment strip |
| Q6 | "Am I concentrated or diversified vs my peers?" | `activity.concentration.{your_class, your_neff, more_concentrated_than_peers}` | Behavior row — Concentration gauge |
| Q7 | "How do the best performers behave differently?" | `activity.segments.best_performers.vs_you` | Segment-contrast strip |

The "average" in Q1 is answered with the **median** by design — the word "average" colloquially means "typical," and for a power-law distribution the median is the honest "typical." The mean is shown, labelled, as a footnote so the curious user can see the pull of the tail, but it never leads.

**What S8 is NOT.** It is not a performance screen (that is S7 — DPI/MOIC/IRR), not an allocation screen (S1/S2), and not a recommendation screen (S14/S20). It carries **no absolute hero** ("€2.4m committed across 12 funds" is forbidden as a headline). It carries no scrolling — like every analytical surface it is a single full-screen view inside the persistent frame.

---

### §2 — Entry & navigation

**Rail entry.** "KPIs" in the left navigation rail (Part 3 ordering: *… Look-through · Performance · **KPIs** · Architect …*). Selecting it swaps the centre stage to S8 instantly, full-screen, no scroll; the ProfileBar, cohort builder, and Ask Alpha pane persist unchanged.

**Deep-link entries.**
- From **Ask Alpha**: answers to Q1–Q7 render an inline mini-Scorecard or mode/concentration chip with a "See all KPIs →" affordance that routes to S8 with the relevant tile pre-focused (e.g. Q6 lands on S8 with the Concentration gauge expanded).
- From **S1 Overview**: a "How do my peers behave?" secondary link under the Strategy-Mix card deep-links to S8 (behavior is the natural follow-on to allocation).
- From **S5 Vintage** and **S10 Pacing**: a "# vintages vs peers" chip deep-links to S8 with the # Vintages Scorecard focused (the pacing surfaces own the *schedule*; S8 owns the *count comparison*).
- From the **KPI detail** card (§3.4): each Scorecard is itself a click-through to its single-metric drill-down (`/api/detail/kpi?metric=…`), which opens as an overlay panel on top of S8, not a new screen.
