3. **Cold-start renders the surface FULLY.** With `cold_start:true` the full target architecture renders against `current = ∅`: band, target BarRows on their markers, the N-fund forward timeline, core/satellite, both comparators — no empty/broken screen; the gap reads as headroom; the FE routes here from Welcome. *(Core S9 requirement.)*
4. **SyntheticTag on every assigned input** — target (A/B/C), risk (slider), wealth (EUR line when banded), horizon (when default). Real data (current series, FO book) carries no SyntheticTag.
5. **Build-to-N is a timeline of N funds over the horizon** — dots sum to `fund_count.n`, placed per `ramp_per_year`; horizon toggle reshapes it; core/satellite encoded as filled/hollow.
6. **Core/satellite renders as two distinct grouped blocks** with correct counts from `fund_count.core/.satellite` and the same strategy colours/ticks as Block B.
7. **Both comparators are side-by-side (not behind a toggle)** and expand in place; E1 shows the `capped` chip when `more_risk.capped_by_real_risk`; E2 shows `narrative_strategy {±max_divergence_ppt}pp` and `fo_pm_pct vs pct`.
8. **Horizon toggle adjusts the architecture** — re-fires the endpoint; Block C reshapes most, Block A may shift one grid step.
9. **Every binding maps to a JSON path in `/api/architect`** per §5; every number has a ProvenancePopover; house-view blocks show `house_view: true` / `model_source` in provenance.
10. **All seven states render** with the §7 visuals + §8 copy — cold-start (primary), thin/wealth-unknown (% not €, no invented EUR), low-coverage FO, risk-unknown (uncapped slider), gated FO (exact paid-viewer copy), error (cached last-good, no fabricated number).
11. **No-advice tone** — no second-person imperatives anywhere in the copy; info-not-advice register.
12. **A11y & responsive** — risk slider is a keyboard-operable ARIA slider with locked-stop cap handling and `aria-live` re-render announcement; timeline has a "view data" table; no chart needs horizontal scroll; over/under and deltas pair colour with glyph+sign; band has a descriptive aria-label.

---
*Build S9 to this brief. It is the first surface a new investor sees and the calmest expression of the proprietary-data advantage: a clear, banded, house-view architecture that re-renders honestly as the investor dials risk and horizon — premium, trustworthy, and simple.*


---

# Alpha — Frontend Surface Brief — S10 · Pacing *(Use case T1-4)*

> One surface, built to the **Frontend Foundation** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md` — design system Part 2, frame Part 3, states Part 9, a11y Part 10) and bound to the **backend contract** `GET /api/pacing` (deep brief `T1-04_Commitment_Pacing.md`). This brief does **not** redefine tokens, the global frame, the cohort builder, Ask Alpha, the component library, or the seven-state catalog — it composes them. It specifies exactly how the Pacing surface looks, binds, behaves, and degrades, with zero open decisions.

---

## Surface S10 — Pacing

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Pacing answers the *when* and *in what increments* question that follows the Architect (S9). The Architect (T1-1) fixes **what** the portfolio should become — the target PM %, the `build_to_n` fund count, the per-strategy model weights. Pacing (T1-4) is the schedule that gets there: how to spread *new* commitments over a 3- or 5-year horizon, by **year**, **vintage**, **strategy**, and **ticket size**, while (a) avoiding single-vintage bunching, (b) phasing each strategy toward its model weight, and (c) damping the J-curve trough in net cashflow. The surface presents a **plan over a horizon**, not a point estimate. It is prescriptive in content but never imperative in tone (info-not-advice; the payload carries no "you should" and neither does the UI).

The user is "a doctor, not a pro." The surface must read as a calendar of sensible, diversified increments — a year-by-year schedule a layperson can act on — with the analytical depth (HHI bunching, J-curve simulation, top-decile overlay) available but never blocking the one-glance answer.

**User questions this surface answers** (mapped to the backend eval ids `Q1–Q7`):

- **Q1 — "How should I spread my commitments over the next 3–5 years?"** → the stacked year-by-year schedule (`plan[]`), with a 3y/5y horizon toggle.
- **Q2 — "Am I too concentrated in the 2025/2026 vintages?"** → the bunching-flag chip + per-vintage verdicts driven by `current_vintage_exposure` (HHI vs even-HHI).
- **Q3 — "How many new commitments should I make next year?"** → the first plan bar's `n_commitments` (rendered as the inline "#" on the bar).
- **Q4 — "Should I use secondaries to accelerate build-out?"** → the J-curve panel's `secondary_acceleration` trigger card.
- **Q5 — "How do best performers pace compared with me?"** → the ghost-line peer overlay (top-decile pacing medians).
- **Q6 — "How much should I commit per strategy and by when?"** → the per-strategy phasing breakdown (stacked-bar segmentation + the year-hover breakdown).
- **Q7 — "What's my net cashflow going to look like as I build this?"** → the J-curve `net_by_year` mini ForecastBand under the schedule.

**Non-goals (out of scope on this surface; deep-link out, do not re-implement):** the target architecture itself (S9 Architect owns it; Pacing echoes `target` read-only), fund *selection* — which named funds fill each slot (S20 Next-Best-Action / S13 Funds), look-through (S6), liquidity-as-a-whole (S11 Liquidity owns the full 8-quarter ForecastBand; S10 shows only the build-out J-curve derived from the plan).

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

**Rail position.** "Pacing" sits in the left-rail surface group between **Architect** (S9) and **Liquidity** (S11) — the natural construct → schedule → cashflow sequence. Selecting it loads the surface full-screen into the centre stage; the global frame (ProfileBar top, CohortBuilder left, Ask Alpha float, PDF export) persists per Part 3. Switching is instant and **no-scroll**.

**Inbound deep-links (the surface must accept and honour these on mount):**

- **From S9 Architect** — the Architect's "build-to-N plan" block has a primary CTA **"Plan how to get there →"** that routes here, carrying nothing extra (Pacing re-reads `architect()` server-side via `/api/pacing`). This is the dominant entry path.
- **From S5 Vintage** — the vintage surface's bunching-flag chip deep-links here with `view=vintage` so the surface leads with the current-exposure column. S5 reads the same `bunching_flag` from `/api/pacing`; clicking it lands the user on the Pacing remediation view.
- **From S20 Next-Best-Action** — when the next action is a pacing-tempo change ("stretch to 4 years"), its rationale chain links back here.
- **From Ask Alpha** — answers to Q1/Q3/Q4 render an inline mini-schedule with a "Open the full pacing plan →" link that routes here, preserving the `horizon` the user asked about.
- **From the Welcome / cold-start flow** — a `n_funds==0` investor, after the Architect CTA, is routed here to see the build-from-zero schedule (§7 state 2).

**URL / view param.** The surface reads `?view={year|vintage|strategy}` (default `year`) and `?horizon={3|5}` (default = the value the backend clamps from `investor.horizon_years`). `view` only changes which column/panel leads; the payload always carries all sections, so switching `view` is a client-side layout swap, **not** a re-fetch. `horizon` *does* re-fetch (it changes the plan; §6).

**Outbound deep-links from this surface:**

- A strategy segment in any stacked bar → **S2 Strategy deep-dive** with that strategy pre-selected.
- The secondaries-acceleration card's "See secondaries" → **S22 Secondary** (NotConnectedState if the feed is dark).
- The "fill these slots with funds" CTA on the plan → **S20 Next-Best-Action** (which sequences the plan into named funds).
- "Adjust your target" on the `target` echo strip → **S9 Architect**.

---

### §3 Layout (grid, no scroll, responsive)

The surface is a single full-screen Detail page composed of a **header strip**, a **dominant centre chart** (the year-by-year commitment schedule), a **right context column** (peer overlay + bunching + J-curve), and a **bottom control bar** (horizon, budget slider, J-curve toggle, view tabs). No analytical region scrolls; everything fits the stage at ≥1280px.

**Desktop (≥1280px) grid:**

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ S10 · Pacing   "How to spread your commitments"          [3y | 5y]  [view: Year ▸]  │  ← header + horizon toggle + view tabs
│ ┌─ target echo strip ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│ │ Building to 18 funds · 4 today · 14 to go  ·  €2.7m still to deploy  ·  bal-comp │  │  ← read-only architect() echo + [Adjust target →]
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┤
│  YEAR-BY-YEAR COMMITMENT SCHEDULE  (dominant, ~64% width)      │  CONTEXT (right ~36%)│
│                                                                │                     │
│   €                                            ·····ghost·····  │ ┌─ Bunching ──────┐ │
│   │   ┌────┐                                  (best-performer   │ │ ⚑ Bunched         │ │
│   │   │PC  │  ┌────┐                            pacing overlay)  │ │ 2026 = 54% of €   │ │
│   │   │Inf │  │PC  │  ┌────┐                                     │ │ HHI 0.40 ▸ 0.33   │ │
│   │   │Sec │  │Inf │  │PC  │   ····················              │ │ [Why flagged ▸]   │ │
│   │   │LC  │  │Sec │  │Inf │                                     │ └───────────────────┘ │
│   │   │ …  │  │ …  │  │ …  │                                     │ ┌─ Peer overlay ──┐ │
│   │   └────┘  └────┘  └────┘                                     │ │ Top 10% pace      │ │
│   │   5 · €964k 5·€902k 4·€837k                                  │ │ 3.1 funds/yr      │ │
│   └───────────────────────────────────────────────              │ │ med ticket €165k  │ │
│       2027     2028     2029                                     │ │ vintage HHI 0.22  │ │
│   Caption: "A flat schedule spreads vintages evenly…"           │ │ n=6 · median chip │ │
│                                                                │ └───────────────────┘ │
│  ┌─ J-curve net cashflow (mini ForecastBand, full width) ─────┐ │ ┌─ Secondaries ───┐ │
│  │  +€ ──────────────────────────────────────                 │ │ │ ⚡ Trigger fired   │ │
│  │   0 ─────────────╲________╱──────────────                  │ │ │ ~€710k of 2027    │ │
│  │  −€        trough −€1.42m (2027)                            │ │ │ as secondaries    │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ [See secondaries] │ │
│                                                                │ └───────────────────┘ │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┤
│  Annual budget: [───●──────] €964k/yr   ☑ Smooth the J-curve     [Fill slots with funds →]│  ← control bar
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Centre — the year-by-year commitment schedule (the hero):**
- **Vertical stacked bars, one per plan year** (2027, 2028, 2029 for a 3y horizon; five bars for 5y). This is an *approved* encoding (Part 2.5 "vertical bars"); the stacking dimension is **strategy**, segment colours pinned to the fixed strategy→`--data-*` map so a strategy is the same colour here as on every other surface.
- **Two numbers rendered inline on each bar** per the brief's "€ and #" requirement: the **€ total** (`commit_eur`, e.g. "€964k") and the **# of commitments** (`n_commitments`, e.g. "5 funds") shown as a two-line label directly under/inside the bar top. Mono/tabular figures. The € drives bar height; the # is the count label.
- A **dashed "even-pacing" reference line** is *not* drawn here (that lives in the bunching gauge); instead the bars are intrinsically flat-by-design, and the **best-performer ghost line** overlays the schedule (next bullet).
- **Ghost-line overlay** — a thin dotted line (`--muted`, 1px, no fill) traces the top-decile peers' *implied* annual commitment count/€ across the same year axis, so the user sees "your plan vs how best performers actually paced." It is visually subordinate (ghost = low-contrast dotted), labelled inline "Top 10% pace" at its right terminus, never a solid series that competes with the bars.
- **CaptionBlock** under the chart (1–2 sentences, mandatory).

**Right context column (three stacked cards, equal-ish height, no scroll):**
- **Bunching card** — the `bunching_flag` chip + the current-vintage HHI mini-gauge (HHI vs even-HHI) + dominant-vintage callout + a "Why flagged" expander.
- **Peer-overlay card** — the top-decile pacing medians as a small Scorecard (funds/yr, median ticket, vintage HHI), each carrying a median ConfidenceBadge (`n=6 · median`).
- **Secondaries card** — the J-curve `secondary_acceleration` trigger; present only when triggered, else collapses to a one-line "J-curve within normal range" note.

**Bottom J-curve strip (full centre width, under the schedule):** a compact **ForecastBand** (Part 2.5 approved) of `net_by_year` — a zero baseline, the net line dipping into the trough and recovering, calls (outflow) and distributions (inflow) as the band components. This is the build-out cashflow consequence of the plan; it is *not* the full S11 Liquidity chart (smaller, plan-derived only).

**Bottom control bar:** horizon toggle (also top-right), the **annual-budget slider**, the **smooth-J-curve toggle**, the **view tabs** (Year / Vintage / Strategy), and the primary **"Fill slots with funds →"** CTA (→ S20).

**Responsive:**
- **768–1279px** — the right context column drops **below** the schedule as a horizontal row of the three cards; the schedule keeps full width; the J-curve strip sits between schedule and cards. CohortBuilder collapses to a drawer, Ask Alpha to a launcher (per Part 3). Bars thin but never require horizontal scroll (max 5 bars always fit).
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go)** — single column, stacked in priority order: target echo → horizon toggle → schedule (bars reflow, labels move below each bar) → bunching chip → J-curve mini → peer overlay → secondaries → control bar (slider + toggle become full-width rows). ProfileBar becomes a sheet. The schedule remains a vertical stacked bar (never a horizontal-scroll table).

---

### §4 Components & charts (exact encodings)

All components are foundation components (Part 2.6) or approved charts (Part 2.5). No new chart families are introduced.

**4.1 Stacked yearly bars (`PacingSchedule`, composed from approved vertical bars).**
- One vertical bar per `plan[i]`. X-axis = plan year (= vintage). Y-axis = `commit_eur` (€). Stacked segments = per-strategy `by_year[].commit_eur` from `strategy_phasing` (so the bar's stack *is* the per-strategy phasing for that year).
- Segment colour = fixed strategy→`--data-*` map. Segment order is consistent across bars (largest-deficit strategy at the bottom, stable ordering by strategy index, never re-sorted per bar — so the eye tracks a strategy band across years).
- **Inline labels (both required):** the **# of commitments** (`n_commitments`) as the primary count label centred under the bar ("5 funds"); the **€ total** (`commit_eur`) as the secondary value above the bar ("€964k"). Both mono/tabular. The `weight_pct` is available on hover, not shown inline (avoids clutter; the bar height already encodes share).
- Bars are **thin** (Part 2.5 rule — not fat), generous gutters between years.
- ConfidenceBadge on the chart is a **provenance** chip (the plan is `synthetic:true` — it inherits the synthetic Architect target), and a **SyntheticTag** "illustrative target" sits on the € axis title because the magnitudes flow from imputed wealth/target. The *shape* (flat weights, counts) is deterministic and is **not** tagged synthetic.
- Empty-state, hover, focus per Part 2.6.

**4.2 Ghost-overlay line (`PeerPacingGhost`).**
- A single dotted polyline over the schedule's plot area. Y-position per year = the peer top-decile *implied* annual commitment translated onto the same € (or count) scale: drawn against the **count** axis by default (best-performers' `median_commitments_per_year` as a flat reference line, since the overlay is a steady-state median not a per-year series), with a secondary mode (toggled in the peer card) to show it on the **€** scale using `median_ticket_eur × median_commitments_per_year`.
- Style: `--muted`, 1px, dotted, no markers, no fill, ~50% opacity. A small right-aligned label "Top 10% pace · 3.1/yr" at the line terminus. It must read as *context behind* the bars, never as a competing data series.
- Carries its own median ConfidenceBadge (`n=6 · top decile`) anchored to the label.
- Hidden entirely (with a one-line "Top-decile pacing unavailable — peer group too thin" note) when `peer_pacing.status == "insufficient_peers"` (§7 state 3).

**4.3 Bunching chip (`BunchingFlag`).**
- A chip in the bunching card. Two visual states:
  - **Flagged** (`bunched==true`): amber-leaning warning chip "⚑ Bunched — {dominant_share}% in {dominant_vintage}" using `--over`-adjacent warning styling **paired with the ⚑ glyph** (never colour-only). Below it a tiny HHI gauge: a horizontal track with the **even-HHI** marked as a dashed target and the **actual HHI** as a fill, with inline values "0.40 ▸ target 0.33".
  - **Clear** (`bunched==false`): a calm "✓ Well spread across {n_vintages} vintages" chip, no warning colour.
- A "Why flagged" expander reveals the per-vintage verdict list (`per_vintage_verdict`) as labelled rows ("2024 balanced · 2025 underweight · 2026 overweight"), each with the ▲/▼ glyph, and the house-view softener note where present ("2026 overweight, but house-view favourable").
- The chip's value carries a ProvenancePopover (method "Herfindahl vintage concentration; threshold 0.30"; snapshot id).

**4.4 Annual-budget slider (`BudgetSlider`).**
- A horizontal slider in the control bar. Domain: from a sensible floor (e.g. `typical_ticket_eur`, one fund/yr) to a ceiling (`remaining_eur`, the whole book in year one). Current value = the plan's per-year € (the flat-weight mid-point), shown as a live mono readout "€964k/yr".
- Dragging recomputes the schedule (§6) — fewer €/yr ⇒ the plan stretches across more years and/or fewer funds/yr; the horizon toggle and the slider are coupled (changing the budget can change the implied years-to-complete, surfaced as a caption note "At this budget you finish building in ~4 years").
- Keyboard-operable (arrow keys ±step), ARIA slider role, value announced. A SyntheticTag near the readout (the € is illustrative).

**4.5 Smooth-J-curve toggle (`SmoothJCurveToggle`).**
- A labelled switch "Smooth the J-curve." When **on**, the request re-fetches with the smoothing applied (the backend's secondaries-acceleration substitution and flatter weights are reflected). When **off**, the raw flat-primary plan is shown. The toggle state is reflected in: (a) the schedule (a portion of the trough year's primary € is annotated as "→ secondaries"), (b) the J-curve strip (the trough visibly lifts), and (c) the secondaries card.
- Default state = follows `secondary_acceleration.triggered`: **on** when the backend fired the trigger, **off** otherwise. The user can override.
- Disabled (greyed, with tooltip "No deep trough to smooth") when the trigger did not fire and would not under any toggle.

**4.6 J-curve ForecastBand (`JCurveBand`).**
- Approved ForecastBand. Zero baseline emphasised (the line crosses it). `net_by_year[].net_eur` is the net line; `calls_eur` (down) and `dists_eur` (up) are the band/components. The **trough** year is marked with a labelled point "trough −€1.42m (2027)". A short caption: "Building front-loads capital calls; distributions catch up by {trough recovery year}."
- This is read-only (no scenario toggles here — those live in S12 Projection); the only lever is the smooth-J-curve toggle which re-fetches the whole plan.

**4.7 View tabs (`PacingViewTabs`).**
- Segmented control: **Year · Vintage · Strategy**. Client-side layout swap (no re-fetch):
  - **Year** (default): schedule leads as described.
  - **Vintage**: the current-vintage exposure (HHI histogram of `by_vintage_eur`/`shares`) promotes to the centre, with the forward plan bars appended to its right on the same vintage axis (current 2024–2026 + planned 2027–2029) — one continuous vintage timeline showing the bunching today healing into a flat forward schedule.
  - **Strategy**: the per-strategy phasing promotes — a small-multiple of BarRows (one per strategy: current_pct vs target_pct with the gap_pp, plus the by-year € deficit closure), the schedule demoted to a thumbnail.

**4.8 Target echo strip (`TargetEcho`).** A read-only one-line summary of `target` (build_to_n, current_n, remaining_n, remaining_eur, risk_class) with an "Adjust target →" link to S9. Carries a SyntheticTag (the target is illustrative) and a ProvenancePopover ("from Architect, T1-1").

**4.9 Supporting:** CaptionBlock (under every chart), ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover (on every number), SyntheticTag (on imputed magnitudes), RelaxBanner (peer-overlay thin), PDFExportButton (frame), Scorecard (peer medians), GatedState — *not used here* (pacing surfaces no peer-held fund names; never gated, §7 state 6).

---

### §5 Data bindings (element ← `/api/pacing` JSON path)

Single fetch: **`GET /api/pacing?investor_id={id}&horizon={3|5}&view={year|vintage|strategy}`**. Every numeric element binds `value` to the visual and `provenance` to its badge/popover (Part 0.2). `investor_id` is optional (omitted = the signed-in/impersonated investor).

**Target echo strip** ← `target`:
- "Building to N funds" ← `target.build_to_n`
- "M today" ← `target.current_n`
- "K to go" ← `target.remaining_n`
- "€X still to deploy" ← `target.remaining_eur` (if `target.eur_basis=="ticket-derived"`, append "(estimated from typical ticket)" — §7 state 5)
- risk-class label ← `target.risk_class`

**Year-by-year schedule (`PacingSchedule`)** ← `plan[]`, one bar per element:
- bar height (€) ← `plan[i].commit_eur`
- "# funds" inline label ← `plan[i].n_commitments`
- "€" inline label ← `plan[i].commit_eur`
- year/vintage axis label ← `plan[i].year` (= `plan[i].vintage`)
- bar stack segments ← `strategy_phasing[strategy].by_year[i].commit_eur` for each strategy (segment colour = strategy→`--data-*`)
- hover tooltip share ← `plan[i].weight_pct`
- ticket band (in hover) ← `plan[i].ticket_eur` + `plan[i].ticket_band_eur` ("≈€193k each, €164k–€222k")

**Ghost-overlay line (`PeerPacingGhost`)** ← `peer_pacing`:
- line level (count mode) ← `peer_pacing.median_commitments_per_year`
- line level (€ mode) ← `peer_pacing.median_ticket_eur × peer_pacing.median_commitments_per_year`
- terminus label ← `peer_pacing.median_commitments_per_year`
- badge ← `peer_pacing.n_top` + `peer_pacing.provenance`
- visibility ← `peer_pacing.status` (`"ok"` shows; `"insufficient_peers"` hides + note)
- RelaxBanner text ← `peer_pacing.relaxed_dims`

**Bunching card (`BunchingFlag`)** ← `current_vintage_exposure`:
- flag state ← `current_vintage_exposure.bunched`
- dominant callout ← `current_vintage_exposure.dominant_vintage` + `dominant_share`
- HHI gauge fill ← `current_vintage_exposure.hhi`; target marker ← `current_vintage_exposure.even_hhi`
- threshold (popover) ← `current_vintage_exposure.threshold`
- "Why flagged" rows ← `current_vintage_exposure.per_vintage_verdict` (keyed by year)
- vintage histogram (Vintage view) ← `current_vintage_exposure.by_vintage_eur` + `shares`
- n vintages ← `current_vintage_exposure.n_vintages`

**Peer-overlay card (`Scorecard`)** ← `peer_pacing`:
- funds/yr ← `peer_pacing.median_commitments_per_year`
- median ticket ← `peer_pacing.median_ticket_eur`
- vintage HHI ← `peer_pacing.median_vintage_hhi`
- strategies/yr ← `peer_pacing.median_strategies_per_year`
- cohort size (popover) ← `peer_pacing.cohort_n`; top n ← `peer_pacing.n_top`

**Secondaries card (`SmoothJCurveToggle` companion)** ← `jcurve.secondary_acceleration`:
- presence/visibility ← `jcurve.secondary_acceleration.triggered`
- recommended € ← `jcurve.secondary_acceleration.recommended_secondary_eur`
- rationale text ← `jcurve.secondary_acceleration.rationale`

**J-curve strip (`JCurveBand`)** ← `jcurve`:
- net line ← `jcurve.net_by_year[i].net_eur`
- calls component ← `jcurve.net_by_year[i].calls_eur`
- dists component ← `jcurve.net_by_year[i].dists_eur`
- trough point ← `jcurve.trough_eur` at `jcurve.trough_year`
- caption depth ← `jcurve.trough_pct_of_target`
- smoothing note ← `jcurve.smoothing_note`

**Strategy view (`PacingViewTabs` → Strategy)** ← `strategy_phasing`:
- per-strategy BarRow current ← `strategy_phasing[s].current_pct`; target marker ← `strategy_phasing[s].target_pct`
- gap badge ← `strategy_phasing[s].gap_pp`
- deficit € ← `strategy_phasing[s].deficit_eur`
- by-year deficit closure ← `strategy_phasing[s].by_year[]`

**Surface-level provenance / badges** ← top-level `provenance`:
- method (all popovers) ← `provenance.method`
- synthetic flag (SyntheticTag visibility on magnitudes) ← `provenance.synthetic`
- source ← `provenance.source`
- vintage scope (caption footnotes) ← `provenance.vintage_scope`
- snapshot id (every badge footer) ← `provenance.snapshot_id`

Header chrome: horizon ← `horizon`; active view tab ← `view`.

---

### §6 Interactions

All re-renders touch **only affected regions**, never a full page reload (Part 10 performance rule). Motion ≤150ms, value-in animates once and settles (Part 2.4).

**6.1 Drag the annual-budget slider → recompute the schedule.**
- The slider is a **client-side recompute by default** for responsiveness during the drag: as the handle moves, the schedule re-derives the per-year € and fund counts using the same flat-weight/integerize logic the backend uses (the FE mirrors `deployment_weights` + `integerize` for the *preview* so the bars move live without a network round-trip). The readout, bar heights, inline €/#, and the J-curve trough estimate update at interactive frame-rate.
- **On release (debounced ~300ms after the last drag event)**, the FE issues a confirming `GET /api/pacing` with the implied `horizon` (if the budget pushed the build-out past the current horizon, the horizon toggle updates and the request carries the new value) so the authoritative plan, J-curve, and secondaries trigger come from the engine. A subtle "recalculating…" shimmer on the affected cards (calm skeleton, not a spinner) during the in-flight request. If the confirming response differs from the live preview, the bars settle to the authoritative values (single subtle value-in).
- A caption updates live: "At €X/yr you finish building ~N funds in ~Y years."
- Provenance after a slider change reflects the new request's `snapshot_id`.

**6.2 Toggle "Smooth the J-curve" → re-fetch.**
- Flipping the toggle **re-fetches** `GET /api/pacing` (the smoothing decision changes the engine's plan + secondaries substitution; this is not a pure client transform). On → the engine returns the smoothed plan; the trough year's bar shows a "→ secondaries ≈€710k" annotation, the J-curve trough visibly lifts (animated once), and the secondaries card expands. Off → back to raw primary plan.
- During the re-fetch: calm shimmer on the schedule + J-curve strip only; the bunching and peer cards (unaffected) stay put.
- If the trigger could never fire (shallow trough), the toggle is disabled with the tooltip "No deep trough to smooth" — flipping is impossible, no fetch.

**6.3 Hover a year bar → per-year breakdown.**
- Hovering (or focusing via keyboard) a year bar opens a breakdown tooltip/popover anchored to the bar: the year, total € + #, the **per-strategy split for that year** (`strategy_phasing[*].by_year[i]` as a tiny inline list with the strategy colour swatches), the **ticket band** ("≈€193k each · €164k–€222k"), the **weight %** of the plan, and the **vintage verdict** if this year overlaps a flagged current vintage. Every number in the tooltip is a provenance chip.
- Hovering a single **strategy segment** within a bar highlights that strategy band across all bars (the eye-track), dims the others slightly, and shows that segment's €; clicking the segment deep-links to **S2** with the strategy selected.

**6.4 Horizon toggle (3y ⇄ 5y) → re-fetch.**
- Switching horizon re-fetches `GET /api/pacing?horizon=…`. The schedule animates from 3 to 5 bars (or back); the ghost line, J-curve, and peer overlay refresh from the new payload. The view tab and slider position persist where sensible (the slider re-centres on the new flat per-year €).

**6.5 View tabs (Year / Vintage / Strategy) → client-side layout swap (no fetch).**
- Switching promotes the relevant section per §4.7. No network call (the payload already carries all sections). The active tab is reflected in the URL `?view=`.

**6.6 Bunching "Why flagged" expander.** Toggles the per-vintage verdict list open/closed in place; no fetch.

**6.7 Cohort change (left rail).** Re-resolves `peer_pacing` only (the peer overlay + peer card); the plan/schedule/J-curve are cohort-independent (they derive from the investor's own Architect target). So a cohort change re-fetches and re-renders **only** the ghost line + peer card + RelaxBanner — never the schedule. (Implementation note: the FE may still issue one `/api/pacing` and bind only the `peer_pacing` block; the rest is identical given a pinned snapshot — G9 determinism.)

**6.8 PDF export.** Exports the current view (schedule + active panels + captions + badges) as a clean PowerPoint-style PDF (frame component).

---

### §7 The seven states (exact branch + render)

Every state renders cleanly; never a blank, a spinner-storm, or a fabricated number.

1. **Happy** — full inputs; `plan[]`, `strategy_phasing`, `current_vintage_exposure`, `jcurve`, `peer_pacing.status=="ok"` all present. The §3 layout in full. `bunched` may be true or false (the chip swaps accordingly — a clean "well spread" chip is still the happy state). This is the §5 worked-payload render.

2. **Cold-start (`n_funds == 0`) — a primary, designed state, not a fallback.** A brand-new investor needs a pacing plan *most*. The backend still returns a full forward `plan[]` from `architect()` with `target.current_n=0, remaining_n=build_to_n`, and `current_vintage_exposure` empty (`by_vintage_eur:{}`, `hhi:null`, `n_vintages:0`, `bunched:false`). Render:
   - The schedule renders **in full** (all `build_to_n` funds spread across the horizon) — the dominant element.
   - The bunching card shows a calm informational state: "No vintages yet — this plan builds them evenly from the start" (no flag, no gauge).
   - The Vintage view shows only the forward bars (no current-exposure histogram).
   - A gentle inbound banner from Welcome/Architect: "Here's how to build your portfolio over {horizon} years." `peer_pacing` still resolves (the cohort exists independent of holdings). The "Fill slots with funds →" CTA is prominent (the natural next step for a new investor).
   - No error, no "your portfolio will appear here" dead-end.

3. **Thin cohort (peer overlay).** `peer_pacing.status=="insufficient_peers"` (cohort `<30`, or `<10` ranked top-decile). The **ghost line is hidden** and replaced with a one-line in-chart note "Top-decile pacing unavailable — peer group too thin to compare." The peer-overlay card shows a RelaxBanner in words from `relaxed_dims` ("Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band") when the relax-ladder fired but still resolved; if it never resolved, the card shows "Not enough comparable investors yet." The plan/schedule/bunching/J-curve are **unaffected and fully rendered** — the surface is fully useful without the overlay.

4. **Low coverage (cashflow curves).** When `cashflow_model` had `<8` quarters for a strategy and fell back to a default curve, the top-level provenance carries that note. The **J-curve strip shows a CoverageBadge / note**: "Cashflow shape estimated from a default curve for {strategy} (limited history)." The net line still renders; it is never implied to be precise. The schedule and bunching are unaffected.

5. **Null data (`investable_wealth_eur` is NULL).** `target.pm_target_eur` is null → `remaining_eur` is **ticket-derived** (`remaining_n × typical_ticket_eur`) and `target.eur_basis=="ticket-derived"`. Render: all € figures carry the note "(estimated from typical ticket)" and a SyntheticTag; the **# of commitments and the year structure are still authoritative** (they come from `build_to_n`, not from €). The budget slider's readout is labelled "estimated." The J-curve `trough_pct_of_target` uses the ticket-derived denominator (noted). No error.

6. **Gated.** Pacing **never gates** — it surfaces no peer-held fund names (only `peer_pacing` medians, no names; `weighted_average_portfolio` is not surfaced here). The GatedState component does **not** appear on this surface. (If a future FE request asked for peer-held example funds to fill slots, those route through the k-anon'd spine on S20, not here.) This state is therefore "not applicable, by design" — and the brief documents it so a builder doesn't bolt on an unnecessary gated card.

7. **Error / degraded.** Unknown investor → the surface shows the frame's standard 404-investor empty state (not a Pacing-specific crash). `cashflow_model` engine error → the surface renders the **cached last-good plan** with a quiet top banner "Showing your last saved plan — live recalculation is temporarily unavailable," and the budget slider + J-curve toggle are disabled (they need the live engine) while the schedule, bunching, and peer overlay (from cache) remain readable. Never a `500`-style blank, never a fabricated number. If only the peer block fails, degrade to state 3 (hide the ghost line) and keep everything else live.

**Cross-cutting (per Part 9):** not-significant deltas render greyed; every imputed magnitude carries a SyntheticTag; loading = calm skeletons (the bars draw in as flat grey placeholders, not spinners); not-connected only ever appears on the secondaries "See secondaries" deep-link target (S22), never on this surface itself.

---

### §8 Copy deck (final strings — English; structure for English + German, EU number/currency formatting)

**Surface title.** "Pacing"
**Surface subtitle.** "How to spread your commitments over the next few years."

**Target echo strip.**
- "Building to {build_to_n} funds · {current_n} today · {remaining_n} to go"
- "€{remaining_eur} still to deploy" (or, ticket-derived: "≈€{remaining_eur} still to deploy (estimated)")
- Link: "Adjust your target →"

**Horizon toggle.** "Plan over: [3 years] [5 years]"

**Schedule caption (happy).** "A flat schedule spreads your commitments evenly across vintages — the simplest way to avoid concentrating in any single year."
**Schedule caption (cold-start).** "Here's how to build to {build_to_n} funds over {horizon} years, a few each year, so no single vintage dominates."
**Per-bar labels.** "{n} funds" / "€{commit_eur}"
**Bar hover.** "{year}: {n} funds · €{commit_eur} · ≈€{ticket_eur} each (€{lo}–€{hi}) · {weight_pct}% of the plan"

**Ghost line.**
- Terminus label: "Top 10% pace · {median_commitments_per_year}/yr"
- Hidden note: "Top-decile pacing unavailable — peer group too thin to compare."

**Bunching chip (flagged).** "⚑ Bunched — {dominant_share}% of your commitments are in {dominant_vintage}"
- Gauge inline: "Concentration {hhi} · evenly spread would be {even_hhi}"
- Why-flagged header: "Why this is flagged"
- Verdict rows: "{year} — {verdict}" (e.g. "2026 — overweight ▲", "2025 — underweight ▼", "2024 — balanced")
- House-view softener: "2026 looks heavy, but the house view favours this vintage."
**Bunching chip (clear).** "✓ Well spread across {n_vintages} vintages"
**Bunching chip (cold-start).** "No vintages yet — this plan builds them evenly from the start."

**Peer-overlay card.**
- Title: "How the top 10% paced"
- Rows: "{median_commitments_per_year} funds/year" · "€{median_ticket_eur} median ticket" · "Vintage spread {median_vintage_hhi}" · "{median_strategies_per_year} strategies/year"
- Badge: "Median across {n_top} top performers"
- Comparison note (when the plan paces faster): "Your plan commits faster than the top 10% — you could stretch it over more years."

**Secondaries card (triggered).**
- Title: "⚡ Consider secondaries to smooth the build"
- Body: "{rationale}" (engine string, e.g. "Trough outflow 19% of target with 14 funds still to build — secondaries call fast and distribute early, lifting the trough.")
- Figure: "≈€{recommended_secondary_eur} of {trough_year} as secondaries"
- Link: "See secondaries →"
**Secondaries card (not triggered).** "Your build-out's cash flow stays within a normal range — no acceleration needed."

**J-curve strip.**
- Title: "Net cash flow while you build"
- Caption: "Building front-loads capital calls, so net cash flow dips early and recovers as distributions arrive. Trough: −€{trough} in {trough_year}."
- Trough point label: "Trough −€{trough} ({trough_year})"
- Low-coverage note: "Cash-flow shape estimated from a default curve for {strategy} (limited history)."

**Annual-budget slider.** "Annual budget: €{value}/yr" · live caption "At this budget you finish building in ~{years} years."
**Smooth-J-curve toggle.** "Smooth the J-curve" · disabled tooltip "No deep trough to smooth."

**View tabs.** "By year · By vintage · By strategy"

**Strategy view (per-strategy rows).** "{strategy}: {current_pct}% today → {target_pct}% target ({gap_pp:+}pp) · €{deficit_eur} to deploy"

**Primary CTA.** "Fill these slots with funds →"
**Info-not-advice disclaimer (footer, always present).** "This is an illustrative pacing plan for information, not investment advice. Talk to your sales associate before committing."

**Degraded banner.** "Showing your last saved plan — live recalculation is temporarily unavailable."

**Empty/loading.** Skeleton bars; no text needed beyond the title.

---

### §9 Badge rendering (medians for the peer overlay; provenance everywhere)

Per the non-negotiables (Part 0.2): no number renders bare; every number embeds provenance, confidence, and coverage where applicable.

**9.1 Peer overlay = medians, always (G6).** The peer pacing values (`median_commitments_per_year`, `median_ticket_eur`, `median_vintage_hhi`, `median_strategies_per_year`) are **medians of the top decile**, never means — the field names carry "median" and the UI labels reinforce it ("median ticket", "Median across {n_top} top performers"). The ConfidenceBadge on the peer card and the ghost line reads **`n={n_top} · median`** (e.g. "n=6 · median"), distinguishing it from mean+CI badges elsewhere in the product. Where the backend can supply an IQR/range it renders as "€{median} (€{lo}–€{hi})"; absent that, the median + n is sufficient and the badge does not fabricate a CI. When the peer group is thin (`status=="insufficient_peers"`) the badge is suppressed along with the value (no greyed median shown — it is hidden, per §7 state 3).

**9.2 Provenance popover (every number).** Clicking any number opens the ProvenancePopover bound to the relevant provenance object:
- Plan magnitudes & target → top-level `provenance` (`method: "flat-weight pacing to architect() target; HHI bunching; cashflow_model J-curve; top-decile peer overlay"`, `source`, `vintage_scope`, `snapshot_id`).
- Peer medians → `peer_pacing.provenance` (`method: "top-decile by realized DPI; pacing medians (G6)"`, `n: n_top`, `synthetic: false`).
- Bunching → method "Herfindahl vintage concentration; threshold {threshold}".
- J-curve → method "cashflow_model deploy/harvest curves; vintage-physics, not a return forecast" + the low-coverage `cashflow_curve:"default"` note where present.

**9.3 SyntheticTag (G8 — what is illustrative).** The amber "illustrative" tag appears on every figure that inherits from the Architect's synthetic target: the **€ axis** of the schedule, the **target echo €**, the **per-strategy deficit €**, the budget slider readout, and the J-curve € magnitudes. It does **not** appear on the *shape* of the plan (fund counts, year structure, the flat weights) — those are deterministic given the target — nor on the **peer medians** (`peer_pacing.provenance.synthetic == false`; these are real, realized-DPI-ranked observations) nor on the **current vintage HHI / by_vintage_eur** (real, from holdings). The tag's tooltip: "Illustrative — derived from your estimated target architecture."

**9.4 House-view source tag.** Where a per-vintage verdict is softened by `vintage_house_view`, the row carries a small "house view" source chip (`source: "house view vREF"`) distinguishing house-view context from a forecast.

**9.5 CoverageBadge.** Only on the J-curve strip, and only in state 4 (default-curve fallback): "estimated from limited history." No coverage % applies to the plan/schedule itself (it's a deterministic schedule, not a sampled estimate).

**9.6 Snapshot footer.** Every badge popover footers with `snapshot_id` so two identical requests are visibly the same snapshot (G9 determinism) — important after a slider/toggle re-fetch, where the new snapshot id confirms the recompute.

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive (per Part 3 breakpoints).**
- **≥1280px** — full three-region layout (schedule 64% / context 36%, J-curve strip full centre width, control bar pinned bottom). No scroll.
- **768–1279px** — context cards drop below the schedule as a 3-up row; schedule full width; CohortBuilder → drawer, Ask Alpha → launcher. Bars thin, still ≤5, no horizontal scroll. Control bar wraps to two rows if needed (slider on its own row).
- **<768px** — single column, priority stack (§3). Bars reflow with labels below each bar; the J-curve becomes a compact full-width band; slider and toggle become full-width rows; view tabs become a scrollable segmented control (the only horizontally-scrollable element, and it is a control, not an analytical chart). ProfileBar → sheet. The advisor-on-the-go use case is fully supported.
- **No analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll** at any breakpoint (Part 10). The stacked bars are vertical and bounded at 5; the J-curve fits the viewport width.

**Accessibility (WCAG-AA, Part 10).**
- **Never colour-only.** The bunching flag pairs colour with the ⚑ glyph and explicit text; per-vintage verdicts pair colour with ▲/▼ and the word ("overweight"/"underweight"/"balanced"); the J-curve net sign pairs colour with +/− and the zero baseline; strategy segments are distinguishable by the fixed colour map *and* labelled on hover/in the legend behind the "view data" affordance.
- **Contrast.** All text, axis labels, and the (low-opacity) ghost line meet AA against `--bg`/`--surface`; the ghost line, though visually subordinate, keeps a readable terminus label at full contrast.
- **Keyboard.** Full keyboard nav: tab through horizon toggle → view tabs → each year bar (focusable, opens the breakdown on focus) → strategy segments → budget slider (arrow keys ± step, Home/End to extremes) → J-curve toggle (Space/Enter) → CTA. Focus states visible on every control.
- **Slider a11y.** ARIA `slider` role, `aria-valuemin/max/now`, `aria-valuetext` announcing "€964 thousand per year, finishes building in about 4 years."
- **Toggle a11y.** ARIA `switch` with `aria-checked`; disabled state announces "no deep trough to smooth."
- **Chart data as a table.** A "view data" affordance behind each chart exposes the underlying values as an accessible table: the schedule table (year, #, €, ticket band, per-strategy split), the vintage-exposure table (year, €, share, verdict), the J-curve table (year, net, calls, dists), the peer-pacing table (the four medians + n). Screen-reader users get the numbers without the visual.
- **ARIA / live regions.** The recompute (slider/toggle/horizon) announces "plan updated" via a polite live region; the "recalculating" shimmer is `aria-busy` on the affected region, not a separate alert.
- **Motion.** ≤150ms, value-in once; respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (bars/lines snap to final state with no animation when reduced).
- **i18n.** All strings externalised for English + German; numbers and currency localised (€, EU grouping/decimal); the disclaimer and house-view tags are translatable; no hard-coded strings; mono/tabular figures keep columns aligned across locales.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

The surface is done when **all** of the following hold (visual + interaction + binding):

**Binding & data**
- [ ] The surface renders from a **single** `GET /api/pacing` call; `target`, `plan[]`, `strategy_phasing`, `current_vintage_exposure`, `jcurve`, `peer_pacing` all bind to their §5 paths.
- [ ] Every number on the surface shows a ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover; the snapshot id appears in popover footers.
- [ ] Placeholder data is shaped exactly like the §5 / backend §5 payload so the live swap is a base-URL change.

**Schedule (the hero)**
- [ ] One vertical stacked bar per plan year; segments = per-strategy phasing, colour = the fixed strategy→`--data-*` map (a strategy is the same colour as on S1/S2).
- [ ] Each bar shows **both** the € total and the # of commitments inline (the brief's "€ and #" requirement).
- [ ] `sum(plan[i].n_commitments) == target.remaining_n` is visibly consistent (the schedule sums to target — backend G-gate, surfaced honestly).
- [ ] No forward year visually dominates (the flat anti-bunching weights; each ≤35% of the plan) — the bars read as evenly spread by design.
- [ ] A CaptionBlock sits under the schedule.

**Ghost overlay (peer pacing)**
- [ ] The best-performer pacing ghost line renders as a thin dotted, low-contrast, subordinate overlay with a terminus label, never a competing solid series.
- [ ] It carries an `n={n_top} · median` badge and hides cleanly (with a note) when `peer_pacing.status=="insufficient_peers"`.

**Bunching**
- [ ] The bunching chip fires (⚑ + colour + text) exactly when `current_vintage_exposure.bunched==true`, and shows a calm "well spread" chip otherwise.
- [ ] The HHI gauge shows actual HHI against the even-HHI target marker; the "Why flagged" expander lists per-vintage verdicts with ▲/▼ glyphs.

**Controls / interactions**
- [ ] Dragging the budget slider recomputes the schedule live (client preview) and confirms with a debounced re-fetch; bars, €/#, and the J-curve update; a live caption shows the implied years-to-complete.
- [ ] Toggling "Smooth the J-curve" **re-fetches**; on → the trough lifts and the secondaries card expands; it is disabled (tooltip) when no deep trough exists.
- [ ] Hovering/focusing a year bar shows the per-year breakdown (#, €, ticket band, per-strategy split, weight %, verdict), every figure a provenance chip.
- [ ] Horizon 3⇄5 re-fetches and re-renders; view tabs (Year/Vintage/Strategy) swap layout **without** a fetch.
- [ ] A cohort change re-renders **only** the peer overlay + peer card (the schedule is cohort-independent).

**States**
- [ ] All seven states render per §7: happy, **cold-start = full plan from a zero book** (designed, not a dead-end), thin-peer (ghost hidden + RelaxBanner), low-coverage (J-curve default-curve note), null-wealth (ticket-derived € + SyntheticTag, counts still authoritative), gated = N/A by design (no gated card bolted on), error = cached last-good + quiet banner + disabled live controls.

**Badges / provenance / tone**
- [ ] Peer values are labelled and badged as **medians** (never means); their SyntheticTag is **absent** (real observations).
- [ ] SyntheticTag is present on the € magnitudes (illustrative target) and **absent** on the plan shape, the current vintage HHI, and the peer medians.
- [ ] The info-not-advice disclaimer is always present; no imperative "you should" language anywhere (the payload carries none and neither does the UI).

**Brand / a11y / frame**
- [ ] Moonfare indigo/mint/cream, real logo, light/dark; no flashy transitions; ≤150ms motion; `prefers-reduced-motion` honoured.
- [ ] WCAG-AA: never colour-only (glyphs on flag/verdict/sign), full keyboard nav, focus states, "view data" tables behind every chart, ARIA on slider/toggle/tabs.
- [ ] Responsive at all three breakpoints with no horizontal scroll on any analytical chart.
- [ ] PDF export available; the global frame (ProfileBar, CohortBuilder, Ask Alpha) persists; Meta⇄Detail switch is instant and no-scroll.


---

## Surface S11 — Liquidity & Cashflow
### Frontend surface spec for Claude Design · use case T1-7 (`cashflow_model`) · binds to `GET /api/liquidity` + `GET /api/projection`

> This spec composes from the **Frontend Foundation** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`): the global frame (Part 3), tokens & chart language (Part 2), the seven-state model (Part 9), and the **ForecastBand** component (Part 2.5 / 2.6). It does **not** redefine those primitives — it specifies how S11 instantiates them and wires them to the T1-7 contract. "ForecastBand" means the Foundation component; every number carries its ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover per Part 0.2. Read alongside `Alpha_UseCase_Briefs/deep/T1-07_Cashflow_Liquidity.md` for the exact JSON shapes.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** S11 is the investor's liquidity cockpit. It answers the one question a "doctor, not a pro" loses sleep over: *can I commit to another fund without getting cash-trapped?* It renders, in one no-scroll screen, (1) the forward **net cash-flow path** — calls minus distributions — over eight quarters as a banded forecast, (2) a single **overcommitment** verdict chip, and (3) a plain-language **investable-capacity** headline ("you can commit ~€X more"). Everything else — illiquidity decomposition, the add-fund overlay, the semi-liquid re-profile — supports or stress-tests that one decision.

The surface leads with **liquidity safety**, not return. It bridges the analytical surfaces (S1–S8: *where you stand*) and the action surfaces (S13 Funds, S14 What's next, S20 Next Best Action: *what to do*). When S20 says "commit €230k to Fund Y," it is S11's `commit_verdict.headroom_eur` and the add-fund overlay that make that number safe.

**The seven user questions this surface must answer** (from the T1-7 brief §1, mapped to on-screen elements):

| # | User question | Eval id | Answered by (on-screen) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "Can I safely make another commitment?" | `should_i_commit` | Capacity headline + overcommit chip + commit-verdict line |
| Q2 | "What calls and distributions should I expect over the next 24 months?" | `projection_8q` | ForecastBand (calls vs distributions, 8 quarters, banded) |
| Q3 | "How illiquid is my PM book in practice?" | `illiquidity_carried` | Illiquidity panel (3 lenses: stock €, % of wealth, practical %) |
| Q4 | "Would adding this fund create too much pressure on future calls?" | `add_fund_overlay` | "What if I add Fund Y" overlay control → ghost band + verdict |
| Q5 | "Should I add semi-liquids/secondaries to stabilize cash flows?" | `reprofile_semiliquid` | Re-profile toggle → flatter ghost band + band-width-reduction caption |
| Q6 | "How much commitment space remains?" | `capacity_estimate` | Capacity headline (`investable_capacity_eur`) |
| Q7 | "How much liquidity stress if markets stall?" | `stress_downside` | Downside band + stress-quarter marker + peak-net-call callout |

**The three guardrails the UI must make visible** (T1-7 §1, restated as render rules):
- **G3 — realized vs projected.** Distributions already received are *fact* (DPI, `realized` branch); future distributions are *projections* (RVPI-derived, `projection` branch). The UI must **never** let a projected distribution read as money in the bank. This drives §9 (Badge rendering).
- **G2 — bands, never points.** Every forward series renders as a shaded band (downside / base / optimistic), never a bare line. A single un-shaded line is a visual build failure.
- **G9 — EUR integers.** All money renders as whole-EUR, EU-formatted (`€231,600`, thin-space thousands in DE locale), no cents, single currency.

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

**Rail position.** S11 sits in the left-rail group **"Liquidity"**, between **S10 · Pacing** and **S12 · Projection** (Foundation Part 3 rail order: …Architect · Pacing · **Liquidity** · Projection · Funds…). The rail label is **"Liquidity"**; the surface title is **"Liquidity & Cashflow."**

**Entry points (all land on the same no-scroll Detail view):**
1. **Direct** — clicking "Liquidity" in the left rail. Instant, full-screen, no scroll (Part 3).
2. **Deep-link from S20 Next Best Action** — the rationale chain "…liquidity capacity €231,600…" is a link; clicking it opens S11 with the capacity headline focus-ringed and the add-fund overlay **pre-seeded** with the fund S20 proposed (`?add_fund={id}&add_eur={int}` reflected into the overlay control). The overlay's ghost band renders immediately.
3. **Deep-link from S13 Funds / fund compare** — the "fit-to-your-gap" banner has a secondary link "check liquidity impact" → opens S11 with that fund pre-loaded into the add-fund overlay.
4. **Ask Alpha** — answering Q1/Q4/Q6/Q7 ("Can I commit more?", "Am I over-committed?") renders an inline mini ForecastBand + capacity chip in the chat bubble, with a "open full liquidity view" link → S11.
5. **Overcommitment cross-surface flag** — when `overcommitted == true`, a small red liquidity chip appears in the ProfileBar (top frame) on every surface; clicking it routes to S11 in state 7.

**Relationship to S12 Projection.** S11 and S12 share the `cashflow_model` engine and the ForecastBand component, but are distinct surfaces: **S11 is liquidity (calls vs distributions, "can I commit?")**; **S12 is value (NAV/DPI path, scenario toggles, "what will it be worth?")**. S11 binds primarily to `/api/liquidity` for its scalars and to `/api/projection` for its bands; S12 binds to `/api/projection` for NAV paths. Do not duplicate the capacity/overcommit logic in S12 — link to S11 instead.

**Mode visibility.** Investor mode: full surface. Advisor mode: same surface, scoped to the selected client (ProfileBar shows the client selector); the overcommit chip and capacity headline feed the cockpit "who to call & why" trigger ("liquidity headroom low"). Internal mode: surface hidden (per-client liquidity is not aggregate-only data).

---

### §3 Layout

Single full-screen Detail view, **no scroll** (Part 0.2 / Part 3). The frame (ProfileBar top, Cohort builder left, Ask Alpha float, PDF export) persists. The centre stage is a **stacked three-zone layout**: a thin **decision strip** across the top, the **dominant ForecastBand** in the middle, and a **supporting trio** along the bottom.

**Desktop ≥1280px:**
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DECISION STRIP                                                                  │
│ ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐  │
│ │ CAPACITY HEADLINE                        │  │ OVERCOMMIT CHIP              │  │
│ │ "You can commit ~€230k more"             │  │ ● Comfortable · ratio 0.01  │  │
│ │ caption: gap to target − next-yr calls   │  │ (or ▲ Overcommitted — red)  │  │
│ └────────────────────────────────────────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘  │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ FORECASTBAND  (the hero — renders large, Part 2.3 "Z-charts render large")      │
│   Calls (outflow, above 0) vs Distributions (inflow, below 0) × 8 quarters      │
│   base line + optimistic/downside shading · stress-quarter marker               │
│   ┌─ toggle: [Net] [Calls vs Dist] ─┐   ┌─ "What if I add Fund Y ▾" ─┐         │
│                                                                                  │
│   ▒▒▒░░░  shaded band, base line, quarter axis 2026Q3 … 2028Q2                   │
│                                                                                  │
│   caption: 1–2 sentences (peak strain, when distributions overtake calls)       │
├──────────────────────┬───────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┤
│ ILLIQUIDITY PANEL     │ STRESS / DOWNSIDE      │ RE-PROFILE CONTROL              │
│ 3 stat lenses:        │ peak net call €50,600  │ [+ Semi-liquid] [+ Secondary]   │
│ • stock €1.43m        │ at 2027Q3 (q5)         │ → ghost band overlay            │
│ • 17.9% of wealth     │ "if markets stall"     │ "would flatten band by ~34%"    │
│ • 100% no redemption  │ downside band link     │                                 │
└──────────────────────┴───────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Why this hierarchy.** The decision strip is read first because it carries the answer (capacity + safe/unsafe). The ForecastBand is the evidence — it gets the most pixels because "Z-charts render large" (Part 2.3) and because the band shading *is* the liquidity-stress story (Q7). The supporting trio is read last; it deepens (Q3 illiquidity), stress-tests (Q7 downside detail), and offers a remedy (Q5 re-profile).

**Tablet 768–1279px.** Cohort builder collapses to a drawer; Ask Alpha to a launcher button (Part 3). Centre stage: decision strip stays a 2-up row; ForecastBand keeps full width (still the hero); the supporting trio reflows to a 3-up row that may wrap to 2+1. No horizontal scroll on the chart — the 8-quarter axis compresses labels to "Q3 / Q4 / Q1…" with year dividers.

**Mobile <768px (advisor on the go).** Single-column stack in reading order: (1) capacity headline, (2) overcommit chip, (3) ForecastBand (reflowed: bars thinner, band shading preserved, axis labels rotated or abbreviated, "view data" table affordance promoted), (4) illiquidity panel (stacked stat rows), (5) stress callout, (6) re-profile control. The add-fund overlay opens as a bottom sheet. ProfileBar becomes a sheet. The ForecastBand never requires horizontal scroll — it reflows, it does not pan.

---

### §4 Components & charts

#### 4.1 ForecastBand (the hero) — full encoding

The S11 ForecastBand instantiates the Foundation **ForecastBand** primitive (Part 2.5: "Forecast bands — cashflow/projection (base line + shaded optimistic/downside band)"). S11 specifics:

**What it plots.** Two related quantities over the same 8-quarter axis, in two switchable views (toggle, default = **Net**):

- **View A — Net (default).** A single banded series of **net cash flow** = calls − distributions per quarter. Plotted around a **zero baseline**: bars/area **above zero = net outflow** (you must find cash); **below zero = net inflow** (distributions exceed calls). The base **line** traces `net.base`; the shaded **band** spans `net.downside` (worst, larger outflow) to `net.optimistic` (best). A second, fainter band traces **cumulative net** (`cum_net`) as an overlaid line set — this is the one that actually answers "peak strain," so the **peak of `cum_net.downside`** carries the stress marker.
- **View B — Calls vs Distributions.** Two stacked banded series sharing the axis: **calls** drawn as outflow (above zero, warm/neutral fill from the data ramp), **distributions** drawn as inflow (below zero, mint/positive fill). Each is itself a band (base line + downside/optimistic shading). This view makes G3 visible: distributions are visually a *different* series, never merged into calls.

**Band shading + ordering (the locked encoding — G2).**
- Three scenarios per series: **downside · base · optimistic.** Render as: a solid **base line** (1.5px, `--ink` at full opacity for net; series colour for calls/dist) plus a filled **band** between downside and optimistic at ~18–24% fill opacity, with the **downside edge** drawn slightly heavier (1px dashed at `--negative`/`--over` hue) because downside is the liquidity-relevant edge.
- **Ordering is enforced and must be visible.** For **cumulative net call** and for **net**: `downside ≥ base ≥ optimistic` (worst liquidity = downside = top of band for outflow). For the **distributions** series: `optimistic ≥ base ≥ downside` (more distributions = better = top of band). The shading direction therefore flips between the calls band (downside on top) and the distributions band (optimistic on top) — this is correct and intentional; the legend states it.
- **The downside band is the headline of Q7.** It must read as the visually dominant edge, not an afterthought. A small inline annotation pins `peak_net_call_eur` to the `stress_quarter` (e.g. a dot + label "€50,600 · 2027Q3").

**Series ordering / z-order (back→front):** optimistic fill → downside fill → band envelope → base line → cumulative-net line → stress marker → hover crosshair. Distributions render behind calls in View B so the calls (the thing that costs you) sit forward.

**Axis & ticks.** X = 8 quarter labels from `projection.quarter_labels` (`2026Q3 … 2028Q2`), year boundaries marked with a faint divider. Y = EUR, EU-formatted, **tabular/mono** (Part 2.3), zero line emphasised (1px `--line` solid). No second Y-axis — calls and distributions share the EUR scale (View B), net is on the same scale (View A).

**Caption (mandatory, 1–2 sentences, Part 2.5).** Generated from the data, e.g.: *"Your calls peak in the next year then taper; distributions overtake calls from 2027Q3, turning your book cash-positive. In a downside market the cumulative call could reach €50,600 by 2027Q3 — well within your €6.6m of liquid capacity."* The caption is `--muted`, caption type, directly under the chart.

**Badges on the chart.** A single ConfidenceBadge/ProvenancePopover anchor on the chart title binds to `projection.provenance` (method, `curve_method` per strategy, `synthetic`, `snapshot_id`). When any strategy's `curve_method == "default-curve"`, a small **"modelled from sector defaults"** badge appears (state 3, §7) — clicking it lists which strategies fell back. The whole projection is RVPI/NAV-derived, so a **projected** marker (§9) sits on the chart frame; the realized DPI fact lives in the badge popover, not on the band.

**"What if I add Fund Y" overlay control.** A segmented/dropdown control docked on the ForecastBand top-right (see §4.5). Selecting a fund + ticket renders a **ghost band** (dashed envelope, lower opacity, distinct hue) of the *after* scenario over the *before* band, plus a verdict pill. This is the Q4 interaction.

#### 4.2 Capacity headline

A MetaCard-styled but **text-led** block (the answer must read as a sentence, not a stat tile):

- **Primary line (display/h1, mono for the number):** **"You can commit ~€230k more."** Bound to `capacity.investable_capacity_eur` (231600 → rounded to "~€230k" for the headline; exact "€231,600" in the supporting line and ProvenancePopover). The "~" signals it is a modelled estimate.
- **Supporting line (caption, `--muted`):** *"Room to your 22% target (€1.76m) is €360k; we reserve €128k for calls due in the next year."* Bound to `capacity.{pm_target_pct (from investor), pm_target_eur, raw_capacity_eur, near_term_calls_eur, near_term_horizon_q}`.
- **Badges:** ConfidenceBadge/ProvenancePopover on the number (method `"pm_target − committed − next-4q base calls"`). Because `pm_target_pct` is typically synthetic (`capacity.provenance.synthetic == true`, `fields_synthetic:["pm_target_pct"]`), a **SyntheticTag** ("target % illustrative") sits on the target reference inside the supporting line — never on the holdings-derived parts.
- **State-dependent face:** see §7 — overcommitted → "No new commitments right now"; cold-start → full target as room; null wealth → "% room shown; € needs your wealth."

#### 4.3 Overcommitment chip

A compact status chip (the Foundation has no dedicated component; build it as a semantic status pill that pairs colour with glyph, Part 2.2):

- **States (colour + glyph, never colour-only):**
  - **Comfortable** — `--positive` dot + "●", label "Comfortable · downside peak uses <1% of liquidity." Shown when `overcommitted == false` and `overcommit_ratio` is small.
  - **Tight** — amber dot + "◐", label "Tight · downside peak uses {round(ratio*100)}% of liquidity." Shown when `0.75 < ratio ≤ 1.0` (derived from overlay verdict thresholds; T1-7 `_verdict`).
  - **Overcommitted** — `--negative`/`--over` + "▲", label "Overcommitted — downside peak exceeds liquid capacity." Shown when `overcommitted == true` (`overcommit_ratio > 1.0`). This is state 7; it also drives the red banner (§7).
  - **Capacity unknown** — `--gated`/neutral + "—", label "Add your wealth to assess overcommitment." Shown when `overcommit_ratio == null` (state 4, null wealth).
- **Binding:** `overcommitment.{overcommitted, overcommit_ratio, peak_net_call_eur, stress_quarter, liquid_capacity_eur}`.
- **Click → ProvenancePopover:** "peak downside cumulative net call €50,600 vs (wealth €8.0m − committed €1.4m = €6.6m liquid capacity)." The chip is the headline of Q1/Q7.

#### 4.4 Illiquidity panel (Q3)

Three small stat lenses in one card (Scorecard-style small-multiples, Part 2.6), each a number + label + badge:

1. **Illiquid stock** — "€1.43m locked up" — `illiquidity.illiquid_stock_eur` (= `nav_total_eur` + `unfunded_total_eur`; both shown in the popover). Caption: "NAV still held + committed-but-uncalled."
2. **% of wealth** — "17.9% of your wealth" — `illiquidity.illiquidity_pct_wealth`. Caption: "share of investable wealth that is illiquid in practice." Null → "—, add wealth" (state 4).
3. **Practical illiquidity** — "100% has no redemption path" — `illiquidity.practical_illiquidity_pct` (= 100 − semiliquid share). Caption: "share of NAV with no semi-liquid exit." This line is the bridge to the re-profile control (§4.7): high % → "consider semi-liquids."

Each lens carries a ProvenancePopover (method `"NAV + unfunded; semiliquid by fund.is_semiliquid"`). No absolute-total hero (Part 0.2 "relative, not absolute") — the % lenses lead, the € is supporting.

#### 4.5 "What if I add Fund Y" overlay control (Q4)

The most interactive element on the surface. Docked on the ForecastBand top-right; expands to a small inline panel (or bottom sheet on mobile):

- **Fund picker** — a searchable select of **open** funds (`fund.is_open == true`), filterable by strategy. Each option shows name + strategy badge + `min_eur`. k-anon: funds with `feeder_n_investors < 10` show as "a {strategy} fund" (bound from the overlay's `weighted_average_portfolio` naming rule). When seeded from S20/S13, the fund is pre-selected.
- **Ticket input** — a € amount with a default = `min_eur` and a slider/stepper; validates ≥ `min_eur`.
- **Apply → overlay render.** Calls `GET /api/projection?add_fund={id}&add_eur={int}` (and reflects into `/api/liquidity` for the post-commit verdict). Renders:
  - a **ghost band** (dashed, distinct hue, ~50% opacity) for the *after* scenario over the live *before* band;
  - a **delta callout**: "Peak downside call rises €50,600 → €{peak_net_call_after}";
  - a **verdict pill** mirroring the chip language, from the overlay `verdict`:
    - **safe** (`ratio_after ≤ 0.75`) — green "Safe to add."
    - **tight** (`0.75 < ratio ≤ 1.0`) — amber "Possible, but tight on liquidity."
    - **unsafe** (`> 1.0`) — red "Would overcommit you — wait for distributions."
    - **blocked** (not open or below `min_eur`) — grey "This fund isn't open / below the minimum."
- **Bindings:** overlay block `{added_eur, min_ok, is_open, peak_net_call_before, peak_net_call_after, overcommit_ratio_after, verdict, fund_id|null, name}`.
- **Clear** — removes the ghost band, returns to the base view.
- **Info-not-advice:** the verdict is framed as liquidity mechanics ("would overcommit"), never as "you should." A small "this is a liquidity check, not investment advice" footnote.

#### 4.6 Stress / downside callout (Q7)

A compact card surfacing the downside band's headline number so it doesn't get lost in the chart: **"If markets stall: peak net call €50,600 at 2027Q3."** Bound to `stress.{downside_peak_eur, stress_quarter}` and `overcommitment.overcommitted`. A link "show the downside path" focuses the ForecastBand on the downside edge (raises its opacity, dims base/optimistic). Caption: "the worst-case cumulative cash you'd need to fund calls if distributions slow." When overcommitted, this card adopts the red treatment and pairs with the §7 banner.

#### 4.7 Re-profile control (Q5)

Two toggles **[+ Semi-liquid] [+ Secondary]** that re-run the model with re-profiled curves (`GET /api/projection?reprofile=Semi-liquid|Secondary`):

- On apply, render a **second ghost band** (the flatter re-profiled path) over the base, plus a caption from the returned `band_width_reduction_pct` and `peak_net_call_delta_eur`: **"Shifting eligible holdings to semi-liquids would flatten your cashflow band by ~34% and cut peak strain by €X."**
- These toggles are mutually exclusive with the add-fund ghost (only one ghost band at a time to keep the chart legible) — applying one clears the other, with a quiet inline note.
- Binding: re-profile block `{band_width_reduction_pct, peak_net_call_delta_eur}` plus the re-profiled bands. SyntheticTag — re-profiling is illustrative ("assumes eligible holdings move to the semi-liquid curve").

---

### §5 Data bindings (← API)

All numeric elements bind `value → visual`, `provenance → badge/popover` (Part 0.2). Primary endpoints: `GET /api/liquidity?investor_id=` (scalars + verdict) and `GET /api/projection?investor_id=&q=8` (bands). Overlays add query params to `/api/projection`.

| On-screen element | ← Endpoint.path |
|---|---|
| **ForecastBand — Net view base line** | `projection.net.base[]` |
| **ForecastBand — Net band (down/opt)** | `projection.net.downside[]`, `projection.net.optimistic[]` |
| **ForecastBand — cumulative-net line + stress marker** | `projection.cum_net.{base,downside,optimistic}[]`; marker at `argmax(cum_net.downside)` ↔ `stress.stress_quarter` |
| **ForecastBand — Calls series (View B)** | `projection.calls.{base,downside,optimistic}[]` |
| **ForecastBand — Distributions series (View B)** | `projection.distributions.{base,downside,optimistic}[]` |
| **X-axis quarter labels** | `projection.quarter_labels[]` |
| **Chart provenance / curve-method badge** | `projection.provenance.{method, curve_method{}, synthetic, snapshot_id}` |
| **Capacity headline number** | `capacity.investable_capacity_eur` |
| **Capacity supporting line** | `capacity.{pm_target_eur, pm_committed_eur, raw_capacity_eur, near_term_calls_eur, near_term_horizon_q}` |
| **Capacity SyntheticTag** | `capacity.provenance.{synthetic, fields_synthetic[]}` |
| **Overcommit chip state + label** | `overcommitment.{overcommitted, overcommit_ratio}` |
| **Overcommit chip popover** | `overcommitment.{peak_net_call_eur, stress_quarter, liquid_capacity_eur}` |
| **Illiquidity lens 1 (stock €)** | `illiquidity.illiquid_stock_eur` (+ `nav_total_eur`, `unfunded_total_eur` in popover) |
| **Illiquidity lens 2 (% wealth)** | `illiquidity.illiquidity_pct_wealth` |
| **Illiquidity lens 3 (practical %)** | `illiquidity.practical_illiquidity_pct` (+ `semiliquid_share`) |
| **Commit-verdict line (in capacity card)** | `commit_verdict.{can_commit, headroom_eur, verdict, note}` |
| **Stress callout** | `stress.{downside_peak_eur, stress_quarter}` |
| **Realized DPI fact (badge/popover only)** | `realized.{distributions_to_date_eur, dpi}` |
| **Add-fund overlay (ghost band + verdict)** | `/api/projection?add_fund=&add_eur=` → overlay `{peak_net_call_before, peak_net_call_after, overcommit_ratio_after, verdict, min_ok, is_open, fund_id, name}` |
| **Re-profile overlay** | `/api/projection?reprofile=Semi-liquid\|Secondary` → `{band_width_reduction_pct, peak_net_call_delta_eur}` + re-profiled bands |
| **Snapshot id (frame footer / PDF)** | `snapshot_id` (both endpoints; must match) |

**Loading order.** Fire `/api/liquidity` and `/api/projection` in parallel on entry. The decision strip (capacity + chip) can render from `/api/liquidity` alone; the ForecastBand waits for `/api/projection`. Skeletons (Part 9) cover each zone independently — the answer (decision strip) should appear first.

**Realized-vs-projected enforcement (G3).** The UI binds the band exclusively to `projection.*`. The `realized.*` branch binds only to the "Realized to date" line inside the provenance popover and the §9 realized badge. **No element may bind a `projection.distributions.*` value into a field labelled or styled as realized/received.** This is a hard rule; see §9 and §11.

---

### §6 Interactions

1. **View toggle (Net ⇄ Calls vs Distributions).** Segmented control on the ForecastBand. Switching re-renders the same data in the other encoding (≤150ms, Part 2.4); the caption updates. State persists per session.
2. **Hover a quarter.** A vertical crosshair snaps to the quarter; a tooltip shows, for that quarter: calls (base + band range), distributions (base + band range), net, and cumulative-net (base + downside) — each as a provenance chip. EU-formatted, mono. On the stress quarter the tooltip is pinned-open by default with the peak label. Keyboard: arrow-left/right move the crosshair quarter-by-quarter; the tooltip is announced via ARIA live region.
3. **Add-fund overlay (Q4).** Pick fund + ticket → Apply → ghost band + verdict (§4.5). Changing the ticket re-fires the overlay call (debounced ~300ms). Clear removes the ghost. Pre-seeded when entered from S20/S13.
4. **Re-profile (Q5).** Toggle [+ Semi-liquid]/[+ Secondary] → flatter ghost band + reduction caption (§4.7). Mutually exclusive with add-fund ghost.
5. **Stress focus.** "Show the downside path" link in the stress callout dims base/optimistic and raises the downside edge on the band; toggles back.
6. **Provenance everywhere.** Every number is a click target → ProvenancePopover (cohort/method/snapshot/fields). The chart title's badge shows `curve_method` per strategy so the user can see which strategies are modelled from defaults vs fitted empirically.
7. **Capacity → action.** A secondary CTA on the capacity card, "see funds that fit" → S13 Funds (carries the headroom € as a filter ceiling). The commit verdict never says "buy"; it says "you can commit up to ~€X" (info-not-advice).
8. **Cohort independence.** S11 is investor-cashflow, not peer-relative, so changing the cohort in the left rail does **not** re-render the bands or capacity (those are your-book figures). The one cohort-aware element is an optional peer-stress comparison line ("peers at your wealth carry ~X% illiquidity") — if present, only that line re-resolves on cohort change; everything else is inert to the cohort. (This avoids the trap of implying liquidity is peer-derived.)
9. **PDF export.** The Foundation PDFExportButton exports the current S11 view (with whichever ghost band/overlay is active) as a clean PowerPoint-style page: capacity headline, chip, the ForecastBand with its caption, the illiquidity trio, and a provenance/snapshot footer.

---

### §7 Seven states (T1-7 §7 → render)

S11 renders all seven Foundation states (Part 9). The driving fields come from the T1-7 contract.

1. **Happy** — full `/api/liquidity` + `/api/projection`. Decision strip + ForecastBand + supporting trio all populated. Chip "Comfortable." (The worked-example snapshot is this state.)
2. **Cold-start** (`cold_start == true`, `n_funds == 0`). No holdings → **no band**. The ForecastBand zone shows an empty-but-designed placeholder: "Your cashflow forecast appears once you make your first commitment." Capacity headline shows the **full target as room** (`investable_capacity_eur == pm_target_eur`, no near-term-call reserve) — framed as headroom, not failure ("You have ~€{X} of room to build your private-markets book"). Illiquidity panel reads all zeros ("nothing locked up yet"). Primary CTA routes to **S9 Architect** ("make your first commitment"). This is the FOMO-safe path (Part 9 Welcome).
3. **Thin / low-history** (`projection.provenance.curve_method[strat] == "default-curve"` for ≥1 strategy). Numbers still render; the chart carries the **"modelled from sector defaults"** badge; clicking it lists the affected strategies. No alarm — calm, factual. (RelaxBanner is not used here; this is a modelling-coverage badge, not a cohort relaxation.)
4. **Null wealth** (`investable_wealth_eur is None` → `capacity == null`, `capacity_blocked == "no investable_wealth"`, `overcommit_ratio == null`). The **bands still render** (they need no wealth). Capacity headline switches to: "Add your wealth to see how much you can commit" with a small inline editor link to the ProfileBar. Overcommit chip → "Capacity unknown" (neutral). Illiquidity shows € but `illiquidity_pct_wealth` → "—". The forecast is fully usable; only the capacity/overcommit assessment is gated on the missing input.
5. **Null perf / null NAV** (all `nav_eur ≤ 0` → `no_nav_for_distributions == true`, `projection.distributions.base == [0,…]`). Distributions band is flat at zero; the net path is **pure calls** (all outflow). Caption: "No NAV to project distributions from yet — your forecast shows calls only." The distributions series renders as a flat zero line (not absent), so the user sees *why* the net is all outflow. Never zero-fill silently.
6. **Fully-funded book** (`fully_funded == true`, every `unfunded == 0`). Calls band flat at zero; the surface is **distribution-only**. Caption: "Your existing commitments are fully called — no future calls; this shows expected distributions." `should_i_commit` keys off the pure capacity gap (no near-term-call reserve to subtract), so capacity = `raw_capacity_eur`.
7. **Overcommitted** (`overcommit_ratio > 1.0`, `overcommitted == true`). The **red liquidity-stress state.** A prominent red banner across the top of the centre stage: **"Overcommitted — in a downside market your cumulative calls (€{peak_net_call_eur}) by {quarter} would exceed your €{liquid_capacity_eur} of liquid capacity. Wait for distributions before committing more."** Overcommit chip → red "▲ Overcommitted." Capacity headline → "No new commitments right now" with `headroom_eur == 0`. Commit verdict → `can_commit:false`, `verdict:"unsafe"`. The downside edge of the band is rendered emphasised by default. The add-fund overlay still works but every verdict will read "unsafe/tight." This state also lights the ProfileBar cross-surface chip (§2).
8. **Error / degraded** (404 unknown investor, or engine failure). 404 → the standard "we couldn't find that investor" empty state. Engine failure → cached last-good snapshot with a quiet "showing your last saved forecast ({snapshot_id})" banner (Part 9 state 7); never a blank chart, never a fabricated number; Ask Alpha falls back gracefully.

**Cross-cutting:** not-significant deltas greyed; **SyntheticTag** on `pm_target_pct`-derived figures (capacity) and on the re-profile illustration; **loading** = calm zone-level skeletons.

---

### §8 Copy deck

All strings are i18n keys (EN shown; structure for DE per Part 10). EU number/currency formatting throughout.

**Titles & nav**
- Rail label: `liquidity.nav` → "Liquidity"
- Surface title: `liquidity.title` → "Liquidity & Cashflow"
- Surface subtitle: `liquidity.subtitle` → "What your private-markets book will demand and return — and how much you can safely commit."

**Capacity headline**
- `capacity.headline` → "You can commit ~{amount} more."
- `capacity.support` → "Room to your {target_pct}% target ({target_eur}) is {raw_capacity}; we reserve {near_term_calls} for calls due in the next year."
- `capacity.synthetic_tag` → "target % illustrative"
- `capacity.null_wealth` → "Add your wealth to see how much you can commit."
- `capacity.cold_start` → "You have ~{amount} of room to build your private-markets book."
