- `capacity.overcommitted` → "No new commitments right now."
- `capacity.cta` → "See funds that fit"

**Overcommit chip**
- `overcommit.comfortable` → "Comfortable"
- `overcommit.comfortable_sub` → "Downside peak uses under {pct}% of your liquid capacity."
- `overcommit.tight` → "Tight"
- `overcommit.tight_sub` → "Downside peak uses {pct}% of your liquid capacity."
- `overcommit.over` → "Overcommitted"
- `overcommit.over_sub` → "Downside peak exceeds your liquid capacity."
- `overcommit.unknown` → "Capacity unknown"
- `overcommit.popover` → "Peak downside cumulative call {peak} vs liquid capacity {capacity} (wealth {wealth} − committed {committed})."

**ForecastBand**
- `band.title` → "Calls vs distributions — next 8 quarters"
- `band.toggle_net` → "Net"
- `band.toggle_split` → "Calls vs distributions"
- `band.legend_band` → "Shaded band = downside to optimistic; line = base case."
- `band.legend_order` → "For calls, the top of the band is the downside (more cash needed); for distributions, the top is the optimistic case."
- `band.caption_happy` → "Calls peak within the next year, then taper; distributions overtake calls from {crossover_quarter}, turning your book cash-positive. In a downside market, cumulative calls could reach {peak} by {stress_quarter} — within your {capacity} of liquid capacity."
- `band.caption_calls_only` → "No NAV to project distributions from yet — your forecast shows calls only."
- `band.caption_dist_only` → "Your commitments are fully called — this shows expected distributions, no future calls."
- `band.default_curve_badge` → "Modelled from sector defaults"
- `band.default_curve_detail` → "These strategies use default curves (not yet enough history): {strategies}."
- `band.empty_cold_start` → "Your cashflow forecast appears once you make your first commitment."

**Illiquidity panel**
- `illiq.title` → "How illiquid your book is in practice"
- `illiq.stock` → "{amount} locked up"
- `illiq.stock_sub` → "NAV still held plus committed-but-uncalled."
- `illiq.pct_wealth` → "{pct}% of your wealth"
- `illiq.practical` → "{pct}% has no redemption path"
- `illiq.practical_sub` → "Share of NAV with no semi-liquid exit."

**Stress callout**
- `stress.title` → "If markets stall"
- `stress.body` → "Peak net call {peak} at {quarter}."
- `stress.sub` → "Worst-case cumulative cash to fund calls if distributions slow."
- `stress.focus_link` → "Show the downside path"

**Add-fund overlay**
- `overlay.label` → "What if I add a fund?"
- `overlay.pick` → "Choose a fund"
- `overlay.ticket` → "Ticket size"
- `overlay.kanon_name` → "a {strategy} fund"
- `overlay.delta` → "Peak downside call: {before} → {after}"
- `overlay.verdict_safe` → "Safe to add"
- `overlay.verdict_tight` → "Possible, but tight on liquidity"
- `overlay.verdict_unsafe` → "Would overcommit you — wait for distributions"
- `overlay.verdict_blocked` → "This fund isn't open, or your ticket is below the minimum."
- `overlay.footnote` → "This is a liquidity check, not investment advice."
- `overlay.clear` → "Clear"

**Re-profile**
- `reprofile.semiliquid` → "+ Semi-liquid"
- `reprofile.secondary` → "+ Secondary"
- `reprofile.result` → "Shifting eligible holdings to {format} would flatten your cashflow band by ~{pct}% and cut peak strain by {amount}."
- `reprofile.synthetic` → "Illustrative — assumes eligible holdings move to the {format} curve."

**Overcommitted banner (state 7)**
- `state7.banner` → "Overcommitted — in a downside market your cumulative calls ({peak}) by {quarter} would exceed your {capacity} of liquid capacity. Wait for distributions before committing more."

**Realized vs projected**
- `realized.tag` → "Received"
- `projected.tag` → "Projected"
- `realized.popover` → "Distributions received to date: {amount} (DPI {dpi}). The forecast below is projected from current NAV and is not money received."

**Degraded**
- `degraded.banner` → "Showing your last saved forecast ({snapshot_id})."
- `error.404` → "We couldn't find that investor."

---

### §9 Badge rendering — realized vs projected (G3)

The single most important visual discipline on this surface: **the user must never read a projected distribution as cash they already have.** The forecast band is entirely projection (NAV/RVPI-derived); the only realized fact is `realized.distributions_to_date_eur` / `realized.dpi`.

**Rendering rules:**
- **The whole ForecastBand carries a "Projected" marker.** A small pill on the chart frame: amber-ish neutral "Projected" (distinct from the SyntheticTag amber, but in the same "not verified fact" family — Part 0.2 "synthetic/illustrative fields visibly labelled"). It anchors a popover: "This forecast is projected from your current NAV (RVPI). It is not money received."
- **Realized fact lives in the provenance popover, visually separated.** Inside the ForecastBand's provenance popover, a clearly delineated "Received to date" block shows `realized.distributions_to_date_eur` and `realized.dpi`, with a divider and the label "Realized (fact)" vs "Forecast (projected)." When `distributions_to_date_eur > 0`, this block is non-empty; on the worked fixture it is €0 / DPI 0.0 and renders "No distributions received yet."
- **No summing.** No on-screen figure adds a `projection.distributions.*` value to a `realized.*` value. The capacity, overcommit, illiquidity, and band numbers are all forward/stock figures; the realized DPI is reported only as context. If a future composite needs both, they render as two labelled rows, never a sum.
- **DPI vs RVPI distinction** (mirrors S7): if a small "to date" strip is shown, DPI (realized) uses a solid/filled treatment, projected distributions use the band/dashed treatment — so realized and projected are visually different at a glance.
- **Provenance method strings are surfaced verbatim** in popovers: realized → `"sum holding.distributions_eur (realized)"`; projection → `"per-holding (commitment−paid_in)×deploy_curve, nav×harvest_curve; bands ×band_multipliers"`. This lets a sceptical user (or compliance) see the band is modelled.
- **Synthetic vs projected are different badges.** SyntheticTag (amber pill) flags *imputed inputs* (e.g. `pm_target_pct` in the capacity card). The "Projected" marker flags *forward model output* (the band). A figure can carry both (capacity is projected output of a partly-synthetic input) — render both badges, SyntheticTag on the input reference, Projected on the output.

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive** (Part 3 breakpoints; §3 layout details):
- **≥1280px:** decision strip (2-up) + hero ForecastBand + supporting trio (3-up). No scroll.
- **768–1279px:** cohort drawer + Ask Alpha launcher; ForecastBand full-width; trio reflows 3→2+1; quarter labels compress with year dividers.
- **<768px:** single-column stack in reading order (capacity → chip → band → illiquidity → stress → re-profile); add-fund/overlay as bottom sheets; ProfileBar as a sheet. The ForecastBand reflows (thinner bars, abbreviated/rotated quarter labels, band shading preserved) — it **never** requires horizontal scroll or panning (Part 10).

**Accessibility** (WCAG-AA, Part 10):
- **Never colour-only.** Calls vs distributions distinguished by **position** (above/below zero) + label + fill pattern, not just hue. Band edges: downside drawn dashed + labelled, optimistic solid-faint — so colour-blind users read the band by line style and position. Overcommit chip and all verdicts pair colour with a **glyph + word** (●/◐/▲/—, "Comfortable/Tight/Overcommitted/Unknown").
- **Chart data as a table.** A "view data" affordance under the ForecastBand exposes the full 8-quarter × {calls, distributions, net, cum_net} × {base, downside, optimistic} grid as a real `<table>` with scope headers — the canonical accessible representation of the band (and what PDF export and screen readers use).
- **Keyboard nav.** Full tab order: view toggle → chart (arrow keys move the quarter crosshair) → add-fund control → re-profile toggles → stress link → "view data" → PDF export. Focus rings on every interactive element. The hover tooltip content is mirrored to an ARIA live region on crosshair move.
- **Tabular figures.** All numbers mono/tabular so columns align (Part 2.3); currency and decimals localized (Part 10).
- **Large titles.** Chart title h2 minimum (Part 2.3); legend never below caption size; the band renders large (Part 2.3 / §3 hero placement).
- **Motion.** Band animates value-in once, subtly (≤150ms, Part 2.4); ghost-band overlays fade in without bounce; respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (no value-in animation when set).
- **Screen-reader summary.** The surface exposes an off-screen one-line summary: "Liquidity forecast: capacity €{X}, overcommitment {status}, peak downside call €{peak} at {quarter}."

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

A build passes S11 when **all** of the following hold (visual + interaction + binding):

**Bands shaded + ordered (G2)**
- [ ] The ForecastBand renders **shaded bands**, never bare lines — base line + downside/optimistic fill on every forward series.
- [ ] Ordering is visible and correct: for net & cumulative-net, downside ≥ base ≥ optimistic (worst liquidity on top); for distributions, optimistic ≥ base ≥ downside. The legend states the flipped direction.
- [ ] The Net ⇄ Calls-vs-Distributions toggle re-renders the same data in both encodings.
- [ ] The stress marker pins `peak_net_call_eur` to `stress_quarter` on the downside edge.

**Capacity present**
- [ ] The capacity headline renders "You can commit ~€X more" bound to `capacity.investable_capacity_eur`, with the supporting line (target, raw capacity, reserved near-term calls) and a SyntheticTag on the synthetic target %.
- [ ] On the worked fixture the headline reads ~€230k (exact €231,600 in the popover), target €1,760,000, near-term calls €128,400.

**Overcommitment**
- [ ] The overcommit chip reflects `overcommitted`/`overcommit_ratio` with colour **+ glyph + word** (Comfortable/Tight/Overcommitted/Unknown).
- [ ] State 7 (overcommitted) renders the red banner naming peak (€{peak}) and `stress_quarter`, sets headroom to €0, and lights the ProfileBar cross-surface chip.

**Add-fund overlay works (Q4)**
- [ ] Selecting a fund + ticket and applying renders a **ghost band** for the after-scenario over the before-band, a peak-delta callout, and a verdict pill (safe/tight/unsafe/blocked) from the overlay block.
- [ ] k-anon: a fund with `feeder_n_investors < 10` shows as "a {strategy} fund" with `fund_id` nulled.
- [ ] Min-ticket / not-open inputs yield the "blocked" verdict; over-large tickets push the verdict to unsafe.
- [ ] Entry from S20/S13 pre-seeds the overlay and renders the ghost band immediately.

**Re-profile (Q5)**
- [ ] Each re-profile toggle renders a flatter ghost band and a caption from `band_width_reduction_pct` / `peak_net_call_delta_eur`; mutually exclusive with the add-fund ghost.

**Realized vs projected (G3)**
- [ ] The whole band carries a "Projected" marker; realized DPI/distributions-to-date appear only in a separated "Received (fact)" block, never summed into any forward figure.
- [ ] No on-screen element binds a `projection.distributions.*` value into a realized/received field.

**Provenance, states, frame**
- [ ] Every number carries ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover; the chart badge surfaces `curve_method` per strategy.
- [ ] All seven states render (happy, cold-start→Architect, thin→default-curve badge, null-wealth→capacity gated/bands still shown, null-NAV→calls-only, fully-funded→dist-only, overcommitted→red banner, error→cached/404).
- [ ] EUR integers, EU-formatted, no cents, single currency (G9).
- [ ] No horizontal scroll on the chart at any breakpoint; "view data" table, keyboard crosshair, and ARIA summary present.
- [ ] PDF export produces a clean single-page view (headline + chip + band + caption + illiquidity + provenance/snapshot footer).
- [ ] `/api/liquidity` and `/api/projection` are the only data sources; `snapshot_id` matches across both; swapping to live is a base-URL change.

---
*Build S11 to this spec; compose the ForecastBand, badges, and states from the Frontend Foundation; bind every number to `/api/liquidity` or `/api/projection` with its provenance. The surface's job is one calm, trustworthy answer: how much can I safely commit — shown, not asserted.*


---

## Surface S12 — Projection

> **Frontend surface spec for Claude Design.** One Moonfare Alpha copilot surface, built to depth. This document inherits the design system, component library, seven-state model, and chart language from the **Frontend Foundation** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md` — Parts 2, 3, 9) and binds to the backend contract of use-case **T1-13 — Forward Portfolio Projection** (`Alpha_UseCase_Briefs/deep/T1-13_Forward_Projection.md`). It does **not** redefine `ForecastBand`, `SyntheticTag`, the seven states, or the `{value, provenance}` numeric envelope — it composes them. Where this spec says "ForecastBand" or "SyntheticTag" it means the foundation component; where it says "the seven states" it means Foundation Part 9. Build every state, not just the happy path.

S12 is the **value telescope** of the copilot. Its sibling S11 (Liquidity & Cashflow) owns the near-term *cash* mechanics — calls minus distributions over 8 quarters. S12 owns the *value* evolution over the long horizon: how NAV grows and then harvests (the J-curve), how the TVPI/MOIC/DPI trajectories unfold, and **when the distribution profile turns net-positive** (the crossover). The two surfaces share one endpoint, one set of quarter labels, and one band-ordering contract; S12 turns on the `value_projection` block whenever a projection trigger (`scenario`, `add_fund`, `reprofile`, `pacing`, or `q>8`) is present. Treat them as two telescopes on the same tripod, never two dashboards.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Answer *"what could my portfolio look like in the future, under different assumptions?"* — as **bands, never points**, with the macro assumptions that produced each band **visibly surfaced and tagged illustrative**, and with the single most human question — *"when do I stop putting money in and start getting it back?"* — answered as a dated, plain-language headline.

The surface is a forward-looking, scenario-driven NAV-and-value picture that a non-technical investor ("a doctor, not a pro") can read in one glance and a sophisticated investor can drill into. It is deliberately *honest in two directions*: it shows the J-curve drag (the short-term dip when you add a fund) right alongside the long-term lift, and it never presents a macro multiplier as a prediction.

**User questions this surface must answer** (each maps to a backend eval id; see T1-13 §1):

- **Q1 — "What could my portfolio look like in two years?"** → `nav_projection`. The banded NAV path is the spine of the surface; the 8-quarter (2-year) view is the default.
- **Q2 — "Show me my J-curve trajectory."** → `jcurve_path`. The TVPI/MOIC trajectory with its phase (`recovery` / `trough` / `harvest`) named in words.
- **Q3 — "Give me a base / optimistic / downside view."** → `scenario_bands`. All three bands at once, with the assumptions that produced them on screen.
- **Q4 — "Project with my current portfolio only."** → `current_only`. The clean baseline with no overlays.
- **Q5 — "What if I keep investing at my current pace?"** → `pacing_continued`. A forward stream of synthetic commitments injected; flagged illustrative.
- **Q6 — "What happens if I add Fund X?"** → `add_fund_proj`. A new curve injected; the familiar short-term dip then long-term lift, with a liquidity verdict.
- **Q7 — "How would a more secondary-heavy / semi-liquid plan change my future liquidity & distributions?"** → `reprofile_proj`. The re-profiled bands plus a two-sided "earlier liquidity vs lower terminal upside" answer.
- **Q8 — "When should my distribution profile improve?"** → `distribution_crossover`. The crossover marker and the dated headline.

**What this surface is not.** It is not the near-term cash planner (that is S11). It is not an advice engine — projection is *information, not a forecast or a recommendation*. Every scenario carries the macro caveat. It is not a single-line "this is what will happen" chart — a bare point line is, per the backend contract (G2), a build failure, and the UI must make a single line literally impossible to render.

---

### §2 Entry & nav

**Rail position.** S12 sits in the left navigation rail as **"Projection,"** in the forecast cluster, immediately after **"Liquidity"** (S11) and before **"Funds"** (S13). Order in the rail: *… Pacing · Liquidity · **Projection** · Funds …*. The adjacency to Liquidity is intentional and must be preserved — the two are a forecast pair (cash then value).

**Entry points** (all land on S12 with the appropriate state and, where relevant, a pre-applied overlay):

1. **Rail click "Projection."** Default entry → happy state, `scenario=all`, `q=8`, current book only.
2. **Deep-link from S11 (Liquidity).** S11's "What if I add Fund Y" overlay and its capacity headline both expose a **"See the long-term picture →"** affordance that opens S12 with the same `add_fund` / `reprofile` overlay pre-applied and the horizon expanded to `q=10` (so the crossover and J-curve are visible). The scenario state and the added fund carry over via query params (`?add_fund=&add_eur=&q=10`), so the two surfaces never disagree.
3. **Deep-link from S13 (Funds).** The fund-compare "fit-to-your-gap" banner exposes **"Project with this fund →"**, opening S12 with `add_fund={that fund_id}` and `add_eur` defaulted to that fund's `min_eur`.
4. **Deep-link from S10 (Pacing).** The pacing schedule exposes **"Project this plan forward →"**, opening S12 with `pacing=continue` (or a posted custom plan) pre-applied.
5. **Ask Alpha.** Any of Q1–Q8 asked in the chat pane renders an inline mini-ForecastBand in the answer and offers **"Open the full projection →"**, which opens S12 with the matching scenario/overlay state. (Ask Alpha rules from Foundation Part 5 apply unchanged: every number a provenance chip, the "grounded · snapshot {id}" footer, the five-sentences/show-detail registers.)
6. **Next Best Action (S20).** When the integrator's rationale chains through a projection ("…adding €X keeps you net-cash-positive by 2027Q3"), the rationale's projection clause links into S12 with that overlay applied.

**Frame.** S12 inherits the persistent global frame (Foundation Part 3): the ProfileBar on top (risk & horizon chips re-render the page — see §6), the CohortBuilder on the left (used here only for the *peer-band* context line, never to slice the projection itself), and Ask Alpha floating bottom-right. PDF export (PowerPoint-style) is present on the view. Meta⇄Detail switching is instant and full-screen; **no vertical scroll** on the analytical view — the chart, scenario controls, assumptions panel, and crossover marker all fit one screen at ≥1280px (see §3 and §10 for how they reflow below that).

---

### §3 Layout

**Desktop ≥1280px — single full-screen analytical view, no scroll.** A three-zone composition: a **left control column** (scenario + overlays + assumptions), a dominant **centre chart stage** (the banded NAV path with the crossover marker), and a **right metrics column** (terminal outcomes + the crossover headline + the J-curve phase). The DPI path lives as a slim companion strip beneath the NAV stage, sharing the x-axis.

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ProfileBar — You · 53 · €8m · target 22% · …            [light/dark][mode][PDF ▾]        │
├──────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────┤
│ COHORT   │  PROJECTION — Forward NAV & value path                         │  TERMINAL @ q8 │
│ (peer    │                                                                │  (2028Q2)      │
│  context │  Scenario:  [ Base ]  [ Optimistic ]  [ Downside ]  [All ▣]    │  NAV  €1.37m   │
│  only)   │  Overlays:  [+ Add a fund ▾]  [Re-profile ▾]  [Keep my pace ◯] │   base, range  │
│          │  Horizon:   ◀ 2y  [●——]  10y ▶   (q = 8)                       │  TVPI 1.247    │
│          │                                                                │  MOIC 1.247    │
│          │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  DPI  0.13     │
│          │  │            BANDED NAV PATH (ForecastBand)                  │ │ ─────────────  │
│          │  │   €                                  ╱⎺⎺ optimistic       │ │  J-CURVE       │
│          │  │       shaded band base↕              ───base              │ │  Phase:        │
│          │  │                       ╲__ downside                        │ │  Recovery ↗    │
│          │  │   ◆ crossover 2027Q3 (distribution profile improves)      │ │  Trough: past  │
│          │  │   └──────────────────────────────────────── quarters ──── │ │ ─────────────  │
│          │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  CROSSOVER     │
│          │  Caption: "Your NAV is projected to climb from €1.02m to …"    │  Distribution  │
│          │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  profile       │
│          │  │   DPI PATH (slim companion strip, shared x-axis)          │ │  improves      │
│          │  │   realized DPI ●—— then projected ····  → 1.0 beyond view  │ │  ~2027Q3       │
│          │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  (base; range  │
│          │                                                                │   Q2–Q1'28)    │
│          │  ▼ ASSUMPTIONS  ⚑illustrative                                  │                │
│          │   Base g×1.0 calls×1.0 dist×1.0 · Opt g×1.3 … · Down g×0.6 …   │                │
└──────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────┘
```

**Centre chart stage — the banded NAV path (ForecastBand).**
- The ForecastBand renders the NAV path: a solid **base line**, a **shaded band** between downside and optimistic, with the band fill in `--brand-tint` at low opacity and the base line in `--brand`. Optimistic is the upper edge, downside the lower edge (band ordering is guaranteed by the backend, G2 — see §6). The chart renders **large** (Foundation: forecast charts render large; chart titles at h2 minimum).
- A **distribution-crossover marker** (`◆` diamond glyph + a thin vertical guide line) sits at the base-band `net_cash_cross_q` quarter, labelled inline **"distribution profile improves."** The marker carries a small range bracket (the optimistic and downside crossover quarters) so the reader sees the marker is itself a band, not a point. (See §4 for the component.)
- A **1–2 sentence CaptionBlock** sits directly under the chart (Foundation rule: a caption under every chart). Example in §8.
- When `scenario` is collapsed to a single band (Base / Optimistic / Downside selected, not "All"), the band shading reduces to that one scenario rendered as a line with a *faint* base reference behind it — never a bare unlabelled line; the assumptions panel still echoes that scenario's multipliers (G2/G5).

**DPI path — slim companion strip.** Beneath the NAV stage, sharing the same x-axis and quarter labels, a short ForecastBand of the DPI trajectory. The **realized DPI anchor** (the starting point, fact) renders as a filled dot with a solid leader; everything forward renders **dotted/marks-style** to encode "projection, not realized" (G3). A `1.0` reference rule marks the unity crossover; if DPI reaches 1.0 only beyond the visible horizon, the rule sits at the right edge with a ghosted "→ beyond view" affordance (see the null/insufficient state, §7).

**Left control column.**
- **Scenario toggle group** — a segmented control: `Base · Optimistic · Downside · All`. Default `All` (the three-band view). This is the primary interaction; selecting a single scenario collapses the band (see §6).
- **Overlay controls** — three composable overlays, each off by default:
  - **`+ Add a fund`** — a dropdown/typeahead of open funds (`is_open`) with a EUR amount field (defaulting to the fund's `min_eur`); injects a new curve (Q6).
  - **`Re-profile`** — a small segmented control `Off · Secondary · Semi-liquid` that swaps eligible forward holdings onto shorter-J-curve / earlier-DPI curves (Q7).
  - **`Keep my pace`** — a toggle that injects a forward stream of synthetic commitments at the investor's historical cadence (Q5); when on, exposes an inline "edit the plan" link that opens the cadence/ticket/strategy-weights mini-form (the T1-4 pacing hook).
- **Horizon control** — a slider/stepper `2y … 10y` (`q ∈ 8..40`, clamped). Default `q=8`; deep-links from S11/S13 may arrive at `q=10`. Moving the horizon re-renders the band and may surface/hide the DPI-unity and self-funding crossovers.
- **Assumptions panel** — see below; pinned to the bottom of the control column, always visible (not behind a toggle by default; it may *collapse* but the headline assumption line and the `⚑ illustrative` SyntheticTag are always shown).

**Assumptions panel (visible, tagged).** This is a first-class, on-screen panel — never a hidden popover — because surfacing the assumptions is an explicit acceptance criterion. It lists, per band that is currently shown, the three multipliers that produced it: the value-creation growth multiplier `g`, the calls multiplier, and the distributions multiplier, plus the worded `scenario_basis` for non-base bands. The entire panel carries a **SyntheticTag** ("illustrative — not a forecast") because the macro multipliers are illustrative stress factors, not estimates (G5). The base band's *curve* is data-driven (fitted from history) and is **not** tagged synthetic; only the optimistic/downside *perturbations* and any thin-strategy default-curve fallbacks carry tags (see §9 for the exact tag placement rules). Example layout:

```
▼ ASSUMPTIONS                                           ⚑ illustrative — not a forecast
 ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ Band        Value-creation   Calls    Distributions   Basis                        │
 │ ● Base        g ×1.00        ×1.00      ×1.00          fitted from your history     │
 │ ● Optimistic  g ×1.30        ×0.80      ×1.20  ⚑       stronger value creation,     │
 │                                                        multiple expansion, faster  │
 │                                                        exits                        │
 │ ● Downside    g ×0.60        ×1.25      ×0.65  ⚑       multiple compression,        │
 │                                                        stalled exits, faster calls  │
 │                                                        (J-curve drag persists)      │
 └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 Curve calibration: Mid-Cap Buyout, Private Credit, Growth/Tech — modelled from
 strategy defaults ⚑   ·   [show how this was computed]
```

**Right metrics column.**
- **Terminal outcomes scorecard** — at the selected horizon (e.g. q8 = 2028Q2): NAV (base, with the downside–optimistic range), TVPI, MOIC, DPI. Each value carries the embedded numeric badges (ConfidenceBadge is not relevant here — these are model outputs, not cohort statistics — but the **ProvenancePopover** is, exposing the NAV-identity method, the scenario assumptions, and the snapshot; see §5/§9).
- **J-curve phase card** — the `jcurve.phase` (Recovery / Trough / Harvest) with a directional glyph (↗ recovery, ↘ approaching trough, ⌐ harvest plateau) and the trough quarter (or "past" if the book is already climbing). A one-line note from `jcurve.note`.
- **Crossover headline card** — the plain-language distribution-profile headline (Q8): *"Your distribution profile improves around 2027Q3"* with the base/optimistic/downside range beneath, and a secondary line for the self-funding quarter and the DPI-unity quarter (or "beyond this horizon — extend to see it").

**Overlay result strip (conditional).** When an overlay is active (add-fund / re-profile / pacing), a thin **result strip** appears between the chart and the caption summarizing the *delta* vs the no-overlay baseline — e.g. for add-fund: *"Apex Mid Buyout IV drags your J-curve for ~4 quarters, then adds ~€426k terminal NAV. Liquidity: safe."* This strip is where the two-sided honesty lives (drag then lift; earlier liquidity vs lower terminal upside).

**Responsive.** See §10. In brief: at 768–1279px the left control column collapses to a control bar above the chart and the right metrics column moves below the DPI strip as a horizontal scorecard row; the assumptions panel becomes a collapsible accordion (collapsed by default but the SyntheticTag remains visible on the collapsed header). At <768px everything stacks single-column: scenario toggles → chart (reflowed, no horizontal scroll) → crossover headline → DPI strip → terminal scorecard → assumptions accordion. The ForecastBand never requires horizontal scroll at any width.

---

### §4 Components & charts

All chart types here are from the Foundation's **approved** list (Part 2.5): the **ForecastBand** (base line + shaded optimistic/downside band) is the only chart type for the NAV and DPI paths. **No** rejected types (no Sankey/capital-flow, no scatter, no radar) appear on this surface. Product-wide chart rules apply: large titles (h2 min), a 1–2 sentence caption under every chart, tabular/mono figures, no flashy transitions (value animates in once, subtly, then settles).

**1. Banded NAV path — `ForecastBand` (the spine).**
- *Encoding.* x-axis: quarters (`quarter_labels`, e.g. `2026Q3 … 2028Q4`); y-axis: NAV in EUR (mono/tabular, localized €, EU formatting). Base line solid `--brand`; band fill between `downside` and `optimistic` in `--brand-tint` at ~18% opacity; band edges as 1px hairlines. The y-axis starts at a sensible floor (not forced to 0) but the floor is labelled so the band height is not misread.
- *Crossover marker* (see component 6) overlaid at the base crossover quarter.
- *Hover.* A vertical crosshair snaps to the nearest quarter; a tooltip shows that quarter's NAV for all visible bands (downside / base / optimistic) with their values, plus the quarter's calls and distributions, plus the per-quarter value-creation growth `g_q` if "show detail" is on. Every number in the tooltip is a provenance chip.
- *Collapsed state.* When a single scenario is selected, the band collapses to that scenario's line with a faint base reference behind it.

**2. DPI path — `ForecastBand` (slim companion).**
- *Encoding.* Shared x-axis with the NAV chart; y-axis DPI ratio (float). The **realized anchor** point renders as a filled `--ink` dot at q0; the forward trajectory renders dotted to encode "projected, not realized" (G3 — realized vs marks are visually distinct and never summed). A horizontal `1.0` reference rule (DPI unity = capital fully returned). The crossover-to-unity marker, if within horizon, mirrors the NAV crossover styling.
- *Caption.* One line, e.g. "DPI starts at your realized 0.13 and is projected to climb toward 1.0; unity falls beyond this 10-quarter view."

**3. Scenario toggle group — segmented control (`Base · Optimistic · Downside · All`).**
- Default `All`. Single-tap collapses to one band; tapping `All` restores the three-band view. Active segment uses `--brand` fill; the three scenario segments each carry a tiny colour swatch matching the band edge colour so the toggle and the chart are visually linked. Keyboard-navigable (arrow keys move between segments; Enter selects). ARIA `role="radiogroup"`.
- This control is the primary interaction and the headline acceptance behaviour: **toggling the scenario must visibly change the band.**

**4. Add-fund control — typeahead + amount.**
- A `+ Add a fund` button opens a typeahead of **open** funds (`is_open=1`); each result row shows the fund name (or, where `feeder_n_investors < 10`, a k-anon bucket label such as "a Mid-Cap Buyout fund"), its strategy badge, and `min_eur`. Selecting a fund reveals a EUR amount field pre-filled with `min_eur`. An "Apply" action injects the curve. A small "remove" chip clears the overlay.
- The control surfaces backend gates inline: if `add_eur < min_eur` or the fund is not open → an inline note "below minimum" / "not currently open" and the resulting `verdict:"blocked"` (bands still render, illustrative). The liquidity verdict (`safe` / `tight` / `unsafe` / `blocked`) renders as a small pill in the overlay result strip, colour-coded with a glyph (never colour alone).

**5. Re-profile control & Keep-my-pace toggle.**
- **Re-profile** — segmented `Off · Secondary · Semi-liquid`. Selecting Secondary or Semi-liquid swaps eligible forward holdings onto the dampened-J / flat curves and shows the *before/after* crossover delta and the two-sided terminal-NAV delta in the result strip.
- **Keep my pace** — a toggle; when on, the projection injects forward commitments at the historical cadence and the result strip notes "modelled at your historical pace — illustrative" (the pacing assumption is itself SyntheticTagged in the assumptions panel). An inline "edit the plan" link opens a mini-form (cadence in quarters, ticket €, strategy weights) that POSTs a custom plan (the T1-4 hook).

**6. Distribution-crossover marker — composite glyph + guide + range bracket.**
- A `◆` diamond on the base line at the `net_cash_cross_q` quarter, a thin vertical `--line` guide from the diamond to the x-axis, an inline label "distribution profile improves," and a small horizontal **range bracket** spanning the optimistic crossover quarter (earlier) to the downside crossover quarter (later) so the marker visibly represents a band, not a point. Hover → a popover with all three crossover definitions (net-cash, self-funding, DPI-unity) and their quarter labels per band, plus the provenance method.
- When `improves_within_horizon` is false (no crossover in the visible window) the marker is replaced by an end-of-axis chip: "no net-distribution crossover within {N} quarters — extend the horizon."

**7. Terminal scorecard & J-curve phase card — `Scorecard` (Foundation component).**
- Small-multiple stat cards (NAV / TVPI / MOIC / DPI at the horizon), each with a ProvenancePopover. The J-curve phase card is a single Scorecard tile with the phase glyph + note.

**8. Assumptions panel — bespoke table + `SyntheticTag`.**
- A compact table (band × {g, calls, dist, basis}); the panel header carries the SyntheticTag. The "curve calibration" footer line lists strategies whose curves fell back to defaults (`curve_method == "default-curve"`) with a SyntheticTag and a "show how this was computed" provenance link. See §9 for exact tag rules.

**9. Reused Foundation components.** `ProvenancePopover` (on every numeric value), `SyntheticTag` (assumptions + default-curve + pacing + add-fund overlay), `PDFExportButton`, `RelaxBanner` (only in the peer-context line, never on the projection itself), `CaptionBlock`. No `CohortBuilder` slicing drives the projection — the cohort rail is present (frame) but its selection affects only the peer-context caption line, not the bands.

---

### §5 Data bindings (← /api/projection)

All bindings are to **`GET /api/projection`** (T1-13 §5). The endpoint is shared with S11; S12 turns on the `value_projection` + `crossover` blocks by sending a T1-13 trigger. The standard call for the default S12 view:

`GET /api/projection?investor_id={id}&q=8&scenario=all`

Query params the surface controls emit:
- `q` ← Horizon control (`8..40`, clamped).
- `scenario` ← Scenario toggle group (`base | optimistic | downside | all`).
- `add_fund` + `add_eur` ← Add-fund control.
- `reprofile` ← Re-profile control (`Secondary | Semi-liquid`).
- `pacing` ← Keep-my-pace toggle (`continue`; or a POST body for a custom plan via the edit-the-plan form).

**Element ← field bindings.** Every numeric value binds the `value` to the visual and the `provenance` to the badge/popover (Foundation 0.2/0.3 — a number never renders bare).

| UI element | ← API field |
|---|---|
| Quarter axis labels (NAV + DPI charts) | `quarter_labels[]` |
| Horizon readout ("q = 8" / "2 years") | `horizon_q` |
| **NAV ForecastBand** — base line | `value_projection.nav.base[]` |
| NAV band — upper edge | `value_projection.nav.optimistic[]` |
| NAV band — lower edge | `value_projection.nav.downside[]` |
| **DPI ForecastBand** — forward trajectory | `value_projection.dpi.{base,optimistic,downside}[]` |
| DPI realized anchor (q0 dot) | `realized.dpi` |
| Terminal NAV scorecard (value + range) | `value_projection.nav.base[-1]` (+ `downside[-1]`, `optimistic[-1]` for range) |
| Terminal TVPI | `value_projection.tvpi.base[-1]` (+ band edges) |
| Terminal MOIC | `value_projection.moic.base[-1]` (+ band edges) |
| Terminal DPI | `value_projection.dpi.base[-1]` |
| J-curve phase card | `value_projection.jcurve.{phase, trough_q, note}` |
| Current/now reference (chart left anchor) | `realized.{nav_now_eur, tvpi_now, dpi}` |
| **Crossover marker** quarter (base) | `crossover.net_cash_cross.base` (index → `quarter_labels`) |
| Crossover range bracket | `crossover.net_cash_cross.{optimistic, downside}` |
| Crossover headline | `crossover.headline` |
| Crossover secondary lines | `crossover.{self_funding_cross, dpi_unity_cross}` (per band) |
| **Assumptions panel** rows | `value_projection.provenance.assumptions.{base,optimistic,downside}.{g,calls,dist}` |
| Assumptions "basis" column | `value_projection.provenance.scenario_basis.{optimistic,downside}` (base = "fitted from your history") |
| Assumptions SyntheticTag | `value_projection.provenance.macro` == `"illustrative; not a forecast"` (G5) |
| Default-curve footer line | `value_projection.provenance.curve_method{}` (keys whose value == `"default-curve"`) |
| ProvenancePopover (all values) | `value_projection.provenance.{method, curve_method, snapshot_id, synthetic}` |
| **Add-fund** result strip | `add_fund.{fund, fund_id, added_eur, min_ok, is_open, terminal_nav_before_eur, terminal_nav_after_eur, terminal_moic_after, jcurve_drag_q, overcommit_ratio_after, verdict}` |
| Add-fund overlaid bands | `add_fund.value_projection_after.{nav,tvpi}` |
| **Re-profile** result strip | `reprofile.{crossover_before_q, crossover_after_q, crossover_pulled_forward_q, peak_net_call_delta_eur, terminal_nav_delta_eur}` |
| Re-profile overlaid bands | `reprofile.value_projection` |
| **Pacing** overlaid bands | `pacing.value_projection` |
| Pacing illustrative tag | `pacing.provenance.assumptions.pacing` (ends in "illustrative") |
| Peer-context caption line (optional) | reuses `resolve_cohort` framing from `/api/cohort` (read-only here; never slices the bands) |
| Snapshot footer | `snapshot_id` |

**Binding rules carried from the contract:**
- **G2 (bands, never points).** Every forward series the UI renders is a 3-key band dict (`{base, optimistic, downside}`); when `scenario` collapses to one band, the UI still renders the assumptions for that band. The UI must **never** render a forward NAV/TVPI/MOIC/DPI as a single point without its band context.
- **G3 (realized vs marks).** `realized.*` (fact) and `value_projection.*` (projection) bind to *separate* visual treatments and are never summed in any UI total. The DPI realized anchor and the projected DPI trajectory are visually distinct (solid dot vs dotted line).
- **G5 (illustrative macro).** The assumptions panel binds `provenance.macro` to the SyntheticTag; `scenario_basis` to the basis column. No element labels a scenario as a prediction.
- **G9 (EUR integers).** NAV / terminal-NAV / delta-EUR values are integers, localized at render (€, EU thousands separators); ratios (TVPI/MOIC/DPI) are floats to 2–3 dp.

**Realized-anchor note.** On a fixture where all `distributions_eur` are NAV-derived projections (realized DPI = 0), the realized anchor renders as a `0.00 (realized)` dot and the DPI trajectory grows only from projected distributions — the UI must not imply any realized return where there is none (G3).

---

### §6 Interactions

**Scenario toggles change the band (primary, acceptance-critical).**
- Tapping `Base` / `Optimistic` / `Downside` collapses the ForecastBand to that single scenario (rendered as a line with a faint base reference behind it); the terminal scorecard, crossover marker, and DPI strip all re-bind to that scenario; the assumptions panel highlights that scenario's row and keeps its multiplier triplet visible (G2: never a bare line). Tapping `All` restores the three-band view. The transition is a single subtle value-in animation (≤150ms), then settle — no flashy morphing.
- This re-render is a *client-side band selection* if all three bands are already loaded (`scenario=all`, the default) — instant, no network round-trip. If the surface was loaded with a single scenario and the user widens to `All`, a fetch with `scenario=all` runs (calm skeleton on the chart only).

**Horizon change.** Moving the horizon slider/stepper re-fetches with the new `q` (clamped 8..40) and re-renders the band, DPI strip, crossover marker, and terminal scorecard. Crossovers that fell beyond the old window may now appear; the crossover card updates its "beyond horizon" lines accordingly. Calm skeleton during fetch.

**Add-fund injects a curve.**
- Selecting a fund + amount + Apply fetches with `add_fund` & `add_eur`. The chart re-renders showing the **overlaid** post-add bands; a *ghost* of the pre-add base line remains faintly visible so the delta is legible. The result strip narrates the two-sided story: the short-term J-curve drag (`jcurve_drag_q` quarters of dip) then the terminal-NAV lift (`terminal_nav_after_eur − terminal_nav_before_eur`), plus the liquidity `verdict` pill.
- Gate behaviour: below-minimum / closed → inline "blocked" note + `verdict:"blocked"`, bands still rendered (illustrative); overcommitting amounts → `verdict:"unsafe"` with the downside-peak quarter named. k-anon: `feeder_n_investors < 10` → the fund shows as a bucket label, `fund_id` null, no deep-link to S13.

**Re-profile.** Selecting Secondary / Semi-liquid fetches with `reprofile` and shows before/after: the crossover marker animates from `crossover_before_q` to `crossover_after_q` (a single move, the pulled-forward case being the common one), and the result strip states the two-sided trade ("liquidity ~2 quarters earlier; terminal NAV slightly lower").

**Keep my pace.** Toggling on fetches with `pacing=continue`; the bands lift (more committed capital → higher terminal NAV) and near-term calls rise (so the crossover may push later). The result strip and the assumptions panel both carry the illustrative pacing tag. "Edit the plan" opens the mini-form; submitting POSTs the custom plan and re-renders.

**Hover / crosshair.** Hovering the NAV chart shows the snapped-quarter tooltip (all visible bands' NAV, calls, dists, and `g_q` in detail mode); hovering the DPI strip shows the snapped DPI per band and whether the realized anchor or a projected point is under the cursor. Hovering the crossover marker opens the three-definition popover. All tooltip numbers are provenance chips.

**Provenance (Foundation 0.2).** Every numeric value — terminal scorecard figures, tooltip values, assumptions multipliers, crossover quarters — exposes a **ProvenancePopover** ("show how this was computed") binding to `value_projection.provenance` (method = the NAV identity `NAV[q]=NAV[q-1]·(1+g)+calls−dists`; the per-key `curve_method`; the scenario `assumptions`; the `macro` label; the `snapshot_id`). The assumptions panel's "show how this was computed" link opens the same popover scoped to curve calibration.

**ProfileBar risk/horizon re-render.** The ProfileBar's **risk** and **horizon** chips are toggles that re-render the page (Foundation Part 3). On S12 they alter the projection inputs: changing risk re-weights the implied forward pacing/strategy mix used by the pacing overlay (and the default-curve selection emphasis); changing the investor horizon adjusts the default `q` and the illiquidity tolerance reflected in the verdicts. These re-render only the affected elements (the bands, crossover, scorecard), not the whole frame.

**No-overlay reset.** A persistent "Current portfolio only" reset chip clears all overlays and returns to `scenario=all`, `q=8` (Q4, the clean baseline).

---

### §7 Seven states

S12 renders all seven Foundation states (Part 9). The defining S12 behaviours: **bands are always ordered** (downside ≤ base ≤ optimistic for NAV/TVPI/MOIC/DPI; the cash net path inverts — guaranteed by the backend G2, so the UI may assert and trust the ordering); **assumptions are always surfaced and tagged**; and **cold-start derives the projection from the Architect** rather than showing a broken chart.

1. **Happy.** Full `value_projection` + `crossover` + cash block. Three ordered NAV bands, the crossover marker placed and ranged, the DPI strip with its realized anchor, the terminal scorecard, the J-curve phase, and the visible assumptions panel with its SyntheticTag. This is the layout drawn in §3.

2. **Cold-start (`n_funds == 0`).** The current book is empty → the current-only bands are empty/zeroed and `crossover.*` are null. The surface does **not** render a broken/empty chart. Instead it shows the **cold-start derivation from the Architect (S9)**: a banner *"You don't hold any funds yet — here's what a projection could look like if you build the architecture we'd suggest,"* with a **"Build from the Architect →"** CTA, and — if the user enables **Keep my pace** or **Add a fund** — the projection is built *entirely from the injected forward commitments* (the backend supports projecting from injected commitments alone), so "what if I start investing at pace X" still answers with a real banded chart. The assumptions panel (with SyntheticTag) and the pacing/add-fund illustrative tags are present. This is the S12 expression of the Foundation rule "cold-start routes to Architect; never a broken analytics screen."

3. **Thin / low-history (default-curve fallback for ≥1 strategy).** When `curve_method[key] == "default-curve"`, the bands still render (MOIC still anchored to the `fund_track_record` band) but the assumptions panel's calibration footer lists those strategies with a SyntheticTag: *"Mid-Cap Buyout, Private Credit, Growth/Tech — modelled from strategy defaults."* The peer-context caption may show a RelaxBanner in words if the cohort was relaxed (cohort affects only the context line, not the bands).

4. **Low coverage / null wealth (`investable_wealth_eur is None`).** The value bands and crossover **still compute** (they need no wealth). Only the overlay **verdict** is suppressed: add-fund shows `verdict: null` + a quiet note *"capacity check unavailable — we don't have your investable wealth"* (`capacity_blocked: "no investable_wealth"`). The bands and crossover are unaffected — the UI must not blank the chart for a missing wealth denominator. (There is no CoverageBadge on the bands themselves; coverage applies to look-through surfaces, not projection.)

5. **Null / insufficient.** (a) **Null NAV** (all `nav_eur ≤ 0`): NAV starts at 0 and grows only from injected calls (no value pulse on zero NAV); the surface flags `no_nav_for_value` with an inline note *"no current NAV to grow — projection reflects future calls only,"* projected dists are 0, and the crossover still works off the cash net path. (b) **DPI-unity beyond horizon** (`dpi_unity_cross == null`): the DPI strip's `1.0` rule sits at the right edge with "→ beyond view," and the crossover card reads *"DPI reaches 1.0 beyond this {N}-quarter horizon — extend the horizon to see it."* Never zero-fill, never imply unity reached. (c) **IRR** anywhere on the trajectory obeys the vintage suppression rule (`irr_note: "too early (<4y)"` for `vintage_year > 2022`) — though S12 leads with NAV/TVPI/DPI, not IRR.

6. **Gated.** Where a projection figure or overlay requires paid entitlement, the affected element shows the Foundation **GatedState**: the locked card with *"Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate"* and the *"5,500 total investors"* headline — never "too small" or "I can't." The rest of the surface renders normally around the gated element.

7. **Error / degraded.** Engine/model down → render the **cached last-good** projection with a quiet banner *"Showing your last saved projection — live engine unavailable,"* never a blank chart and never a fabricated number. `404 unknown investor` → the standard not-found state. Unknown `add_fund` id → the rest of the payload still renders and the add-fund control shows an inline *"we couldn't find that fund"* (from `add_fund.error`) without breaking the bands. Loading anywhere = calm skeletons on the affected element only (the chart, a scorecard tile), not a full-page spinner.

Plus the cross-cutting rules: **not-significant** deltas (rare here, as projection outputs are model values not cohort statistics) render greyed; **SyntheticTag** on every illustrative value (assumptions, default curves, pacing, add-fund overlays); **loading = calm skeletons**.

---

### §8 Copy deck

All copy is structured for English + German (Foundation i18n; DACH-heavy audience). Numbers/currency localized (€, EU formatting). Tone: calm, plain, information-not-advice; the "five sentences" register is the default in chat, the analytical register on the surface.

**Surface title / nav.** Rail label: "Projection" / DE "Projektion." Page title: "Forward NAV & value path" / "Künftige NAV- und Wertentwicklung."

**Chart captions (CaptionBlock).**
- NAV (happy): "Your NAV is projected to climb from €1.02m today to €1.37m in two years (base case), within a range of €1.29m–€1.62m. These are model paths, not a forecast." / DE: "Ihr NAV steigt voraussichtlich von 1,02 Mio. € heute auf 1,37 Mio. € in zwei Jahren (Basisszenario), in einer Spanne von 1,29–1,62 Mio. €. Dies sind Modellpfade, keine Prognose."
- DPI: "DPI starts at your realized 0.13 and is projected to climb toward 1.0; unity falls beyond this 10-quarter view."

**Scenario toggle.** "Base · Optimistic · Downside · All" / "Basis · Optimistisch · Negativ · Alle."

**Assumptions panel.**
- Header: "Assumptions" + tag "illustrative — not a forecast" / "Annahmen — illustrativ, keine Prognose."
- Base basis: "fitted from your history."
- Optimistic basis: "stronger value creation, multiple expansion, faster exits."
- Downside basis: "multiple compression, stalled exits, faster calls (J-curve drag persists)."
- Default-curve footer: "{strategies} — modelled from strategy defaults."

**Crossover headline (Q8).** "Your distribution profile improves around {quarter}." with sub-line "base {q}; optimistic {q}, downside {q}." Self-funding sub-line: "Your book becomes self-funding around {quarter}." DPI-unity (when beyond horizon): "Capital is fully returned (DPI 1.0) beyond this {N}-quarter horizon — extend to see it." / DE: "Ihr Ausschüttungsprofil verbessert sich um {quarter}."

**Overlay result strips.**
- Add-fund: "{Fund} drags your J-curve for ~{n} quarters, then adds ~€{Δ} terminal NAV. Liquidity: {verdict}."
- Add-fund blocked: "Below the fund's minimum / this fund isn't currently open — shown for illustration only."
- Re-profile: "A {Secondary/Semi-liquid} tilt pulls your distribution profile ~{n} quarters earlier; terminal NAV {higher/lower} by ~€{Δ} — the trade-off for earlier liquidity."
- Pacing: "Modelled at your historical pace — illustrative. Continuing to invest lifts terminal NAV and pushes your crossover slightly later."

**J-curve phase.** "Phase: Recovery — your portfolio is past its J-curve trough and climbing." / "Phase: Erholung."

**Cold-start banner.** "You don't hold any funds yet — here's what a projection could look like if you build the architecture we'd suggest." CTA: "Build from the Architect →."

**Null-wealth note.** "Capacity check unavailable — we don't have your investable wealth, so the bands still show but the affordability verdict is hidden."

**Null-NAV note.** "No current NAV to grow — this projection reflects future calls only."

**Error banner.** "Showing your last saved projection — the live engine is unavailable."

**Gated.** "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate." (exact Foundation copy) + "5,500 total investors."

**Disclaimer (persistent, small, under the chart).** "Projections are illustrative model outputs, not financial advice or a forecast of returns." / DE: "Projektionen sind illustrative Modellergebnisse, keine Finanzberatung und keine Renditeprognose."

**Ask Alpha suggested prompts (context chips on S12).** "What could my portfolio look like in two years?" · "When does my distribution profile improve?" · "What if I add a fund?" · "Show me my J-curve."

---

### §9 Badge rendering (SyntheticTag on macro assumptions)

The **SyntheticTag** (Foundation: a soft-amber "illustrative" pill, `--synthetic`; never styled as verified fact) is the load-bearing badge on this surface. Exact placement rules:

1. **Assumptions panel header — always tagged.** The panel carries a SyntheticTag bound to `value_projection.provenance.macro == "illustrative; not a forecast"`. This is mandatory and persistent: the macro multipliers are illustrative stress factors, not estimates (G5). When the panel is collapsed (responsive), the tag stays visible on the collapsed header.

2. **Optimistic / Downside multiplier rows — tagged.** Each non-base scenario row in the assumptions table carries a small inline SyntheticTag next to its `g`/calls/dist multipliers, because the perturbations are illustrative. The **base row is NOT tagged** — the base band's *curve* is data-driven (fitted from history); only its perturbations are synthetic. (If the base *curve* itself fell back to a default, see rule 3.)

3. **Default-curve calibration footer — tagged per strategy.** Strategies whose `curve_method == "default-curve"` (thin history) are listed in the calibration footer with a SyntheticTag and a "show how this was computed" link. Strategies fit empirically (`empirical-ls`) are **not** tagged. This makes the "modelled from strategy defaults vs fitted from your data" distinction visible.

4. **Pacing overlay — tagged.** When Keep-my-pace is on, the injected forward commitments are illustrative; the result strip and the assumptions panel both carry a SyntheticTag bound to `pacing.provenance.assumptions.pacing` (the label ends in "illustrative").

5. **Add-fund overlay — tagged.** The add-fund result strip carries a SyntheticTag (the overlay is an illustrative what-if; `add_fund.provenance.macro` also reads "illustrative; not a forecast"). The injected fund's own curve, if a default, contributes a calibration-footer tag too.

6. **Realized anchor — NOT tagged.** The DPI realized anchor and `realized.*` figures are fact (G3) and must **never** carry a SyntheticTag. The visual distinction between realized (solid dot, solid leader) and projected (dotted) is the encoding; the SyntheticTag belongs only to the projected/illustrative side.

7. **proxy-seeded trajectories.** Where `provenance.proxy_seeded == true` (a `perf_is_proxy=1` holding seeded from a fund-level proxy), the affected trajectory's ProvenancePopover notes "seeded from fund-level proxy"; this is a provenance note, not a SyntheticTag (it is a real-but-approximate seed, not an illustrative assumption).

**Interaction with ProvenancePopover.** SyntheticTag and ProvenancePopover are complementary: the tag *flags* illustrative-ness at a glance; the popover *explains* the computation. A SyntheticTagged value still exposes its provenance popover (the assumptions multipliers, the macro label, the snapshot). A number never renders with only colour to signal its status — the amber pill always carries the word "illustrative."

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive (Foundation Part 3 breakpoints; no analytical chart ever needs horizontal scroll).**

- **≥1280px (desktop / boardroom projector).** The three-zone layout of §3: left control column, dominant centre chart stage (NAV ForecastBand large + DPI companion strip), right metrics column. Everything on one screen, no vertical scroll. This is the primary, must-be-flawless layout.
- **768–1279px (tablet / advisor).** The left control column collapses into a **control bar** above the chart (scenario toggle group + overlay menus + horizon stepper in one row, wrapping if needed). The right metrics column moves **below** the DPI strip as a horizontal scorecard row (NAV / TVPI / MOIC / DPI / J-curve / crossover as side-by-side tiles). The assumptions panel becomes a **collapsible accordion** (collapsed by default, but the SyntheticTag stays on the collapsed header). The chart reflows to full width; no horizontal scroll.
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go).** Single-column stack, in reading order: scenario toggle group → NAV ForecastBand (reflowed, thinner, still no horizontal scroll; crossover marker label may move to a chip beneath the chart if cramped) → crossover headline card → DPI strip → terminal scorecard (2×2 of small tiles) → overlay controls (as a bottom sheet triggered by an "Adjust scenario" button) → assumptions accordion. The ProfileBar becomes a sheet. PDF export remains available.

In all widths the band fill, base line, and crossover marker keep their relative encoding; the chart never degrades to a single point line, and the assumptions remain reachable with their tag.

**Accessibility (Foundation Part 10; WCAG-AA).**

- **Never colour-only.** Band membership is distinguished by line style and labelled edges (optimistic = upper, downside = lower), not colour alone; the crossover marker uses a glyph (`◆`) + label, not just a coloured point; the liquidity verdict pill pairs colour with a glyph and the word (safe/tight/unsafe/blocked); the SyntheticTag pairs amber with the word "illustrative"; realized vs projected uses solid-vs-dotted, not colour.
- **Contrast.** All text and chart strokes meet AA against `--bg`/`--surface` in both light and dark token sets. The band fill opacity is chosen so the base line stays AA-legible over it.
- **Keyboard navigation.** The scenario toggle group is a `radiogroup` (arrow keys move, Enter selects). The horizon stepper, overlay menus, and "Current portfolio only" reset are all tabbable with visible focus rings. The chart exposes a keyboard-accessible "view data" affordance.
- **Chart-as-table.** Every chart (NAV band, DPI strip) offers a **"view data"** affordance that reveals the underlying values as an accessible table — quarter × {downside, base, optimistic} for NAV/DPI, plus the crossover quarters and the assumptions multipliers. This satisfies the Foundation rule that chart data is available as a table behind a "view data" affordance.
- **ARIA.** The ForecastBand has an `aria-label` summarizing the trajectory ("NAV projected from €1.02m to €1.37m base, range €1.29m–€1.62m, over 8 quarters; distribution profile improves around 2027Q3"); interactive controls carry ARIA roles/labels; tooltips are announced on focus.
- **Motion.** Value-in animation once, ≤150ms, then settle; respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (no animation when set). No parallax, bounce, or confetti.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

A build of S12 is accepted when **all** of the following hold (the two headline criteria from the use-case brief are bolded):

1. **Scenario toggles change the band.** Selecting Base / Optimistic / Downside visibly collapses the ForecastBand to that scenario (line + faint base reference); selecting All restores the three-band view; the terminal scorecard, crossover marker, and DPI strip all re-bind to the selected scenario; the change is instant when all bands are pre-loaded (`scenario=all`). A bare unlabelled single line is never rendered (G2).
2. **Assumptions are surfaced and tagged.** The assumptions panel is visible on the analytical view (not hidden behind a popover); it lists, per shown band, the `g`/calls/dist multipliers and the worded basis; it carries a SyntheticTag bound to `provenance.macro == "illustrative; not a forecast"`; the non-base rows and default-curve strategies carry their own SyntheticTags; the base row's fitted curve is not tagged.
3. **Bands are ordered.** For every quarter, the rendered NAV/TVPI/MOIC/DPI bands satisfy downside ≤ base ≤ optimistic (the UI trusts and may assert the backend G2 ordering); the band never inverts.
4. **The crossover marker is present and ranged.** A `◆` marker + guide + inline label sits at the base `net_cash_cross_q`; a range bracket spans the optimistic/downside crossover quarters; the plain-language headline ("distribution profile improves around {quarter}") is shown; when there is no crossover in the horizon, the end-of-axis "extend the horizon" chip replaces the marker.
5. **Realized vs projected are visually distinct and never summed (G3).** The DPI realized anchor (solid dot) is distinct from the dotted projected trajectory; `realized.*` is never SyntheticTagged and never added into a projected total.
6. **Overlays work and tell the two-sided story.** Add-fund injects a curve and shows the short-term J-curve drag then the terminal-NAV lift plus the liquidity verdict pill; re-profile shows the crossover pulled forward plus the two-sided terminal-NAV delta; keep-my-pace lifts the bands, may push the crossover later, and is tagged illustrative; k-anon names funds with <10 investors as bucket labels.
7. **Every number has provenance.** Terminal scorecard figures, tooltip values, assumptions multipliers, and crossover quarters each expose a ProvenancePopover bound to `value_projection.provenance` (NAV-identity method, curve_method, assumptions, macro label, snapshot_id). No number renders bare.
8. **All seven states render.** Happy, cold-start (derives from the Architect; injected-commitment projection still answers), thin (default-curve footer tagged), null-wealth (bands compute; verdict suppressed), null-NAV / DPI-beyond-horizon (no zero-fill), gated (exact paid-viewers copy + 5,500 total), error/degraded (cached last-good, quiet banner, never blank or fabricated).
9. **Chart language matches the Foundation.** Only the ForecastBand is used (no rejected chart types); the chart renders large with an h2+ title; a 1–2 sentence caption sits under each chart; figures are tabular/mono and EUR-localized; no flashy transitions.
10. **Binding is a base-URL swap.** Placeholder data matches the T1-13 `value_projection` / `crossover` / `add_fund` / `reprofile` / `pacing` shapes exactly, so pointing `{{API}}` at the live backend requires no UI change.
11. **Responsive + accessible.** No horizontal scroll at any width; the layout reflows per §10; never colour-only; keyboard-navigable; chart-as-table "view data" available; AA contrast in light and dark; honours `prefers-reduced-motion`.
12. **Info-not-advice tone throughout.** The persistent disclaimer is present; no copy presents a scenario as a prediction or a recommendation; the macro caveat travels with every band.

---

*Build S12 to this document. It is one telescope in one copilot — sharing its endpoint, quarter labels, and band contract with S11 — not a standalone dashboard. The non-negotiables: bands never points, assumptions always visible and tagged illustrative, the crossover answered in plain dated language, and realized facts never confused with projected marks.*


---

## Surface S13 — Funds / Fund-vs-Fund

> **Deep frontend brief for Claude Design.** One surface, built to full depth, wired to the T1-8 backend contract. This document assumes the **Frontend Foundation** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`) is already in hand: the design tokens (Part 2.2), typography (2.3), the chart language (2.5), the component library (2.6 — `FundCard`, `ConfidenceBadge`, `CoverageBadge`, `SyntheticTag`, `ProvenancePopover`, `GatedState`, `RelaxBanner`, `PerfBars`, `PDFExportButton`), the persistent frame (Part 3), Ask Alpha (Part 5), the cohort builder (Part 6), and the seven-state model (Part 9). **This brief does not redefine any of those — it composes them.** Where it says "FundCard," it means the foundation's `FundCard`; where it says "a number renders," the three badges are implied and mandatory. The job here is the *Fund-vs-Fund* surface specifically: how the picker, the twin FundCards, the comparable-vintage table, the consistency and lifecycle gauges, the doc-thesis block, and the fit banner lay out, behave, bind, and degrade.

This is the **POC surface** — the one the sponsor demos. It must be the most polished page in the app. It also hosts two sub-capabilities inside the fund detail: **T2-7 Consistency** (the consistency score) and **T2-8 Lifecycle** (the buying↔harvesting gauge). Those are not separate surfaces; they are fields on the compare object and render inside the FundCards here.

---

### §1 — Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Let an investor — "a doctor, not a pro" — decide between two named private-markets opportunities with intelligence richer than a screener: head-to-head on performance (vintage-honest), fees and terms (apples-to-apples), geography and sector, deployment pace and lifecycle, delivery consistency, a document-grounded strategy thesis, and — the differentiator — **fit to this investor's own portfolio gap**. The page turns "Fund A or Fund B?" from a gut call into a structured, provenance-backed, side-by-side read that a non-expert can act on in two minutes.

**The user questions this surface must answer** (each maps to a visible affordance and to the §9 acceptance):

1. **"Compare Fund X with Fund Y."** → the default flow: pick A, pick B, read the twin FundCards + comparable-vintage table + fit banner.
2. **"Which has the more robust team and strategy?"** → the **consistency score** (T2-7) on each card + the **doc-derived thesis block** with team/key-man citations (or the "corpus not indexed" state).
3. **"Which buyout fund on the platform has the most attractive fees?"** → the **picker filter "most attractive fees"** (sorts within a strategy by fee percentile) — answerable *before* a comparison is even built.
4. **"Compare Fund X with the similar funds I didn't invest in before."** → the **"vs a fund I passed"** swap, which pulls a historical passed fund into the B slot.
5. **"Which of A/B better fits my portfolio?"** → the **fit-to-your-gap banner**, driven by `fit.better_fit`.

**What this surface is not.** It is not advice. Every state carries the BaFin disclaimer ("Information, not advice. Contact your sales associate.") from `compare.disclaimer`; no string anywhere reads "you should buy," "we recommend," or any imperative. The fit banner says a fund *closes a gap*, never that the user *should* commit. The CTA is always "Contact your sales associate," never "Invest."

**Primary persona fit.** Built for the analytical investor (who will read the table and the gauges) but legible to the five-sentences investor (who reads the fit banner, the consistency dots, and the lifecycle gauge labels, then asks Ask Alpha to summarize). Advisors reach it via the cockpit while acting-as a client; the "acting as {client}" banner from Part 7 persists above this surface and the `investor_id` flows into every call so the fit overlay is the client's, not the advisor's.

---

### §2 — Entry & navigation

**Rail entry.** "Funds" in the left nav rail (Part 3 grouping: *…Projection · **Funds** · What's next · Explain…*). Selecting it loads S13 in the centre stage, full-screen, no scroll, frame persists.

**Deep-link entries (all land on S13 with state pre-filled):**

- **From S14 Completion / S20 Next-Best-Action.** A recommended FundCard in those surfaces carries a secondary action **"Compare"**. Clicking it deep-links here with that fund pre-loaded into **slot A** and the picker open on slot B, scrolled to same-strategy candidates. The originating gap is passed through so the fit banner is immediately meaningful. URL/route param shape: `…/funds?a={fund_id}&pick=b`.
- **From S15 Explain.** The Explain surface ("why Moonfare recommends {fund}") carries **"Compare with another fund"** → deep-links with that fund in slot A.
- **From Ask Alpha.** When the user types "compare Fund X with Fund Y," the chat answer renders an inline summary **and** an action chip **"Open full comparison"** → routes here with both A and B resolved (`…/funds?a={x}&b={y}`). If Ask Alpha could only resolve one fund, it deep-links with A filled and B as the picker.
- **From any FundCard elsewhere** (S2 holdings, S13 itself): a fund's overflow menu has "Compare this fund."

**Picker as entry.** Landing on S13 with no params shows the **fund picker** as the hero (the empty/initial state, §7 state 0) — not an error, but an invitation: "Pick two funds to compare." Two large empty slots (A and B) sit above the picker list.

**Back / breadcrumb.** A slim breadcrumb when arrived via deep-link: "Next best action › Compare" with a back affordance to the originating surface. Swapping funds does not change the breadcrumb. The PDF export (Part 2.6 `PDFExportButton`) sits top-right of the stage on every state where a comparison is resolved.

**Route state is shareable.** `a`, `b`, and the picker filters serialize to the URL so an advisor can paste a comparison link; `investor_id` is carried in session/state, never in a shareable URL (it scopes the fit overlay and must not leak identity).

---

### §3 — Layout

The surface has two layout modes that the same page transitions between: **Picker mode** (choosing A and B) and **Comparison mode** (A and B resolved). They share the top region so selecting funds feels continuous, not like a page change.

#### 3.1 Picker mode (the chooser)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Compare two funds            [PDF ▾ (disabled until A&B chosen)]       │
│  ┌───────────── Slot A ─────────────┐  ┌───────────── Slot B ─────────┐ │
│  │  + Choose Fund A                  │  │  + Choose Fund B  ▾[vs passed]│ │
│  │  (empty drop target)              │  │  (empty drop target)          │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────┘  └───────────────────────────────┘│
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ FILTERS:  Strategy [All ▾]   Sort [ Most attractive fees ▾ ]       │  │
│  │           [search funds…]                  Open only [✓]            │  │
│  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤  │
│  │ ▢ Fund name        Strategy   Vintage   Net MOIC   Fee%  Fee-rank  │  │  ← picker list
│  │ ▢ EQT X            Buyout     2023       —          1.8%   ●●●○○ 40%│  │
│  │ ▢ CVC IX           Buyout     2021       1.4×       2.0%   ●●○○○ 62%│  │
│  │ …                                                                   │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- **Two slots, A (left, fixed-meaning) and B (right, swappable).** Empty slots are dashed drop-targets with "+ Choose Fund A/B." Choosing from the list fills the slot with a compact fund chip (name + strategy badge + vintage). Slot B's header carries the **"vs a fund I passed"** menu (§6).
- **Filter bar.** A **Strategy** dropdown (the 9-bucket taxonomy + All). A **Sort** dropdown whose default-notable option is **"Most attractive fees"** (sorts ascending by `fee_percentile` — lowest percentile = most attractive — and reveals the Fee-rank column prominently). Other sort options: Vintage (newest), Net MOIC (where mature), Name. A free-text **search**. An **Open only** toggle (binds `open_only`).
- **Picker list.** One row per fund from `/api/funds`. Columns: select checkbox/click-to-slot, name, strategy (the foundation strategy→colour chip), vintage, Net MOIC (or "—" / "too early" when immature), Fee %, and **Fee-rank** rendered as a 5-dot meter + percentile label (see §9 badge rendering). Clicking a row, when a slot is the active target, fills that slot; otherwise a row click opens a slot-picker ("Set as A / Set as B"). The list is the only scrollable element on the surface (the picker is a list, not an analytical view — Part 0.2's "no scroll on analytical views" applies to the comparison, not to the chooser list).
- **"Most attractive fees" is answerable here alone.** A user can pick Strategy = Buyout, Sort = Most attractive fees, and read the answer to question 3 off the top of the list without ever building a comparison. The top row carries a subtle "Lowest fee in Buyout" pill.

#### 3.2 Comparison mode (the head-to-head)

Once both slots resolve, `GET /api/fund_compare?a=&b=&investor_id=` fires and the stage re-lays into the comparison. No scroll: everything fits one full screen at ≥1280px; the regions are sized so the table and gauges never clip.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Compare two funds          [swap A↔B]  [change A] [change B]  [PDF ▾]  │
│  ┌── FIT-TO-YOUR-GAP BANNER ─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Fund A closes a 4.2pp underweight in Buyout. Fund B doesn't move  │ │  ← from fit.better_fit
│  │  a significant gap.                          [show how ⓘ]          │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│  ┌───────── FundCard A ──────────┐   VS   ┌───────── FundCard B ───────┐ │
│  │ name · access-feeder line      │        │ name · access-feeder line  │ │
│  │ [region badge][strategy badge] │        │ [region][strategy]         │ │
│  │ Target size · Net MOIC · Close │        │ …stats row…                │ │
│  │ Min. investment                │        │                            │ │
│  │ ── Fees/carry/hurdle ──        │        │ ── Fees/carry/hurdle ──    │ │
│  │ ── Consistency  ●●●●○  0.72 ──  │        │ ── Consistency ●●●○○ 0.61 ─│ │  ← T2-7
│  │ ── Lifecycle gauge ──          │        │ ── Lifecycle gauge ──      │ │  ← T2-8
│  │   buying ▭▭▭▭▭░░░░ harvesting   │        │   buying ▭▭░░░░░░ harvesting│ │
│  │ [Contact your sales associate] │        │ [Contact your sales assoc.]│ │
│  └────────────────────────────────┘        └────────────────────────────┘│
│  ┌── COMPARABLE-VINTAGE PERFORMANCE TABLE ───────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Comparable within vintages 2019, 2020.  [caveat line if differ]   │ │
│  │  Vintage  Age   A:DPI RVPI TVPI IRR    B:DPI RVPI TVPI IRR          │ │
│  │  2019     7y    0.9  0.7  1.6  14%     0.6  1.0  1.6  11%           │ │
│  │  2020     6y    0.4  0.9  1.3  too early… 0.3 0.8 1.1 too early…    │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│  ┌── STRATEGY THESIS (doc-derived) ──────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  A: "…differentiated sourcing in DACH mid-market…" [PPM p.12 ⌖]    │ │
│  │  B: corpus not indexed for this fund                              │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Region order top→bottom (priority for the non-expert eye):** (1) **Fit banner** — the personal "so what," first; (2) **Twin FundCards** — the identity + headline structured metrics + consistency + lifecycle, side-by-side; (3) **Comparable-vintage performance table** — the rigorous, vintage-honest numbers; (4) **Strategy thesis** — the qualitative, doc-grounded read. The order descends from "what it means for me" → "what it is" → "how it performed, honestly" → "why the manager says it works." A non-expert can stop after the banner and the cards; an analyst reads on.

**Side-by-side, never toggled.** Per Part 0.2, the A/B comparison is always two columns, never a flip-toggle. The FundCards are twins (identical layout, mirrored), so the eye compares row-aligned: fees against fees, consistency against consistency, lifecycle against lifecycle.

**Responsive.**
- **≥1280px:** the full two-column layout above; fit banner full-width; cards 50/50; table full-width with both funds' columns; thesis two-column.
- **768–1279px:** cohort builder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher (per Part 3). The two FundCards stay side-by-side (they are the comparison — do not stack them here; shrink the stats grid instead). The performance table keeps both funds' column groups but the RVPI/IRR sub-columns may collapse behind a "more" expander per row to avoid horizontal scroll. The thesis stacks A over B.
- **<768px (advisor on the go):** single column. The fit banner stays at top (it is the headline). FundCards stack A then B, each full-width, with a sticky "A / B" jump toggle so the user can flip attention without losing the side-by-side intent — and a "compare row" mode where tapping a metric label shows A and B values together in a small inline diff. The performance table becomes a per-vintage card list (one card per comparable vintage, A and B values stacked). No analytical element requires horizontal scroll. The caveat line and disclaimer are never hidden behind an expander on mobile.

---

### §4 — Components & charts

Every component below either *is* a foundation component or composes them. Numeric leaves embed `ConfidenceBadge` + `ProvenancePopover` (and `CoverageBadge` where coverage applies); imputed/synthetic leaves carry `SyntheticTag`. None of those are re-specified here — only their placement.

#### 4.1 FundPicker

- **Slots A/B.** Reuse the FundCard *compact chip* form for a filled slot (name + strategy badge + vintage; an "×" to clear). Slot A is labelled "A" with a fixed-meaning hint; B is labelled "B" and carries the "vs a fund I passed" menu.
- **FilterBar.** Strategy dropdown (foundation strategy→colour chips inline), Sort dropdown (default options listed in §3.1), search, Open-only toggle. The Sort value "Most attractive fees" promotes the Fee-rank column and applies the "Lowest fee in {strategy}" pill to row 1.
- **PickerList / PickerRow.** Tabular, mono figures for numbers (Part 2.3). The Net MOIC cell shows the value with its `ConfidenceBadge`, or "too early to rank" for immature vintages (never zero-filled). The Fee-rank cell is the 5-dot meter (§9). A row in a "named-blocked" k-anon state (a fund with <10 feeder investors) renders a **bucket label** ("a large-cap buyout fund") instead of the name and cannot be the *named* subject of a comparison — see §9.

#### 4.2 Twin FundCards (the head-to-head identity + headline)

Reuse the foundation `FundCard` exactly (image right, name + access-feeder line, two badges = region + strategy, stats row = Target Fund Size / Target Net MOIC / Expected Close / Min. investment, CTA "Contact your sales associate"). This surface **extends** each FundCard with three appended blocks below the stats row, identical on both twins:

- **Fees / carry / hurdle block.** Three labelled figures: Mgmt fee %, Carry %, Hurdle %, plus the Fee percentile rendered as the same 5-dot meter used in the picker, with the **apples-to-apples note** when a structure difference exists (`fees.structure_note`, e.g. fund-of-fund all-in fee vs single-fund mgmt fee). Each figure carries its provenance; fees show `SyntheticTag` when `provenance.synthetic:true` (e.g. carry not yet in the real export, per §8 of the backend brief).
- **Consistency block (T2-7).** The consistency score as a labelled 5-dot meter + the numeric score (e.g. "●●●●○ 0.72"), with a one-line method tooltip on the `ProvenancePopover` ("steadiness of net TVPI across this fund's vintages; higher = steadier"). When `consistency:null` (single-vintage fund), the block renders **"Consistency not computable — single-vintage fund"** in muted text with no dots, never a zero score (§7 state 3).
