- **Lifecycle gauge (T2-8).** A horizontal **buying↔harvesting** gauge (see 4.4).

The two cards are visually mirrored and row-aligned so fees, consistency, and lifecycle sit at the same vertical position on both — the comparison is read across, not within.

#### 4.3 ComparableVintagePerformanceTable

The rigorous core. **Not** `PerfBars` here (PerfBars is the cohort-relative performance surface S7); this is a fund-A-vs-fund-B per-vintage table because the comparison is two funds, not you-vs-peers, and the vintage rows must be explicit to honour G3.

- **Header line.** "Comparable within vintages {list}." When `performance.comparable:false`, the header instead leads with the **caveat** (the exact `performance.caveat` string) styled as a `RelaxBanner`-weight notice (amber-tinted, not error-red) — see §7 state 2 and §8 copy.
- **Rows.** One row per comparable vintage. Columns: Vintage year · Age (years) · then a **Fund A group** {DPI, RVPI, TVPI, IRR} and a **Fund B group** {DPI, RVPI, TVPI, IRR}. DPI and RVPI are **visually distinct** (DPI = realized, solid weight; RVPI = unrealized marks, lighter weight with a small "marks" tag) so they are never read as one blended return. Each cell carries provenance.
- **IRR suppression.** Any vintage with `age_years < 4` renders **"too early to rank"** in the IRR cell (from the row's null IRR + the note), never a number, never zero. The cell shows the note inline, muted.
- **No cross-vintage ranking.** The table never sums or averages across vintages into a single headline "winner." There is no "A wins" verdict on returns; the table presents within-vintage pairs and lets the reader compare row by row. (The only synthesized verdict on the page is the *fit* banner, which is about portfolio gap, not return ranking.)
- **A subtle row highlight** can mark, *within a single vintage row*, which fund has the higher realized DPI (a small ▲ glyph on the higher value, paired with the over/under token) — this is within-vintage, allowed, and always glyph+colour, never colour-only.

#### 4.4 Consistency meter + Lifecycle gauge (T2-7 + T2-8)

- **Consistency meter.** A 5-dot meter mapping the 0–1 score to filled dots (0.0–0.2 → 1 dot … 0.8–1.0 → 5 dots) + the exact numeric score in mono. Filled dots use `--accent-mint`; empty use `--line`. Always paired with the number (accessibility: never the dots alone). Hover/focus → `ProvenancePopover` with method + n (number of vintages used).
- **Lifecycle gauge.** A single horizontal track from "Buying" (left) to "Harvesting" (right). A filled segment from the left represents `lifecycle.harvest` (= latest net DPI, the realized/distributed share); the unfilled left portion represents `buying_mode` (= 1 − DPI, capital still being put to work). A marker labels the position. Below the track, two muted labels: "Buying {buying_mode×100}%" and "Harvesting {harvest×100}%," and, when present, a `called_pct` line ("~{called_pct}% of committed capital called — {ahead of / behind / on} the typical pace") with provenance. When `lifecycle:null` (insufficient data), the gauge is replaced by "Lifecycle not yet computable" in muted text. The gauge is a bar (approved chart language), not a radial dial (radar/gauge dials are in the rejected list — implement as a horizontal bar with a position marker, which reads as a gauge without being a radial).

#### 4.5 StrategyThesisBlock (doc-derived, with citations)

Two columns (A | B), each rendering `doc_thesis.{a,b}`:

- **When grounded:** the thesis as 1–3 short grounded quotes/summaries, each followed by a **citation chip** (⌖ doc-id · page) that, on click, opens a `ProvenancePopover`-style **DocCitationPopover** showing the doc title, page, and the quoted chunk (§6). Team / key-man continuity, when present, is a sub-line with its own citation. **No uncited claim renders** — if a sentence has no citation, it is not shown (mirrors gate G7).
- **When `doc_thesis.status:"corpus_not_indexed"`** (corpus absent for that fund or globally): the column shows **"Strategy thesis not indexed for this fund yet"** in a muted, dashed `NotConnectedState`-style card (not an error), and the rest of the comparison renders fully. The two columns can be in different states (A grounded, B not indexed) and that is shown plainly.

#### 4.6 FitBanner

A full-width banner above the cards, driven by `fit`:

- **Copy** assembled from `fit.a.closes_ppt`, `fit.b.closes_ppt`, `fit.*.significant`, and `fit.better_fit` (exact templates in §8). It names which fund closes the larger *significant* underweight and by how many percentage points, and says when neither does.
- **"Show how" affordance** opens a `ProvenancePopover` showing the gap math (model target − your allocation for each fund's strategy, with the significance note from cohort dispersion), scoped to `investor_id`.
- **Significance honesty.** When a fund's gap-closing is sub-CI, the banner says "doesn't move a *significant* gap" (the word "significant" carries the meaning) and the "show how" popover shows the not-significant flag greyed. `better_fit:"neither"` renders the neutral copy.
- **Advisor mode** banner is phrased about the client ("For {client}, Fund A closes…"), never about the advisor; `investor_id` is the client's.

#### 4.7 Ask-Alpha integration

Ask Alpha (Part 5) floats over this surface. Context-aware suggested prompts on S13: "Which of these two fits my portfolio better?", "Which has more consistent returns?", "Which buyout fund has the lowest fees?", "Why is Fund A a better fit?". Answers render with provenance chips and may include a "Open full comparison" action that drives slots A/B (§2). The chat never invents a return ranking the table doesn't support.

---

### §5 — Data bindings

All numbers come from the engine. **Bind `value` to the visual, `provenance` to the badge/popover** (foundation Part 0.3). `investor_id` is carried on every call so the fit overlay is the bound investor's. Placeholder data must match these shapes exactly so the live swap is a base-URL change.

#### 5.1 Picker

`GET /api/funds?strategy={opt}&open_only={bool}` → array of fund rows. Per row bind:

| UI element | ← field |
|---|---|
| Row name (or bucket label) | `fund_name` (render bucket label if `k_anon_ok:false` / `feeder_n_investors < 10`) |
| Strategy chip | `strategy` (drives foundation strategy→colour) |
| Vintage | `vintage_year` |
| Net MOIC cell | `net_tvpi` / track-record headline (or "too early to rank" when immature) |
| Fee % | `terms.mgmt_fee` |
| Fee-rank meter + % | `fee_percentile` |
| "Lowest fee in {strategy}" pill | computed FE-side as min `fee_percentile` within the active strategy filter |
| Open-only toggle | `open_only` query param |

The "Most attractive fees" sort orders ascending by `fee_percentile`.

#### 5.2 Single-fund detail (slot fill, hover detail)

`GET /api/fund/{fund_id}` → `{fund_id, name, manager, strategy, vintage, geography, format, min_eur, terms{mgmt_fee,carry,hurdle}, track_record[], consistency, lifecycle, sector_vector, fee_percentile}`. Used to render a slot chip's tooltip and to pre-paint a FundCard's identity while the compare call resolves.

#### 5.3 The comparison (the core call)

`GET /api/fund_compare?a={idOrName}&b={idOrName}&investor_id={opt}` → the compare object. Bindings:

| UI element | ← field |
|---|---|
| FundCard A identity / stats / region+strategy badges | `a.{name, manager, strategy, geography, format, min_eur, ...}` + FundCard fields |
| FundCard B identity / stats | `b.{...}` |
| Fees block A: mgmt/carry/hurdle + meter | `fees.a.{mgmt_fee, carry, percentile}` (+ hurdle from `a.terms.hurdle`) |
| Fees block B | `fees.b.{...}` |
| Apples-to-apples note | `fees.structure_note` (render the note only when non-null) |
| Consistency meter A / B (T2-7) | `consistency.a` / `consistency.b` (null → "not computable") |
| Lifecycle gauge A / B (T2-8) | `lifecycle.a.{buying_mode, harvest, called_pct?}` / `lifecycle.b.{...}` (null → "not yet computable") |
| Performance table header | `performance.comparable`, `performance.comparable_vintages` |
| Performance table caveat | `performance.caveat` (render as the caveat notice when non-null) |
| Performance rows | `performance.rows[]` → `{vintage, age_years, a:{dpi,rvpi,tvpi,irr|null}, b:{...}}` |
| IRR "too early" cell | a row where `a.irr===null` / `b.irr===null` (with `age_years<4`) → render the note |
| Sector/geography (in FundCard detail / hover) | `sector.a.{value, coverage_pct}` (+ `CoverageBadge`), geography from `a.geography` |
| Thesis A / B | `doc_thesis.a` / `doc_thesis.b` or `doc_thesis.status:"corpus_not_indexed"` |
| Citation chips | `doc_thesis.*.citations[]` → `{doc_id, page, quote}` |
| Fit banner | `fit.{a.closes_ppt, a.significant, b.closes_ppt, b.significant, better_fit}` |
| Disclaimer (footer of stage) | `disclaimer` |
| Every badge/popover | the per-block `provenance{method,n,ci95,coverage_pct,significant,synthetic,vintage_scope,snapshot_id}` |

**Honour `comparable_vintage` + `caveat` (the load-bearing rule).** The FE must never render a cross-vintage headline winner. When `performance.comparable:false`, the table leads with `performance.caveat`, shows per-vintage rows only, and suppresses any aggregate/headline "A vs B return" comparison element. When `true`, the header states the comparable vintages and the within-vintage row comparison is allowed (with the per-row ▲ glyph, §4.3).

#### 5.4 "Vs a fund I passed"

The historical-passed set comes from T1-11's data (the backend reuses the passed set if present, else current-only). The FE requests it as the B-slot menu's source; when no passed set exists, the menu shows "No passed funds on record" and offers the regular picker. The chosen passed fund's id flows into `b=` of `/api/fund_compare`.

#### 5.5 Error / degraded

- `400` (a or b missing) → should not occur from the UI (the compare call only fires when both slots are filled); if it does, fall back to picker mode with a quiet notice.
- `404 {"error":"unknown fund","candidates":[...]}` → §7 state 7 (suggest candidates).
- Engine/model down → foundation state 7: cached last-good comparison if available + a quiet "showing last computed comparison" banner; never a blank, never a fabricated number.

---

### §6 — Interactions

- **Pick / fill a slot.** Click a picker row (with a slot active) → fills that slot; or row → "Set as A / Set as B." Filling the *second* slot fires `/api/fund_compare` and transitions to comparison mode (calm value-in animation once, ≤150ms, per Part 2.4 — no flashy transition). A skeleton (Part 9 loading) paints the comparison regions while the call resolves; identity from the slot's cached `/api/fund/{id}` paints first so the cards aren't empty.
- **Swap A ↔ B.** A "swap A↔B" control flips the two columns (and re-issues the compare with a and b transposed so provenance/caveat stay correct). The fit banner re-reads `better_fit` (which is symmetric data, so the banner text updates to lead with whichever is now A only if phrasing is position-based; the *meaning* — which fund closes more — does not change). Swap is instant, no full reload.
- **Change A / Change B.** Re-opens the picker for that slot, keeping the other slot filled; choosing a new fund re-fires the compare.
- **"Vs a fund I passed."** Slot B's menu lists historical passed funds (§5.4); choosing one loads it into B and fires the compare. This directly answers user question 4. A small "passed {year}" tag marks a B that is a historical-passed fund.
- **Click a citation chip.** Opens the **DocCitationPopover**: doc title, page, the quoted chunk, and a "this is a grounded quote" note. No external navigation; the popover is the provenance. (Mirrors the foundation's provenance-popover pattern but for a document chunk.)
- **Provenance everywhere.** Every number's `ProvenancePopover` shows method, n, CI, coverage, vintage_scope, snapshot_id (foundation Part 2.6). The fit banner's "show how" shows the gap math. The consistency meter's popover shows the method + vintage count. The lifecycle gauge's popover shows the latest-DPI source + the pace basis.
- **Filter / sort the picker.** Changing Strategy re-requests `/api/funds?strategy=`; changing Sort re-orders client-side (the data is in hand); "Most attractive fees" re-promotes the Fee-rank column and the "lowest fee" pill. Search filters client-side.
- **PDF export.** `PDFExportButton` exports the current comparison (cards + table + gauges + thesis + fit banner + disclaimer + all visible provenance summarized) as a clean PowerPoint-style PDF; disabled in picker mode until both funds resolve.
- **Cohort builder & ProfileBar.** Changing the bound investor's risk class (ProfileBar toggle, Part 2.6) re-fires the compare so the **fit overlay** re-computes against the new model target — only the fit banner and any fit-dependent provenance re-render, not the whole page. The cohort builder does not slice this surface's fund metrics (fund track records are fund-intrinsic), but it does scope the investor for fit; the helper text makes that clear if a user expects cohort to change the funds.
- **Keyboard.** Full keyboard nav (Part 10): picker rows are a listbox (arrow keys, Enter to set slot, with a roving "set as A/B" choice); the swap, change, and PDF controls are buttons in tab order; citation chips and provenance triggers are focusable and open on Enter/Space; the performance table is a proper table with header associations for screen readers and a "view as data" affordance is redundant here since it already *is* a table.

---

### §7 — The seven states

Every state renders cleanly — never a broken analytics screen, never a fabricated number, never zero-fill. The seven map to the foundation Part 9 catalog and to the backend §7.

**State 0 — Initial / unresolved (picker only).** No A or B chosen. The picker is the hero with two empty slots and "Pick two funds to compare." Not counted as an error. (This is the entry state of §2.)

**State 1 — Happy.** Both funds resolve, shared comparable vintages, full track records, fit is significant, thesis grounded. The full comparison renders: header "Comparable within vintages {list}," per-vintage rows with within-vintage ▲ glyphs, consistency meters, lifecycle gauges, grounded thesis with citations, a decisive fit banner ("Fund A closes a 4.2pp underweight; Fund B doesn't move a significant gap").

**State 2 — Different vintages → caveat, no cross-vintage ranking.** `performance.comparable:false`. The performance table **leads with the exact `performance.caveat` string** (amber notice, not error). It shows per-vintage rows for each fund *separately* where they don't overlap, and clearly does **not** present any cross-vintage headline winner — no aggregate "A returned more than B." The rest of the page (cards, fees, consistency, lifecycle, fit, thesis) renders normally. Copy: §8.

**State 3 — Single-vintage fund → "consistency not computable."** A fund with <2 track-record vintages returns `consistency:null`. Its consistency block reads **"Consistency not computable — single-vintage fund"** (muted, no dots). The other fund's consistency renders normally; the cards remain twins (the null side shows the explanatory line in the same slot). Everything else renders.

**State 4 — Low sector coverage.** `sector.*.coverage_pct < 100`. The sector figures (in the FundCard detail / hover) carry the foundation `CoverageBadge` ("based on {x}% of NAV / commitments"); the page never implies 100% look-through. This is a badge state, not a blocking state — the rest renders.

**State 5 — Young vintage → "too early to rank."** Any row with `age_years < 4` shows **"too early to rank"** in the IRR cell (and Net MOIC in the picker shows the same where the headline is immature). Never a zero, never a fabricated IRR. The note explains why.

**State 6 — Doc corpus absent → thesis not indexed.** `doc_thesis.status:"corpus_not_indexed"` (or a per-fund not-indexed). The thesis block(s) show **"Strategy thesis not indexed for this fund yet"** in a dashed muted card; **the entire rest of the comparison renders fully** — this never blocks the surface. A and B can differ (one grounded, one not).

**State 7 — Unresolved fund → candidates suggestion.** A `404 {"error":"unknown fund","candidates":[...]}` (e.g. from a deep-link or Ask Alpha with a misspelled name). The relevant slot shows **"We couldn't find that fund. Did you mean:"** followed by the `candidates` as clickable chips that fill the slot and re-fire the compare. Not a dead error — a recovery affordance.

**Plus the cross-cutting overlays** (foundation): `SyntheticTag` on any `provenance.synthetic:true` field (e.g. carry until the real field lands, §8 of backend); not-significant deltas render greyed (the fit "doesn't move a significant gap" copy is the user-facing form); gated — if the deeper fund detail is paid-only for this viewer, the affected block shows the foundation `GatedState` ("Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate") while the rest renders; loading = calm skeletons over each region, never spinners; error/degraded = cached last-good + quiet banner.

---

### §8 — Copy deck

All copy is structured for English + German (Part 10); below is the English master. Numbers interpolate from the bound fields. Never use imperative/advice language.

**Surface + picker**
- Title: "Compare two funds."
- Picker empty: "Pick two funds to compare." / Slot A: "Choose Fund A." / Slot B: "Choose Fund B."
- Sort option: "Most attractive fees." / Top-row pill: "Lowest fee in {strategy}."
- Open-only toggle: "Open funds only."
- "Vs a fund I passed" menu: "Compare against a fund you passed." / empty: "No passed funds on record."

**Fit banner** (from `fit.better_fit`)
- `better_fit:"a"`: "Fund {A.name} closes a {a.closes_ppt}pp underweight in {A.strategy}. Fund {B.name} doesn't move a significant gap."
- `better_fit:"b"`: "Fund {B.name} closes a {b.closes_ppt}pp underweight in {B.strategy}. Fund {A.name} doesn't move a significant gap."
- both significant: "Fund {A.name} closes a {a.closes_ppt}pp gap in {A.strategy}; Fund {B.name} closes a {b.closes_ppt}pp gap in {B.strategy}. {larger}.name closes the larger gap."
- `better_fit:"neither"`: "Neither fund moves a significant gap in your current allocation."
- "Show how" link: "Show how this fits your portfolio."
- Advisor variant: prefix "For {client}, …".

**Comparable-vintage caveat** (the load-bearing copy)
- Header when comparable: "Comparable within vintages {comparable_vintages}. Returns are compared only within the same vintage — never across vintages of different ages."
- Caveat when `comparable:false`: render the exact `performance.caveat` string from the backend; default fallback if the backend sends a generic flag: "These funds are different vintages ({A.vintage} vs {B.vintage}), so their headline returns aren't comparable. We show each fund's numbers by vintage below and do not rank one above the other on return."

**Fees apples-to-apples note**
- When `fees.structure_note` is non-null, render it; default fallback: "Fees aren't directly comparable: {A.name} is a {A.format} and {B.name} is a {B.format}, so their fee structures differ. We compare each fund's fee to others in its own strategy (the ranking below)."
- Fee-rank label: "{percentile}th percentile on fees within {strategy} — lower is cheaper."

**Performance table**
- DPI label: "DPI (realized)." / RVPI label: "RVPI (marks)." / TVPI: "TVPI (total)." / IRR: "IRR."
- IRR too-early cell: "too early to rank (<4y)."
- Within-vintage glyph tooltip: "Higher realized DPI in this vintage."

**Consistency (T2-7)**
- Label: "Consistency." / popover method: "Steadiness of net TVPI across this fund's {n} vintages. Higher = steadier delivery. This is a derived measure, not a rating."
- Null: "Consistency not computable — single-vintage fund."

**Lifecycle (T2-8)**
- Track ends: "Buying" (left) / "Harvesting" (right).
- Labels: "Buying {pct}% · Harvesting {pct}%." / pace line: "~{called_pct}% of committed capital called — {ahead of / behind / on} the typical pace for {strategy}."
- Null: "Lifecycle not yet computable."

**Strategy thesis**
- Heading: "Strategy thesis (from fund documents)."
- Not indexed: "Strategy thesis not indexed for this fund yet — structured comparison above is unaffected."
- Citation chip: "{doc_id} · p.{page}". / popover: "Grounded quote from {doc_title}, page {page}."

**Disclaimer (always present)**
- Footer: render `disclaimer` verbatim — "Information, not advice. Contact your sales associate."

**Errors / states**
- Unknown fund: "We couldn't find that fund. Did you mean:" + candidate chips.
- Degraded: "Showing the last computed comparison. Live figures will return when the engine is back."
- Coverage: foundation `CoverageBadge` copy — "based on {coverage_pct}% of commitments."

---

### §9 — Badge rendering

How the surface-specific meters and the inherited badges render. (Inherited badges — `ConfidenceBadge`, `CoverageBadge`, `SyntheticTag`, `GatedState`, `RelaxBanner` — follow the foundation; below is their placement plus the two new meters.)

- **Fee-rank meter** (picker + FundCard fees block). A 5-dot horizontal meter mapping `fee_percentile` to dots **inversely** (lower percentile = cheaper = *more* filled dots, since "most attractive fees" should look "fuller/better"), with the exact percentile in mono beside it and the label "lower is cheaper." Dots use `--accent-mint` filled, `--line` empty. The top picker row under "Most attractive fees" sort additionally carries the "Lowest fee in {strategy}" pill (`--brand-tint` background). Hover → `ProvenancePopover` (method: percentile within the strategy's open-fund fee distribution; n = funds in that distribution).
- **Consistency meter** (T2-7, FundCard). 5-dot meter mapping the 0–1 score to dots (0.2 bands) + the numeric score in mono. `--accent-mint` filled. Null → no dots, the "not computable" line instead. Always number + dots (never colour/dots alone — A11y).
- **Lifecycle gauge** (T2-8, FundCard). Horizontal bar with a position marker (not a radial dial). Filled-from-left = harvest share; the marker + the two % labels carry the meaning textually so it's not colour-only.
- **`ConfidenceBadge`** (foundation). On every numeric leaf: Net MOIC, DPI/RVPI/TVPI/IRR cells, fee figures, consistency, lifecycle, fit ppt. Greys the value when `provenance.significant:false` (the fit banner expresses this as "not a significant gap").
- **`CoverageBadge`** (foundation). On the sector vector figures whenever `coverage_pct < 100` — unmissable, never implies 100%.
- **`SyntheticTag`** (foundation, amber "illustrative" pill). On any field with `provenance.synthetic:true` — notably `carry` until the real export lands (backend §8). Never styled as verified fact.
- **k-anon bucket label.** A fund with `feeder_n_investors < 10` (`k_anon_ok:false`) renders a **bucket label** ("a large-cap buyout fund") instead of its name in the picker, and cannot be the named subject of a comparison; if both A and B would be sub-k-anon the surface explains "this fund can't be named individually yet." (Mirrors backend k-anon gate.)
- **Within-vintage ▲ glyph.** On the higher realized-DPI value within a single performance row — `--over` token + ▲ (colour + glyph). Only ever within one vintage row, never across rows.
- **Caveat notice.** The comparable-vintage caveat renders at `RelaxBanner` visual weight (amber-tinted, calm), distinct from error-red — it's a methodological honesty note, not a failure.

---

### §10 — Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive** (breakpoints per Part 3; detailed in §3.3):
- ≥1280: full two-column comparison, no scroll on the analytical regions; the picker list is the only scroll area.
- 768–1279: cohort builder → drawer, Ask Alpha → launcher; FundCards stay side-by-side (shrink the stats grid, never stack the twins — they *are* the comparison); the performance table may collapse RVPI/IRR behind a per-row "more" rather than scroll horizontally; thesis stacks.
- <768: single column; fit banner first; FundCards stack A→B with a sticky A/B jump toggle and a tap-a-metric inline-diff mode; performance table → per-vintage card list (A and B stacked per card); caveat + disclaimer never hidden behind an expander. No analytical element ever requires horizontal scroll.

**Accessibility** (WCAG-AA, Part 10):
- **Never colour-only.** The within-vintage ▲, the over/under tokens, the fee-rank and consistency meters, the lifecycle fill — all pair colour with a glyph, a numeral, or a text label. The fit banner is text-led.
- **Contrast** AA on all text and on amber caveat/synthetic tints.
- **Keyboard** full nav (§6): picker as listbox; swap/change/PDF as buttons; citation + provenance triggers focusable, open on Enter/Space; logical tab order A-card → B-card → table → thesis.
- **Screen readers.** The performance table is a real `<table>` with `<th scope>` associations so a vintage row reads "Vintage 2019, age 7, Fund A DPI 0.9 realized, RVPI 0.7 marks, …". Meters expose their numeric value via `aria-valuenow`/text, not just dots. The fit banner is an `aria-live` polite region so it announces when funds swap. The caveat notice is announced (it's load-bearing methodology). Citation chips have accessible names ("citation, {doc_id} page {page}").
- **Charts as data.** The lifecycle gauge and meters expose their underlying numbers as text adjacent to the visual (already required by §9), satisfying the "chart data available as a table" rule. The performance table already is data.
- **Focus visible** on every interactive element; motion ≤150ms, no parallax/bounce (Part 2.4) — important for vestibular safety.
- **i18n.** English + German; €, EU number formatting; no hard-coded strings; the caveat, fit, fees-note, and disclaimer are all in the string table.

---

### §11 — Acceptance checklist

The surface is accepted when **all** hold (visual + interaction + the three load-bearing rules called out in the brief brief):

**Load-bearing (the three the task names explicitly):**
- [ ] **Comparable-vintage caveat honoured.** When `performance.comparable:false`, the table leads with the exact `performance.caveat` string, shows per-vintage rows only, and renders **no cross-vintage headline winner / aggregate return comparison**. When `true`, the header names the comparable vintages and only within-vintage row comparison (the ▲ glyph) is allowed. IRR is suppressed ("too early to rank") for any row with `age_years < 4`. DPI (realized) and RVPI (marks) are visually distinct and never blended.
- [ ] **Fees apples-to-apples.** Fees compare each fund to its own strategy's distribution (the fee-rank meter), never a fund-of-fund all-in fee against a single-fund mgmt fee without the `fees.structure_note` (or its fallback) shown. The "Most attractive fees" sort + "Lowest fee in {strategy}" pill answer question 3 from the picker alone.
- [ ] **Fit banner from `fit.better_fit`.** The banner text is driven by `fit.better_fit` + `closes_ppt` + `significant`; it names the fund and the pp gap, says "doesn't move a significant gap" when sub-CI, renders the neutral copy for `"neither"`, and the "show how" popover shows the gap math scoped to `investor_id`.

**Structure & components:**
- [ ] Picker filters by strategy and sorts by "most attractive fees"; selecting A and B fires `/api/fund_compare?a=&b=&investor_id=`.
- [ ] Twin FundCards reuse the foundation `FundCard` (image/name/access-feeder/region+strategy badges/stats/CTA) and append fees, consistency (T2-7), and lifecycle (T2-8) blocks, row-aligned and side-by-side (never toggled).
- [ ] Consistency renders as meter + number; single-vintage → "not computable" (state 3).
- [ ] Lifecycle renders as a buying↔harvesting **bar** gauge (not a radial), with %s and pace line; null → "not yet computable."
- [ ] Strategy thesis renders grounded quotes with clickable citation chips → DocCitationPopover; `corpus_not_indexed` → "not indexed" while the rest renders (state 6); no uncited claim shows.
- [ ] "Vs a fund I passed" pulls a historical passed fund into slot B; "swap A↔B" and "change A/B" re-fire the compare; deep-links from Completion / Next-Best / Explain / Ask Alpha pre-fill slots.

**Provenance, states, brand:**
- [ ] Every numeric leaf carries `ConfidenceBadge` + `ProvenancePopover`; sector figures carry `CoverageBadge` when `coverage_pct<100`; `provenance.synthetic:true` fields (e.g. carry) carry `SyntheticTag`; sub-k-anon funds render a bucket label, not a name.
- [ ] All seven states render cleanly (0 initial/picker, 1 happy, 2 different-vintage caveat, 3 single-vintage, 4 low coverage, 5 young vintage, 6 corpus absent, 7 unresolved→candidates) plus gated/degraded/loading overlays — never a blank, never a fabricated number, never zero-fill.
- [ ] The `disclaimer` renders verbatim on every resolved state; no string contains advice/imperative language ("you should," "we recommend," "invest").
- [ ] Responsive at all three breakpoints with no horizontal scroll on analytical regions; full keyboard nav; performance table is a real `<table>`; fit banner is `aria-live`; never colour-only.
- [ ] Brand: Moonfare indigo/mint/cream, real logo in frame, light/dark, calm ≤150ms motion, `PDFExportButton` exports the comparison; placeholder data matches the §5 shapes so the live swap is a base-URL change.

---

*Build S13 to this brief, composing the foundation's components and honouring the T1-8 contract. The three rules to never break: comparable-vintage caveat (no cross-vintage ranking), fees apples-to-apples, and the fit banner straight from `fit.better_fit`. The surface should feel like the most trustworthy two minutes in the product — the proprietary comparison advantage, made simple enough for a doctor.*


---

## Surface S14 — What's next / Completion

> *Use case T2-1 — Portfolio Completion Engine. Frontend brief for Claude Design.*
> This document is a **deep frontend spec** for one surface. It composes from the design system in `Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md` (Parts 2, 3, 6, 9) and binds to the engine in `Alpha_UseCase_Briefs/deep/T2-01_Portfolio_Completion.md` (`GET /api/recommendations`). It does **not** redefine the FundCard anatomy, the seven-state model, the cohort builder, Ask Alpha, the ProfileBar, or the token set — those are the foundation. Where the foundation already fixes a behaviour, this brief **references** it and specifies only what S14 adds on top. Every decision below is closed: there are no "TBD"s, no "designer's choice," no open questions.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**The job.** S14 is the *act* surface in the construct→compare→understand→forecast→**act**→evaluate→learn arc. The investor (or their advisor, in impersonate mode) has already seen *where they stand* (S1 Overview, S2 Strategy, S6 Look-through). S14 answers the next, action-shaped question: **"My portfolio has a gap — what specifically do I add to close it, from the funds actually open right now?"** It turns the abstract gap analytics into a short, ranked shelf of **real, currently-open Moonfare funds**, each shown in the exact Moonfare fund-listing card the investor already knows from the live product, each with a single non-prescriptive CTA ("Contact your sales associate") and an unmistakable *information-not-advice* disclaimer.

The completion engine is deterministic and multi-axis: it diffs the current book against a chosen **reference allocation vector** across five gap axes — **defensive ballast, liquidity/income, strategy, vintage, geography** — surfaces only *significant* gaps (G2), picks the single most severe axis as "the missing piece," and ranks the open inventory that closes each axis by `gap_closure × fit × k_anon_able`. S14 is the *presentation layer* over that engine. It never re-computes a gap, never re-ranks a fund, never names a fund the engine declined to name (k-anon). It renders `recommendations[]` and the headline axis, full stop.

**The user questions this surface must answer at a glance** (mapped to the engine's eval ids):

| # | The user asks… | Answered by | Rendered as |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "What is the **missing piece** in my portfolio?" | `headline_axis` + `headline_recommendation` | The hero headline "Your missing piece is {X}" + the #1 FundCard, visually promoted |
| Q2 | "Which **current Moonfare fund** best fills that gap?" | `axes[headline_axis].recommendations[0]` | Rank-1 FundCard (the promoted card) |
| Q3 | "What should I add to improve my **vintage** diversification?" | `axes["vintage"]` | The Vintage axis section (holes chip + its FundCards) |
| Q4 | "Am I missing a **stabilising** strategy like secondaries or credit?" | `axes["defensive_ballast"]` + `axes["liquidity_income"]` | Those two axis sections + their FundCards |
| Q5 | "What's missing **vs the best performers** (or a family office)?" | full payload with `reference=top_decile` / `family_office` | The reference selector re-keys the whole surface |
| Q6 | "What should I add next to **{named held fund}**?" | `anchor_fund` block + anchored axis recs | The anchor banner + re-keyed FundCards |
| Q7 | "Show me the best **{strategy / format}** pieces." | `opportunity_*` prefilter | The opportunity-filter chips narrow the shelf |
| Q8 | "Is there **nothing** to add?" | state-4 do-nothing (`complete:true`) | The "well-aligned, no action needed" state |

**Design intent.** Calm, premium, *low-pressure*. This is the most commercially loaded surface in the product (it points at sellable funds), so the bar for trustworthiness is highest here. The dominant feeling must be "here is what the engine found is missing, and here are the real funds that would fill it — judge for yourself," never "buy this now." The disclaimer is not fine print; it is a first-class layout element. The CTA routes to a *human* (the sales associate), never to a checkout. The primary user is "a doctor, not a pro": one headline sentence they understand instantly, then a small number of familiar fund cards, nothing more.

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

**Rail position.** S14 lives in the left-rail surface list (Part 3) under the label **"What's next"**, in the *act* group, immediately after **S13 · Funds / Fund-vs-Fund** and before **S15 · Explain**. Selecting it swaps the centre stage to the Completion view full-screen; the persistent frame (ProfileBar top, Cohort builder left, Ask Alpha float) stays mounted, exactly as every other Detail surface.

**Entry points into S14 (six, all closed):**
1. **Rail click** — "What's next." Default reference `model`, no anchor, all axes. Calls `GET /api/recommendations?investor_id={id}`.
2. **From S20 Next Best Action** — the integrator's single action card has a "See the funds that close this gap" affordance → deep-links to S14 with the same `axis` pre-focused (`?axis={headline_axis}`).
3. **From S1 Overview Z-chart bar** — clicking a strategy bar on the Over/underweight Z-chart can deep-link to S14 with `?opportunity_strategy={strategy}` (the opportunity-type variant) so the investor lands on "best {strategy} pieces."
4. **From S13 Fund-vs-Fund "fit-to-your-gap" banner** — the "Fund A closes a 4.2pp underweight" banner links to S14 to see the *full* ranked completion shelf for that axis.
5. **From a held-fund context menu (S2)** — "What should I add next to this fund?" → S14 with `?anchor_fund_id={fund_id}` (the named-fund variant).
6. **From Ask Alpha** — answers to "what's the missing piece?" / "what should I do next?" render an inline mini-summary with a "Open What's next" button → S14.

**Deep-link / URL state.** S14 round-trips its full request state in the URL query so any entry is shareable and the back-button works: `reference`, `anchor_fund_id`, `opportunity_strategy`, `opportunity_format`, `axis`. These map 1:1 to the engine's typed request params (T2-1 §5). On mount, the FE reads the query, fires one `GET /api/recommendations`, and renders. No state lives only in component memory.

**Cohort independence.** S14's reference is *not* the cohort builder's cohort. The cohort builder drives peer/platform/model comparisons on the analytical surfaces (S1–S8); S14's "reference" is the **completion reference vector** (model / architect / top_decile / family_office / pe / endowment), selected by S14's own ReferenceSelector (§6). Changing the left-rail cohort does **not** re-fire `/api/recommendations`. This is called out explicitly because it is a known foot-gun: the two selectors look similar but drive different endpoints. S14's ReferenceSelector sits *inside* the centre stage, visually distinct from the cohort builder, to keep them from being confused.

---

### §3 Layout

**The shape.** Top-to-bottom, full-screen, single scroll-free analytical region that *may* scroll only when the recommendation shelf is long (this is the one S14 exception to the global no-scroll rule — a *list* of fund cards is a shopping list, not an analytical view, and is expected to scroll like the live Moonfare fund listing; the headline and controls stay pinned). Three bands:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  HERO BAND (pinned)                                                            │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Your missing piece is  Defensive ballast.                              │  │  ← MissingPieceHeadline (display)
│  │  You hold 0% in stabilising strategies; a balanced book targets ~25%.   │  │  ← sub-line (one sentence, from the axis)
│  │  [ Reference: Model ▾ ]   [ All axes ▾ ]   [ + Filter type ]   [ PDF ▾ ] │  │  ← ReferenceSelector · AxisSelector · OpportunityFilter · PDFExport
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│  ⓘ  These are engine-identified gaps vs your chosen reference and the open     │  ← DisclaimerStrip (always visible, first-class)
│     Moonfare funds that would close them. House-view completion, not advice.   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  PROMOTED CARD  (rank-1, the answer to "which fund?")                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  ★ Best fit for your missing piece                                       │  │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┐    │  │
│  │  │ Continuum Infra I                              │                  │    │  │  ← FundCard (Moonfare listing layout)
│  │  │ Access via the Continuum feeder · n=95         │   [ fund image ] │    │  │     name + access-feeder line
│  │  │ [Europe] [Infrastructure]                      │                  │    │  │     region + strategy badges
│  │  │ Tgt Fund Size · Tgt Net MOIC · Exp. Close · Min│                  │    │  │     stats row (4 cells)
│  │  │ Closes 25pp of your ballast gap.               │                  │    │  │  ← gap-closure line (underweight_ppt)
│  │  │ [ Contact your sales associate ]   [ Explain ▸ ] [ Compare ▸ ]    │    │  │  ← CTA + S15/S13 links
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────┘    │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  RANKED SHELF  (the rest, grouped by axis)                                     │
│  Defensive ballast — you 0% vs 25% floor                              [hide ▾] │  ← AxisSectionHeader
│  ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐                                 │
│  │ FundCard   │ │ FundCard   │ │ FundCard   │   (rank 2…N within axis)         │  ← FundCardGrid
│  └────────────┘ └────────────┘ └────────────┘                                 │
│  Liquidity / income — you 0% vs 12% floor                            [hide ▾] │
│  ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐                                 │
│  └────────────┘ └────────────┘ └────────────┘                                 │
│  …Strategy · Vintage · Geography sections, in AXIS_PRIORITY order…             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Hero band (pinned).** Three rows: (1) the **MissingPieceHeadline** — display type, "Your missing piece is **{X}**." where {X} is the human label of `headline_axis`; (2) a one-sentence **sub-line** derived from the headline axis (the gap quantified); (3) the **control row** — ReferenceSelector, AxisSelector, OpportunityFilter, PDFExport. Below the band, the **DisclaimerStrip** is always visible and never collapses. The whole hero band + disclaimer remains pinned while the shelf scrolls.

**Promoted card.** Directly answers Q2. It is the `headline_recommendation` rendered as a *full-width* FundCard with a "★ Best fit for your missing piece" eyebrow. It is the same FundCard component as the shelf, simply rendered at full bleed with the eyebrow and slightly larger image — a promotion, not a different component. If `headline_recommendation` is null (no significant gap, or the headline axis has no nameable open match), the promoted card is replaced by the relevant state block (§7).

**Ranked shelf.** Below the promoted card, the remaining recommendations are grouped into **AxisSection**s, one per *significant* axis, ordered by `AXIS_PRIORITY` = `[defensive_ballast, liquidity_income, strategy, vintage, geography]`. The headline axis appears first (its rank-1 is already promoted above, so its section starts at rank 2). Each AxisSection has a header (`{Axis label} — you {you_pct}% vs {floor_pct}% floor`, or axis-appropriate phrasing — see §8) and a **FundCardGrid** of up to `MIN_REC_PER_AXIS` (3) cards. Each card carries its own gap-closure line, "Contact your sales associate" CTA, and the info-not-advice disclaimer (inline, per-card — see §4).

**The FundCard — Moonfare fund-listing layout (foundation Part 2.6, reproduced here as the contract for S14).** Every card on S14 is the *same* card as the live Moonfare product listing and S13:
- **Image on the right** — the fund's hero/visual, right-aligned, fixed aspect; rounded to card radius; lazy-loaded with a `--surface` skeleton; falls back to a strategy-tinted placeholder (`--data-*` for the strategy) if no image.
- **Name** — fund_name, h2, left of the image.
- **Access-feeder line** — under the name, caption/muted: "Access via the {manager} feeder" + the feeder size where shown ("· n={feeder_n_investors}"). This line is *only* present because the fund is k-anon-nameable (the engine already filtered sub-k-anon funds out, so every card here is safe to name).
- **Two badges** — **region** (geography) + **strategy**, side by side, under the access line. Rendering rules in §9.
- **Stats row** — four cells, left-to-right, mono/tabular figures: **Target Fund Size · Target Net MOIC · Expected Close · Min. investment**. Each cell is `LABEL` (caption, muted) over `VALUE` (body, mono). Exact bindings in §5.
- **Gap-closure line** — S14-specific addition (see §4): "Closes {underweight_ppt}pp of your {axis} gap" for the axis the card serves.
- **CTA** — primary button "Contact your sales associate."
- **Secondary links** — "Explain ▸" (→ S15) and "Compare ▸" (→ S13), inline, ghost style.
- **Per-card disclaimer** — a single muted line: "Information, not investment advice." (the strip carries the long form; the card carries the short form so a card lifted out of context — e.g. into a PDF — still self-discloses).

**Responsive (foundation Part 3 breakpoints).**
- **≥1280px (desktop):** three-column frame; promoted card full-width within the centre stage; FundCardGrid is **3 cards per row**; the FundCard renders image-right, stats row as four columns.
- **768–1279px (tablet):** cohort builder collapses to a drawer, Ask Alpha to a launcher; FundCardGrid is **2 cards per row**; the FundCard keeps image-right but the stats row may wrap to 2×2; the promoted card stays full-width.
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go):** single column; the FundCard **reflows to image-on-top, then name/access/badges/stats stacked**; stats row becomes a 2×2 grid; the hero headline shrinks to h1; the control row becomes a horizontally scrollable chip strip; ProfileBar becomes a sheet. The CTA is full-width and thumb-reachable. No card content is hidden; nothing requires horizontal scroll within a card.

---

### §4 Components & charts

S14 has **no analytical charts** — it is intentionally chart-free (it is the *act* surface; the analysis lives upstream). Its component inventory:

**4.1 MissingPieceHeadline** (new, S14-only).
- Renders "Your missing piece is **{X}**." where {X} = the human label of `headline_axis` (`§8` copy deck maps the six axis keys → labels).
- The axis word {X} is emphasised (brand colour, semibold) but **not** a link.
- A one-sentence sub-line sits under it, generated from the headline axis object:
  - *defensive_ballast / liquidity_income:* "You hold {you_pct}% in {label}; a {risk_class} book targets ~{floor_pct}%."
  - *strategy:* "Your biggest under-allocation is {headline_gap.strategy}, {headline_gap.delta_ppt}pp below the {reference}."
  - *vintage:* "You have a vintage gap in {holes formatted, e.g. 2024–2026} — recent years your book hasn't touched."
  - *geography:* "{headline_gap} is under-covered in your book (under {GEO_COVER_FLOOR_PCT}%)."
- States: when `complete:true` → "Your portfolio is **well-aligned**." (do-nothing copy, §7.4). When `cold_start:true` → it is *not* shown (the cold-start block replaces the whole surface, §7.2).
- A11y: the headline is the surface's single `<h1>`; the axis word carries no semantic colour-only meaning (it is plain emphasis), so no glyph needed.

**4.2 DisclaimerStrip** (new, S14-only, *first-class*).
- A full-width strip directly under the hero band, `--surface` background, hairline top/bottom, ⓘ glyph, body text. **Never collapsible, never dismissable, always rendered** (including in cold-start and do-nothing states).
- Copy is the engine's `disclaimer` field verbatim when present; otherwise the canonical fallback (§8). Binding it to the engine field guarantees FE/BE stay in lockstep on the legal wording.
- This is the single most important non-negotiable on S14: the acceptance check (§11) fails if it is absent.

**4.3 FundCard** (foundation Part 2.6 — *not redefined*, only the S14 additions specified).
Full anatomy, as the S14 contract:

| Slot | Element | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Right | Fund image | `fund.image_url` (or strategy placeholder) | fixed aspect, rounded, lazy, skeleton |
| Top-left | Fund name | `fund_name` | h2 |
| Under name | Access-feeder line | `"Access via the " + manager + " feeder"` (+ `· n={feeder_n_investors}` if present) | caption, muted; always present (k-anon already passed) |
| Under access | Region badge | `geography` | §9 |
| Beside region | Strategy badge | `strategy` | §9, fixed strategy→colour |
| Mid | Stats row, cell 1 | **Target Fund Size** ← `fund.target_fund_size_eur` | mono; "—" if null |
| | Stats row, cell 2 | **Target Net MOIC** ← `fund.prior_net_tvpi` (target net MOIC proxy) | mono; "—" if null (G3 mature-only) |
| | Stats row, cell 3 | **Expected Close** ← `fund.expected_close` / `pipeline_stage` | text; "Open" / date |
| | Stats row, cell 4 | **Min. investment** ← `min_eur` | mono, € formatted |
| Under stats | **Gap-closure line** (S14) | `underweight_ppt` → "Closes {n}pp of your {axis} gap" | brand-tint pill or inline; §5 |
| Bottom | CTA | static "Contact your sales associate" | primary button |
| Bottom | Explain ▸ | → S15 `?fund_id={fund_id}` | ghost link |
| Bottom | Compare ▸ | → S13 `?a={fund_id}` | ghost link |
| Bottom | Per-card disclaimer | "Information, not investment advice." | muted, single line |

S14-specific FundCard behaviours: (a) the **gap-closure line is mandatory on S14** (it does not appear in the S13 listing) and binds to `underweight_ppt`; (b) the **score / fit / gap_closure numerics are NOT shown** on the card face — they drive ordering only and live behind the ProvenancePopover (§5); (c) opening a card (click anywhere on the card body outside the buttons) opens the **Explain (S15)** detail for that fund, the canonical "tell me more."

**4.4 PromotedFundCard** = FundCard at full-width with the "★ Best fit for your missing piece" eyebrow and a larger image. Same component, `variant="promoted"`.

**4.5 AxisSection** (new) = a header row (`AxisSectionHeader`) + a `FundCardGrid`. The header shows the axis label, the quantified gap, and a `[hide ▾]` collapse toggle (UI-only state; collapsing does not re-fire the endpoint). When an axis is significant but has `no_open_match:true`, the section renders the **thin-candidate state block** (§7.3) instead of a grid.

**4.6 FundCardGrid** = responsive grid of FundCards (3 / 2 / 1 per row by breakpoint).

**4.7 ReferenceSelector** (new) = a segmented control / dropdown: `Model · Architect · Best performers · Family office · PE professionals · Endowment`. Drives `reference`. Visually distinct from the cohort builder (it is in the hero band, labelled "Reference:"). When the chosen reference is degenerate (engine returns `reference_degenerate:true`), the control shows the chosen label with a small "≈ model" note and the **RelaxBanner-style** note "This reference is within 5pp of the model — showing the model." (§7.6).

**4.8 AxisSelector** (new) = dropdown `All axes · Defensive ballast · Liquidity / income · Strategy · Vintage · Geography`. Drives `axis`. "All axes" (default) shows every significant section; a single axis focuses the surface to that one section + re-promotes its rank-1.

**4.9 OpportunityFilter** (new) = a "+ Filter type" affordance opening two chip groups: **Strategy** (the 9 strategies) and **Format** (Primary / Secondary / Direct & Co-Invest / Semi-liquid / Fund-of-Fund). Selections drive `opportunity_strategy` / `opportunity_format`. Active filters render as removable chips in the hero. (Q7.)

**4.10 AnchorBanner** (new, conditional) = when `anchor_fund` is present, a banner above the promoted card: "Completing around **{anchor_fund.fund_name}** ({anchor_fund.strategy}, {anchor_fund.vintage_year})." with an "× Clear" to drop the anchor. (Q6.)

**4.11 Reused from the foundation (composed, not redefined):** ProvenancePopover (on every numeric face element, §5), SyntheticTag (on the reference when the reference provenance is synthetic — model / architect — and on the gap floors which are house-view constants), GatedState (state 6, §7.6), RelaxBanner (degenerate-reference note), PDFExportButton (exports the current shelf as a clean PowerPoint-style PDF of the headline + cards), CaptionBlock (the per-section header sub-line). Ask Alpha and the cohort builder are the frame, untouched.

---

### §5 Data bindings

**The one call.** S14 fires exactly one request on mount / on any control change:

```
GET {{API}}/api/recommendations
    ?investor_id={id}
    &reference={model|architect|top_decile|family_office|pe|endowment}   (default model)
    &anchor_fund_id={fund_id?}
    &opportunity_strategy={strategy?}
    &opportunity_format={format?}
    &axis={defensive_ballast|liquidity_income|strategy|vintage|geography?}
```

Response shape is T2-1 §5. Element-by-element bindings (`element ← field`):

**Hero band:**
- MissingPieceHeadline {X} ← human-label(`headline_axis`).
- Sub-line ← the headline axis object in `axes[headline_axis]` (`you_pct`, `floor_pct`, `delta_ppt`, `headline_gap`, `holes`, `under_covered`) per §4.1.
- ReferenceSelector value ← `reference`; degenerate note ← `reference_provenance.reference_degenerate` + `reference_provenance.l1_vs_model`.
- DisclaimerStrip ← `disclaimer` (verbatim).

**Promoted card** ← `headline_recommendation` (a full FundCard object).

**Ranked shelf:** one AxisSection per key in `axes` where `axes[k].significant == true`, ordered by `AXIS_PRIORITY`. Cards ← `axes[k].recommendations[]` (already ranked, already k-anon-filtered, already capped at 3 by the engine — the FE does **no** client-side sorting or filtering).

**FundCard field map** (each card object inside `recommendations[]` / `headline_recommendation`):
- name ← `fund_name`
- access-feeder line ← `manager` (+ `feeder_n_investors`)
- region badge ← `geography`
- strategy badge ← `strategy`
- Target Fund Size ← `target_fund_size_eur` (→ "€{abbrev}", "—" if null)
- Target Net MOIC ← `prior_net_tvpi` (→ "{n}×", "—" if null per G3)
- Expected Close ← `expected_close` || `pipeline_stage` (→ date or "Open" / "Waitlist")
- Min. investment ← `min_eur` (→ "€{formatted}", "—" if null)
- **Gap-closure line ← `underweight_ppt`** → **"Closes {underweight_ppt}pp of your {axis-label} gap."** This is the single most surface-defining binding on S14: `underweight_ppt` is the *signed gap the engine computed for the axis this card serves*, and it is what turns a generic fund card into a *completion* card. If `underweight_ppt` is absent for an axis where the gap is structural (vintage — there is no ppt), the line instead reads the axis-appropriate form (vintage: "Fills your {year-range} vintage gap."; geography: "Adds {region} exposure your book lacks."). The ppt form is preferred wherever `underweight_ppt` is present (strategy / ballast / liquidity / geography-as-ppt).
- CTA → static (no binding).
- Explain link → S15 `?fund_id={fund_id}`.
- Compare link → S13 `?a={fund_id}` (and, if the engine attached `compare_to_holdings`, prefill the "vs your held {strategy} funds" comparison).
- ProvenancePopover (on the card's "ⓘ how this was scored") ← `score`, `gap_closure`, `fit`, and `axes[k].provenance.method` + `snapshot_id`. **The score/fit/gap_closure never render on the card face** — only inside the popover, framed as "how this fund was ranked" (n, method, snapshot), consistent with the global "no bare number" rule.

**Anchor / variants:**
- AnchorBanner ← `anchor_fund` (a FundCard object) when non-null.
- Active opportunity-filter chips ← the request's `opportunity_strategy` / `opportunity_format` (FE-held, mirrored from URL).

**Provenance & synthetic labelling (foundation Part 2.6 / §0.2):**
- The **reference** carries a SyntheticTag when `reference_provenance.synthetic == true` (i.e. `model` and `architect`). Empirical references (`top_decile`, `family_office`, `pe`) carry **no** synthetic tag — they are real cohort allocations.
- The **gap floors** (the "25% ballast floor", "12% income floor", etc.) are house-view constants; the sub-line that quotes a floor carries a small SyntheticTag-style "house view" pill so the user knows the floor is a Moonfare convention, not a market fact.
- Every numeric card-face value (Min, Target MOIC, etc.) is real fund data and renders a ProvenancePopover ("from the fund's mature track record / fund terms").

**No client-side computation.** The FE must not derive a gap, a rank, a score, or a "closes Xpp" number itself. `underweight_ppt` and the ranking arrive computed. The FE's only transforms are *formatting* (currency, abbreviation, axis-key → label) and *grouping/ordering by the engine-given `significant` + `AXIS_PRIORITY`*.

---

### §6 Interactions

**6.1 Open a FundCard → Explain (S15).** Clicking the card body (anywhere outside the CTA/links/badges) opens **S15 · Explain** for that fund (`POST /api/explain {fund_id}`), the canonical "why this fund, with citations." The "Explain ▸" link does the same explicitly. S15 opens as a full Detail surface (the frame persists); a back affordance returns to S14 with its query state intact.

**6.2 Compare ▸ → Fund-vs-Fund (S13).** The "Compare ▸" link opens **S13** with this fund pre-loaded as A (`?a={fund_id}`). If the engine attached `compare_to_holdings` (the T1-8 decoration — "this leads on downside_resilience, trails on expected_moic"), S13 pre-selects the investor's held same-strategy fund as B so the head-to-head opens already populated. A small inline "leads on … / trails on …" micro-summary may also render on the S14 card under the gap-closure line, sourced from `compare_to_holdings`, as a one-line teaser (no chart).

**6.3 CTA "Contact your sales associate."** The single conversion action. It opens the standard Moonfare contact affordance (the product's existing "Contact your sales associate" flow — a mailto/contact sheet pre-filled with the fund name and the investor id, NOT a transaction). It never executes an order, never commits capital, never moves money. The button is identical across the promoted card, every shelf card, and the do-nothing/thin states' fallback CTAs. On click, fire a lightweight UI analytics event (`s14_contact_associate`, fund_id, axis) for the advisor cockpit's "who's interested" feed — but no engine call.

**6.4 ReferenceSelector change.** Re-fires `/api/recommendations` with the new `reference`; the whole surface re-keys (new headline axis is possible — the missing piece *vs the best performers* may differ from *vs the model*, which is the entire point of Q5). Loading shows calm skeletons over the cards (foundation: skeletons, not spinners). If the new reference comes back degenerate, show the "≈ model" note (§7.6) — but still render (the engine already fell back to model).

**6.5 AxisSelector change.** "All axes" → all significant sections; a single axis → only that section, and the **promoted card re-promotes that axis's rank-1**. UI-only re-grouping if all axis data is already in the payload (the default "all axes" call returns every axis), so single-axis focus is instant with no re-fetch *unless* the user arrived via a single-axis deep link (which fetched only that axis) and then picks "All axes," which re-fetches.

**6.6 OpportunityFilter change.** Adds/removes `opportunity_strategy` / `opportunity_format` chips → re-fires the call (the engine pre-filters the candidate inventory, so this must round-trip). Active filters render as removable chips; clearing all returns to the unfiltered shelf.

**6.7 AnchorBanner "× Clear."** Drops `anchor_fund_id`, re-fires, returns to the un-anchored shelf.

**6.8 Section collapse `[hide ▾]`.** Pure UI toggle; no re-fetch.

**6.9 ProvenancePopover.** Click "ⓘ how this was ranked" on any card → popover with score/fit/gap_closure + method + n + snapshot. Click any card-face numeric (Min, MOIC) → its own provenance ("fund terms" / "mature track record, vintage ≤ 2022").

**6.10 PDF export.** Exports the current view (headline + promoted + shelf, with the disclaimer and every per-card disclaimer) as a clean PDF. The per-card short disclaimer is what makes an exported single card still self-disclose.

**6.11 Ask Alpha cross-talk.** Ask Alpha stays floating over S14. Context-aware suggested prompts on this surface: "Why is {headline_axis} my missing piece?", "Show me the best pieces vs the top performers," "What if I add {promoted fund}?" An Ask Alpha answer that names a fund renders that fund's mini-FundCard inline with a "See it in What's next" button that scrolls/links to the matching S14 card.

**All interactions are non-mutating** except the three that re-parameterise the same read endpoint (reference / axis / opportunity / anchor). Nothing on S14 writes state to the backend.

---

### §7 Seven states (foundation Part 9 — each rendered)

Each of the seven global states maps to an exact engine branch (T2-1 §7) and an exact S14 rendering. **S14 must render all seven; the do-nothing state is mandatory and acceptance-checked.**

**7.1 Happy / established** — `cold_start:false`, ≥1 axis `significant:true`, candidates exist.
Render: full layout (§3). MissingPieceHeadline + sub-line; promoted card = `headline_recommendation`; AxisSections for every significant axis in `AXIS_PRIORITY` order, each with up to 3 cards. This is the default. Acceptance: the promoted card equals `axes[headline_axis].recommendations[0]`; every card is k-anon-nameable; the disclaimer is present.

**7.2 Cold-start** — `cold_start:true` (engine short-circuit: `n_funds==0` / no holdings), `redirect:"architect"`.
Render: the **whole surface is replaced** by a cream-hero ColdStartBlock (foundation Welcome aesthetic): headline "There's nothing to complete yet — let's design your architecture first.", the engine `message`, and a primary CTA **"Open the Allocation Architect"** routing to **S9** (the `redirect`). The MissingPieceHeadline and shelf are *not* shown (there is no portfolio to diff). The DisclaimerStrip *is* still shown. This is the **primary hand-off path** for new investors; it must never look like a broken/empty analytics screen. *Derive from Architect:* S14's cold-start is the inverse of S9's cold-start (S9 *is* the cold-start home). S14 simply detects the condition and routes there.

**7.3 Thin candidate set** — an axis is `significant:true` but `recommendations:[]` and `no_open_match:true` (every closing fund filtered by k-anon / affordability / suitability / none-open).
Render: that AxisSection renders, header intact (the gap is *named* — "Defensive ballast — you 0% vs 25% floor"), but in place of the grid a **NoOpenMatchBlock**: a muted card "No currently-open fund closes this gap that fits your profile right now." + the `no_open_match` reason mapped to copy (§8: k_anon / affordability / suitability / none_open) + a "Contact your sales associate" CTA (a human can surface an off-platform option). The gap is shown; the fix is honestly marked unavailable. The promoted card still shows the headline axis's rank-1 *if that axis has a match*; if the *headline* axis is the thin one, the promoted slot shows the NoOpenMatchBlock and the next-priority axis with matches is visually elevated.

**7.4 No-significant-gap → DO NOTHING** — `complete:true`, `headline_axis:null`, `headline_recommendation:null`, every axis `significant:false`, all `recommendations:[]`.
Render: the **DoNothingBlock** replaces the promoted card and shelf. MissingPieceHeadline becomes "Your portfolio is **well-aligned**." Sub-line: the engine `message` ("No significant gaps vs the {reference} — your portfolio is well-completed on every axis."). A calm, *positive* state — a single reassuring panel (a check glyph, not a warning), "No action needed right now." with a secondary "Compare yourself to peers" link back to S1 and "Ask Alpha why" prompt. **No fund cards, no CTA-to-buy.** The DisclaimerStrip stays. This is the engine's purest no-advice expression — it explicitly recommends adding *nothing* — and S14's acceptance fails if this state cannot render.

**7.5 Wealth-unknown** — `investable_wealth_eur` null; engine sets `affordability_skipped:true` in provenance.
Render: the shelf renders normally (affordability filter skipped, so more candidates may appear), and a single muted note in the hero sub-area: "Showing all open funds — add your investable wealth to filter by affordability." The Min. investment cell still renders per fund; no card is hidden for cost. SyntheticTag/house-view labelling unaffected.

**7.6 Reference-degenerate / gated** — `reference_provenance.reference_degenerate:true` (empirical reference within 5pp of model → engine fell back to model), and/or `gated:true` (the chosen cohort is below the cohort gate).
Render (degenerate): the surface renders against the model (the engine already did the fallback); the ReferenceSelector shows the chosen label with a "≈ model" badge and a RelaxBanner-style line "This reference is within 5pp of the model — showing the model view (l1 = {l1_vs_model}pp)." (Q5 honesty: a relabelled-model swap is exposed, never hidden.)
Render (gated): if `gated:true`, the shelf area shows the **GatedState** (foundation): "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate" + the "5,500 total investors" headline, and the reference falls back to model with a note. Exact gated copy is the foundation's, verbatim.

**7.7 Error / degraded** — `404 unknown investor`, `404 unknown fund` (bad `anchor_fund_id`), `400 bad reference`, or transport failure.
Render: a quiet ErrorBlock (foundation: cached-last-good + a calm banner; never a blank, never a fabricated number). `400 bad reference` → reset the ReferenceSelector to `model` and re-fire (the bad value came from a stale deep link). `404 unknown fund` (anchor) → clear the anchor and re-fire un-anchored, with a note "That fund couldn't be found — showing your full completion view." `404 unknown investor` → the global investor-not-found frame. Transport/cache failure → serve the pinned `snapshot_id` cache + "Showing your last saved view."

**Cross-cutting per Part 9:** not-significant deltas never render as cards (the engine already drops them); SyntheticTag on every imputed value (reference when synthetic, floors as house-view); loading = calm skeletons over the card slots, never spinners.

---

### §8 Copy deck

All strings are i18n keys (English shown; German is a sibling key — the DACH audience is first-class). Numbers/currency localised (€, EU formatting). Nothing hard-coded in components.

**Axis labels (key → English label):**
- `defensive_ballast` → "Defensive ballast"
- `liquidity_income` → "Liquidity & income"
- `strategy` → "{strategy name}" (e.g. "Private Credit") for the headline; "Strategy mix" as the section group label
- `vintage` → "Vintage spread"
- `geography` → "{region}" (e.g. "Asia") for the headline; "Geography" as the section group label

**Hero headline.**
- Happy: **"Your missing piece is {X}."**
- Do-nothing: **"Your portfolio is well-aligned."**

**Hero sub-line (by headline axis):**
- ballast: "You hold {you_pct}% in stabilising strategies; a {risk_class} book targets around {floor_pct}%." + house-view pill on "{floor_pct}%".
- liquidity_income: "You hold {you_pct}% in income-oriented strategies; the income floor for your profile is {floor_pct}%."
- strategy: "Your largest under-allocation is {headline_gap.strategy} — {headline_gap.delta_ppt}pp below the {reference} reference."
- vintage: "You have a vintage gap in {year-range} — recent years your portfolio hasn't committed to."
- geography: "{headline_gap} is under-covered in your portfolio (below {GEO_COVER_FLOOR_PCT}% of your committed book)."

**DisclaimerStrip (verbatim from engine `disclaimer`; fallback if absent):**
> "These are engine-identified gaps in your portfolio versus your chosen reference, and the open Moonfare funds that would close them. This is a house-view completion based on portfolio construction — it is **information, not personalised investment advice**. Speak to your sales associate before acting."

**Per-card short disclaimer:** "Information, not investment advice."

**Gap-closure line (per card):**
- ppt form (ballast / liquidity / strategy / geography-as-ppt): **"Closes {underweight_ppt}pp of your {axis label} gap."**
- vintage: **"Fills your {year-range} vintage gap."**
- geography (coverage form): **"Adds {region} exposure your portfolio lacks."**

**AxisSectionHeader:**
- ballast/liquidity: "{Axis label} — you {you_pct}% vs {floor_pct}% floor"
- strategy: "Strategy mix — you're under in {top gaps, comma-list}"
- vintage: "Vintage spread — gap in {year-range}"
- geography: "Geography — under-covered: {regions}"

**Promoted card eyebrow:** "★ Best fit for your missing piece"

**CTA:** "Contact your sales associate" (verbatim, matches the live product).

**Secondary links:** "Explain ▸", "Compare ▸"

**ProvenancePopover (ranking):** "How this fund was ranked — gap closure {gap_closure}, fit {fit}, score {score}. Method: {method}. Snapshot {snapshot_id}. Ranking is engine-computed, not advice."

**ReferenceSelector labels:** "Model", "Architect (house view for you)", "Best performers", "Family office", "PE professionals", "Endowment".

**Degenerate-reference note:** "This reference is within 5pp of the model — showing the model view (difference {l1_vs_model}pp)."

**Gated copy (foundation, verbatim):** "Only for paid viewers — contact your sales associate." + "5,500 total investors."

**Cold-start block:** "There's nothing to complete yet — let's design your architecture first." / engine `message` / CTA "Open the Allocation Architect".

**Do-nothing block:** headline "Your portfolio is well-aligned." / engine `message` / "No action needed right now." / secondary "Compare yourself to peers" + "Ask Alpha why".

**Thin / no-open-match block:** "No currently-open fund closes this gap that fits your profile right now." + reason:
- `k_anon`: "The funds that would close it are too small to name yet."
- `affordability`: "The funds that would close it are above your affordability filter."
- `suitability`: "The funds that would close it sit outside your risk profile."
- `none_open`: "Nothing that closes it is open for commitment right now."
+ "Contact your sales associate to discuss options."

**Wealth-unknown note:** "Showing all open funds — add your investable wealth to filter by affordability."

**Tone rules (enforced, foundation no-advice):** no string anywhere on S14 may match `\b(you should|we recommend you|buy now|sell now)\b`. Every recommendation phrasing is "would add / closes / fills … your book currently lacks," never imperative. The CTA points to a human. The do-nothing state recommends *nothing*, positively.

---

### §9 Badge rendering

Two badges per FundCard: **region** (geography) then **strategy**, left-to-right, under the access-feeder line.

**Region badge.**
- Source: `geography` ∈ {US, Europe, Asia, RoW}.
- Style: pill, hairline outline (`--line`), neutral `--surface` fill, `--ink` text, a small region glyph (no flag emoji). Region is *not* on the strategy colour ramp (it would collide with strategy colour). Region badges are deliberately monochrome so the *strategy* badge carries the only colour.
- "RoW" renders as "Rest of world".
- Null geography → "Global" badge.

**Strategy badge.**
- Source: `strategy` ∈ the 9 strategies.
- Style: pill, **filled with the fixed strategy→colour from the data ramp** (`--data-1…9`), so a strategy is the same colour here, on the S1 Z-chart, on the S2 donuts — one strategy, one colour, product-wide (foundation 2.2/2.5). Text colour auto-contrasts (`--ink`/white) against the tint for WCAG-AA.
- The strategy badge colour is the *only* chromatic element on an otherwise calm card, which intentionally lets the eye group the shelf by strategy at a glance.

**Badge-to-axis resonance (S14-specific).** When a card serves the **defensive_ballast** or **liquidity_income** axis, its strategy badge (Infrastructure / Private Credit / Secondaries / Semi-liquid) is exactly the strategy that *defines* that axis — so the badge already visually justifies the gap-closure line. No extra "ballast" badge is added (it would be redundant); the strategy badge + the gap-closure line together carry the meaning.

**Accessibility.** Badge colour is never the *only* carrier of meaning — the strategy name is always written in the badge; the region glyph is decorative (`aria-hidden`) with the region name as text. Both badges are static (non-interactive) by default; hovering the strategy badge shows a tooltip with the one-line strategy definition (same defs as the cohort builder's risk hovers).

**Promoted card.** Same two badges, rendered slightly larger to match the larger image; no third badge, no "best fit" *badge* (the eyebrow text carries that, not a badge, to avoid badge clutter).

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive (foundation Part 3, S14 specifics):**
- **≥1280:** 3-up FundCardGrid; promoted card full-width; stats row 4-column; hero controls in one row.
- **768–1279:** 2-up grid; stats row may wrap 2×2; cohort builder → drawer, Ask Alpha → launcher; hero controls wrap.
- **<768:** 1-up; FundCard reflows **image-on-top → name → access → badges → stats (2×2) → gap-line → CTA (full-width) → links → disclaimer**; hero headline → h1; control row → horizontally scrollable chip strip; ProfileBar → sheet; DisclaimerStrip stays pinned under the hero.
- The shelf is the *only* scrollable region; the hero band + DisclaimerStrip stay pinned at every breakpoint. No card content is ever hidden behind horizontal scroll or a "more" truncation — a fund card always shows its full stats row.

**Accessibility (foundation Part 10, WCAG-AA):**
- The MissingPieceHeadline is the surface `<h1>`; each AxisSectionHeader is an `<h2>`; cards are `<article>`s with an accessible name = fund_name.
- Full keyboard nav: Tab order = hero controls → promoted card (then its CTA/links) → each section header (collapse toggle) → each card → its CTA/links. Visible focus rings on every interactive element.
- Colour is never the sole signal: the strategy badge always carries the strategy name as text; the do-nothing state pairs its positive colour with a check glyph; any over/under language pairs sign words ("under", "below") with the number.
- Every card-face number and the ranking are available behind a "how this was ranked / view data" affordance (the ProvenancePopover), satisfying the "chart/number data available as a table" rule.
- The DisclaimerStrip uses `role="note"` and is in the reading order immediately after the `<h1>` so a screen-reader user hears the info-not-advice framing before the funds.
- The fund image carries `alt="{fund_name} — {strategy}"`; placeholder images are `aria-hidden` with the name/strategy already in adjacent text.
- CTA buttons have descriptive accessible names ("Contact your sales associate about {fund_name}") so a screen-reader user knows which fund each CTA refers to.
- Touch targets ≥44px on mobile; the CTA is full-width on <768.
- Motion ≤150ms, no parallax/bounce; cards fade-in once on load, then settle (foundation 2.4).
- i18n: English + German keys; €/EU number formatting; no hard-coded strings; the headline and gap-closure lines are template strings with named slots so German word-order works.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

A build of S14 is **done** when all of the following hold (each maps to a §ref and, where applicable, an engine gate):

**Layout & FundCard fidelity**
- [ ] The FundCard matches the **Moonfare fund-listing layout** exactly: image right (image-top on mobile), name, **access-via-feeder line**, **region + strategy badges**, and the **stats row Target Fund Size / Target Net MOIC / Expected Close / Min. investment** — in that order. (§3, §4.3)
- [ ] The strategy badge uses the fixed strategy→colour ramp (same colour as S1/S2 for that strategy); the region badge is monochrome. (§9)
- [ ] The promoted card is the same component at full width with the "★ Best fit for your missing piece" eyebrow, and equals `axes[headline_axis].recommendations[0]`. (§3, §4.4, §7.1)
- [ ] The MissingPieceHeadline renders "Your missing piece is {X}." with {X} = the human label of `headline_axis`. (§4.1, §8)

**Disclaimer (non-negotiable)**
- [ ] The **DisclaimerStrip is present and visible** in every state (happy, cold-start, do-nothing, thin, gated, error) — never collapsed, never dismissed. It binds to the engine `disclaimer` verbatim. (§4.2, §8)
- [ ] Each FundCard carries the per-card short disclaimer "Information, not investment advice." so an exported single card self-discloses. (§4.3, §6.10)
- [ ] No string on S14 matches `\b(you should|we recommend you|buy now|sell now)\b`; every recommendation is phrased "closes / would add … your book lacks," and the CTA points to a human. (§8, no-advice gate)

**Data binding & no client computation**
- [ ] S14 fires exactly one `GET /api/recommendations` per state change; all grouping/ordering follows the engine's `significant` flags + `AXIS_PRIORITY`; the FE does **no** client-side gap, score, or rank computation. (§5)
- [ ] The gap-closure line binds to `underweight_ppt` → "Closes {n}pp of your {axis} gap" (with the vintage/geography variants where `underweight_ppt` is absent). (§5, §8)
- [ ] `score` / `fit` / `gap_closure` never render on the card face — only in the ProvenancePopover. (§4.3, §5)
- [ ] Every card is k-anon-nameable (the engine filtered sub-k-anon funds); no card shows `k_anon_ok:false`. (§5, k-anon gate)
- [ ] The reference carries a SyntheticTag iff `reference_provenance.synthetic == true` (model / architect); empirical references (top_decile / family_office / pe) carry none; gap floors carry a "house view" pill. (§5)

**The seven states (all render; do-nothing mandatory)**
- [ ] **Happy** renders the full headline + promoted + axis-grouped shelf. (§7.1)
- [ ] **Cold-start** (`cold_start:true`) replaces the surface with the ColdStartBlock routing to **S9 Architect** (`redirect`), not a broken empty screen. (§7.2)
- [ ] **Thin** (`no_open_match:true`) names the gap but shows the NoOpenMatchBlock with the mapped reason — the gap is shown, the fix honestly marked unavailable. (§7.3)
- [ ] **DO-NOTHING** (`complete:true`) renders the positive "Your portfolio is well-aligned — no action needed" state with **no fund cards and no buy-CTA**. This state must exist. (§7.4)
- [ ] **Wealth-unknown** shows the "add your wealth to filter by affordability" note and skips the affordability filter. (§7.5)
- [ ] **Reference-degenerate** exposes the "≈ model" note with `l1_vs_model`; **gated** shows the foundation GatedState copy verbatim. (§7.6)
- [ ] **Error** (404 investor / 404 fund / 400 reference / transport) renders a quiet cached-last-good fallback, never a blank or a fabricated number; bad anchor/reference self-heals by clearing and re-firing. (§7.7)

**Interactions**
- [ ] Opening a card → S15 Explain; "Compare ▸" → S13 Fund-vs-Fund (pre-loaded, pre-comparing the held same-strategy fund when `compare_to_holdings` is present); the CTA opens the human contact flow and never executes a transaction. (§6)
- [ ] ReferenceSelector / AxisSelector / OpportunityFilter / AnchorBanner each re-parameterise the same read endpoint and round-trip through the URL query. (§2, §6)

**Accessibility & responsive**
- [ ] 3 / 2 / 1 cards per row at the three breakpoints; FundCard reflows to image-top on mobile; hero + disclaimer stay pinned. (§10)
- [ ] WCAG-AA: `<h1>` headline, `role="note"` disclaimer read before the funds, keyboard nav with focus rings, colour never the sole signal, CTA accessible names name the fund, ranking available behind the ProvenancePopover. (§10)
- [ ] English + German keys; €/EU formatting; template strings with named slots. (§10)

---

*Build S14 to this brief. It composes the FundCard and the seven-state model from the foundation, binds every element to `GET /api/recommendations`, and keeps one promise above all others: it surfaces what the engine found is missing and the real open funds that would close it — premium, calm, and unmistakably information, not advice.*


---

## Surface S15 — Explain (a recommendation)

> **Use case:** T1-3 — Explainable Investment Recommendation Engine. **Endpoint:** `POST /api/explain {fund_id, investor_id, audience}`. **One-line job:** show, transparently and without advice, *why Moonfare's IC recommends a named fund*, *why it fits this investor*, *what risks Moonfare sees*, *who should not invest*, and *how it compares to alternatives* — with **every doc-derived reason carrying a citation chip that opens the source snippet**, and the structured half degrading gracefully when no document corpus is indexed.
>
> This brief composes from the design system, component library, seven-state model, and citation pattern in `Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md` (the "Foundation"). It **references** those primitives (ProfileBar, MetaCard, FundCard, ProvenancePopover, ConfidenceBadge, SyntheticTag, CaptionBlock, PDFExportButton, the seven states, the Part 2.5 chart language) and **does not redefine** them. The one primitive the Foundation does not name — the **CitationChip** + its **doc-snippet popover** — is specified here in full so it lands in the shared library and is reused by S2, S6, S13, S18 (every surface that quotes a document).

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions
