- R2 (concentration): *"with a smaller ticket of about €{target_eur}, keeping your single-manager exposure under {cap_pct}%"*.
- R4 (ticket): *"if your remaining private-markets target room reached the €{min} minimum (currently €{room})"*.
- R5 (fit): *"with a comparable fund in {alt}, which fits your target mix"*.

**Check names** (`checks[i].constraint` → display name):
`risk_class_mismatch` → "Risk profile" · `concentration_breach` → "Concentration" · `liquidity_headroom` → "Liquidity headroom" · `ticket_vs_capacity` → "Ticket vs capacity" · `vintage_or_strategy_fit` → "Vintage / strategy fit".

**Status words** (never colour-only): `pass` → "Pass" (✓) · `fail` → "Doesn't fit" (✗) · `warn` → "Noted (not blocking)" (△) · `n/a` → "Not applicable" (–). The binding row adds the pill **"the driver"**.

**General-merits strip (Q5):** *"This fund is strong on its own merits — {label}, TVPI {net_tvpi}, DPI {net_dpi}. The consideration below is about your situation, not the fund."* (sub-label: *"General merits — for context only, not the verdict."*)

**Why-wait banner (Q4):**
- liquidity: *"Worth waiting: your own distributions are expected to free the €{gap_eur} this needs around {quarter} — about {duration} away."*
- pipeline: *"This opportunity isn't open yet ({pipeline_stage}). There's nothing to fix on your side — it simply isn't available right now."*

**No-blocker state (state b):**
- Headline: *"No blocking constraint detected — we found no suitability issue with this fund for you."*
- Caveat (mandatory): *"This doesn't mean Moonfare recommends it. Whether to invest is your decision — talk to your sales associate."*

**Capacity-unknown caveat (state d):** *"We couldn't fully assess this — your investable-wealth figure isn't on file, so the liquidity and ticket checks were skipped. Add it in your profile for a complete view."*

**Synthetic-soften note (state e):** *"This was flagged but isn't used to block you, because it relies on an estimated figure rather than confirmed data."*

**503 fail-closed (state g):** *"Suitability can't be assessed right now — the rules engine is temporarily unavailable. We won't guess. Please try again shortly."*

**The rules-version footnote (load-bearing):**
> *"Assessed under suitability rules **{rules_version}** (approved {approved_at} by {approved_by}) · data snapshot {snapshot_id} · evaluation {evaluation_id}. This is not investment advice — contact your sales associate to act."*

**Disclaimer.** The phrase **"not investment advice"** appears in the rationale (verbatim from the engine) and in the footnote. The FE never strips it and never adds advice verbs. **Forbidden anywhere in S16 FE-authored copy:** "buy", "you should", "we recommend", "invest now", "great opportunity for you" (the engine's `rationale` is pre-vetted against these; FE copy must match the same bar).

**Microcopy — the "Why not for me?" entry link:** quiet, curiosity-framed, never a rejection: *"Why not for me?"* / (vetoed badge) *"Not right now"* / (S20) *"See why this is on hold"* / (S15) *"Is that me?"*.

---

### §9 Badge rendering

S16 introduces one surface-specific badge family — the **suitability badge** — and reuses the standard badges (Part 2.6). All badges pair colour with a glyph/word (§10).

**(1) Suitability badge (the verdict-at-a-glance, rendered on FundCards and in S16's identity strip).** Driven by `verdict` + `why_wait`:

| condition | badge | colour token | glyph |
|---|---|---|---|
| `binding_constraint == null` (and not capacity-unknown) | "Fits your profile" | `--accent-mint` / `--positive` | ✓ |
| `binding_constraint != null` & `why_wait.is_wait == true` | "Not right now — revisit ~{quarter}" | `--brand-tint` (calm, not red) | hourglass |
| `binding_constraint != null` & `why_wait.is_wait == false` | "Not a fit right now" | `--under` (muted, not alarming red) | – (minus) |
| `binding_constraint == "risk_class_mismatch"` | "Risk-restricted" | `--gated` grey | lock |
| `capacity_unknown == true` | "Can't assess — add wealth" | `--muted` dashed | ? |
| 503 fail-closed | "Assessment unavailable" | `--muted` dashed | – |

The badge is deliberately **calm**: "not right now" is informational, not a failure stamp. The wait-eligible variant uses a hopeful tint and a date, reinforcing "not yet" over "no." On a FundCard, the badge sits as a small chip near the strategy/region badges, never overpowering the fund's identity.

**(2) `hard` rule tag.** On R1's check row, a small **"hard · regulatory"** tag (`--gated` grey) signalling this is a non-negotiable gate, not an economic preference. Distinct from the soft rules (no tag).

**(3) "the driver" pill.** On the binding row, a filled pill in `--under` reading **"the driver"** (or "binding") — the one check that produced the verdict.

**(4) SyntheticTag** (Part 2.6, amber "illustrative"). On any check whose decisive input was imputed (`checks[i].synthetic == true`) — accompanies the `warn` status. Also on the general-merits strip never (those are real track-record figures). Tooltip: *"based on an estimated figure — surfaced but not used to block you."*

**(5) ConfidenceBadge / significance.** On concentration-derived signals (`checks[i].significant`), the standard `n=… ±…` chip; not-significant deltas render greyed (Part 2.5).

**(6) CoverageBadge** (Part 2.6). On the expanded R2 ConcentrationDelta when the post-add concentration is **sector-based and coverage-limited** (`concentration_explanation.coverage_pct < 100`): *"based on {coverage_pct}% of NAV"*. When R2 falls back to the manager dimension (always full-book), coverage is 100% and the badge reads accordingly. Never imply 100% coverage when sector look-through is partial.

**(7) k-anon label.** When `fund.k_anon_ok == false`, the identity strip renders the bucketed label (*"a {strategy} fund"*) with a small *"name withheld — fewer than 10 investors"* note; the verdict still renders.

All badges are keyboard-focusable, carry `aria-label` text equal to their visible word, and never rely on colour alone.

---

### §10 Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive** (inherits Part 3 / Part 10; specifics for S16):
- **≥1280px** — single-column reading spine (~720–840px measure) centred in the three-column frame; cohort builder dimmed with its "doesn't affect suitability" note; Ask Alpha floating.
- **768–1279px** — cohort drawer + Ask Alpha launcher; reading spine widens; check rows full-width; the concentration mini-chart (when expanded) reflows to full width.
- **<768px** — single-column stack in the documented order (§3); ProfileBar → sheet; headline statement stays prominent (wraps gracefully at `h2`+); check rows become tappable expandable cards; the rules-version footnote stays at the content bottom, never buried. **No horizontal scroll** anywhere — including the concentration BarRow, which reflows rather than scrolls.
- The single clear statement is the one element guaranteed visible without interaction at every breakpoint — it is the surface's reason to exist.

**Accessibility** (WCAG-AA, Part 10):
- **Never colour-only.** Pass/fail/warn/n-a are signalled by glyph **and** word **and** colour; over/under and the suitability badge pair colour with a glyph. The binding row is signalled by the "the driver" pill + accent bar + `aria-current`, not colour alone.
- **Contrast.** All ink/surface pairs meet AA; the calm `--brand-tint` why-wait banner and the mint pass state meet AA against their text.
- **Keyboard.** Full tab order: switch-fund picker → check rows (expand on Enter/Space, `aria-expanded`) → cross-surface links → provenance chips → re-evaluate control → PDF export. Focus states visible (Part 2.4). No keyboard trap in the expanders or the picker.
- **Screen reader.** The headline statement is a single coherent labelled region (`role="status"` is *not* used — it isn't a transient alert; it's the page's primary heading content, `aria-level` set). Each check row announces "name, status, binding?" The would-change callout is associated with the binding row via `aria-describedby`. The rules-version footnote is in the document's landmark structure (a `contentinfo`-adjacent region), readable on demand.
- **Charts as data.** The single chart (ConcentrationDelta) exposes a "view data" affordance (Part 10): a tiny table of current vs post-add top-share vs cap. Its caption is always present.
- **Reduced motion.** Honour `prefers-reduced-motion`: expanders snap rather than ease; no value-in animation.
- **Copy clarity.** Plain language by default (the "doctor, not a pro" persona); the analytical signal numbers live behind expanders, not in the user's face. ARIA labels mirror the visible words exactly (no jargon-only labels).

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

S16 is accepted when **all** of the following hold (visual + interaction + binding), aligned with the foundation's Part 11 and the use-case brief's §6/§9:

**The three load-bearing requirements (the task's explicit bar):**
- [ ] **The binding constraint is named.** When `verdict.binding_constraint != null`, the single clear statement renders it explicitly via the §8 label map ("Not right now because {binding constraint}…"), and exactly **one** check row carries the "the driver" pill matching `verdict.binding_rule_id`. The category chip (Liquidity / Risk gate / Concentration / Ticket / Fit) is present.
- [ ] **"Would change" is present.** Whenever there is a binding constraint, the WouldChangeCallout renders the threshold that flips it — the engine's `would_change.human` verbatim plus the structured readout — completing the "…it becomes suitable when {threshold}" half of the statement. (Absent only in state b, where there is nothing to flip.)
- [ ] **`rules_version` is shown.** The rules-version footnote renders a non-empty `rules_version` (equal to the payload's `rules_version`) on **every** view, alongside `snapshot_id` and `evaluation_id`, with the "not investment advice" disclaimer. The version + evaluation id are click-to-copy and a "How was this decided?" popover exposes the fired-rules detail.

**The single clear statement:**
- [ ] The headline reads as the template "Not right now because {binding constraint}; it becomes suitable when {threshold}" (or the state-b clear-pass inversion), at `h1`/`h2` scale, visible without interaction at every breakpoint.
- [ ] The `{binding constraint}` phrase links to its check row; the `{threshold}` clause is bound to `would_change`, never FE-composed math.

**The deterministic reasons, listed:**
- [ ] All five checks (R1 risk · R2 concentration · R3 liquidity · R4 ticket · R5 vintage/strategy) render in fixed R1→R5 order with status glyph + word + colour (never colour-only).
- [ ] Each row expands to its signal numbers (§6); the binding row defaults expanded; each expanded row links to its owning surface (R3→S11, R2→S6, R5→S5/S2, R4→S9/profile, R1→KYC).
- [ ] `warn` rows (synthetic-soften) are visibly distinct from `fail`, carry a SyntheticTag, and never carry the "driver" pill; R1 (hard) is never softened.

**No-advice & audit (regulated core):**
- [ ] No FE-authored copy contains "buy / you should / we recommend / invest now"; the engine `rationale` renders verbatim with its disclaimer intact.
- [ ] State b (`binding_constraint == null`) renders the clear-pass + the mandatory "this is not a recommendation" caveat — never as a buy signal.
- [ ] State g 503 (fail-closed) renders **no verdict** — the surface never shows "suitable" when the engine failed closed.

**States & honesty:**
- [ ] All seven states render (picker prompt, happy, no-blocker, cold-start, null-wealth/capacity-unknown, synthetic-soften, wait, error) per §7; capacity-unknown never asserts a blocker or a clean pass it couldn't verify.
- [ ] The why-wait banner renders iff `why_wait.is_wait == true`, in a calm/hopeful (non-error) treatment, with the resolving quarter.
- [ ] The general-merits strip acknowledges the fund's quality (Q5) and is labelled display-only.

**Bindings & provenance:**
- [ ] Every number is a provenance chip; borrowed numbers (capacity ← T1-7/S11, HHI/top-share ← T1-9/S6) name and link their owning surface; S16 never presents a borrowed number as its own computation.
- [ ] The CoverageBadge appears on sector-based concentration when `coverage_pct < 100`; never implies full coverage.
- [ ] Placeholder data matches the §5 `/api/why_not` shape exactly (incl. `rules_version`, `evaluation_id`) so the live swap is a base-URL change.

**Frame & polish:**
- [ ] Detail-template frame persists; cohort builder dimmed with the "doesn't affect suitability" note; no vertical scroll on the analytical view (detail in expanders); PDF export produces an audit-bearing page (with `evaluation_id` + `rules_version` + disclaimer); brand calm/serious, no flashy transitions, light/dark wired.

---
*Build to this brief and the Foundation. S16's job is the hardest kind of honesty: telling a user that a fund they'd want isn't right yet — and being so precise, so auditable, and so clearly "not yet" rather than "no" that the message builds trust instead of friction. Name the binding constraint, show exactly what would change, stamp it with the rules version, and never, ever turn a clean pass into a recommendation.*


---

## Surface S17 — Missed Opportunities

> **Frontend surface brief for Claude Design.** This is the deep, build-ready spec for the **Missed Opportunities** surface (use case **T1-11**). It inherits everything from the Frontend Foundation (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`): the global frame (Part 3), the design system and tokens (Part 2), the component library (Part 2.6), the chart language (Part 2.5), the non-negotiables (Part 0.2), the seven-state catalog (Part 9), and the accessibility/responsive rules (Part 10). **Do not redefine those here — compose from them.** This brief only specifies what is unique to S17, and binds every element to `GET /api/missed_opportunities` exactly as shaped in the T1-11 deep brief (`Alpha_UseCase_Briefs/deep/T1-11_Missed_Opportunity.md`, §5).
>
> The single hardest discipline this surface must encode visually: **a "missed winner" is graded on realized DPI, never on a NAV mark.** TVPI/RVPI marks may never appear as the headline grade. The second discipline: the engagement-aware layer ("opportunities you actually viewed and declined") needs the 🔴 ENG feed, which is not yet wired — so when absent, the surface must **show an honest note and degrade to fit-only**, never silently mix or fake it. The third: every claim is **ex-post, counterfactual, descriptive** — "investors with your then-profile who took it realized 1.6×" — never a forward promise.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Answer the counterfactual question no screener can: *which good Moonfare opportunities did this investor plausibly should have done — judged by their profile and portfolio gap at the time each fund was offered — but passed on, confirmed good only by realized ex-post performance?* And, for the funds open right now, flag any that **resemble a past missed winner**. This is the reflective "learn" surface in Alpha's arc — not prescriptive. It builds trust by being honest about hindsight, including being honest that hindsight is hindsight.

**The user's questions this surface must answer at a glance (T1-11 §1):**
- *"Which Moonfare investments fit me well but I passed on?"* → the **missed list**, ranked by composite (fit × realized strength).
- *"Which good opportunities did I miss in secondaries / US growth / buyout?"* → the **strategy/vintage filters** on the list.
- *"What pattern explains my missed opportunities?"* → the **pattern summary card** ("you were persistently under-weight Secondaries vs your as-of model").
- *"Did I systematically underinvest in a strategy that suited me?"* → the pattern card's **systematic vs clustered** verdict.
- *"Does this open fund resemble something I passed on that later did well?"* → the **resemblance section** (open FundCards anchored to a past miss).
- *"What would have changed if I'd taken my best miss?"* → the per-row **counterfactual expander** ("would have closed a 12pp Secondaries gap; hypothetical value today ~€243k").

**The framing contract (copy deck §8 enforces this).** Every headline is **ex-post and conditional**: "you passed on X (2020); it realized 1.8× DPI" — a statement about what happened, paired with the "plausibly should have" caveat. The surface never says "you lost 1.8×" or "you should have invested." It says what fit you then, and what it realized since. The distinction is the entire ethical weight of this screen; the visual language must keep it.

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

**Rail position.** S17 sits in the left-rail navigation group as **"Missed"** — between *Why-not* (S16) and *Managers* (S18), inside the "evaluate / learn" cluster (Foundation Part 3). Selecting it swaps the centre stage full-screen; the ProfileBar, cohort builder, and Ask Alpha persist around it (Foundation Part 3 frame).

**Entry points into S17:**
1. **Direct** — the user clicks "Missed" in the rail.
2. **From Ask Alpha** — a chat answer to "what did I miss?" or "did I underinvest in secondaries?" renders an inline mini-list with a **"See your missed opportunities →"** deep-link that opens S17, carrying any strategy/vintage filter the question implied (e.g. the secondaries question pre-applies the Secondaries facet).
3. **From S20 Next Best Action** — when a recommended action references a strategy the user has historically under-built, a small **"You've passed on this before →"** cross-link routes to S17 pre-filtered to that strategy.
4. **From the Follower push (Part 8)** — the "you're underweight {Strategy}" alert offers a secondary link into S17's pattern view for that strategy.

**Exits from S17:**
- A **resemblance match → S13 (Funds / Fund-vs-Fund)**, opening the open fund pre-loaded as "A" with the anchor past-miss pre-loaded as "B" via the `vs a fund I passed` affordance (T1-11 resemblance → Fund-vs-Fund handoff; see §6).
- A **resemblance match → S15 (Explain)** via a secondary "Why this fund" action.
- The **cold-start state → S9 (Allocation Architect)** — an investor with no history has nothing to have missed, so the empty state routes forward to build (Foundation Part 9, state 2).

**Mode scope.** Investor mode and Advisor mode (advisor sees it inside an impersonated client's copilot, with the "acting as {client}" audit banner from Part 7). It is **not** an Internal-mode surface — there is no firm-aggregate version; missed opportunities are inherently per-investor.

---

### §3 Layout

S17 is a **full-screen Detail page, no scroll on the primary view** (Foundation Part 0.2: analytical views don't scroll). It is organized as three stacked bands inside the centre stage, top to bottom, each a titled region. The desktop (≥1280px) layout:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Missed Opportunities                                  [PDF export ▾]      │
│  Good funds you plausibly should have done — graded on realized returns.   │  ← header + sub
│  [ Strategy ▾ ] [ Vintage ▾ ]   ·   showing 5 misses · 4 confirmed good    │  ← filter bar + summary
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  BAND A — THE MISSED LIST  (≈48% height)                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ MissRow: Helios Secondaries II · Secondaries · 2020                   │ │
│  │   You passed on this. Investors with your then-profile realized 1.6× │ │
│  │   [REALIZED 1.62× DPI ✓]  [as-of fit 0.79]  [confirmed good]  ▸why    │ │
│  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │
│  │ MissRow: …  (rows ranked by composite; ≤ TOP_N_MISSES=10)            │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┤
│  BAND B — PATTERN SUMMARY  (≈26% height)      │  (shares row with Band A    │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │   tail on wide screens)    │
│  │ Pattern: Systematic underinvestment       │ │                            │
│  │ You were persistently under-weight        │ │                            │
│  │ Secondaries (mean as-of gap +10.4pp)      │ │                            │
│  │ across 3 offer windows.                    │ │                            │
│  │ [by vintage ▮▮▯] [by geography Eur/US]    │ │                            │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ │                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  BAND C — CURRENT FUNDS THAT RESEMBLE A PAST MISSED WINNER  (≈26% height)  │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐                         │
│  │ FundCard    │  │ FundCard    │  │ FundCard    │   ← resembling opens     │
│  │ Helios Sec. │  │ …           │  │ …           │     (horizontal row)     │
│  │ IV  ~0.96   │  │             │  │             │                          │
│  │ resembles → │  │             │  │             │                          │
│  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘                         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Band A — The missed list.** A vertical list of **MissRow** components (defined §4), one per `missed[]` entry, ranked by `composite` descending, capped at `TOP_N_MISSES`. This is the hero band and gets the most vertical real estate. Each row carries the ex-post realized headline, the as-of fit, the tier badge, and a "why" expander. The list is the answer to "what did I miss."

**Band B — The pattern summary.** A single **PatternCard** (defined §4) that states the systematic-vs-clustered verdict in one sentence, names the dominant strategy and the mean as-of gap, and shows two small distribution strips (by vintage, by geography). It answers "is there a pattern" — and crucially renders an honest *"not enough signal for a pattern"* state when `pattern.kind="insufficient"`.

**Band C — Resemblance to a past missed winner.** A horizontal row of **FundCards** in the Moonfare fund-listing layout (Foundation FundCard, Part 2.6), one per `resemblance[]` entry. Each card is a *currently open* fund, badged with its resemblance score and a one-line anchor note ("resembles Helios Secondaries II, which you passed on and which realized 1.62× DPI"). Clicking a card routes to Fund-vs-Fund (§6). This is the only forward-looking, actionable band — and it is deliberately framed as "resembles," not "will perform like."

**Why this order.** Past misses (the evidence) → the pattern (the synthesis) → the open funds (the only thing still actionable). Reflection earns the right to the call-to-action at the bottom; the list grounds the pattern, the pattern motivates the resembling opens.

**Responsive.**
- **≥1280px (desktop):** the three bands stack as drawn; Band A list shows ~4–5 rows before its own internal scroll (the row list may scroll *within* Band A; the page itself does not). Band C shows 3 FundCards in a row.
- **768–1279px (tablet):** cohort builder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher (Foundation Part 3). Bands stay stacked full-width. Band A row list scrolls internally; Band C shows 2 FundCards per row, wrapping. MissRow drops its inline as-of fit chip to a tap-to-reveal to preserve the realized-DPI headline.
- **<768px (mobile, advisor on the go):** single column. The filter bar becomes a sticky sheet trigger. Band A rows become stacked cards (realized DPI badge on its own line, large). PatternCard full-width. Band C FundCards stack one per row. The whole page scrolls vertically on mobile only (the no-scroll rule is a desktop analytical-view rule; mobile reflow is permitted per Foundation Part 3). No chart or row ever requires *horizontal* scroll.

---

### §4 Components & charts

S17 introduces three surface-specific compositions (MissRow, PatternCard, ResembleFundCard) built from foundation primitives, plus the standard badges. There are **no heavy charts** — the surface is deliberately list-and-card driven because the content is judgmental and per-fund, not distributional. The only chart-like elements are two micro horizontal-bar distribution strips inside the PatternCard (an approved encoding, Part 2.5).

#### 4.1 MissRow (the missed-list row with ex-post outcome)

A horizontal row component, full-width of Band A. Anatomy left → right:

- **Fund identity block:** fund name (h2-ish, the largest text in the row), then a meta line: `{strategy} · {manager} · {geography} · vintage {vintage_year}` in caption/muted. If the fund is **sub-k-anon** (named-fund k-anon fails), render a bucket label instead — "a large-cap Secondaries fund (2020)" — and suppress the manager name (Foundation k-anon discipline; T1-11 §8).
- **The ex-post realized headline (the load-bearing line):** one plain sentence directly under the identity, e.g. *"You passed on this. Investors with your then-profile who took it realized 1.62× DPI."* This binds to `expost.realized_dpi`. It is the emotional and factual core of the row and must read as past-tense, conditional, realized.
- **Badge cluster (right-aligned on desktop, wrapping below on narrow):**
  - **RealizedBadge** — `1.62× DPI ✓` rendered with `--positive` and an upward glyph (▲) when good, in the **mono/tabular** number style. The label **DPI (realized)** is always present — never just "1.62×" bare, and never "TVPI." See §9 for the full badge-rendering contract. Suppressed/replaced when ex-post is unknown (young vintage / no record) — see tier handling below.
  - **FitBadge** — `as-of fit 0.79` — a neutral chip showing `fit.value` (0–1), with a ProvenancePopover (it carries `asof_date`, `gap_term`, method "as-of historical fit"). This is the only place we surface the bitemporal fit; it earns its space because it justifies *why this was a miss for you specifically at the time*.
  - **TierBadge** — one of: `confirmed good` (solid, `--positive` tint), `provisional` (outline, neutral — for `tier="provisional_unrealized"`, young/unrealized vintages). Never a third value; T1-11 only emits these two tiers in the surfaced set.
  - **SyntheticTag** — amber "as-of identity extrapolated" pill when `asof.is_synthetic_asof=true` (the as-of risk/wealth fields are reconstructed-stable from a single KYC capture, T1-11 §3.0/§8). This is honest about the bitemporal limitation and must appear on essentially every row, because real exports are single-capture.
- **"▸ why" expander.** Click expands the row in place (no navigation) to reveal:
  - The **as-of reconstruction line:** "At {offer_open_date} you had committed {n_prior_funds} funds; you were {asof_gap_pts}pp under-weight {strategy} vs your as-of model." Binds to `asof.{n_prior_funds, asof_gap_pts}`.
  - The **counterfactual block** (only present when `counterfactual` is in the payload, i.e. `include_counterfactual=true`): "Had you committed ~your then-typical ticket, this would have added {added_strategy_pts} to Secondaries, lifted private-markets to {new_pm_pct}%, and be worth ~€{hypothetical_value_today} today (closing {gap_closed_pts}pp of gap)." Binds to `counterfactual.*`. **Marked with a "what-if" SyntheticTag** because `counterfactual.provenance.counterfactual=true` — this is explicitly a hypothetical, not a guarantee (T1-11 §3.7, copy deck §8).
  - A ProvenancePopover trigger ("show how this was computed") exposing the full provenance object: method, n, asof_date, vintage_scope, source `fund_track_record.net_dpi`, snapshot_id.

**Row ordering & truncation.** Rows render in `composite` order as delivered; the FE does not re-rank. If more than `TOP_N_MISSES` arrive (it won't, the engine caps), show only the first 10. Confirmed-good rows visually lead; provisional rows are visually quieter (lower contrast tier badge, no `--positive` realized badge — instead a neutral "too early to grade" chip).

#### 4.2 PatternCard (the pattern summary)

A single MetaCard-style card (Foundation MetaCard, Part 2.6). Anatomy:

- **Verdict headline:** the `pattern.kind` rendered as a human label — `Systematic underinvestment` / `Clustered misses` / `Not enough signal for a pattern`. The systematic verdict uses a quiet emphasis treatment (a small flag glyph + `--over`/`--under` neutral, *not* alarm red — this is reflective, not an error).
- **The one-sentence note:** binds directly to `pattern.note` ("you were persistently under-weight Secondaries vs your as-of model and passed on fitting, later-realized funds in it"). This is the synthesis sentence.
- **The dominant-strategy stat line:** `dominant: Secondaries · {share×100}% of your misses · mean as-of gap +{mean_asof_gap}pp · under-weight in {windows_underweight} offer windows`. Binds to `pattern.{dominant, share, mean_asof_gap, windows_underweight}`.
- **Two micro distribution strips** (the only "charts" on the surface):
  - **By vintage** — a tiny horizontal micro-bar strip, one bar per year, height/length ∝ count, with the count inline (% inline rule, Part 2.5). Binds to `pattern.by_vintage`.
  - **By geography** — same, binds to `pattern.by_geography`.
  These are intentionally small (caption-scale) — supporting evidence, not the headline. They use the fixed strategy/region colour map where applicable.
- **CaptionBlock** under the card: one sentence, e.g. "Based on {n_misses} qualifying misses; a pattern is only claimed at 4 or more." Binds to `pattern.provenance.n_misses` and the `MIN_MISSES_FOR_PATTERN` constant framing.
- **ConfidenceBadge / ProvenancePopover:** the card's numbers carry the standard badges; the popover exposes `pattern.provenance.method` ("as-of model gap across offer windows").

When `pattern.kind="insufficient"`, the card collapses to a quiet single line: *"Not enough qualifying misses yet to call a pattern."* — no fabricated strips, no dominant strategy (Foundation Part 9 state 3 "thin"; T1-11 §7 state 3).

#### 4.3 ResembleFundCard (resemblance matches as FundCards)

Each `resemblance[]` entry renders as a **FundCard** (Foundation Part 2.6 — image right, name + access-feeder line, region + strategy badges, stats row Target Fund Size / Target Net MOIC / Expected Close / Min. investment, "Contact your sales associate" CTA). S17 adds, above the standard CTA:

- A **ResemblanceBadge** — `resembles a past miss · {score}` (e.g. `0.96`), neutral tint, with a ProvenancePopover exposing `provenance.method` ("strategy+manager+thesis(sector-cosine)+geo/cap resemblance").
- An **anchor line** — binds to `note` + `anchor_miss`: "Resembles **Helios Secondaries II**, which you passed on and which realized **1.62× DPI**." The anchor fund name links back to its MissRow in Band A (scroll-into-view + highlight). The realized-DPI figure here re-uses the RealizedBadge styling (§9) and is, again, **DPI realized, never a mark**.
- A **k-anon guard:** when `k_anon_ok=false`, the card renders the open fund as a bucketed label ("a Secondaries fund"), `open_fund_id=null`, image suppressed, and the card is non-clickable into Fund-vs-Fund (you can't compare a fund you can't name). The anchor note still renders if the anchor itself is k-anon-OK.

The cards sit in a horizontal row (Band C), max 3 visible on desktop, the rest reachable by an in-band horizontal pager (not page scroll). Ordered by `score` descending as delivered.

---

### §5 Data bindings

**Single source.** The entire surface binds to one call: **`GET /api/missed_opportunities?investor_id={id}&mode={misses|pattern|resemblance}&group_by={strategy|vintage?}&fit_lens=profile&rank_by=composite&engagement_aware={bool}&include_counterfactual={bool}&strategy={?}&geography={?}`** (T1-11 §5). The default page load calls it once with `mode=misses, rank_by=composite, include_counterfactual=true` and consumes `misses[]`, `pattern`, `resemblance[]`, `engagement`, and `summary` from the single response (the assembler returns all sections regardless of `mode`; `mode` only shifts which is the headline). The FE does **not** make three separate calls — one call hydrates all three bands.

**Element ← field map:**

| UI element | ← API field |
|---|---|
| Band A MissRow — fund name | `misses[].fund` |
| MissRow — meta line | `misses[].{strategy, manager, geography, vintage_year}` |
| MissRow — offer window (in "why") | `misses[].{offer_open_date, offer_close_date}` |
| MissRow — **RealizedBadge value** | `misses[].expost.realized_dpi` (label fixed "DPI realized") |
| MissRow — realized known/good flags | `misses[].expost.{expost_known, is_good}` |
| MissRow — FitBadge | `misses[].fit.value` (+ `fit.provenance` → popover) |
| MissRow — TierBadge | `misses[].tier` (`confirmed_good_miss` → "confirmed good"; `provisional_unrealized` → "provisional") |
| MissRow — SyntheticTag (as-of) | `misses[].asof.is_synthetic_asof` |
| MissRow "why" — as-of reconstruction | `misses[].asof.{n_prior_funds, asof_gap_pts, asof_pm_actual_pct, risk_class}` |
| MissRow "why" — counterfactual block | `misses[].counterfactual.{added_strategy_pts, new_pm_pct, hypothetical_value_today, gap_closed_pts}` (+ `provenance.counterfactual=true` → what-if tag) |
| MissRow — ProvenancePopover | `misses[].expost.provenance` + `misses[].fit.provenance` (method, asof_date, vintage_scope, source, snapshot_id) |
| Band B PatternCard — verdict | `pattern.kind` |
| PatternCard — note | `pattern.note` |
| PatternCard — dominant stat line | `pattern.{dominant, share, mean_asof_gap, windows_underweight}` |
| PatternCard — by-vintage strip | `pattern.by_vintage` |
| PatternCard — by-geography strip | `pattern.by_geography` |
| PatternCard — caption/confidence | `pattern.provenance.{method, n_misses}` |
| Band C ResembleFundCard — open fund | `resemblance[].{open_fund, open_fund_id}` |
| ResembleFundCard — score badge | `resemblance[].score` |
| ResembleFundCard — anchor note | `resemblance[].note` + `resemblance[].anchor_miss.{fund, fund_id, realized_dpi}` |
| ResembleFundCard — k-anon guard | `resemblance[].k_anon_ok` |
| Filter bar / summary | `summary.{n_misses, n_confirmed_good, n_provisional, top_composite, snapshot_id}` |
| Engagement note | `engagement.{engagement_aware, available, degraded_to, note}` |

**`ex_post_tvpi` handling (explicit).** The realized grade binds **only** to `expost.realized_dpi` (sourced from `fund_track_record.net_dpi`). If the payload ever carries a `tvpi`/`ex_post_tvpi`/`net_rvpi` field, the FE **must not** render it as the headline grade. At most it may appear *inside* the ProvenancePopover, clearly labelled "mark (unrealized) — not used for grading," so a curious analyst can see it without it ever promoting a fund visually. This is the front-end enforcement of T1-11 gate G3. A row whose only positive number is a mark must visually read as **not** a confirmed winner.

**Engagement-aware note when feed absent (explicit).** When `engagement.available=false` (the 🔴 ENG feed is not wired), the surface renders a persistent, calm **engagement note** in the header region: *"This shows funds that fit you but you didn't hold. A richer 'viewed and declined' view turns on when engagement data connects."* — bound to `engagement.note` / `engagement.degraded_to="fit_only"`. The note is informational (not an error, not a `NotConnectedState` card that blocks content) — the fit-based misses still render fully beneath it. If the user toggles an "only funds I viewed & declined" control (see §6), and the feed is absent, that toggle is **disabled** with the same note as its tooltip — never silently returning the fit-only set as if it were the engagement set (T1-11 §6 engagement gate; §7 state 6).

**Snapshot/provenance.** The whole view footer shows `grounded · snapshot {summary.snapshot_id}` consistent with the rest of Alpha. Every number on the surface arrives as the standard `{value, provenance}` shape and gets its ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover (Foundation Part 0.2 — no bare numbers).

---

### §6 Interactions

**Open a resemblance match → Fund-vs-Fund / Explain.** Clicking a ResembleFundCard (Band C) opens **S13 (Funds / Fund-vs-Fund)** with the open fund pre-loaded as slot **A** and the anchor past-miss pre-loaded as slot **B**, using S13's native "vs a fund I passed" historical-fund affordance (Foundation S13 interactions). The comparable-vintage caveat from S13 applies — the FE passes `a=open_fund_id`, `b=anchor_miss.fund_id` to `/api/fund_compare`. A secondary action on the card, **"Why this fund,"** opens **S15 (Explain)** for the open fund (`POST /api/explain {fund_id: open_fund_id}`). Both handoffs require `k_anon_ok=true`; bucketed (un-named) resemblance cards expose neither action.

**Filter by strategy / vintage.** The filter bar (header) has two segmented/dropdown controls:
- **Strategy** — re-issues the call with `&strategy={value}` (and re-renders Band A + re-derives the pattern + filters the resemblance anchors to that strategy). Maps to T1-11 Q2 ("good opportunities I missed in secondaries").
- **Vintage** — applies `group_by=vintage` (or a client-side facet over `vintage_year` for the loaded set) to bucket the missed list by offer vintage. Maps to T1-11 "missed by vintage."
Filters re-resolve and re-render **only the affected bands**, not a full page reload (Foundation performance rule, Part 10). Clearing filters restores the composite-ranked full set. The active filter is reflected in the summary line ("showing 3 Secondaries misses · all confirmed good").

**Cohort builder interaction.** The left-rail cohort builder is present but **note its limited role here**: S17 is intrinsically about *this investor's own* history, not a peer slice — the "investors with your then-profile who took it" phrasing is an as-of peer construct computed engine-side, not the live cohort. So cohort-builder changes do **not** re-slice the missed list. The FE should visually de-emphasize the cohort builder on this surface (or show a small helper: "Missed opportunities are about your own history; the cohort builder doesn't change this view"). This prevents a confusing no-op.

**Engagement toggle.** A header toggle **"Only funds I viewed & declined"** maps to `engagement_aware=true`. When `engagement.available=true` it filters the missed set to genuinely-considered-and-declined funds (and the engine boosts their composite). When `available=false`, the toggle is **disabled** with the explanatory tooltip (§5). This is the visible front for the engagement gate.

**Row expand.** "▸ why" toggles the in-place MissRow expander (as-of reconstruction + counterfactual + provenance); only one row need be expanded at a time on mobile (accordion) to control height; multiple may expand on desktop.

**Provenance everywhere.** Every number — realized DPI, fit, as-of gap, counterfactual value, pattern shares — has a ProvenancePopover ("show how this was computed") exposing cohort/n/method/asof_date/vintage_scope/snapshot_id. The realized-DPI popover explicitly states **"source: fund_track_record.net_dpi (realized distributions; excludes unrealized marks)"** so the G3 discipline is visible to any user who clicks (T1-11 §3.3).

**PDF export.** The PDFExportButton (header) exports the current filtered view as a clean PowerPoint-style PDF — the missed list, the pattern, the resemblance cards, with all badges and the snapshot footer preserved (Foundation PDFExportButton).

**Ask Alpha cross-talk.** Asking Alpha "what's my biggest miss?" or "did I underinvest in X?" returns an inline mini-answer with a deep-link back into S17 (pre-filtered), and conversely the "▸ why" provenance is the same data Ask Alpha cites — consistent grounding.

---

### §7 Seven states

Every state from the Foundation catalog (Part 9) renders; below is the **exact S17 branch** for each, bound to the T1-11 §7 state machine.

1. **Happy** — `summary.n_confirmed_good ≥ 1`, `pattern.kind ∈ {systematic_underinvestment, clustered}`, `resemblance[]` non-empty. Band A lists confirmed-good (and any provisional) rows ranked by composite; PatternCard shows the verdict + strips; Band C shows resembling open FundCards. The reference render.

2. **Cold-start** (`summary` reflects no history / `misses:[]` + `cold_start:true`, because `reconstruct_asof` returned `exists=False` for every offer window — an investor with no history "should have done" nothing yet, T1-11 §7 state 2). Do **not** show an empty list. Replace the whole centre stage with a calm cold-start panel: *"Nothing to look back on yet — you don't have an investment history to compare against. Let's build forward instead."* + a primary CTA routing to **S9 Allocation Architect** (Foundation Part 9 state 2). The engagement note is still echoed.

3. **Thin** (`n_misses < MIN_MISSES_FOR_PATTERN=4`; T1-11 §7 state 3). Band A still lists the misses it has. **PatternCard collapses** to the "Not enough qualifying misses yet to call a pattern" line — no dominant strategy, no fabricated systematic claim. A RelaxBanner-style note is *not* used here (this isn't a relaxed cohort; it's genuinely few misses) — instead the honest "not enough signal" copy.

4. **Low coverage** (offering windows unresolved for many funds — `summary.coverage_note` / `offering_resolved_pct` present; T1-11 §7 state 4). Surface the available misses normally, but render a **CoverageBadge** in the header band: *"Based on {offering_resolved_pct}% of the offering history we could reconstruct."* Never invent windows; never imply the list is exhaustive.

5. **Null ex-post / young vintage** (every candidate miss is young/has no realized DPI; `summary.n_confirmed_good=0`, `no_confirmed_good:true`; T1-11 §7 state 5). All rows render as **provisional** tier with a neutral **"too early to grade"** chip in place of the RealizedBadge (no `--positive`, no DPI number presented as a grade). A prominent caption: *"None of these have realized returns yet — we make no 'good opportunity' claim until distributions land."* The PatternCard may still describe clustering by fit, but makes **no** ex-post-strength claim. This is G3 honesty made visible.

6. **Engagement-gated** (`engagement_aware=true` requested but `engagement.available=false`; `degraded_to="fit_only"`; T1-11 §7 state 6). The missed set is unchanged (fit-only); the header engagement note is shown; the "viewed & declined" toggle is disabled with tooltip. **No silent mixing** — the set shown equals the `engagement_aware=false` set exactly.

7. **Error / degraded** (404 unknown investor, or engine down). On 404, the standard "we couldn't find this investor" frame. On engine/model degradation, show cached last-good with a quiet banner (Foundation Part 9 state 7) — never a blank screen, never a fabricated DPI. A `422` from a bad `anchor_fund_id` (resemblance) degrades just Band C to "couldn't resolve a comparison fund," leaving Bands A/B intact.

Cross-cutting: **not-significant** deltas render greyed; **SyntheticTag** on every `is_synthetic_asof` row and every counterfactual; **loading** = calm skeleton rows in each band, not spinners.

---

### §8 Copy deck

All strings English-first, structured for German localization (Foundation Part 10 i18n). The voice is reflective, honest, never blaming, never promising.

**Header**
- Title: **"Missed Opportunities"**
- Subtitle: **"Good funds you plausibly should have done — judged by your profile at the time, and graded on realized returns."**
- Engagement note (feed absent): *"This shows funds that fit you but you didn't hold. A richer 'viewed and declined' view turns on when engagement data connects."*
- Summary line: *"Showing {n_misses} misses · {n_confirmed_good} confirmed good · {n_provisional} too early to grade."*

**The ex-post framing (the row headline) — these are the load-bearing strings**
- Confirmed good: *"You passed on this. Investors with your then-profile who took it realized {dpi}× DPI."*
- The "plausibly should have" caveat (always visible on the row, caption-scale): *"Fit your profile and portfolio gap at the time — this is hindsight, not a missed certainty."*
- Provisional / young: *"You passed on this. It fit you then, but it's too early to know how it did."*
- Counterfactual (in "why"): *"Had you committed your then-typical ticket, this would have added {pts}pp to {strategy} and be worth ~€{value} today — a what-if, not a guarantee."* (carries the what-if SyntheticTag)
- As-of reconstruction (in "why"): *"At {date} you'd committed {n} funds and were {gap}pp under-weight {strategy} versus your model then."*

**Realized-grade microcopy (G3 discipline)**
- Badge label, always: **"DPI · realized"** (never "TVPI," never a bare "×").
- Provenance line: *"Source: realized distributions (fund_track_record.net_dpi). Excludes unrealized marks."*
- Young-vintage chip: **"too early to grade"** (tooltip: *"This vintage hasn't realized enough distributions to grade on DPI."*)

**Pattern card**
- Systematic: *"Systematic underinvestment — you were persistently under-weight {strategy} versus your model and passed on fitting funds that later realized."*
- Clustered: *"Your misses cluster in {strategy}, but not systematically."*
- Insufficient: *"Not enough qualifying misses yet to call a pattern."*
- Caption: *"Based on {n} qualifying misses; a pattern is only claimed at 4 or more."*

**Resemblance**
- Section title: **"Open funds that resemble a past missed winner"**
- Anchor note: *"Resembles {anchor_fund}, which you passed on and which realized {dpi}× DPI."*
- Resemblance badge: *"resembles a past miss · {score}"*
- Caveat caption: *"Resemblance is structural (strategy, manager, what it invests in) — not a forecast."*
- CTA: **"Compare side by side →"** (to S13) · secondary **"Why this fund"** (to S15) · standard FundCard CTA **"Contact your sales associate."**

**Cohort helper:** *"Missed opportunities are about your own history; the cohort builder doesn't change this view."*

**Cold-start:** *"Nothing to look back on yet — you don't have an investment history to compare against. Let's build forward instead."* + **"Open the Allocation Architect →"**

**Tone guardrails (do / don't):**
- Do: "realized 1.6× DPI," "fit you at the time," "a what-if," "hindsight," "resembles."
- Don't: "you lost," "you should have invested," "guaranteed," "would have made," "TVPI," "you missed out on returns." Never present a mark as a grade. Never imply the user erred — only that something fit and later realized.

---

### §9 Badge rendering (realized, not marks)

This is the most consequential rendering rule on the surface; it gets its own section.

**RealizedBadge — the only "grade" badge.**
- Binds **exclusively** to `expost.realized_dpi` (engine source `fund_track_record.net_dpi`).
- Always carries the explicit label **"DPI · realized"** adjacent to the number. The number is in `mono`/tabular figures. Format: `1.62× DPI · realized`.
- Colour: `--positive` with an upward glyph **▲** only when `expost.is_good=true` (i.e. realized DPI ≥ engine threshold). A realized DPI present but below "good" (`is_good=false`) would mean the fund didn't actually make the surfaced set (the engine drops under-realized funds), so the FE never has to render a "bad realized" badge in Band A — but if such a value ever arrives, it renders **neutral**, never green, and the row reads provisional.
- When `expost.expost_known=false` (young vintage suppressed, or no track record): **do not render a DPI number at all.** Render the neutral **"too early to grade"** chip instead. This is the visual enforcement of G3 young-vintage suppression (T1-11 §3.3, §6).

**Marks are never a grade.**
- TVPI / RVPI / NAV-mark values must **never** appear as a badge, headline, or coloured grade anywhere on S17. If present in the payload, they live only inside the ProvenancePopover, under the literal label **"mark (unrealized) — not used for grading."**
- A fund whose only strong number is a mark must visually read as **not confirmed** (provisional tier, neutral chip). The whole point of T1-11 is that marks can falsely promote young or write-up-heavy funds; the badge layer is the last line of defence.

**Other badges (composed from Foundation primitives):**
- **FitBadge** — neutral chip, `as-of fit {0.79}`, ProvenancePopover with `asof_date` + `gap_term`. Distinct styling from RealizedBadge so the two are never confused (fit is a model score; DPI is realized truth).
- **TierBadge** — `confirmed good` (solid positive tint) vs `provisional` (outline neutral).
- **SyntheticTag** — amber, on `is_synthetic_asof` (as-of identity extrapolated) and on the counterfactual (what-if). Standard Foundation SyntheticTag.
- **ResemblanceBadge** — neutral, `resembles a past miss · {score}`, with method popover.
- **CoverageBadge** — header, only in the low-coverage state.
- **ConfidenceBadge** — standard `n=… · ±…` where the engine supplies CI; greys the value when not significant.

**Accessibility of badges:** colour is always paired with a glyph/sign (▲ for good realized; outline shape for provisional) and an explicit text label — never colour-only (Foundation Part 10).

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive** (extends §3; Foundation Part 3 breakpoints):
- **≥1280px:** three stacked bands; Band A internal-scrolls its row list if >5 rows; Band C shows 3 FundCards. No page scroll.
- **768–1279px:** cohort builder → drawer, Ask Alpha → launcher; bands full-width stacked; Band C 2-up wrapping; MissRow as-of-fit chip becomes tap-to-reveal so the realized headline keeps priority.
- **<768px:** single column; sticky filter sheet; MissRow → stacked card with the realized DPI badge on its own large line; PatternCard full-width with strips stacked; Band C FundCards one-per-row; vertical page scroll permitted (mobile reflow), no horizontal scroll anywhere.
- No micro-bar strip, badge, or FundCard ever requires horizontal scrolling at any breakpoint.

**Accessibility** (Foundation Part 10, WCAG-AA):
- **Never colour-only:** realized-good = green **+ ▲ + "realized" text**; provisional = neutral + outline + "too early to grade" text; over/under in counterfactual paired with sign.
- **Keyboard:** the filter controls, the "▸ why" expanders, the engagement toggle, the resemblance cards, and every ProvenancePopover trigger are fully keyboard-operable with visible focus states; the MissRow expander is an `aria-expanded` disclosure; Band C is a keyboard-navigable list.
- **Screen readers:** each MissRow is a labelled region: "{fund}, {strategy}, vintage {year}, you passed on this, realized {dpi} times DPI, as-of fit {0.79}, confirmed good." The realized badge has an `aria-label` that includes "realized DPI" so it's never read as a generic multiplier. SyntheticTag and CoverageBadge content is in the accessible name, not only visual.
- **Chart data behind a table:** the PatternCard micro-strips expose a "view data" affordance giving the by-vintage / by-geography counts as a table (Foundation Part 10).
- **Motion:** value-in animation once, ≤150ms; no parallax/confetti (Foundation Part 2.4). The "why" expander animates height calmly; respects `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- **i18n:** all strings externalized; numbers/currency EU-formatted (€, German number formatting); DPI rendered as `1,62×` in German locale. The "DPI · realized" label localizes; the "×" stays.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

A build of S17 is correct when **all** of the following hold:

**The G3 discipline — ex-post realized, never marks (the headline acceptance):**
- [ ] Every "grade" badge in Band A binds to `expost.realized_dpi` and carries the explicit **"DPI · realized"** label — never a bare "×," never "TVPI."
- [ ] No TVPI / RVPI / NAV-mark value renders as a badge, headline, or coloured grade anywhere; if present in payload it appears only inside the ProvenancePopover, labelled "mark (unrealized) — not used for grading."
- [ ] A row with `expost.expost_known=false` (young vintage / no record) shows the neutral **"too early to grade"** chip and **no DPI number**; it renders as `provisional` tier, never `confirmed good`.
- [ ] The realized-DPI ProvenancePopover states the source is `fund_track_record.net_dpi` (realized distributions, excludes marks).

**The pattern is shown:**
- [ ] PatternCard renders the `pattern.kind` verdict, the `pattern.note` sentence, the dominant-strategy stat line (`dominant`, `share`, `mean_asof_gap`, `windows_underweight`), and the by-vintage + by-geography micro-strips.
- [ ] When `pattern.kind="insufficient"` (`n_misses < 4`), the card collapses to "Not enough qualifying misses yet to call a pattern" with no fabricated dominant strategy or systematic claim.
- [ ] Systematic vs clustered is visually distinguishable; "systematic" uses quiet emphasis, not alarm red.

**The engagement note when the feed is absent:**
- [ ] When `engagement.available=false`, the header shows the calm engagement note (bound to `engagement.note` / `degraded_to="fit_only"`) and the content still renders fully beneath it (not a blocking NotConnectedState).
- [ ] The "only funds I viewed & declined" toggle is **disabled** with an explanatory tooltip when the feed is absent; it never silently returns the fit-only set as if it were the engagement set.

**Structure, bindings, framing:**
- [ ] All three bands (missed list, pattern summary, resemblance) hydrate from a **single** `/api/missed_opportunities` call.
- [ ] Band C resemblance entries render as Moonfare-layout FundCards with a resemblance score, an anchor note bound to `anchor_miss`, and the standard "Contact your sales associate" CTA.
- [ ] Clicking a (k-anon-OK) resemblance card opens **S13 Fund-vs-Fund** with open fund as A and anchor miss as B; the secondary action opens **S15 Explain**.
- [ ] Strategy and vintage filters re-resolve only the affected bands (no full reload) and update the summary line.
- [ ] k-anon: sub-threshold named funds (in both `misses[]` and `resemblance[]`) render as bucketed labels with `id=null`, image suppressed, and no Fund-vs-Fund handoff.
- [ ] Every row's "why" expander shows the as-of reconstruction; the counterfactual block (when present) is tagged as a what-if (`provenance.counterfactual=true`).
- [ ] Every number carries ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover; every `is_synthetic_asof` row and every counterfactual carries a SyntheticTag.
- [ ] All seven states render: happy, cold-start (→ Architect), thin (insufficient pattern), low-coverage (CoverageBadge), null-ex-post (all provisional / "too early to grade"), engagement-gated, error (404 / 422 degrade Band C only).

**Frame, brand, a11y:**
- [ ] The surface composes the persistent frame (ProfileBar, rail, Ask Alpha) and de-emphasizes the cohort builder with the helper note (it doesn't re-slice this view).
- [ ] Copy is ex-post and conditional throughout; the "plausibly should have / hindsight, not certainty" caveat is always visible; no blaming or forward-promise language.
- [ ] Responsive across the three breakpoints with no horizontal scroll; full keyboard nav; colour always paired with glyph/text; PatternCard strips have a "view data" table; reduced-motion respected; English + German localization, EU number formatting.
- [ ] PDF export reproduces the current filtered view with badges and the `snapshot_id` footer.

---
*Build S17 to this brief; bind every element to `/api/missed_opportunities` per §5; render all seven states per §7. The surface's job is to make hindsight honest — realized returns, not marks; a named pattern, not a vague feeling; resembling open funds, framed as resemblance not prophecy — and to be unmistakably truthful when the engagement feed isn't there yet.*


---

## Surface S18 — Manager Patterns

> *Frontend surface spec for Claude Design. Use case **T2-9 — Top Manager Pattern Intelligence**. Bind to `GET /api/manager_patterns?strategy={str}` (optional `&top_n={int,default 3}&audience={LP|INTERNAL,default LP}`). This surface inherits the global frame (Foundation Part 3), the design system (Part 2), the component library (Part 2.6), and renders all seven states (Part 9). It does **not** redefine any of those — it composes from them. Where this spec and the Foundation disagree, the Foundation wins; where this spec is more specific, it governs S18.*

This surface answers one question: **what do top-ranked managers systematically do differently?** It is an aggregator over *managers*, not over investors — so there is **no cohort builder dependency, no "Peers" cohort, and no paid-viewer gating** on this surface. That single fact reshapes the frame for S18 (see §2). The whole surface is built on two non-negotiable disciplines the backend enforces and the UI must make legible: (1) the ranking is **vintage-cohorted skill** (mean like-vintage z of net TVPI over mature funds), never raw IRR or raw TVPI — the UI must say so structurally and never let a raw multiple read as a rank; (2) every team/people claim is **either a DD-doc citation or it is omitted** — the UI must render citation chips on every team claim and visibly omit (not blank, not fabricate) where the corpus is absent.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Surface the construction "DNA" that separates the top quartile of managers in a strategy from the rest, transparently enough that a wealth-grade reader trusts it: a vintage-cohorted ranking, the shared patterns of the leaders, what they avoid, where they concentrate by industry (coverage-bound), whether they are converging on the same subsegments, and how their teams are built (DD-doc-cited). The differentiator the surface must *feel*: not "Fund X is good," but "the managers who consistently beat their vintage cohort run more concentrated books, syndicate less, realize faster, keep stable senior benches — here is the evidence, the citation, the coverage."

**Who reads it.** Primarily **Internal mode** (Moonfare sales / product / IC) as one of the aggregate-only consoles (Foundation Part 8). It is also reachable in **Investor — analytical** mode (the "Managers" rail entry) as an educational, read-only lens — there is no per-investor data here, so it is safe in any mode. It is **never** per-identifiable-client. Advisor mode shows it read-only too.

**The seven user questions this surface must answer (each maps to a backend eval id and a region of the layout):**

| # | User question | Backend eval | Where it surfaces in S18 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "What do top {strategy} managers consistently focus on?" | `shared_dna(strategy)` | Shared-DNA list (§3c) + industry-focus map focus sectors (§3d) |
| Q2 | "What do leading {strategy} managers avoid?" | `avoidance_patterns(strategy)` | Avoid list (§3c) + industry-focus map avoided sectors (§3d) |
| Q3 | "What do the top three {strategy} managers have in common?" | `top_n_commonality(strategy, n=3)` | Top-N commonality strip under the ranking (§3b) |
| Q4 | "Which team structures correlate with top outcomes?" | `team_blueprint` (RAG, cited) | Team-stability blueprint with citation chips (§3e) |
| Q5 | "Are the top players converging on the same subsegments?" | `convergence(strategy)` | Convergence readout on the industry-focus map (§3d) |
| Q6 | "What is the industry-focus map of top vs rest?" | `industry_focus_map` (coverage-bound) | Industry-focus map (§3d) |
| Q7 | "Rank the managers in {strategy} by vintage-cohorted performance" | `rank_managers(strategy)` | The ranking table (§3b) |

**What the surface must never do.** It must never rank by a raw multiple or raw IRR; never show a manager's raw mean TVPI styled as the rank; never phrase a pattern as advice ("you should buy this manager") — patterns are descriptive observations ("the top quartile concentrates {x}pp more in {sector}"); never show a team fact without a citation chip; never imply 100% look-through coverage on the industry map; never renormalize a coverage-bound vector onto the uncovered slice; never display a named fund with `<10` feeder investors or a named company with `<10` holders (k-anon).

---

### §2 Entry & nav

**Rail entry.** "Managers" in the global left rail (Foundation Part 3, between "Missed" and "Macro"). Selecting it is an instant, full-screen, no-scroll Detail switch; the global frame (ProfileBar top, rail left, Ask Alpha float) persists.

**Frame deltas specific to S18 (this is the important part).** Because S18 aggregates over managers, not investors:
- The **left cohort builder is replaced by a Strategy Selector rail** (§3a) for this surface only. There is no peer cohort to build, no within-dim/across-dim set-algebra, no live `n=312` readout, no "5,500 total investors" line, and **no GatedState** ("paid viewers only" never appears on S18). The cohort builder collapses to the slim strategy rail; the Follower controls that normally live in that panel are hidden on S18.
- The **ProfileBar** stays mounted but its chips (age, wealth, target %, risk, horizon) **do not drive anything** on S18 and do not re-render it — the surface ignores `investor_id`. Show the ProfileBar in its normal collapsed state; do not show SyntheticTags on profile chips here because they are inert. (In Internal mode the ProfileBar's "You" slot reads "Internal — aggregate view.")
- The **ComparisonSelector** (Peers/Platform/Model/endowments) is **not used** on S18. The only comparison axis here is **Top quartile vs Rest**, which is intrinsic to the data and rendered side-by-side, not behind a selector.

**Deep links in.**
- From **S7 Performance** or a consistency view: "see what top {strategy} managers do" → opens S18 with `strategy` preset.
- From **S2 Strategy deep-dive**: clicking a strategy chip can offer "how do top managers in {strategy} construct?" → S18 with that strategy.
- From **Ask Alpha**: any of Q1–Q7 routes here with the strategy resolved from the question, and the relevant region scroll-highlighted (e.g. Q4 highlights the team blueprint).

**Deep links out.**
- Click a manager row → **Manager Detail panel** (§6) — a within-surface drawer, not a route change.
- Click a citation chip → **DD snippet popover** (§6) — the quote + doc/page/section.
- The Strategy Selector is a query-param change (`?strategy=`), shareable as a URL.

**PDF export** (Foundation `PDFExportButton`) is present, top-right of the stage, and exports the current strategy view as a clean board-ready PDF including the ranking, the DNA lists, the industry map, and the team blueprint with citations rendered as footnotes.

---

### §3 Layout

Desktop ≥1280px. A persistent **Strategy Selector rail** on the left (replacing the cohort builder), then a full-screen, no-scroll Detail stage that is a **2-row composition**: the top row is the ranking + commonality; the bottom row is a 2-up of the industry-focus map (with convergence) and the team-stability blueprint. The shared-DNA / avoid lists sit as a slim band between the ranking and the bottom row. No analytical region requires vertical scrolling at ≥1280px; the team blueprint is the one region allowed an internal scroll if a manager has many claims (it is a list, not a chart).

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ProfileBar (collapsed; inert on S18)                          [light/dark][mode][PDF]│
├────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ STRATEGY   │  Manager Patterns — Mid-Cap Buyout                                       │
│ SELECTOR   │  Top managers, ranked by how they beat their own vintage cohort.         │
│ (left rail)│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│ ○ All      │  │ §3b  RANKING TABLE (vintage-cohorted)        [skill z | not raw IRR] ││
│ ● Mid-Cap  │  │  rank · manager · n mature · skill z (bar) · pct · TOP · consistency ││
│   Buyout   │  │  ── top-quartile band shaded; rest below; unrankable footnote ───── ││
│ ○ Large-Cap│  │  Top-N commonality strip: "Apex · Meridian · Cobalt share: Mid · …"  ││
│ ○ Growth   │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│ ○ Infra    │  ┌── §3c  SHARED DNA ─────────────┐ ┌── AVOID ──────────────────────────┐│
│ ○ Venture  │  │ ▲ more concentrated (HHI 0.31  │ │ ✕ small-cap deals (0% vs 40%)     ││
│ ○ Private  │  │   vs 0.19) ·71%cov             │ │ ▼ Consumer −9pp · Other −6pp      ││
│   Credit   │  │ ▼ syndicate less (9 vs 16)·68% │ │   Energy −4pp                     ││
│ ○ …        │  │ ▼ realize faster (4.0 vs 5.6y) │ └───────────────────────────────────┘│
│            │  └────────────────────────────────┘                                      │
│            │  ┌── §3d  INDUSTRY-FOCUS MAP ──────┐ ┌── §3e  TEAM BLUEPRINT ────────────┐│
│            │  │ Top vs Rest sector bars        │ │ Apex Capital   [DD_PACK ▸p9]      ││
│            │  │ Industrials +12 ▲ Health +9 ▲  │ │  "stable senior bench; key-man…"  ││
│            │  │ Tech +4 ▲   Consumer −9 ▼      │ │  team 14 [cited ▸p9]              ││
│            │  │ coverage 66% · uncovered 29/34 │ │ Meridian       [MGR_EVAL ▸p4]     ││
│            │  │ Converging ✓ cosine 0.86 →     │ │ Cobalt — team claim omitted (no   ││
│            │  │ converged on: Industrials      │ │   DD docs)                         ││
│            │  └────────────────────────────────┘ │ Q4: 2 of 3 managers have team docs││
│            │                                      └───────────────────────────────────┘│
└────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### §3a — Strategy Selector (left rail; replaces the cohort builder)

A vertical single-select list of the **9 strategies** from the taxonomy (`reference_kv.taxonomies`) plus an **"All managers"** option at top (= `strategy` omitted → ranks across all managers). Each option is a radio-style row; the selected one is filled with `--brand-tint-1` and a left brand bar. The rail is fixed when expanded and collapses to icons + tooltips at the 768–1279 breakpoint.

- Strategy labels render from the canonical 9-bucket taxonomy so they exactly match every other surface's strategy colour mapping (Foundation fixed strategy→colour map).
- A small count chip on each row shows `n_rankable` for that strategy when known (e.g. "Mid-Cap Buyout · 8"), greyed to "—" until the payload for that strategy is fetched. This is a navigational hint, not an analytical number, so it carries no badge.
- An "unknown strategy" cannot be selected from this rail (the rail is built from the taxonomy), so the `404` path (§7.7) is only reachable via a hand-edited URL; if it occurs, the rail highlights nothing and the stage shows the error state.
- Below the strategy list, a persistent **method footnote** (always visible, not a popover): *"Ranked by vintage-cohorted skill — how each manager's funds beat their own vintage's cohort, not by raw returns."* This is the surface-level honesty anchor for G3.

#### §3b — Ranking table (vintage-cohorted) + Top-N commonality strip

The hero region. A dense, mono-figure table (tabular numerals, Foundation Part 2.3) with the top quartile visually banded.

**Columns (left→right):**
1. **Rank** — integer; the top-quartile rows get a subtle `--brand-tint-3` row fill and a thin left brand bar; the rest are on `--surface`.
2. **Manager** — golden-record canonical name; clickable → Manager Detail (§6). If a row references a named *fund* that fails k-anon it is masked to "a {strategy} fund" — but the *manager* name itself is never masked (managers are the grain, not the privacy unit).
3. **n mature** — count of mature funds (`vintage_year ≤ 2022`) backing the score; a tiny "the score is built from N funds" tooltip.
4. **Skill (z)** — a **horizontal divergence micro-bar** per row, diverging from a centre 0 line: `--over`/▲ for positive z, `--under`/▼ for negative z, with the numeric z inline at the bar end (`+0.45`, `−0.41`). This is the sort key and the visual emphasis. The column header reads **"Skill (like-vintage z)"** with a persistent sub-label "not raw IRR / not raw TVPI." Each z value is a provenance chip → ProvenancePopover (method: "mean like-vintage z of net TVPI over mature funds (≤2022)"; n = n_mature; snapshot_id; significant flag).
5. **Percentile** — `100.0`, `87.5`, …; the cut at `≥75` is marked with a hairline rule across the table between the last top-quartile row and the first "rest" row, labelled "top-quartile cut · ≥75th pct."
6. **TOP** — a pill ("Top quartile") on `is_top` rows only; absent otherwise (never a "rest" badge — avoid implying failure).
7. **Consistency** — the T2-7 like-vintage consistency value (0–1), rendered as a small filled meter. This lets the reader distinguish a *reliable* top manager (high z **and** high consistency) from a *lucky* one (high z, low consistency). Null consistency renders "—" (e.g. a single-mature-fund manager); never zero-fill.
8. **Raw mean TVPI** — **deliberately de-emphasized**: muted, smaller, hover note "raw multiple — shown for transparency; not the rank." It exists only so a skeptical reader can confirm the rank is *not* the raw multiple (worked example: Cobalt 1.55× ranks above lower-z managers whose raw could be higher — z, not raw, decides). Never sortable; header not clickable.

**Unrankable managers** (no mature fund in the strategy) render in a collapsed footnote row beneath the table: *"2 managers not yet rankable — no mature fund in this strategy: Vell, Orsk"* with a "why?" link → "A manager needs at least one fund from vintage 2022 or earlier to be ranked on vintage-cohorted skill." They are counted in `n_unrankable` and **never** assigned a number or placed in the ranking body.

**Top-N commonality strip** sits directly under the table: a single horizontal band reading the top-3 manager names followed by what they share, e.g. *"Apex · Meridian · Cobalt share: Mid-cap deals · Industrials top sector · ~4.2y hold (cv 0.10)."* Each shared item is a chip; the numeric ones (hold years, cv) are provenance chips. If fewer than 3 top managers exist, it falls back to the available top managers and labels itself "top {k}."

#### §3c — Shared-DNA list + Avoid list

A slim two-column band between the ranking and the bottom row. Both are **lists, not charts** — they are the verbal distillation of the numeric differences, each line carrying its evidence inline.

- **Shared DNA** (left, "What the top quartile does"): one line per *significant, top-tight* feature, ranked by |delta|. Each line = a direction glyph (▲ "more"/▼ "less"/"faster"), a plain-language label, the top-vs-rest evidence in parentheses, and a coverage chip where the feature is look-through-derived. Examples (worked snapshot):
  - "▲ Runs a more concentrated sector book — HHI 0.31 vs 0.19 · 71% coverage"
  - "▼ Syndicates less — 9 vs 16 companies per feeder · 68% coverage"
  - "▼ Realizes faster — 4.0 vs 5.6 years to first material distribution"
  - "▲ More consistent — 0.73 vs 0.50 (like-vintage consistency)"
  Each numeric is a provenance chip; each look-through feature shows a CoverageBadge; **non-significant features do not appear here** (they fall to the Manager Detail's full feature table, greyed).
- **Avoid** (right, "What the top quartile avoids"): the categorical deal-type avoidance plus the industry-axis under-weights.
  - Categorical (from `cap_bucket_mix`): "✕ Small-cap deals — 0% of top managers vs 40% of the rest." The share split (top% vs rest%) is the evidence and is shown inline; if the top group's mass in a category is `0` it reads "avoid," if merely low it reads "under-use."
  - Industry axis (from `top_avoided_sectors`): the three most under-weighted sectors with their negative delta: "▼ Consumer −9pp · Other −6pp · Energy −4pp," each a provenance chip, the whole row carrying the map's coverage chip.

Both lists carry a single shared caption beneath: *"Descriptive patterns, not advice — what the data shows the leaders doing, vs everyone else in this strategy."* (Foundation rule: no advice phrasing.)

#### §3d — Industry-focus map (from look-through, coverage-bound) + Convergence

The bottom-left card. A **grouped horizontal bar set** (Foundation approved chart: grouped bars; **not** a radar, **not** a Sankey), one pair of bars per sector — **Top** (filled brand) and **Rest** (muted) — across the 9 sectors, with **% inline on every bar** and the **top−rest delta** as a small ▲/▼ chip at the row end. Sectors are sorted by descending top−rest delta so the top group's *focus* sectors float to the top and its *avoided* sectors sink to the bottom (the same ordering the avoid list reads).

- **CoverageBadge is the headline of this card** (Foundation rule: low coverage is unmissable): a prominent chip "Look-through covers 66% of these managers' NAV" = `min(top, rest)` coverage. Below it, both uncovered figures are shown explicitly: "uncovered: top 29% · rest 34%." The bars are **never renormalized** onto the uncovered slice — the covered slice sums to ~100% and the uncovered % sits beside it, not folded in (G4).
- A 1–2 sentence CaptionBlock: *"Top Mid-Cap Buyout managers tilt toward Industrials (+12pp) and Healthcare (+9pp) and away from Consumer (−9pp) — measured across the 66% of NAV we can see through."*
- **Convergence readout** docked at the bottom of the card: a single line driven by `convergence`: a check/cross glyph + "Converging" / "Not converging" / "Not enough look-through to tell," the `mean_pairwise_cosine` value as a provenance chip, and the `converged_subsegments` rendered as sector chips: "Converging ✓ · cosine 0.86 · converged on: Industrials." The method is in the chip's popover ("mean pairwise cosine of the top managers' look-through sector vectors; converged subsegment = centroid ≥15% & cross-manager sd ≤8pp"). When `converging` is `null` (`<2` top managers with look-through) the line reads "Not enough look-through coverage to assess convergence" and the cosine chip is omitted, not zeroed.

#### §3e — Team-stability blueprint (DD-doc-cited)

The bottom-right card. A **list of managers**, each a sub-block; this is the qualitative half and the strictest on the citation contract.

- **Per manager with available docs:** the manager name, then each cited **claim** as a short paraphrase line followed by a **citation chip** (Foundation citation-chip pattern). The chip face reads the doc type + page: `DD_PACK ▸ p9` or `MGR_EVAL ▸ p4`. Clicking it opens the DD snippet popover (§6) with the verbatim quote, `section_path`, `effective_date`, and `sim`. Slots render in fixed order: team_structure → key_man_stability. A **team-size figure** ("Team of 14") renders as its own line **only when** `team_size` is present, with its own citation chip and the parsed headcount traceable to the quote (G7b — the number "14" appears in the quoted text, which the popover shows).
- **Per manager without docs:** an explicit **omission line** — "Cobalt — team claim omitted: no DD or manager-eval documents indexed for this manager." This is rendered in `--muted` with a small info glyph; it is **not** blank and **not** a fabricated fact. The manager still appears in the ranking and contributes its structural features (sector, deal size, hold, syndication) elsewhere — only its team claim is withheld.
- **Q4 evidence-base footer** (always present): "Team structures computed over {n_managers_with_team_docs} of {n_top + n_rest} managers with indexed DD docs" + the `team_correlation` line where computable: "Top managers' teams average 14 professionals; rest not computable (fewer than 2 with team docs)." When `team_correlation.delta` is null, show the reason verbatim ("<2 rest managers with team docs") rather than a blank delta. This footer is what stops a 2-manager signal from reading as universe truth.
- **Confidentiality:** `audience="LP"` (default) means `INTERNAL_ONLY` chunks never appear; in **Internal mode** the surface may request `audience=INTERNAL` and an "internal DD notes included" tag appears on the card header so the viewer knows the corpus scope changed.

#### §3f — Responsive

- **≥1280px:** the layout above; strategy rail fixed-left, ranking full-width top, DNA/avoid band, then the 2-up of map + blueprint. No vertical scroll on the stage.
- **768–1279px:** strategy rail collapses to an icon strip (or a top dropdown "Strategy: Mid-Cap Buyout ▾"); the stage stacks to one column in this order: ranking (table may scroll its body internally, headers sticky), Top-N strip, DNA list, avoid list, industry-focus map, team blueprint. Grouped bars reflow but never require horizontal scroll. The ranking's "Raw mean TVPI" column is the first to drop (moved into the per-row provenance popover) to keep the core columns legible.
- **<768px (mobile, internal-on-the-go / advisor):** single column, full stack; the strategy selector becomes a sheet picker; the ranking table becomes a card-per-manager list (rank · manager · skill-z bar · TOP pill · consistency meter), with the de-emphasized raw TVPI tucked into the card's expand; the industry-focus map shows the top 5 sectors by |delta| with a "show all 9" expander; the team blueprint is a stacked list with the omission lines preserved. The CoverageBadge stays pinned at the top of the map card on every breakpoint.

---

### §4 Components & charts

All composed from the Foundation component library (Part 2.6); S18 adds no new primitives, it parameterizes existing ones. Encodings are locked to the Foundation chart language (Part 2.5) — approved charts only.

**Ranking table (`RankingTable`, S18 instance of a data table).**
- Rows = managers; the **skill-z column is a per-row ZChart micro-bar** (divergence from 0, `--over`/`--under` with ▲/▼ + inline mono value). This is the only place the divergence encoding appears at row scale; it is intentionally the visual sort key.
- Top-quartile rows banded `--brand-tint-3`; the ≥75 cut is a labelled hairline rule. Consistency = a small `BarRow`-style filled meter (0–1). Raw-mean-TVPI = muted mono text, no chart.
- Sort is **fixed** (skill_z desc, n_mature desc, manager asc — deterministic, matches the backend sort); the table is **not** user-sortable, because re-sorting by raw TVPI would defeat G3. A locked-sort affordance (a small lock glyph on the header) explains "ranked by vintage-cohorted skill — sort is fixed."
- Each numeric cell embeds ConfidenceBadge (n=n_mature) + ProvenancePopover (Foundation rule: no bare numbers).

**Top-N commonality strip (`CommonalityStrip`).** A single-row chip band; categorical shared values (cap bucket, top sector) as plain chips, numeric shared values (hold years + cv) as provenance chips. Derived from `top_n_commonality.common`.

**Shared-DNA list & Avoid list (`PatternList` ×2).** Plain styled lists (not charts). Each item: glyph + label + inline evidence + optional CoverageBadge. The shared-DNA items are sourced from `shared_dna.features` filtered to `significant:true`, ordered by |delta|; the avoid items from `avoidance.cap_bucket_mix.avoided_by_top` (categorical) and `avoidance.top_avoided_sectors` (industry). Non-significant features are excluded here and surfaced only in Manager Detail / a "view all features" table, greyed (Foundation: sub-CI deltas render greyed / "not significant").

**Industry-focus map (`GroupedBars`, S18 instance).** Grouped horizontal bars, two series (Top filled, Rest muted) per sector, 9 sectors, sorted by top−rest delta desc; % inline on each bar; a ▲/▼ delta chip per sector row; fixed sector→colour is **not** applied to the two series (Top/Rest are the series, distinguished by fill weight + a two-item legend), but each sector label uses the canonical 9-sector naming. CoverageBadge pinned as the card headline; `uncovered_pct_top`/`uncovered_pct_rest` shown beneath. CaptionBlock under the chart. The convergence readout is a sub-component (`ConvergenceReadout`) docked at the card foot.

**Team blueprint (`TeamBlueprint` + `CitationChip`).** A list of manager sub-blocks.
