**What this surface is NOT.** It is not a screener (that is S13 Funds). It is not the gap analysis (S14 What's next). It is not the liquidity forecast (S11). It does not let the user transact — the only terminal action anywhere on this surface is **"Contact your sales associate."** S20 deep-links *into* those measuring surfaces so the user can verify each link of the chain, but it owns none of their numbers.

---

### §2 — Entry & navigation

S20 has **two entry surfaces**: a prominent ribbon on the Overview, and a dedicated full-screen page in the rail. Both bind to the same `GET /api/next_best` call; the ribbon is a compact projection of the action, the page is the full chain.

**2.1 The "What should I do next?" ribbon (on S1 Overview).**
A full-width, single-row ribbon pinned **directly under the ProfileBar and above the 2×2 MetaCard grid** on the Overview. It is the highest-priority element on the landing screen after the profile, because "what do I do" is the question that brings the non-technical persona back. The ribbon is **calm, not loud** — `--surface` background with a single hairline `--brand-tint-3` left border (a 3px accent rail), never a saturated alert colour. It must not read as a notification or a warning.

Ribbon contents, left to right:
- A small lightbulb-style glyph in `--brand` (informational, never an alert triangle).
- The label **"What should I do next?"** in h2.
- A one-line projection of the current decision:
  - *Action present:* `"One option fits your portfolio: {strategy} — {fund}."` (no verb, no imperative).
  - *Do-nothing:* `"No action needed right now — {short reason}."`
  - *Cold-start:* `"Set your target allocation first."`
- A right-aligned chevron affordance **"See why →"** that deep-links to the dedicated S20 page.

The ribbon renders **all seven states in miniature** (see §7): action → the option line; do-nothing → the reason line; overcommitted → "Wait for distributions"; not-connected feeds do not change the ribbon (the named-fund core still answers); error → cached last-good line with a quiet "as of {snapshot}" suffix; cold-start → the architect routing line. The ribbon never shows a ticket size or a number bare — numbers live on the page where they can carry their badges.

**2.2 The dedicated page.**
Rail label **"What's next"** is S14 (Completion); S20 sits in the rail as **"Next best action"** in the *act* group of the rail (Part 3 grouping). Clicking the rail item, or the ribbon's "See why →", or any Ask Alpha answer chip for "what should I do," opens the full S20 page in the centre stage — full-screen, no scroll, the frame (ProfileBar, CohortBuilder, Ask Alpha, PDF export) persisting per Part 3.

**2.3 Inbound deep-links.** Ask Alpha's suggested-prompt chip "What should I do next?" (Part 5) opens this page. The Advisor cockpit's RMQueueRow (Part 7) — each row carries a client's Next-Best-Action trigger reason — opens this page inside the impersonated client's copilot (with the "acting as {client}" audit banner). The Follower push (Part 8), when it cites a gap, deep-links here.

**2.4 Outbound deep-links (the chain is navigable).** Every contributing-engine reference on this page is a link, not just a label. The gap reference opens S14 / S2; the liquidity reference opens S11; the vintage-diversification reference opens S5/S10; the suitability reference opens S16 Why-Not; the fund itself opens S13. This is the surface's defining interaction (§6): the rationale is not a paragraph, it is a set of doors back to the engines that produced it.

---

### §3 — Layout

Full-screen, no scroll, on the persistent frame (Part 3). The centre stage is a **single primary column with a secondary column** at desktop ≥1280px; it reflows to stacked at smaller breakpoints (§10). The page is dominated by **one** thing — the action (or the do-nothing) — because the surface's entire value is decisiveness. Ranked alternatives are deliberately subordinate.

**3.1 Desktop grid (≥1280px).**
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Next best action            as of {snapshot}        [PDF export ▾]        │  ← page header
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┤
│                                                    │                       │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐    │   CONTRIBUTING        │
│   │  THE ACTION CARD  (or DO-NOTHING CARD)     │    │   ENGINES             │
│   │  ───────────────────────────────────────  │    │   (provenance chain   │
│   │  [kind pill] Private Credit                │    │    accordion)         │
│   │  Meridian Private Credit                   │    │                       │
│   │  Indicative ticket  €43,200  [bounds ⓘ]    │    │   ▸ Gap (T2-1)        │
│   │  ───────────────────────────────────────  │    │   ▸ Liquidity (T1-7)  │
│   │  RATIONALE (chains gap+liquidity+fit)      │    │   ▸ Vintage (T1-4)    │
│   │   · Fills your Private Credit underweight  │    │   ▸ Suitability (T3-2)│
│   │     (8.0 ppt, significant) →S14            │    │                       │
│   │   · Within your liquidity headroom         │    │   SIGNAL FEEDS        │
│   │     (capacity €480k) →S11                  │    │   ▸ Demand   ⌁ not    │
│   │   · Ticket sized to the sleeve gap         │    │      connected        │
│   │   · Suitability clear →S16                 │    │   ▸ Secondary ⌁ not   │
│   │  ───────────────────────────────────────  │    │      connected        │
│   │  [ConfidenceBadge] [SyntheticTag]          │    │   ▸ Pipeline ⌁ not    │
│   │  Disclaimer (info-not-advice)              │    │      connected        │
│   │  [ Contact your sales associate ]  (CTA)   │    │                       │
│   └──────────────────────────────────────────┘    │                       │
│                                                    │                       │
│   ── RECOMMENDED FUND (FundCard, Moonfare layout) ─┤                       │
│   [img] Meridian Private Credit · access-feeder    │                       │
│         [Europe] [Private Credit] · stats row · CTA│                       │
│                                                    │                       │
│   ── RANKED ALTERNATIVES ──────────────────────────┤                       │
│   2 · Helios Secondaries II   Secondaries   0.453  │                       │
│   3 · Atlas Infra Core        Infrastructure 0.453 │  oversized-for-gap ⚑  │
│                                                    │                       │
│   ── DO NOTHING ───────────────────────────────────┤                       │
│   "Staying put is also a valid option because …"   │                       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘
```

**3.2 The action card (primary, top-left).**
The single ranked move. Contents top to bottom:
1. **Kind pill** — a small pill labelling the action `kind`: `Open fund` (`named_fund`), `Secondary` (`secondary_buy`), `Wait` (`wait`), `Rebalance` (`sell_rebalance`). The pill uses `--brand-tint-2`; never a semantic over/under colour.
2. **Strategy** (caption, `--muted`) and **fund name** (h1, `--ink`).
3. **Indicative ticket** — the ticket size rendered as `€43,200` in mono/tabular, labelled **"Indicative ticket"** (never "you should invest"). An inline `ⓘ` opens a small popover showing `ticket_bounds` (`min €25,000 · capacity €480,000 · sleeve gap €43,200`) so the number is never bare. If `ticket_eur` is `null` (synthetic wealth unknown), render the `ticket_note` ("size by sleeve %") plus `sleeve_gap_eur` and a SyntheticTag — never a fabricated €.
4. **Rationale** — the chained reasons (§4.2), one row per link, each terminating in a deep-link chevron to the contributing surface.
5. **Badges row** — ConfidenceBadge, SyntheticTag if any input is synthetic, and a small "grounded · {snapshot_id}" footer.
6. **Disclaimer** — the info-not-advice line (§8), always present, never collapsible.
7. **CTA** — **"Contact your sales associate"**, the only terminal action.

**3.3 The recommended FundCard.**
Directly beneath the action card, the recommended fund renders as a full **FundCard** in the Moonfare-listing layout (Part 2.6 / S14): image right, name + access-feeder line, two badges (region + strategy), stats row (Target Fund Size / Target Net MOIC / Expected Close / Min. investment), and its own "Contact your sales associate" CTA. This gives the named fund the same premium treatment it gets in S13/S14 so the action feels concrete, not abstract. The FundCard is suppressed in every do-nothing and cold-start state (there is no recommended fund).

**3.4 Ranked alternatives (subordinate).**
A compact list below the FundCard, headed **"Other options considered."** Each row: rank index, fund name, strategy chip, and the engine `score` rendered as a ProvenancePopover-bearing chip (so even the score is not bare). Rows carrying `flag: "oversized_for_remaining_gap"` show a small `⚑` with a tooltip: "Would overshoot the remaining sleeve gap." Rows are **not** primary CTAs — clicking a row expands it inline to show its sub-scores (the same chain, smaller) and a "See this fund →" deep-link to S13. The list is deliberately quiet: it exists for the analytical persona who wants to see the field, not to dilute the single recommendation.

**3.5 The do-nothing card (replaces the action card).**
When `do_nothing: true`, the action card position is occupied by an equally-designed **do-nothing card** (§4.3) — same footprint, same calm surface, never a greyed-out or empty treatment. The FundCard and the CTA-to-act are suppressed; the disclaimer and the contributing-engine accordion remain (the chain still explains *why* nothing is the move).

**3.6 Contributing-engines column (secondary, right).**
The provenance-chain accordion (§4.4) lives in the right column at desktop. It is always present in every state except cold-start (where there is no chain to show — the surface defers to the Architect). It lists the contributing engines as expandable rows and, below them, the **signal feeds** as `NotConnectedState` sub-rows.

---

### §4 — Components & charts

S20 introduces **no new chart types** (it has no analytical chart of its own — the foundation's approved chart language, Part 2.5, is consumed only via deep-links into other surfaces). It introduces four S20-specific composite components, all built from foundation primitives.

**4.1 `ActionCard` (new composite).**
A `MetaCard`-family card (Part 2.6) specialised for the single decision. Props bind to `action.*`. Internal structure per §3.2. It embeds:
- `KindPill` — maps `action.kind` → label + glyph.
- `TicketField` — the ticket number + `ⓘ` bounds popover; respects `ticket_eur === null`.
- `RationaleChain` (4.2).
- `ConfidenceBadge` + `SyntheticTag` + grounded footer (foundation primitives).
- `DisclaimerLine` (§8).
- `PrimaryCTA` — "Contact your sales associate."

States: `loading` (calm skeleton — a card-shaped shimmer, never a spinner), `error/degraded` (cached last-good with a quiet "as of {snapshot}" banner). Hover/focus: the whole card is a focus container; the CTA and each rationale chevron are independently keyboard-focusable.

**4.2 `RationaleChain` (new composite — the surface's signature).**
The rationale is **not** a free-text paragraph. It is a structured list that *visibly chains the contributing surfaces*: **gap + liquidity + fit (+ vintage, + suitability)**. Each link is one row:

```
●─ Fills your Private Credit underweight (8.0 ppt, significant)        [T2-1] →
│
●─ Within your liquidity headroom (capacity €480,000)                  [T1-7] →
│
●─ Ticket €43,200 sized to the remaining sleeve gap                    (sizing) →
│
●─ Suitability clear                                                   [T3-2] →
```

Encoding rules:
- A thin vertical connector line (`--line`) joins the link nodes top-to-bottom, so the chain reads as an assembled argument, not a bullet list.
- Each link's **number** (8.0 ppt, €480,000, €43,200) is a `ProvenancePopover` chip — clicking it shows cohort/method/snapshot for that figure, sourced from `action.provenance.provenance_chain[]`. A number in the rationale is never bare (Part 0.2).
- Each link carries an **engine tag** chip (`T2-1`, `T1-7`, `T1-4`, `T3-2`) and a **deep-link chevron** to the contributing surface (§2.4). The tag is the visible proof that the rationale references *only engine numbers* — never a free assertion.
- The text of each link is bound verbatim from `action.rationale` decomposed into its sentences, OR (preferred, more robust) reconstructed from `action.provenance.provenance_chain[]` so each row maps 1:1 to an engine field. The chain array is the source of truth for which engines contributed; the prose is the human-readable skin over it.
- **No imperative verbs.** Links are stated as facts about fit ("Fills your underweight…", "Within your headroom…"), never as instructions ("Buy…", "You should…"). See §8.

**4.3 `DoNothingCard` (new composite — first-class state).**
Same footprint as the ActionCard. Bound to the do-nothing body. Structure:
- Header: **"No action needed right now"** (h1) with a calm check-style glyph in `--muted` (never a red stop).
- A single clear reason line, bound to `do_nothing_reason` → human copy (§8):
  - `overcommitted` → "Your committed capital exceeds your near-term liquidity headroom." + the `crossover_quarter` chip ("liquidity is expected to recover around **2027 Q2**") when present, deep-linking to S11.
  - `aligned_to_model` → "Your allocation already matches your model within statistical noise."
  - `no_actionable_candidate` → "The options that fit your gaps aren't suitable for you right now." + the vetoed list (4.5).
- The contributing-engine accordion stays (the chain that produced "nothing" is still inspectable: which gate fired, what capacity number, which suitability veto).
- Disclaimer line (info-not-advice) stays.
- **No** primary CTA-to-act and **no** FundCard. A quiet secondary affordance "See your liquidity →" (overcommitted) or "Review your allocation →" (aligned) deep-links to the relevant surface.

**4.4 `ProvenanceChainAccordion` (new composite — which engines contributed).**
The right-column accordion. One expandable row per contributing engine, bound from `action.provenance.provenance_chain[]` (and from the gate / vetoed blocks). Each collapsed row: engine label + the one field it contributed + that field's value as a provenance chip. Expanded row: the method note for that engine's contribution and a "Open {surface} →" deep-link.

```
CONTRIBUTING ENGINES
▾ Gap — Completion                          T2-1
    gap_closure_ppt = 8.0  (significant)    [provenance ⓘ]
    "Largest significant underweight this option fills."   Open S14 →
▸ Liquidity — Cashflow model                T1-7
    investable_capacity_eur = €480,000      [provenance ⓘ]
▸ Vintage diversification — Pacing          T1-4
    vintage_div_benefit = 0.088             [provenance ⓘ]
▸ Suitability — Why-not                     T3-2
    binding_constraint = none               [provenance ⓘ]
```

The accordion header carries a one-line method caption bound from `action.provenance.method` — the exact scoring formula ("weighted candidate score: 0.40·gap_closure + 0.25·fit + 0.15·vintage_div + 0.20·liquidity − 0.20·demand_risk"), in caption type, so the *how-it-was-computed* is always one glance away. A `weights_version` chip is shown next to it.

**4.5 Signal-feed sub-rows (`NotConnectedState`).**
Below the contributing engines, the three signal feeds render as `NotConnectedState` sub-rows (foundation primitive — dashed outline, `--not-connected`), bound from `signal_feeds.{demand,secondary,pipeline}`. Each: a dashed mini-row labelled with the feed and the copy "Live when the {feed} connects." These explain *why* the secondary-buy, wait, and rebalance options are absent from the ranked list — they are not errors, they are unwired inputs. The demand penalty's inertness (weight redistributes to positive terms while `demand_risk = 0.0`) is noted in the demand sub-row's tooltip: "Allocation-pressure signal not yet connected; not affecting the ranking."

**4.6 `VetoedList` (within DoNothingCard / accordion).**
When `vetoed[]` is populated, render a small list under the do-nothing reason: each `{fund_id, veto, would_change}` as a row — fund name (if k-anon allows; else bucket label), the binding constraint stated plainly, and the `would_change` threshold ("becomes suitable when {threshold}"), each deep-linking to S16 Why-Not. This is the honest face of "no actionable candidate": the surface shows what it considered and why each was set aside.

**4.7 Foundation components reused (do not redefine).**
`FundCard` (recommended fund), `ProvenancePopover` (every number), `ConfidenceBadge`, `SyntheticTag`, `NotConnectedState` (signal feeds), `CaptionBlock` (method caption), `PDFExportButton` (page header), `RelaxBanner` (if the underlying cohort relaxed), `GatedState` (if a sub-fund is paid-only). The page header's PDF export produces the clean PowerPoint-style export of the decision + chain.

---

### §5 — Data bindings

All bindings are to a single call: **`GET /api/next_best?investor_id={str?}`**. One call drives the entire surface; no element fetches independently. `investor_id` is optional (defaults to the bound demo investor); in Advisor-impersonation mode it carries the client's id.

**5.1 Action-present body → elements.**

| UI element | ← API field |
|---|---|
| KindPill label | `action.kind` |
| Fund name (h1) | `action.fund` |
| Strategy (caption) | `action.strategy` |
| TicketField value | `action.ticket_eur` (render `null` → `ticket_note` + `sleeve_gap_eur` + SyntheticTag) |
| Ticket bounds popover | `action.ticket_bounds.{min, capacity, sleeve_gap}` |
| RationaleChain prose | `action.rationale` (decomposed) |
| RationaleChain links (source of truth) | `action.provenance.provenance_chain[]` `{engine, field, value}` |
| Method caption | `action.provenance.method` |
| Weights-version chip | `action.provenance.weights_version` |
| SyntheticTag | `action.provenance.synthetic === true` |
| Grounded footer / "as of" | `action.provenance.snapshot_id` (and top-level `snapshot_id`) |
| ConfidenceBadge | derived from the contributing gap's significance (the gap link is only present when significant per G2) |
| Disclaimer | `action.disclaimer` |
| Score chips (action + alternatives) | `action.score`, `ranked[].score` |
| Sub-score breakdown (expanded rows) | `action.subscores.{gap_closure,fit,vintage_div,liquidity,demand_risk}` |
| Recommended FundCard | `action.fund_id` → hydrate via `GET /api/fund/{id}` for the listing fields (image, target size, MOIC, close, min) |
| Ranked alternatives list | `ranked[]` `{kind, fund_id, fund, strategy, score, flag?}` |
| Oversized flag | `ranked[].flag === "oversized_for_remaining_gap"` |

**5.2 Contributing-engine accordion → fields.**
Each accordion row binds to one entry of `action.provenance.provenance_chain[]`:
- Gap (T2-1) ← `{engine:"T2-1 completion", field:"gap_closure_ppt", value}`.
- Liquidity (T1-7) ← `{engine:"T1-7 cashflow_model", field:"investable_capacity_eur", value}` — **`liquidity_capacity_eur` is shown explicitly** here and again inline in the rationale's liquidity link. It is the surface's most important secondary number (it is what gates the whole decision).
- Vintage (T1-4) ← `{engine:"T1-4 pacing", field:"vintage_div_benefit", value}`.
- Suitability (T3-2) ← `{engine:"T3-2 why_not", field:"binding_constraint", value}` (null = "clear").
Gate block ← `gate.{overcommitment_flag, investable_capacity_eur}`.

**5.3 Signal feeds → NotConnectedState sub-rows.**
`signal_feeds.demand`, `signal_feeds.secondary`, `signal_feeds.pipeline` each `{status, feed}`. When `status === "not_connected"`, render the dashed sub-row. **Secondary and demand inputs render as NotConnectedState sub-rows** (per the brief): the secondary feed governs the secondary-buy and rebalance branches; the demand feed governs the (inert) penalty term; the pipeline feed governs the wait branch. When a feed flips to live, the corresponding candidate kinds appear in `ranked[]` with no frontend change — the sub-row simply disappears and the candidate rows populate.

**5.4 Do-nothing body → elements.**

| UI element | ← API field |
|---|---|
| DoNothingCard header | static "No action needed right now" |
| Reason line | `do_nothing_reason` → copy map (§8) |
| Crossover chip | `crossover_quarter` (overcommitted; null → omit) |
| Gate block | `gate.{overcommitment_flag, investable_capacity_eur}` |
| VetoedList | `vetoed[]` `{fund_id, veto, would_change}` |
| Disclaimer | `disclaimer` |

**5.5 Cold-start body.** `cold_start === true` → render the Architect-routing state (§7.2), binding `defer_to` ("T1-1 Architect") to the CTA target (S9).

**5.6 Error handling.** `404 {"error":"unknown investor"}` → the error state (§7.7). The contract guarantees **no 500 on missing data** — degraded bodies always return 200, so the frontend treats any non-200 as the hard-error path only.

---

### §6 — Interactions

S20 is a low-interaction surface by design — it is meant to be *read* and acted on, not manipulated. The interactions it does have all serve transparency.

**6.1 Expand the provenance chain.** Clicking any contributing-engine row in the right-column accordion expands it to show the method note + value + "Open {surface} →" link. Clicking the method-caption "ⓘ" expands a small panel showing the full scoring formula with the live sub-scores filled in (`action.subscores`) so the user can see how the weights produced the `score`. Keyboard: each accordion header is a `button[aria-expanded]`; Enter/Space toggles.

**6.2 Open a contributing surface (the chain is navigable).** Every engine tag / chevron in the RationaleChain and every "Open {surface} →" in the accordion deep-links to the owning surface, carrying the same `investor_id` and the relevant strategy/fund context so the user lands on the exact view that produced the number:
- Gap link → S14 What's next (or S2 with the strategy pre-selected).
- Liquidity link → S11 Liquidity & Cashflow.
- Vintage link → S5 Vintage / S10 Pacing.
- Suitability link → S16 Why-Not (with `fund_id`).
- Fund name / FundCard → S13 Funds (with the fund pre-loaded).
- Ranked-alternative "See this fund →" → S13.
Navigation is instant, full-screen, frame-persistent (Part 3). A small back affordance returns to S20.

**6.3 Provenance chips.** Every number — ticket, capacity, gap ppt, vintage benefit, each `score` — is a `ProvenancePopover` chip; clicking shows cohort/n/method/snapshot (foundation behaviour). This is non-optional: a number never renders without its chip.

**6.4 Ticket bounds.** The `ⓘ` next to the ticket opens the bounds popover (`min · capacity · sleeve gap`). This is how the user verifies the ticket is constrained to `[fund.min_eur, capacity]` — the engine's core acceptance guarantee made visible.

**6.5 Expand a ranked alternative.** Clicking an alternative row expands it inline to show its sub-scores and the "See this fund →" link. Only one expands at a time; it does not become a competing CTA.

**6.6 The CTA — "Contact your sales associate."** The only terminal action on the surface. It does **not** execute anything (the product never transacts — Part 1, and the foundation's premium-trust posture). It opens the contact affordance (mailto / RM contact sheet, per the product's contact convention). Present on the action card and on the recommended FundCard; absent on do-nothing and cold-start.

**6.7 Cohort changes.** If the user changes the cohort in the left rail, the surface re-resolves `/api/next_best` and re-renders the affected pieces (the decision can change if the model/peer reference shifts). Re-render is calm (skeleton on the action card only), not a full-page reload (Part 10 performance rule).

**6.8 Ask Alpha.** Asking "what should I do next?" in the pane returns the same decision as an inline answer with provenance chips; the answer carries a "See the full reasoning →" chip that opens this page. The pane and the page never disagree (one engine, one truth).

---

### §7 — The seven states

Every state renders fully and calmly (Part 9). S20's defining requirement: **the do-nothing state is a first-class outcome, not an empty or error screen**, and signal-gated inputs render as `not_connected` sub-rows, never as failures.

**7.1 Happy (action present).** `do_nothing: false`, `action` populated. The ActionCard + RationaleChain + recommended FundCard + ranked alternatives + the full contributing-engine accordion, with signal feeds shown as `not_connected` sub-rows (they do not block the named-fund core). Ticket within `[min, capacity]`. The ribbon on Overview shows the one-line option projection. This is the §3 worked layout: *Meridian Private Credit · €43,200 · fills 8.0 ppt PC underweight · within €480k headroom · suitability clear.*

**7.2 Cold-start (`cold_start: true`, `n_funds == 0`).** There is no portfolio, so there is no gap to score and no action to rank. The page does **not** show an empty action card. Instead it shows a calm routing card: header "Start with your target allocation," body "Once you set your target, this page will show your single best next move," and a primary CTA **"Open the Allocation Architect →"** (S9), bound to `defer_to`. The ribbon on Overview reads "Set your target allocation first." No FundCard, no chain, no do-nothing — this is the architect hand-off (the FOMO-safe new-investor path, Part 9 Welcome).

**7.3 No significant gap (`do_nothing_reason: "aligned_to_model"`).** The DoNothingCard with reason "Your allocation already matches your model within statistical noise." This is the *positive* do-nothing — frame it as alignment, not absence. A quiet "Review your allocation →" link to S1/S14. The contributing accordion shows the gap engine returned no significant underweight (the G2 filter is the visible explanation). Ribbon: "No action needed right now — your allocation matches your model."

**7.4 Overcommitted (`do_nothing_reason: "overcommitted"`, the headline gate).** The most important do-nothing. The liquidity gate fired: committed capital exceeds near-term headroom (`gate.investable_capacity_eur <= 0` or `overcommitment_flag`). The DoNothingCard reason: "Your committed capital exceeds your near-term liquidity headroom." The `crossover_quarter` chip ("liquidity is expected to recover around **2027 Q2**") deep-links to S11. **No buy-side option may appear** — the ranked list is empty (or contains only a `sell_rebalance` if the secondary feed is live). The accordion's liquidity row shows the negative/zero capacity number as the gate proof. Ribbon: "No action needed right now — wait for distributions." This state must never be reachable while showing a fund to buy; the gate's hard precedence over score is the surface's safety property.

**7.5 All candidates vetoed (`do_nothing_reason: "no_actionable_candidate"`).** Suitability (T3-2) vetoed every fitting fund. The DoNothingCard reason: "The options that fit your gaps aren't suitable for you right now." The VetoedList (4.6) shows each vetoed fund's binding constraint + `would_change` threshold, deep-linking to S16. This is honest: it shows the field was considered and set aside on suitability, not silence.

**7.6 Signal-gated inputs not_connected (overlaps every state).** `signal_feeds.{demand,secondary,pipeline}.status == "not_connected"` → the three `NotConnectedState` sub-rows render in the accordion. The secondary-buy, wait, and rebalance candidate kinds are therefore absent from `ranked[]` — the sub-rows are the visible reason. Q3 "now vs wait" degrades to a quiet line in the rationale or accordion: "No better visible option right now (pipeline feed not connected)." The demand penalty's inertness is noted in its sub-row tooltip. These render as designed dashed previews, **never as errors** — the named-fund core always answers.

**7.7 Error / degraded.** Hard error is only `404 {"error":"unknown investor"}` → a calm "We couldn't find that investor" card with no fabricated content. For a stale/degraded engine, the contract returns a degraded 200 (do-nothing / not_connected), so the frontend shows the last-good decision with a quiet "as of {snapshot_id}" banner in the header — never a blank screen and never a fabricated number (Part 9 state 7).

---

### §8 — Copy deck

The tone constraint is absolute: **information, not advice. Never the imperative "you should." Never "buy," "invest now," or "we recommend you."** The rationale states facts about *fit and constraint*; it never instructs. This mirrors the engine's own no-advice gate (§6 of the backend contract: the rationale is asserted to contain none of `{"buy", "you should", "we recommend you", "invest now"}` and must contain "not investment advice"). The frontend copy honours the same rule.

**8.1 Page header.** `Next best action` · subhead `as of {snapshot_id}`.

**8.2 Overview ribbon.**
- Label: `What should I do next?`
- Action: `One option fits your portfolio: {strategy} — {fund}.`
- Do-nothing (aligned): `No action needed right now — your allocation matches your model.`
- Do-nothing (overcommitted): `No action needed right now — wait for distributions.`
- Cold-start: `Set your target allocation first.`
- Affordance: `See why →`

**8.3 Action card.**
- Kind pills: `Open fund` · `Secondary` · `Wait` · `Rebalance`.
- Ticket label: `Indicative ticket` (never "recommended amount," never "invest").
- Ticket note (null wealth): `Wealth unknown (illustrative) — sized by sleeve %.`
- Rationale links (fact-form, no verbs):
  - `Fills your {strategy} underweight ({ppt} ppt, significant).`
  - `Within your liquidity headroom (capacity {€capacity}).`
  - `Ticket {€ticket} sized to the remaining sleeve gap.`
  - `Suitability clear.`
- Disclaimer (always present, verbatim from `action.disclaimer`): `Educational, not investment advice. Contact your sales associate to act.`
- CTA: `Contact your sales associate`.

**8.4 Do-nothing copy (the load-bearing copy).**
- Header: `No action needed right now`
- `aligned_to_model`: `Your allocation already matches your model within statistical noise. Staying put is a valid option.`
- `overcommitted`: `Your committed capital exceeds your near-term liquidity headroom. Waiting for distributions before committing more keeps your liquidity comfortable.` + crossover chip: `Liquidity is expected to recover around {crossover_quarter}.`
- `no_actionable_candidate`: `The options that fit your gaps aren't suitable for you right now.`
- Vetoed row: `{fund} — {binding_constraint}. Becomes suitable when {would_change}.`
- Disclaimer: `Educational, not investment advice.`

Note the do-nothing copy is deliberately *reassuring and neutral* — "staying put is a valid option," "keeps your liquidity comfortable" — never framed as failure, missed opportunity, or fault. A new investor seeing "no action needed" must not read it as "you're behind."

**8.5 Signal-feed sub-rows.**
- Demand: `Allocation-pressure signal — live when {feed} connects. Not affecting this ranking.`
- Secondary: `Secondary-market options — live when {feed} connects.`
- Pipeline: `Upcoming-pipeline options — live when {feed} connects.`

**8.6 Accordion.**
- Header method caption: bound verbatim from `action.provenance.method`.
- Engine rows: `Gap — Completion (T2-1)`, `Liquidity — Cashflow model (T1-7)`, `Vintage diversification — Pacing (T1-4)`, `Suitability — Why-not (T3-2)`.
- Suitability clear: `binding constraint: none`.

**8.7 Forbidden strings (assert in QA, case-insensitive).** `you should`, `we recommend you`, `buy`, `invest now`, `must`, `need to invest`. Required string anywhere a recommendation renders: `not investment advice`.

---

### §9 — Badge rendering

Per Part 0.2 and 2.6, no number renders bare. On S20:

- **ProvenancePopover** — on the ticket (€), `liquidity_capacity_eur` (€), `gap_closure_ppt`, `vintage_div_benefit`, every `score`, and the ticket-bounds figures. Source: `action.provenance` + per-figure entries in `provenance_chain[]`.
- **ConfidenceBadge** — attached to the gap link, since the gap is only present when CI-significant (G2). It renders the significance the gap engine guarantees ("significant") rather than a bare n±CI invented here; if the upstream gap carries n/CI, surface it.
- **SyntheticTag** — wherever `action.provenance.synthetic === true`. Specifically: the **ticket** (depends on synthetic `investable_wealth_eur` / `pm_target_pct`), the **fit** sub-score (depends on synthetic `risk_class`), and any do-nothing reason that depends on a synthetic capacity. The tag is the amber "illustrative" pill (foundation `--synthetic`); the rationale text must never assert a synthetic field as verified fact.
- **CoverageBadge** — not typically used on S20 (it owns no look-through figure); if a contributing engine's number carries coverage, surface its CoverageBadge on that link unchanged.
- **NotConnectedState (dashed `--not-connected`)** — the three signal-feed sub-rows.
- **Weights-version chip** — `action.provenance.weights_version` next to the method caption, a small neutral mono chip (so the user can see which scoring version produced the rank).
- **"grounded · {snapshot_id}" footer** — on the action/do-nothing card and on every Ask Alpha answer that mirrors this decision.
- **`oversized_for_remaining_gap` flag (`⚑`)** — on ranked-alternative rows carrying that flag, with the explanatory tooltip.

Badge precedence when stacked: SyntheticTag (amber) is always visible and never hidden behind a hover; ProvenancePopover is click-to-open; ConfidenceBadge sits inline with its number. None may be suppressed to "clean up" the layout.

---

### §10 — Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive (Part 3 breakpoints).**
- **≥1280px** — two columns: primary decision column + contributing-engines column (§3.1).
- **768–1279px** — single column: ActionCard → RationaleChain → recommended FundCard → ranked alternatives → contributing-engine accordion (which collapses to a full-width accordion beneath, signal feeds nested). CohortBuilder collapses to a drawer; Ask Alpha to a launcher (Part 3). The action card stays full-width and dominant.
- **<768px (mobile — advisor on the go)** — single column stacked, the ActionCard first and dominant, the FundCard reflowing to the foundation's mobile FundCard, the accordion fully collapsed by default (tap to expand each engine). The ProfileBar becomes a sheet. No element requires horizontal scroll. The ribbon on Overview wraps the option line to two lines if needed; "See why →" stays tappable (≥44px target).

**Accessibility (Part 10, WCAG-AA).**
- **Never colour-only.** The kind pill, do-nothing check, and crossover chip pair colour with a glyph/label. The oversized flag pairs `⚑` with a tooltip and `aria-label`. Over/under semantics do not appear on this surface, but any inherited delta keeps its sign/glyph.
- **Keyboard.** Full tab order: ribbon affordance → action card → ticket `ⓘ` → each rationale chevron → CTA → ranked-alternative rows → accordion headers → signal-feed rows. Accordions are `button[aria-expanded]`; popovers are dismissible with Esc and focus-trapped while open.
- **Screen reader.** The RationaleChain is a semantic ordered list (`<ol>`); each link's engine tag is in the accessible name ("Fills your Private Credit underweight, 8.0 percentage points, significant, source Completion engine T2-1"). The do-nothing card announces "No action needed right now" as a heading so it is never mistaken for an empty region. The ticket announces "Indicative ticket, 43,200 euros, illustrative" when synthetic.
- **View-data affordance.** Per Part 10, the contributing-engine accordion *is* the "view the data" affordance for this surface — every contributing number is reachable as text + value, not locked inside a graphic.
- **Focus visible** on every interactive element; CTA has a clear focus ring distinct from hover.
- **Motion** ≤150ms ease, accordion expand is a height transition only — no parallax, no bounce (Part 2.4). Honour `prefers-reduced-motion`.

---

### §11 — Acceptance checklist

A build of S20 is accepted when **all** of the following hold:

1. **One call drives the surface.** Every element binds to a single `GET /api/next_best`; no element fetches independently except the recommended FundCard's listing hydration via `GET /api/fund/{id}`.
2. **The do-nothing card can render** — fully, calmly, first-class — for all three reasons (`overcommitted`, `aligned_to_model`, `no_actionable_candidate`), with no empty/greyed/error treatment, no FundCard, no act-CTA, but with the chain and disclaimer intact.
3. **Overcommitted shows no buy-side option.** When `do_nothing_reason === "overcommitted"`, the ranked list contains no `named_fund`/`secondary_buy`/`wait`; the negative/zero `liquidity_capacity_eur` is shown as the gate proof; the crossover chip deep-links to S11.
4. **Tone is non-prescriptive.** No rendered copy anywhere on the surface contains `you should`, `we recommend you`, `buy`, `invest now`, `must`, or `need to invest` (case-insensitive QA assertion). The "not investment advice" disclaimer is present on every action and do-nothing card.
5. **The rationale references only engine numbers.** Every RationaleChain link maps 1:1 to an entry in `action.provenance.provenance_chain[]`, carries its engine tag (T2-1/T1-7/T1-4/T3-2), and contains no number that is not sourced from the chain. No free-text assertion of a value.
6. **The chain is navigable.** Each rationale link and each accordion engine row deep-links to its owning surface (S14/S11/S5/S10/S16) carrying `investor_id` + context; the fund deep-links to S13.
7. **No bare numbers.** Ticket, `liquidity_capacity_eur`, gap ppt, vintage benefit, and every `score` carry a ProvenancePopover chip; ticket carries its bounds `ⓘ`.
8. **Ticket integrity is visible.** The bounds popover shows `min · capacity · sleeve_gap`, demonstrating `ticket_eur ∈ [fund.min_eur, capacity]`; `ticket_eur === null` renders the sleeve-% note + SyntheticTag, never a fabricated €.
9. **Signal feeds render as NotConnectedState sub-rows** (secondary + demand + pipeline), not errors; the absence of secondary-buy/wait/rebalance candidates is explained by these sub-rows; the demand-penalty inertness is noted.
10. **SyntheticTag** appears on the ticket and on any reason depending on `provenance.synthetic === true`; no synthetic field is asserted as verified.
11. **Seven states all render.** Happy, cold-start (routes to S9 Architect, no empty action card), aligned, overcommitted, all-vetoed, signal-gated, and 404-error — each per §7, with degraded data returning a calm last-good view (never blank, never fabricated).
12. **The ribbon on Overview** renders under the ProfileBar, projects the current decision in one line for every state, is calm (not an alert), and "See why →" opens the dedicated page.
13. **CTA never transacts.** "Contact your sales associate" is the only terminal action and opens the contact affordance; it appears on action + FundCard, never on do-nothing/cold-start.
14. **A11y + responsive** per §10: keyboard-complete, screen-reader-labelled chain as `<ol>`, do-nothing announced as a heading, no horizontal scroll at any breakpoint, ≤150ms motion, `prefers-reduced-motion` honoured.
15. **Consistency with the engines.** The fund shown equals what Completion + Liquidity + Why-Not independently imply (the integrator's signature): when un-gated, un-vetoed, unique-max, `action.fund_id` matches the top gap candidate; do-nothing renders whenever `gate.overcommitment_flag`; the vetoed set matches funds Why-Not constrained. The frontend never overrides or recomputes — it presents.


---

## Surface S21 — Platform Demand

> **Frontend surface spec for Claude Design.** This is the deep build sheet for one Alpha copilot surface — **S21 · Platform Demand** (use case **T3-1**, signal-gated). It composes entirely from the design system and components defined in the **Frontend Foundation** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`) — ProfileBar, CohortBuilder, AskAlphaPane, MetaCard, HeatMap, ForecastBand, BarRow, ConfidenceBadge, ProvenancePopover, SyntheticTag, **NotConnectedState**, the 7-state model (Part 9), the chart language (Part 2.5), and the API-contract value+provenance binding (Part 0.3). Where the foundation already defines a token, component, badge or state, this spec **references it, never redefines it**.
>
> The defining property of S21: it is a **fully-designed live surface that renders in its NotConnectedState by default.** The data feed (`ALPHA_FEED_DEMAND`) is not wired, so `GET /api/demand` returns `{status:"not_connected"}` and the surface shows a dashed, greyed *preview of the exact intended layout* plus a one-line "live when the feed connects" message and a synthetic-preview toggle for demos. When the feed connects, the **same layout fills with live data — the swap is data-only, zero layout refactor.** S21 is therefore the canonical demonstration of the adapter-swap pattern: the live design and the not-connected default are the *same component tree* under two data states.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Make Moonfare's own platform demand legible and turn it into decision-support — *which open funds are filling fastest, how likely the investor is to get a full allocation in a named fund, which strategies are heating up, and how quickly comparable funds historically sold out.* It is **part platform-wide intelligence** ("which funds are hottest right now") and **part personal actionable** ("how likely am I to get my €150k into Fund X"). It is the upstream **producer for S20 · Next Best Action** — the `demand_pressure(fund_id) ∈ [0,1]` and `fill_time_estimate` signals that order and penalize NBA candidates originate here.

**The hard contract S21's frontend must respect.** The live demand book — who is currently subscribing, how fast, how oversubscribed — is **not** in the canonical export. There is nothing real to display until the feed connects. So the surface's *default and overwhelmingly-most-common* rendered state is **NotConnectedState**, and that state must read as **"designed and ready, pending a feed"** — never as an error, never as empty, never as broken. The live layout is fully specified so that connecting the feed is a pure data swap.

**No-advice / no-FOMO is structural (G-NOADVICE).** Every fill-time and allocation-odds output is framed as an *illustrative estimate with explicit assumptions*. The surface never instructs the user to subscribe, never renders a bare countdown ("28 days left!"), never manufactures scarcity ("act now or miss out"). Fill-times always render as a **band with a probability**, allocation-odds always render the **expected scale-down** alongside the odds, and every read carries the "Illustrative — the GP/platform sets final allocation" note. This is a wealth-grade tool, not a fintech scarcity nudge.

**The seven user questions this surface answers (eval ids):**
- **Q1 — "Which funds are filling fastest right now?"** → the **fund-demand heatmap** + **fill leaderboard** (open funds sorted by velocity / shortest expected time-to-fill).
- **Q2 — "How likely am I to get a full allocation in Fund X?"** → the **personal allocation-odds** panel for one named fund + named ticket.
- **Q3 — "Which strategies are currently in highest demand?"** → the **demand momentum** strip (hottest-now ranking).
- **Q4 — "How quickly did comparable funds sell out in the past?"** → the **comparable fill-history** read in the time-to-fill predictor (the survival training basis).
- **Q5 — "Which open funds are most at risk of closing oversubscribed?"** → oversubscription-risk ordering on the heatmap (oversub ratio + 30-day sell-out prob).
- **Q6 — "Is demand accelerating or cooling for AI / Secondaries?"** → per-strategy momentum trend sign (accelerating / steady / cooling).

---

### §2 Entry & nav

**Mode & placement.** S21 lives in the left-rail surface list as **"Demand"** (foundation Part 3 rail, grouped near the signal-gated trio Demand · Secondary · Sentiment). It is visible in **Investor mode** (the personal allocation-odds + leaderboard reads are investor-actionable) and in **Internal mode** (the platform-wide heatmap/momentum board, aggregate-only). The two modes share one layout; mode only changes scope and which panel leads:

- **Investor mode** — the **personal allocation-odds** panel (Q2) is foregrounded; the investor lands wanting "my odds in a named fund." The heatmap and momentum strip are platform context around it. Allocation-odds is computed for the *asking investor's own* named request, so there is **no k-anon issue on the requester's own ticket** — the user sees their real `requested_eur` / `expected_granted_eur`.
- **Internal mode** — the **fund-demand heatmap** (Q1/Q5) leads as a platform monitoring board; the personal allocation-odds panel is hidden (there is no "you" in internal mode). All fund/strategy statistics are **k-anon cohort aggregates** (`feeder_n_investors >= 10` to name a fund; else `"a {strategy} fund"`) and never name a counterparty.

**Entry points.**
1. **Left rail → "Demand."** Instant, full-screen, no scroll (foundation Part 3). The frame (ProfileBar, CohortBuilder, Ask Alpha, PDF export) persists.
2. **Deep-link from S20 · Next Best Action.** An NBA card whose rationale references fill-time / demand pressure links into S21 with that `fund_id` pre-selected in the allocation-odds panel ("see your odds for this fund"). When demand is not connected, the link still resolves — S21 opens in NotConnectedState with the fund pre-selected and the message "demand intelligence pending; your allocation odds will appear here when the feed connects."
3. **Deep-link from S13 · Funds / Fund-vs-Fund.** A FundCard offers "How likely am I to get allocated?" → S21 allocation-odds for that fund.
4. **Ask Alpha.** Any of Q1–Q6 asked in chat renders an inline mini-answer (foundation Part 5) and offers "open the demand board" → S21. When not connected, Ask Alpha returns the plain pending message, never a fabricated number.

**Nav within the surface.** No internal scroll. The surface is a single full-screen grid (§3); the `action` parameter (`heatmap` / `fill_time` / `allocation_odds` / `oversub` / `momentum` / `comparables` / `fill_leaderboard`) maps to panels that are *all visible at once* on desktop, not tabbed — the user sees the whole demand picture in one glance. On smaller breakpoints panels reflow (§10).

---

### §3 Layout

S21 uses the foundation **Detail template**: a single full-screen, no-scroll centre stage of **MetaCards** inside the persistent frame. Every panel below is designed **first as its live form**, then the entire grid is wrapped in the **NotConnectedState** so that by default it renders as a dashed greyed preview of this exact layout. **The two are the same component tree under two data states.**

**Desktop (≥1280px) — the intended live layout, 12-column grid, four regions:**

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Platform Demand                                  [synthetic-preview ▢ off] [PDF ▾] │  ← surface header
│  How demand is moving on the platform, and your odds of getting in.                │     (title h1 + sub-caption)
├───────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┤
│  A — FUND-DEMAND HEATMAP            (8 col) │  B — DEMAND MOMENTUM          (4 col) │
│  open funds × demand intensity grid        │  per-strategy hottest-now strip      │
│  rows = open funds, cells = fill% /        │  9 strategy rows, heat bar +         │
│  oversub / velocity (HeatMap component)    │  ▲ accelerating / ▼ cooling glyph    │
│  + fill-leaderboard ordering (fastest top) │  hottest 3 pinned at top             │
│  CaptionBlock · ConfidenceBadge · Prov.    │  CaptionBlock · Prov.                │
├───────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│  C — TIME-TO-FILL PREDICTOR        (8 col) │  D — YOUR ALLOCATION ODDS     (4 col) │
│  ForecastBand survival curve for the       │  per-open-fund allocation-probability │
│  selected fund: median days-to-fill +      │  fund selector + ticket input        │
│  p25/p75 band + sell-out prob 30/60d       │  → allocation-odds gauge (odds bar)  │
│  + comparable-fill-history overlay (Q4)    │  + expected scale-down + regime tag  │
│  CaptionBlock · demand_source flag · Prov. │  no-advice note · SyntheticTag · Prov│
└───────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Region A — Fund-demand heatmap (Q1, Q5).** A **HeatMap** (foundation Part 2.5/2.6) where each **row is an open fund** and the row is ordered top-to-bottom by demand intensity (fastest-filling at top — this *is* the fill-leaderboard ordering). Each row carries three encoded cells: **fill-fraction** (% subscribed, the headline, `--data-*` intensity gradient), **oversub ratio** (with an "oversubscribed" flag chip when `> 1.0×`), and **velocity** (€/day, EWMA). A right-hand mini-column shows **expected days-to-fill** as a small band chip (`6 · [3–11]d`) and **sell-out prob 30d** as a tiny meter. Funds below k-anon (`feeder_n_investors < 10`) render as **"a {strategy} fund"** with `fund_id:null` — named only when ≥10. A `demand_source` dot on each row distinguishes `own_prints` (solid) from `cohort_prior` / `default_prior` (hollow, "estimate from comparable funds"). CaptionBlock: *"Open funds ranked by current subscription pace. Estimates, not guarantees."*

**Region B — Demand momentum (Q3, Q6).** A compact **9-row strip** (one row per strategy), each row a horizontal **heat bar** (`heat = 0.5·oversub + 0.5·momentum`, squashed to [0,1]) with a **trend glyph** — `▲ accelerating` (`--over`/positive), `– steady` (`--muted`), `▼ cooling` (`--under`) — and the fractional acceleration inline (`+41%`). The **hottest 3** strategies pin to the top with a subtle "hottest now" label; the coolest 3 sit at the bottom. Fixed strategy→colour mapping per foundation 2.2. CaptionBlock: *"Which strategies are heating up vs cooling — trend in subscription velocity, not just level."*

**Region C — Time-to-fill predictor (Q4 + the survival core).** A **ForecastBand** chart (foundation Part 2.5 — base line + shaded band, the approved encoding) showing the **survival curve** for the fund selected in Region A or D: the x-axis is days from now, the line is `S(t)` = probability still open, the band is the **p25/p75** fill window, and two markers call out **median days-to-fill** and the **sell-out prob at 30d / 60d**. A faint **comparable-fill-history overlay** (Q4 — how the fund's `(strategy, cap_bucket, vintage_bucket)` cohort historically filled: `fill_d50`, `fill_d90`) sits behind the live curve as a ghost reference. A prominent `demand_source` flag tells the user whether this is the fund's *own* curve (`own_prints`) or a borrowed **cohort prior** (a 2-day-old fund with no own data). CaptionBlock: *"Expected time to fully subscribe, as a range with probabilities — never a countdown."*

**Region D — Your allocation odds (Q2, investor mode only).** The personal-actionable panel. A **fund selector** (open funds, defaults to the deep-linked fund or the fastest-filling) + a **requested-ticket input** (EUR, defaults to `fund.min_eur`, validates `≥ min_eur`). On change it computes and renders: an **allocation-odds bar** (a horizontal gauge, 0–100%, the `prob_full_allocation`), the **regime tag** (`undersubscribed` / `oversubscribed` / `closed` — so "57% odds" is never ambiguous about *why*), the **expected granted €** and **expected scale-down %** ("expect ~43% of your ticket cut"), and the **priority-weight** breakdown (tier · wealth · early · repeat) shown as small contributing chips. A persistent no-advice footer: *"Illustrative odds — the GP/platform sets final allocation. Not a guarantee, not advice."* In Internal mode this region is hidden and Region A widens to 12 columns.

**The NotConnectedState default (what actually renders today).** By default `GET /api/demand → {status:"not_connected"}`, so the **entire grid above renders inside a single NotConnectedState wrapper** (foundation Part 2.6) — the dashed-outline, greyed treatment:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Platform Demand                                  [synthetic-preview ▢ off] [PDF ▾] │
│  How demand is moving on the platform, and your odds of getting in.                │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ┐ │
│  │   ●  Live when the demand feed connects                                       │ │  ← NotConnectedState banner
│  │      The platform demand intelligence is built and tested — it activates      │ │     (dashed outline, --not-connected)
│  │      the moment the subscription/allocation feed is wired.                     │ │
│  │                                                       [ Preview with sample ▸] │ │  ← synthetic-preview toggle
│  │ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ │ │
│  │  ╎ A heatmap (dashed, greyed)   ╎  ╎ B momentum (dashed, greyed)        ╎     │ │  ← the SAME four-region layout,
│  │  ╎ rows + cells previewed as    ╎  ╎ strategy rows previewed empty      ╎     │ │     rendered as a dashed
│  │  ╎ skeleton bars, no numbers    ╎  ╎                                    ╎     │ │     greyed PREVIEW of the
│  │  ╎ C time-to-fill (dashed)      ╎  ╎ D allocation odds (dashed)         ╎     │ │     intended live grid
│  │  ╎ band-chart skeleton, no data ╎  ╎ selector + gauge skeleton          ╎     │ │
│  └ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ┘ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Key properties of the default render:
- The **four regions A–D are still drawn**, in their exact grid positions, as **dashed greyed silhouettes** of their live selves (the HeatMap rows, the ForecastBand frame, the momentum strip, the allocation-odds gauge — all present as low-contrast skeletons). The user sees *what this surface will become*, not a blank slate or an error card. This is what makes the live swap data-only: the component tree is identical; only the data state differs.
- A single calm banner anchors it: **"Live when the demand feed connects."** No error iconography, no red, no spinner. Uses `--not-connected` (dashed outline) and `--muted` text per foundation 2.2.
- A **synthetic-preview toggle** (off by default) lets a presenter populate the dashed preview with clearly-labelled illustrative sample data for a demo (§6). When on, every populated number carries a **SyntheticTag** ("illustrative — sample demand, not live platform data") and the banner changes to "Showing sample demand for preview — not live data."

**Responsive behaviour** is specified in §10 (the grid collapses 8/4 → stacked; the NotConnectedState banner stays pinned at the top of the stack).

---

### §4 Components & charts

All components are foundation components; this section specifies the **exact encodings** S21 uses and the one composite (`DemandHeatRow`) it assembles from primitives.

**4.1 The heatmap — `DemandHeatMap` (composes foundation HeatMap + BarRow).**
- **Structure.** A vertical list of `DemandHeatRow`s, one per open fund, ordered by fill-leaderboard rank (fastest first). Header row: `Fund · Strategy · Fill % · Oversub · Velocity · ETA (band) · Sell-out 30d`.
- **`DemandHeatRow` encoding.** Left: fund name (or `"a {strategy} fund"` if `feeder_n_investors < 10`) + a strategy colour pip (fixed map, foundation 2.2) + a `demand_source` dot (filled = own_prints, hollow ring = cohort_prior/default_prior, tooltip "estimate from comparable funds"). Centre: a **fill-fraction bar** (BarRow style, `--data-*` intensity, **% inline on the bar** per foundation 2.5, never legend-only). Then an **oversub chip** (`1.55×`, amber-flagged with a "▲ oversubscribed" glyph when `> 1.0`; grey when `≤ 1.0`). Then **velocity** (`€980k/day`, mono/tabular). Right: an **ETA band chip** (`6d · [3–11]`) and a tiny **sell-out-30d meter** (a 0–1 micro-bar).
- **Charts language compliance.** Horizontal bars only (approved); % inline; large title (h2 min); 1–2 sentence caption; cohort labelled in k-anon terms not "All". No rejected encodings (no beeswarm, no scatter).
- **Ordering toggle.** A segmented control above the heatmap: `Fastest filling (default) · Most oversubscribed · Highest velocity` — this is the Q1 vs Q5 read switch, not a data refetch (re-sorts the same payload).

**4.2 Fill-time gauge — `FillTimeBand` (foundation ForecastBand).**
- **Encoding.** ForecastBand: x = days from now (0–180, clamped to horizon), the **survival line** `S(t)` (probability still open) descending from 1.0, the **shaded band** = p25→p75 fill window. Two callout markers: a **median marker** at the `S=0.5` crossing labelled "median ~6 days to full," and **probability markers** at day 30 / day 60 labelled with `selloff_prob_30d` / `selloff_prob_60d` ("96% likely closed within 30 days").
- **Comparable-history ghost (Q4).** A faint dashed reference line/band behind the live curve showing the cohort's historical `fill_d50` / `fill_d90` (`comparable_fill_history`), labelled "comparable funds historically." This is the survival model's training basis surfaced directly.
- **Never a countdown.** The component has **no** bare-number "days left" mode. It always renders a band + probability. G2 (bands, never points) is enforced at the component level — `FillTimeBand` will not render without `fill_band_days`.
- **demand_source flag.** A prominent inline flag: "Based on this fund's own demand" (own_prints) vs "Estimated from comparable {strategy} funds — this fund is too new to have its own curve" (cohort_prior/default_prior, with `shrink_w` near 0).

**4.3 Allocation-odds bar — `AllocationOddsGauge`.**
- **Encoding.** A horizontal gauge 0–100% with the `prob_full_allocation` as a filled bar, **% inline**. A **regime tag** above it: `Undersubscribed` (the only risk is closing before you act), `Oversubscribed` (pro-rata scale-down applies), or `Closed` (`already_full`). Below: **Expected to receive €X of €Y requested** and **Expected scale-down: ~43%** (the honest counterpart to the odds — never a lone "you'll get it" boolean). A **priority-weight breakdown** row: small chips `tier ×1.0 · wealth ×1.1 · early +0.30 · repeat +0.15 → weight 1.55`, so the odds are explainable.
- **Inputs.** A fund selector (open funds) + a ticket input (EUR). Ticket validates `≥ fund.min_eur`; below-minimum renders an inline notice "below fund minimum (€X)," not the gauge.
- **No-advice footer** (always): *"Illustrative odds — the GP/platform sets final allocation. Not a guarantee, not advice."*
- **Wealth-context line.** When `investable_wealth_eur` is present: "this ticket is ~X% of your investable wealth." When null (state 4): suppressed (`wealth_context:null`), and the priority weight falls back to tier+earliness (wealth factor 1.0) — the gauge still renders.

**4.4 Demand-momentum strip — `MomentumStrip`.**
- **Encoding.** 9 rows (one per strategy, fixed colour), each a horizontal **heat bar** (`heat` [0,1]) + a **trend glyph + sign** (`▲ accelerating +41%` / `– steady` / `▼ cooling −12%`, colour paired with glyph per foundation accessibility rule). Hottest-3 pinned top under a "Hottest now" subhead; coolest-3 at bottom under "Cooling." Each row's number is a ProvenancePopover chip.

**4.5 The NotConnectedState wrapper — `DemandNotConnected` (foundation NotConnectedState).**
- **Role.** Wraps the entire A–D grid. When `status === "not_connected"` it renders: the dashed `--not-connected` outline, the **"Live when the demand feed connects"** banner (§8 copy), the **dashed greyed preview** of all four regions (their live components rendered in a `preview/skeleton` variant — real structure, no numbers, low contrast), and the **synthetic-preview toggle**.
- **Critical design rule.** `DemandNotConnected` is **not a separate screen** — it is a state of the same `<PlatformDemand>` component. The four regions are mounted in both states; in not_connected they receive a `notConnected`/`skeleton` prop. This guarantees the live swap is data-only (§5, §11).
- **`ready` / `scaffold_validated` signal.** The payload carries `ready:true` and `scaffold_validated:true`. The banner copy reflects this ("built and tested — activates the moment the feed is wired") so the state reads as *engineered and pending*, not *missing*.

---

### §5 Data bindings

**Single endpoint:** `GET /api/demand?action={…}&investor_id={?}&fund_id={?}&requested_eur={?}&window_days={=90}`. Base URL `{{API}}` per foundation Part 0.3. Every numeric value follows the value+provenance contract: bind the **value** to the visual, the **provenance** to the ConfidenceBadge / ProvenancePopover.

**Default (feed not wired) — the render-today binding:**
```jsonc
GET /api/demand            // no action → defaults to heatmap; feed off
{
  "status": "not_connected",
  "reason": "ALPHA_FEED_DEMAND not set",
  "ready": true,
  "scaffold_validated": true,
  "provenance": { "snapshot_id":"synthetic-2026-06-14",
                  "method":"adapter-gated; no live platform-demand signal" }
}
```
→ `status === "not_connected"` selects the `DemandNotConnected` render of `<PlatformDemand>`. `ready`/`scaffold_validated` drive the "built and tested" banner copy. **No `fill_leaderboard` / `allocation_odds` / `demand_momentum` keys are present** — the FE must not assume them; it renders the dashed preview, not a partial.

**Live shape when fed — `action=fill_leaderboard|heatmap`:**
```
status, window_id
fill_leaderboard[] each:
  fund (or "a {strategy} fund") ← DemandHeatRow label (k-anon: name iff feeder_n_investors>=10)
  fund_id (or null)             ← row id / deep-link target
  strategy, vintage_year        ← colour pip, comparability label
  fill_fraction_now             ← DemandHeatMap fill-bar (% inline)
  oversub_ratio                 ← oversub chip (flag when >1.0)
  velocity_eur_per_day          ← velocity column (int EUR)
  expected_days_to_fill         ← ETA band chip (median)
  fill_band_days [p25,p75]      ← ETA band chip (range) + FillTimeBand band
  selloff_prob_30d              ← sell-out-30d meter
  demand_source                 ← source dot (own_prints / cohort_prior / default_prior)
fastest_filling { fund, expected_days_to_fill, oversub_ratio } ← "Fastest filling" callout
provenance { method, macro, synthetic:false, snapshot_id, feed_version } ← Confidence/Provenance badges
```

**`action=allocation_odds&fund_id&investor_id&requested_eur`** → `allocation_odds{ fund, fund_id, requested_eur, available_eur, target_raise_eur, oversub_ratio, regime, prob_full_allocation, expected_granted_eur, expected_scaledown_pct, priority_weight, demand_source, provenance }` → binds to `AllocationOddsGauge` (odds bar ← `prob_full_allocation`; regime tag ← `regime`; expected/scale-down ← `expected_granted_eur`/`expected_scaledown_pct`; priority chips ← `priority_weight`). Inline `{error:"below fund minimum", min_eur}` → the below-minimum notice, not the gauge.

**`action=fill_time&fund_id`** → single `fill_time_survival` object (`expected_days_to_fill`, `fill_band_days`, `selloff_prob_30d/60d`, `demand_source`, `shrink_w`); `&full=1` adds `survival_curve` → the `FillTimeBand` line. **`action=comparables&fund_id`** → `comparable_fill_history{ strategy, vintage_bucket, n_comparables, median_days_to_fill, fill_d50, fill_d90, oversub_rate }` → the ghost overlay (Q4). **`action=momentum`** → `demand_momentum{ by_strategy{ strategy:{velocity_eur_per_day, momentum, oversub_mean, heat, trend} }, hottest[], coolest[] }` → `MomentumStrip`. **`action=oversub`** → `oversubscription_risk[]` (leaderboard row shape + `prob_close_oversubscribed`) → heatmap "Most oversubscribed" ordering.

**Binding rules the FE must hold.**
- **One state switch drives everything:** `status`. `"not_connected"` → dashed preview; `"connected"` → live grid. No other branch decides layout.
- **k-anon naming is server-supplied** — the FE renders `fund` and `fund_id` exactly as returned; when `fund_id` is `null` the row is a bucket label and is non-deep-linkable. The FE never re-derives names.
- **Allocation-odds requester exemption** — the *fund label* still follows k-anon (a sub-10 fund shows as `"a {strategy} fund"`, `fund_id:null`), but the requester's **own** `requested_eur` / `expected_granted_eur` are shown unmasked (their own ticket, not a disclosure about others).
- **`synthetic` flag** — when `provenance.synthetic === true` (fixture / preview), every number carries a SyntheticTag. Live data is `synthetic:false` and carries no tag.
- **EUR fields are integers** (G9) — render with EU formatting (€, thousands separators), mono/tabular.
- **The S20 producer signals (`demand_pressure`, `fill_time_estimate`) are not S21 visuals** — they are consumed internally by S20. S21 displays the human-readable survival/odds; it does not need to render the `[0,1]` scalar. When not connected, S20's demand terms are zeroed (its existing stub), so S21's not_connected state and S20's "demand inputs not connected" sub-state are consistent.

---

### §6 Interactions

**Synthetic-preview toggle (the demo affordance).**
- A control in the surface header (and inside the NotConnectedState banner): **"Preview with sample"** — off by default. It is a **client-side UI-only** toggle; it does **not** call the live endpoint and does **not** alter the feed status. When on, the FE renders the four live components populated with a **bundled, clearly-labelled illustrative sample** (the same shape as the live payload, drawn from the documented fixture figures so the demo is coherent and reproducible — e.g. Fund P at 87% / 1.55× / ~6 days, AI hottest +41%).
- **Every value under preview carries a SyntheticTag** ("illustrative — sample demand, not live platform data"); the banner switches to "Showing sample demand for preview — not live data," and the dashed `--not-connected` outline remains visible around the grid so a viewer can never mistake preview for live. Turning the toggle off restores the dashed empty preview.
- **Why it exists:** it lets a presenter walk the *intended live experience* without a feed and without the FE ever fabricating production numbers on the real route. It is the safe, honest way to demo a signal-gated surface. (Consistent with the foundation's "synthetic/illustrative fields are visibly labelled and never styled as verified fact," Part 0.2.)

**Live-state interactions (when connected).**
- **Heatmap row → time-to-fill + odds.** Clicking a `DemandHeatRow` selects that fund: Region C (`FillTimeBand`) renders its survival curve + comparable history, and Region D (`AllocationOddsGauge`) pre-selects it. A bucketed (`fund_id:null`) row is **not** clickable (no fund to drill into) and shows a tooltip "named when ≥10 investors."
- **Heatmap ordering toggle** — `Fastest filling / Most oversubscribed / Highest velocity` re-sorts the same payload client-side (no refetch). This is the Q1↔Q5 switch.
- **Allocation-odds inputs** — changing the fund selector or the ticket input recomputes via `action=allocation_odds` (debounced); below-`min_eur` shows the inline notice. The priority-weight chips update with the result so the user sees *why* the odds changed.
- **Momentum row → strategy filter** — clicking a strategy in `MomentumStrip` filters the heatmap to that strategy's open funds (Q6 → Q1 within a strategy).

**Hover.**
- **Heatmap cell hover** — fill-bar shows "{fund}: 87% subscribed, oversubscribed 1.55×, ~€980k/day"; the ETA chip shows "median ~6 days, 25–75% window 3–11 days"; the source dot shows "based on this fund's own demand" / "estimated from comparable funds."
- **FillTimeBand hover** — at any day `t`, "≈{S(t)·100}% chance still open on day {t}"; the band shows the p25/p75 boundaries; the comparable ghost shows "comparable funds: 50% filled by ~18 days."
- **Momentum bar hover** — "{strategy}: heat {heat}, demand {accelerating/cooling} {±%} vs prior window, mean oversub {oversub_mean}."
- **Allocation-odds hover** — the priority chips explain each factor; the scale-down explains "if demand holds, expect ~43% of your €150k ticket cut to ~€85k."

**Provenance (foundation Part 0.2 / 2.6 — non-negotiable).** Every number is a **clickable provenance chip** → ProvenancePopover showing `method` (the survival/pro-rata description), `demand_source` (own_prints / cohort_prior / default_prior + `n_comparables`), `macro` (the "illustrative; fill timing depends on live demand & GP decisions" note), `snapshot_id`, `feed_version`, and `synthetic`. A **ConfidenceBadge** ("based on this fund's own demand" or "estimate from N comparable funds") sits on each survival/odds read. No number renders bare — even under synthetic preview.

**No-FOMO interaction constraints.** No interaction ever produces a bare countdown, a "act now" CTA, or a flashing urgency animation. The strongest urgency the surface can express is "~96% likely to close within 30 days (illustrative)" — a probability with a caveat, never an imperative. The "Contact your sales associate" CTA (where present, on a high-odds named fund) is informational, matching the foundation's info-not-advice posture.

---

### §7 Seven states

S21 renders all seven foundation states (Part 9). **State 1 (not_connected) is the DEFAULT** and the dominant lived experience until the feed is wired.

1. **not_connected — DEFAULT.** `status:"not_connected"`. The full A–D grid renders inside `DemandNotConnected`: dashed greyed preview + "Live when the demand feed connects" banner + synthetic-preview toggle. **Not an error** — no red, no error glyph, `ready:true` drives "built and tested." S20's demand terms are zeroed in parallel (consistent stub). This is what users see today.
2. **happy (connected, open funds present).** Live `fill_leaderboard` / `heatmap` / `fill_time` / `oversub` / `momentum` / `allocation_odds` / `comparables` populate the grid. All four regions live, all badges present, `synthetic:false`.
3. **thin / new-fund.** A fund with `age_days ≤ 2` or no comparable cohort → `demand_source` = `cohort_prior` / `default_prior`, `shrink_w` low/zero. Numbers still render (never blank); the source dot is hollow and the FillTimeBand flag reads "estimated from comparable {strategy} funds — too new to have its own curve." (Foundation thin-cohort discipline; uses the RelaxBanner pattern in spirit but the message is the demand_source flag.)
4. **null wealth (`investable_wealth_eur is None`).** Heatmap / momentum / fill-time render fully (they need no wealth). Allocation-odds still computes (priority weight falls back to tier+earliness, wealth factor 1.0); the "% of your wealth" line is suppressed (`wealth_context:null`). SyntheticTag on any imputed profile input per foundation.
5. **null target / already-full (zero available).** `target_raise_eur` missing or `available_eur ≤ 0` → fill-time clamps `expected_days_to_fill → 0`, `selloff_prob_30d → 1.0`; allocation-odds `regime:"closed"`, `prob_full_allocation → 0.0`, `already_full:true`. The heatmap row shows a "Closed / fully subscribed" chip; the odds gauge reads "this fund is fully subscribed — no allocation available." Never crashes, never zero-fills silently.
6. **scaffold-only / synthetic preview.** The fixture-fed render (tests, or the demo toggle): `synthetic:true`, every number SyntheticTag'd, dashed outline retained under the preview toggle. Never exposed on the live route.
7. **error / degraded.** `404 {"error":"unknown investor"}` / `{"error":"unknown fund"}` → a quiet inline notice in the affected region (not a full-screen error). `allocation_odds` below-minimum → inline "below fund minimum (€X)." Engine failure → cached last-good demand snapshot + a quiet "showing last available demand" banner (foundation degraded pattern); Ask Alpha falls back to the plain pending message. Never a blank screen, never a fabricated number.

Plus foundation cross-cutting: **not-significant** deltas greyed; **SyntheticTag** on every illustrative/preview value; **loading** = calm skeletons (which here visually rhyme with the not_connected dashed preview — intentional, since the surface's resting state already *is* a skeleton).

---

### §8 Copy deck

**Surface header.**
- Title (h1): **"Platform Demand"**
- Sub-caption: *"How demand is moving on the platform, and your odds of getting in."*

**NotConnectedState (the default state — the load-bearing copy).**
- Banner headline: **"Live when the demand feed connects."**
- Banner body: *"The platform demand intelligence is built and tested — it activates the moment the subscription and allocation feed is wired. You'll see which funds are filling fastest, how quickly each is expected to fill, and your odds of getting a full allocation."*
- Synthetic-preview toggle label (off): **"Preview with sample"**
- Synthetic-preview state banner (on): *"Showing sample demand for preview — illustrative only, not live platform data."*

**Heatmap (Region A).**
- Title: **"Funds filling fastest"** · Caption: *"Open funds ranked by current subscription pace. Estimates from platform demand, not guarantees."*
- Oversubscribed chip: **"1.55× — oversubscribed"** · Source dot tooltip (hollow): *"Estimated from comparable funds — this fund is too new to have its own demand curve."*
- Bucketed-row tooltip: *"Named once at least 10 investors have subscribed."*

**Time-to-fill (Region C).**
- Title: **"Expected time to fill"** · Caption: *"Expected time to fully subscribe, shown as a range with probabilities — never a countdown."*
- Median marker: *"Median ~{n} days to full"* · 30d marker: *"~{p}% likely to close within 30 days"*
- Comparable overlay: *"Comparable {strategy} funds historically: 50% filled in ~{d50} days, 90% in ~{d90} days."*
- demand_source flag (own): *"Based on this fund's own demand."* (cohort): *"Estimated from comparable {strategy} funds — too new to have its own curve."*

**Allocation odds (Region D).**
- Title: **"Your allocation odds"** · Sub: *"For a named fund and your requested ticket."*
- Regime tags: **"Undersubscribed"** / **"Oversubscribed"** / **"Closed"**
- Undersubscribed read: *"Likely a full allocation — the main risk is the fund closing before you act."*
- Oversubscribed read: *"~{p}% chance of a full €{x} allocation. It's oversubscribed ({r}×), so expect a partial fill around €{g} (~{s}% scaled down) if demand holds."*
- Closed read: *"This fund is fully subscribed — no allocation available right now."*
- Below-minimum: *"Below this fund's minimum of €{min}. Enter at least €{min}."*
- Priority chips: *"Your priority weight ({w}) reflects an early, repeat request."* (assembled from the present factors)
- No-advice footer (always): **"Illustrative odds — the GP and platform set the final allocation. Not a guarantee, not advice."**

**Momentum (Region B).**
- Title: **"What's heating up"** · Caption: *"Which strategies are accelerating vs cooling — trend in subscription velocity, not just level."*
- Subheads: **"Hottest now"** / **"Cooling"** · Trend labels: *"accelerating +{m}%"* / *"steady"* / *"cooling −{m}%"*

**Ask Alpha fallback (not connected):** *"Platform demand intelligence is pending — it will be available once the subscription feed connects. I can't estimate fill-times or allocation odds without live demand data."* (Never invents a number.)

**Error/degraded:** *"Showing last available demand."* / *"We couldn't load demand for this fund."*

---

### §9 Badge rendering

Per foundation 2.6, numeric components embed the three badges; S21 adds the `demand_source` and `regime` indicators specific to this surface.

- **ConfidenceBadge.** On every survival and odds read. For `own_prints`: "based on this fund's own demand." For `cohort_prior` / `default_prior`: "estimate from {n} comparable {strategy} funds" (greys/softens the value to signal lower fund-specificity, consistent with the foundation's not-significant greying). The heatmap ETA chip and the odds gauge each carry one.
- **ProvenancePopover.** Click any number → method (the survival / pro-rata description), `demand_source` + `n_comparables`, `macro` caveat, `snapshot_id`, `feed_version`, `synthetic`. The `method` string always ends "Illustrative estimate, not a guarantee" — surfaced verbatim in the popover (G-NOADVICE).
- **CoverageBadge.** Not the primary badge here (this surface is not look-through-coverage-bound), but the **`demand_source` flag plays the analogous role**: it tells the user how much of the estimate is the fund's own signal vs borrowed cohort prior. Render it as a small inline chip on the heatmap row (source dot) and prominently on the FillTimeBand.
- **SyntheticTag** (amber, foundation 2.2). On **every** value under the synthetic-preview toggle and in any fixture/scaffold render (`provenance.synthetic === true`). Also on imputed profile inputs feeding allocation-odds (e.g. an illustrative `wealth_bucket` or `priority_tier`). Never appears on live (`synthetic:false`) data.
- **`--not-connected` (dashed outline).** The default-state wrapper treatment. Distinct from `--gated` (S21 is not paywall-gated; it is feed-gated) and from `--synthetic` (amber). The dashed outline persists even under synthetic preview so preview is never mistaken for live.
- **Regime tag.** The allocation-odds `regime` rendered as a small labelled tag (`Undersubscribed` neutral, `Oversubscribed` amber-attention, `Closed` grey). Always paired with text, never colour-only (accessibility).
- **Oversubscribed flag.** On any `oversub_ratio > 1.0`: an amber chip with a `▲` glyph (colour + glyph, never colour alone).

---

### §10 Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive (foundation Part 3 breakpoints).**
- **≥1280px (desktop / boardroom projector).** The 8/4 two-column, four-region grid as drawn in §3. No scroll. Charts render large (foundation 2.5 — large titles, Z/forecast charts large).
- **768–1279px (tablet).** Grid collapses to a single column, region order **A (heatmap) → D (allocation odds) → C (time-to-fill) → B (momentum)** in investor mode (personal read prioritised after the leaderboard), **A → B → C** in internal mode (D hidden). CohortBuilder collapses to a drawer; Ask Alpha to a launcher (foundation Part 3). The heatmap may show fewer columns (fold velocity into the hover) but never requires horizontal scroll.
- **<768px (mobile, advisor on the go).** Single-column stacked; each region a full-width card; the heatmap rows become compact stacked cells (fill% + oversub chip + ETA band, velocity to hover/expand); FillTimeBand reflows to full width with the band intact; AllocationOddsGauge stacks selector → ticket → gauge → scale-down. ProfileBar becomes a sheet. **No analytical chart requires horizontal scroll** (foundation hard rule).
- **NotConnectedState across breakpoints.** The dashed banner stays pinned at the top of the stack at every breakpoint; the four dashed region silhouettes reflow with the same order as their live counterparts, so the preview always previews *the layout the user will actually get* at that size.

**Accessibility (foundation Part 10, WCAG-AA).**
- **Never colour-only.** Oversubscribed = amber **+ ▲ glyph + "oversubscribed" text**; momentum trend = colour **+ ▲/–/▼ glyph + signed %**; regime = colour **+ label**; demand_source = filled-vs-hollow dot **+ tooltip text**. All semantic state carries a glyph and/or text.
