- **Contrast.** The `--not-connected` dashed grey and `--muted` banner text meet AA against `--bg`; the dashed preview is intentionally low-contrast but the *banner copy* (the load-bearing message) is full-contrast and AA-compliant.
- **Keyboard.** Full keyboard nav: tab through heatmap rows (Enter selects → drives C and D), the ordering toggle, the fund selector, the ticket input, the synthetic-preview toggle, every provenance chip. Visible focus states.
- **Charts as data.** Each chart (heatmap, FillTimeBand, MomentumStrip) exposes a "view data" affordance → the underlying table (foundation Part 10) — the survival curve as a table of `day, S(t)`; the heatmap as a fund × metric table; momentum as strategy × heat/trend. The not_connected state's "view data" reads "no data yet — feed not connected."
- **ARIA.** Live regions announce odds recomputation ("allocation odds updated: 57% chance of full allocation"); the synthetic-preview toggle announces "previewing sample demand, not live data"; the not_connected banner is announced as a status region, not an alert (it is not an error).
- **i18n (EN/DE).** All copy externalised (no hard-coded strings); EUR and EU number formatting; "Live when the demand feed connects" / "Live, sobald der Nachfrage-Feed verbunden ist" both fit the banner.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

**The surface is correct when:**

**Default / not-connected (the load-bearing acceptance):**
- [ ] On a clean environment, `GET /api/demand` returns `status:"not_connected"` and S21 renders the **NotConnectedState as its default** — dashed greyed preview of the full four-region layout + "Live when the demand feed connects" + synthetic-preview toggle.
- [ ] The not_connected render reads as **engineered-and-pending, not an error**: no red, no error glyph, no spinner; `ready:true` / `scaffold_validated:true` drive "built and tested" copy.
- [ ] The FE makes **no assumption** that `fill_leaderboard` / `allocation_odds` / `demand_momentum` keys exist in the not_connected payload — it renders the preview, not a partial/crash.
- [ ] S20 · Next Best Action's demand terms are zeroed in parallel (consistent stub) — S21's not_connected and S20's "demand not connected" sub-states agree.

**Adapter-swap / data-only (the pattern this surface demonstrates):**
- [ ] The four regions A–D are **the same component tree** in both states — the only difference between not_connected and live is the `status` value and the data props. **No layout/component refactor is needed to go live**; flipping `status` to `"connected"` with a live payload fills the existing dashed grid.
- [ ] Connecting the feed (monkeypatched `feed_status → connected`, fixture loaded) makes the same `<PlatformDemand>` render the live heatmap / time-to-fill / odds / momentum with **zero structural change**.

**Live correctness:**
- [ ] Heatmap rows ordered fastest-filling first; oversubscribed flagged (`>1.0×`, amber + glyph); k-anon honoured (sub-10 funds show `"a {strategy} fund"`, `fund_id:null`, non-clickable).
- [ ] FillTimeBand renders a **band + probability, never a bare countdown** (G2); comparable-history ghost (Q4) present; `demand_source` flag distinguishes own_prints vs cohort/default prior.
- [ ] AllocationOddsGauge shows odds **and** expected scale-down **and** regime tag; below-`min_eur` shows the inline notice; requester's own ticket shown unmasked even for a sub-10 fund (with the fund still bucket-labelled).
- [ ] MomentumStrip ranks hottest/coolest with trend glyph + signed %; fixed strategy colours.

**Cross-cutting (foundation parity):**
- [ ] Every number is a provenance chip → ProvenancePopover; ConfidenceBadge / `demand_source` flag on every survival & odds read; no number renders bare (even under preview).
- [ ] Synthetic-preview toggle populates the dashed grid with **SyntheticTag'd** illustrative data, keeps the dashed `--not-connected` outline, and never calls the live route or fabricates production numbers.
- [ ] No-advice / no-FOMO: no "act now," no countdown, no scarcity nudge; every read carries the "illustrative — GP/platform sets final allocation" note (G-NOADVICE).
- [ ] All seven states render (state 1 = default); responsive 1280/768/<768 with no horizontal chart scroll; WCAG-AA, colour-never-alone, keyboard nav, charts-as-data, EN/DE copy externalised.
- [ ] Placeholder data matches the Part 0.3 / §5 shapes so the live swap is a base-URL (and feed) change only.

---
*Build S21 to this sheet. Its whole reason to exist as a spec: prove the adapter-swap pattern visually — a fully-designed surface that ships in its NotConnectedState today, fills with live demand the moment the feed connects, and never once fabricates a fill-time, an allocation odd, or a scarcity nudge in between.*


---

## Surface S22 — Secondary Market

*(Use case T2-6 · signal-gated · binds `GET /api/secondary` · default state `not_connected`)*

> **Read alongside the Frontend Foundation** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`). This brief does not redefine the design system, the global frame (Part 3), the seven-state catalogue (Part 9), the component primitives (Part 2.6), or the chart language (Part 2.5) — it **composes** from them and specifies only what is particular to S22. Where the foundation already locks a token, a component, or a rule, this brief references it by name and adds the S22-specific binding. The one non-negotiable that governs this surface above all others: **the secondary feed is not wired, so the default and the thing Claude Design must build first is the `NotConnectedState` — a fully-designed, dashed, greyed preview of the live layout that flips to live data with zero layout change when `ALPHA_FEED_SECONDARY` connects.** The live layout is specified in full so the swap is purely a data binding; it is *not* the default. No modelled discount, clearing probability, or sell-vs-hold verdict ever renders as if it were market data until the feed connects.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** S22 turns Moonfare's internal secondary market into a **liquidity, sell-decision, and buy-side completion engine** rendered as one calm analytical surface. It answers the question a holder of illiquid fund positions cannot otherwise answer: *if I needed cash, which of my positions could I actually clear inside Moonfare, how fast, and at what give-up to NAV — and is holding on projected to be worth more than selling today?* It also runs the inverse: *are there secondary lots available right now, at attractive discounts, that fill my allocation gap?*

Three things make this surface distinct from every other analytical surface in Alpha, and the frontend must honour all three structurally:

1. **It is signal-gated and `not_connected` by default.** The backing data class — completed and attempted internal secondary placements, demand by fund, time-to-sale, realized discounts, fill rates — is **not in the canonical export**. The live endpoint returns `{status:"not_connected"}` until the feed is wired. The surface therefore ships as a designed preview, never an error, never a fabricated market.

2. **It is per-investor actionable and decision-adjacent.** Unlike the aggregate-only internal consoles (S21 Demand, S23 Sentiment), S22 answers "should I sell *my* Fund X." That raises the no-advice bar: every verdict is framed as an **illustrative decision-support lean** with explicit assumptions, never an instruction to transact. The copy deck (§8) and badge rules (§9) enforce this.

3. **Its hold-value leg reuses S12/T1-13 verbatim.** "Sell now vs keep" compares *expected secondary proceeds today* against the *vintage-aware projected hold value* — two like-for-like present-EUR numbers, never two unlike numbers. The hold side is the same J-curve projection used on the Projection surface, so it is **vintage-aware by construction** (§9).

**User questions → the live sub-views they drive** (these are the questions Ask Alpha routes here, and the section labels the live layout exposes):

| # | User question | Live sub-view | API action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "Which of my positions could I likely sell well through Moonfare?" | Liquidity ranking (top-k + best-exit) | `action=liquidity` |
| Q2 | "Which funds were most in demand in prior secondary windows?" | Demand leaderboard | `action=demand` |
| Q3 | "Should I sell Fund X or keep it?" | Sell-vs-hold card (one holding) | `action=sell_vs_hold&fund_id=` |
| Q4 | "Which funds are practically most liquid inside Moonfare?" | Liquidity ranking by strategy | `action=liquidity` |
| Q5 | "If I need liquidity, what are my best exit candidates?" | Liquidity ranking sorted by clearing-adjusted proceeds | `action=liquidity` |
| Q6 | "Which of my positions would clear at the narrowest discount?" | Discount ranking | `action=discount` |
| Q7 | "Are there secondary buys that fit my gap?" | Buy-side opportunity matches | `action=buyside` |

The surface answers all seven from a single layout whose three regions (liquidity ranking · sell-vs-hold · buy-side) cover the whole question set; `demand` and `discount` are re-sorts/re-binds of the liquidity region, not separate pages.

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

**Rail placement.** S22 sits in the left-rail surface list (Part 3) in the signal-gated cluster **S21 Demand · S22 Secondary · S23 Sentiment**, after the analytical surfaces and the Funds/What's-next group. In **Investor mode** it appears with a small **`not connected`** dot on the rail label (a hollow dashed dot using `--not-connected`, not a red error dot) so the user understands before clicking that this surface is pending. In **Internal mode** it appears in the consoles cluster identically gated. The rail label reads **"Secondary"** with a sublabel **"liquidity & sell-vs-hold"**.

**Entry points into S22:**
- **Rail click** — default entry; lands on the liquidity-ranking region with the `not_connected` preview by default.
- **From S20 Next Best Action** — when the Next-Best-Action rationale references a liquidity input, its "not connected" sub-state (foundation S20 states) deep-links here.
- **From S11 Liquidity & Cashflow** — the "what are my exit candidates" affordance links to S22's liquidity ranking; conceptually S11 answers *cashflow timing*, S22 answers *can I sell the asset itself*.
- **From S14 What's-next / S2** — the buy-side region is the T2-1/T1-1 completion hook; a "is this available on the secondary market at a discount?" affordance on a recommended-fund card deep-links to the S22 buy-side region with that strategy pre-filtered.
- **From Ask Alpha** — any of Q1–Q7 routes here; if the feed is not connected, Alpha answers with the pending-state language (§8) and offers the synthetic-preview toggle.

**Within-surface navigation.** A **segmented control** at the top of the stage switches the active question lens across the same data: `Liquidity · Narrowest discount · In-demand funds · Sell vs hold · Buy-side`. This is a *view selector over one surface*, not a page switch — the frame (ProfileBar, cohort builder, Ask Alpha) persists, there is **no scroll** (Part 0.2), and switching lenses re-sorts/re-binds only the affected region (Part 10 performance rule). In `not_connected` state the segmented control still renders and is interactive against the dashed preview (or the synthetic preview when toggled), so the user can explore the *intended* layout of every lens before the feed exists.

**Mode scoping.** Investor mode shows the per-investor liquidity ranking, sell-vs-hold, and buy-side regions for "You" (or the impersonated client in Advisor mode, with the acting-as banner from Part 7). Internal mode shows only the **aggregate demand leaderboard** region (the k-anon fund-level demand stats) and suppresses the per-holding liquidity/sell-vs-hold/buy-side regions, because those are per-identifiable-investor and internal mode is aggregate-only (Part 1).

---

### §3 Layout (live + `NotConnectedState` default)

The stage is a **no-scroll, three-region analytical layout** that fills the centre stage exactly like every other Detail surface (Part 3). The same grid renders in two skins: the **live skin** (real data bound from `/api/secondary`) and the **`NotConnectedState` skin** (the identical grid drawn dashed/greyed with placeholder geometry and the "Live when the secondary feed connects" message). **The grid geometry is byte-identical between the two skins** — that is the load-bearing design constraint, because the acceptance gate (§11) requires the live swap to be data-only.

**Desktop ≥1280px — the default lens (`Liquidity`):**

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Secondary — liquidity & sell-vs-hold        [Liquidity▾ · Discount · ...]  │ ← segmented lens control + title
│ ⚠ Live when the secondary feed connects · [▢ Show synthetic preview]      │ ← NotConnectedBanner (only in not_connected)
├──────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ LIQUIDITY RANKING OF YOUR HOLDINGS    │  SELL-VS-HOLD                       │
│ (HoldingsLiquidityTable)              │  (SellHoldCard stack)               │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │  ┌────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ Fund      Clear%  Disc  Proceeds  │ │  │  Fund C · Growth/Tech · 2021   │ │
│ │ ▓▓▓▓ B    91%    6%    €171k  ★best│ │  │  Sell today  ⇄  Hold (T1-13)   │ │
│ │ ▓▓▓ C     41%    28%   €154k      │ │  │  €154k        →   €465k PV      │ │
│ │ ▓▓ A      58%    16%   €146k      │ │  │  lean: hold leans richer       │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────┘ │  │  J-curve: recovery · vintage ◷ │ │
│ best exit ★ → "your most liquid is B" │  └────────────────────────────────┘ │
│ caption: most-likely-to-clear first   │  (one card per holding, expandable) │
├──────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ BUY-SIDE OPPORTUNITIES THAT FIT YOUR GAP   (FundCard row, secondary-flavour) │
│ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐   "You're 8pp under on Secondaries"  │
│ │ Sec. fund│ │ Sec. fund│ │ Infra... │   each card: discount + fills-gap pill│
│ │ -14% ·fit│ │ -9%  ·fit│ │ ...      │   CTA: Contact your sales associate   │
│ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘                                       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**The three regions:**

**(a) Liquidity ranking of holdings — top-left, the anchor region.** A **HoldingsLiquidityTable** (§4): one row per holding, **sorted most-likely-to-clear first** (by `liquidity_score`, tie-break clearing-adjusted proceeds). Each row carries a horizontal clearing-probability bar (the `▓` bar, % inline per Part 2.5), the expected discount with its band, the clearing-adjusted proceeds in EUR, and a `demand_source` provenance chip. The **best-exit** row is flagged with a `★ best exit` pill and echoed in the region caption ("Your most practically-liquid position is your Private Credit fund — ~91% likely to clear at ~6% discount"). When the lens is `Narrowest discount`, the same table re-sorts ascending by `expected_discount` (the layout does not change — only the sort and a one-word caption swap). When the lens is `In-demand funds`, the table re-binds to the **demand leaderboard** rows (fund-level, k-anon, with a `repeat_liquidity` "consistently liquid" chip) instead of the per-holding rows — same column scaffold, different source.

**(b) Sell-vs-hold candidates — top-right.** A vertical stack of **SellHoldCard** components (§4), one per holding (or one focused card when entered via Q3 / a row click). Each card is a **side-by-side, two-pane comparison** (never behind a toggle — Part 0.2): left pane "Sell today" (clearing-adjusted proceeds, clearing %, expected discount + band), right pane "Hold" (projected terminal NAV, projected future distributions, **present value** discounted at the illustrative illiquidity rate, J-curve phase). Between them a centred **lean indicator** — `hold leans richer` / `sell leans richer` / `roughly balanced` — rendered as a directional chip with a glyph (▲ hold / ▼ sell / ≈ balanced), never an imperative. A **vintage-phase badge** (§9) sits on the hold pane making the vintage-awareness visible. The default-rendered card is the highest-edge holding; the rest collapse to a slim summary row that expands to the full two-pane card on click.

**(c) Buy-side opportunity matches — bottom band, full width.** A horizontal row of **secondary-flavoured FundCards** (§4) for `status='live'` lots that fill the investor's allocation gap, ranked by attractiveness. A lead-in line states the gap being filled ("You're 8pp under on Secondaries"). Each card shows the **discount to NAV**, a **fills-gap pill** (the strategy + the underweight it closes), a `subscribable` state, k-anon naming, and the standard **"Contact your sales associate"** CTA plus the info-not-advice disclaimer. When there is no gap, the band shows the do-nothing state ("No allocation gap detected — no buy-side matches surfaced").

**`NotConnectedState` default skin.** By default, all three regions render inside the **`NotConnectedState` wrapper** (Part 2.6 / §4): the exact grid above drawn with dashed `--not-connected` outlines, greyed placeholder bars and rows (representative geometry — e.g. three table rows of descending bar length, two sell-vs-hold panes with a muted lean chip, three buy-side card outlines), and a single calm banner spanning the top: **"Live when the secondary feed connects."** A sublabel: *"Secondary intelligence is built and validated — it activates when Moonfare's internal secondary feed is connected."* A **synthetic-preview toggle** (§6) sits in the banner; when on, the dashed placeholders fill with the deterministic fixture numbers, every one carrying a visible `synthetic / illustrative` tag (Part 2.6 SyntheticTag) so the preview can never be mistaken for live market data.

**Responsive (foundation Part 3 breakpoints, applied to S22):**
- **≥1280px** — three-region layout as drawn: liquidity table top-left, sell-vs-hold top-right, buy-side full-width band below. Cohort builder fixed-left, Ask Alpha floating. No scroll.
- **768–1279px** — cohort builder collapses to a drawer, Ask Alpha to a launcher. The two top regions **stack vertically** (liquidity table, then sell-vs-hold stack), buy-side cards reflow to a 2-up row. The lens segmented control becomes a dropdown. Still no horizontal scroll on any table or bar.
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go)** — single column: lens dropdown → liquidity table (rows become compact cards, the clearing bar full-width with % inline) → sell-vs-hold (the two panes stack as labelled blocks with the lean chip between) → buy-side cards 1-up. ProfileBar becomes a sheet. No chart or table requires horizontal scroll; the discount band and proceeds always render inline, never in a side legend.

---

### §4 Components & charts

S22 introduces **four S22-specific compositions** built from foundation primitives, plus the `NotConnectedState` wrapper which governs the whole surface. None invents a new chart type outside the approved Part 2.5 set (horizontal bars, scorecards, FundCards, forecast-band reuse via T1-13).

**1. `NotConnectedState` wrapper (foundation Part 2.6; S22 is its primary home).** The surface-level wrapper that draws the dashed/greyed preview of the live grid and the "Live when the {feed} connects" message. S22 binds `{feed}` to **"secondary"** → "Live when the secondary feed connects." It composes:
- a **NotConnectedBanner** — full-width, calm (not an error banner): `--not-connected` dashed top-and-bottom hairline, an outline info glyph (never a red ⚠ alarm), the headline, the sublabel, and the synthetic-preview toggle.
- **dashed placeholder geometry** for each of the three regions, sized to the live geometry so the live swap shifts nothing. Placeholders animate in once, subtly (Part 2.4), then settle — no looping shimmer that reads as "broken."
- the **`ready: true` semantics** surfaced quietly: a small footnote "built & validated — pending feed" (binds `ready` / `scaffold_validated` from the payload). This signals the surface is finished, not unbuilt.
The wrapper is a hard requirement of the seven-state catalogue (Part 9 state 6 / S21–S23 acceptance): it must render as a *designed pending state*, never as an empty or errored screen.

**2. `HoldingsLiquidityTable`.** A dense, no-scroll analytical table (mono/tabular figures — Part 2.3) with these columns, in order:
- **Fund** — `fund` (k-anon: real name, or "a {strategy} fund" when `fund_id` is null), with the strategy as a small `--data-*` colour chip (the pinned strategy→colour map, Part 2.2) and the vintage year as a caption-size suffix.
- **Clear %** — `clearing_prob` rendered as a **thin horizontal BarRow** (Part 2.6) with the % **inline on the bar** (Part 2.5), not a side legend; bar fill uses the strategy colour. This is the most-likely-to-clear signal that drives the default sort.
- **Discount** — `expected_discount` as a % with its `discount_band` `[lo,hi]` shown as a small bracketed range beneath (e.g. `16% · [10–22%]`); never a bare point (the band is mandatory, §11 / G2). Narrower discount renders calmer; the surface never colour-codes discount as "good/bad" because that would read as advice — it is presented, not judged.
- **Proceeds** — `clearing_adjusted_proceeds_eur` (the rank value: proceeds × P(clear)) as the bold EUR figure, with `expected_proceeds_eur` ("if it clears") and `proceeds_band_eur` available in the ProvenancePopover. EUR integers (G9), EU formatting (Part 10).
- **Source** — a `demand_source` provenance chip: `own prints` / `cohort prior` / `default prior`, with a hover-def explaining a thin estimate borrowed a cohort prior. This is the surface's honesty about estimate quality and must never be hidden.
- Each row also carries the standard **ConfidenceBadge** (here: `n_distinct_lots` + `shrink_w` describing how much is own-data vs prior) and **ProvenancePopover** (method, demand blend, shrink weight, macro caveat, snapshot, feed version). The `★ best exit` pill marks the top row and binds `best_exit`.
Re-sorts client-side for the `Narrowest discount` lens; re-binds to demand-leaderboard rows for the `In-demand funds` lens (adding a `repeat_liquidity` "N windows · consistently liquid" chip and dropping the per-holding-only columns).

**3. `SellHoldCard`.** The decision-support comparison card — a MetaCard-shaped container with a **two-pane side-by-side body** and a centred lean indicator:
- **Header** — fund (k-anon), strategy colour chip, vintage year, and the **vintage-phase badge** (§9).
- **Left pane "Sell today"** — three stat lines: clearing probability (with the same thin bar as the table), expected discount + band, and **clearing-adjusted proceeds** in EUR (the headline of this pane). A caption: "expected proceeds today, probability-weighted."
- **Right pane "Hold"** — projected terminal NAV at horizon, projected **future** distributions only (the realized-excluded note, §9 / G3), and the **present value** of holding on (the headline of this pane), with the horizon (`horizon_q` quarters) and `scenario: base (T1-13)` shown as a caption "vintage-aware projection, discounted at an illustrative illiquidity rate." A tiny **ForecastBand sparkline** (reusing the S12/T1-13 ForecastBand component, Part 2.5) shows the NAV path to horizon so the hold leg is visibly a projection, not a single number.
- **Centre lean indicator** — `lean` rendered as a directional chip: ▲ `hold leans richer` / ▼ `sell leans richer` / ≈ `roughly balanced`, with `edge_eur` shown as the magnitude ("≈ €311k richer to hold, illustrative"). Glyph + word + sign together (never colour-only — Part 2.2 / Part 10).
- **Footer** — the no-advice line (§8) and a ProvenancePopover binding the full method string (which by contract ends in "not advice"), the illiquidity discount rate, demand source, and macro caveat.
- **Horizon control** — a small horizon stepper (`horizon_q`, clamped [1,40] per the endpoint) re-runs the hold leg and re-renders only this card. This is the one interactive analytic on the card; it visibly moves the present value and can flip the lean, demonstrating the vintage-aware projection live.

**4. Buy-side `FundCard` (secondary-flavour).** The standard Moonfare-listing **FundCard** (Part 2.6) with secondary-specific additions: a **discount-to-NAV** badge (`discount`, e.g. "-14% to NAV"), a **fills-gap pill** ("closes 8pp Secondaries underweight" — binds `fills_gap_strategy` + `gap_under_pct`), an **attractiveness** indicator (binds `attractiveness`, shown as a small ranked position not a raw score), a **`subscribable` state** (CTA enabled only when `subscribable:true`; otherwise the card shows "not currently subscribable" and disables the CTA), k-anon naming, and the standard region/strategy badges + stats row + **"Contact your sales associate"** CTA + info-not-advice disclaimer. Cards are ordered by attractiveness left-to-right.

**Chart-language compliance.** Clearing % uses approved thin horizontal bars with inline %; the hold leg reuses the approved ForecastBand; demand leaderboard reuses bars; buy-side reuses FundCards. **No rejected chart type appears** (no radar, no Sankey/capital-flow, no scatter — Part 2.5). Every region carries a 1–2 sentence CaptionBlock. No region scrolls.

---

### §5 Data bindings (← `GET /api/secondary`)

All bindings are to `GET /api/secondary?investor_id={?}&fund_id={?}&action={liquidity|discount|demand|sell_vs_hold|buyside}&window_id={?}&horizon_q={int=20}`. `investor_id` is the optional query param (the ProfileBar "You" or, in Advisor mode, the impersonated client). The base URL is `{{API}}` (Part 0.3) — swapping placeholder→live is a base-URL change.

**Default payload (feed not wired) — the binding the FE renders by default:**
- `status` = `"not_connected"` → render the `NotConnectedState` skin.
- `reason` (`"ALPHA_FEED_SECONDARY not set"`) → available in the banner's "why" popover, not shown inline.
- `ready` / `scaffold_validated` → the quiet "built & validated — pending feed" footnote on the wrapper.
- `provenance.snapshot_id` / `method` → the wrapper's provenance popover.
There are **no** `liquidity_ranking` / `sell_vs_hold` / `demand_leaderboard` / `buyside_matches` keys in the default payload — the FE must not expect them and must render the preview from its own placeholder geometry, not from data.

**Live — `action=liquidity` (default lens):**
- **HoldingsLiquidityTable rows** ← `liquidity_ranking[]`. Per row: `fund` (+ `fund_id` null ⇒ k-anon bucket) → Fund column; `clearing_prob` → Clear% bar; `expected_discount` + `discount_band` → Discount column; `clearing_adjusted_proceeds_eur` → Proceeds (sort key); `expected_proceeds_eur` → popover; `liquidity_score` → sort order; `demand_source` → Source chip; `strategy` / `vintage_year` → chip + suffix; `listed_nav_eur` → popover.
- **Best-exit pill** ← `best_exit.fund` / `clearing_adjusted_proceeds_eur` → the `★` row + region caption.
- **window_id** ← `window_id` → a "current window: 2025-H2" caption.
- **Provenance** ← `provenance.method` / `macro` / `synthetic` / `snapshot_id` / `feed_version` → ProvenancePopover + SyntheticTag (when `synthetic:true`).

**Live — `action=discount`:** `discount_ranking[]` (same row shape as liquidity) → the same table, re-sorted ascending by `expected_discount`. Binding is identical column-for-column; only the array source and sort differ.

**Live — `action=demand`:** `demand_leaderboard[]` → the table re-bound: `fund` (k-anon) / `demand_score` / `fill_rate` / `median_discount` / `repeat_liquidity`. `repeat_liquidity` → "consistently liquid · N windows" chip. `window_id` → caption.

**Live — `action=sell_vs_hold&fund_id=`:** `sell_vs_hold` object → one focused SellHoldCard:
- `sell_today.{clearing_prob, expected_discount, expected_proceeds_eur, clearing_adjusted_proceeds_eur, discount_band}` → left pane.
- `hold.{horizon_q, projected_terminal_nav_eur, projected_future_distributions_eur, present_value_eur, vintage_phase, scenario}` → right pane + ForecastBand sparkline + vintage-phase badge.
- `edge_eur` → lean magnitude; `lean` → lean chip direction/word.
- `provenance.{method, hold_discount_rate_annual, demand_source, macro, synthetic, snapshot_id}` → footer popover + no-advice line.
- Inline error `{sell_vs_hold:{error:"not a holding of this investor"}}` → the card shows a quiet "not one of your holdings" empty state (not a global error).

**Live — `action=buyside`:** `buyside_matches[]` → the buy-side FundCard row: `fund` (k-anon) / `strategy` / `vintage_year` / `listed_nav_eur` / `discount` / `fills_gap_strategy` / `gap_under_pct` / `attractiveness` / `subscribable`. `note` (`"no allocation gap detected"`) → the do-nothing state.

**Edge bindings (the seven states, §7):** `liquidity_ranking:[]` + `note:"no positions to evaluate"` → empty cold-start; per-holding `"no_nav":true` → that row sorted last with a "no current NAV" flag; `wealth_context:null` → suppress any "% of wealth freed" framing; `404 {error:"unknown investor"}` → global error state; engine failure → cached-last-good + quiet banner (Part 9 state 7).

**Provenance is structural (Part 0.2):** every numeric leaf in S22 — clearing %, discount, proceeds, present value, edge — renders its ConfidenceBadge and ProvenancePopover from the `provenance` block; a number never appears bare. The `synthetic` flag drives the SyntheticTag globally on this surface (true in synthetic-preview / fixture, false on live).

---

### §6 Interactions

**Synthetic-preview toggle (the headline S22 interaction).** In the `NotConnectedState` banner, a labelled toggle **"Show synthetic preview"** (off by default). Off → dashed empty placeholders. On → the placeholders fill with the **deterministic fixture data** (the worked-example numbers from the use-case brief: B = Private Credit best exit, 91% clear / 6% discount / €171k; C = Growth/Tech hold-leans-richer; A = Mid-Cap; one Secondaries buy-side lot at 14% discount filling an 8pp gap), and **every value is wrapped in a SyntheticTag** (amber "illustrative" pill, Part 2.2) with a persistent surface-level banner "Synthetic preview — illustrative numbers, not live market data." Toggling off returns to dashed empties. This satisfies the demo requirement (S21–S23 layout) while making it structurally impossible to mistake the preview for live data. The toggle is local UI state; it does not call the API (the API still returns `not_connected`). When the feed connects and the live route returns `connected`, the toggle disappears (there is real data; no preview needed).

**Sort holdings by liquidity (and the lens re-sorts).** The default sort is **most-likely-to-clear first** — by `liquidity_score`, tie-break clearing-adjusted proceeds (the engine returns rows pre-sorted; the FE preserves order). The lens segmented control re-sorts/re-binds the same table without a page change:
- `Liquidity` → `liquidity_ranking` order (most-likely-to-clear).
- `Narrowest discount` → re-sort ascending by `expected_discount` (or re-fetch `action=discount` — both yield the same row order; the FE may sort client-side from the already-fetched liquidity rows to avoid a round-trip).
- `In-demand funds` → re-bind to `demand_leaderboard` (fund-level, k-anon).
Column headers are also clickable for ad-hoc re-sort (Clear%, Discount, Proceeds), with a sort glyph; the active sort is announced to assistive tech (Part 10). Re-sorting re-renders only the table region (Part 10 performance).

**Row → sell-vs-hold focus.** Clicking a liquidity-table row focuses the corresponding SellHoldCard in the top-right region (or, on narrow viewports, scrolls it into the stacked position and expands it). This is the Q1→Q3 path: "this is my most liquid position" → "so should I actually sell it?"

**Horizon stepper on the SellHoldCard.** Adjusting `horizon_q` (clamped [1,40]) re-runs the hold leg via `action=sell_vs_hold&horizon_q=` and re-renders that one card, visibly moving the present value and possibly flipping the lean — the live demonstration of vintage-aware projection. Loading is a calm in-card skeleton, not a spinner (Part 9).

**ComparisonSelector — not used here.** S22 has no cohort comparison in the live data shape (the comparison is sell-vs-hold and own-holding-vs-own-holding, not You-vs-Peers). The ComparisonSelector is therefore absent from this surface; the cohort builder still re-resolves "You" (the investor scope) but does not drive a peer overlay. This is intentional and must not be retrofitted.

**Provenance (everywhere).** Every number is a click target → ProvenancePopover (Part 2.6): for liquidity rows it shows method, demand source, shrink weight, demand blend, macro caveat, snapshot, feed version; for sell-vs-hold it shows the full method string, the illiquidity discount rate, the demand source for the sell leg, the T1-13 scenario for the hold leg, and the macro caveat. In Ask Alpha (Part 5), any S22 number in an answer is a provenance chip identically. The `demand_source` chip is itself a provenance affordance: hovering explains whether the estimate is fund-specific (`own prints`) or borrowed (`cohort prior` / `default prior`).

**Buy-side card interactions.** Clicking a buy-side FundCard opens its detail (reusing the S13/Funds fund-detail pattern where the fund is named; k-anon bucket cards open a bucket explainer, not a fake fund page). The CTA "Contact your sales associate" is enabled only when `subscribable:true`. The fills-gap pill links back to S9 Architect / S14 What's-next to show the underweight in context.

**No transactional affordance.** Nowhere on S22 is there a "sell" or "buy" button. The only CTA is "Contact your sales associate" (information routing, not execution). This is a hard rule — the surface is decision-*support*, never decision-*execution*.

---

### §7 Seven states (foundation Part 9; `not_connected` is the default)

S22 renders all seven foundation states; the branch is driven by the payload `status` and the edge fields in §5.

1. **`not_connected` — DEFAULT.** `status=="not_connected"` → the `NotConnectedState` skin: dashed three-region preview, "Live when the secondary feed connects," the "built & validated — pending feed" footnote (`ready`/`scaffold_validated`), and the synthetic-preview toggle. **This is the state the surface ships in and the one acceptance scrutinises most** (§11). It must read as a finished, designed, calm pending state — not an error, not an empty shell. No live-data keys are expected.

2. **Happy (connected, investor has holdings).** `status=="connected"` with populated `liquidity_ranking` / `sell_vs_hold` / `demand_leaderboard` / `buyside_matches` → the full live skin per lens. The `not_connected` banner and synthetic toggle are gone; data is real, `synthetic:false`.

3. **Thin-demand (estimate borrowed a prior).** A holding whose fund has `<N_MIN_SECONDARY` own prints → `demand_source` = `cohort_prior` / `default_prior`, low `shrink_w`. The row still renders with full numbers (never blank); the Source chip flags it amber-adjacent and the ConfidenceBadge shows the low own-data weight. This is the foundation "thin" state expressed as estimate provenance rather than a relax banner (no cohort relax applies on this surface).

4. **Null wealth (`investable_wealth_eur is None` → `wealth_context:null`).** Rankings and sell-vs-hold still compute (they are absolute EUR, needing no wealth denominator). Only any "% of your wealth you could free up" framing is suppressed — the FE hides that one caption and shows absolute EUR proceeds only. No SyntheticTag forced; this is a genuine null, not an imputed value.

5. **Null / no NAV (`nav_eur <= 0` → `"no_nav":true`).** That holding clamps `listed_nav_eur` to 0, `expected_proceeds_eur==0`; the row still renders (never dropped), is sorted **last**, and shows a quiet "no current NAV — not currently sellable" flag. The clearing probability is still modelled and shown for completeness but greyed.

6. **Scaffold-only / synthetic preview.** Reached on the live route never — only via the synthetic-preview toggle in `not_connected` (or in the backend fixture tests). When shown, every value carries the SyntheticTag and the surface banner declares "Synthetic preview — illustrative, not live market data" (§6 / G8). The live route never exposes `synthetic:true`.

7. **Error / degraded.** `404 {error:"unknown investor"}` → a global quiet error state ("we couldn't find this investor"); `sell_vs_hold` inline `{error:"not a holding of this investor"}` → a per-card empty state, **not** a global error; engine failure → cached last-good with a quiet "showing last available" banner (Part 9). Never a blank screen, never a fabricated number.

**Cross-cutting (Part 9):** not-significant deltas n/a here (no peer deltas); loading = calm skeletons in the affected region only; the dashed `--not-connected` styling is reserved for state 1 and the synthetic-preview's empty cells, never used to style a real-but-degraded live state (which uses the cached-last-good banner instead).

---

### §8 Copy deck

All copy is structured for English + German (Part 10); no hard-coded strings. The governing voice: calm, factual, **no-advice**, "live when connected."

**`NotConnectedState` (default):**
- Banner headline: **"Live when the secondary feed connects."**
- Sublabel: *"Secondary intelligence is built and validated. It activates when Moonfare's internal secondary market feed is connected."*
- Footnote (binds `ready`/`scaffold_validated`): *"Built & validated — pending feed."*
- Synthetic toggle label: **"Show synthetic preview"**; helper: *"Illustrative numbers to preview the layout — not live market data."*
- Rail sublabel: *"liquidity & sell-vs-hold."*

**Synthetic-preview surface banner (toggle on):**
- *"Synthetic preview — illustrative numbers, not live market data. Every figure below is modelled scaffold, not a quote or firm bid."*

**Liquidity ranking region:**
- Title: **"Liquidity ranking of your holdings."**
- Caption (most-likely-to-clear first): *"Sorted by how practically you could clear each position inside Moonfare — most likely to clear, narrowest discount first. Illustrative estimates, not quotes."*
- Best-exit: *"Your most practically-liquid position is {fund} — about {clearing_prob}% likely to clear at a ~{discount}% discount, ≈{proceeds} expected proceeds."*
- Source chip hover-defs: `own prints` → *"estimated from this fund's own internal secondary prints."* `cohort prior` → *"this fund hasn't traded internally; estimated from its strategy/vintage cohort."* `default prior` → *"estimated from a frozen strategy prior — thin signal."*

**Narrowest-discount lens caption:** *"Sorted by smallest expected give-up to NAV — the positions that would clear at the narrowest discount."*

**In-demand-funds lens caption:** *"Funds with the strongest demand in the {window} secondary window. 'Consistently liquid' marks funds that cleared lots across consecutive windows. Aggregate, anonymised."*

**Sell-vs-hold card:**
- Title: **"Sell today vs hold."**
- Sell pane caption: *"Expected proceeds today, probability-weighted — what you'd net if it clears, scaled by how likely it is to clear."*
- Hold pane caption: *"Vintage-aware projected value of holding on, discounted to today at an illustrative cost of illiquidity ({rate}/yr). Based on the same projection used on the Projection view."*
- Lean chips: **"Holding leans richer"** (▲) / **"Selling leans richer"** (▼) / **"Roughly balanced"** (≈), with magnitude *"≈ {edge} {direction} to {hold/sell}."*
- **No-advice line (mandatory, every card):** *"Decision-support only — an illustrative comparison of two estimates, not advice or a recommendation to transact. Talk to your sales associate before acting."*
- Realized-excluded note: *"Projected future distributions only — distributions you've already received are excluded."*

**Buy-side region:**
- Title: **"Secondary buys that fit your gap."**
- Lead-in: *"You're {gap}pp under on {strategy}. These live secondary lots could help close it."*
- Card pill: *"Closes {gap}pp {strategy} underweight."*
- Subscribable-off: *"Not currently subscribable."*
- Do-nothing: *"No allocation gap detected — no buy-side matches surfaced. Your allocation is well-aligned."*
- CTA: **"Contact your sales associate."**
- Disclaimer: *"Information, not advice. Availability and price are subject to the live market."*

**States copy:** cold-start empty (`no positions to evaluate`): *"No positions to evaluate yet — your holdings will appear here once you hold fund positions."* No-NAV flag: *"No current NAV — not currently sellable."* Null-wealth: silently suppress the "% of wealth" caption. Unknown investor (404): *"We couldn't find this investor."* Engine-degraded: *"Showing the last available estimate."*

**Forbidden strings (acceptance, §11 / G-NOADVICE):** the surface must contain **no** "you should," "we recommend," "sell now," "buy now," or any imperative to transact. The verdict is always the noun `lean`, never a command.

---

### §9 Badge rendering — hold-value vintage-aware

The badges on this surface are the mechanism by which two foundation invariants — provenance and no-advice — become structural, and by which the **vintage-awareness of the hold leg is made visible** rather than hidden in the math. Four badge families render on S22:

**1. Vintage-phase badge (the load-bearing one — on every SellHoldCard hold pane).** The hold value is produced by T1-13's `project_holding_value` indexed by **fund age in quarters**, so the same wall-clock holding yields a *different* projected hold value depending on its vintage — and can flip the lean. The badge makes this visible. It binds `hold.vintage_phase ∈ {trough, recovery, harvest, late}` and renders as:
- **`trough`** (young lot at its J-curve bottom, e.g. a 2024 vintage at ~8q) — a "↗ early — steep projected lift" chip. Implication surfaced in the caption: a fund still climbing its J-curve projects a high hold value, tilting the lean toward hold; this is *why* a decent seller can still "hold lean richer."
- **`recovery`** — a "↗ recovering" chip; moderate projected lift.
- **`harvest`** — a "→ harvesting" chip; distributions flowing, modest remaining NAV lift.
- **`late`** (old lot, e.g. a 2018 vintage at ~32q) — a "→ late — little projected lift" chip; the hold value ≈ current NAV, so any modest secondary discount tilts the lean toward sell.
The badge sits on the hold pane next to the vintage year, with a hover-def: *"The projected hold value is vintage-aware — it depends on where this fund sits in its J-curve. A fund still early in its life projects more remaining lift than a late-life fund."* This badge is the visible face of the G3 gate; it must render on every sell-vs-hold card and must change with the vintage. **Two holdings identical except vintage must show different vintage-phase badges and can show opposite lean chips** — the FE must not collapse them to one styling.

**2. SyntheticTag.** Renders on every value in the synthetic-preview (state 6) and whenever `provenance.synthetic==true`; absent on live (`synthetic:false`). Amber "illustrative" pill (Part 2.2). On S22 it is especially load-bearing: in a sell-decision context, an un-tagged modelled discount would be actively misleading, so the tag is non-negotiable wherever the data is scaffold.

**3. `demand_source` provenance chip.** On every liquidity/discount row and on the sell-vs-hold sell pane: `own prints` (estimate is fund-specific) / `cohort prior` / `default prior` (estimate is borrowed). Styled progressively muted as the source gets thinner, with a hover-def. This is how the surface refuses to let a thin estimate masquerade as fund-specific.

**4. ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover.** The foundation pair on every number. The ConfidenceBadge on S22 carries `n_distinct_lots` and `shrink_w` ("estimated from N own prints · 30% own-data") so the user can see how trustworthy the estimate is. The ProvenancePopover carries the full method string — which by contract ends in "not advice" / "illustrative" — plus the macro caveat ("illustrative; not a quote or firm bid"), the snapshot id, and the feed version. The popover's presence of the no-advice method string is itself part of the no-advice enforcement.

**Badge ↔ no-advice coupling.** The lean chip (§4 SellHoldCard) is rendered with glyph + word + sign and is *never* an imperative — it is a badge, not a button. The no-advice line (§8) renders beneath every verdict. Together with the SyntheticTag and the "illustrative; not a quote" macro caveat in every popover, these badges are the structural guarantee that S22 presents decision-support, not advice.

---

### §10 Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive** (foundation Part 3, §3 above): ≥1280 three-region; 768–1279 stack the two top regions, buy-side 2-up, lens→dropdown, cohort→drawer; <768 single column with table-rows-as-cards, sell-vs-hold panes stacked with the lean chip between, buy-side 1-up. **No table or bar ever requires horizontal scroll** (Part 0.2 / Part 10) — the clearing-% bar, discount band, and proceeds always render inline; on narrow viewports the table collapses to cards rather than scrolling sideways. The sell-vs-hold side-by-side panes stack (never hide behind a toggle, even on mobile — the comparison that matters stays visible, Part 0.2).

**Accessibility (WCAG-AA, Part 10):**
- **Never colour-only.** The lean indicator pairs colour with a glyph (▲/▼/≈) and a word; the discount column is never red/green coded (it carries no semantic colour — discount is presented, not judged); the strategy colour chips also carry the strategy name as text. Over/under framing on the gap pill pairs colour with sign + word.
- **Tables behind a "view data" affordance.** The clearing-% bars and the demand leaderboard expose their underlying numbers as a focusable, screen-reader-readable data table (the bars are already number-labelled inline, but the "view data" affordance gives the full provenance fields).
- **Keyboard nav.** Full keyboard traversal: lens segmented control (arrow keys), table rows (tab + enter to focus a sell-vs-hold card), horizon stepper (arrow keys), buy-side cards (tab), every ProvenancePopover and SyntheticTag (focusable, dismissible with Esc). Active sort state is announced via `aria-sort` on column headers.
- **Focus states** on every interactive element (Part 2.6); the synthetic-preview toggle has a clear focus ring and an `aria-pressed` state.
- **ARIA.** The `not_connected` banner is an `role="status"` (polite), not an `alert` (it is informational, not an error). The lean chip has an `aria-label` spelling out the full "holding leans richer by approximately €311,000, illustrative, not advice." The vintage-phase badge has an `aria-label` describing the J-curve phase. Synthetic-preview values carry an `aria-label` prefix "illustrative."
- **Mono/tabular figures** for all numbers so columns align (Part 2.3); EU number/currency formatting (€, German formatting in DE locale, Part 10).
- **Motion** ≤150ms, no looping shimmer on the dashed placeholders (a one-time settle), no flashy transitions (Part 0.2 / 2.4); the horizon-stepper re-render animates the present-value bar value-in once.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

A reviewer can sign off S22 only when **all** of the following hold. The three starred items are the load-bearing gates for this surface.

- ★ **Renders `not_connected` cleanly by default.** With the feed unwired (`status=="not_connected"`), the surface shows the fully-designed dashed three-region preview, the "Live when the secondary feed connects" banner, the "built & validated — pending feed" footnote (from `ready`/`scaffold_validated`), and the synthetic-preview toggle — as a calm, finished pending state, **not an error and not an empty shell.** No live-data keys are expected or required to render.
- ★ **No-advice throughout.** No "you should," "we recommend," "sell now," "buy now," or any transactional imperative appears anywhere on the surface. The verdict is the noun `lean` (`hold leans richer` / `sell leans richer` / `roughly balanced`) rendered as a badge with glyph + word + sign, never a button. Every sell-vs-hold card and every ranking/buy-side region carries the no-advice line and a provenance method string ending in "not advice"/"illustrative." The only CTA is "Contact your sales associate"; there is no sell or buy execution affordance.
- ★ **The layout swap is data-only.** The `NotConnectedState` grid geometry is byte-identical to the live grid; flipping `status` from `not_connected` to `connected` (and binding the live arrays) changes only the data, never the layout. Switching the base URL from placeholder to live is the only change required to go live.
- **Liquidity ranking sorts most-likely-to-clear first** by `liquidity_score` (tie-break clearing-adjusted proceeds); the best-exit row carries the `★` pill and matches `best_exit`; the `Narrowest discount` lens re-sorts ascending by `expected_discount`; the `In-demand funds` lens re-binds to `demand_leaderboard` — all without a page change or scroll.
- **Bands, never bare points.** Every `expected_discount` shows its `discount_band [lo,hi]`; proceeds carry the `proceeds_band_eur` in the popover; the sell-vs-hold hold leg is visibly a projection (ForecastBand sparkline + "base (T1-13)" caption). No discount or proceeds figure renders as a lone scalar (G2).
- **Hold-value vintage-aware (G3) is visible.** Every SellHoldCard shows a vintage-phase badge bound to `hold.vintage_phase`; two holdings identical except vintage render different vintage-phase badges and can show opposite lean chips; the realized-distributions-excluded note is present on every hold pane.
- **Provenance & confidence on every number.** Every numeric leaf renders ConfidenceBadge (`n_distinct_lots`/`shrink_w`) + ProvenancePopover; the `demand_source` chip (`own prints`/`cohort prior`/`default prior`) renders on every row and sell pane; a thin estimate never masquerades as fund-specific.
- **k-anon naming.** Funds with `fund_id:null` render as "a {strategy} fund," never named, in every region (rankings, leaderboard, sell-vs-hold, buy-side).
- **Synthetic-preview is unmistakable.** The toggle fills placeholders with fixture numbers each wearing a SyntheticTag, under a surface banner declaring "not live market data"; the live route never shows `synthetic:true`; in a sell-decision context no modelled number ever renders untagged.
- **All seven states render** per §7 (not_connected default, happy, thin-demand, null-wealth, no-NAV, synthetic-preview/scaffold, error), each as a designed state — never blank, never a fabricated number; the inline "not a holding" error stays per-card, not global.
- **Buy-side fits the gap, subscribable-gated.** Buy-side cards show only `strategy in gap`, ranked by attractiveness, with the fills-gap pill, discount badge, and a CTA enabled only when `subscribable:true`; the do-nothing state renders when there is no gap.
- **Mode scoping.** Investor/Advisor mode shows per-holding regions for You/impersonated client; Internal mode shows only the aggregate demand leaderboard and suppresses per-holding regions.
- **Responsive + a11y.** No table/bar needs horizontal scroll at any breakpoint; sell-vs-hold panes stay side-by-side-then-stacked, never behind a toggle; the not_connected banner is `role="status"` not `alert`; full keyboard nav, focus states, `aria-sort`, mono figures, EU/DE number formatting, no colour-only signals, motion ≤150ms.
- **Chart language.** Only approved Part 2.5 charts (thin horizontal clearing bars with inline %, ForecastBand reuse, FundCards, leaderboard bars); no rejected type; a 1–2 sentence caption under every region; "Peers" never appears (S22 has no peer comparison — ComparisonSelector is intentionally absent).
- **Placeholder data matches the Part 0.3 / §5 shapes** so the live swap is a base-URL change; EUR monetary leaves are integers (G9), EU-formatted.


---

## Surface S23 — Sentiment & Intent (Internal)

> **Frontend surface spec for Claude Design.** This is the deep build brief for one surface of the Alpha copilot: **S23 — Sentiment & Intent** (use case **T1-10**, *the conversational-signal console*). It composes from the design system, the seven-state model, and the components defined in the **FRONTEND FOUNDATION** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md` — Parts 2, 3, 8, 9). It does **not** redefine the ProfileBar, CohortBuilder, AskAlphaPane, HeatMap, MetaCard, ProvenancePopover, ConfidenceBadge, SyntheticTag, GatedState, RelaxBanner, **NotConnectedState**, or the token sets — it references them. It binds to the backend contract `GET /api/sentiment` (T1-10).
>
> **S23 is a signal-gated, Internal-mode-only console.** Three things set it apart from the analytical investor surfaces (S1–S17): (1) its data class — call/advisory transcripts, question logs, engagement telemetry — is **not in the 13-file real export**, so the live endpoint returns `{status:"not_connected"}` by default and the *shipping* state is a fully-designed `NotConnectedState` preview, not a populated dashboard; (2) it is **aggregate-only and role-gated** — Internal mode, visible only to staff with the internal-console role, and *by construction never drills to a single identifiable client*; (3) it is **consent-gated** — every figure is computed over consent-eligible events only (GDPR Art.6 `granted | legitimate_interest`), behind a strict k-anonymity floor of **N_MIN = 20** (stricter than the ≥10 used elsewhere, because sentiment is sensitive). The frontend's job is to make these three constraints *visible and load-bearing* — the surface reads as trustworthy precisely because it refuses to show too much.
>
> **The design promise: the swap is purely data.** S23 is built once, in full, as the live layout. The `NotConnectedState` default is that exact layout behind a dashed, greyed wrapper with a `synthetic-preview` toggle. When `ALPHA_FEED_CONVERSATION` and `ALPHA_FEED_ENGAGEMENT` connect, the engine flips `status` to `connected` and the same components bind to real figures — **zero layout refactor, zero new components**. This brief specifies *both* in equal depth, because both ship.

---

### §1 — Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** S23 answers what Moonfare's investor base is *thinking, worrying about, and preparing to do before they act* — derived from conversations (calls, advisory notes, chat) and engagement telemetry (page views, downloads, calculator and secondary-marketplace use), aggregated to **cohort** sentiment and intent. It is the firm's early-warning and mood instrument: are investors leaning in or pulling back, what concerns dominate right now, which segments diverge, and what demand signal is rising versus the trailing window. It is an *internal* instrument for sales, product, and the investment committee — never a client-facing screen, never a per-person view.

This is the one place in the product where the displayed quantity is *people's expressed feelings and intentions*, not their portfolio, raising the sensitivity bar in every direction: privacy (PII redacted upstream, before any scoring), consent (denied/unknown events never counted), re-identification (k-anon floor 20, not 10), and tone (descriptive and cautious, never gossipy or predictive-about-an-individual). The frontend makes the **aggregate-only** and **consent-basis** facts structural, not footnotes — they ride on every card as badges, like every analytical number rides its ConfidenceBadge.

**User questions this surface answers (mapped to the engine's eval ids T1-10 §1/§9):**
- **Q1 — "Are investors currently leaning in or pulling back?"** → the **Mode Mix board** (buying / waiting / selling / neutral share) + the headline `dominant` mode and `caution_index`.
- **Q2 — "What concerns dominate client conversations now?"** → the **Concern Heatmap** (concern theme × segment), top-k ranked.
- **Q3 — "Are people planning to shift toward semi-liquids or secondaries?"** → the **Reallocation Intent** vector (directional pull bars: semiliquid / secondaries / credit / liquidity / opportunity).
- **Q4 — "How is sentiment among PE professionals different from general HNWI clients?"** → a **segment-delta** read of the heatmap and mode boards filtered `profession=Investor/PE` vs `wealth=HNW`.
- **Q5 — "Are my peers in Germany more cautious than similar investors in the US?"** → segment-delta, `region=DACH` vs `region=US/CA`, comparing `caution_index`.
- **Q6 — "Is liquidity-seeking rising vs last window?"** → the **Demand Early-Warning** cards (`rising` flag on `delta` vs the trailing window).

**What this surface is NOT.** It is not a client list, a CRM screen, or a transcript viewer — no row, hover, tooltip, popover, export, or drill anywhere on S23 ever resolves to a single investor, a single conversation, or any free text from a conversation. It is not an investor-mode surface — it does not appear in the Investor or Advisor rails at all. It is not a predictor of an individual's behaviour — it reports cohort distributions with confidence intervals. It does not let anyone transact. It is, deliberately, a *less* capable surface than its data could power, and that restraint is the feature.

---

### §2 — Entry & navigation (Internal mode only; role-gated)

**2.1 Mode gating.** S23 exists only when the global mode (Part 3 mode switch: *Investor · Advisor · Internal · Demo*) is **Internal**, and only for a session carrying the internal-console role. In Investor and Advisor modes the rail's *Consoles* group is **absent entirely** — not greyed, not locked: it does not render, and no deep-link, suggested prompt, or Ask Alpha answer in those modes can route here. This is a navigation-level guarantee complementing the backend's role check; the frontend never *displays* the console to a non-internal viewer and so never relies on copy to refuse it.

**2.2 Role gate within Internal mode.** Internal mode itself is staff-only, but the *console* role is a finer grant. If an Internal-mode user lacks the console role, the rail shows the **Consoles** group but S23 renders a `GatedState` (Part 2.6) with internal-appropriate copy — **not** the investor "paid viewers" string. Copy: *"Sentiment & Intent is restricted to the internal-console role. Request access from your admin."* No data, no preview, no synthetic toggle is reachable in this state.

**2.3 Rail placement.** Within Internal mode, S23 sits in the rail's **Consoles** group (Part 3 rail grouping: *…Macro · (Advisor) Cockpit · (Internal) Consoles*), alongside **S21 Demand** and **S22 Secondary** — the three signal-gated internal consoles. Rail label: **"Sentiment & Intent."** A small **signal-pending dot** (a hollow `--not-connected` ring) sits on the rail label whenever the surface's `status` is `not_connected`, so an internal user can see at a glance, from the rail, which consoles are still awaiting their feed. The dot fills solid `--brand` when the feed connects.

**2.4 Entry behaviour.** Selecting the rail item opens S23 full-screen in the centre stage, the persistent frame (ProfileBar — here labelled with the internal user and the active cohort scope, not "You"; CohortBuilder; Ask Alpha; PDF export) wrapping it per Part 3. No analytical surface deep-links *into* S23 (it is not part of the investor decision chain). Ask Alpha, in Internal mode, may answer aggregate sentiment questions and offer a chip "Open Sentiment & Intent" that routes here; in Investor/Advisor mode that chip is never generated.

**2.5 No mobile entry.** S23 is a desktop/large-tablet internal tool. On `<768px` it does not collapse to a usable analytical view — it renders a single-card notice *"Open Sentiment & Intent on a larger screen"* (see §10). It is never a phone surface.

---

### §3 — Layout

S23 is a single full-screen console with a fixed header band and a four-region body, the same skeleton whether the body is **live** or rendered in **NotConnectedState**. The body regions, in reading order, are: **(A) Mode Mix board**, **(B) Concern Heatmap**, **(C) Segment sentiment board**, **(D) Reallocation Intent + Demand Early-Warnings**. Crucially: **the default shipping state is the not-connected preview** — the entire layout below is what renders behind the dashed wrapper, greyed and non-interactive, with illustrative placeholder geometry, the moment `status === "not_connected"` (which is *now*, until the feeds wire).

**3.0 Header band (always present).** Pinned under the ProfileBar:
- Title **"Sentiment & Intent"** (display), subtitle (caption) *"What the investor base is thinking and preparing to do — aggregate only."*
- A persistent **AggregateBadge** (§9): a small lock-shield chip reading **"Aggregate only · k-anon ≥ 20"** — present in *every* state, including not-connected. It signals the surface's fundamental contract before any data appears.
- A persistent **ConsentBadge** (§9): **"Consent-eligible only · GDPR Art.6"** with a popover (§8 copy).
- The **window selector** (segmented control: `30d · 90d (default) · 180d`) → drives `window_days`.
- A **status pill**: `Signal pending` (amber-grey, not-connected) or `Live · snapshot {id}` (mint, connected).
- The **CohortScope** readout — the active cohort `n` (when live) and the resolved segment filters as chips (e.g. `region: DACH · wealth: HNW`). When not-connected, it reads *"No cohort — feed not connected."*

**3.A Mode Mix board (top-left).** Answers Q1. A **100% stacked horizontal bar** (Part 2.5) of `mode_mix` (`buying / waiting / selling / neutral`), % inline, fixed mode→colour map (buying brand-tint, waiting grey, selling `--negative`-leaning, neutral `--muted`). Above it: the **`dominant` mode** in h1 (*"Dominant mode: Waiting"*) and the **`caution_index`** as a −1…+1 divergence gauge (centre-0 bar, `--under` cautious / `--over` bullish — ZChart grammar, not a new chart). CaptionBlock + embedded ConfidenceBadge (`n`+`ci95` from `sentiment`). In segment-comparison mode (§6) it splits into **two side-by-side stacked bars** with a delta caption.

**3.B Concern Heatmap (top-right).** Answers Q2. A **HeatMap** (theme rows × segment columns), cells coloured by `score` on the `--data-*` ramp with the score printed in-cell. Rows = top-8 ranked concern tags from `concern_heatmap` (the frozen 12-tag `CONCERN_VOCAB`: fees, illiquidity, macro_rates, recession_risk, gp_quality, vintage_timing, denominator_effect, fx_risk, tax, regulation, performance_lag, capital_calls). Columns = the chosen segment dimension (default single "All (cohort)" column; switchable to region / risk / profession / competence). Each column header carries its per-segment `n` and a **column-level k-anon guard**: any column with eligible `n < 20` collapses to a greyed locked "·" cell with the label struck — *the heatmap never paints a sub-20 segment*. CaptionBlock names the top concern.

**3.C Segment sentiment board (bottom-left).** Answers Q4/Q5 — sentiment and caution *by segment*. A small-multiple **Scorecard** grid: one card per segment value of the chosen dimension, each showing `sentiment.value` (sign+glyph, never colour-only), `caution_index`, `n`, and a CI bar. Dimension set by a **segment-dimension selector** (`Region · Risk class · Profession · Competence`). Sub-N_MIN segments render as **locked cards** (lock glyph + *"Segment too small to report"*), never a zero or a faint number. This is where Q4 (PE pros vs HNWI) and Q5 (DACH vs US) read directly. A two-card **pinned comparison** (§6) yields an explicit delta readout.

**3.D Reallocation Intent + Demand Early-Warnings (bottom-right).** Two stacked sub-panels:
- **Reallocation Intent** (Q3): **horizontal bars** (BarRow grammar), one per `reallocation_intent` direction (`semiliquid · secondaries · credit · liquidity · opportunity`), each 0…1, % inline, strongest pull emphasised and named in a CaptionBlock (*"Strongest pull: toward secondaries (41%)"*). Fixed direction→colour map.
- **Demand Early-Warnings** (Q6): a list of **EarlyWarningCard**s (§4), one per `early_warning[]` tag — tag, `score_now`, `delta` vs the trailing window (▲/▼ + sign), and an amber "Rising" pill *only* when `rising === true`. Non-rising items sit below a hairline, greyed, for context — never alarmist. CaptionBlock summarises.

**3.E NotConnectedState — the default body.** Until the feeds connect, regions A–D render **inside a single NotConnectedState wrapper** (Part 2.6): a dashed `--not-connected` outline around the whole four-region body, the regions greyed to ~40% with their *real layout geometry intact* (placeholder stacked bar, placeholder heatmap grid, placeholder scorecards, placeholder warning cards — illustrative shapes, no real or fabricated numbers presented as truth). Centred over the dimmed body, a **NotConnectedPanel**: the headline *"Live when the conversation & engagement feeds connect,"* a one-line explanation, the list of awaited feeds (`ALPHA_FEED_CONVERSATION`, `ALPHA_FEED_ENGAGEMENT`), and the **synthetic-preview toggle** (§6.4). The header band (3.0) stays fully live and un-dimmed in this state — its badges, window selector, and status pill are real chrome, not preview. This is the surface's *shipping* appearance.

**3.F Responsive.** `≥1280px`: 2×2 region grid, equal weight, no scroll, frame three-column per Part 3. `768–1279px`: regions stack to a single column in reading order A→B→C→D, the CohortBuilder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher (Part 3); heatmap columns reduce to the cohort-level + top-2 segments with a "more segments" expander; no chart horizontally scrolls. `<768px`: the §10 large-screen notice only.

---

### §4 — Components & charts

S23 introduces **no new chart types** — it composes entirely from the approved Part 2.5 set and the Part 2.6 library. Three small composite components are specified here as compositions, not new primitives.

**4.1 ModeMixBar** *(100% stacked bar + ZChart caution gauge).* Bar binds `mode_mix.{buying,waiting,selling,neutral}`; gauge binds `caution_index`. Fixed mode→colour map declared once in tokens. Embeds ConfidenceBadge (`sentiment.{n,ci95}`) and ProvenancePopover. Hover → mode label + %; the popover never reveals more than the aggregate share.

**4.2 ConcernHeatMap** *(Part 2.6 HeatMap, configured).* Rows = ranked concern tags; columns = segment values (or single "All"). Cell = `score` on `--data-*` ramp, printed in-cell (colour never the sole carrier). **Column k-anon guard built in:** a column with `n < 20` renders struck/locked and *paints no cells* (§7, §9). Cells click only to a ProvenancePopover (method + segment `n` + `ci95`) — never an event, never a person.

**4.3 SegmentScorecard** *(Part 2.6 Scorecard small-multiples).* One card per segment: `sentiment.value` (sign+glyph), `caution_index`, `n`, CI bar. Locked-card variant for sub-N_MIN segments. Two-card **pinned comparison** with an explicit delta line (*"DACH is +0.12 more cautious than US/CA (n 41 vs 38)"*), greyed + *"not significant"* when CIs overlap.

**4.4 ReallocationBars** *(BarRow set).* One bar per `reallocation_intent` direction, 0…1, % inline, fixed colour, strongest pull emphasised, CaptionBlock + ProvenancePopover.

**4.5 EarlyWarningCard** *(new small composite).* Tag label humanised from the controlled vocab (*seek_liquidity → "Liquidity-seeking"*), `score_now`, `delta` (▲/▼ + sign), and a **Rising pill** (labelled "Rising," distinct from the synthetic tag) shown only when `rising === true`. Carries ConfidenceBadge (`n`) and ProvenancePopover (*"current window vs equal trailing window; rising = delta > 0.10 and in top-8"*). Non-rising items render greyed below a hairline.

**4.6 NotConnectedState wrapper** *(Part 2.6 component).* Dashed `--not-connected` outline; the four real regions dimmed to ~40% behind it; centred NotConnectedPanel with the awaited-feed list and synthetic-preview toggle. No fabricated numbers render as truth; placeholder geometry is visibly illustrative (no badges on dimmed shapes). It is the surface's default; live regions are revealed by simply removing the wrapper when `status === "connected"`.

**4.7 Header chrome.** AggregateBadge, ConsentBadge, window selector, status pill, CohortScope readout — per §3.0/§9, never dimmed in any state.

**Chart-language compliance.** Only Part 2.5 approved charts appear: 100% stacked horizontal bar (mode mix), divergence/Z grammar (caution gauge), heat-map (concerns), peer-scorecard small-multiples (segment sentiment), horizontal bars (reallocation). No rejected chart (radar, sankey/flow, beeswarm, ridgeline, etc.) appears. Every chart carries a 1–2 sentence caption; titles are h2 minimum; numbers are mono/tabular; % is inline.

---

### §5 — Data bindings (← `GET /api/sentiment`)

All of S23 binds to a single call: **`GET /api/sentiment?filters={json?}&window_days={int=90}`** (T1-10 §5). The `filters` JSON carries the cohort/segment selection (resolved server-side by PART 3 `resolve_cohort`); `window_days` from the header window selector. The frontend issues exactly one call per state of the controls and renders whatever `status` comes back — it never branches on a guessed default.

**5.1 Not-connected default → `200`** (the *current, shipping* response):
```jsonc
{ "status":"not_connected",
  "reason":"ALPHA_FEED_CONVERSATION / ALPHA_FEED_ENGAGEMENT not set",
  "ready":true, "scaffold_validated":true,
  "provenance":{"snapshot_id":"synthetic-2026-06-14","method":"adapter-gated; no live signal"} }
```
→ Renders §3.E. The header chrome reads `Signal pending`. **No `mode_mix` / `sentiment` keys are present**, and the frontend asserts their absence rather than zero-filling.

**5.2 Live → `200`** (when both feeds wire; same code path, no refactor):
```jsonc
{ "status":"connected",
  "cohort":{"n":22,"strict_n":24,"relaxed_dims":[],"gated":false,"headline_total":2000},
  "mode_mix":{"buying":0.27,"waiting":0.46,"selling":0.12,"neutral":0.15,"dominant":"waiting"},
  "sentiment":{"value":-0.08,"ci95":[-0.17,0.01],"n":22},
  "caution_index":0.31,
  "concern_heatmap":[{"tag":"macro_rates","score":0.38},{"tag":"illiquidity","score":0.22},{"tag":"fees","score":0.14}],
  "top_questions":[{"tag":"when_distributions","count":19},{"tag":"how_secondaries_work","count":11}],
  "reallocation_intent":{"semiliquid":0.31,"secondaries":0.41,"credit":0.12,"liquidity":0.18,"opportunity":0.22},
  "early_warning":[{"tag":"seek_liquidity","score_now":0.27,"delta":0.13,"rising":true}],
  "provenance":{"method":"EWMA sentiment + weighted mode mix over consent-eligible contexts","n":22,
                "ci95":[-0.17,0.01],"synthetic":false,"consent_basis":"granted|legitimate_interest only",
                "snapshot_id":"real-2026-06-12"} }
```

**5.3 Element ← field map.**
| Element | ← field |
|---|---|
| Header status pill | `status` + `provenance.snapshot_id` |
| CohortScope `n` / total | `cohort.n` · `cohort.headline_total` · `cohort.relaxed_dims` (→ RelaxBanner) |
| ModeMixBar segments | `mode_mix.{buying,waiting,selling,neutral}` |
| Dominant headline | `mode_mix.dominant` |
| Caution gauge | `caution_index` |
| ModeMix ConfidenceBadge | `sentiment.{n,ci95}` |
| ConcernHeatMap cells | `concern_heatmap[].{tag,score}` (segment columns via per-segment `filters` calls) |
| SegmentScorecard cards | per-segment `aggregate_segment` results (`sentiment`, `caution_index`, `n`) |
| ReallocationBars | `reallocation_intent.{semiliquid,secondaries,credit,liquidity,opportunity}` |
| EarlyWarningCard | `early_warning[].{tag,score_now,delta,rising}` |
| All ProvenancePopovers | `provenance.{method,n,ci95,synthetic,consent_basis,snapshot_id}` |
| ConsentBadge | `provenance.consent_basis` |
| SyntheticTag (preview) | `provenance.synthetic` |

**5.4 Segment columns/cards are separate resolved cohorts.** Each heatmap column and each scorecard card is the result of `aggregate_segment(filters)` for that segment's `filters` (e.g. `{profession:['Investor/PE']}`). The frontend may fetch them per-segment or receive a batched payload; either way each carries its own `n` and is independently k-anon-guarded. **No element ever binds to anything below the segment-aggregate level** — there is no investor-level binding in this surface by design.

**5.5 Gated / consent-empty payloads** (see §7) bind to the appropriate guard render, never to a number.

---

### §6 — Interactions

**6.1 Window selector.** `30d · 90d · 180d` → sets `window_days`, re-issues `/api/sentiment`, re-renders all regions with a calm skeleton during fetch (Part 9). The selection is UI state (remembered per session). Changing the window also recomputes the early-warning trailing comparison server-side.

**6.2 Segment-dimension selector.** A segmented control (`Region · Risk class · Profession · Competence`) that sets the breakdown dimension for the Concern Heatmap columns **and** the Segment Scorecard cards simultaneously (they share one dimension so the console reads coherently). Switching re-issues per-segment aggregates. Default is the cohort-level single column / single card until a dimension is chosen. This is the control that powers Q4 and Q5 (pick Profession, read the PE-vs-HNWI delta; pick Region, read DACH-vs-US).

**6.3 Cohort/segment filters (CohortBuilder).** The shared CohortBuilder (Part 6) sets the base `filters` for the whole console (e.g. scope to `wealth: HNW`). Within-dimension selections widen (UNION), across-dimension narrow (INTERSECT), with the live `n` ticking per Part 6. When the backend relaxes the cohort, the **RelaxBanner** renders in words from `cohort.relaxed_dims`. **Pinned comparison:** an internal user may pin two segment cards (§4.3) to get an explicit delta line; the delta greys and reads "not significant" when CIs overlap.

**6.4 Synthetic-preview toggle (not-connected only).** Inside the NotConnectedPanel, a toggle **"Show synthetic preview"** (default OFF). When ON, the dimmed body fills with the **fixture** figures (the T1-10 §3 worked example — `mode_mix.waiting=0.46`, `caution_index=0.31`, top concern `macro_rates`, strongest pull `secondaries=0.41`, rising `seek_liquidity`), rendered through the *real* live components so a sponsor can walk the layout. In this mode **every region carries a prominent SyntheticTag** ("Illustrative — fixture data, not real investors") and the pill reads `Preview (synthetic)`. The toggle exists *only* in the not-connected state; it is impossible to reach when live. Turning it OFF restores the empty preview. The fixture always announces itself (§9; T1-10 §8 "synthetic = scaffold, not truth").

**6.5 Provenance everywhere.** Every number is a clickable chip → ProvenancePopover showing **method**, **n**, **ci95**, **consent_basis** (*"granted | legitimate_interest only"*), **synthetic**, and **snapshot_id**, plus one mandatory S23-only line: **"Aggregate over ≥20 consent-eligible investors. No individual data shown."** A number cannot render without it.

**6.6 No drill-down — by construction.** No interaction on S23 navigates toward an individual: no row opens a person, no cell opens a conversation, no card opens a client, no hover reveals a name or transcript snippet, no export contains per-investor rows. Hover/focus reveals only the aggregate value and its segment `n` (§11). The most granular thing any interaction yields is a segment aggregate of n≥20.

**6.7 PDF export.** The Part 3 PDF export captures the current console view (header badges included) as an aggregate report. The export carries the AggregateBadge, ConsentBadge, snapshot id, and — if synthetic-preview was on — the SyntheticTag and a "PREVIEW — illustrative" watermark, so an exported preview can never be mistaken for a real readout.

---

### §7 — Seven states (not_connected is the default; aggregate-only throughout)

S23 renders all seven foundation states (Part 9), but its **default and primary state is not_connected**, and several states carry extra guards because of the sensitivity of the data. No state ever resolves below the segment aggregate.

1. **not_connected (DEFAULT — the shipping state).** `status==="not_connected"`. Renders §3.E: the full four-region layout inside the dashed NotConnectedState wrapper, dimmed, header chrome live, NotConnectedPanel centred with the awaited-feed list and the synthetic-preview toggle. Status pill `Signal pending`; rail dot hollow. This is what an internal user sees today. It is a *designed* state, never an error or a blank.

2. **Happy (connected, cohort ≥ 20 eligible).** `status==="connected"`, `cohort.gated===false`, `n≥20`. The live layout (§3.A–D) fully populated, every number badged, status pill `Live · snapshot {id}`, rail dot solid.

3. **Gated / thin (k-anon).** `gated===true, reason==="k_anon"`, `n<20`. The cohort (or a chosen segment column/card) is too small to report without re-identification risk. Render the **k-anon guard**, not a number: header-level → a centred GatedState card *"Segment too small to report sentiment without re-identification risk (n {n} < 20)."*; segment-level (a single heatmap column or scorecard card) → that column struck/locked and that card a locked miniature, the rest of the console unaffected. If `resolve_cohort` relaxed dimensions to reach 20, the RelaxBanner states it in words.

4. **Low coverage.** Feed connected but fewer than N_MIN of the resolved cohort have *any* context row (`reason==="low_coverage"`). Render a coverage-style guard: *"Too few investors in this cohort have recent conversation or engagement signal to report (coverage below threshold)."* Distinct copy from k-anon (it is a data-density problem, not a privacy floor), but the same no-number outcome.

5. **Consent-empty.** Events exist but all are `denied/unknown` (`reason==="no_consent_eligible_signal", n:0`). Render a consent guard tied to the ConsentBadge: *"No consent-eligible signal in this cohort. Only investors with a documented lawful basis (consent or legitimate interest) are included."* This state visibly demonstrates the consent gate working — it is reassuring, not a failure.

6. **Scaffold-only (synthetic preview).** Reachable only via the §6.4 toggle in the not-connected state. The fixture renders through the live components, every region tagged SyntheticTag, status pill `Preview (synthetic)`, `provenance.synthetic===true`. **Never reachable on the live route** — the live route never returns `synthetic:true`.

7. **Error / degraded.** Engine/feed read error, or a `404` for an unknown anchor investor referenced in `filters`. Render the last-good cached console behind a quiet *"as of {snapshot}"* banner if a cache exists, otherwise the not_connected default with an *"unable to reach the sentiment engine"* note. Never a blank, never a fabricated number, never a partial drill. Missing/relaxable dims are *not* errors (they relax per state 3).

**Cross-cutting:** in *every* state the AggregateBadge and ConsentBadge are present; not-significant segment deltas render greyed/"not significant" (Part 9); loading is a calm skeleton, not a spinner.

---

### §8 — Copy deck

All copy is structured for English + German (Part 10 i18n); the strings below are the English source, never hard-coded inline.

**Header.**
- Title: **"Sentiment & Intent"**
- Subtitle: *"What the investor base is thinking and preparing to do — aggregate only."*
- Status pill: `Signal pending` · `Live · snapshot {id}` · `Preview (synthetic)`
- CohortScope (live): *"{n} investors · of {total} · {segment chips}"*
- CohortScope (not-connected): *"No cohort — feed not connected."*

**Badges (always present).**
- AggregateBadge: **"Aggregate only · k-anon ≥ 20"**
- ConsentBadge: **"Consent-eligible only · GDPR Art.6"**
