- ConsentBadge popover (GDPR/consent note): *"This console only counts conversations and engagement signals where the investor has a documented lawful basis under GDPR Art.6 — explicit consent or legitimate interest. Denied and unknown-consent signals are never processed or counted. All personal identifiers are removed before any analysis, and every figure is an aggregate over at least 20 investors. No individual conversation, transcript, or client is ever shown."*

**Region captions (live examples — bind to data, shown here illustratively).**
- Mode Mix: *"The cohort is mostly in a waiting posture (46%); buying is 27%. Caution index +0.31 — mildly cautious."*
- Concern Heatmap: *"Macro & rates is the dominant concern this window, ahead of illiquidity and fees."*
- Segment sentiment: *"Sentiment is comparable across regions; DACH reads slightly more cautious than US/CA, within the confidence interval."*
- Reallocation: *"Strongest reallocation pull is toward secondaries (41%), then semi-liquids (31%)."*
- Early-warning: *"One rising demand signal this window: liquidity-seeking (+0.13 vs the prior window)."*

**Not-connected (default).**
- NotConnectedPanel headline: **"Live when the conversation & engagement feeds connect."**
- Body: *"This console reads cohort sentiment and intent from advisory conversations and product engagement. Those feeds are not yet connected. The layout below is the live design — it will fill with real, aggregate figures the moment the feeds wire."*
- Awaited feeds line: *"Awaiting: ALPHA_FEED_CONVERSATION · ALPHA_FEED_ENGAGEMENT"*
- Synthetic toggle label: **"Show synthetic preview"** · helper: *"Illustrative fixture data for a layout walkthrough — not real investors."*

**Guard states.**
- k-anon (header): *"Segment too small to report sentiment without re-identification risk (n {n} < 20)."*
- k-anon (segment): *"Too small to report."*
- Low coverage: *"Too few investors in this cohort have recent signal to report."*
- Consent-empty: *"No consent-eligible signal in this cohort. Only investors with a documented lawful basis are included."*
- Role gate (no console role): *"Sentiment & Intent is restricted to the internal-console role. Request access from your admin."*
- Large-screen notice: *"Open Sentiment & Intent on a larger screen."*

**Provenance popover (mandatory extra line):** *"Aggregate over ≥20 consent-eligible investors. No individual data shown."*

**Tone rules.** Descriptive, never predictive-about-a-person; cautious, never gossipy; "the cohort," "investors in this segment," never "they want to" about an individual. Caution index is *described* ("mildly cautious"), never used as advice. No imperative verbs.

---

### §9 — Badge rendering (aggregate k-anon; synthetic; consent)

S23 carries the foundation's badge contract (Part 0.2: a number never renders bare) plus two console-specific persistent badges. Badge rules, exhaustively:

**9.1 AggregateBadge (S23-specific, persistent).** A small lock-shield chip **"Aggregate only · k-anon ≥ 20"** pinned in the header in *every* state. It is the surface's identity badge — it declares the contract before any data loads, so even the not-connected default carries it. Its popover restates §6.5's mandatory line. It never disappears.

**9.2 k-anon rendering (the floor is 20).** Wherever an aggregate is below the **N_MIN = 20** floor:
- A **cohort-level** sub-20 → the §7 state-3 GatedState card (no chart).
- A **segment column** (heatmap) sub-20 → the column header struck through, a single locked "·" cell, *no scores painted in that column.*
- A **segment card** (scorecard) sub-20 → a locked-card miniature (lock glyph + "Too small to report"), no number.
- The floor (20) is shown in the guard copy so the rule is legible, not mysterious.
This is stricter than the product-wide ≥10 k-anon and the copy says so where relevant.

**9.3 ConsentBadge (S23-specific, persistent).** **"Consent-eligible only · GDPR Art.6"** in the header, every state, with the §8 GDPR popover. When `provenance.consent_basis` is present it confirms *"granted | legitimate interest only."* In the consent-empty state (§7.5) the badge is the anchor of the guard message.

**9.4 SyntheticTag (synthetic-preview only).** When and only when `provenance.synthetic === true` (i.e. the §6.4 fixture preview), every region carries the amber SyntheticTag **"Illustrative — fixture data, not real investors,"** the status pill reads `Preview (synthetic)`, and the PDF export watermarks "PREVIEW." On the live route (`synthetic:false`) no SyntheticTag ever appears — the live numbers are real or absent, never illustrative. This enforces T1-10 §8: "scaffold never masquerades as real."

**9.5 ConfidenceBadge.** Every aggregate number embeds its `n` + `ci95` chip (Part 2.6); deltas whose CIs overlap render greyed + "not significant" (Part 9). Mode-mix and segment-sentiment cards bind their badge from `sentiment.{n,ci95}`.

**9.6 ProvenancePopover.** On every number (§6.5), carrying method, n, ci95, consent_basis, synthetic, snapshot_id, and the mandatory aggregate-only line. No popover ever exposes an event, a transcript, or a person.

**9.7 RelaxBanner.** When `cohort.relaxed_dims` is non-empty, the banner states the relaxation in words (Part 6), e.g. *"Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band to reach a reportable group of 20."*

---

### §10 — Responsive + accessibility

**Responsive (Part 3 breakpoints).**
- **≥1280px:** 2×2 region grid, equal weight, no scroll; three-column frame; header band full-width.
- **768–1279px:** regions stack to a single column A→B→C→D; CohortBuilder → drawer, Ask Alpha → launcher; Concern Heatmap shows cohort + top-2 segment columns with a "more segments" expander; Segment Scorecard wraps to 2-up; no chart horizontally scrolls.
- **<768px:** S23 does **not** render an analytical view — it shows only the §8 large-screen notice. This is intentional: a sensitive internal console is not a phone surface.

**Accessibility (Part 10, WCAG-AA).**
- **Never colour-only:** mode-mix segments, sentiment sign, caution direction, and over/under all pair colour with a glyph/sign (▲/▼, +/−) and an in-element label; the heatmap prints the score in every cell so colour is never the sole carrier.
- **Contrast:** all tokens meet AA in light and dark; the dimmed not-connected body keeps the NotConnectedPanel text at full-contrast (the dim affects only the illustrative geometry behind it).
- **Keyboard:** full keyboard nav across window selector, segment-dimension selector, segment pins, synthetic toggle, every provenance chip; visible focus states; logical tab order header → A → B → C → D.
- **Screen reader:** each chart exposes its data as a table behind a "view data" affordance (Part 10) — that table is *also* aggregate-only and k-anon-guarded (it can never expose a sub-20 segment or a person). ARIA roles on all interactive controls; the AggregateBadge and ConsentBadge are announced as persistent landmarks so a screen-reader user hears the contract.
- **Motion:** value-in animates once, ≤150ms, then settles; no parallax/bounce (Part 2.4). Loading is a calm skeleton.
- **i18n:** all strings externalised for English + German; numbers/percentages in EU formatting; the caution index and CI render with localized decimal separators.

---

### §11 — Acceptance checklist

**Role & mode gating.**
- [ ] S23 renders **only** in Internal mode; the *Consoles* rail group does not exist in Investor or Advisor mode (absent, not greyed), and no deep-link, prompt, or Ask Alpha answer can route there from those modes.
- [ ] An Internal-mode user **without** the console role gets the internal-role GatedState (not the investor "paid viewers" copy) — no data, no preview, no synthetic toggle reachable.
- [ ] Rail signal-pending dot is hollow when `not_connected`, solid when `connected`.

**Not-connected default (must render cleanly, never as an error).**
- [ ] On a clean env, `GET /api/sentiment` returns `status:"not_connected"` and S23 renders the full §3.E NotConnectedState: dashed wrapper, dimmed real layout, live header chrome, NotConnectedPanel with awaited-feed list and synthetic toggle.
- [ ] No `mode_mix`/`sentiment` numbers appear in this state; nothing is zero-filled; the surface reads as designed-pending, not broken.
- [ ] The status pill reads `Signal pending`; AggregateBadge and ConsentBadge are present.

**Live swap (zero refactor).**
- [ ] With the feeds connected (or monkeypatched), the *same components* bind to the §5.2 live payload — no new component, no layout change; status pill flips to `Live · snapshot {id}`.

**Aggregate-only — never drill to a person (load-bearing).**
- [ ] No interaction anywhere (hover, click, focus, popover, export, "view data" table) resolves to a single investor, a single conversation, or any transcript text.
- [ ] The most granular thing any element binds to is a segment aggregate of n≥20; there is no investor-level binding in the surface.
- [ ] Every ProvenancePopover carries the mandatory line *"Aggregate over ≥20 consent-eligible investors. No individual data shown."*

**k-anon (floor 20, stricter than ≥10).**
- [ ] A cohort with n<20 renders the GatedState guard (no chart); a single segment column/card with n<20 renders struck/locked with no scores painted — the rest of the console is unaffected.
- [ ] The "≥ 20" floor is visible in the AggregateBadge and the guard copy.

**Consent (GDPR Art.6).**
- [ ] ConsentBadge present in every state with the GDPR/consent popover; consent-empty cohort renders the consent guard, not a number.
- [ ] Copy states that only `granted | legitimate_interest` signals are counted and that identifiers are removed before analysis.

**Synthetic preview (scaffold never masquerades as real).**
- [ ] The synthetic-preview toggle exists only in the not-connected state and is unreachable on the live route.
- [ ] When ON, every region carries the SyntheticTag, the pill reads `Preview (synthetic)`, and the PDF export watermarks "PREVIEW"; the live route (`synthetic:false`) shows no SyntheticTag.

**States, charts, a11y.**
- [ ] All seven §7 states render (not_connected default · happy · k-anon · low-coverage · consent-empty · synthetic-preview · error); error shows cached last-good or the not-connected default, never a blank or a fabricated number.
- [ ] Only Part 2.5 approved charts are used; every chart has a caption; titles ≥ h2; numbers mono/tabular; % inline; not-significant deltas greyed.
- [ ] Never colour-only; full keyboard nav + focus; chart data available as an aggregate-only "view data" table; English + German externalised strings; light/dark parity.
- [ ] `<768px` shows only the large-screen notice; no analytical chart horizontally scrolls at any breakpoint.


---

## Surface S24 — Scenario Rebalancing

> **Frontend surface brief for Claude Design — one surface, at depth.** Read with the **Frontend Foundation** (`Alpha_Frontend_Design_Spec_for_ClaudeDesign.md`, PART 0–11: design system, tokens, chart language §2.5, component library §2.6, global frame §3, seven states §9, accessibility §10) and the **backend brief** (`Alpha_UseCase_Briefs/deep/T2-04_Scenario_Rebalancing.md`, use case **T2-4**). This document **references** the foundation; it does not redefine tokens, the ProfileBar, the cohort builder, Ask Alpha, or the badge primitives. It specifies how the **Scenario Rebalancing** surface looks, binds, behaves, and reads — wired to `GET /api/rebalance?investor_id=&objective=&horizon_commitments=`.
>
> **The one law that governs this surface (from T2-4 §1).** This is the single closest surface to investment advice in the entire product, and it is therefore the surface where the no-advice posture must be most visible and most structurally enforced. Every output is **information + future-commitment options** — never a directive. The UI shows *what new commitments would move your exposure toward a chosen objective*. It **never** renders a "sell," "exit," "trim," "reduce position in," or "liquidate" affordance for anything the investor already holds. There is no sell button anywhere on this surface. The sell-side (secondary) path exists only as a gated, not-connected information scaffold. Forward-only is enforced in the contract (`forward_only: true`, additions-only `future_mix`) and must be enforced in the chrome.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**Purpose.** Help an investor steer their **current portfolio exposure** toward a chosen objective — more defense, more growth, less credit, less hidden concentration, less macro sensitivity, less inflation sensitivity — by re-shaping the **next few future commitments** (the next `N` tickets), **without ever being told to sell** what they already hold. The investor picks an objective; the engine solves for the forward-commitment mix that best closes the objective gap under their real liquidity budget; the surface shows the resulting **before → after exposure**, a **projected exposure-shift** under the relevant macro scenario, and the **funds that fit the gap**. The held portfolio is never touched in the plan — over-weight sleeves dilute (their % falls because the denominator grows), they are never reduced in EUR.

This surface is the visible face of the most delicate engine in Alpha. Its job is to make a genuinely useful rebalancing answer feel *calm, factual, and non-prescriptive* — to give the "doctor, not a pro" persona a way to act on a worry ("I'm nervous about credit") that is honest, bounded, and never crosses into "do this trade."

**The user questions this surface answers** (T2-4 §1, each maps to an `objective`):

| User question (plain language) | `objective` enum | Scenario applied |
|---|---|---|
| "How do I become more defensive without selling too much?" | `defense` | `credit_stress` |
| "Rebalance toward more growth." | `growth` | `base` |
| "How should I rebalance if I'm worried about credit stress?" | `less_credit` | `credit_stress` |
| "Reduce my hidden concentration." | `reduce_concentration` | `base` |
| "Reduce my macro sensitivity (shift to more resilient funds)." | `reduce_macro` | `gdp_slowdown` |
| "Reduce my inflation sensitivity." | `reduce_inflation` | `inflation_shock` |
| "Rebalance vs my model portfolio / peers / an excellence group." | `vs_model` · `vs_peers` · `vs_excellence` | `base` |
| "Should I use secondaries or new commitments to rebalance?" | (contrast) `defense` forward path vs the gated `secondary_option` | per objective |

The surface answers all of these in **one page** — the objective selector swaps which question is being answered; everything below recomputes. The "secondaries vs new commitments" question (Q4) is answered structurally: the forward-commitment plan is live and actionable-as-information; the secondary path renders as a designed-but-not-connected option beside it, so the contrast is visible without ever surfacing a sell instruction.

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

S24 is reachable three ways and is the same surface in all three — entry only changes the **pre-selected objective** and the source-context note.

1. **As its own page in the left rail.** Listed in the Detail rail (PART 3) under the *construction* group, label **"Rebalancing."** Default objective on cold open = `defense` (the most-asked question; T2-4 §3.4 default). Clicking the rail item loads S24 with no source-context note.

2. **From Macro / Regime (S19).** The S19 offense/defense panel and factor-sensitivity grouped bars each carry a quiet text link — **"See how future commitments would shift this →"**. Clicking from the offense/defense gauge deep-links to S24 with objective pre-set to `reduce_macro`; clicking from the inflation factor bar pre-sets `reduce_inflation`. The S24 header shows a context chip: *"From Macro — reducing macro sensitivity."* The investor can change the objective freely once here.

3. **From Look-through / Concentration (S6).** The S6 concentration headline (HHI / company-overlap) carries the link **"See how future commitments could reduce this →"**, deep-linking to S24 with `reduce_concentration` pre-set and the context chip *"From Concentration — reducing hidden concentration."* When coverage is low (T2-4 state 4), the link still works but S24 shows the low-coverage fallback (§7).

4. **From Ask Alpha and Next Best Action.** A chat answer to any rebalancing-flavoured question ("can I get more defensive?", "should I worry about inflation?") renders an inline **"Open the rebalancing view →"** chip that deep-links to S24 with the inferred objective. The S20 Next-Best-Action rationale, when its contributing chain includes a rebalancing move, links into S24 with the matching objective.

**Nav rules (inherit PART 3).** S24 is a full-screen Detail surface inside the persistent frame: ProfileBar on top, cohort builder left, Ask Alpha floating, PDF export available. Switching to/from S24 is instant and **no-scroll** on desktop — the whole surface fits one viewport at ≥1280px. The objective and any `vs_*` comparison are reflected in the URL/route state so a deep-link reproduces the exact view (determinism, T2-4 §6). Changing the cohort (left rail) only affects the `vs_peers` / `vs_excellence` objectives' target reference — for those it re-resolves and re-renders the affected panels; for the model-tilt objectives the cohort is inert (the target is the model, not a cohort) and the surface shows the cohort selector visually dimmed-for-this-objective with a one-line note.

---

### §3 Layout (grid)

**Desktop ≥1280px — a three-band, no-scroll layout.** The surface is one screen: a thin objective-selector band across the top, a wide before→after exposure band in the middle (the hero), and a two-up footer band — projected shift on the left, the forward-commitment fund plan on the right — with the gated secondary option as a slim trailing card.

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ S24 · Rebalancing      [context chip: From Concentration]      [PDF ▾]          │
│ "Move your exposure toward an objective — using only future commitments."        │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ OBJECTIVE SELECTOR  (segmented, single-select)                                   │
│ [ More defense ][ More growth ][ Less credit ][ Reduce concentration ]           │
│ [ Reduce macro sensitivity ][ Reduce inflation ]   ·  vs ▾ [ Model|Peers|Excel ] │
│  forward budget: €360k  ⓘ  (min of your target headroom & investable capacity)   │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ BEFORE → AFTER  EXPOSURE                                          [Forward only] │
│  Per-strategy paired bars: current % (left) → after-plan % (right), Δ inline.    │
│  Defensive sleeves grow; offensive sleeves' % falls by DILUTION (note glyph).    │
│  Objective metric chip:  weighted gap to target  41.2 → 27.9  ▼ improved          │
│  Caption: "Your held funds are unchanged — over-weight sleeves shrink in % only   │
│            because the portfolio grows."   [n+CI badge] [provenance]              │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┤
│ PROJECTED EXPOSURE-SHIFT                  │ FORWARD-COMMITMENT PLAN              │
│  ForecastBand (T1-13) under {scenario}:   │  "Where the next €360k could go"     │
│  NAV / resilience band, before vs after.  │  ┌ Private Credit · €180k · 50% ───┐ │
│  resilience 0.42 → 0.55 ▲                  │  │  e.g. Helios Credit IV (min €50k)│ │
│  [coverage 71%] [synthetic] [provenance]  │  ├ Infrastructure · €120k · 33% ───┤ │
│  scenario: credit_stress (illustrative)   │  │  a Infrastructure fund (k-anon)  │ │
│  [Project this in detail → S12]           │  ├ Secondaries · €60k · 17% ───────┤ │
│                                            │  │  e.g. Polaris Secondaries II     │ │
│                                            │  └──────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                            │  disclaimer · "Contact your associate" │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────┤
│ SECONDARY PATH (sell-side)   — NotConnectedState (dashed, greyed)                │
│  "Faster exposure change by selling existing positions on the secondary market"  │
│  "Live when the secondary feed connects."   [synthetic-preview toggle · demo]    │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Band 1 — Header + objective selector + budget readout.** Title, optional source-context chip, PDF export. Below it the **ObjectiveSelector** (segmented single-select, six tilt objectives) plus a separate `vs ▾` control for the three reference objectives (Model / Peers / Excellence). Directly under the selector sits the **forward-budget readout** — a single number with an info popover explaining it is `min(target headroom, investable capacity)` from T1-7 — because the budget is the constraint that makes the whole plan honest, it must be visible before the investor reads the plan.

**Band 2 — Before → after exposure (the hero).** A horizontal **paired-bar** block, one row per strategy that changes, showing current % and after-plan %, with the per-strategy delta inline and a dilution glyph on any sleeve whose % falls. A prominent **objective-metric chip** states the single headline measure (e.g. weighted gap to target 41.2 → 27.9, improved) with its before/after provenance. This band carries the load-bearing caption that explains the forward-only mechanic in one sentence. A persistent **"Forward only"** badge sits in the top-right of the band.

**Band 3 (left) — Projected exposure-shift.** A **ForecastBand** (the approved projection chart, §2.5) showing the resilience / NAV path *before vs after* the plan, under the objective's named scenario. Carries the macro/concentration coverage and synthetic badges, the scenario name (illustrative), and a link to project in full detail in S12 (T1-13).

**Band 3 (right) — Forward-commitment plan.** The plan itself: one row per funded strategy (commitment EUR + share of budget), each with one or two **example open funds** (named when k-anon passes, bucket-labelled when not), and the no-advice disclaimer + "Contact your sales associate." This is the only place on the surface that names funds, and it names them only as *additions that fit the gap*, never as anything to act on against a held position.

**Band 4 — Secondary path (gated).** A slim, full-width **NotConnectedState** card showing the *intended* sell-side layout in dashed/greyed preview with the "live when the feed connects" message and a demo-only synthetic-preview toggle. It is visually subordinate (lower contrast, dashed border) so it never reads as an available action.

**Responsive.**
- **≥1280px:** the no-scroll three-band layout above; Band 3 is a two-column split; Band 4 is a trailing slim card.
- **768–1279px (tablet):** cohort builder collapses to a drawer and Ask Alpha to a launcher (PART 3). The objective selector wraps to two rows; Band 3 stacks (projected-shift above the plan); Band 4 remains full-width at the bottom. One vertical scroll is permitted here (the surface is explicitly allowed to scroll below 1280; the no-scroll rule binds only the desktop analytical viewport).
- **<768px (mobile, advisor on the go):** single column, ProfileBar becomes a sheet. The objective selector becomes a horizontally-scrollable chip row (or a dropdown if width is tight). The before→after bars reflow to stacked paired rows (no horizontal scroll on the chart). The plan rows stack; the ForecastBand renders compact with a "view data" table affordance (PART 10). The secondary NotConnectedState collapses to a single dashed line + tap-to-expand. The budget readout pins under the selector. No analytical chart ever requires horizontal scroll.

---

### §4 Components & charts (exact encodings)

All components inherit the foundation library (§2.6) and chart language (§2.5). New-or-specialised pieces below are defined precisely; nothing here introduces a rejected chart type.

**4.1 ObjectiveSelector (segmented single-select + a `vs` sub-control).**
A segmented control, one selection at a time, in two visual groups:
- **Tilt group (six segments):** `More defense` · `More growth` · `Less credit` · `Reduce concentration` · `Reduce macro sensitivity` · `Reduce inflation`. Maps to `objective ∈ {defense, growth, less_credit, reduce_concentration, reduce_macro, reduce_inflation}`.
- **Reference group (`vs ▾`):** a single secondary control to the right — `vs Model · vs Peers · vs Excellence` — mapping to `objective ∈ {vs_model, vs_peers, vs_excellence}`. Selecting one of these de-selects the tilt group (still single-select across the whole control) and lights the cohort builder for Peers/Excellence.
- **States:** default, hover (token `--brand-tint-3` wash), selected (filled `--brand`, white label), focus (visible focus ring, keyboard-reachable as a radio group), disabled (a tilt objective is never disabled; the `vs` options for cohort-based references show a tooltip if no cohort resolved). Selected objective persists in route state.
- Each segment has a hover micro-definition (one line) explaining what the objective tilts toward — e.g. *Less credit: "Shifts the target away from Private Credit; redistributes to the rest."* These are plain-language, never prescriptive.

**4.2 ForwardBudgetReadout.**
A single mono number (`€360,000`) labelled **"Forward budget"** with an info `ⓘ` opening a ProvenancePopover: method `min(target_envelope, investable_capacity_T1-7)`, the two candidate values, which one bound, and a SyntheticTag if `target_envelope` derives from a synthetic `pm_target_pct` / `investable_wealth_eur` (T2-4 §8, state 5). When `B == 0` it renders greyed with "no forward capacity now" (state 6). The readout is the surface's honesty anchor — the plan can never exceed it.

**4.3 BeforeAfterExposureBars (the hero — paired horizontal bars).**
This is an **approved horizontal-bar** encoding (§2.5), specialised into a *before→after paired* form:
- One row per strategy that appears in `allocation_before` or `allocation_after`. Each row shows two thin horizontal bars: **current %** (token `--brand-tint-2`) and **after-plan %** (token `--brand`), stacked or adjacent within the row, with **% inline at the end of each bar** (never legend-only).
- A **delta marker**: the change `Δ = after − before` rendered inline at the right of the row, paired colour+glyph — `--over`/`▲` when the sleeve grows, `--under`/`▼` when it shrinks (accessibility: never colour alone, §2.5).
- A **dilution glyph** (a small ⓘ "diluted" tag) on any sleeve whose **% falls while its EUR is unchanged** — i.e. offensive sleeves that the plan does not add to. Hover/focus reveals: *"EUR unchanged (€160k); % fell because the portfolio grew by €360k. Nothing was sold."* This glyph is the visible proof of the forward-only mechanic and must appear on every diluted sleeve (T2-4 §6 forward-only on C+F).
- Rows are ordered: funded/growing sleeves first (descending Δ), then diluted sleeves (the visual story is "these grow, these shrink only by dilution").
- A **target marker** (dashed) on each row shows the objective's target % `t[s]` for that strategy, so the investor sees how far the after-bar moved toward target. Inline, per §2.5 (show the actual target).
- **ObjectiveMetricChip** above or beside the bars: the single headline metric from `objective_metric` — `name` (humanised: "weighted gap to target", "sector concentration (HHI)", "inflation sensitivity") and `before → after` with a `▼ improved` / state, both values carrying ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover. This chip is the at-a-glance answer to "did the objective move."
- Caption (CaptionBlock, mandatory): the forward-only sentence (see §8).

**4.4 ProjectedShiftBand (ForecastBand).**
The approved **ForecastBand** (§2.5): a base line with shaded band, rendered twice — **before** (greyed, ghosted) and **after** (solid) — under the objective's named `scenario`. The y-metric is objective-appropriate: resilience / NAV path for macro objectives, NAV path for tilt objectives. Shows:
- `resilience_before → resilience_after` (or the relevant scalar) as a small ▲/▼ stat beside the band, with CoverageBadge (`coverage_pct`) and SyntheticTag (betas/scenario are illustrative, T2-4 §8 G5/G8).
- The scenario name as a labelled, illustrative chip (`credit_stress`, `inflation_shock`, `gdp_slowdown`, `base`).
- `crossover_quarter_before → crossover_quarter_after` as a secondary annotation when present.
- A **"Project this in detail → S12"** link that deep-links to the Projection surface (T1-13) carrying the same scenario + overlays. Caption explains the band is illustrative and the held book is unchanged in the projection.

**4.5 ForwardCommitmentPlan (the plan card).**
A card titled **"Where the next {budget} could go."** One **PlanRow** per funded strategy (`future_mix[]`):
- Strategy name · `commitment_eur` (mono) · `share_of_budget_pct` (inline %, mini bar).
- One or two **example open funds** (`example_funds[]`): when `k_anon_ok: true` and `fund_id` present, render the **fund name** + `min_eur` (a compact FundCard-lite, not the full S13 FundCard — name, strategy badge, min. investment, no "sell" anything); when `k_anon_ok: false` / `fund_id: null`, render the **bucket label** ("a Infrastructure fund") greyed, per the `weighted_average_portfolio` k-anon convention (PART 2.6, T2-4 §6 k-anon).
- Rows sum to the forward budget (the card footer states "Total {budget} · sums to your forward budget").
- Card footer: the **disclaimer** (from `disclaimer`, verbatim) + **"Contact your sales associate"** CTA (information CTA only; it does not place anything).
- **There is no "sell," "swap," "trim," or "reduce" control anywhere in this card or its rows.** Every affordance is additive.

**4.6 SecondaryOptionCard (NotConnectedState).**
The foundation **NotConnectedState** (§2.6, dashed/greyed), holding the *designed* sell-side layout so the live swap is data-only (T2-4 §3.5, state — gated):
- Dashed border, low-contrast, a small lock/feed glyph.
- Title: "Secondary path (sell-side)." Body from `secondary_option.note`: "Secondary-market rebalancing (faster exposure change via sell-side) requires the secondary feed; not available in this build." + "Live when the secondary feed connects."
- A ghosted preview of the intended layout (a ranked list of "positions with deepest secondary demand," all greyed/blurred placeholder rows) — **never** with a sell button, even in preview; the preview frames it as *information* ("positions the market is buying"), consistent with the gate that forbids a sell directive even when fed.
- A **synthetic-preview toggle** (demo mode only, PART 9) that fills the scaffold with clearly-labelled illustrative rows.
- This card is always present (T2-4 §5: a `secondary_option` block is always in the payload), even when `defense` is selected and the forward plan is rich — its presence is how the surface answers "secondaries vs new commitments" honestly.

**4.7 Inherited components.** ProfileBar, CohortBuilder, AskAlphaPane (PART 5 — its answers' numbers are provenance chips; a rebalancing answer can deep-link here), ConfidenceBadge, CoverageBadge, SyntheticTag, ProvenancePopover, RelaxBanner (for `vs_peers` thin cohort), GatedState (not used for the plan; only the secondary path is gated, via NotConnectedState), PDFExportButton.

---

### §5 Data bindings (element ← `GET /api/rebalance` field)

Single endpoint: **`GET /api/rebalance?investor_id={id}&objective={enum}&horizon_commitments={int=3}`** (T2-4 §5). Every numeric element binds `value` to the visual and `provenance` to its badge/popover (PART 0.2). Bindings, element ← JSON path:

**Header / selector / budget**
- ObjectiveSelector selection ← `objective` (echoed in response; also drives the request).
- Scenario chip (in Band 3) ← `scenario`.
- ForwardBudgetReadout value ← `forward_budget_eur.value`; popover ← `forward_budget_eur.provenance` (method, synthetic, snapshot_id).
- Forward-only badge ← `forward_only` (must be `true`; if ever falsy, suppress the whole plan and show the error state — see §7).
- Context chip text ← route param (entry source), not the payload.

**Band 2 — before→after exposure**
- Per-strategy current-% bar ← `projected_exposure_shift.allocation_before[strategy]`.
- Per-strategy after-% bar ← `projected_exposure_shift.allocation_after[strategy]`.
- Per-row Δ ← `allocation_after[s] − allocation_before[s]` (computed FE-side from the two bound values).
- Dilution glyph trigger ← a strategy where `allocation_after[s] < allocation_before[s]` **and** that strategy is absent from `future_mix` (i.e. its EUR is unchanged; the % drop is pure dilution). The hover EUR ("€160k unchanged") is derived from `allocation_before[s]/100 × committed_now` where `committed_now` is recoverable as `Σ future_mix.commitment_eur / (denominator growth)` — if `committed_now` is not directly present, the dilution copy uses the qualitative form ("EUR unchanged; % fell because the portfolio grew") and omits the exact EUR.
- Target marker per row ← the objective target `t[s]`; if `t` is not echoed in the payload, the marker is omitted and the caption still explains direction (graceful).
- ObjectiveMetricChip name ← `projected_exposure_shift.objective_metric.name` (humanised via a copy map); before ← `objective_metric.before.value` (+ `.provenance`); after ← `objective_metric.after.value` (+ `.provenance`); improved state ← `objective_metric.improved`.

**Band 3 left — projected shift**
- ForecastBand after-path/band ← `projected_exposure_shift.projection.nav_path_bands_after` (T1-13 object); before ghost ← `...nav_path_bands_before`.
- Crossover annotations ← `projection.crossover_quarter_before` / `...after`.
- Resilience stat ← `projected_exposure_shift.macro.resilience_before` / `resilience_after`; CoverageBadge ← `macro.provenance.coverage_pct`; SyntheticTag ← `macro.provenance.synthetic`; method popover ← `macro.provenance.method`.
- Scenario chip ← `projected_exposure_shift.projection.scenario` (== top-level `scenario`).
- For `reduce_concentration`: the headline metric is HHI — `objective_metric.before/after` carry it; if the payload includes a low-coverage flag, render the low-coverage fallback (§7).

**Band 3 right — forward plan**
- One PlanRow per element of `future_mix[]`.
- Strategy ← `future_mix[i].strategy`; commitment ← `future_mix[i].commitment_eur`; share ← `future_mix[i].share_of_budget_pct`.
- Example fund name ← `future_mix[i].example_funds[j].fund` (shown as name iff `example_funds[j].k_anon_ok === true && fund_id !== null`; else render the same string as a greyed bucket label); min ← `example_funds[j].min_eur`; id (for any internal linking) ← `example_funds[j].fund_id`.
- Plan total / reconciliation note ← `Σ future_mix.commitment_eur` (must equal `forward_budget_eur.value` ± one ticket; if mismatch beyond tolerance, show the reconciliation note quietly and do not fabricate).
- Disclaimer ← `disclaimer` (verbatim).

**Band 4 — secondary**
- Available ← `secondary_option.available` (false → NotConnectedState).
- Status ← `secondary_option.status` (`"not_connected"`).
- Note ← `secondary_option.note`.
- Provenance ← `secondary_option.provenance`.

**Reference / cold-start flags**
- `cohort_or_reference.objective_kind` (`model_tilt` | reference) → drives whether the cohort builder is active (reference) or dimmed (model_tilt).
- `cold_start` (T2-4 state 2) → routes to Architect (§7).
- `no_budget` / `limited_budget` / `low_coverage` / `null` envelope flags → drive the matching state (§7).

**Cohort coupling.** For `vs_peers` / `vs_excellence`, the cohort builder's `POST /api/cohort` result (PART 6) supplies the `ids` behind the reference target; changing the cohort re-issues `/api/rebalance` for those objectives and re-renders Bands 2–3. For all tilt objectives the cohort is inert.

---

### §6 Interactions

**6.1 Pick an objective → recompute.** Selecting a segment (or a `vs ▾` option) issues `GET /api/rebalance?objective={new}` and re-renders Bands 2–3–4 (the header budget can stay if unchanged). The transition is a calm value-in animation (≤150ms, §2.4), no flashy motion; bars re-tween from old % to new %, the objective-metric chip cross-fades. The selected objective is written to route state so the view is deep-linkable and deterministic (T2-4 §6). Only the affected panels re-render — the ProfileBar, cohort builder, and Ask Alpha persist.

**6.2 Toggle the `vs` reference.** Switching among `vs Model / vs Peers / vs Excellence` recomputes the target reference. For Peers/Excellence the cohort builder lights up (its current cohort defines the reference); a thin cohort triggers the RelaxBanner in words (PART 6, §7). For Model the cohort dims with a one-line note: *"This objective compares to your model portfolio, not a peer cohort."*

**6.3 Toggle / inspect the secondary path.** The secondary card is read-only when gated. In **Demo mode** a synthetic-preview toggle fills the scaffold with labelled illustrative rows so a walkthrough can show the *intended* sell-side information layout — still framed as "positions with deepest secondary demand," still with no sell affordance. Outside demo, the toggle is absent. This interaction is how the surface visibly answers Q4 ("secondaries or new commitments?") — the investor sees the forward path is live and the sell-side path is not.

**6.4 Inspect the forward-only mechanic.** Hovering/focusing a **dilution glyph** on a shrinking sleeve reveals the explanation ("EUR unchanged; % fell because the portfolio grew — nothing was sold"). Hovering a **delta marker** shows before/after % and the EUR change (always ≥ 0 for funded sleeves, exactly 0 for diluted sleeves). This is the primary self-service reassurance interaction and must be keyboard-reachable.

**6.5 Project in detail → S12 (T1-13 link).** The "Project this in detail" link in Band 3 deep-links to the **Projection** surface (S12) carrying the current `scenario` and the forward `overlays` (the `future_mix` as additions-only), so S12 opens already showing the rebalanced path. The link round-trips: S12 carries a "back to Rebalancing" affordance. No negative position is ever passed (T2-4 §3.4 — overlays are additions only).

**6.6 Explore the plan.** Clicking an example fund name (k-anon-passing rows only) opens a lightweight fund detail (or deep-links to S13 Fund compare with that fund pre-loaded as the gap-fitting candidate). Bucket-labelled rows are not clickable (nothing to open). The "Contact your sales associate" CTA is an information action (opens the contact affordance); it never executes a commitment.

**6.7 Provenance everywhere.** Every number — budget, each before/after %, each delta, the objective metric, resilience, crossover quarters, each commitment EUR — is a provenance chip: click opens the ProvenancePopover (method, n, CI where applicable, coverage %, snapshot, synthetic flag) per PART 0.2. No number renders bare. The objective-metric before/after each show their `provenance` (`‖a−t‖_w` etc., n=1 for the deterministic gap metric — render n=1 honestly, no fabricated CI).

**6.8 Cohort change (for `vs_*`).** A chip change in the cohort builder re-resolves the reference and re-renders only Bands 2–3 (PART 6). For tilt objectives the cohort change is a no-op on this surface (dimmed).

**6.9 Ask Alpha coupling.** Asking a rebalancing question in Ask Alpha returns an answer whose numbers are provenance chips and which offers "Open the rebalancing view →" deep-linking here with the inferred objective; conversely, an "Explain this plan" affordance on the surface can hand the current objective + payload to Ask Alpha for a five-sentence summary.

**6.10 PDF export.** Exports the current S24 view (selected objective, before→after bars, projected shift, plan, disclaimer, the not-connected secondary note) as a clean PowerPoint-style PDF, badges and disclaimer included (the disclaimer must survive export — it is part of the no-advice posture).

---

### §7 The seven states (every one renders; branch matches T2-4 §7)

Each state is styled once (PART 9) and reused. S24's branches:

1. **Happy.** Full payload (§5): objective selected, budget present, before→after bars with deltas and dilution glyphs, objective-metric improved, projected shift with resilience ▲, a forward plan summing to budget, secondary NotConnectedState. The default, e.g. the worked `defense` example.

2. **Cold-start** (`cold_start: true`, `n_funds == 0`). There is no current exposure to move, so the "before" side is empty. The surface shows a calm card: *"You don't have holdings yet, so there's nothing to rebalance — let's build your target architecture first,"* with the `future_mix` (built purely from the target) shown as a **preview of where to start**, and a primary CTA routing to the **Allocation Architect (S9)**. Band 2's before bars are suppressed (after-only, labelled "your starting architecture"); the projected-shift "before" deltas are omitted. `forward_only` still true. Never a broken analytics screen.

3. **Thin / limited budget** (`limited_budget: true`, small but > 0 `B`). The plan solves on the small budget and surfaces the **single highest-impact sleeve only** (one PlanRow), with a quiet note: *"Your forward capacity is limited right now — this is the one move that shifts your {objective} the most."* The before→after bars still render; deltas are small and honestly shown (sub-CI deltas render greyed, §2.5). For `vs_peers` thin **cohort**, the RelaxBanner shows in words (PART 6).

4. **Low coverage** (`low_coverage: true`, for `reduce_concentration` / `reduce_macro` / `reduce_inflation` with `coverage_pct < 50`). The objective metric falls back to the **allocation-gap** headline (T2-4 §3.3/state 4); the HHI/beta shift is still shown but **caveated and visually secondary**, with an unmissable CoverageBadge (*"based on look-through of {x}% of your NAV"*). The surface never implies full coverage. Caption: *"We can only see part of your underlying exposure, so the concentration figure is indicative."*

5. **Null target envelope** (`investable_wealth_eur` NULL → `target_envelope = None`). Budget `B = investable_capacity` (T1-7) only; the ForwardBudgetReadout popover states the method is liquidity-capacity-only and **never invents wealth**; a SyntheticTag is absent here (no fabricated wealth) but the method note is explicit. Plan and shift render normally on the capacity budget.

6. **No budget** (`no_budget: true`, `B == 0`; e.g. overcommitted / capacity ≤ 0). The plan is empty (`future_mix == []`). The surface shows a calm, non-alarming message: *"You don't have forward capacity right now, so new commitments can't move this objective at the moment — this usually resolves as distributions come in."* The budget readout renders greyed at €0. **Crucially, even here there is no sell suggestion** — the surface does not offer to free cash by selling; it points to waiting for distributions (and the gated secondary path remains not-connected). Before→after bars collapse to "no change" (after == before).

7. **Error / degraded** (`404` unknown investor, `422` unknown objective, or engine fault). 404 → a quiet "we couldn't find this profile" with a retry. 422 → the selector resets to a valid objective and shows "that objective isn't available; showing {default}." Engine fault → cached last-good payload with a quiet "showing your last saved view" banner; never a blank screen and never a fabricated number. **Forward-only failsafe:** if `forward_only` is ever falsy or any sell/exit/trim token appears in the payload (which the backend forbids, T2-4 §6), the FE suppresses the plan entirely and shows the degraded banner rather than rendering a non-compliant plan — the chrome enforces the law even against a misbehaving payload.

Plus the global decorations: not-significant deltas greyed; SyntheticTag on every illustrative input (target %, betas, scenario multipliers); loading = calm skeletons (the bars, the band, and the plan rows skeleton in), never spinner spam.

---

### §8 Copy deck (the strongest no-advice framing in the product)

**Voice law.** Information + future-commitment options, *never* a directive. Allowed verbs about action: *move toward, shift, tilt, close the gap, add, commit, consider, explore.* **Forbidden anywhere on this surface (copy or affordance):** *sell, exit, trim, reduce position, liquidate, dump, get out of, cut, dispose.* The word "reduce" is allowed **only** about a *metric or sensitivity* ("reduce your inflation sensitivity," "reduce concentration"), **never** about a held position ("reduce your AI position" is forbidden). Every screen state ends able to render the disclaimer.

**Header**
- Title: **"Rebalancing"** · subtitle: **"Move your exposure toward an objective — using only future commitments. Your current holdings stay put."**
- Source-context chips: *"From Concentration — reducing hidden concentration."* / *"From Macro — reducing macro sensitivity."*

**Objective selector (segment labels + hover micro-defs)**
- *More defense* — "Tilts future commitments toward credit, infrastructure, and secondaries."
- *More growth* — "Tilts future commitments toward growth, tech, and mid-cap buyout."
- *Less credit* — "Shifts the target away from private credit; redistributes to the rest."
- *Reduce concentration* — "Steers new commitments toward funds that broaden your underlying exposure."
- *Reduce macro sensitivity* — "Favours future commitments in lower-beta, more resilient sleeves."
- *Reduce inflation* — "Favours future commitments that hold up better under an inflation shock."
- *vs Model / Peers / Excellence* — "Compares your mix to a reference and shows commitments that close the gap."

**Forward-budget readout**
- Label: **"Forward budget"** · popover: *"The most you could commit next, taken as the smaller of your target headroom and your investable capacity. The plan never exceeds this."*
- Synthetic case: *"Your target % is illustrative, so this headroom is indicative; the budget falls back to your liquidity capacity."*

**Before→after hero (the load-bearing caption — must appear)**
- Caption: **"Your held funds are unchanged. Over-weight sleeves shrink in percentage only because the portfolio grows — nothing here is sold."**
- Dilution glyph hover: *"EUR unchanged. The percentage fell because your portfolio grew by {budget}. Nothing was sold."*
- Objective-metric chip examples: *"Weighted gap to your defensive target: 41.2 → 27.9 ▼ improved."* / *"Sector concentration (HHI): 2,140 → 1,780 ▼ lower."* / *"Inflation sensitivity: 0.61 → 0.44 ▼ lower."*
- "Forward only" badge tooltip: *"This plan only adds future commitments. It never sells, trims, or exits anything you hold."*

**Projected shift**
- Caption: *"How your resilience could shift under a {scenario} scenario if you make these future commitments. Illustrative; your existing holdings are unchanged in this projection."*
- Resilience stat: *"Resilience 0.42 → 0.55 under a credit-stress scenario (based on look-through of 71% of your NAV; illustrative)."*
- Detail link: *"Project this in detail →"*

**Forward-commitment plan**
- Title: **"Where the next {budget} could go"**
- Row: *"Private Credit — €180,000 (50% of the budget) · e.g. Helios Credit IV, min. €50,000."*
- k-anon bucket row: *"Infrastructure — €120,000 (33%) · a infrastructure fund."*
- Footer total: *"Total {budget} · this sums to your forward budget."*
- Disclaimer (verbatim from payload): **"Information about future-commitment options that move your exposure toward this objective. Not a recommendation to sell or change existing holdings. Contact your sales associate."**
- CTA: **"Contact your sales associate"**

**Secondary path (NotConnectedState)**
- Title: **"Secondary path (sell-side)"**
- Body: *"There's a faster way to change exposure — selling existing positions on the secondary market. That needs the secondary feed, which isn't available in this build."*
- Status line: **"Live when the secondary feed connects."**
- Demo preview note: *"Illustrative preview — when connected, this shows positions the market is buying most actively. It is information, never an instruction to sell."*

**State copy**
- Cold-start: *"You don't have holdings yet, so there's nothing to rebalance. Let's build your target architecture first."* → CTA *"Open the Allocation Architect."*
- Limited budget: *"Your forward capacity is limited right now — this is the single move that shifts your {objective} the most."*
- Low coverage: *"We can only see part of your underlying exposure, so this concentration figure is indicative."*
- No budget: *"You don't have forward capacity right now, so new commitments can't move this objective at the moment. This usually resolves as distributions come in."*
- Error/degraded: *"We're showing your last saved view."* / *"That objective isn't available — showing {default}."*

**Ask Alpha hand-offs:** *"Open the rebalancing view →"* (from chat) · *"Explain this plan in five sentences"* (from surface to chat).

---

### §9 Badge rendering (which badge, where, from which field)

Per PART 0.2 and §2.6, numeric elements embed badges structurally. On S24:

- **ConfidenceBadge** (`n · ±CI`) — on the objective-metric before/after values where the engine supplies n/CI. The deterministic gap metric is `n=1` with no CI (T2-4 §5 `"n":1`); render it honestly as a method note ("computed, not sampled") rather than fabricating a CI. Cohort-derived `vs_peers` references carry the real `n`/CI from `POST /api/cohort` and show the standard chip; sub-CI deltas in the before→after bars render greyed/"not significant."
- **CoverageBadge** (`based on look-through of {x}% of NAV`) — mandatory and unmissable on the projected-shift resilience stat and on any `reduce_concentration` HHI / `reduce_macro|inflation` beta figure (`macro.provenance.coverage_pct`, T1-9/T3-3). In the low-coverage state (§7) the badge is the headline, never implying 100%.
- **SyntheticTag** (amber "illustrative" pill) — on: the forward budget when it derives from a synthetic `pm_target_pct` / `investable_wealth_eur`; the scenario chip and all macro betas (T2-4 §8 G5/G8 — scenario multipliers and factor betas are illustrative); any imputed ProfileBar input surfaced here. Never styled as verified fact.
- **ProvenancePopover** — on every number (budget, each %, each delta, objective metric, resilience, crossover quarter, each commitment EUR): method, n, CI/coverage where applicable, snapshot_id, synthetic flag. The forward-budget popover names which of `{target_envelope, investable_capacity}` bound (the `min`).
- **"Forward only" badge** — a distinct, always-on chip on the before→after band (from `forward_only: true`), tooltip per §8. Visually it reads as a posture badge, not a data badge.
- **NotConnectedState chrome** — the secondary card's dashed border + feed glyph is itself the badge that this is a gated, not-live, not-actionable surface.
- **RelaxBanner** — words-form banner for `vs_peers`/`vs_excellence` thin cohorts (from `relaxed_dims`, PART 6).
- **Snapshot footer** — the surface (and any Ask Alpha answer about it) carries the `snapshot_id` so the view is reproducible.

No GatedState/paid-viewer card appears on this surface (the plan is not entitlement-gated); the only gating here is the **signal-gated** secondary path, which uses NotConnectedState, not GatedState.

---

### §10 Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive** (breakpoints inherit PART 3; §3 above gives the per-band reflow). Key invariants:
- No analytical chart (before→after bars, ForecastBand) ever requires horizontal scroll at any width; below 1280px the bars reflow to stacked paired rows and the band renders compact.
- The objective selector degrades gracefully: segmented at desktop → wrapped two-row at tablet → horizontally-scrollable chip row or dropdown at mobile, always single-select, always keyboard-reachable.
- Desktop ≥1280 is strictly no-scroll (the analytical viewport law); tablet/mobile may scroll vertically.
- The forward-budget readout pins directly under the selector at all widths (it is the constraint the investor must see before the plan).

**Accessibility** (WCAG-AA, PART 10):
- **Never colour-only.** Every delta pairs colour with a glyph/sign (`▲`/`▼`, `+`/`−`); the dilution glyph is a labelled tag, not a colour. Over/under and improved/worse states carry text or glyph, not hue alone.
- **Keyboard:** the objective selector is a radio group (arrow-key navigation, Enter/Space to select); the `vs ▾` is a standard menu; every provenance chip, the dilution glyphs, the detail/project links, and the plan rows are tab-reachable with visible focus rings.
- **Charts as data:** the before→after bars and the ForecastBand each expose a "view data" affordance opening the underlying table (before %, after %, Δ, target, commitment EUR) — so the rebalancing answer is available non-visually.
- **Screen-reader semantics:** the objective-metric chip announces "weighted gap to target, before 41.2, after 27.9, improved"; each diluted sleeve announces "EUR unchanged, percentage fell by dilution, nothing sold." The "Forward only" badge and the disclaimer are in the reading order, not decorative-only — the no-advice posture must be perceivable by assistive tech.
- **Contrast & motion:** tokens meet AA; value-in animation ≤150ms, respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (bars snap rather than tween when reduced).
- **i18n:** copy structured for English + German (DACH audience); EU number/currency formatting (€, thousands separators); no hard-coded strings — the forbidden-verb list and disclaimer are localised with the same no-advice intent preserved in German.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

The surface is **done** when:

- The ObjectiveSelector offers all nine objectives (six tilt segments + `vs Model/Peers/Excellence`), single-select, deep-linkable; picking an objective re-issues `GET /api/rebalance?objective=` and re-renders Bands 2–4 only; the view is deterministic (same objective → same render).
- The ForwardBudgetReadout shows `forward_budget_eur` with a popover naming the `min(target_envelope, investable_capacity)` method and the synthetic fallback; the plan total reconciles to the budget (±one ticket); `B == 0` renders the no-budget state.
- The before→after exposure band binds to `allocation_before`/`allocation_after`, shows inline % on every bar, per-strategy deltas with colour+glyph, and the objective-metric chip from `objective_metric` (before → after, improved).

The surface is **defensible** (the no-advice / forward-only law) when:

- **Forward-only, no sell instruction:** there is no sell / exit / trim / reduce-position / liquidate affordance or copy anywhere on the surface (selector, hero, plan, secondary card, tooltips, PDF export). The "Forward only" badge is always present (from `forward_only: true`). If the payload ever lacks `forward_only: true` or contains any sell/exit token, the FE suppresses the plan and shows the degraded state rather than rendering it (chrome-side failsafe).
- **Dilution proven visually:** every sleeve whose % falls while its EUR is unchanged carries the dilution glyph + the "EUR unchanged; nothing was sold" explanation; no held sleeve is ever shown as reduced in EUR.
- **Objective reduces the targeted metric in projection:** the objective-metric chip shows a strict improvement (`after < before` for gap/HHI/beta objectives; resilience `after > before`), bound to `objective_metric.improved` / the resilience before-after; the projected-shift band visibly shifts in the improving direction under the named scenario.
- **No-advice tone throughout:** every state can and does render the disclaimer ("Not a recommendation to sell or change existing holdings"); the plan CTA is "Contact your sales associate" (information only); the secondary path renders as NotConnectedState with no sell affordance even in the demo preview; the word "reduce" appears only about metrics/sensitivities, never about a held position.

And the standard foundation gates hold:

- Every number carries ConfidenceBadge/method + ProvenancePopover; concentration/macro figures carry CoverageBadge; illustrative inputs (budget-from-synthetic-target, betas, scenario) carry SyntheticTag; the snapshot id is present.
- All seven states render (happy, cold-start→Architect, thin/limited-budget, low-coverage, null-envelope, no-budget, error/degraded) — each calm, none broken, none fabricating numbers.
- k-anon honoured on example funds (named iff `k_anon_ok && fund_id`, else greyed bucket label).
- The T1-13 "project in detail" link carries the scenario + additions-only overlays into S12 and round-trips back; no negative position is ever passed.
- Charts are from the approved set only (horizontal paired bars, ForecastBand) with % inline, large titles, captions present, "Peers" labelling on the `vs_peers` reference; no rejected chart type; no-scroll at ≥1280; charts reachable as data tables; full keyboard nav; AA contrast; English + German copy.

---
*Build to this surface brief within the foundation. Bind every element to `GET /api/rebalance` (T2-4 §5); attach provenance to every number; and above all hold the line that defines this surface: it informs about future commitments, it never tells anyone to sell. The hardest and most important thing this screen does is be genuinely useful about rebalancing while never once crossing into advice.*


---

## Surface S25 — Smart Questions & Due Diligence

> **Frontend surface spec for Claude Design.** One copilot surface inside the Alpha frame (PART 3 of the Frontend Foundation). This surface renders the output of use case **T2-5**. It has **two halves that ship on different timelines** and must degrade independently:
> - **Half A — "Questions to ask about {Fund}, given your portfolio"** — a doc-grounded, generated DD-question list. **Works now (🟢).** Binds to `GET /api/dd_questions?fund_id=&investor_id=`. Every generated DOC question carries a **citation chip** (the inherited pattern from the Foundation, §2.6 ProvenancePopover / Foundation S15 Explain). This is the load-bearing half.
> - **Half B — "Top questions your peers, PE pros & best performers ask"** — a segment-filtered, peer-mined question cluster. **Not wired yet (🔴).** Binds to `GET /api/peer_questions` which returns `{status:"not_connected"}` by default. Renders in the **NotConnectedState** (Foundation §2.6) — a designed, dashed preview, never an error — until `ALPHA_FEED_CONVERSATION` connects.
>
> This brief **references, does not redefine**, the Foundation: the citation chip / ProvenancePopover pattern (§2.6), NotConnectedState (§2.6), SyntheticTag (§2.6), CaptionBlock, the global frame (PART 3), Ask Alpha (PART 5), the seven-state catalog (PART 9), and the chart language (PART 2.5). Where this spec says "citation chip," it means the Foundation's grounded-claim chip exactly as used on Foundation S15 (Explain). Where it says NotConnectedState, it means the Foundation component verbatim, parameterized to `feed = "investor conversation"`.

---

### §1 Purpose & user questions

**One-line purpose.** Teach the investor *better diligence* in two registers — the **content** of the right questions (doc-grounded, live now) and the **social proof** of who asks them (peer-mined, pending feed). The user lands here asking "what should I actually ask before I commit to this fund?" and leaves with a citable, copy-able checklist anchored to the fund's own documents and their own portfolio arithmetic.

**The differentiator this surface must *feel*.** Most retail platforms show a fund and a "Contact sales" button. Alpha shows the **questions a professional would ask**, each one traceable to a line in the fund's PPM / LPA / manager evaluation, and each annotated with *why it matters for this specific portfolio* ("this would push your Mid-Cap Buyout exposure to ~31%"). It is the proprietary-data advantage rendered as a diligence coach. No generated question exists without a citation or an explicit portfolio justification.

**User questions this surface answers** (mapped to the T2-5 eval ids; A = Half A generative, B = Half B peer-mining):

| # | User question | Half | How it renders here |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "What should I ask before investing in {Fund}?" | **A** | The generated DD-question list, each DOC question with a citation chip. |
| Q2 | "What are the top 10 questions my peers ask about {Fund}?" | **B** | Peer cluster, default cohort, `fund_id` filter → NotConnectedState until feed. |
| Q3 | "What do PE professionals ask that others don't?" | **B** | Segment-vs-baseline lift, `segment=profession&values=["Investor/PE"]&compare_to=peers`. |
| Q4 | "What do the best-performing investors ask?" | **B** | `segment=top_performer` cluster. |
| Q5 | "What should I ask, given my portfolio and risk profile?" | **A** | Portfolio-context STRUCT hooks drive `why_it_matters`; runs even with no `fund_id`. |
| Q6 | "What do competent investors ask that my cohort misses?" | **B** | Set-diff `segment=competent&compare_to=peers&mode=miss`. |
| Q7 | "What does the Moonfare investment team ask in diligence?" | **B→A fallback** | `segment=moonfare_ic`; when not_connected, served from Half A's IC/DD doc themes. |

**The no-advice contract (carried from T3-x / T1-3, enforced in the UI copy layer).** Everything on this surface is phrased as **a question to ask** ("Ask the GP how…", "Ask the GP what…") plus *Moonfare's published DD framing* plus *the investor's own portfolio arithmetic*. It is **never** a buy/sell instruction and **never** asserts a document's answer as fact unless that text is a quoted citation. STRUCT hooks read "your portfolio shows…" / "this commitment would…", never "you should…". The `disclaimer` string from the payload renders as persistent footnote chrome on the surface (§8).

---

### §2 Entry & navigation

This surface is reachable from three places; each pre-loads a different parameter set, but they all resolve to the same `/api/dd_questions` call and the same layout.

1. **From a fund (the primary path).** On Foundation **S13 (Funds / Fund-vs-Fund)** and **S14 (What's next / Completion)**, every FundCard carries a **"Questions to ask"** affordance (a quiet text link beside "Contact your sales associate," not a competing CTA). Clicking it deep-links to S25 with `fund_id` pre-set and the caller's `investor_id` attached. The fund's name, manager, and strategy header are taken from the `fund` block of the response.

2. **From Explain (S15).** Foundation S15 ("Why Moonfare recommends this fund") ends its "Top reasons" list with a **"What should I ask about this fund?"** link → S25 with the same `fund_id`. This is the natural read-order: *why it's recommended → what to ask before you act.* The two surfaces share the citation-chip pattern so the transition feels continuous.

3. **From Fund-vs-Fund (S13 compare view).** When the user has A and B selected side-by-side, a **"Questions for {A}"** / **"Questions for {B}"** pair of links sits under each FundCard. Each opens S25 scoped to that single fund (S25 is single-fund; comparing question *sets* across two funds is out of scope for this surface and is not faked).

4. **From the rail / Ask Alpha (no fund context — Q5).** S25 also appears in the left rail (Foundation PART 3) under the "Understand" group as **"Smart Questions"**. Entered with no `fund_id` (e.g. the user typed "what should I ask given my portfolio?" into Ask Alpha), the surface runs the portfolio-context-only path: Half A returns DOC questions for the most-relevant open fund context where available, plus the doc-free DD-map themes, with `why_it_matters` driven by STRUCT hooks. When Ask Alpha answers a "what should I ask" question, its answer includes a **"Open the full question list"** chip that navigates here with the resolved `fund_id`.

**Within the frame.** S25 is a Detail-template surface (Foundation PART 3): the ProfileBar persists on top, the cohort builder rail persists on the left, Ask Alpha floats bottom-right, PDF export is available. Switching to S25 from any other surface is instant and full-screen. The cohort builder's **segment-relevant chips** (profession, performance, competence) are the same controls that drive Half B's segment selector — selecting "Best performers (Top 10%)" in the rail and selecting it in the S25 segment selector are wired to the same `resolve_segment` resolution, and the surface keeps them in sync.

---

### §3 Layout

**Grid (desktop ≥1280px).** S25 is a **two-section vertical stack inside the centre stage**, not a 2×2. This is one of the few surfaces where a short, deliberate vertical scroll is allowed *within the centre stage* — because the content is a reading list, not an analytical dashboard. (The Foundation's "no scroll on analytical views" rule, PART 0.2, governs chart grids; a question checklist is a document, and documents read top-to-bottom.) The ProfileBar, rail, and Ask Alpha stay fixed; only the question list scrolls.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ProfileBar (persistent)                                                   │
├───────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────┤
│ COHORT    │  FUND HEADER STRIP                                  │ Ask     │
│ BUILDER   │  Apex Buyout III · Apex · Mid-Cap Buyout · Open     │ Alpha   │
│ (left,    │  [Disclaimer footnote chip] [PDF export ▾]         │ (float) │
│ segment   │ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  │         │
│ chips     │  SECTION A  🟢  WORKS NOW                           │         │
│ mirror    │  "Questions to ask about Apex Buyout III,           │         │
│ Half B    │   given your portfolio"                             │         │
│ selector) │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │         │
│           │  │ DDQuestionCard (DOC)   [cite chip] [theme tag] │ │         │
│           │  │ DDQuestionCard (DOC)   [cite chip] [theme tag] │ │         │
│  Follower │  │ … DOC questions, ranked …                     │ │         │
│  controls │  │ DDQuestionCard (TEMPLATE) [theme tag] [why]    │ │         │
│           │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │         │
│           │  Dropped-themes footnote (collapsed)                │         │
│           │ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  │         │
│           │  SECTION B  🔴  NOT CONNECTED                       │         │
│           │  "Top questions your peers, PE pros &               │         │
│           │   best performers ask"                              │         │
│           │  [Segment selector: Peers · Cohort+Risk ·           │         │
│           │   Profession · Competent · Top performers ·         │         │
│           │   Moonfare IC]   [Lift ⇄ Miss mode toggle]          │         │
│           │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │         │
│           │  │  NotConnectedState (dashed, greyed preview)   │ │         │
│           │  │  "Live when the investor conversation feed    │ │         │
│           │  │   connects." + IC-theme fallback list         │ │         │
│           │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │         │
└───────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────┘
```

**Section A — "Questions to ask about {Fund}, given your portfolio" (🟢 WORKS NOW).**
A titled section with a small green-dot "Live" status pip (Foundation `--positive`/glyph, never colour-only — pip + the word "Live"). The title interpolates the fund name from `fund.fund_name`. Below it: a vertically stacked, **ranked list of `DDQuestionCard`s** (one per returned question, in `rank` order). DOC questions come first (ranked 1..n), TEMPLATE questions sort last (their `rank` is already ≥100 offset by the backend — §3.2 of T2-5). Each card is full-width, generous line-height (body 15/22), reads like a checklist item. Below the list, a quiet, collapsed **"Themes with no document match"** footnote lists `dropped_themes` in words so the user knows the surface didn't silently omit a theme — it *couldn't ground it* (this is a trust signal, not an error).

**Section B — "Top questions your peers, PE pros & best performers ask" (🔴 NOT CONNECTED).**
A titled section with an amber-dot "Not connected" status pip (`--not-connected`, dashed-outline language; pip + words). Directly under the title: the **SegmentSelector** (a segmented control) and a **Lift ⇄ Miss mode toggle**. Below them: the **NotConnectedState** card — the Foundation component, rendered as a designed dashed/greyed preview of the *intended* layout (a ranked list of peer questions with asker-share bars) overlaid with the not-connected message and the IC-theme fallback list. The selector and toggle are **fully interactive even in the not-connected state** (they change which segment/mode *would* be requested and update the message copy), but they resolve to the same not-connected payload until the feed lands — exactly as the Foundation prescribes for signal-gated surfaces (PART 9 state 5): "the layout is designed so the swap is purely data."

**Responsive.**
- **≥1280px** — three-column frame as above; both sections full-width in the centre stage; question cards single-column (they are wide reading rows, never side-by-side).
- **768–1279px** — cohort builder collapses to a drawer (Foundation PART 3); Ask Alpha collapses to a launcher pill; the two sections stack; the SegmentSelector wraps to two rows if needed; question cards stay single-column.
- **<768px (mobile / advisor on the go)** — single-column stacked; ProfileBar becomes a sheet; the fund header strip pins to the top of the centre stage; the SegmentSelector becomes a horizontally-scrollable chip strip (the *only* permitted horizontal scroll on this surface, and it carries no analytical content); citation chips remain tappable and open the snippet sheet as a bottom sheet rather than a popover. No question text is ever truncated; cards grow taller instead.

---

### §4 Components & charts

This surface is mostly **text + chips**, deliberately. There are no analytical charts in Section A (a question list is not a chart). Section B's *intended* (connected) state introduces one bar form. Components below are listed with their exact composition. Reused Foundation components are named, not re-specified.

#### 4.1 `FundHeaderStrip` (new, small)
A single-line header binding the `fund` block: `fund_name` (h2, large), then a row of meta — `manager` · `strategy` · open/closed pill from `is_open` (open = `--positive` dot + "Open"; closed = `--muted` + "Closed"). The persistent `disclaimer` footnote chip and the `PDFExportButton` sit at the right end of the strip. When entered with no `fund_id` (Q5), the strip reads "Smart Questions · your portfolio context" and the open/closed pill is suppressed.

#### 4.2 `DDQuestionCard` (new — the core component of Section A)
The repeating unit of Section A. Two visual variants keyed on `kind`:

**DOC variant** (`kind:"DOC"` — the preferred, cited form):
- **Rank index** — a small `--muted` numeral ("1", "2", …) at the leading edge, mono. Establishes "this is a ranked checklist."
- **Question text** — the interrogative `question` string, body 15/22, the visual focus of the card. Always an interrogative (the no-advice contract).
- **ThemeTag** — a small pill rendering the human label of `theme` (e.g. "Fees & waterfall", "Key-person risk"). Uses a fixed `dd_theme → colour` map from the `--data-*` ramp so a theme is the same colour everywhere on the surface (mirrors the Foundation's fixed strategy→colour rule). Hover shows the one-line "what it probes" definition from the frozen `dd_theme_map`.
- **Citation chip** — **the load-bearing element**, the inherited Foundation citation chip (§2.6 / S15 pattern). Renders as `[doc PPM · p.12]` (doc_type abbreviation + page). Clicking it opens the **doc-snippet popover** (§6) showing `section_path`, the verbatim `quote`, `doc_type`, `effective_date`, and `sim`. The chip is **mandatory** on every DOC card — a DOC card cannot render without at least one citation chip (it is a hard FE invariant mirroring backend gate G7; if `citations` is empty on a `kind:"DOC"` item, the FE drops the card and logs, never renders an uncited DOC question). When a card has multiple citations (post-dedupe union), the chip becomes `[PPM · p.12 +1]` and the popover paginates.
- **`why_it_matters` line** — when present (non-null), a single muted line below the question, prefixed by a small portfolio glyph: *"Why it matters for you — this fund's 20.0% carry is above the 17.5% median for open Mid-Cap Buyout funds…"*. This is the STRUCT hook. If its `evidence.provenance.synthetic == true`, a **SyntheticTag** (Foundation amber "illustrative" pill) attaches to the `why_it_matters` line (NOT to the question itself — the question is doc-grounded fact; the hook may rest on imputed fields like `risk_class`/`horizon_years`). The `evidence.value` is itself a number, so it carries a ProvenancePopover on click (Foundation invariant: no bare numbers).
- **Copy affordance** — a quiet "Copy" icon button on hover/focus (§6) that copies the question text (and optionally the citation reference) to clipboard.

**TEMPLATE variant** (`kind:"TEMPLATE"` — the degraded, doc-free form):
- Same layout, but **no citation chip** (it has no `citations`). In its place, a small `--muted` label **"Best-practice question"** so the user can tell at a glance it is a frozen, generic prompt rather than a document-grounded one.
- The `why_it_matters` line is **always present** on a TEMPLATE card (a TEMPLATE only exists because a STRUCT hook justified it — backend `HOOK_IS_SUFFICIENT`), and it carries its SyntheticTag/ProvenancePopover as above.
- TEMPLATE cards render with a slightly lighter card fill (`--surface` at reduced contrast against `--bg`) and always sort below DOC cards, so the visual hierarchy reads "grounded first, best-practice fallback after."

#### 4.3 `DroppedThemesFootnote` (new, small)
A collapsed, single-line `--muted` row beneath the Section A list: **"3 themes had no document match: fund leverage, ESG & conflicts, liquidity terms."** Bound to `dropped_themes`. Expanding it shows each dropped theme with its one-line "what it probes" definition and the reason ("no document chunk cleared the relevance threshold, and your portfolio didn't flag it"). This makes the *absence* of a question legible and trustworthy — the surface is saying "we didn't skip this; we couldn't ground it." Suppressed entirely when `dropped_themes` is empty.

#### 4.4 `SegmentSelector` (Section B — the segment control)
A segmented control (Foundation ComparisonSelector idiom) with the segments from `resolve_segment`:
`Peers · Cohort + Risk · Profession · Competent · Top performers · Moonfare IC`.
- "Peers" is the default and resolves to the anchor investor's cohort (people-like-you).
- "Profession" expands to a small secondary chip group (e.g. "Investor/PE", "Financial Services") when selected, binding `values`.
- Selecting a segment sets the `segment` (and `values`) that *would* be requested from `/api/peer_questions`. In not_connected, this updates the NotConnectedState message ("Top questions PE professionals ask — live when the feed connects") without firing a successful data render.
- This control is **mirrored by the cohort-builder rail's** profession/performance/competence chips (§2): changing one updates the other.

#### 4.5 `LiftMissToggle` (Section B — mode control)
A small two-state toggle: **"Asks more than peers" (lift, Q3)** ⇄ **"My cohort misses" (miss, Q6)**. Disabled/greyed for segments where the comparison is undefined (e.g. "Peers" has no lift-vs-itself; "Moonfare IC" has no miss). When enabled, it sets `compare_to=peers` and `mode=lift|miss`. In not_connected it only reshapes the preview/message copy.

#### 4.6 `PeerQuestionList` — the *intended* (connected) Section B render
**Specified now so the connect is purely data (Foundation acceptance).** When `status:"connected"` and not gated: a ranked list of `PeerQuestionRow`s, each = the `question_norm` paraphrase (display text — never raw transcript), a `dd_theme` ThemeTag (or "general" when `dd_theme` is null), and a **horizontal asker-share bar** (the one chart on this surface) showing `asker_share` as a `--data-*` fill with the **% inline on the bar** (Foundation chart rule). In lift mode each row also shows `lift` (e.g. "2.4× vs peers") and `baseline_share`; in miss mode, `cohort_share` and the `gap`. A CaptionBlock under the list: *"Share of distinct investors in this segment who asked each question. Counts each investor once."* A ConfidenceBadge renders `n_askers`. This entire block sits **inside** the NotConnectedState as the greyed, dashed preview until the feed connects.

#### 4.7 `NotConnectedState` (Section B default — Foundation component, verbatim)
Parameterized `feed = "investor conversation"`. Renders the dashed, greyed `PeerQuestionList` preview (above) behind a calm overlay: the headline **"Live when the investor conversation feed connects,"** the `reason` line ("Peer question logs aren't connected yet"), and — crucially — the **IC-theme fallback list** from `fallback.themes` (Q7 answerable now): *"Until peer logs connect, these are the themes Moonfare's own IC and diligence documents emphasize for this fund: Key-person risk · Fees & waterfall · Prior-fund consistency · Deployment pace · Valuation policy."* The fallback themes render as ThemeTags identical to Section A's, each linking back to the matching Section A card (so "what the IC asks" is grounded in the same cited questions). A small `ready` indicator ("Built and tested — connects with zero rework") sits in the footer, matching the Foundation's "designed so the swap is purely data" promise. This is **never** an error state — no red, no broken-chart iconography.

---

### §5 Data bindings (element ← endpoint.field)

**Half A — `GET /api/dd_questions?fund_id={fund_id}&investor_id={investor_id?}&audience=LP` (🟢 live).**

| UI element | ← binds to |
|---|---|
| FundHeaderStrip name / manager / strategy / open pill | `fund.{fund_name, manager, strategy, is_open}` |
| Section A "Live" pip | `corpus_ready` (true → "Live, document-grounded"; false → "Portfolio-context only", §7 state 2) |
| `DDQuestionCard` list (ordered) | `questions[]` sorted by `rank` |
| Card rank index | `questions[i].rank` |
| Card question text | `questions[i].question` |
| Card ThemeTag | `questions[i].theme` (label + colour from frozen `dd_theme_map`) |
| Card variant (DOC vs TEMPLATE) | `questions[i].kind` |
| Citation chip(s) | `questions[i].citations[]` → chip label from `citation.doc_type` + `citation.page` |
| Doc-snippet popover body | `citation.{section_path, quote, doc_type, effective_date, sim}` |
| `why_it_matters` line | `questions[i].why_it_matters` (suppress when null) |
| SyntheticTag on `why_it_matters` | `questions[i].evidence.provenance.synthetic == true` |
| `why_it_matters` ProvenancePopover | `questions[i].evidence.{metric, value, provenance}` |
| DroppedThemesFootnote | `dropped_themes[]` |
| `dd_map` (theme taxonomy, used for tag labels & dropped-theme defs) | `dd_map[]` (always present — frozen, doc-free) |
| Persistent disclaimer footnote | `disclaimer` |
| Surface-level provenance footer | `provenance.{snapshot_id, embed_model, reference_version}` |
| Section B inlined status (when reading the combined payload) | `peer_questions.{status, ready}` |

Notes on Half A binding behaviour:
- `investor_id` omitted (Q5 no-anchor) → every `why_it_matters`/`evidence` is null → all `why_it_matters` lines suppress, TEMPLATE cards are absent (none returned), only DOC cards render.
