- `corpus_ready:false` → `questions[]` is TEMPLATE-only → no citation chips anywhere; the "Live" pip switches to "Portfolio-context only" (amber, not error); `dd_map` still renders the full taxonomy.
- `404 {"error":"unknown fund"}` → surface renders the refusal state (§7 state 7), no fabricated cards.

**Half B — `GET /api/peer_questions?segment={segment}&fund_id={fund_id?}&values={json?}&anchor_investor_id={investor_id?}&compare_to={str?}&mode={lift|miss?}` (🔴 not_connected default).**

| UI element | ← binds to |
|---|---|
| Section B status pip + NotConnectedState | `status` (`"not_connected"` → render NotConnectedState; `"connected"` → render PeerQuestionList) |
| NotConnectedState reason line | `reason` |
| "Built and tested" footer indicator | `ready` && `scaffold_validated` |
| IC-theme fallback ThemeTags | `fallback.themes[]` (when `fallback.source == "moonfare_ic_doc_themes"`) |
| `PeerQuestionRow` text | `questions[i].question_norm` |
| Row ThemeTag | `questions[i].dd_theme` (or "general" when null) |
| Row asker-share bar (% inline) | `questions[i].asker_share` |
| Row lift annotation (lift mode) | `questions[i].lift`, `questions[i].baseline_share` |
| Row miss annotation (miss mode) | `questions[i].cohort_share`, `questions[i].gap` |
| ConfidenceBadge | `provenance.n` / `segment.resolved_n` / `n_askers` |
| Gated card | `gated == true` → `reason` (`k_anon`|`no_consent_eligible_signal`), `n`, `min`, `gated_message` |
| Synthetic flag on a connected-but-fixture cluster | `provenance.synthetic` → SyntheticTag on the whole Section B block |

Notes on Half B binding behaviour:
- The surface reads Section B from the **inlined `peer_questions`** field of the combined `/api/dd_questions` response for the default render, and re-fetches the standalone `/api/peer_questions` only when the user changes the SegmentSelector / LiftMissToggle (so the segment-specific message copy is correct). Both default to not_connected.
- Single base-URL swap to a live backend turns the NotConnectedState into the PeerQuestionList with **no FE change** — the dashed preview *is* the connected layout.

---

### §6 Interactions

1. **Segment selector (Section B).** Click a segment → updates the `segment` (+ secondary `values` chips for Profession) → re-requests `/api/peer_questions` → in not_connected, the NotConnectedState message updates to name the segment ("Top questions best performers ask — live when the feed connects") and the fallback IC themes remain shown. Selection is reflected in the cohort-builder rail and vice-versa. Keyboard: arrow keys move between segments, Enter selects (it's a radio-group segmented control, ARIA `role="radiogroup"`).

2. **Lift ⇄ Miss toggle.** Switches Q3 (lift) vs Q6 (miss) framing. Disabled where undefined (Peers/IC). In not_connected it reshapes the preview header ("What PE pros ask *more* than peers" vs "What your cohort *misses*") without a successful data render.

3. **Click a citation chip → doc snippet (Section A, the signature interaction).** Clicking `[PPM · p.12]` opens a **doc-snippet popover** (desktop) / **bottom sheet** (mobile) anchored to the chip. It shows: `section_path` as a breadcrumb ("Economic Terms › Distribution Waterfall"), the verbatim `quote` in a quoted/monospace block (the exact text that grounds the question — this is the provenance), `doc_type` + `effective_date` + `sim` ("relevance 0.69") in a meta row. A "view full document" link is present but **gated** (Foundation GatedState copy if the user lacks doc access) — the *quote* is always shown (it's what grounds the displayed question), but the full document may require entitlement. The popover is the same component family as the Foundation ProvenancePopover; it dismisses on outside-click / Esc. Multiple citations paginate within the popover.

4. **Copy a question.** Hovering (or focusing) a `DDQuestionCard` reveals a "Copy" button. Clicking copies the question text to clipboard and shows a brief, calm "Copied" toast. A small caret on the Copy button offers **"Copy with citation"** which appends a plain-text reference line (e.g. *"— Apex Buyout III PPM, p.12, effective 2026-01-20"*) for TEMPLATE questions it copies the "Best-practice question" label instead of a citation. A surface-level **"Copy all questions"** action sits beside the PDF export, copying the full checklist as a numbered list with citations — this is the "take this into your call with the GP" affordance.

5. **Provenance everywhere a number appears.** The `why_it_matters` numeric (`evidence.value`, e.g. "+2.5pp carry vs peer median") is a clickable provenance chip → ProvenancePopover showing `metric`, `value`, and `evidence.provenance.{method, synthetic, snapshot_id, vintage_scope}`. The Section B asker-share % (when connected) and `n_askers` likewise carry ConfidenceBadge + ProvenancePopover. No number on this surface renders bare (Foundation PART 0.2).

6. **Theme tag hover/click.** Hovering a ThemeTag shows the frozen "what it probes" definition from `dd_theme_map`. The dropped-themes footnote expands the same definitions for un-grounded themes. A ThemeTag in the Section B IC-fallback list is clickable → scrolls/anchors to the matching Section A DOC card for that theme (Q7's "what the IC asks" → the actual cited question).

7. **PDF export.** Exports the current S25 view as a clean, PowerPoint-style PDF (Foundation PDFExportButton): the fund header, the full ranked DD-question list with citations rendered as footnote references, the dropped-themes note, and — for Section B — the not-connected notice with the IC fallback themes (so a printed checklist is honest about what isn't peer-mined yet). The disclaimer prints as a footer on every page.

8. **Ask Alpha hand-off.** Asking Ask Alpha "what should I ask about this fund?" produces a five-sentence summary whose numbers are provenance chips, plus an "Open the full question list" chip → navigates to S25 with the resolved `fund_id`. Conversely, S25 cards have no inline chat; the conversation lives in the floating pane (one chat, Foundation PART 5).

---

### §7 Seven states (Foundation PART 9 — each branch made exact for S25)

Generative (Half A) **works now**; peer-mining (Half B) is **not_connected** by default. Both render the full catalog.

1. **Happy.** `corpus_ready:true`; Section A shows the full ranked list — DOC cards (each cited, G7) ranked first, TEMPLATE card(s) last; `why_it_matters` lines present where hooks fired; `dropped_themes` footnote present and honest. Section B shows the NotConnectedState with the IC-theme fallback (this *is* the happy state for Section B until the feed lands). Combined payload: Half A live + inlined `peer_questions:{status:"not_connected"}`.

2. **Corpus-not-indexed (Half A).** `corpus_ready:false`. Section A "Live" pip → amber **"Portfolio-context only"** (not error). `questions[]` are TEMPLATE-only — no citation chips; a quiet banner: *"Document-grounded questions aren't available for this fund yet — showing portfolio-context prompts. The best-practice question map is still below."* `dd_map` still renders the full 10-theme taxonomy as a reference list. Requires a non-cold-start investor (TEMPLATE needs a hook); a cold-start investor in this state → see state 4.

3. **Thin docs / thin segment.** Half A: some themes cite, others fall into `dropped_themes`; TEMPLATE cards backfill the HOOK_IS_SUFFICIENT themes. The dropped-themes footnote carries the (likely longer) list. Half B (when connected): `resolve_cohort` relaxes dims → a **RelaxBanner** (Foundation) renders in the Section B header in words ("Broadened to investors in your wealth & risk band"); or the segment falls below k-anon → state-6-style gated card.

4. **Cold-start investor** (`investor.n_funds == 0`). Half A: `struct_hook` returns None for every theme → **no TEMPLATE cards, no `why_it_matters` lines** — but **DOC cards still render** (the documents don't depend on holdings). A gentle note: *"These are document-grounded questions for this fund. Once you hold investments, we'll also flag which matter most for your specific portfolio."* `cold_start:true` drives the note. Section B: the anchor cohort is still resolvable by KYC dims, so the segment selector works, but the data is still not_connected.

5. **Not connected (Half B — the DEFAULT).** `/api/peer_questions` → `status:"not_connected"`. Section B renders the NotConnectedState with the `moonfare_ic_doc_themes` fallback and the "Built and tested — connects with zero rework" footer. The SegmentSelector and LiftMissToggle are interactive and reshape the message but never error. This is the expected, designed steady state for Section B, framed as "pending," not "broken."

6. **Consent-empty (Half B, when feed wired but no consent-eligible signal).** `gated:true, reason:"no_consent_eligible_signal", n:0`. Section B shows a calm gated card: *"No investors in this segment have consented to share their questions yet."* No counts, no fabrication. Distinct from k-anon gating (`reason:"k_anon"`, `n` below `min:15`): *"Too few investors in this segment asked questions to report without re-identification risk."* — uses the Foundation GatedState language family but **not** the paid-viewer copy (this is a privacy floor, not an entitlement gate).

7. **Error / out-of-universe.** Unknown `fund_id` → `404 {"error":"unknown fund"}` → S25 renders a **refusal state**: a calm card *"We don't have this fund in the platform universe, so we can't generate diligence questions for it,"* no fabricated fund name, no cards, an Ask-Alpha prompt to search known funds. Unknown anchor `investor_id` → `404 unknown investor` (Section B only). Unknown `segment` → `400` (selector reverts to "Peers" with a quiet inline notice). Engine/model degraded → Foundation error state: cached last-good question list (if any) under a quiet banner; never a blank, never a fabricated question.

Plus the always-on overlays: **not-significant** deltas greyed; **SyntheticTag** on every imputed `why_it_matters`; **loading** = calm skeleton cards (grey question-row placeholders, no spinners).

---

### §8 Copy deck

All strings are i18n keys (English shown; structure for English + German per Foundation PART 10).

**Surface & section titles**
- Surface (rail label): **"Smart Questions"**
- Section A title: **"Questions to ask about {fund_name}, given your portfolio"**
- Section A title (no fund / Q5): **"Questions to ask, given your portfolio"**
- Section A "Live" pip: **"Live · document-grounded"** · amber variant: **"Portfolio-context only"**
- Section B title: **"Top questions your peers, PE pros & best performers ask"**
- Section B status pip: **"Not connected · pending feed"**

**Section A body**
- DOC card meta (none — the question carries itself).
- TEMPLATE card label: **"Best-practice question"**
- `why_it_matters` prefix: **"Why it matters for you — "**
- Citation chip format: **"{DOC_TYPE} · p.{page}"** (e.g. "PPM · p.12"); multi: **"{DOC_TYPE} · p.{page} +{k}"**
- Doc-snippet popover meta: **"{DOC_TYPE} · effective {effective_date} · relevance {sim}"**
- Dropped-themes footnote (collapsed): **"{n} themes had no document match — {comma-list}."**
- Dropped-themes expanded reason: **"No document passage cleared the relevance threshold, and your portfolio didn't flag this theme."**
- Corpus-empty banner: **"Document-grounded questions aren't available for this fund yet — showing portfolio-context prompts. The best-practice question map is still below."**
- Cold-start note: **"These are document-grounded questions for this fund. Once you hold investments, we'll also flag which matter most for your specific portfolio."**
- Copy toast: **"Copied"** · Copy menu: **"Copy question" / "Copy with citation" / "Copy all questions"**

**Section B body**
- NotConnectedState headline: **"Live when the investor conversation feed connects."**
- NotConnectedState reason: **"Peer question logs aren't connected yet."**
- NotConnectedState IC-fallback lead-in: **"Until peer logs connect, these are the themes Moonfare's own investment committee and diligence documents emphasize for this fund:"**
- "Built and tested" footer: **"Built and tested — connects with zero rework."**
- Gated (k-anon): **"Too few investors in this segment asked questions to report without re-identification risk."**
- Gated (no consent): **"No investors in this segment have consented to share their questions yet."**
- Segment labels: **"Peers" · "Cohort + Risk" · "Profession" · "Competent investors" · "Top performers" · "Moonfare IC"**
- Lift/Miss toggle: **"Asks more than peers" / "My cohort misses"**
- Connected list caption: **"Share of distinct investors in this segment who asked each question. Counts each investor once."**

**Refusal / errors**
- Out-of-universe: **"We don't have this fund in the platform universe, so we can't generate diligence questions for it."**
- Degraded: **"Showing the last available questions. Live generation is temporarily unavailable."**

**Disclaimer (persistent footnote, also on PDF footer)**
- **"A due-diligence question checklist grounded in Moonfare's documents and your portfolio context. Not personalized investment advice."** (bound from `disclaimer`).

**No-advice copy guard (enforced).** No string on this surface may match `\b(you should|we recommend you|buy now|sell now)\b`. Questions are interrogative ("Ask the GP…"); hooks read "your portfolio shows…". This mirrors the backend's no-advice assertion (T2-5 §6) and is checked in the FE copy lint.

---

### §9 Badge rendering — provenance & the citation chip (gate G7)

This is the surface's defining trust mechanic, so it is specified explicitly.

**The citation chip = the rendering of gate G7.** Backend gate G7 says: *every generated DOC question is grounded in a retrieved document chunk and carries a citation, or it is dropped.* The frontend mirrors this as a hard invariant: **a `DDQuestionCard` of `kind:"DOC"` MUST render at least one citation chip; if `citations` is empty, the FE does not render the card.** There is no "DOC question without a chip" visual — it cannot exist. This is the on-screen embodiment of "the engine grounds; the UI presents" (Foundation PART 0.2). The chip is the Foundation's grounded-claim chip (the same one used on S15 Explain), so a user who has seen Explain recognizes it instantly.

**What the chip carries.** Label = `doc_type` abbreviation + page (`PPM · p.12`). Click → popover with `section_path` (where in the doc), `quote` (the verbatim grounding text), `effective_date` (how current), `sim` (how relevant). The `quote` is the heart of provenance: the user reads the exact line that produced the question. This satisfies the Foundation rule that every claim show "show how this was computed / show the data."

**TEMPLATE cards carry no citation chip — by design.** A TEMPLATE question is doc-free (no chunk cleared the floor) and is justified instead by the portfolio STRUCT hook. It renders the **"Best-practice question"** label in the chip's place so the user never mistakes a generic prompt for a document-grounded one. Its `why_it_matters` line carries the provenance of the *hook* (not of a document).

**SyntheticTag placement.** The amber "illustrative" SyntheticTag (Foundation §2.6) attaches **only to the `why_it_matters` hook line**, never to the question text — because the question (DOC) is grounded fact, while the hook may rest on synthetic fields (`risk_class`, `horizon_years`, `pm` projections) per gate G8. Concretely: `concentration` and `liquidity_terms` hooks carry SyntheticTag (`evidence.provenance.synthetic == true`); `fee_waterfall` and `prior_fund_consistency` hooks (real `carry` / `fund_track_record`) carry **no** SyntheticTag (`synthetic == false`). The FE reads `synthetic` per-question and tags accordingly — it does not blanket-tag the surface.

**ConfidenceBadge (Section B, connected).** `n_askers` renders as a ConfidenceBadge (`n=31`) on the peer cluster; an asker-share below the k-anon floor never reaches the FE (backend gates first). In not_connected, no ConfidenceBadge renders (there is no n) — the NotConnectedState carries the "built and tested" indicator instead.

**Provenance footer.** The surface footer renders `provenance.{snapshot_id, embed_model, reference_version}` as a quiet "grounded · snapshot {id} · {embed_model}" line, matching Ask Alpha's per-answer footer (Foundation PART 5) so provenance reads consistently across the product.

---

### §10 Responsive & accessibility

**Responsive** (recap of §3, with the rules made explicit):
- The **question list is always single-column** at every breakpoint — questions are reading rows, never tiled. Cards grow taller to fit text; text is never truncated or clipped.
- The **only permitted horizontal scroll** on this surface is the mobile SegmentSelector chip strip, which carries no analytical content (Foundation: no analytical chart needs horizontal scroll — honoured, because Section A has no chart and Section B's one bar is in a vertical list).
- Citation chips: popover on desktop, **bottom sheet** on mobile (full-width, dismiss by swipe/Esc) so the quote is legible on a phone.
- The cohort-builder rail collapses to a drawer 768–1279px and a sheet <768px; its segment chips stay synced to the SegmentSelector.

**Accessibility** (WCAG-AA, Foundation PART 10):
- **Never colour-only.** Status pips pair colour with a word ("Live" / "Not connected") and a glyph (filled dot / dashed ring). DOC-vs-TEMPLATE distinction is carried by the **"Best-practice question" label**, not by fill colour alone. The lift/miss direction is carried by the multiplier text ("2.4×") and a glyph, not colour.
- **Keyboard.** Full tab order: fund header → each DDQuestionCard (card focusable; Enter activates the citation popover; the Copy button is a separate tab stop) → dropped-themes disclosure → SegmentSelector (radiogroup, arrow keys) → LiftMissToggle. Focus rings on every interactive element (Foundation focus states). Esc closes popovers/sheets and returns focus to the chip.
- **Screen readers.** Each DDQuestionCard is a `listitem` in an ordered `list` with an accessible name = the question text; the citation chip has `aria-label="Citation: {doc_type} page {page}, opens source quote"`; the ThemeTag has `aria-label="Theme: {theme label}"`; the SyntheticTag is announced as "illustrative — based on estimated data." The NotConnectedState region has `aria-live="polite"` so segment changes are announced ("Now showing: top questions PE professionals ask — pending feed").
- **Chart-as-table.** The Section B asker-share bars (connected state) expose a "view data" affordance giving a table of question / asker-share / lift|gap (Foundation PART 10 chart-data rule).
- **Quote legibility.** The citation `quote` renders in a quoted block with sufficient contrast and a min 15px size; long quotes scroll within the popover, never truncate (the quote is the evidence — it is never cut off).
- **Reduced motion.** No flashy transitions (Foundation PART 0.2); the "Copied" toast and popover fades respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.

---

### §11 Acceptance checklist

A reviewer can verify S25 against these. The two headline acceptances (from the brief) are first.

- [ ] **Generated questions cite the fund document (G7, headline).** Every `DDQuestionCard` of `kind:"DOC"` renders at least one citation chip; clicking it shows the verbatim `quote`, `section_path`, `doc_type`, `effective_date`, and `sim`. No DOC card renders without a citation chip (uncited DOC items are dropped by the FE, never shown).
- [ ] **Peer-mining shows not_connected until fed (headline).** Section B renders the NotConnectedState (dashed, designed preview), the `moonfare_ic_doc_themes` fallback list, and the "built and tested — connects with zero rework" indicator. It is never an error. A base-URL swap to a live, connected backend turns the same layout into the `PeerQuestionList` with no FE change.
- [ ] Section A title interpolates `fund.fund_name`; entered with no `fund_id` it reads the portfolio-context title and suppresses the open/closed pill.
- [ ] DOC cards rank above TEMPLATE cards; TEMPLATE cards show the "Best-practice question" label and **no** citation chip.
- [ ] `why_it_matters` renders only when non-null; carries a SyntheticTag iff `evidence.provenance.synthetic == true` (concentration/liquidity tagged; fee/prior-fund not); its `evidence.value` is a clickable provenance chip — no bare number.
- [ ] DroppedThemesFootnote binds `dropped_themes` and explains the absence; suppressed when empty. `dd_map` always renders the full 10-theme taxonomy (even with empty corpus or no investor).
- [ ] Corpus-empty (`corpus_ready:false`) → TEMPLATE-only, no chips, amber "Portfolio-context only" pip + banner, no error.
- [ ] Cold-start (`n_funds==0`) → DOC cards render, no TEMPLATE cards, no `why_it_matters`, cold-start note shown.
- [ ] Out-of-universe `fund_id` → calm refusal card, no fabricated fund name, no cards.
- [ ] SegmentSelector (Peers/Cohort+Risk/Profession/Competent/Top performers/Moonfare IC) and Lift⇄Miss toggle are interactive in not_connected and reshape the message copy; synced with the cohort-builder rail; Lift/Miss disabled where undefined.
- [ ] Gated states distinct: k-anon (`n < 15`) and no-consent (`n:0`) use privacy copy, **not** the paid-viewer copy.
- [ ] Copy-a-question works (with/without citation); "Copy all questions" produces a numbered checklist with citation references; PDF export prints the list + citations + not-connected notice + disclaimer footer.
- [ ] Persistent `disclaimer` footnote present; no string matches the no-advice forbidden patterns; all questions are interrogative.
- [ ] Provenance footer renders `snapshot_id`/`embed_model`/`reference_version`; Section B ConfidenceBadge renders `n_askers` only when connected.
- [ ] A11y: status pips and DOC/TEMPLATE distinction never colour-only; full keyboard nav; citation popover ↔ mobile bottom sheet; quotes never truncate; `aria-live` on the not-connected region; chart-as-table on connected asker-share bars.
- [ ] Responsive: question list single-column at every breakpoint; no analytical horizontal scroll; rail collapses per Foundation breakpoints.

---
*Build to this brief on top of the Frontend Foundation. The citation chip, NotConnectedState, SyntheticTag, ProvenancePopover, ComparisonSelector idiom, GatedState, RelaxBanner, PDFExportButton, and the seven-state catalog come from the Foundation — reuse them, don't redefine them. The one thing this surface must get exactly right: a generated question never appears without the document line that grounds it, and the peer half never pretends to be connected when it isn't.*
